EDCS-00380791.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudius Eutyches Claudii Catulli Galli clarissimi viri libertus sibi et Rosulae \n
EDCS-00380791.translation: To the Divine Shades. Claudius Eutyches, freedman of the most distinguished Claudius Catullus Gallus, for himself and Rosula.
EDCS-00380791.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Claudius Eutyches, a freedman, for himself and his companion Rosula, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-00380792.text_edition: \n Apollini Gangarensi Aurelius Capito votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380792.translation: To Apollo Gangarensis, Aurelius Capito gladly paid his vow, deservedly.
EDCS-00380792.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Apollo Gangarensis by Aurelius Capito, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-00380793.text_edition: \n Tattaris Veneti filius? et Temus Batonis filia Narensai sic Annaei nnorum XXV hic sita est\n
EDCS-00380793.translation: Tattaris, son of Venetus(?), and Temus, daughter of Batonis, Narensis, wife of Annaeus, aged 25, is buried here.
EDCS-00380793.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Temus, wife of Annaeus, recording her age and parentage, likely from a provincial context.

EDCS-00380795.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus ? Cornelio liae Pontinae Photina et Iu parentibus posuerunt\n
EDCS-00380795.translation: To the Divine Shades. Photina and Iu [---] placed [this] for their parents Cornelio [---]iae Pontinae.
EDCS-00380795.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where Photina and Iu commemorate their parents, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-00380796.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Varia Valentina.
EDCS-00380796.summary: This is a simple funerary epitaph dedicating a tomb to Varia Valentina, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-00380797.text_edition: \n Marcus Conius Marci libertus Quartus negotiator cretarius \n
EDCS-00380797.translation: Marcus Conius Quartus, freedman of Marcus, a Cretan merchant.
EDCS-00380797.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Conius Quartus, a merchant from Crete, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-00380798.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records military service or honors, but is too incomplete for a definitive summary.

EDCS-00380801.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Cai filius Velina domo Pessinunte veteranus legionis VII nnorum XLV stipendiorum XXV hic situs est Caius Mummius posuit\n
EDCS-00380801.translation: Gaius Julius Velina, son of Gaius, from Pessinus, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, aged 45, with 25 years of service, is buried here. Gaius Mummius placed [this].
EDCS-00380801.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Velina, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, detailing his age and service, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-00380802.text_edition: \n Severus Triochari filius Ubius eques alae Tungrorum nnorum XXVI stipendiorum V hic situs est Urbanus fratri posuit\n
EDCS-00380802.translation: Severus, son of Triocharus, an equestrian of the Wing of the Tungri, aged 26, with 5 years of service, is buried here. Urbanus placed [this] for his brother.
EDCS-00380802.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Severus, an auxiliary cavalryman, detailing his age and service, with his brother Urbanus as the dedicator.

EDCS-00380803.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Barcenei filius Theodorus miles cohortis II Cyrrhestarum nnum LXV stipendiorum XLII hic situs est posuit Caius Iulius Scapula frater\n
EDCS-00380803.translation: Gaius Julius Theodorus Barcenei, a soldier of the Second Cohort of Cyrrhestarum, aged 65, with 42 years of service, is buried here. Gaius Julius Scapula, his brother, placed [this].
EDCS-00380803.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Theodorus Barcenei, a soldier in the auxiliary infantry, detailing his age and service, erected by his brother.

EDCS-00380804.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Andromachus miles cohortis II Cyrrestarum nnorum LXV stipendiorum XXXX Caius Iulius Apolla sodalis votum posuit\n
EDCS-00380804.translation: Caius Julius Andromachus, soldier of the 2nd Cohort of Cyrrestae, aged 65, with 40 years of service, erected this vow as a member of the sodality. Caius Julius Apollia also participated.
EDCS-00380804.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Caius Julius Andromachus, a soldier, likely to himself or a fellow member of his cohort, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380805.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Papiriae Metellicae defunctae nnorum XXXIII Lucius Oclatius Hyginus coniugi bene merenti et Arriis Mercurio et Aristioni et Papiriae Isiadi et I Papiriae Thasidi \n
EDCS-00380805.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Papiria Metellica, deceased, aged 33. Lucius Oclatius Hyginus placed this for his deserving wife, and for Arrius Mercurius, Arrius Aristio, Papiria Isiadi, and Papiria Thasidi.
EDCS-00380805.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucius Oclatius Hyginus for his wife Papiria Metellica and other relatives, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380806.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Prima mater Crispinae Titi libertae defunctae nnorum XVIII posuit\n
EDCS-00380806.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Prima placed this for Crispinia, freedwoman of Titus, deceased, aged 18.
EDCS-00380806.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Prima for her deceased relative Crispinia, a freedwoman, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380808.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Marcellina mater Aureliae Marcellae filiae pientissimae defunctae nnnorum XVIII sibi et posteris suis posuit\n
EDCS-00380808.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aurelia Marcellina placed this for her most dutiful daughter Aurelia Marcella, deceased, aged 18, for herself and her descendants.
EDCS-00380808.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Aurelia Marcellina for her daughter Aurelia Marcella, also reserving a place for herself and her descendants, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380809.text_edition: \n Publiae Aeliae Publi filiae Panoni coniugi karissimae Titus Curiatius Seneca maritus IIvir quinquennalis Delmin(iensium) \n
EDCS-00380809.translation: To Publia Aelia Panon, daughter of Publius, his dearest wife. Titus Curiatius Seneca, husband, quinquennial magistrate of the Delminians.
EDCS-00380809.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Titus Curiatius Seneca, a magistrate, to his wife Publia Aelia Panon, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380810.text_edition: \n Publius Aelius Quintus scriba publicus Delminiensium votum reddidit libens\n
EDCS-00380810.translation: Publius Aelius Quintus, public scribe of the Delminians, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-00380810.summary: This is a dedication recording the fulfillment of a vow by Publius Aelius Quintus, a public official of the Delminians, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380811.summary: This is a brief dedicatory inscription to Jupiter by Valeria Rentina, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380812.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Oppia Tertulla Volsi filia votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-00380812.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Volsi daughter Oppia Tertulla willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-00380812.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Oppia Tertulla, daughter of Volsius, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380815.text_edition: \n T Arthinus |(centurio |(milliariae voto suscepto Herculi votum solvit\n
EDCS-00380815.translation: T. Arthinus, centurion of the milliary cohort, fulfilled his vow to Hercules after undertaking it.
EDCS-00380815.summary: This is a votive inscription by T. Arthinus, a centurion, to Hercules, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380816.text_edition: \n Priscus et Cinnamia se vivus sic arcam posuerunt et locum sepulturae aedificaverunt COI si quis volueret sepultus sic vexare dabet fisco folles CC\n
EDCS-00380816.translation: Priscus and Cinnamia placed this tomb and built this burial place for themselves while alive. If anyone wishes to be buried here and disturb it, they will owe a fine of 200 coins to the treasury.
EDCS-00380816.summary: This is an inscription establishing a family burial plot with specific rules and penalties for unauthorized use, set up by Priscus and Cinnamia during their lifetime, dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-00380817.text_edition: \n vixit annos vixit nnos vixit nnos vixit nnos ius Marci filius Epic et Hermione \n
EDCS-00380817.translation: He lived [number illegible]. He lived [number illegible]. He lived [number illegible]. He lived [number illegible]. [Name illegible], son of Marcus, Epic and Hermione.
EDCS-00380817.summary: This is a funerary inscription likely recording the lifespans and names of individuals, with significant portions missing.

EDCS-00380819.text_edition: \n Gemello Calpurni Gemelli servo vixit nnos XXV Servius Calpurnius Epaphroditus bene merenti\n
EDCS-00380819.translation: To Gemellus, slave of Calpurnius Gemellus, who lived 25 years. Servius Calpurnius Epaphroditus placed this for him as deserving.
EDCS-00380819.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a master, Servius Calpurnius Epaphroditus, for his deserving slave Gemellus, who died at age 25.

EDCS-00380820.summary: This fragment of an inscription records the name Aper and the beginning of a lifespan notice, with the rest illegible.

EDCS-00380821.text_edition: \n Titus Carienus? --- Capitonis filius testamento f(ieri)? i(ussit)? hoc? monimentum heeredem non sequetur FVIS \n
EDCS-00380821.translation: Titus Carienus? Capito's son, perhaps ordered this monument to be made by will; it will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-00380821.summary: This inscription seems to be a funerary monument ordered by Titus Carienus? Capito, specifying that the monument should not be inherited.

EDCS-03000733.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Diana by Claudius Niger, with the rest of the inscription lost.

EDCS-03000737.summary: This inscription is a fragment that likely contains names, with both the beginning and end lost.

EDCS-03000740.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Urgulanio Theophilo Sempronia Quarta marito rarissimo bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-03000740.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Sempronia Quarta placed this for her very rare husband, Publius Urgulanius Theophilus, for his deserving merit.
EDCS-03000740.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a wife, Sempronia Quarta, to her exceptional husband, Publius Urgulanius Theophilus.

EDCS-03000741.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Semproniae Quartae Publius Urgulanius Ursus matri piissimae posuit\n
EDCS-03000741.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Publius Urgulanius Ursus placed this for his most dutiful mother, Sempronia Quarta.
EDCS-03000741.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a son, Publius Urgulanius Ursus, to his devoted mother, Sempronia Quarta.

EDCS-03000742.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Praetorinae Licinianus pientissimae conservae posuit annorum XXXXI\n
EDCS-03000742.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Licinianus placed this for his most dutiful companion, Praetorina, aged 41.
EDCS-03000742.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Licinianus for his faithful companion, Praetorina, who died at the age of 41.

EDCS-03000743.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Modesto defuncto nnorum XXXX Convictus posuit\n
EDCS-03000743.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Convictus placed this for Modestus, deceased at the age of 40.
EDCS-03000743.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Convictus for Modestus, who died at the age of 40.

EDCS-03000744.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Laeliae Saturninae matri bene merenti\n
EDCS-03000744.translation: To Laelia Saturnina, a most deserving mother.
EDCS-03000744.summary: This is a funerary dedication to a mother, Laelia Saturnina.

EDCS-03000745.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording a person's name, Euphrosyne.

EDCS-03000747.text_edition: \n marito o<p=R>timo Caetennia Thiodora sic uxor posuit bene merenti \n
EDCS-03000747.translation: Caetennia Thiodora set this up for her most excellent husband, a deserving man.
EDCS-03000747.summary: This funerary monument was erected by a wife, Caetennia Thiodora, for her husband.

EDCS-03000748.text_edition: \n Caius Lollius Cai filius Papiria Valens veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis vivus fecit sibi et Hostiliae Publi filiae Secundae coniugi sanctissimae\n
EDCS-03000748.translation: Gaius Lollius Valens, son of Gaius, of the Papiria tribe, veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion, pious and faithful, made this in his lifetime for himself and for Hostilia Secunda, daughter of Publius, his most chaste wife.
EDCS-03000748.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a veteran of the Seventh Claudian Legion and his wife, made by the veteran himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-03300741.text_edition: \n Finis novus saltus territorii Tariotarum ex decreto Publi Corneli Dolabellae pro \n
EDCS-03300741.translation: New boundary of the territory of the Tariotarum, by decree of Publius Cornelius Dolabella, for [restored]
EDCS-03300741.summary: This inscription records a territorial boundary marker for the Tariotarum people, established by decree of Publius Cornelius Dolabella.

EDCS-03300742.text_edition: \n Saltus territorii Tariotarum ex decreto Dolabellae legati\n
EDCS-03300742.translation: Territory of the Tariotarum, by decree of Dolabella, the legate.
EDCS-03300742.summary: This inscription marks the territory of the Tariotarum people, established by decree of Dolabella, the legate.

EDCS-03300743.text_edition: \n Lucius Attius Luci filius Sergia Augusta annorum XXX stipendiorum VIII miles legionis VII hic situs est\n
EDCS-03300743.translation: Lucius Attius, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, from Augusta, 30 years old, with 8 years of service, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, lies here.
EDCS-03300743.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Attius, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, who died at the age of 30 after 8 years of service.

EDCS-03300744.text_edition: \n Lucius Mummius Luci filius Fabia Ancyra miles legionis VII nnorum XXXVI stipendiorum XVI hic situs est Arma bis octonis felicia qui tulit annis hic situs est fato Mummius ipse suo si sapiunt obiti data munera percipis ampla et recipis nostrae pignus amicitiae hoc tibi cum titulo posuit Cornelius Chrestus et dixit lachrymans sic sit tibi terra levis Marcus Cornelius Marci libertus Chrestus posuit\n
EDCS-03300744.translation: Lucius Mummius, son of Lucius, of the Fabia tribe, from Ancyra, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, 36 years old, with 16 years of service, lies here. He who bore arms with good fortune for eighteen years lies here by his own fate. If the departed understand, you receive great gifts and take our pledge of friendship; Cornelius Chrestus places this for you with this inscription and said weeping, 'May the earth lie lightly upon you.' Marcus Cornelius Chrestus, freedman of Marcus, placed this.
EDCS-03300744.summary: This is a complex funerary epitaph for Lucius Mummius, a soldier of the Seventh Legion, who died at 36 after 16 years of service, including 18 years of bearing arms, and includes a poem and a dedication by his freedman, Cornelius Chrestus.

EDCS-03700656.text_edition: \n Rufo Titae matri Titua viva fecit\n
EDCS-03700656.translation: To Rufo, mother of Tita, Titua set this up while alive.
EDCS-03700656.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Titua for her mother, Rufo.

EDCS-03700657.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, to Titus Julius Ampliatus.

EDCS-03700658.text_edition: \n Arruntiae Cai filiae Celerinae Caius Arruntius Sedatus filiae carissimae posuit\n
EDCS-03700658.translation: Gaius Arruntius Sedatus erected this for his dearest daughter, Arruntia Celerina, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-03700658.summary: A funerary monument set up by Gaius Arruntius Sedatus for his daughter, Arruntia Celerina.

EDCS-03700659.text_edition: \n Veratiae Cai filiae Priscillae Caius Arruntius Sedatus uxori posuit\n
EDCS-03700659.translation: Gaius Arruntius Sedatus erected this for his wife, Veratia Priscilla, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-03700659.summary: A funerary monument set up by Gaius Arruntius Sedatus for his wife, Veratia Priscilla.

EDCS-03700661.translation: For Vesia Paulina, daughter of Titus.
EDCS-03700661.summary: A funerary inscription for Vesia Paulina, daughter of Titus.

EDCS-03700662.text_edition: \n Vesiae Moderatae Gellius Chrysogonus uxori karissimae\n
EDCS-03700662.translation: Gellius Chrysogonus erected this for his dearest wife, Vesia Moderata.
EDCS-03700662.summary: A funerary monument set up by Gellius Chrysogonus for his wife, Vesia Moderata.

EDCS-03700663.translation: Quinta, daughter of Arruntia Secundilla, set this up for her mother.
EDCS-03700663.summary: A funerary monument set up by Quinta for her mother, Arruntia Secundilla.

EDCS-03700664.text_edition: \n Ephebo actori Elpis sibi et filio posuit\n
EDCS-03700664.translation: Elpis set this up for herself and her son, Ephebus the actor.
EDCS-03700664.summary: A funerary monument set up by Elpis for herself and her son, Ephebus, who was an actor.

EDCS-03700666.text_edition: \n Rubriae Titi filiae Maximillae Valeria Marci filia Marcella filiae posuit\n
EDCS-03700666.translation: Valeria Marcella, daughter of Marcus, erected this for her daughter, Rubria Maximilla, daughter of Titus.
EDCS-03700666.summary: A funerary monument set up by Valeria Marcella for her daughter, Rubria Maximilla.

EDCS-03700667.translation: For Oppia Paulina, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-03700667.summary: A funerary inscription for Oppia Paulina, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-03700668.text_edition: \n Aufidiae Cai filiae Balbinae annorum V Caesia Luci filia Paula avia nepti suae viva fecit\n
EDCS-03700668.translation: Caesia Paula, daughter of Lucius, and grandmother, made this for her granddaughter, Aufidia Balbina, daughter of Caius, aged five.
EDCS-03700668.summary: A funerary monument made by the grandmother, Caesia Paula, for her five-year-old granddaughter, Aufidia Balbina.

EDCS-03700672.text_edition: \n Valeriae Titi filiae Lucullae Aulus Caecina filius Heracula uxori\n
EDCS-03700672.translation: Aulus Caecina Heracles, son of Heracles, erected this for his wife, Valeria Luculla, daughter of Titus.
EDCS-03700672.summary: A funerary monument set up by Aulus Caecina Heracles for his wife, Valeria Luculla.

EDCS-03700673.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Clodius Sabinus, likely a tombstone inscription.

EDCS-03700676.summary: This is a funerary epitaph consisting of the name Irene, likely a tombstone inscription.

EDCS-03700677.text_edition: \n Cai Iuli Cis Cai Iuli Cis\n
EDCS-03700677.translation: Caius Julius Cis, son of Caius Julius Cis.
EDCS-03700677.summary: This inscription appears to record a patronymic or filiation, likely for identification purposes.

EDCS-03700678.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus ovio nnorum X pientissim-\n
EDCS-03700678.translation: To the departed spirits, of a son aged ten years, most dutiful.
EDCS-03700678.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicating the monument to a dutiful son who died at the age of ten.

EDCS-04201255.text_edition: \n Arruntiae Titi filiae Secundinae nnorum VII Cla<u>dia Secunda n<e=I>p<t=I>i \n
EDCS-04201255.translation: To Arruntia Secundina, daughter of Titus, aged seven years; Claudia Secunda, her mother, set this up.
EDCS-04201255.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Arruntia Secundina, erected by her mother Claudia Secunda, recording her age at death.

EDCS-04201329.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio V Callisto V Gemellina coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-04201329.translation: To the departed spirits, to Gaius Valerius Callistus, and to Gemellina, his deserving wife.
EDCS-04201329.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Gaius Valerius Callistus and his deserving wife Gemellina.

EDCS-04201550.translation: Appuleia Aretusa, mother, places this for Gaius Trosius Liberalis, a soldier of the VI Praetorian Cohort, aged nineteen years.
EDCS-04201550.summary: This funerary epitaph records the placement of a monument by a mother for her son, a nineteen-year-old soldier in the VI Praetorian Cohort.

EDCS-04201619.translation: To Appuleia Heroidis, from Marcus Appuleius Ascanius, her grandfather.
EDCS-04201619.summary: This funerary epitaph records a dedication from a grandfather, Marcus Appuleius Ascanius, to his granddaughter, Appuleia Heroidis.

EDCS-04202098.translation: Trosia Tertia, daughter, placed this for Lucius Trosius Crescens.
EDCS-04202098.summary: This funerary epitaph records the placement of a monument by a daughter, Trosia Tertia, for her father, Lucius Trosius Crescens.

EDCS-04900821.translation: To Augustus, from Valerius Lusamanhus, son of Mars.
EDCS-04900821.summary: This inscription is a dedication, possibly of a votive nature, to the deified Augustus by Valerius Lusamanhus.

EDCS-04900827.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum Aelius Catius li<b=D>en<s> posuit \n
EDCS-04900827.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus; Aelius Catius Lentulus dedicated it.
EDCS-04900827.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus Augustus by Aelius Catius Lentulus, likely on a private shrine or altar.

EDCS-04900830.translation: His most devoted daughter placed this for him as one who deserved well.
EDCS-04900830.summary: This epitaph records a daughter's loving memorial for her deceased father, highlighting his worthiness.

EDCS-04900831.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Rutilio Agaclyto Rutilia Homonya filia\n
EDCS-04900831.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld; Rutilia Homonya, his daughter, for Lucius Rutilius Agaclytus.
EDCS-04900831.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the gods of the underworld (Dis Manibus) for Lucius Rutilius Agaclytus, set up by his daughter Rutilia Homonya.

EDCS-04900832.text_edition: \n Marco Cumareno Carisio annorum XVI mater\n
EDCS-04900832.translation: His mother for Marcus Cumarenus Carisius, aged 16.
EDCS-04900832.summary: This epitaph records the death of Marcus Cumarenus Carisius at the age of 16, with his mother as the dedicator.

EDCS-04900833.text_edition: \n Feresiae Valentinae nnorum IV fecit Caius Feresius Clytus delicatae\n
EDCS-04900833.translation: Caius Feresius Clytus made this for Feresia Valentina, aged 4, his beloved.
EDCS-04900833.summary: This epitaph records that Caius Feresius Clytus dedicated this monument to his beloved daughter, Feresia Valentina, who died at the age of 4.

EDCS-04900834.summary: This fragmentary epitaph records a dedication for a woman named Ursa, wife of Cestius.

EDCS-04900836.text_edition: \n Tito Cassidio Bitho Licinia Hygia D \n
EDCS-04900836.translation: Licinia Hygia for Titus Cassidius Bithus...
EDCS-04900836.summary: This inscription likely records a dedication or memorial set up by Licinia Hygia for Titus Cassidius Bithus.

EDCS-04900837.summary: This fragmentary epitaph likely records a child's death after only 3 months, with the father as the dedicator.

EDCS-04900838.summary: This inscription is a dedication or memorial for Quintus Medullius Ursio.

EDCS-04900839.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quintus Caelius Aphros Capro liberto bene merenti\n
EDCS-04900839.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld; Quintus Celius Aphros for Capro, his freedman, as one who deserved well.
EDCS-04900839.summary: This is a funerary dedication to the gods of the underworld (Dis Manibus) for the freedman Capro, set up by his former master Quintus Celius Aphros.

EDCS-04900920.text_edition: \n Nemesi Augustae Caianus votum posuit cum suis\n
EDCS-04900920.translation: Caianus set up this offering to the Augustan Nemesis with his dependents.
EDCS-04900920.summary: A private dedication to the goddess Nemesis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-05000665.summary: A dedication to Publius Aelius Firminianus, possibly a tomb inscription from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05000667.text_edition: \n Quintus Valerius Quinti filius Cornelia Edessa miles legionis VII nnorum XXXV stipendiorum XII testamento fieri iussit Lucius Valerius heres posuit et Valeria Quinta liberta hic sita est\n
EDCS-05000667.translation: Quintus Valerius, son of Quintus, of the Cornelia tribe, from Edessa, a soldier of the 7th legion, aged 35, with 12 years of service, ordered this to be made by his will. Lucius Valerius, his heir, erected it, and Valeria Quinta, his freedwoman, is buried here.
EDCS-05000667.summary: A funerary monument for a soldier of the 7th legion, erected by his heir and including his freedwoman, dating likely to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05000668.text_edition: \n Quintus Mettius Quinti filius Voltinia Valens miles legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis annorum XXXVIII stipendiorum XIIX testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-05000668.translation: Quintus Mettius Valens, son of Quintus, of the Voltinia tribe, a soldier of the 7th Claudian legion, pious and faithful, aged 38, with 12 years of service, ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-05000668.summary: A funerary inscription for a soldier of the 7th Claudian legion, indicating he ordered it to be made by his will, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05200553.text_edition: \n us Publi et libertus? Mus hic situs est VX Publius Servilius P F vivus fecit\n
EDCS-05200553.translation: Here lies Mus, freedman of Publius. Publius Servilius, son of Publius, made this while alive.
EDCS-05200553.summary: A tombstone set up by Publius Servilius for himself and his freedman Mus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05200554.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Luci Claudi Ianuari annorum XXX item A \n
EDCS-05200554.translation: To the Divine Shades of Lucius Claudius Ianuarius, aged 30, and also A...
EDCS-05200554.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription for Lucius Claudius Ianuarius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-05200555.text_edition: \n Pro salute C Balbiniani templum cum portico sic adampliavit Magnus vilicus Libero Patri libens fecit\n
EDCS-05200555.translation: For the health of C. Balbinianus, Magnus, the steward, willingly enlarged the temple with its portico, thus.
EDCS-05200555.summary: An inscription recording the enlargement of a temple with a portico for the well-being of C. Balbinianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100478.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri sacrum Marcus Aurelius Attenio miles legionis I adiutricis Antoninianae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-06100478.translation: To Silvanus Silvestris, Marcus Aurelius Attenio, a soldier of the 1st Antoninian legion Adjutrix, willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-06100478.summary: A votive inscription to the god Silvanus Silvestris, dedicated by a soldier of the 1st Adjutrix legion, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-06100479.text_edition: \n D(eo) Silvano Silvestri Augusto sacrum Quintus Bovius Rufinus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-06100479.translation: To the August God Silvanus Silvestris, Quintus Bovius Rufinus willingly and deservedly paid his vow.
EDCS-06100479.summary: A votive inscription to the god Silvanus Silvestris, dedicated by Quintus Bovius Rufinus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100480.text_edition: \n Deo Invicto Soli Mithrae Terentius Dalmata MANTIVS votum solvit in honorem amicorum suorum\n
EDCS-06100480.translation: To the Unconquered God Sol Mithras, Terentius Dalmata, MANTIVS, paid his vow in honor of his friends.
EDCS-06100480.summary: A votive inscription to the god Sol Mithras, dedicated by Terentius Dalmata in honor of his friends, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-06100482.text_edition: \n Aurelius Lupus sibbi et coniugi suae Iuliae Maximae piscinam fecit\n
EDCS-06100482.translation: Aurelius Lupus built this swimming pool for himself and his wife, Iulia Maxima.
EDCS-06100482.summary: This inscription records the construction of a swimming pool by Aurelius Lupus for his family.

EDCS-06100483.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Titus Aelius Pasiphilus A Iuliae Gemellae coniugi bene merenti se vivo fecit et sibi et Petronio Staphylo filio hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur locus donatus a FAB aera proditio longum pedes octo latum pedes quattuor\n
EDCS-06100483.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Titus Aelius Pasiphilus, while alive, made this monument for his well-deserving wife, Iulia Gemella, and for himself and his son, Petronius Staphyllus; he will not follow an heir. The plot, granted by FAB, is eight feet long and four feet wide.
EDCS-06100483.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Titus Aelius Pasiphilus for his wife, son, and himself, specifying the plot dimensions and a condition about not following an heir.

EDCS-06100484.text_edition: \n Varius Sabinus Salonitanus ex collegio fabrorum vivo sibi posuit\n
EDCS-06100484.translation: Varius Sabinus of Salona, from the guild of craftsmen, set this up for himself while alive.
EDCS-06100484.summary: This is a self-dedicated funerary monument by Varius Sabinus, a member of the craftsmen's guild.

EDCS-06100485.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelia Florentina cum suis ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-06100485.translation: Aelia Florentina, with her family, set this up in fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-06100485.summary: This votive inscription records Aelia Florentina dedicating a monument to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-06100486.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Billianius Severus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-06100486.translation: Caius Billianius Severus, a beneficiarius of the consular governor, fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-06100486.summary: This inscription records Caius Billianius Severus fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-06100487.text_edition: \n Silvano Lucius Titiusnus |(centurio) cohortis I miliariae libens posuit\n
EDCS-06100487.translation: Lucius Titiusnus, centurion of the 1st Cohort, Miliares, set this up willingly for Silvanus.
EDCS-06100487.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Lucius Titiusnus, a military officer, to Silvanus.

EDCS-06100489.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording a dedication or funerary notice by Gaius' freedman Illyricus.

EDCS-06100491.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ursus et Victoria quae vixit NT \n
EDCS-06100491.translation: To the Divine Shades. Ursus and Victoria, who lived...
EDCS-06100491.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Ursus and Victoria.

EDCS-07000813.text_edition: \n sacerdotium C onius Luci filius Sergia Fronto us rei publicae \n
EDCS-07000813.translation: priest of the republic. Conius Fronto, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe...
EDCS-07000813.summary: This fragmentary inscription refers to Conius Fronto, a priest and possibly a public official.

EDCS-07400592.text_edition: \n Valeriae Pudentilles sic nnrum XXXV Titus Flavius Maxiarcus coniugi bene merenti posuit item Flavius Bennus et Flavia Sabina filius et filia eius\n
EDCS-07400592.translation: To Valeria Pudentilla, aged 35. Titus Flavius Maxiarcus set this up for his well-deserving wife. Likewise Flavius Bennus and Flavia Sabina, her son and daughter.
EDCS-07400592.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Titus Flavius Maxiarcus for his deceased wife Valeria Pudentilla, and is also noted by her children.

EDCS-07400593.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Liciniae matri annorum XL Arpagio fratri annorum VI Valerius posuit filius piissimus mitri sic et fratri\n
EDCS-07400593.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Licinia, mother, aged 40, and for Arpagius, brother, aged 6. His most dutiful son Valerius erected this for his mother and also for his brother.
EDCS-07400593.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to a mother and brother by a son, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400594.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ammatisae vixit annos XXX Canius Dasas matri fecit pro pietate\n
EDCS-07400594.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ammatisa lived for 30 years. Canius Dasas made this for his mother out of piety.
EDCS-07400594.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Canius Dasas for his mother Ammatisa, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400595.text_edition: \n Libero Patri sacrum Ulpia Victorina pro salute sua posuit\n
EDCS-07400595.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater. Ulpia Victorina erected this for her own health.
EDCS-07400595.summary: A dedication to the god Liber Pater, set up by Ulpia Victorina for her well-being, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400597.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning the emperor Lucius Verus, likely an official dedication or milestone fragment from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400600.translation: Paia, daughter of Totius Implanthus, of Cuio.
EDCS-07400600.summary: A fragmentary funerary or dedicatory inscription identifying Paia as the daughter of Totius Implanthus, possibly indicating a place name or tribal affiliation.

EDCS-07400601.text_edition: \n Daico Samuio Versonis filia annorum III\n
EDCS-07400601.translation: Daica, daughter of Samuio Versonis, aged 3.
EDCS-07400601.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Daica, daughter of Samuio Versonis, at the age of three, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400602.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lottiae Dracontillae Lotteius Leo et Lottia Cereia filiae infelicissimae quae vixit nnos XIIII menses IX parentes posuerunt\n
EDCS-07400602.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Lottia Dracontilla. Lotteius Leo and Lottia Cereia erected this for their most unhappy daughter, who lived 14 years and 9 months.
EDCS-07400602.summary: A funerary inscription by parents Lotteius Leo and Lottia Cereia for their young daughter Lottia Dracontilla, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07400604.text_edition: \n Apolleni sic Aurelius Iullianus sic centurio libens possuit sic\n
EDCS-07400604.translation: To Apollo, willingly erected by Aurelius Iullianus, centurion.
EDCS-07400604.summary: A dedication to the god Apollo made by Aurelius Iullianus, a centurion, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-07600348.summary: An inscription recording the name and title of Gnaeus Tamphilus Vala as proconsul, likely from a public monument or honorific base in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08300491.text_edition: \n Herculi sacrum Publius Plotius |(centurio) legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08300491.translation: Sacred to Hercules. Publius Plotius, centurion of the 7th Claudian legion, pious and faithful, willingly paid his vow, as deserved.
EDCS-08300491.summary: A votive inscription to Hercules by Publius Plotius, a centurion in the 7th Claudian legion, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08500496.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto Marcus Aurelius Maximus libens posuit\n
EDCS-08500496.translation: Marcus Aurelius Maximus gladly erected this for Silvanus Augustus.
EDCS-08500496.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus Augustus, erected by Marcus Aurelius Maximus.

EDCS-08500497.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Publius Volusius Se \n
EDCS-08500497.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Publius Volusius Se...
EDCS-08500497.summary: This is a sacred dedication to Jupiter Best and Greatest, begun by Publius Volusius Se.

EDCS-08500498.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Valerius Severianus veteranus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-08500498.translation: Titus Valerius Severianus, a veteran, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-08500498.summary: This records a vow fulfilled by Titus Valerius Severianus, a veteran, to Jupiter Best and Greatest.

EDCS-08500499.text_edition: \n Hercu<l>i Augusto Aurelius Licinius IIvir posuit\n
EDCS-08500499.translation: Aurelius Licinius, aedile, erected this for Hercules Augustus.
EDCS-08500499.summary: This is a dedication to Hercules Augustus, erected by Aurelius Licinius, an aedile.

EDCS-08500501.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aureliae Scenutae quae vixit annos XXXV Aurelius Plares coniugi karissmae et Lavius et Delmanna filiae pientissimae et sibi vivi posuerunt\n
EDCS-08500501.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Scenuta, who lived 35 years, Aurelius Plares for his most beloved wife, and Lavius and Delmanna for their most dutiful daughter, and for themselves, erected this while alive.
EDCS-08500501.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aurelius Plares, Lavius, and Delmanna for Aurelia Scenuta, their wife, daughter, and for themselves.

EDCS-08500502.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Cohortali Aurelius Lavius et Aelia Tito libentes posuerunt\n
EDCS-08500502.translation: Aurelius Lavius and Aelia Tito gladly erected this for Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Companion.
EDCS-08500502.summary: This is a dedication erected by Aurelius Lavius and Aelia Tito to Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Companion.

EDCS-08500503.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Severinus veteranus vixit annos XIX Temantia mater et fratres posuerunt\n
EDCS-08500503.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Severinus, a veteran, who lived 19 years; his mother Temantia and his brothers erected this.
EDCS-08500503.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Temantia and her sons for Severinus, a veteran.

EDCS-08500505.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Flaviae Marcellae quae vixit annos XXXIV Nantius Sexti coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-08500505.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Nantius Sexti erected this for Flavia Marcella, who lived 34 years, his deserving wife.
EDCS-08500505.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Nantius Sexti for his wife Flavia Marcella.

EDCS-08500506.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Cipio Fausto et Frunitae filiae Lucius Paconius Barbario cognatus et Luria Frunita fratri et nepti bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-08500506.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Paconius Barbario, a relative, and Luria Frunita erected this for Lucius Cipio Faustus and his daughter Frunita, who deserved well.
EDCS-08500506.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucius Paconius Barbario and Luria Frunita for Lucius Cipio Faustus and his daughter Frunita.

EDCS-08500507.text_edition: \n Quinto Valerio Quadrato annorum LXI Lucius Valerius Celer \n
EDCS-08500507.translation: For Quintus Valerius Quadratus, aged 61. Lucius Valerius Celer...
EDCS-08500507.summary: This records a dedication or memorial for Quintus Valerius Quadratus, erected by Lucius Valerius Celer.

EDCS-08500508.text_edition: \n Herculi sacrum Cornificius Verus libens voto posuit\n
EDCS-08500508.translation: Sacred to Hercules. Cornificius Verus willingly erected [this] in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-08500508.summary: A private dedication to Hercules by Cornificius Verus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08600576.text_edition: \n Lucius Gaviarius Luci filius Titi nepos augur Caius Iulius Caesaris libertus Meges IIviri quinquennales portam et turrim ex decreto decurionum de pecunia publica reficiundas coeraverunt eidemque probaverunt constat HS |||(trium milium)?D\n
EDCS-08600576.translation: Lucius Gaviarius, son of Lucius, grandson of Titus, augur, and Caius Iulius Meges, freedman of Caius Julius Caesar, as dual magistrates for five years, undertook the repair of the gate and tower from public funds, as decreed by the council of decurions, and they approved it, costing 3,500 sesterces.
EDCS-08600576.summary: This inscription records the repair of a gate and tower by two magistrates, Lucius Gaviarius and Caius Iulius Meges, in a municipality, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08600577.text_edition: \n Lucius Gaviarius Luci filius Titi nepos augur Caius Iulius Caesaris libertus Meges IIviri quinquennales murum ex decreto decurionum reficiundum curaverunt i<s>demque probaverunt constat HS ||(quattuor milium)CC\n
EDCS-08600577.translation: Lucius Gaviarius, son of Lucius, grandson of Titus, augur, and Caius Iulius Meges, freedman of Caius Julius Caesar, as dual magistrates for five years, undertook the repair of the wall as decreed by the council of decurions, and they approved it, costing 4,200 sesterces.
EDCS-08600577.summary: This inscription records the repair of a wall by two magistrates, Lucius Gaviarius and Caius Iulius Meges, in a municipality, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08900510.text_edition: \n Prosalute ddominorum [nnostrorum Imperatoris] Caesaris Gai Iuli Veri Maximini Augusti et Gai Iuli Veri M[aximi] nobilissimi Caesaris res publica Naronitanorum\n
EDCS-08900510.translation: For the health of our lords, the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus Augustus and the most noble Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, the state of the Naronitani [erected this].
EDCS-08900510.summary: A public dedication for the health of Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus Caesar, by the community of Naronitae, from the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08900511.translation: Pusilla Allia, daughter of Spurius, lies here.
EDCS-08900511.summary: A simple tombstone inscription for Pusilla Allia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900512.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippi sic Pio Felici Augusto \n
EDCS-08900512.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus and Marcus Julius Philippus the most noble Caesar.
EDCS-08900512.summary: A dedication to Emperor Philip the Arab and his son Philip II Caesar, likely from the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08900513.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II imperator III consul pater patriae et Caius Iulius Verus Maximus nobilissimus Caesar Augusti\n
EDCS-08900513.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, the pious, fortunate, pontifex maximus, invested with tribunician power for the second time, Imperator for the third time, consul, father of the country, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, the most noble Caesar, [son of an] Augustus.
EDCS-08900513.summary: This is a titulature inscription listing the full imperial titles of Emperors Maximinus Thrax and his son Maximus Caesar, dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08900514.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Augusto et Marco Iulio Philippo nobilissimo Caesari XXIIII\n
EDCS-08900514.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Julius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus and Marcus Julius Philippus the most noble Caesar, 24 [years of service?].
EDCS-08900514.summary: A dedication to Emperor Philip the Arab and his son Philip II Caesar, possibly recording a military tenure or honor, from the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-08900517.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerio Valeriano veterano legionis XIIII geminae Valerius Valerianus filius patri pientissimo\n
EDCS-08900517.translation: To the divine shades. Valerius Valerianus, a veteran of the Legio XIIII Gemina, [this monument was erected by] Valerius Valerianus, his son, to his most devoted father.
EDCS-08900517.summary: A funerary dedication to a veteran of the Legio XIV Gemina by his son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900521.text_edition: \n Cornificius aro nomine suo et Cai Cornifici Cornificiae Gavillae filiorum aedem ae faciendam curavit idemque dedicavit et populo decurio adlectus \n
EDCS-08900521.translation: Cornificius erected this temple with his own funds, both in his own name and in the names of his sons, Caius Cornificius and Cornificius Gavilla, and he himself dedicated it, having also been elected to the people's council.
EDCS-08900521.summary: A dedication of a temple by Cornificius, who also funded its construction and was a member of the local council, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900522.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelia Masso Publio Aelio Victori Proculo defuncto nnorum L Publio Aelio Victorino filio defuncto nnorum X mensium VI dulcissimo et marito optimo viva bene merentibus posuit\n
EDCS-08900522.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelia Masso placed this for Publius Aelius Victor Proculus, deceased, aged 50, and for her son Publius Aelius Victorinus, deceased, aged 10 years and 6 months, most sweet, and for her excellent husband, while she lived, for those who deserved well.
EDCS-08900522.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Aelia Masso for her deceased son and husband.

EDCS-08900523.text_edition: \n Tiberio Caesari divi Augusti filio divi Iuli nepoti Augusto pontifici maximo consuli IIII imperatori VIII tribunicia potestate XXV Lucio Volusio Saturnino legato pro praetore\n
EDCS-08900523.translation: To Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the deified Augustus, grandson of the deified Julius, chief priest, consul for the fourth time, emperor for the eighth time, with tribunician power for the twenty-fifth time, for Lucius Volusius Saturninus, his legate and propraetor.
EDCS-08900523.summary: This inscription is a dedication or honorific title for Emperor Tiberius, mentioning his official titles and a governor Lucius Volusius Saturninus, likely from the early 1st century AD.

EDCS-08900524.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelio Celso coniugi bene merenti con sic quo vixit annos XXXII et Viviae L defunctae annorum LIII Ulpia Successa et sibi vivae posuit\n
EDCS-08900524.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ulpia Successa placed this for her well-deserving husband, Aelius Celsus, with whom she lived for 32 years, and for Vivia, deceased, aged 53, and for herself, while she lived.
EDCS-08900524.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Ulpia Successa for her deceased husband Aelius Celsus and Vivia, and also for herself.

EDCS-08900525.translation: To Liber Pater Augustus. Cordius Ursinus.
EDCS-08900525.summary: This is a dedication to the god Liber Pater Augustus by Cordius Ursinus.

EDCS-08900526.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aplis Pantonis sibi et Catoni uxori vivus fecit\n
EDCS-08900526.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For himself and for his wife Caton, [Aplis Pantonis] made this while alive.
EDCS-08900526.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Aplis Pantonis for himself and his wife Caton.

EDCS-08900527.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelia Trito viva sibi posuit\n
EDCS-08900527.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Trito placed this for herself while alive.
EDCS-08900527.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Aurelia Trito for herself.

EDCS-08900529.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Macximae sic quae vixit annos XX et Maustae quae vixit annos IIII sorori et filiae defunctis et Celtemioni matri vivae Surilla suis et sibi viva posuit\n
EDCS-08900529.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Surilla placed this for herself while alive and for her own people: for Maxima, who lived 20 years, and for Mausta, her sister and daughter, who lived 4 years, and for Celtemion, her mother, deceased.
EDCS-08900529.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Surilla for her deceased mother, sister, and daughter, and for herself.

EDCS-08900530.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Narens vixit annos XXXVIII Amacae Aeliae Pantonis serva viva sibi aet sic conpari posuit\n
EDCS-08900530.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Narens lived 38 years. Amaca Aelia Pantonis, a slave, placed this for herself while alive and for her companion.
EDCS-08900530.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by the slave Amaca Aelia Pantonis for herself and her companion.

EDCS-08900531.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerius Optatus vixit annos LXX Statia Lavo viva sibi et Patae nepti suae quae vixit annos V\n
EDCS-08900531.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Valerius Optatus lived 70 years. Statia Lavo placed this for herself while alive and for her granddaughter Pata, who lived 5 years.
EDCS-08900531.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Statia Lavo for herself and her granddaughter Pata.

EDCS-08900532.text_edition: \n Iaetto viva matri Dusoni defunctae annorum LXX et Ambae defunctae annorum XII posuit\n
EDCS-08900532.translation: [Iaetto] placed this for her mother Dusoni, deceased, aged 70, and for Amba, deceased, aged 12 years.
EDCS-08900532.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Iaetto for her deceased mother and sister.

EDCS-08900533.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Secundus Augusti libertus AT et Albia Ingenua cum Secundina filia ex voto pientissimi posuerunt\n
EDCS-08900533.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Secundus, freedman of Augustus, and Albia Ingenua with their daughter Secundina, out of a most dutiful vow, erected this.
EDCS-08900533.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter, erected by a freedman and his family out of a vow.

EDCS-08900534.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Tito Aurelio Severo Celsiano qui vixit annos LXXX Aurelius Plares filius patri pientissimo posuit\n
EDCS-08900534.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the departed. Aurelius Plares erected this for his most dutiful father, Titus Aurelius Severus Celsianus, who lived 80 years.
EDCS-08900534.summary: This is a tombstone for Titus Aurelius Severus Celsianus, erected by his son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08901077.text_edition: \n Lucius Gavius Luci filius Publilia Verona veteranus legionis XV se vivo fecit et Marco Gavio fratri et liberto Hilaro\n
EDCS-08901077.translation: Lucius Gavius, son of Lucius, of the Publilia tribe, Verona, a veteran of the XV Legion, made this for himself while alive, and for his brother and freedman, Marcus Gavius Hilarus.
EDCS-08901077.summary: This is a tombstone or funerary monument made by Lucius Gavius for himself and his brother and freedman, a veteran of the XV Legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08901080.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Granio Luci filio Proclino decurioni coloniae Aequensium flamini beneficiario consularis legionis XIIII geminae istupendiorum sic XIIII defuncto nnorum XXXVIIII Epidia Procula mater filio dulcissimo et sibi libertis libertabusque posterisque eorum in fronte pedes XL in agro pedes XL\n
EDCS-08901080.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the departed. Epidia Procula erected this for her most cherished son, Lucius Granio Proclinus, son of Lucius, of the Proclina tribe, a decurion of the colony of Aequa, a beneficiary official of the consular legate of the XIV Gemina Legion, who died after serving 12 years in the stupendia (a military bonus), at the age of 39 years; and for herself, and for her freedmen and freedwomen and their descendants, with 40 feet in front and 40 feet in the field.
EDCS-08901080.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Granio Proclinus, a decurion and military official, erected by his mother, indicating his military service and age at death, and reserving space for family and descendants.

EDCS-08901081.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Pompeius |(centurio) cohortis III Alpinorum Antoninianae curam agens FAB AMP MEN et Vibius Vibianus protector consularis\n
EDCS-08901081.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Titus Flavius Pompeius, centurion of the III Alpinorum Antoniniana cohort, while in charge of the provision office [FAB AMP MEN], and Vibius Vibianus, protector of the consular legate.
EDCS-08901081.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter, likely related to military service, mentioning a centurion and a protector of a consular legate.

EDCS-08901082.translation: Valeria Chrysis paid her vow to Asclepius.
EDCS-08901082.summary: This is a votive offering to Asclepius by Valeria Chrysis.

EDCS-08901084.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Paconiae Montanae Risiniensi quae vixit annos XXXV Aurelia Panto G mater filiae dulcissimae et sibi vivae infelicissimae posuit\n
EDCS-08901084.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the departed. Aurelia Panto erected this for her most cherished daughter, Paconia Montana of Risinium, who lived 35 years, and for herself, who lived unhappily, while alive.
EDCS-08901084.summary: This is a tombstone for Paconia Montana, erected by her mother, who also reserved a spot for herself.

EDCS-09001555.translation: Sacred to Isis, Raecia Marcella [made this].
EDCS-09001555.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Isis by Raecia Marcella.

EDCS-09001558.text_edition: \n Quarta Livia se viva sibi fecit\n
EDCS-09001558.translation: Quarta Livia made this for herself while alive.
EDCS-09001558.summary: This is a funerary monument or endowment made by Quarta Livia for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-09001560.text_edition: \n Romae Augustae sacrum Cornelia Nobilis votum solvit\n
EDCS-09001560.translation: Sacred to Imperial Rome, Cornelia Nobilis paid her vow.
EDCS-09001560.summary: This is a votive offering to the deified Rome by Cornelia Nobilis.

EDCS-09001561.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Pius conpar titulum posuit atque vixit con sic illo annos VIII diem I cecidit annorum XXIII Crispinilla\n
EDCS-09001561.translation: To the Divine Shades. Pius set up this memorial stone and lived with him for 8 years and 1 day, dying at the age of 23. Crispinilla.
EDCS-09001561.summary: A funerary epitaph for Crispinilla, set up by Pius, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-09200514.text_edition: \n Lucius Didius Luci libertus Eros Didia Luci liberta Arbuscula Didia Luci liberta Auca havete et tu\n
EDCS-09200514.translation: Lucius Didius Eros, freedman of Lucius, Didia Arbuscula, freedwoman of Lucius, Didia Auca, freedwoman of Lucius. Greetings to you too.
EDCS-09200514.summary: A funerary epitaph for Didia Arbuscula and Didia Auca, set up by Lucius Didius Eros, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-09200515.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Minidia Quintina vixit nnos XXX Lucius Apertio vixit annos XVIII - Apertio Felix vixit annos XVIII quibus memoriam fecit Quintus Apertio Dexter coniugi et filiis suis\n
EDCS-09200515.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Minidia Quintina lived 30 years. Lucius Apertio lived 18 years. Apertio Felix lived 18 years. For whom Quintus Apertio Dexter made this memorial for his wife and sons.
EDCS-09200515.summary: A funerary epitaph for Minidia Quintina, Lucius Apertio, and Apertio Felix, set up by Quintus Apertio Dexter, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-09300007.text_edition: \n Invicto Mithrae Titus Aurelius Severus libens posuit\n
EDCS-09300007.translation: To the Unconquered Mithras, Titus Aurelius Severus willingly set this up.
EDCS-09300007.summary: A dedication to the god Mithras by Titus Aurelius Severus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-09300467.summary: A dedication or epitaph for Raecius Eumolpus, date unknown.

EDCS-09300468.text_edition: \n Caio Iulio Celeri Murgiae Risinitano Marcus Prifernius Laetus amico bene merenti\n
EDCS-09300468.translation: To Gaius Julius Celer, a Murgian man from Risinitano, Marcus Prifernius Laetus for his well-deserving friend.
EDCS-09300468.summary: A dedication for a friend, Gaius Julius Celer, by Marcus Prifernius Laetus, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-09300469.text_edition: \n Flavius Narcissianus Lupae coniugi carissimae et dulcissimae bene merenti vixit nnos XXII menses V dies XXI\n
EDCS-09300469.translation: To Lupa, my dearest and sweetest wife, well-deserving. Lived 22 years, 5 months, and 21 days.
EDCS-09300469.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lupa, set up by her husband, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-09300470.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Liciniae Inttiae quae vixit annos XXXXV menses VIIII dies XIIII Licinia Dextrilia matri pientissimae posuit\n
EDCS-09300470.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Licinia Inttia, who lived 45 years, 9 months, and 14 days. Licinia Dextrilia set this up for her most devoted mother.
EDCS-09300470.summary: A funerary epitaph for Licinia Inttia, set up by her daughter Licinia Dextrilia, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-09300471.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caese<l=I>liae Victoriae coniugi incomparabili quae vixit mecum nnos XXIIII Caesellii Chrysanthianus maritus et Chrysogonus filius bene merenti \n
EDCS-09300471.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Caesia Julia Victoria, my incomparable wife, who lived with me for 24 years. Caesellii Chrysanthianus her husband and Chrysogonus her son for her well-deserving.
EDCS-09300471.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caesia Julia Victoria, set up by her husband and son, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-09300472.text_edition: \n Invicto Mithrae Publius Aelius Clemens Iunior IIvir et quinquennalis CTI veteranus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09300472.translation: To the Unconquered Mithras, Publius Aelius Clemens Iunior, a IIvir and quinquennalis, a veteran of the CTI, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-09300472.summary: A dedication to Mithras by Publius Aelius Clemens Iunior, a magistrate and veteran, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-09301171.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Romula mater filiae pientissimae posuit\n
EDCS-09301171.translation: To the Divine Manes. Romula erected this for her most devoted daughter.
EDCS-09301171.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a mother for her daughter, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09301172.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Mascellioni simo sermano Surio rimano renti O Germano R M H \n
EDCS-09301172.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Surio, son of Mascellioni and Simo, of the Rimanian tribe, erected this for his kinsman Germano.
EDCS-09301172.summary: A funerary epitaph for Germano, erected by his kinsman Surio, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09400588.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulio Euphemo bene merenti Crispinius Valentinus adfinis\n
EDCS-09400588.translation: To the Divine Manes. Crispinius Valentinus, relative, erected this for Iulius Euphemus, who deserved well.
EDCS-09400588.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Euphemus, set up by his relative Crispinius Valentinus, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-09400589.text_edition: \n Apollini Augusto sacrum Aurelius Verus decurio equitum cohortis III Alpinorum iterata statione libens posuit\n
EDCS-09400589.translation: Sacred to Apollo Augustus. Aurelius Verus, a decurion of the cavalry of the 3rd Cohort of Alpiners, serving for the second time, gladly erected this.
EDCS-09400589.summary: A dedication to Apollo Augustus by Aurelius Verus, a decurion in a military unit, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09400590.text_edition: \n Iulia Musa Iuliaes sic Veraes sic votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-09400590.translation: Iulia Musa gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow for Iuliaes and Veraes.
EDCS-09400590.summary: A votive inscription by Iulia Musa, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09400592.text_edition: \n Verzo Balierius Plati filius decurio vivus fecit sibi et Platori Balierio Plati filio decurioni fratri et Sextoni matri suae defunctis\n
EDCS-09400592.translation: Verzo Balierius, son of Platus, a decurion, erected this while alive for himself, for his brother Plator Balierius, son of Platus, a decurion, and for his mother Sextonia, now deceased.
EDCS-09400592.summary: A funerary monument erected by Verzo Balierius for himself, his brother, and his mother, indicating he was a decurion and chose his burial place while alive, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09401518.text_edition: \n V Seneca vixit annis XXV Marcus Ulpius Severus F N bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-09401518.translation: Seneca lived 25 years. Marcus Ulpius Severus, son of Numerius, erected this for her as she deserved well.
EDCS-09401518.summary: A funerary epitaph for Seneca, erected by Marcus Ulpius Severus, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-09500129.text_edition: \n et Silvano deo conservatori sacrum pro salute Maximi Or et Maximi Maximiani a militiis |(centuriae) IIIIC ex voto succepto sic\n
EDCS-09500129.translation: ...and sacred to Silvanus the god, the preserver, in fulfillment of a vow for the health of Maximinus Or and Maximinus Maximianus from military service in the 400th century.
EDCS-09500129.summary: A votive inscription for the health of two individuals associated with military service, dedicated to the god Silvanus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09500130.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius manius OCnia Euca<r>pia Silvestri Silvano ex voto\n
EDCS-09500130.translation: Titus Flavius Manius gladly and deservedly gave this offering to Silvanus for Eucarpia, wife of Silvester.
EDCS-09500130.summary: A votive inscription by Titus Flavius Manius to Silvanus for Eucarpia, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-09500131.text_edition: \n Bato Peiius Silvano donum dedit libens merito\n
EDCS-09500131.translation: Bato Peius gladly and deservedly gave this gift to Silvanus.
EDCS-09500131.summary: A votive inscription by Bato Peius to Silvanus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000180.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Conservatori sacrum Publius Aelius Pla<d>omenus posuit\n
EDCS-10000180.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest, Protector. Publius Aelius Pladomenus erected this.
EDCS-10000180.summary: An altar dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Conservator by Publius Aelius Pladomenus.

EDCS-10000185.text_edition: \n Serapidi et Isidi Marcus Ulpius Gellianus eques Romanus curator Arbensium Metulensium Splonistarum Malvesatium\n
EDCS-10000185.translation: To Serapis and Isis. Marcus Ulpius Gellianus, Roman knight, curator of the people of Arbensium, Metulensium, Splonistarum, and Malvesatium.
EDCS-10000185.summary: A dedication to Serapis and Isis by Marcus Ulpius Gellianus, a Roman knight serving as curator for several communities, likely in the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10000188.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto sacrum Titus Aelius Lavianus IIvir et Cassius Firmus votum libentes merito solverunt\n
EDCS-10000188.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Titus Aelius Lavianus, as chief magistrate (IIvir), and Cassius Firmus, willingly and deservedly, fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-10000188.summary: A votive inscription to Hercules Augustus, dedicated by Titus Aelius Lavianus and Cassius Firmus, who were local magistrates.

EDCS-10000189.text_edition: \n Libero Patri C Titus Aurelius Nigrinus sacerdos pecunia publica\n
EDCS-10000189.translation: To Liber Pater. Titus Aurelius Nigrinus, priest, by public money.
EDCS-10000189.summary: A dedication to Liber Pater funded by public money and set up by Titus Aurelius Nigrinus, a priest.

EDCS-10000196.text_edition: \n MalvI et Flavi- Bat parentibus posuit\n
EDCS-10000196.translation: MalvI and Flavi- Bat erected this for his parents.
EDCS-10000196.summary: A funerary monument erected by someone named Bat to his parents, identified with the Malvi and Flavi families.

EDCS-10000197.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Petronius Maximus vivo fecit sibi et suis et Aeliae Rufinae uxori defunctae\n
EDCS-10000197.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Petronius Maximus, while alive, made this for himself, his family, and his deceased wife, Aelia Rufina.
EDCS-10000197.summary: A tombstone set up by Lucius Petronius Maximus for himself, his family, and his deceased wife, Aelia Rufina.

EDCS-10000198.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Aurelius Nepos filius et Aurelia Ursina coniunx titulum posuerunt Tito Aurelio Carvo veterano annorum LXX\n
EDCS-10000198.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Aurelius Nepos, his son, and Aurelia Ursina, his wife, erected this monument to Titus Aurelius Carvus, a veteran aged 70.
EDCS-10000198.summary: A tombstone erected by a son and wife for Titus Aurelius Carvus, a 70-year-old veteran.

EDCS-10000199.text_edition: \n dus I consul fetialis cum Publicia Quarta et Catiis Maximina C Clementino Clemente LI us Afrodisius flam[en ------\n
EDCS-10000199.translation: ..., consul, fetialis, with Publicia Quarta, and the Catii, Maximina and Clementinus, Clementia LI, from Afrodisius, flam[en]...
EDCS-10000199.summary: A fragment of an inscription recording a fetialis (priest of international relations) and other individuals, possibly commemorating a religious or public event.

EDCS-10000200.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpia Paula viva sibi et \n
EDCS-10000200.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ulpia Paula, while alive, for herself...
EDCS-10000200.summary: A funerary inscription begun by Ulpia Paula for herself while she was still alive, but left incomplete.

EDCS-10000201.text_edition: \n Ego iustus olarius et manus meas rugetus sic et fetetus sic\n
EDCS-10000201.translation: I, Justus, a potter, and my hands... so...
EDCS-10000201.summary: A very fragmented inscription likely made by a potter named Justus, perhaps an epitaph or a marker of ownership.

EDCS-10000205.summary: A very brief inscription stating that Ingenuus IIIGevus commissioned the monument.

EDCS-10000212.text_edition: \n Zanati Ziraei filio defuncto nnum XXX Annaia Zanatis filia viro et filio et Plarens Ziraei filius patri et fratri\n
EDCS-10000212.translation: For Zanati, son of Ziraeus, 30 years old when he died. Annaia Zanatis, daughter, for her husband and son. And Plarens, son of Ziraeus, for his father and brother.
EDCS-10000212.summary: A funerary inscription honoring Zanati, with dedications from his daughter Annaia and son Plarens.

EDCS-10000213.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Divi filio Augusto pontifici maximo consuli XI imperatori XI tribunic(ia) [potestate XI ------\n
EDCS-10000213.translation: To Emperor Caesar, son of the Divine Augustus, chief priest, consul for the 11th time, imperator for the 11th time, holder of tribunician power for the 11th time...
EDCS-10000213.summary: The beginning of a dedication to Emperor Augustus, listing his titles and honors, likely from an official monument.

EDCS-10000214.text_edition: \n sacrum ob honorem IIviratus Cai Vibi Restituti Caius Iulius Maximus posuit\n
EDCS-10000214.translation: For the honor of the duoviral office, Caius Julius Maximus erected this.
EDCS-10000214.summary: An inscription recording the erection of a monument by Caius Julius Maximus in honor of Caius Vibius Restitutus holding the duoviral office.

EDCS-10000215.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Septimiae Primae bene merenti posuit Glavius SI \n
EDCS-10000215.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Glavius erected this for Septimia Prima, deserving well.
EDCS-10000215.summary: A funerary dedication to Septimia Prima, erected by Glavius.

EDCS-10000216.text_edition: \n Divo Augusto et Tiberio Caesari Augusti filio Augusto sacrum veterani pagi Scunastici quibus colonia Naronitana agros dedit Divo Augusto et Tiberio Caesari Augusti filio Augusto sacrum veterani pagi Scunastici quibus colonia Naronitana agros dedit\n
EDCS-10000216.translation: Sacred to Divus Augustus and Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of Augustus. The veterans of the Scunastic district, to whom the colony of Narona gave lands. Sacred to Divus Augustus and Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of Augustus. The veterans of the Scunastic district, to whom the colony of Narona gave lands.
EDCS-10000216.summary: A dedication by veterans of the Scunastic district, who received land from the colony of Narona, to the deified Augustus and Tiberius Caesar.

EDCS-10000218.text_edition: \n Betulo Karonis filius domo Eguius miles cohortis III Alpinorum annorum XL stipendiorum XXII hic situs est testamento fieri iussit Valerius signifer heres posuit\n
EDCS-10000218.translation: Betulus, son of Karonis, from Eguium, a soldier of the 3rd Alpine Cohort, 40 years old with 22 years of service, is buried here. He ordered this to be made in his will. Valerius, the standard-bearer, his heir, erected it.
EDCS-10000218.summary: A tombstone inscription for Betulus, a soldier of the 3rd Alpine Cohort, detailing his age, service, and burial arrangements made in his will.

EDCS-10000219.text_edition: \n eques cohortis III Alpinae domo Vercianus annorum XXXVIII stipendiorum XIIII testamento fieri iussit heres posuit hic situs est\n
EDCS-10000219.translation: A cavalryman of the 3rd Alpine Cohort, from Verciana, 38 years old with 14 years of service, ordered this to be made in his will. His heir erected it. He is buried here.
EDCS-10000219.summary: A tombstone inscription for an unnamed cavalryman of the 3rd Alpine Cohort, specifying his age, service, and that his burial was arranged in his will.

EDCS-10000220.text_edition: \n Tito Flavio Blodi filio Plasso patri pientissimo annorum L et Flaviae Tattae matri annorum XXXX bene meritae et Tito Flavio Epicado fratri annorum XII et Tito Flavio Laedioni aedili IIIIviro iure dicundo Naronae Laedio filius vivos sic sibi et suis fecit\n
EDCS-10000220.translation: Tito Flavio Blodi filius Plassus for his most devoted father, 50 years old, and for Flavia Tatta, his mother, 40 years old, well-deserving, and for Tito Flavio Epicadus, his brother, 12 years old, and for Tito Flavio Laedion, formerly aedile and duovir for administering justice in Narona. Laedio, son of Laedio, made this while alive for himself and his family.
EDCS-10000220.summary: A family tomb inscription commissioned by Laedio for his parents, brother, and himself, also mentioning his own magistracies in Narona.

EDCS-10000223.summary: A very brief inscription expressing good wishes for the city of Salona.

EDCS-10000224.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Martia Iulia Valeria Salona Felix, likely a tombstone from Salona.

EDCS-10000226.text_edition: \n Aurelio Valerino exceptori Impperatorum in officio memoriae qui aput sic civitatem Nicomediensium fati munus complevit vixsit sic annis XXXII et VI mensibus Aurelius Leontius vir docenarius sic et decurio coloniae Salonitanorum ex curatoribus eiusdem civitatis filio dulcissimo adque sic obsequentissimo pater infelix qui quod primo mihi hoc facere debuerat ego feci\n
EDCS-10000226.translation: To Aurelius Valerinus, Emperor's Exactor in the office of records, who completed his duty in the city of Nicomedia, lived 32 years and 6 months. Aurelius Leontius, a man of the rank of 'docenarius' and a decurion of the colony of Salona, one of its curators, [erected this] for his dearest and most obedient son, an unhappy father who should have done this for me first, but I have done it.
EDCS-10000226.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Valerinus, an imperial official in Nicomedia, erected by his father, Aurelius Leontius, a prominent citizen of Salona, likely dating to the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000227.summary: This inscription is a fragmentary dedication or funerary marker to a person named Caius.

EDCS-10000228.text_edition: \n Tito Flavio Titi filio Trofimati Smyrnaeo\n
EDCS-10000228.translation: To Titus Flavius Trofimatus, son of Titus, from Smyrna.
EDCS-10000228.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Trofimatus, a man from Smyrna.

EDCS-10000229.text_edition: \n Maceria privata cum solo suo Sexti Gelli Primi\n
EDCS-10000229.translation: A private dwelling with its land belonging to Sextus Gellius Primus.
EDCS-10000229.summary: This inscription marks private property, indicating ownership of a house and land by Sextus Gellius Primus.

EDCS-10000230.text_edition: \n Pontiae Ursulae infanti infelicissimae nnorum duum Aennia Lucida avia\n
EDCS-10000230.translation: To Pontia Ursula, a child of two years, her most unhappy grandmother Aennia Lucida.
EDCS-10000230.summary: This is a funerary inscription for the infant Pontia Ursula, erected by her grandmother Aennia Lucida.

EDCS-10000241.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription stating that Fabius Auctus is buried here.

EDCS-10000242.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription indicating that an 'Invictus' (likely a deity or epithet) was the recipient of a dedication or honorific monument.

EDCS-10000243.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Numini Hippi fluminis Cnaeus Tullius Faventinus beneficiarius consularis legionis I adiutricis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000243.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and to the divine spirit of the Hippius river, Gnaeus Tullius Faventinus, a beneficiarius of a consular legate of the I Adiutrix legion, fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-10000243.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gnaeus Tullius Faventinus, a soldier of the I Adiutrix legion, dedicated to Jupiter and the spirit of the Hippius river, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000244.text_edition: \n Cnaeo Minicio Faustino Iulio Severo tribuno legionis XIIII geminae quaestori provinciae Macedoniae tribuno plebis praetori legato legionis XIIII geminae \n
EDCS-10000244.translation: To Gnaeus Minicius Faustinus Iulius Severus, tribune of the XIIII Gemina legion, quaestor of the province of Macedonia, tribune of the plebs, praetor, legate of the XIIII Gemina legion.
EDCS-10000244.summary: This is an honorific inscription listing the distinguished career of Gnaeus Minicius Faustinus Iulius Severus, a Roman official and military commander.

EDCS-10000245.text_edition: \n Dasantillae Caminariae defunctae annorum IIII A \n
EDCS-10000245.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Dasantilla, dated by context to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000247.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Arsae sorori enfilci sic Ioses memoria sic posuic sic\n
EDCS-10000247.translation: To the Divine Manes. Ioses set this up in memory of Arsa, his sister, most unhappy.
EDCS-10000247.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Ioses for his sister Arsa, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000253.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelio Varroni filio anno[rum ------\n
EDCS-10000253.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Aelius Varrus, his son, aged [---].
EDCS-10000253.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for a boy named Aelius Varrus, indicating his age is missing, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000254.text_edition: \n Terrae Matri sacrum pro salute dd[d](ominorum) nn[n](ostrorum) Imperatorum Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici et Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti [et Publi Septimi Getae Caesaris et] Iuliae Augustae matris castrorum et Augusti et Caesaris TTiti Fllavii Verecundus procurator Auggustorum nnostrorum et Callimorphus vilicus XI Kalendas Maias Pompeiaiano et Avito consulibus\n
EDCS-10000254.translation: Sacred to Mother Earth, for the safety of our lords, the Emperors Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus [and Publius Septimius Geta Caesar and] Julia Augusta, mother of the camp and of the Emperor. TTitus Flavius Verecundus, procurator of our Emperors, and Callimorphus, vilicus, erected this on the 11th day before the Kalends of May, during the consulship of Pompeianus and Avitus.
EDCS-10000254.summary: A dedication to Terra Mater (Mother Earth) for the safety of the imperial family, mentioning specific emperors and officials, and dated to the consulship of Pompeianus and Avitus (likely 208 AD).

EDCS-10000255.text_edition: \n Terrae Matri sacrum pro salute domini nostri Imperatoris Marci Aureli Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti et Iuliae Mamaeae Augustae ma<tr=RT>is castrorum sub cura Marci Iuli Macri viri egregii procuratoris Augusti Heliodorus vilicus officinae ferrariae posuit XI Kalendas Maias Modesto II et Probo consulibus \n
EDCS-10000255.translation: Sacred to Mother Earth, for the safety of our lord Emperor Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus and Julia Mamaea Augusta, mother of the camp. Under the supervision of Marcus Julius Macr, a distinguished man and procurator of the Emperor. Heliodorus, vilicus of the iron workshop, erected this on the 11th day before the Kalends of May, during the second consulship of Modestus and Probus.
EDCS-10000255.summary: A dedication to Terra Mater for the safety of Emperor Severus Alexander and Julia Mamaea, from an iron workshop and dated to the consulship of Modestus II and Probus (likely 228 AD).

EDCS-10000259.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus et perpetuae securitati Tatoniae Proculae nnorum XXXIII coniugi pitentissimae Marcus Aurelius Surus veteranus legionis X geminae ex beneficiario et MMarci Aur<e=I>lii Optatus et Firminianus matri infelicissimae et liberti Aurelii Baosus et Sumpa et Zipandus et Quintus et Caroni liberto |(obito) hheredes faciendum curaverunt \n
EDCS-10000259.translation: To the Divine Manes and eternal security. For Tatonia Procula, aged 33, a most devoted wife. Marcus Aurelius Surus, veteran of the Tenth Gemina legion, formerly beneficarius, and Marcus Aurelius Optatus and Firminianus, for [their] most unfortunate mother, and the freedmen Aurelius Baosus, Sumpa, Zipandus, Quintus, and Carus, a freedman, [and] the heirs have seen to the construction.
EDCS-10000259.summary: A funerary monument for Tatonia Procula, erected by her husband, a veteran, and other relatives and freedmen, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000260.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpia Matera annorum XXXV Aelius Quartus uxori fecit\n
EDCS-10000260.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Ulpia Matera, aged 35. Aelius Quartus made this for his wife.
EDCS-10000260.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ulpia Matera, erected by her husband Aelius Quartus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000263.text_edition: \n Iovi Capitolino Victor decurio Del(minensium civitatis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000263.translation: To Jupiter Capitolinus. Victor, decurion of the civitas Delminensium, gladly paid his vow, deserving it.
EDCS-10000263.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter Capitolinus by Victor, a decurion of the Delminensium community, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000264.text_edition: \n Herculi sacrum Gaius Trosius Cripus votum lib(ens) posuit la<e>tus donum merito solvit\n
EDCS-10000264.translation: Sacred to Hercules. Gaius Trosius Cripus gladly set up this vow and willingly paid the offering, deserving it.
EDCS-10000264.summary: A votive inscription to Hercules by Gaius Trosius Cripus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000265.text_edition: \n Apriclus Silvano Silvestri sacrum Montanus posuit\n
EDCS-10000265.translation: Sacred to Apricus Silvanus Silvestris. Montanus set this up.
EDCS-10000265.summary: A dedication to the deities Apricus Silvanus and Silvestris by Montanus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000266.text_edition: \n Prevo Masio Platoris filia Platori filio f<e=I>cit \n
EDCS-10000266.translation: Prevo made this for his son Plator, son of Platoris.
EDCS-10000266.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Prevo for his son Plator, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000267.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Pinsus <G=C>ermanicus Turi filius \n
EDCS-10000267.translation: Pinsus Germanicus, son of Turi, [dedicated this] to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-10000267.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter by Pinsus Germanicus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000268.text_edition: \n MAC Planus Verzulus et Paio Terminio votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-10000268.translation: Mac Planus Verzulus and Paio Terminio gladly and deservedly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-10000268.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Mac Planus Verzulus and Paio Terminio, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000269.translation: Atisus deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-10000269.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to Silvanus by Atisus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000270.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri sacrum Maximus Germanici libertus\n
EDCS-10000270.translation: Maximus, freedman of Germanicus, dedicated this sacred offering to Silvanus Silvestris.
