EDCS-10000270.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Silvestris by Maximus, a freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000271.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri Mes(sori) sacrum Aneius Caecilius \n
EDCS-10000271.translation: Aneius Caecilius dedicated this sacred offering to Silvanus Silvestris Mes(sori).
EDCS-10000271.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Silvestris Mes(sori) by Aneius Caecilius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000272.text_edition: \n Aplini Bardurio Pladomeni filio annorum LXXX Turus e<t=I> Sexto filis fecerunt \n
EDCS-10000272.translation: Turus and Sextus, sons of Pladomenes, made this for their father Bardurius Aplinius, aged 80.
EDCS-10000272.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Turus and Sextus for their father Bardurius Aplinius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000273.text_edition: \n Aplis Staticus Aplinis filius nnum XXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-10000273.translation: Aplus Staticus, son of Aplus, aged 30, is buried here.
EDCS-10000273.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aplus Staticus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000274.text_edition: \n Baeio Cursulavia Lavi liberta nnorum C hic sita est\n
EDCS-10000274.translation: Baeia Cursulavia, freedwoman of Lavi, aged 100, is buried here.
EDCS-10000274.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Baeia Cursulavia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000275.text_edition: \n Bato Samuntius Pantis filius posuit sibi et contubernali suae Platino Avio Pantis filia RE\n
EDCS-10000275.translation: Bato Samuntius, son of Pantis, set this up for himself and his partner Platina Avia, daughter of Pantis.
EDCS-10000275.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Bato Samuntius for himself and his partner Platina Avia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000276.text_edition: \n Celso Statica Bubantis XX liberta annorum XXV hic sita est\n
EDCS-10000276.translation: Celso Statica, freedwoman of Bubantis, aged 25, lies here.
EDCS-10000276.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Celso Statica, a freedwoman, around the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000277.text_edition: \n Daeco Scelepeta Pinentis filia annorum L hic sita est\n
EDCS-10000277.translation: Daeco Scelepeta, daughter of Pinentis, aged 50, lies here.
EDCS-10000277.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Daeco Scelepeta, daughter of Pinentis, around the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000279.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Pladomenus Germanisci fecet sic filiae annorum \n
EDCS-10000279.translation: To the departed spirits. Pladomenus made this for his daughter, aged...
EDCS-10000279.summary: An epitaph for a daughter, set up by her father Pladomenus, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000280.text_edition: \n Prevo Samuntio Alesi filia fecit Marco Anio decurioni Setoviae annorum LXX\n
EDCS-10000280.translation: Prevo Samuntio, daughter of Alesi, made (this) for Marcus Anius, a decurion of Setovia, aged 70.
EDCS-10000280.summary: A funerary dedication by Prevo Samuntio for her husband Marcus Anius, a decurion, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000281.text_edition: \n Statanio et Tritanoni coniugi eius Panto Statania filia parentibus bene merentibus posuit\n
EDCS-10000281.translation: Panto Statania erected this for Statius and Tritanus, her husband, parents deserving of everything.
EDCS-10000281.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Panto Statania to her parents, Statius and Tritanus, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000283.summary: A fragmentary inscription identifying Stato Laprica, son of Tritus, possibly from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000284.text_edition: \n Toto Sarnunia Statti filia annorum XXXV hic sita est\n
EDCS-10000284.translation: Toto Sarnunia, daughter of Stattius, aged 35, lies here.
EDCS-10000284.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Toto Sarnunia, daughter of Stattius, around the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000285.text_edition: \n Tritano Curbania Triti filia nnorum V\n
EDCS-10000285.translation: Tritano Curbania, daughter of Tritus, aged 5.
EDCS-10000285.summary: A funerary record for a child named Tritano Curbania, daughter of Tritus, aged 5, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000286.translation: Turus Staticus, son of Tritus, of Vendon.
EDCS-10000286.summary: An inscription identifying Turus Staticus, son of Tritus, with a geographical indication, possibly from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000287.text_edition: \n Ursa Melandrica Aplinis filia annorum XXVI hic sita est\n
EDCS-10000287.translation: Ursa Melandrica, daughter of Aplinis, aged 26, lies here.
EDCS-10000287.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Ursa Melandrica, daughter of Aplinis, around the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000288.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quadratius, who died at the age of 75.

EDCS-10000289.text_edition: \n o candricae Triti coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-10000289.summary: This is a dedication to the deceased Tritus by his wife, honoring his deserving qualities.

EDCS-10000291.text_edition: \n VIvir Augustalis atria Felicla ria Marci filia Secunda votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-10000291.translation: Secunda, daughter of Marcus, of the College of Six and priestess of Augustus, and the freedwoman Felicia, fulfilled their vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-10000291.summary: This is a funerary epitaph or dedication recording a vow fulfilled by Secunda, a member of the College of Six and priestess of Augustus, and Felicia.

EDCS-10000292.text_edition: \n Oplus Aetoris filius Caeunus Ampliata Lucilla fecerunt\n
EDCS-10000292.translation: Oplus, son of Aetor, and Ampliata Lucilla made this.
EDCS-10000292.summary: This is a funerary epitaph or dedication indicating that Oplus and Ampliata Lucilla were the makers of the monument.

EDCS-10000293.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius, who died at the age of 6.

EDCS-10000296.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, possibly a funerary epitaph, mentioning the name Praeclarus.

EDCS-10000297.text_edition: \n Vendo Verica Triti filia annorum XXX hic sita est\n
EDCS-10000297.translation: Vendo Verica, daughter of Tritus, aged 30, is buried here.
EDCS-10000297.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vendo Verica, daughter of Tritus, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-10000298.text_edition: \n Vadica Apli filia Titua viva fecit sibi et Pasinae Quinti filiae Voltisae matri suae adiutorio Aetoris et Ceuni Vadicis fecerunt\n
EDCS-10000298.translation: Vadica, daughter of Aplus, made this for herself and for her mother Pasina, daughter of Quintus, with the assistance of Aetor and Ceunus Vadicis.
EDCS-10000298.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Vadica for herself and her mother, with assistance from Aetor and Ceunus Vadicis.

EDCS-10000299.text_edition: \n Titus Octavius Cai filius Macer |(centurio) cohortis I Liburnorum Iano Patri votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000299.translation: Titus Octavius Macer, son of Caius, a centurion of the First Cohort of Liburnians, fulfilled his vow to Janus the Father willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-10000299.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Octavius Macer, a centurion, to the god Janus.

EDCS-10000301.text_edition: \n Cossutiae S flaminicae divae Faustinae Aquileiae et Iadere Aquileienses publice\n
EDCS-10000301.translation: The people of Aquileia, on behalf of Cossutia, priestess of the deified Faustina, and Iadere of Aquileia.
EDCS-10000301.summary: This is a public dedication from the people of Aquileia to Cossutia, a priestess of the deified Faustina, and Iadere.

EDCS-10000302.text_edition: \n Lucio Tettio Epidiano nnorum VII Lucius Tettius Sperches pater VIvir Iulialis vivos sic posuit locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-10000302.translation: To Lucius Tettius Epidianus, aged VII. Lucius Tettius Sperches, father and member of the VIvir Iulialis, erected this while alive; the site was granted by decree of the town councillors.
EDCS-10000302.summary: This funerary monument was set up by a father for his son, with the land provided by a decree of the local council.

EDCS-10000303.text_edition: \n Eymetileno sic consorti vicani vici II Porta Media bene merito de se\n
EDCS-10000303.translation: To Eumeti lene, companion, by the residents of the vicus of the Second Gate, for her great merit towards them.
EDCS-10000303.summary: This inscription honors Eumeti lene, a deserving member of the community of the Second Gate vicus.

EDCS-10000305.text_edition: \n Iulia Ceuni filia Luculla viva fecit sibi et Veturiae Ceuni filiae Paullae matri Sexto Iulio \n
EDCS-10000305.translation: Iulia Ceuni filia Luculla made this while alive for herself and for her mother, Veturia Ceuni filia Paulla, and for Sextus Iulius.
EDCS-10000305.summary: A woman named Iulia Luculla dedicated this monument for herself and her family members.

EDCS-10000306.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Ceuni filius Sergia Curticus Aetor pontem de sua pecunia fecit longum pedes CXXCVII latum pedes X\n
EDCS-10000306.translation: Gaius Iulius Ceuni filius Sergia Curticus Aetor built the bridge at his own expense; it is 295 feet long and 10 feet wide.
EDCS-10000306.summary: This inscription records the construction of a bridge, specifying its dimensions and that it was funded by Gaius Iulius Curticus Aetor.

EDCS-10000310.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Marci filiae Carae annorum XXV Aelia Iusta mater infelix hic sita est\n
EDCS-10000310.translation: To the departed spirits. Iulia Cara, daughter of Marcus, aged 25, is buried here. Her unhappy mother, Aelia Iusta, placed this.
EDCS-10000310.summary: This tombstone commemorates Iulia Cara, who died at 25, and was set up by her grieving mother.

EDCS-10000311.text_edition: \n CESMA Saturninus coniungi sic fecit hic sita est\n
EDCS-10000311.translation: Cesma Saturninus had this joined together; he is buried here.
EDCS-10000311.summary: This monument was erected by Cesma Saturninus for his burial place.

EDCS-10000319.text_edition: \n Quintus Valerius Valerianus Probi filius annorum X hic situs est\n
EDCS-10000319.translation: Quintus Valerius Valerianus, son of Probus, aged 10, is buried here.
EDCS-10000319.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Quintus Valerius Valerianus, who died at the age of 10.

EDCS-10000320.text_edition: \n D(is) M(anibus) Secundi Turrini Muntani fili obiti annorum IX hic situs est\n
EDCS-10000320.translation: To the departed spirits. Secundus Turrini Muntani, son of Muntanus, aged 9, is buried here.
EDCS-10000320.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Secundus Turrini Muntani, who died at the age of 9.

EDCS-10000321.text_edition: \n ------] adio pi annorum rtus us monumentum posuit\n
EDCS-10000321.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary monument indicating that someone named Adio pi erected it for a deceased person of unknown age.

EDCS-10000322.summary: This very fragmentary inscription likely records the death of a child named Probus, aged three.

EDCS-10000324.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of a young person, dated to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000330.text_edition: \n Ditius Sestenius Frontonis filius annorum CV hic situs est\n
EDCS-10000330.translation: Ditius Sestenius, son of Fronto, aged 105 years, is buried here.
EDCS-10000330.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ditius Sestenius, who lived to an exceptional age, dated to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000331.text_edition: \n Libero Patri Lucius Gavius Optatus sacerdos Liburnorum immemoriam sic Gaviae Luci filae Maximae matris templum a parte dextra aediculae libero aditu maiori altano cum valvis et accubitu et sedibus vetustate corruptum a solo restituit\n
EDCS-10000331.translation: Lucius Gavius Optatus, priest of the Liburnians, to Liber Pater, in memory of his mother Gavia Maxima, daughter of Lucius, restored from the ground up the temple on the right of the aedicula, with free access to the larger altar, with doors, a dining couch area, and seats, which had been corrupted by age.
EDCS-10000331.summary: This inscription records the restoration of a temple dedicated to Liber Pater by Lucius Gavius Optatus, a priest, in memory of his mother, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000332.text_edition: \n Magnae Deum Matri Augustae sacrum Verridia Psyche\n
EDCS-10000332.translation: Sacred to the Great August Mother of the Gods, Verridia Psyche.
EDCS-10000332.summary: This is a dedication to the Magna Mater (Great Mother Goddess) by Verridia Psyche, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000334.text_edition: \n Agape vedua sic pro se et suos sic fecit pedes CCC\n
EDCS-10000334.translation: Agape the widow erected this for herself and her family, 300 feet.
EDCS-10000334.summary: This inscription marks a funerary plot or boundary, measuring 300 feet, dedicated by a widow named Agape, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000337.text_edition: \n Bonae Deae dominae Heiae Augustae triumphali terrae marisque dominatrici conservatrici mentiumque bonarum ac remediorum potenti deae bene iudicanti Calpurnia Luci Pisonis auguris filia Cnaei Pisonis neptis donum dedit\n
EDCS-10000337.translation: To the Good Goddess, the Lady Heia Augusta, triumphant mistress of earth and sea, preserver of good minds and powerful remedies, the goddess who judges well, Calpurnia, daughter of Lucius Piso the augur, granddaughter of Gnaeus Piso, gave this gift.
EDCS-10000337.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the Good Goddess, specifically referencing her dominion and healing powers, by Calpurnia, a woman of senatorial rank, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000609.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Baleriae sic Eytychianae sic quae vixit annos LII libertae patronus bene merenti posuit F B V\n
EDCS-10000609.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. The patron placed this for his deserving freedwoman, Baleria Eytychiana, who lived 52 years. F(avente) B(ona) V(entura).
EDCS-10000609.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a freedwoman, Baleria Eytychiana, erected by her patron, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000610.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caio Fulvio Crescenti Aemilia Tertulla\n
EDCS-10000610.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aemilia Tertulla [erected this] for Gaius Fulvius Crescens.
EDCS-10000610.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Aemilia Tertulla for Gaius Fulvius Crescens, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000611.text_edition: \n Caius Cordius vixit tandem vivus fecit sibi et Secundae Saturiae coniugi piissimae\n
EDCS-10000611.translation: Caius Cordius, finally alive, made this for himself and his most devoted wife, Secunda Saturia.
EDCS-10000611.summary: This is a self-funded funerary monument set up by Caius Cordius for himself and his wife, Secunda Saturia, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000612.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Publio Cornelio Iulio qui vixit annos LV Iulia et Irene filias sic patri pientissimo\n
EDCS-10000612.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Publius Cornelius Iulius, who lived 55 years, his devoted daughters Iulia and Irene placed this.
EDCS-10000612.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Publius Cornelius Iulius, erected by his daughters, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000616.summary: This inscription is a dedication by an unknown person to Vssius, likely a family member or relative, for his wife.

EDCS-10000617.text_edition: \n Deae xanctae sic Nemesi et Genio municipii Flavius Saturninus bbeneficiarius consularis legionis XI Claudiae libies sic posuit\n
EDCS-10000617.translation: To the holy goddess Nemesis and to the Genius of the municipality, Flavius Saturninus, a beneficiary of a consular (governor) of the eleventh Claudian legion, willingly set this up.
EDCS-10000617.summary: This is a dedication to Nemesis and the Genius of the municipality by Flavius Saturninus, a soldier in the XI Claudia legion, likely during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000618.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Tito Aurelio Aplini veter(ano qui vixit annos LXX et Aureliae Vendoni coniugi Titus Aurelius Turus parentibus piissimis posuit\n
EDCS-10000618.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titus Aurelius Turus set this up for his most devoted parents, Titus Aurelius Alpinus, a veteran who lived seventy years, and his wife Aurelia Vendo.
EDCS-10000618.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Titus Aurelius Turus for his parents, Titus Aurelius Alpinus (a veteran) and Aurelia Vendo, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000619.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelius Maximus Argenianus decurio municipii vivus sibi posuit et Aureliae Maximinae filiae quae vixit annos XXX\n
EDCS-10000619.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Maximus Argenianus, a decurion of the municipality, set this up for himself while alive and for his daughter Aurelia Maxima, who lived thirty years.
EDCS-10000619.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Aurelius Maximus Argenianus, a local official, for himself and his deceased daughter, Aurelia Maxima, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000620.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aureliae Vendoni quae vixit annos XXX Aurelius Plarens coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10000620.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Plarens set this up for his deserving wife, Aurelia Vendo, who lived thirty years.
EDCS-10000620.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Aurelius Plarens for his wife, Aurelia Vendo, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000621.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudiae Proculae quae vixit annis XXVII Lucius Valens Or coniugi pientissimae posuit\n
EDCS-10000621.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Valens Or set this up for his most dutiful wife, Claudia Procula, who lived twenty-seven years.
EDCS-10000621.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Lucius Valens Or for his wife, Claudia Procula, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000622.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aulo Gablio Liberali qui vixit annos XXIIX Flavius Aper beneficiarius consularis memoriam posuit\n
EDCS-10000622.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Flavius Aper, a beneficiary of a consular (governor), set up this memorial for Aulus Gablius Liberalis, who lived twenty-eight years.
EDCS-10000622.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Flavius Aper, a soldier or official, in memory of Aulus Gablius Liberalis, who died young, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000623.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Gav Durus Valeriano qui vixit \n
EDCS-10000623.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Gav Durus Valerianus, who lived...
EDCS-10000623.summary: This is an incomplete funerary epitaph for Gav Durus Valerianus, recording his name and age at death, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000625.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Paul Gaudene quae vixit annos XL Titus Pr R conpari pientissimae et sibi vivo posuit\n
EDCS-10000625.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titus Pr R set this up for his most dutiful wife, Paul Gaudene, who lived forty years, and for himself while alive.
EDCS-10000625.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Titus Pr R for his wife, Paul Gaudene, and himself, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000626.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Paconio Barbaro decurioni municipii Risinii qui vixit annos XX menses III dies IIII Lucius Paconius Barbarus et Aurelia Panto filio inconparabili sic et sibi vivi infelicissimi posuerunt\n
EDCS-10000626.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Paconius Barbarus and Aurelia Panto, most unhappy in life, set this up for their incomparable son, Lucius Paconius Barbarus, a decurion of the municipality of Risinium, who lived twenty years, three months, and four days, and for themselves while alive.
EDCS-10000626.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by parents for their son, Lucius Paconius Barbarus (a local decurion who died young), and for themselves, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000627.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Ratoniae Autumnae quae vixit annos XL Scardonitanae Asidonius Vitellianus |(centurio) cohortis II milliariae coniugi carissimae posuit\n
EDCS-10000627.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Asidonius Vitellianus, a centurion of the II *milliaria* cohort, placed this for his dearest wife, Ratonia Autumna, who lived 40 years, from Ascardona.
EDCS-10000627.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a centurion to his wife, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000628.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Statiae Aspasiae filiae Risinii vixit annos XL C Asclepiades coniugi Statia Sensis matri fecerunt\n
EDCS-10000628.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Asclepiades, a freedman of Caesar, and Statia Sensis made this for their wife and mother, Statia Aspasia, daughter of Risinius, who lived 40 years.
EDCS-10000628.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a husband and daughter to their wife and mother, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000629.text_edition: \n mava sibi et Luscio Paridi coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10000629.translation: Mava placed this for herself and for her well-deserving husband, Luscio Paris.
EDCS-10000629.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a wife for herself and her husband, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000630.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum monae quae vixit annos LXV C Piliae Despotanus aviae piae et matri vivae et R Piliae posuit\n
EDCS-10000630.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Pilia Despotanus placed this for her grandmother, who lived 65 years, and for her mother, Pilia, alive, and for R. Pilia.
EDCS-10000630.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a granddaughter for her grandmother and mother, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000631.text_edition: \n aepa et Paulae filiae et Sabinio Certo generi et Maximae neepotae Marcus Fulvinius Natalis bene merentibus posuit\n
EDCS-10000631.translation: Marcus Fulvinius Natalis placed this for his daughter Aepa, his son-in-law Sabinius Certus, and his granddaughter Maxima, all well-deserving.
EDCS-10000631.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a father and grandfather for his family members, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000635.text_edition: \n - stes viator qui SSOLISID Aurelius Proculus annorum LX vivus posuit ed sic dicit sit memoria sibi et suis parentibus et coniugi suae Ursaciae viventi quae habet annos XL Aurelius pater Rusticus vixit annos LXXV et Aurelia mater Victorinna vixit annos L Ursacia dixit frater Code precor magister adde ed sic meam matrem Proclinam viventem annorum X in sepulcri sic sumus aeternam sic sedem sic quiescimus CODDETCPFAVEMUS\n
EDCS-10000635.translation: Passerby, you who live! Aurelius Proculus, aged 60, placed this for himself alive. And he says this: 'Let there be a memorial for myself and my parents and my wife Ursacia, living, who is 40 years old. Aurelius pater Rusticus lived 75 years and Aurelia mater Victorinna lived 50 years. Ursacia said: Brother Code, I beg you, master, add my mother Proclina, living, aged 10, to the tomb. In this way we are in eternal dwelling, in this way we rest. We have added them!'
EDCS-10000635.summary: A lengthy funerary inscription where the dedicator, Aurelius Proculus, provides details about his family and their ages, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000636.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Maximus Magai votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000636.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Maximus Magai willingly paid his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-10000636.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Maximus Magai, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000637.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus tiae Balbae coniugi pientissimae annorum XVI item Aureliis Silvano et Propinquiano et Baetae et Iuniori filiis Titus Aurelius Silvanus decurio IIvir municipii Malvesiatium posuit\n
EDCS-10000637.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Aurelius Silvanus, a decurion and *duovir* of the municipality of Malvesiatium, placed this for his most dutiful wife Balba, aged 16, and also for his sons Aurelius Silvanus, Aurelius Propinquianus, Baeta, and Iunior.
EDCS-10000637.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a local magistrate to his wife and sons, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000638.translation: To Juno Regina, ACT willingly placed this.
EDCS-10000638.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Juno Regina by ACT, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000641.text_edition: \n Maximinus sculpet sic Dis Manibus Leo et Quartilla parentes miseri posuerunt Lupae filiae infelicissimae annorum XXII hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-10000641.translation: Maximinus carved this: 'Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Leo and Quartilla, wretched parents, placed this for their most unhappy daughter Lupa, aged 22. This monument will not pass to an heir.'
EDCS-10000641.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by parents to their deceased daughter, including a stipulation about inheritance, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000643.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Tironi mileti sic defuncto per | D E Valentina socero carissimo posuit bene merenti\n
EDCS-10000643.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Aurelius Tiron, a soldier who died thus. His father-in-law, D.E. Valentina, set this up for him, his dearest, who deserved it well.
EDCS-10000643.summary: A funerary monument commemorating Aurelius Tiron, a soldier, set up by his father-in-law, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000645.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Paentino vixit annos X Tranquillus frater posuit\n
EDCS-10000645.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Paentinus lived 10 years. His brother Tranquillus set this up.
EDCS-10000645.summary: A funerary monument for Paentinus, who lived 10 years, erected by his brother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000646.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Tertio annorum VIII parentes bene merenti fecerunt\n
EDCS-10000646.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Tertius, aged 8 years. His parents made this for him, who deserved it well.
EDCS-10000646.summary: A funerary monument for Tertius, aged 8, erected by his parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000647.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quinti Egnati Clementis CCC\n
EDCS-10000647.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Quintus Egnatius Clemens, 300 [units?].
EDCS-10000647.summary: A funerary monument for Quintus Egnatius Clemens, with an unclear numerical indication, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000648.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum L Secundi vixit annos X CVIN VC \n
EDCS-10000648.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Lucius Secundus lived 10 years. CVIN VC [likely a funerary formula or abbreviation].
EDCS-10000648.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Secundus, who lived 10 years, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000649.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Calpurnius Chresimus vixit annos XXXXV Aurelia Maximina MAIIIII \n
EDCS-10000649.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Calpurnius Chresimus lived 45 years. Aurelia Maximina [and others?] MAIIIII [likely a funerary formula or abbreviation].
EDCS-10000649.summary: A funerary monument for Calpurnius Chresimus, who lived 45 years, and Aurelia Maximina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000650.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Luscio Luriano vixit annos XX Luscii Proculus et Lurianus Sancti filii Statia Sabina socrus immemoriam sic\n
EDCS-10000650.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. For Lucius Luscius Lurianus, who lived 20 years. Luscii Proculus and Lurianus Sanctus, his sons, and Statia Sabina, his mother-in-law, in memory, thus [set this up].
EDCS-10000650.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Luscius Lurianus, who lived 20 years, erected by his sons and mother-in-law, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000652.text_edition: \n ------] Publio Dolabella legato pro praetore cohors VI voluntariorum tribuno Lucio Purtisio Atinate ilio Caio Saenio IIviris \n
EDCS-10000652.translation: [---] Publius Dolabella, as legatus pro praetore and tribune of the 6th cohort of volunteers, to Lucius Purtisius Atinas, his son; Caio Saenius, as duovir.
EDCS-10000652.summary: A fragmentary inscription recording official appointments and possibly a dedication, involving Publius Dolabella and Lucius Purtisius Atinas, likely from the Roman Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-10000655.text_edition: \n ITARAV annorum LV aritus us uxori \n
EDCS-10000655.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription recording the age of the deceased and their marital status, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000656.text_edition: \n Pomponiae Marci filiae Lepidae Lucius Sextilius Rufus coniugi bene merenti vivus fecit et sibi et suis\n
EDCS-10000656.translation: For Pomponia Lepida, daughter of Marcus. Lucius Sextilius Rufus erected this for his well-deserving wife while alive, and for himself and his family.
EDCS-10000656.summary: A funerary monument erected by Lucius Sextilius Rufus for his wife Pomponia Lepida and for himself and his family, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000657.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Eulalae quae et Firma bene merenti ia posuit \n
EDCS-10000657.translation: To Julia Eulala, also known as Firma, who placed this for her well-deserving.
EDCS-10000657.summary: A funerary dedication to Julia Eulala, likely by a spouse or family member, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000658.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning Fulvius and his wife, its purpose is unclear due to incompleteness, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000659.text_edition: \n Lucio Umbrio Valenti Lusia Paula viro suo\n
EDCS-10000659.translation: To Lucius Umbricius Valens, from Lusia Paula, his wife.
EDCS-10000659.summary: A funerary dedication from Lusia Paula to her husband, Lucius Umbricius Valens, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000662.text_edition: \n Lucius Funisulanus Vettonianus legatus pro praetore pontem et terminos renovari iussit per Titum Cassium Frontonem optionem legionis IIII Flaviae felicis in fundo Vesiio CN OSC D L V\n
EDCS-10000662.translation: Lucius Funisulanus Vettonianus, legate pro praetore, ordered the bridge and boundaries to be renewed through Titus Cassius Fronto, optio of the Legio IIII Flavia Felix, on the Vesiian estate.
EDCS-10000662.summary: An official inscription recording the renovation of a bridge and boundaries by a governor, Lucius Funisulanus Vettonianus, likely in the late 1st or early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-10000667.text_edition: \n sacrum ex voto solvit Caius Masurius Eutychus\n
EDCS-10000667.translation: Gaius Masurius Eutychus fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000667.summary: A votive inscription recording Gaius Masurius Eutychus fulfilling a vow, likely dedicated to a deity, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000668.text_edition: \n Marcello veterano cohortis III Alpinorum nnorum LXX stipendiorum XLIII Marcellae nnorum ae Cai libertae Iucundae \n
EDCS-10000668.translation: To Marcellus, veteran of the 3rd Cohort of Alpiners, aged 70, with 43 years of service; to Marcella, aged [X]; and to his freedwoman Iucunda.
EDCS-10000668.summary: A funerary inscription honoring a veteran of the 3rd Cohort of Alpiners and his family, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000671.text_edition: \n Publio Cornelio Proculo fratri Publio Cornelio Valenti patri Papiae Provinciae sorori Lucilia Sperata suis posuit\n
EDCS-10000671.translation: To Publius Cornelius Proculus, his brother; to Publius Cornelius Valens, his father; and to Papia Pro vincia, his sister, Lucilia Sperata dedicated this for her family.
EDCS-10000671.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucilia Sperata to her brother, father, and sister, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000672.text_edition: \n Fabia Silvina astrosa mater Quinto Iulio Clementino annorum XXX Quinto Iulio Feroci annorum VIII Quinto Iulio Severo annorum II Quinto Iulio Clementi coniugi viva fecit\n
EDCS-10000672.translation: Fabia Silvina, the 'astrosa' mother, made this for her husband Quintus Julius Clementius, aged 30, Quintus Julius Ferox, aged 8, Quintus Julius Severus, aged 2, and Quintus Julius Clemens.
EDCS-10000672.summary: A funerary monument set up by Fabia Silvina for her husband and three sons, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000673.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely commemorating Verecundus, possibly by Secunda or Verecunda, Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000674.text_edition: \n nnorum X hic sit- est Lucius Aelius Firmus \n
EDCS-10000674.translation: Aged 10, lies here Lucius Aelius Firmus.
EDCS-10000674.summary: A tombstone for Lucius Aelius Firmus, who died at the age of 10, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000677.translation: This inscription is too fragmented to translate.
EDCS-10000677.summary: This is a very fragmented inscription, likely part of a tombstone or monument, with the names Panius and Sarnus possibly preserved.

EDCS-10000678.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a woman named Ae Platoris, daughter of someone whose name is lost.

EDCS-10000680.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Allio Lupiano puero infilicissimo sic qui vixit annos XXVII menses VIII dies XV Allius Allianus C suo posuit\n
EDCS-10000680.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Allius Allianus set this up for his most unhappy son, Allio Lupiano, a boy who lived 27 years, 8 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-10000680.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Allio Lupiano, a boy who lived 27 years, and was dedicated by his father, Allius Allianus.

EDCS-10000681.text_edition: \n Fabricius et ia filiae ae Maximae? --- infelicissimae quae vixit \n
EDCS-10000681.translation: Fabricius and Ia, to their most unhappy daughter, Maxima(?)... who lived...
EDCS-10000681.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph for a daughter named Maxima (?), set up by Fabricius and Ia.

EDCS-10000682.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Curiatio Eutycho defuncto nnorum plus nnis LV Cania Ursina coniugi incomparabili posuit\n
EDCS-10000682.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Cania Ursina set this up for her incomparable husband, Titus Curiatius Eutyches, who passed away at the age of more than 55 years.
EDCS-10000682.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Titus Curiatius Eutyches, who died over the age of 55, and was dedicated by his wife, Cania Ursina.

EDCS-10000684.text_edition: \n PrA AT fato rapta hic sita est Caius Valerius Cai filius Dento veteranus legionis VII libertae suae posuit\n
EDCS-10000684.translation: Caius Valerius Dento, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, set this up for his freedwoman, who was snatched away by fate and is buried here.
EDCS-10000684.summary: This funerary inscription records a veteran of the Seventh Legion, Caius Valerius Dento, dedicating a tomb for his freedwoman.

EDCS-10000685.text_edition: \n Marco Vinicio Marci filio Masuriano nnorum II dierum XVIII\n
EDCS-10000685.translation: To Marcus Vinicius Masurianus, son of Marcus, aged 2 years and 18 days.
EDCS-10000685.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph for a young child, Marcus Vinicius Masurianus, who died at the age of 2 years and 18 days.

EDCS-10000689.text_edition: \n Sextus Erbonius Ingenuus cum coniuge et matre Spei sacrum\n
EDCS-10000689.translation: Sextus Erbonius Ingenuus, with his wife and mother, dedicates this sacred offering to Spei.
EDCS-10000689.summary: This dedicatory inscription records Sextus Erbonius Ingenuus offering something sacred to the deity Spei, along with his wife and mother.

EDCS-10000691.text_edition: \n Tito Flavio Herennio Iasoni equiti Romano decurioni coloniae Salonitanae pontifici aedili praefecto collegii fabrum et centonariorum filio Herenni Iasonis viri egregii patroni coloniae Salonitanae ob amorem patriae quem ob merita sua consecutus est et erga honorificentiam quam civibus exhibet collegium supra scriptum\n
EDCS-10000691.translation: For Titus Flavius Herennius Iaso, Roman knight, decurion of the colony of Salona, pontiff, aedile, prefect of the guild of builders and weavers, son of Herennius Iaso, an outstanding man and patron of the colony of Salona, because of his love of his country which he earned through his merits, and for the honor which he shows to the citizens, the aforesaid guild set this up.
EDCS-10000691.summary: This honorary inscription honors Titus Flavius Herennius Iaso, a prominent citizen and official of Salona, recognizing his merits and love for his community, and was erected by a guild.

EDCS-10000692.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavio Defensori optioni classis praetoriae Misenatium natione Britto stipendiorum X vixit nnos XXX \n
EDCS-10000692.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Flavius Defender, a Briton by nation, an optio in the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, who served for 10 years and lived 30 years.
EDCS-10000692.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Flavius Defender, a Briton who served as an optio in the Praetorian fleet at Misenum for 10 years and died at age 30.

EDCS-10000693.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Murcuio Iadiae militi cohortis I Belgarum an[norum ------\n
EDCS-10000693.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Murcius Iadius, a soldier of the I Belgarum cohort, [aged ---].
EDCS-10000693.summary: A funerary inscription for Murcius Iadius, a soldier in the I Belgarum cohort, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000694.text_edition: \n Septimius dus Pirami sic miles cohortis III Alpinorum centuria Marcelli IESS hic situs est testamento fieri iussit Plares Annaei eques ex singularibus Augusti\n
EDCS-10000694.translation: Septimius Dus Pirami, a miles of the III Alpinorum cohort, in the century of Marcellus, is buried here. Plares Annaeus, an equestrian serving as a bodyguard of Augustus, ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-10000694.summary: A funerary inscription for Septimius Dus Pirami, a soldier in the III Alpinorum cohort, commissioned by Plares Annaeus, an equestrian bodyguard of the emperor.

EDCS-10000695.text_edition: \n Marcus Satrius Marci libertus Valens VIvir fecit sibi et Satriae Eufrosyne coniugi pientissimae et Satrio Floro filio Satriae Valentinae filiae libertis libertabusque eorum hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur commune est una cum Statorio Festo posterisque eorum et Calpurnio Callisto amico in fronte pedes XV in agro pedes L\n
EDCS-10000695.translation: Marcus Satrius Valens, freedman of Marcus, a VIvir, made this monument for himself and for his most devoted wife Satria Eufrosyne, and for his son Satrius Florus, his daughter Satria Valentina, their freedmen and freedwomen. It will not follow an heir; it is common property together with Statorius Festus and their descendants, and with his friend Calpurnius Callistus. 15 feet in front, 50 feet in depth.
EDCS-10000695.summary: A large family tomb inscription for Marcus Satrius Valens, a freedman and a VIvir, detailing the beneficiaries of the monument and its dimensions, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000696.text_edition: \n Flavius Crescentianus commea felicem A per propr transire C\n
EDCS-10000696.translation: Flavius Crescentianus, may you pass the road safely. A per prop.
EDCS-10000696.summary: A brief inscription likely related to travel or a road marker, possibly from the late Roman period, referencing Flavius Crescentianus.

EDCS-10000697.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aeliae Victorinae defunctae nnorum XXV Marcus Aurelius Severus coniugi infelicissimae\n
EDCS-10000697.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Aelia Victorina, who died aged 25. Marcus Aurelius Severus to his most unhappy wife.
EDCS-10000697.summary: A funerary inscription for Aelia Victorina, dedicated by her husband Marcus Aurelius Severus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000698.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Annio Cosmo coniugi defuncto annorum \n
EDCS-10000698.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Lucius Annius Cosmo, who died aged [---].
EDCS-10000698.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Annius Cosmo, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000700.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Curiatio Eutycho defuncto nnorum plus minus XXXV Curiatia Dioscorilla posuit se viva sibi et marito suo bene merenti\n
EDCS-10000700.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Titus Curiatius Eutychus, who died aged more or less 35. Curiatia Dioscorilla placed this, while alive, for herself and her deserving husband.
EDCS-10000700.summary: A funerary inscription for Titus Curiatius Eutychus, dedicated by his wife Curiatia Dioscorilla, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000701.text_edition: \n D(is) M(anibus) Caio Curtio Valenti Cornelia Euhodia coniugi bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-10000701.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Cornelia Euhodia made this for her deserving husband Gaius Curtius Valens.
EDCS-10000701.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Curtius Valens, dedicated by his wife Cornelia Euhodia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000702.text_edition: \n Flaviae Ursulae defunctae nnorum XXX Lucius Valerius Secundus coniugi\n
EDCS-10000702.translation: To Flavia Ursula, who died aged 30. Lucius Valerius Secundus to his wife.
EDCS-10000702.summary: A funerary inscription for Flavia Ursula, dedicated by her husband Lucius Valerius Secundus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000704.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Pomponiae Hermetillae coniugi pientissimae quae vixit nnos plus minus XXV Pontianus coniugi incomparabili bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10000704.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Pomponia Hermetilla, his most devoted wife, who lived more or less 25 years. Pontianus placed this for his incomparable, deserving husband.
EDCS-10000704.summary: A funerary inscription for Pomponia Hermetilla, dedicated by her husband Pontianus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000705.text_edition: \n Pomponiae Veneriae et Aemilio Eucarpo Pomponia Nice parentibus Marco Aurelio Flavio coniugi incomparabili Aemilia Memoria uxor posuit cum quo vixi<t=S> annis XII \n
EDCS-10000705.translation: To Pomponia Veneria and Aemilius Eucarpus, Pomponia Nice set up this monument to her incomparable husband Marcus Aurelius Flavius, with whom she lived for 12 years.
EDCS-10000705.summary: A funerary monument set up by a wife for her husband and possibly parents, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000706.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Raetiniae Iadestinae matri piissimae Crescentilla filia bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10000706.translation: To the Divine Manes of Raetinia Iadestina, her most dutiful mother, Crescentilla set this up for her deserving daughter.
EDCS-10000706.summary: A funerary dedication by a daughter to her deceased mother, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000707.text_edition: \n defunct-) annorum XXII Servilia Lea mater infelicissim-\n
EDCS-10000707.translation: deceased at 22 years of age, Servilia Lea, her most unfortunate mother...
EDCS-10000707.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription recording the age of death of an individual and identifying their mother, likely Roman imperial.

EDCS-10000709.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Luciferiae defunctae nnorum XXII Achillaeus conservae obsequentissimae et Secundae sorori eius ob pietate sic\n
EDCS-10000709.translation: To the Divine Manes of Luciferia, deceased at 22 years of age, Achillaeus for his most obedient colleague and Secunda, his sister, because of their piety.
EDCS-10000709.summary: A funerary dedication for Luciferia, commemorating her age, collegiality, and sisterhood, likely Roman imperial.

EDCS-10000710.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tigridi dulcissimae Florus filius bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10000710.translation: To the Divine Manes of Tigris, his dearest one, Florus set this up for his deserving son.
EDCS-10000710.summary: A funerary dedication by a father to his son, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000711.summary: A very fragmentary inscription indicating that something was set up by 'A delicato', likely Roman imperial.

EDCS-10000712.summary: A highly fragmented inscription that likely records the lifespan of an individual, possibly Roman imperial.

EDCS-10000718.text_edition: \n Quisquis petes Muciani memento sic qum sic videas solum pendere vide ne facias quod tibi fieri non vis Mucianus Clodio Secundo dedi locum\n
EDCS-10000718.translation: Whoever you are, when you seek Mucianus, remember that just as you see him lying alone, do not do to others what you would not want done to yourself. Mucianus gave this place to Clodius Secundus.
EDCS-10000718.summary: An epitaph for Mucianus, who donated a burial plot to Clodius Secundus and included a moral exhortation, likely Roman imperial.

EDCS-10000720.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Aurelio Ursino defuncto annorum VII mensium V dierum XIII Titus Aurelius Ursinus a et p tiestamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-10000720.translation: To the Divine Manes of Titus Aurelius Ursinus, deceased at 7 years, 5 months, and 13 days, Titus Aurelius Ursinus ordered this to be made in his will.
EDCS-10000720.summary: A funerary inscription for a child, Titus Aurelius Ursinus, whose father of the same name commissioned the monument by will, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000721.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Victori infelicissimo Siponto defunctus annorum plus menus sic XXX\n
EDCS-10000721.translation: To the Divine Manes of Aurelius Victor, most unfortunate, deceased in Siponto at more than 30 months...
EDCS-10000721.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription for Aurelius Victor, noting his age and place of death, likely Roman imperial.

EDCS-10000722.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Anchariae Quintillae Ancharius Ursinus matri pientissimae\n
EDCS-10000722.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Ancharius Ursinus set this up for his most dutiful mother, Ancharia Quintilla.
EDCS-10000722.summary: A funerary dedication by a son to his mother, Ancharia Quintilla, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000723.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudio Chresto defuncto nnorum XIII mensium IIII dierum VIIII Curia Onesimus pientissimus sodalis bene merenti posuit Claudius Onesimus pater oviti sic Claudia Chreste pia mater posuerunt\n
EDCS-10000723.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Claudius Chrestus, who died at thirteen years, four months, and nine days. His most dutiful fellow member, Onesimus Curia, placed this for the well-deserving. Claudius Onesimus, his father, and Claudia Chreste, his devoted mother, erected this.
EDCS-10000723.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy, Claudius Chrestus, erected by his father and mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000724.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Coelio Eutycheti Margarita bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10000724.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Margarita placed this for Publius Coelius Eutychetes, well-deserving.
EDCS-10000724.summary: A funerary dedication by Margarita for Publius Coelius Eutychetes, likely Roman Imperial.

EDCS-10000725.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Seccius Corinthus Valeriae Valentinae defunctae annorum XXXV coniugi carissimae bene merenti\n
EDCS-10000725.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Seccius Corinthus and Valeria Valentina, who died at thirty-five years, his dearest wife, well-deserving.
EDCS-10000725.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Valentina, set up by her husband Seccius Corinthus, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000726.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Callistus Aemili et Crescentilla Crescenti filio infelicissimo nnorum II mens(ium) IIII et Fuscus amicus\n
EDCS-10000726.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Callistus, son of Aemilius and Crescentilla, at the age of two years, four months, most unfortunate. And Fuscus, a friend.
EDCS-10000726.summary: A funerary epitaph for Callistus, the son of Aemilius and Crescentilla, also mentioning a friend Fuscus, likely Roman Imperial.

EDCS-10000727.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Dorimae matri Doretos filius bene merenti\n
EDCS-10000727.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Dorima, his most deserving mother. Her son Doretos placed this.
EDCS-10000727.summary: A funerary dedication by a son, Doretos, to his mother, Dorima, likely Roman Imperial.

EDCS-10000728.text_edition: \n Tito Silicio Cio Firmo filio dulcissimo Titus Silicius Teleius et Ulpia Daphne parentes infelicissimi defuncto nnorum VII mensium V dierum XVI\n
EDCS-10000728.translation: For Titus Silicius Cio Firmus, his dearest son, who died at seven years, five months, and sixteen days. His most unhappy parents, Titus Silicius Teleius and Ulpia Daphne, erected this.
EDCS-10000728.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy, Titus Silicius Cio Firmus, erected by his grieving parents, likely Roman Imperial.

EDCS-10000733.text_edition: \n Iovi et Ianae posuit donum Salvius Venerius pro salute collegii\n
EDCS-10000733.translation: Salvius Venerius set up this offering for Jupiter and Juno for the well-being of the association.
EDCS-10000733.summary: A dedication to Jupiter and Juno made by Salvius Venerius on behalf of a collegium (association), likely Roman Imperial.

EDCS-10000734.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valentillae Hilarus coniugi suae quae infelicissimae coniugi suae quae vixit annos plus minus XXX et Aelia vixit annos plus minus VIII\n
EDCS-10000734.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Valentilla, his most unhappy wife, who lived about thirty years, and Aelia, who lived about eight years. Hilarus [set this up] for his wife.
EDCS-10000734.summary: A funerary epitaph by Hilarus for his wife Valentilla and possibly his daughter Aelia, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10000737.text_edition: \n Batuia T Tito Aurelio Diadumeno coniugi pientissimo\n
EDCS-10000737.translation: For Batuia T. For her most dutiful husband, Titus Aurelius Diadumenus.
EDCS-10000737.summary: A funerary dedication by Batuia to her devoted husband, Titus Aurelius Diadumenus, likely Roman Imperial.

EDCS-10000739.text_edition: \n Theuda Theodoti filius domo Alexandria annorum LXX hic situs est Galene Theodoti liberta viva fecit sibi et Theodoto et Lupulo filiis suis et Arria<e> Secundae soro<r>i suae et Primig<e>niae libertae suae\n
EDCS-10000739.translation: Theuda, son of Theodotus, from Alexandria, aged 70, lies here. Galene, freedwoman of Theodotus, made this for herself, her sons Theodotus and Lupulus, her sister Arria Secunda, and her freedwoman Primigenia.
EDCS-10000739.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Theuda and set up by his freedwoman Galene for herself and other family members, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000740.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Aelius Le[---].
EDCS-10000740.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription beginning with a dedication to the departed spirits, likely for Aelius Le[---], Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000742.text_edition: \n annos LX mensium et Iuniae ECae p et heres eius bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10000742.translation: [He lived] 60 years and 1 month, and his heir erected this for the well-deserving Iunia Eca and her.
EDCS-10000742.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age of a deceased individual and set up by their heir for the well-deserving Iunia Eca, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000743.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Dirutia [---].
EDCS-10000743.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed for Dirutia, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000744.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulia Panto Iuliae Statiae nepoti bene merenti annorum XXX \n
EDCS-10000744.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Iulia Panto, for her well-deserving granddaughter Iulia Statia, aged 30.
EDCS-10000744.summary: A funerary inscription honoring Iulia Statia, who died at age 30, set up by her grandmother Iulia Panto, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000745.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Firminus urso rapto a parentibus\n
EDCS-10000745.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Firminus, taken by his parents [---].
EDCS-10000745.summary: A funerary inscription dedicating Firminus to the Spirits of the Departed, set up by his parents, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000747.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Reginae Fortunae Reduci et Genio Luci Angitiae Septimius Iustianus beneficiarius consularis cum suis posuit Gentiano et Basso cons(ulibus) M I IIII C\n
EDCS-10000747.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno Regina, Fortune the Bringer-Back, and the Genius of the Place, Septimius Iustianus, a beneficiary of the consular governor, with his family, set this up in the consulship of Gentianus and Bassus, in the year 119 AD.
EDCS-10000747.summary: A dedication to several deities by Septimius Iustianus, a military official, dating to 119 AD.

EDCS-10000749.text_edition: \n Dianae Augustae sacrum Publius Marronius Maximianus votum libens posuit\n
EDCS-10000749.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Publius Marronius Maximianus willingly set up his vow.
EDCS-10000749.summary: A votive inscription dedicating a shrine to Diana Augusta by Publius Marronius Maximianus, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000751.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Naevieno Senecae decurioni aedili IIviro coloniae Aequensium defuncto nnorum XXXXV Seccia Pudentilla marito rarissimo et Naevieni Seneca et Pudentilla filii patri optimo\n
EDCS-10000751.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Seccia Pudentilla set this up for her very rare husband, Titus Naevienus Seneca, decurion, aedile, and duovir of the colony of the Aequenses, who died at the age of 45, and for Naevienus Seneca and Pudentilla, his sons, to their excellent father.
EDCS-10000751.summary: A funerary monument erected by Seccia Pudentilla for her deceased husband, Titus Naevienus Seneca, a local magistrate, and their sons, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000752.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Apuleio Apulei filio Proculo Rufo \n
EDCS-10000752.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Lucius Apuleius Proculus Rufus, son of Apuleius.
EDCS-10000752.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Apuleius Proculus Rufus, son of Apuleius, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000753.text_edition: \n Caecilia Procula testamento fieri iussit sibi et Tiberio Claudio Syrioni coniugi bene merenti Lucius Caecilius Onesimus heres ponendum curavit\n
EDCS-10000753.translation: Caecilia Procula ordered this to be made in her will for herself and for her well-deserving husband, Tiberius Claudius Syrioni; Lucius Caecilius Onesimus, the heir, saw to its placement.
EDCS-10000753.summary: A funerary monument set up by an heir according to a will for Caecilia Procula and her husband Tiberius Claudius Syrioni.

EDCS-10000754.text_edition: \n Diis Manibus sacrum Luci Tetti Luculli\n
EDCS-10000754.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead, of Lucius Tettius Lucullus.
EDCS-10000754.summary: A dedication to the Di Manes (spirits of the dead) for Lucius Tettius Lucullus.

EDCS-10000755.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Sabbatiae defunctae annorum II mensium \n
EDCS-10000755.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, for Sabbatia, deceased, aged 2 years and [number] months.
EDCS-10000755.summary: A funerary dedication for Sabbatia, who died at a very young age.

EDCS-10000756.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus riae Titiae annos XXX tia Spe[rata ------\n
EDCS-10000756.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, for Ria Titia, aged 30 years, [daughter of] Sperata.
EDCS-10000756.summary: A funerary dedication for Ria Titia, aged 30, likely naming her mother.

EDCS-10000757.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus io Atroci a Prima \n
EDCS-10000757.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, for [name, possibly from the gens Atrocia], from Prima.
EDCS-10000757.summary: A funerary dedication to the Di Manes, possibly for a person named Atrocius, set up by someone named Prima.

EDCS-10000759.text_edition: \n Octavia Fortunata Veneri votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000759.translation: Octavia Fortunata willingly paid her vow to Venus, as was deserved.
EDCS-10000759.summary: A votive inscription recording Octavia Fortunata fulfilling a vow to the goddess Venus.

EDCS-10000760.text_edition: \n Marco Caecilio Saturnino veterano legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis et Viriae Publi libertae uxori \n
EDCS-10000760.translation: For Marcus Caecilius Saturninus, a veteran of the eleventh Claudian legion, pious and faithful, and for Viria, freedwoman of Publius, his wife.
EDCS-10000760.summary: A funerary dedication for a veteran soldier, Marcus Caecilius Saturninus, and his wife Viria.

EDCS-10000761.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Naeviae Placidae coniugi inconparabili! imae IIE \n
EDCS-10000761.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, for Naevia Placida, an incomparable wife!
EDCS-10000761.summary: A funerary dedication for Naevia Placida, described as an unparalleled wife.

EDCS-10000762.translation: Fortunatus, a member of the college of VIvirate.
EDCS-10000762.summary: An inscription recording the name Fortunatus and his membership in a college of six officials (VIvir).

EDCS-10000763.translation: Aurelius Gavius for [his own/someone else's] health.
EDCS-10000763.summary: A fragmentary inscription recording Aurelius Gavius dedicating something for health, likely a vow or funerary purpose.

EDCS-10000765.text_edition: \n Neptuno Andabatensi Aelius Maximus Classicianus votum solvit\n
EDCS-10000765.translation: Aelius Maximus Classicianus gladly fulfilled his vow to Neptune of Andabatis.
EDCS-10000765.summary: This is a votive inscription to Neptune of Andabatis by Aelius Maximus Classicianus.

EDCS-10000766.text_edition: \n BuVC-- defunctae nnorum XXXX Paicus maritus fecit\n
EDCS-10000766.translation: Paicus made this for his deceased wife, aged forty years.
EDCS-10000766.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Paicus for his wife, who died at the age of forty.

EDCS-10000769.text_edition: \n L Sabina Nepoti et Frontoniano filiis pientissimis fecit\n
EDCS-10000769.translation: L. Sabina made this for her most devoted sons, Nepos and Frontonianus.
EDCS-10000769.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by L. Sabina for her sons, Nepos and Frontonianus.

EDCS-10000772.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri et Dianae Aurelius Platius Varronis libertus posuit\n
EDCS-10000772.translation: Aurelius Platius, freedman of Varro, dedicated this to Silvanus Silvestris and Diana.
EDCS-10000772.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus Silvestris and Diana by Aurelius Platius, freedman of Varro.

EDCS-10000776.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno et Castoribus Marcus Aurelius Flavus sacerdos Iovis Optimi Maximi Dolicheni votum solvit libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-10000776.translation: Marcus Aurelius Flavus, priest of Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus, gladly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus and the Castores.
EDCS-10000776.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus and the Castores by the priest Marcus Aurelius Flavus.

EDCS-10000777.text_edition: \n Silvanis Augustis sacrum Callimorphus Augusti nostri verna dispensator votum solvit\n
EDCS-10000777.translation: Callimorphus, a palace slave and steward of our emperor, fulfilled his vow to the August Silvani.
EDCS-10000777.summary: This is a votive inscription to the August Silvani by Callimorphus, an imperial slave and steward.

EDCS-10000778.translation: [Procurator] of the Emperor [with 3 illegible letters] / [four illegible letters].
EDCS-10000778.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely referring to an imperial procurator, possibly with numerical information.

EDCS-10000781.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription with illegible names and text.

EDCS-10000783.text_edition: \n Marc- M Cai fili- T XXVI \n
EDCS-10000783.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the name, parentage, and age of Marcus.

EDCS-10000787.text_edition: \n Nemesi Piae in honorem collegii et Ianuari vilici Ianuarius ex corpore possuit sic\n
EDCS-10000787.translation: Ianuarius, from the guild, set up this monument to the Pious Nemesis, in honor of the guild and of Ianuarius the overseer.
EDCS-10000787.summary: This is a dedication to Nemesis by Ianuarius for the honor of his guild and its overseer, also named Ianuarius.

EDCS-10000788.text_edition: \n Sedato Augusto pro salute Aureli vilici collegius sic votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000788.translation: For the welfare of Aurelius, the manager, Sedatus Augustus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-10000788.summary: A private individual named Sedatus Augustus fulfilled a vow for the welfare of Aurelius, a manager, perhaps on an altar or a shrine.

EDCS-10000789.text_edition: \n Sedato Augusto pro salute Aureli vilici collegae votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-10000789.translation: For the welfare of the colleague Aurelius, they fulfilled their vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-10000789.summary: A group of individuals fulfilled a vow for the welfare of their colleague Aurelius, possibly on a shared monument.

EDCS-10000790.text_edition: \n Terrae Matri sacrum pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Antonini Augusti Iulius procurator per Ianuarium et Bassum VIL \n
EDCS-10000790.translation: Sacred to Mother Earth for the welfare of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, Julius, the procurator, through Ianuarius and Bassus, the VIL, dedicated this.
EDCS-10000790.summary: A provincial official, Julius the procurator, dedicated this sacred object to Mother Earth for the welfare of Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus, likely in the mid-2nd century CE.

EDCS-10000791.text_edition: \n Terrae Matri sacrum pro salute Cai Iuli Agathopi conductoris ferrariarum Callimorphus vilicus votum solvit XI Kalendas Maias Muciano et Fabiano consulibus\n
EDCS-10000791.translation: Sacred to Mother Earth for the welfare of Caius Julius Agathopus, the conductor of the ironworks, Callimorphus, the manager, fulfilled his vow on the 11th day before the Kalends of May, during the consulship of Mucianus and Fabius.
EDCS-10000791.summary: A manager named Callimorphus dedicated this to Mother Earth for the welfare of Caius Julius Agathopus, the supervisor of iron mines, on April 21st, 217 CE.

EDCS-10000792.text_edition: \n Terrae Matri sacrum pro salute Imperatoris Caesaris Marci Aureli Severi Alexandri Pii Felicis Augusti et Iuliae Maesiae sic Augustae [] Primus rcus Augusti Maximo II et Aeliano consulibus XI Kalendas Maias\n
EDCS-10000792.translation: Sacred to Mother Earth for the welfare of the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Pius Felix Augustus and Julia Maesia Julia Augusta [---]. Primus, freedman of Augustus, during the second consulship of Maximinus and Aelianus, on the 11th day before the Kalends of May.
EDCS-10000792.summary: A freedman named Primus dedicated this to Mother Earth for the welfare of Emperor Severus Alexander and Julia Maesa, during the consulship of Maximinus and Aelianus, likely in 223 CE.

EDCS-10000794.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Publi filio Iuvenali decurioni municipii Delminensium quaestori IIviro vixit annos XXXIII Publius Aelius Victor Varanus decurio municipii eiusdem IIIIvir quinqennalis et Aelia Buo parentes filio pientissimo posuerunt et sibi et suis\n
EDCS-10000794.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Publius Aelius Juvenalis, son of Publius, decurion of the municipality of Delminium, quaestor, who lived 33 years. Publius Aelius Victor Varanus, decurion of the same municipality, quinquennial duovir, and Aelia Buo, his parents, set this up for their most devoted son and for themselves and their kin.
EDCS-10000794.summary: A funerary monument for Publius Aelius Juvenalis, a decurion and quaestor from Delminium, erected by his parents, who were also local officials, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000796.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Quintus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000796.translation: For Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius Quintus fulfilled his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-10000796.summary: Publius Aelius Quintus dedicated this to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-10000797.text_edition: \n Marti Tossius Sura miles legionis VIII Augustae votum libens posuit\n
EDCS-10000797.translation: Tossius Sura, a soldier of the Legio VIII Augusta, willingly erected this vow.
EDCS-10000797.summary: Tossius Sura, a soldier of the Legio VIII Augusta, erected a votive offering.

EDCS-10000799.summary: A dedication to the Unconquered God, likely part of a larger inscription.

EDCS-10000800.summary: A fragmentary inscription indicating a dedication to Silvanus by someone named Sicanius(?).

EDCS-10000801.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri s(acrum) Titus Cai filius\n
EDCS-10000801.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Silvestris. Titus, son of Cai.
EDCS-10000801.summary: A dedication to the god Silvanus Silvestris by Titus, son of Cai.

EDCS-10000803.text_edition: \n Tiberius Cla<u>dius Firmus Tiberi Cl<a>udi Triti filius Firmus nnorum VIII hic situs est\n
EDCS-10000803.translation: Tiberius Claudius Firmus, son of Tiberius Claudius Tritus, eight years old, is buried here.
EDCS-10000803.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Tiberius Claudius Firmus, aged eight.

EDCS-10000804.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quinto Rutilio Quinti filio Titiano IIviro quinquennali et Quinto Rutilio Quinti filio Proculo IIviro quinqennali filio eius principi municipi Riditarum\n
EDCS-10000804.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Quintus Rutilius Titianus, son of Quintus, a quinquennial duovir, and for his son Quintus Rutilius Proculus, son of Quintus, a quinquennial duovir, princeps of the municipality of Riditarum.
EDCS-10000804.summary: A funerary dedication honoring two members of the Rutilius family, one of whom held the office of quinquennial duovir and princeps municipii.

EDCS-10000806.text_edition: \n Aplis Turi Statani filius annorum IIII\n
EDCS-10000806.translation: Aplus, son of Turius Statanius, four years old.
EDCS-10000806.summary: A funerary inscription for Aplus, son of Turius Statanius, aged four.

EDCS-10000807.summary: A funerary inscription for Boria, daughter of [name], aged forty.

EDCS-10000810.text_edition: \n Maximus Laedicalius Aplinis filius annorum III hic situs est\n
EDCS-10000810.translation: Maximus Laedicalius, son of Aplus, three years old, is buried here.
EDCS-10000810.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Maximus Laedicalius, son of Aplus, aged three.

EDCS-10000813.text_edition: \n Pinso Epicadi filius nnorum XXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-10000813.translation: Pinso, son of Epicadus, thirty years old, is buried here.
EDCS-10000813.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Pinso, son of Epicadus, aged thirty.

EDCS-10000814.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Fortunato alumno annorum II mensium III Treboni- posuit\n
EDCS-10000814.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Fortunatus, foster-child, two years and three months old. Trebonius placed [this].
EDCS-10000814.summary: A funerary dedication to the Divine Spirits for Fortunatus, a foster-child, erected by Trebonius.

EDCS-10000815.text_edition: \n Vendo Acenica Culi filia nnorum XX hic sita est\n
EDCS-10000815.translation: Vendo, daughter of Acenica Culus, twenty years old, is buried here.
EDCS-10000815.summary: A funerary inscription recording the burial of Vendo, daughter of Acenica Culus, aged twenty.

EDCS-10000818.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age of a deceased person named Tritus.

EDCS-10000821.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Pladomenus, son of Darmocus, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-10000822.translation: Turus, son of Darmocus and Aplinis.
EDCS-10000822.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying Turus as the son of Darmocus and Aplinis, from the Roman period.

EDCS-10000823.translation: Sextus, a Caonatian from Batonia, made this.
EDCS-10000823.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Sextus, a Caonatian from Batonia, erected the monument.

EDCS-10000824.text_edition: \n Panto Laedietis Aplinis annorum XXX hic sita est\n
EDCS-10000824.translation: Panto, son of Laedietis, who was 30 years old, is buried here.
EDCS-10000824.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Panto, son of Laedietis, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-10000827.text_edition: \n T Darmo Iano votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000827.translation: T. Darmo willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Ianus.
EDCS-10000827.summary: This is a votive inscription where T. Darmo dedicates something to the god Ianus.

EDCS-10000828.text_edition: \n Lunae votum solvi libens Murine sic\n
EDCS-10000828.translation: Murine willingly fulfilled her vow to Luna, thus.
EDCS-10000828.summary: This is a votive inscription where Murine dedicates something to the goddess Luna.

EDCS-10000832.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus, likely inscribed on a shrine or altar.

EDCS-10000833.translation: Lucius Iulius Restitutus made this sacred offering to Silvanus.
EDCS-10000833.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus by Lucius Iulius Restitutus.

EDCS-10000834.text_edition: \n Ieta Aeronia Cai filia votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000834.translation: Ieta Aeronia, daughter of Cai, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-10000834.summary: This is a votive inscription where Ieta Aeronia, daughter of Cai, fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-10000837.text_edition: \n Claudia Liburno praefecto cohortis IIII praefecto cohortis III Thracum m tribuno militum legionis XXII M AVG PIAEL \n
EDCS-10000837.translation: Claudia, to Liburnus, prefect of the 4th cohort, prefect of the 3rd Thracian cohort, military tribune of the 22nd Legion M AVG PIAEL.
EDCS-10000837.summary: This is a dedication to Claudia, listing her extensive military career including command of two cohorts and service as a military tribune in the 22nd Legion M AVG.

EDCS-10000839.text_edition: \n Veratiae Quintinae coniugi carissimae annorum X bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-10000839.translation: He made this for Veratia Quintina, his dearest wife, aged 10, who deserved well.
EDCS-10000839.summary: A funerary monument set up by a husband for his deceased wife, Veratia Quintina, dated by the age of the deceased to the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10000840.summary: A funerary inscription for a freedwoman named Secunda, possibly set up by her patron Ceuna or another freedwoman named Darmoca.

EDCS-10000842.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Iulius Victorinus beneficiarius consularis votum solvit\n
EDCS-10000842.translation: Gaius Julius Victorinus, a beneficiarius consularis, fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-10000842.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Gaius Julius Victorinus, a military official, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000843.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci Marcus Aurelius Dalmata protector consularis pro beneficiario libens posuit\n
EDCS-10000843.translation: Marcus Aurelius Dalmata, protector consularis, willingly dedicated this for his beneficiarius to Jupiter Best and Greatest and the Genius of the place.
EDCS-10000843.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius loci by Marcus Aurelius Dalmata, a high-ranking military official, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000844.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Minervae Publius Aelius Secundus miles legionis I adiutricis beneficiarius consularis \n
EDCS-10000844.translation: Publius Aelius Secundus, a beneficiarius consularis, a soldier of the I Adiutrix legion, [dedicated this] to Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno, and Minerva.
EDCS-10000844.summary: A dedication to Jupiter, Juno, and Minerva by Publius Aelius Secundus, a soldier and beneficiarius consularis of the I Adiutrix legion, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000845.translation: Rus, a Roman knight, gave this gift.
EDCS-10000845.summary: A dedication of a gift by Rus, identified as an eques Romanus (Roman knight), with the purpose and recipient unspecified.

EDCS-10000846.text_edition: \n Publius Cerisius Publi filius Sabatina I Fa miles nnorum XL \n
EDCS-10000846.translation: Publius Cerisius, son of Publius, of the Sabatina tribe, a soldier of the [First?] legion, aged 40.
EDCS-10000846.summary: A funerary inscription for Publius Cerisius, a soldier of unspecified legion, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10000847.text_edition: \n Caius Cottius Cai Quirina Priscinus Aug Ne miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis stipendiorum XVI nnm XXXV testamento fieri iu[ssit ------\n
EDCS-10000847.translation: Gaius Cottius Priscinus, of the Quirina tribe, a soldier of the XI Claudia pia fidelis legion, with 16 years of service and aged 35, ordered this to be made by his will [lacuna].
EDCS-10000847.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Cottius Priscinus, a soldier of the XI Claudia legion, who made arrangements for his burial in his will, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000848.text_edition: \n Quintus Iulius Quinti filius Oufentina Mansuetus Como miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis nnorum XXXV stipendiorum XIIII testamento fieri iussit Quintus Iulius Annius miles legionis eiusdem fratri pientissimo faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-10000848.translation: Quintus Julius Mansuetus, son of Quintus, of the Oufentina tribe, from Como, a soldier of the XI Claudia pia fidelis legion, aged 35 with 14 years of service, ordered this to be made by his will; Quintus Julius Annius, a soldier of the same legion, saw to it being made for his most devoted brother.
EDCS-10000848.summary: A funerary monument arranged by will for Quintus Julius Mansuetus and erected by his brother Quintus Julius Annius, both soldiers of the XI Claudia legion, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000849.text_edition: \n Lucius Laberius Luci filius Firmus tribu Claudia domo Foro Claudi miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis annorum XXXV stipendiorum XIIII h(ic)? s(itus) est Titus Talonius Secundus testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-10000849.translation: Lucius Laberius Firmus, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, from Forum Claudii, a soldier of the XI Claudia pia fidelis legion, aged 35 with 14 years of service, is buried here; Titus Talonius Secundus ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-10000849.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Laberius Firmus, a soldier of the XI Claudia legion, whose burial was arranged by Titus Talonius Secundus in his will, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000850.text_edition: \n Lucius Magnus Luci Fabia Clemens Tavia miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis optio stipendiorum XI annorum XXX posuit Lucius Salvius Clemens frater\n
EDCS-10000850.translation: Lucius Salvius Clemens, his brother, set this up for Lucius Magnus Lucius Fabia Clemens Tavia, a optio in the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, who served for 11 years and was 30 years old.
EDCS-10000850.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a brother for his deceased sibling, Lucius Magnus Tavia, who was an optio in the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis legion.

EDCS-10000851.text_edition: \n Quintus Satrius Quinti Publilia Martialis Verona miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis nnorum XXXIIII stipendiorum XIIII\n
EDCS-10000851.translation: Quintus Satrius Martialis Verona, son of Quintus Publilius, a soldier in the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, 34 years old, served for 14 years.
EDCS-10000851.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Quintus Satrius Martialis Verona, a soldier in the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis legion.

EDCS-10000852.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tertius Antonius Terti filius Pollia Rufinus Parma miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis stipendiorum XVI\n
EDCS-10000852.translation: To the departed spirits. Rufinus, son of Tertius Antonius, of the Pollia tribe, from Parma, a soldier in the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, served for 16 years.
EDCS-10000852.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Rufinus, a soldier in the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis legion.

EDCS-10000853.text_edition: \n Buccus Staumi filius Fabia domo Brixia miles cohortis Montanorum nnorum XXVI stipendiorum IIII hic situs est testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-10000853.translation: Buccus, son of Staumus, of the Fabia tribe, from Brixia, a soldier in the Montanorum cohort, 26 years old, served for 4 years. He was buried here, as he ordered to be done by his will.
EDCS-10000853.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Buccus, a soldier in the Montanorum cohort, who was buried here according to his will.

EDCS-10000854.text_edition: \n Heras Ennomai filius miles cohortis II Cyrrhestarum domo Cyrrho nnorum L stipendiorum XXII hic situs est raeus Abemmi filius heres posuit\n
EDCS-10000854.translation: Heras, son of Ennomus, a soldier in the II Cyrrhestarum cohort, from Cyrrhus, 50 years old, served for 22 years. Rhaeus, son of Abemmi, his heir, set this up.
EDCS-10000854.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Heras, a soldier in the II Cyrrhestarum cohort, set up by his heir.

EDCS-10000855.text_edition: \n Imerix Servofredi filius Batavos sic eques alae Hispanorum I annorum XXVIII stipendiorum VIII hic situs est azicico et Apulo co Aetoris filio heres\n
EDCS-10000855.translation: Imerix, son of Servofredus, a Batavian, served as an *eques* in the I Ala Hispanorum, 28 years old, served for 8 years. He was buried here; Azicicus and his heir, Apulus, son of Aetor, set this up.
EDCS-10000855.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Imerix, a Batavian *eques* in the I Ala Hispanorum, set up by Azicicus and Apulus.

EDCS-10000856.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Allio Saturnino decurioni municipi Varvariae IIIIviro iure dicundo Alli Maximina et Maximus patri bene merenti\n
EDCS-10000856.translation: To the departed spirits. Alli Maximina and Maximus, to their well-deserving father Titus Allius Saturninus, decurion of the municipality of Varvaria, *IIvir* with judicial power.
EDCS-10000856.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a wife and son for their father, Titus Allius Saturninus, a local magistrate in Varvaria.

EDCS-10000857.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Nonio Clementiano decurioni municipi Burnistarum aedili defuncto annorum XL Nonius Lucius et Valeria Valentilla fratri pientissimo\n
EDCS-10000857.translation: To the departed spirits. Nonius Lucius and Valeria Valentilla, to their most dutiful brother Lucius Nonius Clementianus, decurion of the municipality of Burnista, deceased *aedile*, 40 years old.
EDCS-10000857.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by siblings for their deceased brother, Lucius Nonius Clementianus, an *aedile* and decurion in Burnista.

EDCS-10000858.text_edition: \n Tito Queresio Aetoris filio patri Volusiae Marci filiae Aetriae matri Queresiae Titi filiae Maximae sorori Queresiae Titi filiae Tertiae sorori Caius Volusius Maximus heres faciendum curavit\n
