EDCS-10000858.translation: Caius Volusius Maximus, the heir, arranged for this to be made for Titus Queresius, father, son of Aetor; Aetria, mother, daughter of Marcus; Queresia Maxima, sister, daughter of Titus; and Queresia Tertia, sister, daughter of Titus.
EDCS-10000858.summary: This is a funerary monument arranged by Caius Volusius Maximus for his father, mother, and two sisters.

EDCS-10000859.text_edition: \n Caius Virius Cai libertus Vitalis nnum VI hic situs est Caius Virius et Neuma mater posuere\n
EDCS-10000859.translation: Caius Virius Vitalis, freedman of Caius Virius, 6 years old. Caius Virius and his mother Neuma set this up.
EDCS-10000859.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young boy, Caius Virius Vitalis, set up by his father and mother.

EDCS-10000860.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Veneriae et Felicissimae filiabus infelicissimis Iulia Secunda mater posuit\n
EDCS-10000860.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Secunda erected this for her most unfortunate daughters Veneria and Felicissima.
EDCS-10000860.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Iulia Secunda to her daughters Veneria and Felicissima.

EDCS-10000861.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely recording the name of Lucius Fundanius Quintus Fabius, possibly an epitaph or dedication.

EDCS-10000862.text_edition: \n Lucius Mart filius annorum Lucius Ols<t=I>ius --- fecit \n
EDCS-10000862.translation: Lucius, son of Mart, aged... Lucius Olsitius made this.
EDCS-10000862.summary: A funerary inscription stating that Lucius Olsitius erected this monument for Lucius, son of Mart, whose age is partly lost.

EDCS-10000867.text_edition: \n Volltisa Iovi votum solvit libens merito Caminis liberta\n
EDCS-10000867.translation: Volltisa, freedwoman of Caminis, gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Iuppiter.
EDCS-10000867.summary: A dedication to Jupiter by Volltisa, a freedwoman, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-10000868.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Titia Ursula filio infilicissimo sic qui vixit annos XXIII\n
EDCS-10000868.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Marcus Aurelius, her most unfortunate son, who lived 23 years.
EDCS-10000868.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius, erected by his mother Titia Ursula.

EDCS-10000869.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Aurelio Paetilio filio dulcissimo nnorum IIII mensium VIIII dierum VIII Marcus Aurelius Surillio et Apuleia Titia parentes piissimi posuerunt\n
EDCS-10000869.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Marcus Aurelius, her sweetest son, aged 4 years, 9 months, and 8 days. Marcus Aurelius Surillius and Apuleia Titia, his most devoted parents, erected this.
EDCS-10000869.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Aurelius, erected by his parents Marcus Aurelius Surillius and Apuleia Titia.

EDCS-10000871.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulius Eutyches et Cassia Iunia parentes Iulio Rufino filio infilicissimo sic qui vixit annos XIII menses VI dies XVIIII\n
EDCS-10000871.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulius Eutyches and Cassia Iunia, parents, for their most unfortunate son Iulius Rufinus, who lived 13 years, 6 months, and 19 days.
EDCS-10000871.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulius Rufinus, erected by his parents Iulius Eutyches and Cassia Iunia.

EDCS-10000872.translation: His sister erected this for Lucius Petilius Celer.
EDCS-10000872.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Lucius Petilius Celer by his sister.

EDCS-10000873.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Primio sibi et suis vivos sic fecit\n
EDCS-10000873.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Primius made this for himself and his family while alive.
EDCS-10000873.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Primius for himself and his family, prepared during his lifetime.

EDCS-10000874.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Saturninus.
EDCS-10000874.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely an epitaph for Saturninus.

EDCS-10000875.translation: Prisca erected this for her brother Titus Mamaestrus.
EDCS-10000875.summary: A funerary epitaph erected by Prisca for her deceased brother, Titus Mamaestrus.

EDCS-10000876.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Helvio Felici Helvius Felix in memoriam patri sic fecit patri bene merenti\n
EDCS-10000876.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Helvius Felix made this in memory of his deserving father, Helvius Felix.
EDCS-10000876.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Helvius Felix for his father, also named Helvius Felix, honoring his deserts.

EDCS-10000879.text_edition: \n Rubria Ceuni filia Pola Saluti votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000879.translation: Pola, daughter of Rubria Ceunia, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Salvius.
EDCS-10000879.summary: A dedication by Pola, daughter of Rubria Ceunia, fulfilling a vow to Salvius.

EDCS-10000880.text_edition: \n Caius Veronius Aetor vivus fecit sibi et Veturiae Ae<t=I>ae uxori e<t=I> Ce<u>no <f=E>(ilio) e<t=I> Caio Iulio nepoti \n
EDCS-10000880.translation: Gaius Veronius Aetor made this while alive for himself and for his wife Veturia Aetia, and for his son Ceno, and for his grandson Gaius Julius.
EDCS-10000880.summary: A dedication made by Gaius Veronius Aetor for himself and his family members, including his wife, son, and grandson.

EDCS-10000881.text_edition: \n Lucius Sentius Patri Iano v(otum) s(olvit) libens merito\n
EDCS-10000881.translation: Lucius Sentius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Janus Pater.
EDCS-10000881.summary: A dedication by Lucius Sentius fulfilling a vow to the god Janus.

EDCS-10000882.text_edition: \n Triviis sacrum Caius Iulius Celer votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000882.translation: Sacred to the Triviae. Gaius Julius Celer willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10000882.summary: A dedication to the goddesses Triviae by Gaius Julius Celer, who fulfilled his vow.

EDCS-10000883.text_edition: \n Sl Cervolae Decius Herma bene merenti coniugi\n
EDCS-10000883.translation: For Cervola, Decius Herma [erected this] for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-10000883.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Decius Herma for his deserving wife, Cervola.

EDCS-10000887.summary: A fragmentary inscription, likely part of a funerary epitaph, mentioning a daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-10000892.translation: To the Great Mother of the Gods [---]
EDCS-10000892.summary: A dedication to the Magna Mater (Great Mother of the Gods), with a lacuna in the text.

EDCS-10000894.text_edition: \n Gaio Arruntio Gaius Arruntius tus patri \n
EDCS-10000894.translation: Gaius Arruntius [erected this] for his father Gaius Arruntius.
EDCS-10000894.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Gaius Arruntius for his father, also named Gaius Arruntius.

EDCS-10000895.text_edition: \n ------ nnos] XIIII hic situs est Lucius Cominius Luci? filius Marcus Magius Marci filius soceio sic suo fecerunt\n
EDCS-10000895.translation: Lucius Cominius, son of Lucius, and Marcus Magius, son of Marcus, made this for their fellow partner [aged] 14 years.
EDCS-10000895.summary: A funerary inscription recording the joint commission of a monument by two partners, likely from the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-10000896.text_edition: \n Sexto Cornelio Sexti liberto Acasto ex testamento in fronte pedes XII et in agro pedes XI\n
EDCS-10000896.translation: To Acastus, freedman of Sextus Cornelius, from his will: 12 feet in front and 11 feet in the field.
EDCS-10000896.summary: A funerary inscription specifying the dimensions of a plot of land bequeathed by will to a freedman named Acastus, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-10000897.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Fabius Secundus sibi suisque testamento fieri iussit hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-10000897.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Fabius Secundus ordered this monument to be made for himself and his family by his will; the heir shall not inherit it.
EDCS-10000897.summary: A funerary inscription stating that Lucius Fabius Secundus commissioned his own tomb via his will, explicitly excluding it from his heir's inheritance, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000898.text_edition: \n doloroso iussu pravi laboris qui semper fui cupidus meorum disparsa nomina restituere in unum Sextus Fuficius Clemens in agro pedes XX in fronte pedes XV\n
EDCS-10000898.translation: By sorrowful command of a difficult task, who was always eager to restore my scattered possessions to unity, Sextus Fuficius Clemens [donated] 20 feet in the field and 15 feet in front.
EDCS-10000898.summary: A funerary inscription where Sextus Fuficius Clemens records his purchase of land (20 feet by 15 feet) to unify disparate plots, possibly to establish his own burial site, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-10000899.text_edition: \n Caius Vettidius Hilarus Caius Plotius Blandus pro indiviso in fronte pedes XV in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-10000899.translation: Gaius Vettidius Hilarus and Gaius Plotius Blandus: jointly 15 feet in front and 20 feet in the field.
EDCS-10000899.summary: An inscription recording a property division or joint ownership of land, with dimensions of 15 feet by 20 feet, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-10000900.text_edition: \n Stieu Barnainu filius domo Beroea nnum XXX miles cohortis II Crestarum stipendio[rum ------\n
EDCS-10000900.translation: Stieu, son of Barnainus, from Beroea, 30 years old, a soldier of the 2nd Cohort of Cresta, for his [stipend]...
EDCS-10000900.summary: A partial inscription recording a soldier named Stieu, from Beroea, aged 30, serving in the 2nd Cohort of Cresta, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000901.text_edition: \n - Arruncius Sexti libertus VIvir us vivus fecit Aufidiae Secundae karissimae et Caio Aufidio Cai filio \n
EDCS-10000901.translation: Arruntius, freedman of Sextus, a member of the college of VIviri, made this in his lifetime for Aufidia Secunda, most beloved, and for Gaius Aufidius, son of Gaius.
EDCS-10000901.summary: A funerary inscription commissioned during his lifetime by Arruntius, a freedman and former official (VIvir), for his wife and son, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000902.text_edition: \n Apuleiae defunctae nnorum XVIII <F=P>asifae filiae in<f=E>elicissimae \n
EDCS-10000902.translation: To Apuleia, deceased, 18 years old, daughter of Asifas, most happy.
EDCS-10000902.summary: A funerary inscription for Apuleia, who died at age 18, the daughter of Asifas, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000903.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Daphnes Eucharistus conservae bene merenti\n
EDCS-10000903.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. To Daphnes, well-deserving fellow servant.
EDCS-10000903.summary: A funerary inscription honoring Daphnes, a "fellow servant" (conserva), likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10000907.translation: ...of a mother named Oria or Florida.
EDCS-10000907.summary: A fragment of an inscription mentioning a mother, possibly named Oria or Florida, its context and date are uncertain.

EDCS-10000910.text_edition: \n Marco Magio Galatae posuit Novia Severa uxor\n
EDCS-10000910.translation: Novia Severa, wife of Marcus Magius Galata, erected this.
EDCS-10000910.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Novia Severa for her husband, Marcus Magius Galata.

EDCS-10000911.text_edition: \n Lucio Caleio beneficiario Lucius Valerius Caridus decurio equitum de suo fecit\n
EDCS-10000911.translation: Lucius Valerius Caridus, decurion of the knights, made this at his own expense for Lucius Caleius, his subordinate.
EDCS-10000911.summary: This dedication was made by Lucius Valerius Caridus, a decurion, for his subordinate Lucius Caleius, likely commemorating military service.

EDCS-10000916.summary: This is a dedication to the god Vulcan, likely marking a sacred site or offering.

EDCS-10000917.text_edition: \n Luciae Aeliae Luci filiae Maximae matri et Tito Iullio Gai filio Proculo fratri Iulia Gai filia Procula viva fecit\n
EDCS-10000917.translation: Iulia Procula, daughter of Gaius, made this while alive for her mother Maxima, daughter of Lucius, and for her brother Proculus, son of Gaius.
EDCS-10000917.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Iulia Procula for her mother Maxima and brother Proculus.

EDCS-10000918.summary: This inscription simply records the name of Gaius Annius Statianus, possibly marking a tomb or property.

EDCS-10000919.text_edition: \n Caio Ennio Oepli filio Aetori Ennia Cai filia Ceuna viva fecit sibi et viro suo in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XIIII\n
EDCS-10000919.translation: Aetoria Ennia Ceuna, daughter of Caius, made this while alive for her son Caio Ennio, son of Aetor, and for her husband.
EDCS-10000919.summary: This funerary monument, specifying plot dimensions, was erected by Ennia Ceuna for her son Caio Ennio and her husband.

EDCS-10000920.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Atticus sibe sic et suis in fronte pedes X in agro pedes XV\n
EDCS-10000920.translation: Gaius Julius Atticus, for himself and his family, [acquired] 10 feet in front and 15 feet in the rear.
EDCS-10000920.summary: This inscription records the acquisition of land for a tomb by Gaius Julius Atticus for himself and his family, specifying plot dimensions.

EDCS-10000921.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Passienae Voltissae Publius Passienus \n
EDCS-10000921.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Publius Passienus [dedicated this] for Passiena Voltissa.
EDCS-10000921.summary: This funerary dedication was made by Publius Passienus for Passiena Voltissa.

EDCS-10000922.text_edition: \n Liburniae Luci libertae Callistae Liburnius Luci libertus Smaragdus coniugi\n
EDCS-10000922.translation: Smaragdus, freedman of Lucius, for his wife Callista, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-10000922.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Smaragdus for his wife Callista, noting their former status as freedmen.

EDCS-10000924.text_edition: \n Lucius I Luci filius Se matri fecit \n
EDCS-10000924.translation: Lucius made this for his mother Se, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-10000924.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucius for his mother, Se.

EDCS-10000926.text_edition: \n Iovi Optumo Maximo Cornelia Cai filia Tertia votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000926.translation: Cornelia Tertia, daughter of Caius, gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-10000926.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Cornelia Tertia.

EDCS-10000927.text_edition: \n Iovi Sabasio Iico Lucius Plotius Eperastus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000927.translation: Lucius Plotius Eperastus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Sabasius.
EDCS-10000927.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Sabasius by Lucius Plotius Eperastus.

EDCS-10000929.text_edition: \n Deanae Augustae sacrum in memoriam Carienae I matris Lucius Valerius Priscus cum coniuge sua Veratia Paulla fecerunt\n
EDCS-10000929.translation: Lucius Valerius Priscus and his wife Veratia Paulla made this sacred offering to the August Diana in memory of Carina, their mother.
EDCS-10000929.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to the August Diana, made by Lucius Valerius Priscus and Veratia Paulla in memory of Carina.

EDCS-10000930.text_edition: \n Ex decreto Publi Corneli Dollabellae legati pro praetore Augusti [] inter Begos et Ortoplinos\n
EDCS-10000930.translation: By decree of Publius Cornelius Dolabella, legate of the Emperor with praetorian authority, between the Begi and the Ortoplini.
EDCS-10000930.summary: This inscription records a decree made by Publius Cornelius Dolabella concerning the tribes or peoples known as the Begi and Ortoplini.

EDCS-10000931.text_edition: \n Soli Invicto Mithrae Faustus Titi Iuli Saturnini praefecti vehiculorum et conductoris publici portorii servus vilicus pro se et suis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000931.translation: Faustus, slave and steward of Titus Julius Saturninus, prefect of the transport service and public tax collector, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Sol Invictus Mithras for himself and his family.
EDCS-10000931.summary: This is a votive inscription to Sol Invictus Mithras by Faustus, a slave and steward.

EDCS-10000932.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Nonius Probus Licinio Leonino bene merenti\n
EDCS-10000932.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed: Publius Nonius Probus for Licinius Leoninus, who deserved well.
EDCS-10000932.summary: This is a tombstone inscription honoring Licinius Leoninus, dedicated by Publius Nonius Probus.

EDCS-10000934.text_edition: \n posuit simae pientissimae ater fecit me soroXVII\n
EDCS-10000934.translation: He erected me, his most dutiful wife, her father made me, Sima, [---] XVII.
EDCS-10000934.summary: This is a funerary inscription likely commemorating a woman named Sima, erected by her father.

EDCS-10000938.text_edition: \n Dianae Augustae sacrum Euhemer(us) servus Augusti votum libens solvit merito\n
EDCS-10000938.translation: Euhemerus, slave of Augustus, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the August Diana.
EDCS-10000938.summary: This is a votive inscription to the August Diana by Euhemerus, a slave of the emperor.

EDCS-10000939.text_edition: \n tiae Luci filiae Secundae ius Severus aedilis IIvir matri posuit\n
EDCS-10000939.translation: Severus, aedile and duovir, erected this for his mother, Lucia Secunda, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-10000939.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Severus for his mother, Lucia Secunda.

EDCS-10000940.text_edition: \n Caius Volumnius Cai filius miles nnnorum stipendiorum hic situs est\n
EDCS-10000940.translation: Gaius Volumnius, son of Gaius, a soldier with [XX] years of service, is buried here.
EDCS-10000940.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Gaius Volumnius, a soldier who served for twenty years.

EDCS-10000941.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cervola vixit annis LV fecit Vitalis coniugi bene merenti et sibi\n
EDCS-10000941.translation: To the departed spirits. Cervola lived 55 years. Vitalis made this for his deserving wife and for himself.
EDCS-10000941.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cervola, set up by her husband Vitalis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000942.text_edition: \n xsa sic Frontonis filia quae veixit sic annos XXVIII heic sic sita est\n
EDCS-10000942.translation: Thus, the daughter of Frontonis, who lived 28 years, is placed here.
EDCS-10000942.summary: A funerary epitaph for the daughter of Frontonis, recording her age at death, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000943.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vernacula vixit annis XL fecit Eurialis sic coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-10000943.translation: To the departed spirits. Vernacula lived 40 years. Eurialis made this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-10000943.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vernacula, set up by her husband Eurialis, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000944.text_edition: \n Vestina Apro coniugi bene merenti qui vixit annis XLI \n
EDCS-10000944.translation: Vestina for Apro, her deserving husband, who lived 41 years.
EDCS-10000944.summary: A funerary epitaph for Apro, set up by his wife Vestina, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000951.text_edition: \n Marcus Antonius Sabatina Pullus domu Ma \n
EDCS-10000951.translation: Marcus Antonius Sabatina Pullus from Ma[...]
EDCS-10000951.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription mentioning Marcus Antonius Sabatina Pullus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000952.translation: To Quintus Pomponius Rufus, legate of the Emperors.
EDCS-10000952.summary: An incomplete inscription honoring Quintus Pomponius Rufus, a legate, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000953.text_edition: \n cus Cleni filius us Cleni filius pro memoria faciundum curaverunt\n
EDCS-10000953.translation: [...] son of Clenus, [...] son of Clenus, arranged for this to be made for their memory.
EDCS-10000953.summary: An incomplete inscription recording the commissioning of a monument for memory by sons named Clenus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10000954.text_edition: \n Marti sacrum Turus Longini filius decurio et sacerdotalis pro suis et cognatione Nantania de suo votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10000954.translation: Sacred to Mars. Turus, son of Longinus, decurion and priest, fulfilled his vow for himself and the Nantania clan with good will and merit.
EDCS-10000954.summary: A votive inscription to Mars by Turus, a local official, likely from the Roman provincial period.

EDCS-10000957.text_edition: \n Impperatoribus Caessaribus Caio Vibio Gallo et Caio Vibio Volusiano Piis Felicibus Auggustis \n
EDCS-10000957.translation: For the Emperors Caesar Gaius Vibius Gallus and Gaius Vibius Volusianus, Pious, Fortunate, Augusti.
EDCS-10000957.summary: An inscription dedicating something to the co-emperors Trebonianus Gallus and Volusianus, dated 251-253 AD.

EDCS-10001021.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Caius Iulius Verus Maximinus Pius Felix pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II imperator III consul pater patriae et Caius Iulius Verus Maximus nobilissimus Caesar A I\n
EDCS-10001021.translation: For the Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, Pious, Fortunate, Pontifex Maximus, holding tribunician power for the second time, Imperator for the third time, consul, father of the country, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-10001021.summary: An inscription dedicating something to Emperor Maximinus Thrax and his son Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximus, dated between 236 and 238 AD.

EDCS-10001035.text_edition: \n Imperatori domino nostro Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Augusto\n
EDCS-10001035.translation: To our Lord the Emperor Julius Philippus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-10001035.summary: This inscription honors Emperor Philip the Arab, likely dating to his reign (244-249 AD).

EDCS-10001042.text_edition: \n Imppperatoribus dddominis nnnostris Marco Aurelio Caro Augusto et Marco Aurelio Carino et Marco Aurelio Numeriano nobilissimis Caesaribus perpetuis et Numeriano Caesari \n
EDCS-10001042.translation: To our Emperors, our Lords Marcus Aurelius Carus Augustus and Marcus Aurelius Carinus and Marcus Aurelius Numerian, the most noble Caesars, perpetual, and to Numerianus Caesar.
EDCS-10001042.summary: This is a dedication to the emperors Carus, Carinus, and Numerian, dating to their joint rule between 283 and 284 AD.

EDCS-10100455.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Aelius Celsinus decurio libens posuit COR ALB\n
EDCS-10100455.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Titus Aelius Celsinus, a decurion, willingly set this up.
EDCS-10100455.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Titus Aelius Celsinus, a local official, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-10100488.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Maximo defuncto annorum XL Annia filio pientissimo posuit\n
EDCS-10100488.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Annia set this up for her son Maximo, who died at the age of 40, a most dutiful son.
EDCS-10100488.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maximo, erected by his mother Annia.

EDCS-10100489.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Tessio Marcellino p q(ui) vixit annos XXII Caio Tessio Paterno coiov vixit nnos VIII Ulpia Marci f(ilia) Bidna filio t marito pientissimis\n
EDCS-10100489.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caio Tessio Marcellino, who lived 22 years; Caio Tessio Paterno, who lived 8 years; Ulpia Bidna, daughter of Marcius, for her most dutiful son and husband.
EDCS-10100489.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of Caio Tessio Marcellino and Caio Tessio Paterno, erected by Ulpia Bidna.

EDCS-10100492.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Celeo Cresiano ANISVI VERMABI DANO VARETO pientissimis\n
EDCS-10100492.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Celeus Cresianus, a gift.
EDCS-10100492.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Celeus Cresianus, indicating a posthumous dedication or a gift related to burial.

EDCS-10100493.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Aurelius Silvanus decurio municipii vixit annos LXX hic situs est Titus Aurelius Proculus patri pientissimo posuit\n
EDCS-10100493.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Aurelius Silvanus, decurion of the municipality, lived 70 years, is buried here. Titus Aurelius Proculus set this up for his most dutiful father.
EDCS-10100493.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titus Aurelius Silvanus, a local official, erected by his son Titus Aurelius Proculus.

EDCS-10100494.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Silviana vixit annos XLV Gaia filia suprascriptae viixit sic annos III Flavius Dalmatius defunctae c<o=U>niugi pientissimae et dulcissimae filiae merentibus posuit \n
EDCS-10100494.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Silviana lived 45 years; Gaia, the daughter, lived 3 years. Flavius Dalmatius set this up for his most dutiful and dearest wife and daughter.
EDCS-10100494.summary: This funerary epitaph records the deaths of Aurelia Silviana and her young daughter Gaia, erected by Flavius Dalmatius.

EDCS-10100495.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo VOIIDA pro valetudine Augusti nostri vovit\n
EDCS-10100495.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. VOIIDA vowed for the health of our Augustus.
EDCS-10100495.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter for the health of the emperor, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10100496.text_edition: \n AHO Aurelius Valerius arcarius fisci Dalmatiae simul cum <G=C>audentia ex voto \n
EDCS-10100496.translation: Aurelius Valerius, a cashier of the treasury of Dalmatia, together with Gaudentia, fulfilled a vow.
EDCS-10100496.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Aurelius Valerius, an official of the provincial treasury, likely from the later Roman Empire.

EDCS-10100501.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpio Nonntioni annorum XL Campanius Marcianus vivus sibi et Iulia Marciana parentes pii IIVET \n
EDCS-10100501.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Ulpius Nonntionus, aged 40. His parents, Campanius Marcianus and Iulia Marciana, set this up while alive.
EDCS-10100501.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Ulpius Nonntionus, aged 40, and was set up by his parents.

EDCS-10100502.text_edition: \n Dis Inferis Manibus Restituto bene merito Marcella coniux memoriam fecit vixsit! annos LV\n
EDCS-10100502.translation: To the gods of the Underworld. For Restitutus, well-deserving. His wife Marcella made this memorial. He lived 55 years.
EDCS-10100502.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Restitutus, aged 55, and was set up by his wife Marcella.

EDCS-10100504.translation: Sacred to the health of Maecilia Hilaria.
EDCS-10100504.summary: This dedication was made for the well-being of Maecilia Hilaria.

EDCS-10100505.text_edition: \n Serapi et Isidi Cnaeus Pompeius Politianus tribunus cohortis II miliariae Dalmatarum \n
EDCS-10100505.translation: To Serapis and Isis. Cnaeus Pompeius Politianus, tribune of the 2nd cohort of Dalmatians (miliaria), [set this up].
EDCS-10100505.summary: This dedication to Serapis and Isis was made by Cnaeus Pompeius Politianus, a military tribune.

EDCS-10100507.text_edition: \n Invicto Deo Optimo Grudius Aetor Super libentes posuerunt\n
EDCS-10100507.translation: To the Invincible and Supreme God, Grudius Aetor Super, gladly set this up.
EDCS-10100507.summary: This dedication to the god Grudius Aetor Super was made willingly by the dedicant.

EDCS-10100508.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus IIORNINI AFIAI annorum XXVI hic siti sunt CIS IN AINNS INCIV posuit\n
EDCS-10100508.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Iiorninus Afius, aged 26, buried here. ... Cis placed this.
EDCS-10100508.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iiorninus Afius, aged 26, and was set up by someone named Cis.

EDCS-10100509.text_edition: \n Iovi Fulguratori sacrum Titus Aurelius Marcus veteranus cum suis libens posuit\n
EDCS-10100509.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Fulgurator. Titus Aurelius Marcus, a veteran, gladly set this up with his family.
EDCS-10100509.summary: This dedication to Jupiter Fulgurator was made by Titus Aurelius Marcus, a veteran, and his family.

EDCS-10100510.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Quintus Valerius Satianus libens posuit\n
EDCS-10100510.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Quintus Valerius Satianus gladly set this up.
EDCS-10100510.summary: This dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus was made by Quintus Valerius Satianus.

EDCS-10100511.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Dussona vixit annos LXXX Maetius compari et sibi vivus et Victorianus filius sibi vivi titulum ponendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-10100511.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelia Dussona lived 80 years. Maetius, for his partner, and for himself while alive, and his son Victorinus, for himself while alive, saw to the setting up of this monument.
EDCS-10100511.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Aurelia Dussona, aged 80, and was set up by her partner Maetius and son Victorinus, who prepared it during their own lifetimes.

EDCS-10100512.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerius is vivus CVOAurelio IAL defuncto annorum X posuit\n
EDCS-10100512.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Valerius is [set this up] for Aurelius Ial, deceased, aged 10, while alive.
EDCS-10100512.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Aurelius Ial, aged 10, and was set up by Valerius while he was alive.

EDCS-10100514.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iunoni Minervae Aelius Aprilis beneficiarius consularis v(otum)? s(olvit) libens merito\n
EDCS-10100514.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, and Juno, Minerva, Aelius Aprilis, a consular beneficiary, willingly fulfilled his vow, as deserved.
EDCS-10100514.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Aelius Aprilis to Jupiter, Juno, and Minerva.

EDCS-10100516.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Parthicus Britannicus G consul IIIII tribunicia potestate c curante C\n
EDCS-10100516.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, conqueror of Parthia and Britain, consul for the fifth time, with tribunician power, under the supervision of C.
EDCS-10100516.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely refers to Emperor Trajan and his titles, possibly commemorating a public work.

EDCS-10100517.text_edition: \n Aesculapio et Hygiae Auggustis ssacrum Caius Iulius Herculanus beneficiarius consularis legionis I Italicae Moesiae inferioris libens posuit\n
EDCS-10100517.translation: Sacred to Aesculapius and the August Hygieia. Caius Iulius Herculanus, a consular beneficiary of the I Italica legion in Lower Moesia, willingly dedicated this.
EDCS-10100517.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a sacred object by Caius Iulius Herculanus to Aesculapius and Hygieia.

EDCS-10100518.text_edition: \n Dianae Augustae sacrum Cocceia Maxima votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10100518.translation: Sacred to the August Diana. Cocceia Maxima willingly fulfilled her vow, as deserved.
EDCS-10100518.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Cocceia Maxima to the goddess Diana.

EDCS-10100519.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Aemilius Ingenuus miles legionis XI Claudiae beneficiarius consularis\n
EDCS-10100519.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Caius Aemilius Ingenuus, a soldier of the XI Claudia legion and a consular beneficiary.
EDCS-10100519.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by Caius Aemilius Ingenuus, a soldier and consular beneficiary.

EDCS-10100520.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, ECM Maximus.
EDCS-10100520.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter by ECM Maximus.

EDCS-10100525.text_edition: \n Marti Augusto Iulius Longinus beneficiarius consularis ex legione V Macedonica votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10100525.translation: To Mars Augustus, Iulius Longinus, a consular beneficiary from the V Macedonica legion, willingly fulfilled his vow, as deserved.
EDCS-10100525.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Iulius Longinus to Mars Augustus.

EDCS-10100526.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum Claudius Maximus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10100526.translation: Sacred to Silvanus. Claudius Maximus willingly fulfilled his vow, as deserved.
EDCS-10100526.summary: This inscription records a votive offering by Claudius Maximus to Silvanus.

EDCS-10100529.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Aurelius Baebianus.
EDCS-10100529.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Aurelius Baebianus.

EDCS-10100530.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Licinius defunctus nnorum LXXII Aurelia Thana patrono de se bene meri[to ------\n
EDCS-10100530.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Licinius, who died at seventy-two years of age, [dedicated this] to Aurelia Thana, his patroness, for her well-deserved kindness towards him.
EDCS-10100530.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Aurelius Licinius to his patroness Aurelia Thana.

EDCS-10100531.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudiae Catiae Claudia Annia filiae dulcissimae posuit\n
EDCS-10100531.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudia Annia set this up for her most beloved daughter, Claudia Catia.
EDCS-10100531.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Claudia Annia for her daughter, Claudia Catia.

EDCS-10100533.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Sergio Iuliano |(centurioni II militavit annos XXV ------] II Heonia Vera coniugi pientissimo posuit\n
EDCS-10100533.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Heonia Vera set this up for her most devoted husband, Gaius Sergius Iulianus, a centurion who served for 25 years.
EDCS-10100533.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Sergius Iulianus, a centurion, set up by his wife, Heonia Vera.

EDCS-10100537.text_edition: \n avii equitis Romani clarissimi viri matris clarissimae feminae Claudius Ca\n
EDCS-10100537.translation: Of Avii, a most distinguished Roman knight, mother of a most distinguished woman, Claudius Ca[...]
EDCS-10100537.summary: A fragment of an inscription likely commemorating a distinguished Roman family, mentioning Avii and Claudius Ca[...].

EDCS-10100538.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Bae, who has departed.
EDCS-10100538.summary: A very brief funerary epitaph for a deceased individual named Bae.

EDCS-10100540.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus IRI municipes municipii IIviratu functo vixit annos LXV Aurelia avi Aurelius iv t pientissimo o s XIII posuerunt\n
EDCS-10100540.translation: To the spirits of the departed. The councillors of the municipality, having performed the office of duovir, lived for 65 years. Aurelia Avi [and] Aurelius iv t [set this up] for the most devoted o s XIII.
EDCS-10100540.summary: A funerary epitaph for individuals who served as duovirs in a municipality, erected by Aurelia Avi and Aurelius iv t.

EDCS-10100546.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Verecundus and [...]
EDCS-10100546.summary: The beginning of a funerary inscription for Verecundus and at least one other person.

EDCS-10100547.translation: To the spirits of the departed. CLAI IVI [...]
EDCS-10100547.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription, possibly for individuals named CLAI and IVI.

EDCS-10100548.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Silvanus decurio M(--- municipii Flavi \n
EDCS-10100548.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Flavius Silvanus, decurion of the municipality of Flavi [...]
EDCS-10100548.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Flavius Silvanus, a decurion in the Flavi municipality.

EDCS-10100549.text_edition: \n Valeriano et Marcellinae Luci filiis vivis posuit\n
EDCS-10100549.translation: Lucius set this up for Valerianus and Marcellina, his living children.
EDCS-10100549.summary: An inscription recording that Lucius dedicated something for his living children, Valerianus and Marcellina.

EDCS-10100555.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Clemens IIvir votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-10100555.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Publius Aelius Clemens willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-10100555.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, recording the fulfillment of a vow by Publius Aelius Clemens, who served as duovir.

EDCS-10100556.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Ulpius Apollo \n
EDCS-10100556.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Marcus Ulpius Apollo [dedicated this].
EDCS-10100556.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter by Marcus Ulpius Apollo, likely on a shrine or altar.

EDCS-10100563.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Caio Valerio Diocletiano Pio Felici Invicto Augusto res publica Aquarum S\n
EDCS-10100563.translation: The state of Aquae Suli [dedicated this] to the Emperor Caesar Gaius Valerius Diocletian, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible Augustus.
EDCS-10100563.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the community of Aquae Suli to Emperor Diocletian, likely on a public building or monument.

EDCS-10100565.text_edition: \n Ulpiae Titi filiae Proculae annorum XX Titus Flavius Valens Varronis filius princeps Desitiatium et Aelia Iusta Scenobarbi filia \n
EDCS-10100565.translation: To Ulpia Procula, aged 20, daughter of Titus; Titus Flavius Valens, son of Varro, chief of the Desitiates, and Aelia Iusta, daughter of Scenobarbus, [dedicated this].
EDCS-10100565.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpia Procula, daughter of Titus, and records the dedication by Titus Flavius Valens and Aelia Iusta, likely her parents or patrons.

EDCS-10100566.text_edition: \n Aelia Marcella annorum XX Publius Aeliusmael\n
EDCS-10100566.translation: To Aelia Marcella, aged 20; Publius Aelius Mael[...]
EDCS-10100566.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription likely for Aelia Marcella, aged 20, with Publius Aelius Mael... as the dedicator or deceased.

EDCS-10100567.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication to an individual named Aelius Plantentus.

EDCS-10100568.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Aurelius Acutus [dedicated this].
EDCS-10100568.summary: This is a dedication to the underworld deities (Dis Manibus) by Aurelius Acutus, likely on a tomb or memorial.

EDCS-10100569.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus defuncto --- Bonoso puero annorum XV Iustinus pater \n
EDCS-10100569.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed; to the deceased Bonosus, a boy aged 15; his father Iustinus [dedicated this].
EDCS-10100569.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Iustinus dedicates a monument to his deceased son, Bonosus, who died at age 15.

EDCS-10100571.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Clario annorum posuerunt Ursus et Dassius patri hic situs est\n
EDCS-10100571.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed; to Clarius, aged [...]; Ursus and Dassius erected [this] to their father; he is buried here.
EDCS-10100571.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a monument by Ursus and Dassius to their father, Clarius.

EDCS-10100574.text_edition: \n Batoni Liccai filio Teuta Vietis Sceno sic Batonis filius maxime natus Scenocalo Batonis filio Scaevae Batonis filio Calloni Batonis filio Prorado Batonis filio Scenus Batonis filius minime natus ex eis sibi et suis de sua pecunia fieri iussit\n
EDCS-10100574.translation: To Batonis, son of Liccai, Teuta, daughter of Vietis Sceno; thus to Batonis, son of Scenocalo; to Batonis, son of Scaeva; to Batonis, son of Callo; to Batonis, son of Proradus; Scenus, son of Batonis, the youngest born of them, ordered this to be made for himself and his kin from his own funds.
EDCS-10100574.summary: This inscription records the commissioning of a monument by Scenus, son of Batonis, for himself, his family, and other relatives named Batonis, indicating a significant family lineage.

EDCS-10100577.text_edition: \n Aurelia Nava annorum XLV Aurelius ginianus ] Aurelia pa mater eius viva sibi fecit et nepoti suo Marcellino defuncto \n
EDCS-10100577.translation: Aurelia Nava, aged 45, and Aurelius Ginianus [dedicated this]. Aurelia [Nava], his mother, made this for herself while alive, and for her deceased grandson Marcellinus.
EDCS-10100577.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Nava, aged 45, and her deceased grandson Marcellinus, commissioned by Aurelia Nava herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-10100578.summary: This inscription, likely a tombstone, records a brief eulogy for a servant.

EDCS-10100586.text_edition: \n Publi filius et infelic dolore et ius et con quod pater optabat fieri sibi quod pia mater infelix natis fecit uterque parentum\n
EDCS-10100586.translation: Publius, his son, and the unhappy ones, and the right and the bond, which the father wished to be made for himself, which the unhappy mother made for her born children; both parents.
EDCS-10100586.summary: This inscription is a complex and somewhat fragmented funerary text, likely commemorating parents and children with a focus on familial bonds and perhaps unfulfilled desires.

EDCS-10100594.text_edition: \n Lucio puero qui vixit nnos XX Lupus et Cresa filio pientissimo et sibi vivi posuerunt\n
EDCS-10100594.translation: To Lucius, a boy who lived 20 years, Lupus and Cresa, for their most devoted son and for themselves while alive, placed this.
EDCS-10100594.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lupus and Cresa for their son Lucius, who died at the age of 20.

EDCS-10100600.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicated to an 'Invictus' deity, possibly Sol Invictus or Mithras.

EDCS-10100605.text_edition: \n Minervae sacrum ex voto Lucius Publicius Telesphorus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10100605.translation: Sacred to Minerva, from a vow. Lucius Publicius Telesphorus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100605.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Lucius Publicius Telesphorus to the goddess Minerva.

EDCS-10100606.translation: Sextus Julius Gracilis fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100606.summary: This inscription records Sextus Julius Gracilis fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-10100608.text_edition: \n Caio Minicio Luci filio Papiria Fundano VIIviro epulonum tribuno legionis VII fulminatae quaestori tribuno plebis praetori legato legionis XV Apollinaris PIAE UR \n
EDCS-10100608.translation: To Gaius Minicius, son of Lucius, son of Papirius Fundanus, member of the college of seven epulones, tribune of the VII Legion 'Thunderbolt', quaestor, tribune of the plebs, praetor, legate of the XV Apollinaris Legion, [and to his] pious wife.
EDCS-10100608.summary: This is a dedication to Gaius Minicius, detailing his extensive career in Roman magistracies and military commands.

EDCS-10100609.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tertullae infelicessimae sic defunctae annorum XII plus minus\n
EDCS-10100609.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Tertulla, most unhappy, who died thus at the age of twelve years, more or less.
EDCS-10100609.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a young girl named Tertulla, who died at approximately twelve years of age.

EDCS-10100612.text_edition: \n Silvano Cor Aelius Maximus Proculini libens posuit\n
EDCS-10100612.translation: To Silvanus. C. Aelius Maximus, son of Proculinus, willingly placed this.
EDCS-10100612.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Silvanus by C. Aelius Maximus.

EDCS-10100615.text_edition: \n Invicto aras Sisimbrius decurionum decreto libens posuit\n
EDCS-10100615.translation: To the Unconquered. Sisimbrius, by decree of the decurions, willingly erected the altars.
EDCS-10100615.summary: This inscription records the erection of altars to an 'Invictus' deity by Sisimbrius, acting on the decree of local decurions.

EDCS-10100619.text_edition: \n Nymphis et Silvano sacrum Surus Pirami libens posuit\n
EDCS-10100619.translation: A sacred offering to the Nymphs and Silvanus, willingly erected by Surus, son of Piramus.
EDCS-10100619.summary: This is a dedication to the Nymphs and Silvanus by Surus, son of Piramus, likely erected in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10100620.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri sacrum Dasius Gemelli filius libens merito\n
EDCS-10100620.translation: A sacred offering to Silvanus the Forest God, willingly and deservedly erected by Dasius, son of Gemellus.
EDCS-10100620.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Silvanus Silvestris (the Forest God) by Dasius, son of Gemellus, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10100622.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci Aelius Anteros beneficiarius consularis legionis XIIII geminae votum libens solvit Imperatore domino nostro Gallieno IIII et Volusiano consulibus Kalendis Aprilibus\n
EDCS-10100622.translation: Aelius Anteros, aide to a consular legate of the Legio XIIII Gemina, willingly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius of the place, on the Kalends of April, during the fourth consulship of our Emperor Lord Gallienus and Volusianus.
EDCS-10100622.summary: This is a military dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus and the Genius loci by Aelius Anteros, a serving soldier, dating to April 1st, 261 CE.

EDCS-10100624.text_edition: \n et Genio loci Titus Coninius Ursus |(centurio) cohortis VIII voluntariorum gratias agens cum suis votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-10100624.translation: And to the Genius of the place, Titus Coninius Ursus, centurion of the 8th cohort of volunteers, giving thanks with his family, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100624.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled to the Genius loci by Titus Coninius Ursus, a centurion, and his family, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10100625.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto sacrum Publius Aelius Celsinus votum libens solvit Imperatore domino nostro Gordiano Augusto II et Pompeiano consulibus\n
EDCS-10100625.translation: A sacred offering to Hercules Augustus, Publius Aelius Celsinus willingly fulfilled his vow, during the consulship of our Emperor Lord Gordianus Augustus II and Pompeianus.
EDCS-10100625.summary: This is a dedication to Hercules Augustus by Publius Aelius Celsinus, dating to the consulship of Gordian III and Pompeianus, which occurred in 241 CE.

EDCS-10100630.text_edition: \n e Victorinae et Secundparentes pientissimi bene merentibus posuerunt\n
EDCS-10100630.translation: Her parents, most devoted, erected this for Victorina and Secundus, who were most deserving.
EDCS-10100630.summary: This inscription is a memorial or dedication erected by parents for their deceased children, Victorina and Secundus.

EDCS-10100635.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri sacrum Cassius Aurelius CAEMI COSIRE POPI\n
EDCS-10100635.translation: A sacred offering to Silvanus the Forest God; Cassius Aurelius [erected this].
EDCS-10100635.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Silvanus Silvestris by Cassius Aurelius, with some illegible words following.

EDCS-10100636.text_edition: \n Aurelia Cassia mulier cum ais coniugi et filiae defunct\n
EDCS-10100636.translation: Aurelia Cassia, a woman, [erected this] for her husband and daughter who died.
EDCS-10100636.summary: This inscription is a dedication or memorial for a deceased husband and daughter by Aurelia Cassia.

EDCS-10100638.text_edition: \n Aelius Maximus Karus Epatinoni coniugi et Aelius Varro filius SINDOC\n
EDCS-10100638.translation: Aelius Maximus Karus [erected this] for his wife Epatinonis and his son Aelius Varro.
EDCS-10100638.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication or memorial for a wife named Epatinonis and a son named Aelius Varro, set up by Aelius Maximus Karus.

EDCS-10100639.translation: To Silvanus, Titus Aurelius Proculus [erected this].
EDCS-10100639.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus by Titus Aurelius Proculus.

EDCS-10100640.text_edition: \n mius Ai usXXI o suis f invaratr suo bene merenti pe apsuit ruti suae socrui pientitissimae\n
EDCS-10100640.translation: To her most pious mother-in-law, Rutilia, with a good heart, her daughter-in-law, his mother, made this.
EDCS-10100640.summary: This funerary inscription honors a mother-in-law, erected by her daughter-in-law, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-10100643.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Maximus Diti annorum LXX p v fecit hic situs est\n
EDCS-10100643.translation: To the Divine Shades. Maximus, aged 70, made this; he is buried here.
EDCS-10100643.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Maximus, who lived to be 70, dedicated to the gods of the underworld.

EDCS-10100645.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Flavia Procula solvit votum libens\n
EDCS-10100645.translation: Flavia Procula willingly paid her vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-10100645.summary: This dedicatory inscription records Flavia Procula fulfilling a vow to Jupiter, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-10100646.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Metilia Domitia ex voto\n
EDCS-10100646.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, from a vow, by Metilia Domitia.
EDCS-10100646.summary: This dedicatory inscription records Metilia Domitia fulfilling a vow to Jupiter, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-10100647.summary: This very brief inscription likely records the age of Turus Sarius, possibly on a tombstone.

EDCS-10100649.summary: This brief inscription indicates that Martinus erected something, possibly a monument or a marker.

EDCS-10100650.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Privato servo hic situs est\n
EDCS-10100650.translation: To the Divine Shades. Privato, a slave, is buried here.
EDCS-10100650.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a slave named Privato, dedicated to the gods of the underworld.

EDCS-10100651.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quintianus annorum LXV posuit titulum Amata SIII suo c V Valen\n
EDCS-10100651.translation: Quintianus, aged 65, set up this monument; Amata SIII, his wife, and Valen [erected it/are remembered here].
EDCS-10100651.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Quintianus at age 65, with his wife Amata and Valen mentioned, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-10100652.text_edition: \n ritus Sili servos sic hic situs est annorum IIII \n
EDCS-10100652.translation: Silius, a slave, is buried here, aged 4.
EDCS-10100652.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a young slave named Silius, aged 4.

EDCS-10100653.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Veneto Diti annorum XXV\n
EDCS-10100653.translation: To the Divine Shades. Veneto, aged 25.
EDCS-10100653.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Veneto at age 25, dedicated to the gods of the underworld.

EDCS-10100655.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus ni annorum XXV coniux posuit\n
EDCS-10100655.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. His wife placed [this] for him, aged 25.
EDCS-10100655.summary: A funerary inscription for a man who died at age 25, set up by his wife.

EDCS-10100657.text_edition: \n Liberae Iulia Maximina votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10100657.translation: Liberae. Iulia Maximina fulfilled her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-10100657.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Libera, set up by Iulia Maximina.

EDCS-10100659.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Maximus.
EDCS-10100659.summary: A funerary inscription for Iulius Maximus.

EDCS-10100660.summary: A fragmentary inscription with names that cannot be fully reconstructed.

EDCS-10100661.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus ENDI I Secunannorum LXX VNE filius OM matri pientissimae posuit hic sita est\n
EDCS-10100661.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. [...] Secundus for his most devoted mother, who lived 70 years, placed [this]; she is buried here.
EDCS-10100661.summary: A funerary inscription for a mother who lived 70 years, dedicated by her son Secundus.

EDCS-10100663.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto Mercurius argenti actor votum libens posuit\n
EDCS-10100663.translation: To Silvanus Augustus. Mercurius, the silver agent, willingly set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-10100663.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Augustus by Mercurius, an agent managing silver.

EDCS-10100664.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Cesonius.

EDCS-10100672.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto Titus Aurelius Codalianus libens posuit\n
EDCS-10100672.translation: To Hercules Augustus. Titus Aurelius Codalianus willingly set this up.
EDCS-10100672.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Hercules Augustus by Titus Aurelius Codalianus.

EDCS-10100673.text_edition: \n Iovi Cohortali sacrum Maximus ex voto libens posuit pro suis omnibus\n
EDCS-10100673.translation: Sacred to Iuppiter Cohortalis. Maximus set this up willingly in fulfillment of a vow for all his family.
EDCS-10100673.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Iuppiter Cohortalis by Maximus for his entire family.

EDCS-10100674.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Vurus vilicus HA Secandoni parinti sic qui vixit annos LXXX et Sicundoni coniugi quae vixit annos XX et si<b=R>i vivus memoriam posuit \n
EDCS-10100674.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Vurus, the foreman, set up [this] in memory of his father Secundonius, who lived 80 years, and of his wife Sicundonia, who lived 20 years.
EDCS-10100674.summary: A funerary inscription for Vurus's father Secundonius (80 years old) and wife Sicundonia (20 years old), set up by Vurus.

EDCS-10100675.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum G Suricino et Septimiae Candidae avis qui vixerunt annos LXXV et illa annos LX et Aurelio Maximo decurioni municipii qui vixit annos LV et Aureliae Maditae quae vixit annos XXXX parentibus et Aureliae Venuconi sorori quae vixit annos XVI Aurelius Lavius decurio municipi et sibi vivus posuit\n
EDCS-10100675.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Lavius, decurion of the municipality, and his wife Aurelia Venuco, for Aurelius Maximus, decurion of the municipality, who lived 55 years, and Aurelia Madita, who lived 40 years, his parents, and for his sister Aurelia Venuco, who lived 16 years, and for his grandparents G. Suricino, who lived 75 years, and Septimia Candida, who lived 60 years, he set this up for himself while alive.
EDCS-10100675.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Aurelius Lavius for his parents, sister, and grandparents, including a decurion and his wife, and himself.

EDCS-10100678.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription referring to a Christian pontifex under Christ's authority, likely dating from the early Christian period.

EDCS-10100683.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus <s=C>(acrum) Aemilii Antonius et Antoninus Aemilio Calvioni defuncto Salonis annorum? LXI \n
EDCS-10100683.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Aemilius Antonius and Antoninus for Aemilius Calvionus, deceased, from Salona, aged 61 years.
EDCS-10100683.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Aemilius Antonius and Antoninus for Aemilius Calvionus of Salona, who died at 61.

EDCS-10100684.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Tito Aurelio Ingenuo qui vixit annos L et Pantoni coniugi eius quae vixit annos XXXV Titus Aurelius Maximus filius parentibus pro pietate\n
EDCS-10100684.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Aurelius Maximus, the son, out of filial duty, set this up for his parents: Titus Aurelius Ingenuus, who lived 50 years, and Pantonia, his wife, who lived 35 years.
EDCS-10100684.summary: This is a funerary epitaph erected by a son for his parents, Titus Aurelius Ingenuus and his wife Pantonia.

EDCS-10100686.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aureliae Titullae Argurianae quae vixit annos LX Aurelia Titulla Cambria amitae pro parte\n
EDCS-10100686.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Titulla Cambria, on behalf of her aunt Aurelia Titulla Arguriana, who lived 60 years, for her share.
EDCS-10100686.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Aurelia Titulla Cambria for her aunt Aurelia Titulla Arguriana.

EDCS-10100688.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caesiae Asteridi quae vixit annos XXXV Caius Egnatius Montanus coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10100688.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Egnatius Montanus set this up for Caesius Asteridus, his wife, who deserved well, who lived 35 years.
EDCS-10100688.summary: This is a funerary epitaph erected by Caius Egnatius Montanus for his deserving wife, Caesius Asteridus.

EDCS-10100689.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Publio Gavieno Candidiano qui vixit annos XLV et Egnatiae Soteridi coniugi vivae Gavieni Valerianus et Egnatius et Candida filii parentibus posuerunt\n
EDCS-10100689.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Gavienus Valerianus, Egnatius, and Candida, the children, set this up for their parents: Publius Gavienus Candidianus, who lived 45 years, and Egnatia Soteris, his wife, while alive.
EDCS-10100689.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by three children for their father, Publius Gavienus Candidianus, and their mother, Egnatia Soteris.

EDCS-10100690.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Paconio Barbarioni qui vixit annos XXXL etbuni coniugi eius vivae Valerius Faustus parentibus pientissimis posuit\n
EDCS-10100690.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Valerius Faustus set this up for his most dutiful parents: Lucius Paconius Barbarionus, who lived 30 years, and his wife [etbuni?], while alive.
EDCS-10100690.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Valerius Faustus for his parents, Lucius Paconius Barbarionus and his wife.

EDCS-10100691.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marco Plaetorio Ursino qui vixit annos LV Lucius Paconius Barbarioni et Ursina bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-10100691.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Paconius Barbarionus and Ursina set this up for Marcus Plaetorius Ursus, who lived 55 years, well-deserving.
EDCS-10100691.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Lucius Paconius Barbarionus and Ursina for Marcus Plaetorius Ursus.

EDCS-10100692.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iarito Arvi sibi posuit et Fuscae Anae coniugi suae dulcissimae quae vixit annos L\n
EDCS-10100692.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Departed. Iaritus Arvus set this up for himself and for his most sweet wife, Fusca Ana, who lived 50 years.
EDCS-10100692.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Iaritus Arvus for himself and his deceased wife, Fusca Ana.

EDCS-10100693.summary: This is a funerary inscription fragment, likely recording the names of the deceased.

EDCS-10100695.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quintus Aelius Faustus et \n
EDCS-10100695.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Aelius Faustus and...
EDCS-10100695.summary: This is the beginning of a funerary inscription, with the dedicator Quintus Aelius Faustus mentioned.

EDCS-10100697.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelia Vendo sibi viva posuit et Aurelio Maximo militi filio qui vixit annos XXV\n
EDCS-10100697.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Vendo set this up for herself while alive, and for her son Aurelius Maximu, a soldier, who lived 25 years.
EDCS-10100697.summary: This funerary inscription records Aurelia Vendo dedicating a tomb for herself and her deceased son, Aurelius Maximu, a soldier, who died at age 25.

EDCS-10100702.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelio Feliciano militi legionis XIII geminae qui vixit annos XXV filio dulcissimo Maximus et Firma parentes\n
EDCS-10100702.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Aelius Feliciano, a soldier of the 13th Gemina legion, who lived 25 years, a most beloved son. His parents Maximus and Firma [set this up].
EDCS-10100702.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Aelius Feliciano, a soldier of the 13th Gemina legion, who died at age 25, by his parents Maximus and Firma.

EDCS-10100703.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Titus Aurelius Rufus Belzeius vivus sibi et Aureliae Fuscae beneene annorum XX mensium rarissimae mulieri domum aeternam fecit posuit o v b\n
EDCS-10100703.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titus Aurelius Rufus Belzeius, while alive, made and set up this eternal home for himself and for Aurelia Fusca, his wife, 20 years old, a most rare woman. He did this willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-10100703.summary: This funerary inscription records Titus Aurelius Rufus Belzeius dedicating an eternal home for himself and his wife Aurelia Fusca, who died at age 20.

EDCS-10100704.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum AITVIQ C ICP XII Sextus CP \n
EDCS-10100704.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. AITVIQ C ICP XII Sextus CP
EDCS-10100704.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription with some names or abbreviations and possibly a numerical reference.

EDCS-10100706.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelia Madussa Aurelio Mauritano coniugi pientissimo qui vixit annos XXVII\n
EDCS-10100706.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aurelia Madussa [set this up] for Aurelius Mauritanus, her most devoted husband, who lived 27 years.
EDCS-10100706.summary: This funerary inscription records Aurelia Madussa dedicating a tomb for her husband Aurelius Mauritanus, who died at age 27.

EDCS-10100707.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Fusco filio Aurelia Fusca mater qui vixit annos L\n
EDCS-10100707.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Aurelius Fuscus, her son. Aurelia Fusca, his mother, [set this up]. He lived 50 years.
EDCS-10100707.summary: This funerary inscription records Aurelia Fusca dedicating a tomb for her son Aurelius Fuscus, who died at age 50.

EDCS-10100708.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aur Er defunct-- annos LV et Aur Co defunct-- annos XIIX et Aur Amand defunct-- annos IIII Aur Teu ETAVROSI TGASIOVER suis defunctis\n
EDCS-10100708.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Aur. Er., deceased, 55 years old, and Aur. Co., deceased, 18 years old, and Aur. Amand, deceased, 4 years old. Aur. Teu and his deceased family members.
EDCS-10100708.summary: This funerary inscription lists several deceased individuals, including Aur. Er. (55), Aur. Co. (18), Aur. Amand (4), and Aur. Teu with his family.

EDCS-10100710.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Publio Aplio Plasso patri pientissimo decurioni municipii Dilunti et Anniae filiae infelicissimae defunctae annorum VIIII Publius Aplius Annius vivus sibi et suis fecit\n
EDCS-10100710.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Publius Aplius Annius, while alive, made and set up [this tomb] for his most devoted father, Titus Aurelius Rufus Belzeius, a decurion of the municipality of Diluntium, and for his most unhappy daughter, Annia, deceased, 9 years old, and for himself and his family.
EDCS-10100710.summary: This funerary inscription records Publius Aplius Annius dedicating a tomb for his father, a decurion, his deceased daughter Annia (aged 9), and himself and his family.

EDCS-10100712.text_edition: \n Deae Iunoni sanctae aram posuit Iunia Varena\n
EDCS-10100712.translation: Iunia Varena placed this altar to the sacred goddess Juno.
EDCS-10100712.summary: This inscription records the dedication of an altar to Juno by Iunia Varena.

EDCS-10100713.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureli-- V coniugi NIISN \n
EDCS-10100713.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Aurelius (?) placed this for his wife NIISN.
EDCS-10100713.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the dedication by Aurelius (?) to his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100716.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelius o Valeriae quae defuncta annorum VIIII parentesNIS SFII filiae infelicissimae\n
EDCS-10100716.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Aelius (?) placed this for his most unfortunate daughter Valeriae, who died at the age of nine.
EDCS-10100716.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the dedication by Aelius (?) for his nine-year-old daughter Valeriae, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100719.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aeliae Victorinae defunctae annorum LXXV Flavia Flaccilla matri\n
EDCS-10100719.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Flavia Flaccilla placed this for her mother Aelia Victorina, who died at the age of seventy-five.
EDCS-10100719.summary: This funerary inscription records Flavia Flaccilla dedicating a monument to her mother, Aelia Victorina, who died at seventy-five.

EDCS-10100720.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Flavio Liciniano decurioni municipii Bistuensium IIviro defuncto annorum \n
EDCS-10100720.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Titus Flavius Licinianus, decurion and former duovir of the municipality of Bistuensium, who died at the age of [...].
EDCS-10100720.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Licinianus, a local official in the municipality of Bistuensium.

EDCS-10100722.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Severus posuit sibi et suis \n
EDCS-10100722.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Severus placed this for himself and his family.
EDCS-10100722.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Severus records the dedication of a monument for himself and his relatives.

EDCS-10100723.text_edition: \n Flaviae Iuliae Domitillae uxori me posuit \n
EDCS-10100723.translation: I placed this for Flavia Iulia Domitilla, my wife.
EDCS-10100723.summary: This funerary inscription records an unnamed husband dedicating a monument to his wife, Flavia Iulia Domitilla.

EDCS-10100728.translation: [...], most dutiful, and Buvo, his wife.
EDCS-10100728.summary: This fragment appears to be part of a funerary inscription, possibly mentioning a dutiful individual and his wife Buvo.

EDCS-10100730.text_edition: \n sacrum Publius Aelius Pinnes? v(otum) solvit loco dato publice\n
EDCS-10100730.translation: Publius Aelius Pinnes (?) fulfilled his sacred vow on ground granted publicly.
EDCS-10100730.summary: This inscription records Publius Aelius Pinnes (?) fulfilling a vow on publicly provided land.

EDCS-10100731.text_edition: \n annorum LXXII dierum XXVII Aelia Tertulla marito inconparabili sic et Aeli Saturninus et Rufinus patri piissimo posuerunt et sibi et suis\n
EDCS-10100731.translation: Aelia Tertulla placed this for her incomparable husband, seventy-two years old, twenty-seven days old; Aelius Saturninus and Rufinus also placed this for their most dutiful father, and for themselves and their families.
EDCS-10100731.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument dedicated by Aelia Tertulla to her husband, and later by their sons Aelius Saturninus and Rufinus to their father, and for their own families.

EDCS-10100732.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus qui vixit annos LXV posuit Aelius Titus Batonis et sibi\n
EDCS-10100732.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aelius Titus placed this for Batonis, who lived 65 years, and for himself.
EDCS-10100732.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aelius Titus for Batonis and himself, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100733.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus coniux et filii sui Aelio Varroni Loisci qui vixit annis XXXX\n
EDCS-10100733.translation: To the Divine Shades. His wife and sons placed this for Aelius Varro Loiscus, who lived 40 years.
EDCS-10100733.summary: This funerary inscription was dedicated by the wife and sons of Aelius Varro Loiscus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100734.text_edition: \n ANTV Aurelius Litus Zuni coniugi karissimae posuit et sibi et suis\n
EDCS-10100734.translation: To ANTV. Aurelius Litus placed this for his dearest wife Zuni, and for himself and his family.
EDCS-10100734.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Aurelius Litus for his wife Zuni and their family, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100735.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Maximus Beusantis vixit annos LX posuerunt fili pientissimo bene merenti\n
EDCS-10100735.translation: To the Divine Shades. His sons placed this for Maximus Beusantis, who lived 60 years, most devoted and well-deserving, and for himself.
EDCS-10100735.summary: This funerary inscription honors Maximus Beusantis, who lived 60 years, and was set up by his devoted sons.

EDCS-10100736.translation: To the Divine Shades. Iaetius Varro Pinnetis.
EDCS-10100736.summary: A simple funerary inscription dedicating a monument to Iaetius Varro Pinnetis.

EDCS-10100741.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelius Titus Dasantis se vivo posuit et Aeliae Seioni IMPL coniugi piae defunctae annorum LX posuit et suis\n
EDCS-10100741.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aelius Titus placed this for himself while alive, and for his pious wife Aelia Seion IMPL, deceased at 60 years of age, and for his family.
EDCS-10100741.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Aelius Titus for himself and his deceased wife Aelia Seion.

EDCS-10100742.text_edition: \n Iato Nepotis vixit annos L posuerunt fili et frater \n
EDCS-10100742.translation: Iato Nepotis, who lived 50 years. His sons and brother placed this.
EDCS-10100742.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by the sons and brother of Iato Nepotis, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-10100743.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Secundi posuit filiae Quiconiugi et suis\n
EDCS-10100743.translation: To the Divine Shades, sacred to Secundus. He placed this for his daughter Quiconiuga and for his family.
EDCS-10100743.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by Secundus for his daughter Quiconiuga and their family.

EDCS-10100744.text_edition: \n Varronis vixit annos posuit filiae et suorum\n
EDCS-10100744.translation: He lived X years. He placed this for his daughter and for his family.
EDCS-10100744.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, recording a person who lived an unspecified number of years and was honored by their daughter and family.

EDCS-10100745.summary: This is a religious dedication to the god Apollo, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100746.summary: This inscription appears to be a fragment of a dedication or epitaph, naming Maximus as a son, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100747.translation: Publius Aelius Procul of the town...
EDCS-10100747.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription identifying Publius Aelius Procul as a citizen of a Roman municipality, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100748.text_edition: \n O vi Surionis votum solvit merito libens\n
EDCS-10100748.translation: Surio gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100748.summary: This inscription records Surio fulfilling a vow, likely dedicating a monument or offering, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100750.text_edition: \n Firmus onis filius d VC votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-10100750.translation: Firmus, son of Onis, a decurion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100750.summary: This inscription records Firmus, son of Onis and a decurion, gladly fulfilling a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100751.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription indicating someone placed an object or monument gladly and deservedly, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100753.text_edition: \n annorum XXXII Aur(elii) unaius et Supera parentes filio carissimo - Aurelius Iucundus fratri pienti[ssimo ------\n
EDCS-10100753.translation: For their dearest son, aged 32. Aurelius Iucundus to his most devoted brother...
EDCS-10100753.summary: This epitaph records Aurelius Iucundus dedicating a tomb to his dearest son, aged 32, and also possibly to his brother, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-10100756.text_edition: \n Nepos cum filiis s(olvit) votum libens merito\n
EDCS-10100756.translation: Nepos with his sons gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10100756.summary: This inscription records Nepos and his sons fulfilling a vow, likely dedicating a religious offering or monument, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100757.text_edition: \n Messiae defunctae annorum XVII parentes et sibi\n
EDCS-10100757.translation: For Messia, deceased at 17 years, her parents and for themselves...
EDCS-10100757.summary: This epitaph records parents setting up a memorial for their daughter Messia, who died at 17, and also for themselves, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100758.text_edition: \n ii decurio municipii o patri suo matri carissimae a posuerunt\n
EDCS-10100758.translation: ... decurion of the municipality to his father and his dearest mother they placed.
EDCS-10100758.summary: This inscription records a decurion of a municipality placing something in honor of his father and dearest mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100759.text_edition: \n tori filio infelicissimo decurioni municipii defuncto annorum XXV \n
EDCS-10100759.translation: ... to his most unhappy son, a decurion of the municipality, deceased at 25 years...
EDCS-10100759.summary: This epitaph records someone setting up a monument for their son, a decurion of the municipality, who died at the age of 25, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100763.translation: He placed this for his wife.
EDCS-10100763.summary: A funerary inscription recording the placement of a monument for a wife, likely a tombstone or similar marker.

EDCS-10100765.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri Primus Vepi libens posuit\n
EDCS-10100765.translation: Primus Vepi placed this willingly for Silvanus Silvestris.
EDCS-10100765.summary: A dedication to the god Silvanus Silvestris, set up by Primus Vepi.

EDCS-10100767.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus filiae vixit annos XVI Plator Carvius Batonis posuit\n
EDCS-10100767.translation: To the Divine Shades. His daughter lived for 16 years. Carvius Batonis Plator placed this.
EDCS-10100767.summary: A funerary inscription for a daughter who died at age 16, set up by her father Carvius Batonis Plator.

EDCS-10100768.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Fulgerali Vesii Ravonius et Hermes votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-10100768.translation: For Jupiter Best and Greatest, Thunderer. Vesii Ravonius and Hermes gladly and deservedly paid their vow.
EDCS-10100768.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Fulgeralis, recording the fulfillment of a vow by Vesii Ravonius and Hermes.

EDCS-10100769.text_edition: \n Valent militi legionis II adiutricis defuncto annorum XXX memoriam posuit Tata pientissima coniux\n
EDCS-10100769.translation: His devoted wife Tata placed this memorial for Valent, a soldier of the II Adiutrix legion, who died at 30 years of age.
EDCS-10100769.summary: A funerary monument for Valent, a soldier of the II Adiutrix legion, erected by his wife Tata.

EDCS-10100770.text_edition: \n Publius Aelius Firmus filius bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10100770.translation: Publius Aelius Firmus placed this for his deserving son.
EDCS-10100770.summary: A funerary inscription recording a father's memorial for his deserving son.

EDCS-10100771.summary: A fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary monument, mentioning Aelius Maximus.

EDCS-10100772.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Antonio Gordiano Pio Felici Augusto decreto\n
EDCS-10100772.translation: For the Emperor Caesar Marcus Antonius Gordianus Pius Felix Augustus by decree...
EDCS-10100772.summary: The beginning of an inscription recording an honor granted by decree to Emperor Gordian III.

EDCS-10100773.text_edition: \n ae Priscillae coniugi parentes vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-10100773.translation: Her parents, alive, made this for Priscilla, their wife.
EDCS-10100773.summary: A funerary monument erected for Priscilla by her parents.

EDCS-10100774.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum T Frontoni patri pientissimo qui vixit annos LV L Marcus filius ei et sibi vivus posuit\n
EDCS-10100774.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For T. Frontonius, my most dutiful father, who lived 55 years. Marcus, his son, placed this for him and for himself while alive.
EDCS-10100774.summary: A funerary inscription set up for T. Frontonius by his son Marcus, who also dedicated it for himself.

EDCS-10100775.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto Sextus Aelius Domitianus pius posuit\n
EDCS-10100775.translation: Sextus Aelius Domitianus piously dedicated this to Hercules Augustus.
EDCS-10100775.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument to Hercules Augustus by Sextus Aelius Domitianus, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-10100776.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucius Valerius Licinius decurio IIvir vixit annos XXXV Procilla coniunx ipsius vixit annos XX Lucius Valerius Montanus et Baebia Iulla parentes filio pientissimo et sibi vivis memoriam posuerunt\n
EDCS-10100776.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Valerius Licinius, decurio and IIvir, lived 35 years. Procilla, his wife, lived 20 years. Lucius Valerius Montanus and Baebia Iulla, parents, erected this memorial for their most devoted son and for themselves while alive.
EDCS-10100776.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commemorating Lucius Valerius Licinius and his wife Procilla, with a memorial erected by their parents for their son and themselves, dating likely to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-10100777.text_edition: \n Anancae pro salute Valeri Liciniani fili Lucius Coelius Valerianus pater libens laetus\n
EDCS-10100777.translation: For the safety of Valerius Licinianus, his son, Lucius Coelius Valerianus, father, gladly and joyfully [dedicated this].
EDCS-10100777.summary: This inscription records a father, Lucius Coelius Valerianus, dedicating something for the well-being of his son Valerius Licinianus, probably in the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-10100778.text_edition: \n Dianae Augustae Candaviensi Titus Flavius Dionysius donum posuit\n
EDCS-10100778.translation: Titus Flavius Dionysius placed this gift for Diana Augusta of Candavium.
EDCS-10100778.summary: This inscription records a gift dedicated to Diana Augusta of Candavium by Titus Flavius Dionysius, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100779.text_edition: \n Deis omnibus Casticius Tertullinus ex voto\n
EDCS-10100779.translation: To all the gods, Casticius Tertullinus, according to his vow.
EDCS-10100779.summary: This inscription records a dedication to all the gods by Casticius Tertullinus in fulfillment of a vow, typical of the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100780.text_edition: \n Iovi Antra--rim sacrum Titus Flavius Liberalis ex voto succepto sic\n
EDCS-10100780.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Antra--rim. Titus Flavius Liberalis, according to his undertaken vow, thus [dedicated this].
EDCS-10100780.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Antra--rim by Titus Flavius Liberalis in fulfillment of a vow, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100781.text_edition: \n Iovi Cohortali Augusto Iulius Eutyches libertus pro salute patroni sui Iuli Vinciani votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-10100781.translation: To Jupiter Cohortalis Augustus, Iulius Eutyches, freedman, gladly fulfilled his vow for the safety of his patron, Iulius Vincianus.
EDCS-10100781.summary: This inscription records a freedman, Iulius Eutyches, fulfilling a vow for his patron's safety to Jupiter Cohortalis Augustus, probably from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-10100782.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Eponae Reginae Genio loci Caius Ocratius Lacon beneficiarius consularis legionis I adiutricis votum solvit libens merito Crispino et Aeliano consulibus\n
EDCS-10100782.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, to Epona Regina, and to the Genius of the place, Caius Ocratius Lacon, a beneficiarius of the consular governor of the I Adiutrix legion, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow, when Crispinus and Aelianus were consuls.
EDCS-10100782.summary: This inscription records a soldier, Caius Ocratius Lacon, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter, Epona, and the local genius during the consulship of Crispinus and Aelianus, likely in the 2nd century AD.

EDCS-10100784.text_edition: \n ------] Domno et Domnae Petronia Valentina votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-10100784.translation: [...] Domnus and Domna, Petronia Valentina gladly fulfilled her vow.
