EDCS-10100784.summary: This inscription records Petronia Valentina fulfilling a vow to the patrons referred to as Domnus and Domna, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100786.text_edition: \n Marco Antonio Euticho collegium fabrum fecit\n
EDCS-10100786.translation: The guild of craftsmen made this for Marcus Antonius Eutychus.
EDCS-10100786.summary: This inscription records a guild of craftsmen honoring Marcus Antonius Eutychus, possibly with a public monument, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100787.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Tito Cassio Valerio Epidiano qui vixit annos XV menses XI dies XXVII Valerius Marcianus et Epidia Tatta parentes filio pientissimo\n
EDCS-10100787.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Titus Cassius Valerius Epidianus, who lived 15 years, 11 months, 27 days. Valerius Marcianus and Epidia Tatta, his parents, [erected this] for their most devoted son.
EDCS-10100787.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young boy, Titus Cassius Valerius Epidianus, and was set up by his parents.

EDCS-10100788.text_edition: \n T(ito) Flavio Titi filio Quirina Basso decurioni ex testamento eius \n
EDCS-10100788.translation: For Titus Flavius Bassus, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, decurion, by his will.
EDCS-10100788.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Titus Flavius Bassus, a decurion, made according to his will.

EDCS-10100789.text_edition: \n Flavia Phoebe quae vixit nnos VI Iulia Domitia mater fecit\n
EDCS-10100789.translation: Flavia Phoebe, who lived 6 years. Her mother, Iulia Domitia, made this.
EDCS-10100789.summary: This funerary inscription records the short life of Flavia Phoebe and was erected by her mother.

EDCS-10100790.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Marcio Cilici decurioni Calabro domo Lypias sic vixit annos XX Caius Marcius Firmus filio piissimo fecit\n
EDCS-10100790.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Caius Marcius Cilix, decurion, from Calabra, originally from Lypias, who lived 20 years. Caius Marcius Firmus, his most devoted son, erected this.
EDCS-10100790.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Caius Marcius Cilix, a decurion from Lypias, and was dedicated by his son.

EDCS-10100791.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Numeriae Clementillae coniugi Verrius Mercator maritus\n
EDCS-10100791.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Numeria Clementilla, his wife. Verrius Mercator, her husband, [erected this].
EDCS-10100791.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Numeria Clementilla and was dedicated by her husband, Verrius Mercator.

EDCS-10100792.text_edition: \n Tito Oclatio Severo et Laetiliae Trhepte sic et suis Dis Manibus\n
EDCS-10100792.translation: For Titus Oclatius Severus and Laetilia Trhepte, and for their own [family members], to the spirits of the departed.
EDCS-10100792.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Titus Oclatius Severus and Laetilia Trhepte and their family.

EDCS-10100793.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription likely dedicated to Lucius Titius Cl...

EDCS-10100794.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Verrio Firmo qui vixit annos XXXIII Marcia Procula marito optimo pientissimo bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-10100794.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Verrius Firmus, who lived 33 years. Marcia Procula, his most excellent and devoted wife, who deserved well, erected this.
EDCS-10100794.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Verrius Firmus and was erected by his wife, Marcia Procula.

EDCS-10100795.text_edition: \n Caio Aa Felici domo Salona defuncto annorum XXX Octavia Acantis marito\n
EDCS-10100795.translation: For Caius Aa Felix, originally from Salona, who died at 30 years of age. Octavia Acantis, his wife, [erected this].
EDCS-10100795.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Caius Aa Felix and was erected by his wife.

EDCS-10100796.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Faustino patri piissimo qui vixit annos IX avia filia fecit\n
EDCS-10100796.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Faustinus, a most devoted father, who lived 9 years. His granddaughter, the daughter, erected this.
EDCS-10100796.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young boy named Faustinus and was erected by his granddaughter.

EDCS-10100797.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum miserrimo et infelicissimo Libonio qui vixit annos LV menses VIII dies V er Procula fratri pientissimo\n
EDCS-10100797.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Procula dedicated this to her most devoted and most unfortunate brother Libonius, who lived 55 years, 8 months, and 5 days.
EDCS-10100797.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death and burial of Libonius, dedicated by his sister Procula, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100798.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Temusa, Baiula, and Boia.
EDCS-10100798.summary: A funerary inscription dedicating the space to the spirits of Temusa, Baiula, and Boia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100803.text_edition: \n Plaetoriae Titullae Plaetoria Iulla matri pientissimae\n
EDCS-10100803.translation: For Plaetoria Iulla, to her most devoted mother Plaetoria Titulla.
EDCS-10100803.summary: An epitaph dedicating a tomb to Plaetoria Titulla by her daughter Plaetoria Iulla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100804.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Crinnetisa Harmoniae Libonia Placida munus cum natis suis fecerunt\n
EDCS-10100804.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Crinnetisa Harmonia and Libonia Placida, with their children, made this offering.
EDCS-10100804.summary: A funerary inscription recording an offering made by Crinnetisa Harmonia and Libonia Placida for themselves and their children, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100805.text_edition: \n Nynphis sic Auggustis sacrum Marcus S M cum suis\n
EDCS-10100805.translation: Sacred to the August Nymphs. Marcus S M with his family.
EDCS-10100805.summary: A dedication to the Nymphs by Marcus S M and his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100806.text_edition: \n Marcus Caesius Epidianus vivus fecit sibi et suis sepulturam\n
EDCS-10100806.translation: Marcus Caesius Epidianus made this burial place for himself and his family while he was alive.
EDCS-10100806.summary: A funerary inscription recording the self-funded burial plot for Marcus Caesius Epidianus and his family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100807.text_edition: \n Agirro Epicadi filio principi kastelli Salthua et Temeiae Glavi filiae fecit Epicadus filius ut primum aetatis complerunt tempora vitae et genitor fato conditus est tumulo protinus inscripsit pietas nomen matrisque patrisque ut legerent cuncti genitoris nomina saepe hoc decet ut nati componant ossa parentum et cineri sedem substituat pietas\n
EDCS-10100807.translation: Epicadus, his son, made this for his son Agirro, son of Epicadus, prince of the fortress Salthua, and for Temeia, daughter of Glavius, as soon as they completed the times of their lives; and when the father was consigned to fate, he immediately inscribed the tomb with their names, it is fitting that sons always honor their parents' names and that devotion provides a resting place for the ashes of parents.
EDCS-10100807.summary: A funerary inscription by Epicadus for his deceased son Agirro and wife Temeia, emphasizing filial piety and the duty to honor parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100808.text_edition: \n Caius Epicadi filius princeps civitatis Docleatium hic situs hoc fieri iussit genitor sibi et suis set sic fili eius Plassus Epicadus Scerdis Verzo et summa adiecta eff<e=I>cit sic istud opus est pietas natique hoc auxsisse sic videntur et decorant facto et docent esse pios \n
EDCS-10100808.translation: Caius, son of Epicadus, prince of the city of the Docleatians, lies here; his father ordered this to be made for himself and his family, but his son Plassus Epicadus, son of Scerdis Verzo, completed this work with additional care; indeed, the sons appear to have enhanced this and adorn it with their actions, and they teach that one should be devoted.
EDCS-10100808.summary: A funerary inscription detailing the construction of a tomb for Caius and his family, completed by his son Plassus Epicadus, highlighting filial duty and civic leadership, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100810.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Asellioni annorum III Alexandria icin \n
EDCS-10100810.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Asellio, aged 3 years, from Alexandria.
EDCS-10100810.summary: A funerary epitaph for a three-year-old named Asellio, from Alexandria, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100811.text_edition: \n Statia Luci filia Clementia Iunoni Lucinae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10100811.translation: Statia Clementia, daughter of Lucius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Juno Lucina.
EDCS-10100811.summary: A votive inscription recording Statia Clementia fulfilling a vow to the goddess Juno Lucina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100812.text_edition: \n Egnatia Maximae liberta Myrtale filio Silvino fecit ed sic sibi\n
EDCS-10100812.translation: Myrtale, freedwoman of Egnatia Maxima, made this for her son Silvinus and for herself.
EDCS-10100812.summary: This funerary monument, likely a tombstone, was dedicated by a freedwoman for her son and herself, possibly in the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10100813.text_edition: \n Iulia Temus Egnatio Felici coniugi suo et filiae bene merentibus viva fecit\n
EDCS-10100813.translation: Iulia Temus made this for her husband Egnatius Felix and her daughter, deserving women, while she was alive.
EDCS-10100813.summary: This funerary monument, probably a tombstone, was erected by Iulia Temus for her husband and daughter during her lifetime, likely in the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10100818.text_edition: \n Aesculapio sacrum Marcus Caesius Marci libertus Primus Sextus Herennius Sexti Sexti libertus Eros IIIIIIviri magistri Mercuriales ob honorem\n
EDCS-10100818.translation: Sacred to Aesculapius. Marcus Caesius Primus, freedman of Marcus, and Sextus Herennius Eros, freedman of Sextus Sextus, as the four officials of the Mercuriales association, offered this in honor.
EDCS-10100818.summary: This dedication to Aesculapius was made by two officials of the Mercuriales religious association, likely in the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10100819.text_edition: \n Fortunae Augustae sacrum Atilia Tranquilla votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10100819.translation: Atilia Tranquilla willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Fortuna Augusta.
EDCS-10100819.summary: This votive inscription records Atilia Tranquilla fulfilling a vow to the goddess Fortuna Augusta, likely in the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10100820.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo aeterno sacrum Valerius Alexander ex voto posuit pro coniuge sua\n
EDCS-10100820.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Aeternus. Valerius Alexander set this up in fulfillment of a vow for his wife.
EDCS-10100820.summary: This dedicatory inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Aeternus was set up by Valerius Alexander in fulfillment of a vow for his wife, likely in the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10100821.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Dolicheno sacrum posuerunt sacerdotes Flavius Faladus et Domitius Apollinaris pro salute Imperatoris Helvi Pertenacis sic et populi <c=R>oloniae Iuliae Naronae \n
EDCS-10100821.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Dolichenus. The priests Flavius Faladus and Domitius Apollinaris set this up for the safety of the Emperor Pertinax and also of the people of Colonia Julia Narona.
EDCS-10100821.summary: This dedication to Jupiter Dolichenus was made by priests for the health of Emperor Pertinax and the citizens of Colonia Julia Narona, dated to 193 CE.

EDCS-10100822.text_edition: \n Marcus Paenius Augurinus locus datus publice decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-10100822.translation: Marcus Paenius Augurinus. The site was granted publicly by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-10100822.summary: This inscription records the granting of public land to Marcus Paenius Augurinus by local council decree, likely in a municipium during the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10100825.summary: This is a dedication or honorific inscription to Publius Servilius Isauricus, who served as consul, likely dating to his consulship in the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-10100826.text_edition: \n Aulo Ducenio Gemino consuli XVviro sacris faciundis sodali Augustali curatori vectigalium publicorum legato pro praetore patrono\n
EDCS-10100826.translation: To Aulus Ducenius Geminus, member of the XVvir sacris faciundis, Augustalis sodalis, curator of public revenues, legate pro praetore, and patron.
EDCS-10100826.summary: This is an honorific inscription listing the distinguished career and patron status of Aulus Ducenius Geminus, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10100828.text_edition: \n Quintus Lusius Seleucus Quintus Lusius Secundus Lucius Afidius Pisaurio Caius Valerius Castrensis Quintus Iulius Ursio Publius Licinius Leo Quintus Lusius Liberalis Quintus Lusius Glycon Quintus Stertinius Paris Publius Saenius Lupus Tiberius Claudius Latini libertus Fortunatus Quintus Antonius Felix Marcus Vinicius Masurius Severus Publius Annaeus Philetus Publius Publicius Tryphon Lucius Cerennius Fortunatus Lucius Ostorius Dionysius\n
EDCS-10100828.translation: Quintus Lusius Seleucus, Quintus Lusius Secundus, Lucius Afidius Pisaurio, Caius Valerius Castrensis, Quintus Iulius Ursio, Publius Licinius Leo, Quintus Lusius Liberalis, Quintus Lusius Glycon, Quintus Stertinius Paris, Publius Saenius Lupus, Tiberius Claudius Fortunatus, freedman of Latinius, Quintus Antonius Felix, Marcus Vinicius Masurius Severus, Publius Annaeus Philetus, Publius Publicius Tryphon, Lucius Cerennius Fortunatus, Lucius Ostorius Dionysius.
EDCS-10100828.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly of donors, members of a group, or beneficiaries of a dedication, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10100830.text_edition: \n ------ Publi]cio Quinti filio Nic aedili IIIIviro ai Quinti filiae Secundinae - Publicius Cai filius Nice[phorus ------\n
EDCS-10100830.translation: To Publius Quintilius Nicephorus, son of Caius, aedile and magistrate four times, [dedicated by] his daughter Secundina, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-10100830.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Publius Quintilius Nicephorus, likely from the early imperial period given the titles aedile and IVvir.

EDCS-10100831.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Claudio Mascelioni annorum XXXIIII IIIIIIviro qui libertatem tus et \n
EDCS-10100831.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Claudius Mascelio, aged 34, who was a magistrate four times and who [achieved] freedom...
EDCS-10100831.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudius Mascelio, a freedman who held the high civic office of IVvir, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10100832.text_edition: \n Sextus Herennius Sexti libertus Eros IIIIIIvir Licinia Quinti filia Iucunda Herennia Sexti filia Tyche Herennia Sexti liberta \n
EDCS-10100832.translation: To the departed spirits. Eros, freedman of Sextus, IVvir; Iucunda, daughter of Quintus; Tyche, freedwoman of Sextus, daughter of Herennius; [and] daughter of Herennius, Sextus.
EDCS-10100832.summary: This funerary inscription lists several individuals, including Eros, a freedman and IVvir, and members of the Herennia family, suggesting a burial plot shared by freedmen and their patrons or relatives.

EDCS-10100833.text_edition: \n Caio Iunio Cai liberto Cisso IIIIIIviro collegium fabrum\n
EDCS-10100833.translation: To Caius Iunius Cissus, freedman of Caius, IVvir, by the guild of artisans.
EDCS-10100833.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the guild of artisans to Caius Iunius Cissus, a freedman who served as a IVvir, indicating his importance within the community.

EDCS-10100834.text_edition: \n Papiae Luci filiae Maximae Caius Agrius Cai libertus Fortunatus IIIIIIvir coniugi bene merenti et suis vivus fecit\n
EDCS-10100834.translation: To his well-deserving wife, Maxima, daughter of Lucius, Caius Agrius Fortunatus, freedman of Caius and a IVvir, erected this for her and for himself while alive.
EDCS-10100834.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Caius Agrius Fortunatus, a IVvir and freedman, for his wife Maxima and himself, likely dating to the early imperial period.

EDCS-10100836.text_edition: \n Quinto Nassio Iuliano haruspici Iulianus et Rufinus filii parenti optimo\n
EDCS-10100836.translation: To Quintus Nassius Julianus, the haruspex, by his sons Julianus and Rufinus, to their excellent father.
EDCS-10100836.summary: This inscription is a dedication by two sons, Julianus and Rufinus, to their father Quintus Nassius Julianus, who held the religious office of haruspex.

EDCS-10100837.text_edition: \n Umbrio Lu coniugi incomparabili qui vixit annis XXXII Queta posuit\n
EDCS-10100837.translation: To Umbrio Lu, incomparable husband, who lived 32 years. Queta placed this.
EDCS-10100837.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Queta for her husband Umbrio Lu, who died at the age of 32, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10100838.text_edition: \n Valeriae Piae nnorum XIIII Ravonia Procilla mater filiae pientissimae viva et sibi et posteris suis fecit\n
EDCS-10100838.translation: To Valeria Pia, aged 14. Ravonia Procilla, her mother, made this for her most devoted daughter, for herself and her descendants while alive.
EDCS-10100838.summary: This funerary inscription is a testament erected by a mother, Ravonia Procilla, for her young daughter Valeria Pia and for herself, indicating a family burial monument.

EDCS-10100842.text_edition: \n o Luci filio Ti no Ae\n
EDCS-10100842.translation: [This is a fragment] of Lucius, son of Titus...
EDCS-10100842.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly a funerary epitaph or a dedication, mentioning a man named Lucius.

EDCS-10100845.translation: [This is a fragment] to Rusticus, son of Spurius...
EDCS-10100845.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records a dedication or a funerary monument to an individual named Rusticus.

EDCS-10100849.text_edition: \n Quintus Trebianus Auctus hic situs est\n
EDCS-10100849.translation: Quintus Trebianus Auctus is buried here.
EDCS-10100849.summary: This is a tombstone recording the burial of Quintus Trebianus Auctus.

EDCS-10100850.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Amaerimno generi pientissimo bene merenti vixit nnos XXV Restitutianus et Calliste\n
EDCS-10100850.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Amaerimnus, most devoted and deserving son-in-law, who lived 25 years. Restitutianus and Calliste set this up.
EDCS-10100850.summary: This funerary epitaph, set up by Restitutianus and Calliste, commemorates their deserving son-in-law Amaerimnus.

EDCS-10100852.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records names, possibly of dedicants or the deceased.

EDCS-10100853.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caio Fresio Valentino veterano ex |(centurione) cohortis II miliariae Dalmatarum \n
EDCS-10100853.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Sacred to Gaius Fresius Valentinus, a veteran ex-centurion of the II miliaria Dalmatarum cohort.
EDCS-10100853.summary: This funerary dedication honors Gaius Fresius Valentinus, a veteran ex-centurion.

EDCS-10100855.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Flavius Fortunatus maritus Atiliae Quartae uxori bene merenti fecit vixit nnos XXXV\n
EDCS-10100855.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Sacred to Flavia Fortunata. Her husband set this up for his most deserving wife, who lived 35 years.
EDCS-10100855.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by a husband for his deserving wife, Flavia Fortunata.

EDCS-10100857.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelia Marciana defuncta annorum XX Aelius Dracontius maritus coniugi pientissimae posuit\n
EDCS-10100857.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Aelia Marciana, deceased, aged 20. Her husband, Aelius Dracontius, placed this for his most devoted wife.
EDCS-10100857.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Aelius Dracontius for his deceased wife, Aelia Marciana.

EDCS-10100861.text_edition: \n Lucius Oppius Luci filius Sergia Segninus domo Conana veteranus legionis VII nnum LXX\n
EDCS-10100861.translation: Lucius Oppius, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, from Segninus (Conana), a veteran of the VII legion, aged 70.
EDCS-10100861.summary: This epitaph records Lucius Oppius, a veteran of the VII legion, and his age.

EDCS-10100863.text_edition: \n Pollia miles legionis I adiutricis annorum XXX stipendiorum X Attius Domitius miles legionis II adiutricis procurator posuit\n
EDCS-10100863.translation: Pollia, soldier of the I adiutrix legion, aged 30, with 10 years of service. Attius Domitius, soldier of the II adiutrix legion, as procurator, set this up.
EDCS-10100863.summary: This inscription records two soldiers, Pollia and Attius Domitius, noting their legions, ages, and service years, with Attius Domitius acting as procurator.

EDCS-10100864.text_edition: \n Marcus Livius Marci filius Palatina domo Aloro veteranus legionis VII annorum L stipendiorum XXVI hic situs est testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-10100864.translation: Marcus Livius, son of Marcus, of the Palatina tribe, from Aloro, a veteran of the VII legion, aged 50, with 26 years of service, ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-10100864.summary: This tombstone, erected according to his will, records Marcus Livius, a veteran of the VII legion, detailing his age and service.

EDCS-10100865.text_edition: \n Titus Varius Titi filius Velina domo Pessinunte veteranus legionis VII annorum L stipendiorum XXIX hic situs est heres posuit\n
EDCS-10100865.translation: Titus Varius, son of Titus, of the Velina tribe, from Pessinunte, a veteran of the VII legion, aged 50, with 29 years of service. His heir placed this.
EDCS-10100865.summary: This funerary monument was erected by the heir of Titus Varius, a veteran of the VII legion, noting his age and service.

EDCS-10100866.text_edition: \n Q(uinto) Caenio Quinti filio equiti cohortis III Alpinorum domo Velauno nnorum stipendiorum XX \n
EDCS-10100866.translation: To Quintus Caenius, son of Quintus, cavalryman of the I Alpine Cohort, from Velauno, aged [?], with 20 years of service.
EDCS-10100866.summary: This inscription records the details of a cavalryman named Quintus Caenius from Velauno, who served in the I Alpine Cohort.

EDCS-10100868.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iuliae B coniugi bene merenti quae vixit mecum annos XXII et tulit aetatis suae annos XL Flavius Aurelianus decurio cohortis I Belgarum posuit natione Pannonia\n
EDCS-10100868.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Julia B., his well-deserving wife, who lived with me for 22 years and was 40 years old. Flavius Aurelianus, decurion of the I Belgic Cohort, of Pannonia origin, erected this.
EDCS-10100868.summary: This funerary inscription honors Julia B., the deceased wife of Flavius Aurelianus, a decurion of the I Belgic Cohort, who lived 22 years with her husband and died at age 40.

EDCS-10100869.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Verecundo |(centurioni) cohortis I Belgarum vixit nnos XXXVIIIII stipendiorum XXI testamento fieri iussit ex HS mille nummum Postimia Restituta uxor pientissimo fieri curavit ex HS MDCmille sescentis nummum\n
EDCS-10100869.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Gaius Julius Verecundus, centurion of the I Belgic Cohort, who lived 39 years and served 21 years. He ordered this to be made by his will for 1,000 sesterces. Postimia Restituta, his wife, had it made for the most devoted man for 1,600 sesterces.
EDCS-10100869.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Gaius Julius Verecundus, a centurion of the I Belgic Cohort, who died at age 39 after 21 years of service, with expenses specified for his memorial.

EDCS-10100870.text_edition: \n to equiti cohortis I Belgarum stipendiorum posuit Aurelia Marina nepoti piissimo\n
EDCS-10100870.translation: To [his] cavalryman of the I Belgic Cohort, with [?]-years of service. Aurelia Marina erected this for her most devoted grandson.
EDCS-10100870.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Aurelia Marina to her grandson, who was a cavalryman in the I Belgic Cohort.

EDCS-10100871.text_edition: \n Dassius Bastarni filius domo Maezaeus miles cohortis I Belgarum centuria Restituti signifer nnum stipendiorum XV testamento fieri iussit curavit Valerius Maximinus heres\n
EDCS-10100871.translation: Dassius, son of Bastarnus, from Maezaeus, soldier of the I Belgic Cohort, centuria of Restitutus, standard-bearer, with 15 years of service. He ordered this to be made by his will. Valerius Maximinus, his heir, had it made.
EDCS-10100871.summary: This inscription records the details of Dassius, a soldier and standard-bearer in the I Belgic Cohort, whose heir Valerius Maximinus fulfilled his will for his memorial.

EDCS-10100872.text_edition: \n Meduttus Caturonis filius miles cohortis I Bracaraugustanorum nnorum XXX stipendiorum \n
EDCS-10100872.translation: Meduttus, son of Caturonus, soldier of the I Bracaraugustanorum Cohort, aged 30 years, with [?]-years of service.
EDCS-10100872.summary: This inscription records the details of Meduttus, a soldier of the I Bracaraugustanorum Cohort, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-10100873.text_edition: \n sca miles cohortis I Bracaraugustanorum annorum X hic situs est Cambi heres posuit\n
EDCS-10100873.translation: [?]-sca, soldier of the I Bracaraugustanorum Cohort, aged 10 years, is buried here. Cambi, his heir, erected this.
EDCS-10100873.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of a 10-year-old soldier of the I Bracaraugustanorum Cohort, identified as Cambi's heir.

EDCS-10100874.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavio Maxima filio suo et Octavius signifer amico dulcissimo fecerunt nnorum duum bene merenti paremtalem sic dolente animo\n
EDCS-10100874.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Flavius Maximus, their son, and Octavius, standard-bearer, their dearest friend, who lived [?]-years. [They erected this] for the well-deserving, with hearts grieving.
EDCS-10100874.summary: This inscription is a joint dedication to a son named Flavius Maximus and a friend, Octavius the standard-bearer, by grieving parents.

EDCS-10100875.summary: This brief inscription likely records the military roles of standard-bearer and vexillary for an unnamed individual.

EDCS-10100878.text_edition: \n Tiberio Claudio Maximo filio annorum XII Aelia Maxima mater infelicissimo filio et Aeliae Annaeae matri hoc monumentum heredem <n=M>(on) sequetur et Primus pater \n
EDCS-10100878.translation: To Tiberius Claudius Maximus, aged 12 years, [erected by] Aelia Maxima, his mother, for her most unfortunate son, and Aeliae Annaeae, the mother. [This monument will not follow an heir] and Primus, the father.
EDCS-10100878.summary: This monument was erected by Aelia Maxima for her 12-year-old son Tiberius Claudius Maximus and for Aeliae Annaeae, his mother, with the father Primus also mentioned.

EDCS-10100880.text_edition: \n Marco Antonio Maximo signifero veterano legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis nnum L stipendiorum XXX et Iuliae Sabinae Caius Antonius Augurinus filius faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-10100880.translation: To Marcus Antonius Maximus, standard-bearer veteran of the XI Claudian legion, the Pious, the Faithful, with 50 years of service and 30 years in the army, Caius Antonius Augurinus, his son, saw to the construction.
EDCS-10100880.summary: This is a funerary monument for Marcus Antonius Maximus, a veteran standard-bearer, dedicated by his son.

EDCS-10100881.text_edition: \n Aurelius Saturninus ER defunctus annorum XXAR Severus PSIS SD\n
EDCS-10100881.translation: Aurelius Saturninus, deceased at the age of 20 years.
EDCS-10100881.summary: This is a tombstone recording the death of Aurelius Saturninus at the age of 20.

EDCS-10100883.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum Iulius Cassianus miles cohortis \n
EDCS-10100883.translation: Dedicated to Silvanus Augustus, Julius Cassianus, a soldier of the cohort.
EDCS-10100883.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus Augustus by Julius Cassianus, a soldier.

EDCS-10100888.text_edition: \n Titiae Septimae filiae infelici Larcia Gemella marito et filiae dulcissimae posuit\n
EDCS-10100888.translation: Larcia Gemella set this up for her unfortunate daughter Titia Septima and her dearest daughter.
EDCS-10100888.summary: This monument was erected by Larcia Gemella for her deceased daughter Titia Septima and another daughter.

EDCS-10100889.text_edition: \n Minervae Augustae sacrum Lucius Sulpicius Proculus actuarius cohortis VIII voluntariorum ex adiutore corniculariorum connsularis libens posuit\n
EDCS-10100889.translation: Lucius Sulpicius Proculus, actuarius of the VIII cohort of volunteers, formerly an assistant to the cornicularii of the governor, willingly set this up for Minerva Augusta.
EDCS-10100889.summary: This is a dedication to Minerva Augusta by Lucius Sulpicius Proculus, a military official.

EDCS-10100890.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Tito Aelio Hadriano Antonino Augusto Pio patri patriae consuli IIII cohors VIII voluntariorum turrem ad aquam tollendam fecit Sexto Aemilio Equestre legato Augusti pro praetore Marco Caecilio Africano praefecto\n
EDCS-10100890.translation: The VIII cohort of volunteers built this tower to carry water for the Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, Father of the Fatherland, Consul for the 4th time, with Sextus Aemilius Equestris, Imperial Legate with praetorian power, and Marcus Caecilius Africanus, prefect.
EDCS-10100890.summary: This records the construction of a tower for water management by the VIII cohort of volunteers, during the reign of Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-10100891.text_edition: \n Lucius Fabius Cai filius Sergia Conana nnorum XX hic situs est Caius Fabius pater filio posuit\n
EDCS-10100891.translation: Lucius Fabius, son of Caius, of the Sergia tribe, 20 years old, lies here. Caius Fabius, his father, placed this for his son.
EDCS-10100891.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Fabius, erected by his father Caius Fabius.

EDCS-10100892.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Cai filius Fabia Ninica miles legionis VII annorum XXXIX stipendiorum XVII hic situs est hospes resiste et tumulum contempla meum lege et moraris iam scies quae debeas vixi qua potui carus sum Opiorum hic sum sepultus hic ossua sic requiescum sic mea vale et bene facito vitae dum fatum venit Faustus libertus patrono\n
EDCS-10100892.translation: Caius Julius, son of Caius, of the Fabia tribe, Ninica, soldier of the VII legion, 39 years old, 17 years in service, lies here. Stranger, stop and look at my tomb, read it, and linger; you will then know what you owe. I lived as best I could; I am dear. I am buried here; here my bones rest. Thus fare ye well, and do well while fate lasts. Faustus, freedman, for his patron.
EDCS-10100892.summary: This is a tombstone for Caius Julius Ninica, a soldier, with a lengthy epitaph urging passersby to reflect on life and death, erected by his freedman Faustus.

EDCS-10100897.text_edition: \n ODLI annum mensem filio infelicissimo LBL et Attia Caesia parentes vivi f(ecerunt) et? s(ibi)\n
EDCS-10100897.translation: Parents, alive, made this for their most unfortunate son, [aged] 50 years, [and] a month, and for themselves.
EDCS-10100897.summary: This monument was erected by living parents for their deceased son and for themselves.

EDCS-10100899.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caetenio Secundo infelicissimo defuncto annorum XV menserum sic X dierum XX Caetenius Maxsimus sic et Raia Ianuaria parentes i<f=E>elio sic posuerunt \n
EDCS-10100899.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Caetenius Secundus, deceased at the age of 15 years, 10 months, and 20 days. Caetenius Maxsimus and Raia Ianuaria, parents, placed this for their unfortunate son.
EDCS-10100899.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Caetenius Secundus, who died at the age of 15 years, 10 months, and 20 days, erected by his parents.

EDCS-10100901.text_edition: \n eriuno aberio Flavius tiscum vivus nio tis filio\n
EDCS-10100901.translation: Flavius Tiscum erected this for his son, Aberio Eriuno, during his lifetime.
EDCS-10100901.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the Roman period, records a father's dedication to his son during his own lifetime.

EDCS-10100902.text_edition: \n Genio Osiniatium Publius Rapidius signifer cohortis \n
EDCS-10100902.translation: To the Genius of the Osiniates, Publius Rapidius, standard-bearer of the cohort...
EDCS-10100902.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Publius Rapidius, a standard-bearer, to the Genius of the Osiniates tribe, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10100903.text_edition: \n Tiberius Claudius Priscus veteranus alae Claudiae novae testamento fieri iussit arbitratu Sexti Viniusi\n
EDCS-10100903.translation: Tiberius Claudius Priscus, a veteran of the new Claudia cavalry wing, ordered this to be made by his will, under the supervision of Sextus Vinius.
EDCS-10100903.summary: This funerary inscription records a veteran of the ala Claudiae novae, Tiberius Claudius Priscus, who posthumously commissioned his monument through his will, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10100904.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Naevio Firmo decurioni coloniae Aequensium aedili duumviro qui vixit nnis Naevio Natalioni patri eiusdem Marci Naevi Firmi qui vixit annis LXXX Claudia Syra mater filio piissimo et marito obsequentissimo posuit\n
EDCS-10100904.translation: To the Divine Shades. Claudia Syra set up this monument for her most pious son, Marcus Naevius Firmus, decurion, aedile, and duumvir of the colony of Aequa, who lived 80 years, and for his father, also named Marcus Naevius Firmus, and for her most obedient husband.
EDCS-10100904.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Marcus Naevius Firmus, a prominent official in the colony of Aequa, and his father, dedicated by his wife and mother, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10100906.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quinto Plaetorio Quinti filio Sergia Valenti defuncto annorum XV Quintus Plaetorius Pudens et Nevia Secunda parentes filio infelicissimo posuerunt in fronte pedes in agro pedes\n
EDCS-10100906.translation: To the Divine Shades. Quintus Plaetorius Pudens and Nevia Secunda, parents, placed this for their most unhappy son, Quintus Plaetorius Valens, son of Quintus, of the Sergia tribe, who died at the age of 15 years. In front, X feet; in the field, X feet.
EDCS-10100906.summary: This funerary inscription records the parents' lament for their son, Quintus Plaetorius Valens, who died at 15, likely from the Roman imperial period, and specifies plot dimensions.

EDCS-10100908.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Septimio Gratiano militi legionis VII Claudiae exacto consularis vixit nnos XXIIII dies XI Genialis Augusti nostri libertus et Apuleia Salvia filio karissimo posuerunt\n
EDCS-10100908.translation: To the Divine Shades. Genialis, freedman of our Augustus, and Apuleia Salvia, placed this for their dearest son, Lucius Septimius Gratianus, a soldier of the Legio VII Claudia, who served as a consular attendant, lived 24 years and 11 days.
EDCS-10100908.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Septimius Gratianus, a soldier in Legio VII Claudia, dedicated by his freedman parents, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10100909.translation: Valerius Messor, the standard-bearer, set this up.
EDCS-10100909.summary: This short inscription records the erection of a monument by Valerius Messor, identified by his military rank of imaginifer (standard-bearer), likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10100910.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aeliae Dassiae infilicissimae qui sic vixit annos XXII menses VII diis sic V Platon \n
EDCS-10100910.translation: To the Divine Shades of Aelia Dassia, most unhappy, who lived 22 years, 7 months, and 5 days. Platon [erected this].
EDCS-10100910.summary: This funerary inscription records the premature death of Aelia Dassia at the age of 22 years, 7 months, and 5 days, dedicated by Platon, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10100911.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ingenuiae Valeriae defunctae nnorum XXV Aurelius Rufinus coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10100911.translation: To the Divine Shades of Ingenuia Valeria, deceased at 25 years. Aurelius Rufinus set this up for his deserving wife.
EDCS-10100911.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Ingenuia Valeria at the age of 25, dedicated by her husband Aurelius Rufinus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10100913.text_edition: \n Nerius Epicadus Varonis et Su nepotis filiae dulcissimae quae vixit nnos VI parentes\n
EDCS-10100913.translation: Her parents [set this up] for Nerius Epicadus, daughter of Varo and of Su..., and granddaughter of..., sweetest, who lived 6 years.
EDCS-10100913.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young girl named Nerius Epicadus, daughter of Varo and Su..., at the age of six, dedicated by her parents, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-10100914.text_edition: \n Aureliae Seioni Aurelia Maxima matri pientissimae defunctae annorum \n
EDCS-10100914.translation: To Aurelia Seiona, her most devoted mother, Aurelia Maxima, deceased at [age].
EDCS-10100914.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Aurelia Maxima to her mother, Aurelia Seiona, likely from a tombstone or a similar memorial, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100915.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Octaviae Severinae Octavia Secundina nepotiae sic\n
EDCS-10100915.translation: To the spirits of the underworld of Octavia Severina, her granddaughter Octavia Secundina.
EDCS-10100915.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by Octavia Secundina to her grandmother, Octavia Severina, indicating a family memorial, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100916.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Clevatae Clevati Licinia Vera aviae bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10100916.translation: To Clevata, her well-deserving grandmother, Clevati Licinia Vera placed this.
EDCS-10100916.summary: This funerary inscription records that Clevati Licinia Vera dedicated this monument to her deserving grandmother, Clevata, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100919.translation: To a veteran of the Twenty-second Legion Primigenia.
EDCS-10100919.summary: This fragmented inscription likely identifies a veteran of the Twenty-second Legion Primigenia, possibly part of a tombstone or public record, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100922.text_edition: \n AI Viatori nnorum XV menssium sic IIII bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10100922.translation: To Viator, aged fifteen years and four months, placed this [monument] for him, who deserved well.
EDCS-10100922.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication to Viator, who died at the age of fifteen years and four months, likely a tombstone from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100926.text_edition: \n Binius Buri filius sibi et suis fecit locum privatum\n
EDCS-10100926.translation: Binius Buri son made this private place for himself and his family.
EDCS-10100926.summary: This inscription records Binius Buri son dedicating a private burial plot for himself and his family, likely a funerary monument from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100928.text_edition: \n Aureliae Ulpiae Aurelius Candidus coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-10100928.translation: To Aurelia Ulpia, Aurelius Candidus [erected this] for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-10100928.summary: This funerary dedication by Aurelius Candidus to his deserving wife, Aurelia Ulpia, is likely from a tombstone or memorial, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100946.text_edition: \n Annius Lucianus Aurelia Secundina Annio Lucio marito bene merenti posuit qui vixit annos LXXXX\n
EDCS-10100946.translation: Annius Lucianus and Aurelia Secundina placed this for their well-deserving husband [husband of Aurelia Secundina], Annius Lucius, who lived for 90 years.
EDCS-10100946.summary: This inscription records that Annius Lucianus and Aurelia Secundina dedicated a monument to Annius Lucius, husband of Aurelia Secundina, who lived to 90, likely a tombstone from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100947.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Plotius Maximus homo infelicissimus qui vixit nnos plus minus XL Argentia uxor ipsius con sic quo vixit annos XX \n
EDCS-10100947.translation: To the spirits of the underworld of Plotius Maximus, a most unfortunate man, who lived about 40 years. Argentia, his wife, herself [placed this], who lived for 20 years.
EDCS-10100947.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Plotius Maximus, who died around 40 years old, and his wife Argentia, who died at 20, likely from a tombstone from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100948.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Valerius Maximus heres OCI \n
EDCS-10100948.translation: To the spirits of the underworld of Gaius Valerius Maximus, his heir [placed this].
EDCS-10100948.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication by the heir to Gaius Valerius Maximus, likely from a tombstone or memorial, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100949.text_edition: \n Am Lucia bene merenti vixit annos LX CI\n
EDCS-10100949.translation: To Lucia, who deserved well, she lived 60 years.
EDCS-10100949.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucia, who lived 60 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100952.text_edition: \n Antheo sacrum Thallus Campani servus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10100952.translation: To Antheos, sacred. Thallus, slave of Campanus, gladly paid his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-10100952.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Antheos by his slave Thallus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100953.text_edition: \n Genio coloniae Martiae Iuliae Salonitanorum Attalus Aquilinae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10100953.translation: To the Genius of the colony of Martia Julia Salonitana, Attalus of Aquilina gladly paid his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-10100953.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Genius of the colony of Martia Julia Salonitana by Attalus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100954.text_edition: \n Genio coloniae Salonitanorum Lucius Sextilius Marullus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10100954.translation: To the Genius of the colony of Salonitana, Lucius Sextilius Marullus gladly paid his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-10100954.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the Genius of the colony of Salonitana by Lucius Sextilius Marullus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100955.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aulus Laberius Synegdemianus votum sovit libens merito\n
EDCS-10100955.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Aulus Laberius Synegdemianus gladly paid his vow, as was deserved.
EDCS-10100955.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aulus Laberius Synegdemianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100956.text_edition: \n Ex imperio domini Iovis Optimi Maximi iussit sibi aedem fieri cum suo consentio deorum dearumque silvestrium Nymphis fontanis cum Silvano Nymphis silvestrium cum Silvano Fescenia Astice cum suo pare coniuge Tito o Fausto VIviro et Augustali a solo restituit\n
EDCS-10100956.translation: By the command of the lord Jupiter Optimus Maximus, she ordered a temple to be made for herself with the consent of all the gods and goddesses, the mountain nymphs, the fountain nymphs, with Silvanus and the mountain nymphs, with Silvanus. Fescenia Astice with her husband, Titus Faustus, VIvir and Augustalis, restored it from the ground up.
EDCS-10100956.summary: This is a dedication inscription recording the restoration of a temple by Fescenia Astice and her husband Titus Faustus, a local magistrate, under divine command, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100957.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. M Dereccandis.
EDCS-10100957.summary: This is a dedication of a shrine to Silvanus Augustus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100960.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Alasinio Secundo defuncto nnorum LXXXVII Caius Alasinius Secundinus miles cohortis VIII voluntariorum duplarius Alasinia Quintina patri bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-10100960.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Caius Alasinius Secundinus, a soldier of the 8th cohort of volunteers, a duplarius, and Alasinia Quintina, placed this for their well-deserving father, Caius Alasinius Secundus, who died at the age of 87.
EDCS-10100960.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a soldier and his sister for their deceased father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100961.text_edition: \n Kapitoni defuncto nnorum XIX Attia Saturnina mater\n
EDCS-10100961.translation: To Capitō, who died at the age of 19. Attia Saturnina, mother.
EDCS-10100961.summary: This is a funerary epitaph erected by a mother for her deceased son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100962.text_edition: \n ntilla Aurelio Dassio coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10100962.translation: Attilla placed this for her well-deserving husband, Aurelius Dassius.
EDCS-10100962.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a wife for her deceased husband, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10100963.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Nereis mater Aureliae Primae nnorum XXVI Aurelio Iannuario sic nnorum XXVIII mater misera \n
EDCS-10100963.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Prima, mother, to her daughter Nereis, aged 26, and to Aurelius Iannuarius, aged 28. The grieving mother placed this.
EDCS-10100963.summary: This is a tombstone set up by a grieving mother for her deceased children, Nereis and Aurelius Iannuarius, who died at ages 26 and 28 respectively.

EDCS-10100964.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus A Veneria filio pientissimo Suacrio qui vicxit sic annos viginti infelicissimo posuit\n
EDCS-10100964.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Suacrius placed this for his most devoted son, Venerius, who lived for twenty unfortunate years.
EDCS-10100964.summary: This tombstone was erected by Suacrius for his son Venerius, who died at the age of twenty.

EDCS-10100965.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Bae Firminae infanti infelicissimae quae vixit nnos \n
EDCS-10100965.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For the most unfortunate infant Firmina, who lived [for] years.
EDCS-10100965.summary: This is a tombstone for the infant Firmina, who died at an unspecified age.

EDCS-10100967.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Clodio Liberali defuncto annorum XXV\n
EDCS-10100967.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Clodius Liberalis, deceased, aged 25.
EDCS-10100967.summary: This tombstone records the death of Clodius Liberalis, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-10100968.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Fundanio Secundo et Iuliae Laeonini sic parentibus et Caio Iulio Fundanio Caius Iulius Secundus fratri bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10100968.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Iulius Secundus placed this for his parents, Gaius Fundanius Secundus and Iulia Laenis, and for his deserving brother.
EDCS-10100968.summary: This tombstone was dedicated by Caius Iulius Secundus to his parents and his brother.

EDCS-10100969.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulia Hilara Hilaro vernaculo defuncto annorum V\n
EDCS-10100969.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Iulia Hilara, for Hilarus, a household slave, deceased, aged 5.
EDCS-10100969.summary: This tombstone commemorates the death of Hilarus, a household slave and son of Iulia Hilara, at the age of 5.

EDCS-10100970.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Maltilio Pudenti et Aureliae Maximae Maltilia Pudentilla et Maltilia Maximina sorores parentibus dulcissimis posuerunt bbene mmerentibus\n
EDCS-10100970.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Maltilia Pudentilla and Maltilia Maximina, sisters, placed this for their most beloved parents, Maltilius Pudentus and Aurelia Maxima, who deserved well.
EDCS-10100970.summary: Two sisters, Maltilia Pudentilla and Maltilia Maximina, erected this tombstone for their parents, Maltilius Pudentus and Aurelia Maxima.

EDCS-10100971.text_edition: \n collegii fabrum magistro quinquennali Publius Marcius Vitalis patri pientissimo\n
EDCS-10100971.translation: Publius Marcius Vitalis, master of the guild of artisans for five years, for his most devoted father.
EDCS-10100971.summary: This inscription honors the father of Publius Marcius Vitalis, who served as master of the guild of artisans.

EDCS-10100972.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publiciae Epictesi Quintus Publicius Paroclus collibertae et coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-10100972.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus Publicius Paroclus for his deserving freedwoman and wife, Publicia Epictesis.
EDCS-10100972.summary: Quintus Publicius Paroclus dedicated this tombstone to his freedwoman and wife, Publicia Epictesis.

EDCS-10100973.translation: For Sabinus, from Gaius Tadusius Quartio, his grandson.
EDCS-10100973.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Gaius Tadusius Quartio to his grandson, Sabinus.

EDCS-10100974.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Decimo Balerio sic Clementi Baebia Tertulla coniugi bene mernti posuit\n
EDCS-10100974.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Baebia Tertulla set this up for her deserving husband, Decimus Balerius Clementus.
EDCS-10100974.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her deceased husband, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100975.text_edition: \n Valerius Stratco Proannorum Xvixit annorum vixit annorum XL uxsor sic IENBRIA vicina titulum posuit state AMIC ST SITE ET \n
EDCS-10100975.translation: Valerius Stratco lived 15 years. His wife Ien(i)bria and a neighbor set up this monument. Stay well and be at peace.
EDCS-10100975.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and death of Valerius Stratco, set up by his wife and a neighbor, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100976.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Hilaro defuncto nnorum V mensium XI Maximus \n
EDCS-10100976.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Hilarius, deceased, aged 5 years and 11 months. Maximus [set this up].
EDCS-10100976.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young child named Hilarius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100994.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Messiae defunctae annorum XXVI Marcus Africanus Trophimus coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-10100994.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Julia Messia, deceased, aged 26 years. Marcus Africanus Trophimus [set this up] for his deserving wife.
EDCS-10100994.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his deceased wife, Julia Messia, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100998.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Nepoti defuncto nnorum XXXV Quinta conservo et Nepotiano filio infelicissimo nnorum IIII\n
EDCS-10100998.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Nepos, deceased, aged 35 years. Quinta [set this up] for her unfortunate slave and son Nepotianus, aged 4 years.
EDCS-10100998.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of an adult, Nepos, and a young child, Nepotianus, set up by Quinta, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10100999.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Salonina Suro coniugi defuncto annorum XXIIII bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10100999.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Salonina Sura, deceased, aged 24 years. [She] set this up for her deserving husband.
EDCS-10100999.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of a husband, set up by his wife Salonina Sura, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101001.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Barbaracio Hermadioni coniuci sic bene merenti \n
EDCS-10101001.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Gaius Barbaracius Hermadion, [her] deserving husband.
EDCS-10101001.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Gaius Barbaracius Hermadion, set up by his wife, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101002.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Clodio Crescenti Crescentilla fratri\n
EDCS-10101002.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Lucius Clodius Crescens. Crescentilla [set this up] for her brother.
EDCS-10101002.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Lucius Clodius Crescens, set up by his sister Crescentilla, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101003.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aprili Phoebus frater sorori \n
EDCS-10101003.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Aprilis. Phoebus [set this up] for his sister.
EDCS-10101003.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Aprilis, set up by her brother Phoebus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101004.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Securo defuncto annorum XX Stratonicus et Fusca parentes posuerunt\n
EDCS-10101004.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Securus, deceased, aged 20 years. His parents Stratonicus and Fusca set this up.
EDCS-10101004.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Securus, aged 20, set up by his parents, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101006.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Fabio Fortunato Caius verna bene merenti\n
EDCS-10101006.translation: To the Divine Spirits of the departed. Caius, a freedman, [erected this] for Lucius Fabio Fortunatus, his deserving slave.
EDCS-10101006.summary: This is a tombstone inscription dedicated to Lucius Fabio Fortunatus by his freedman Caius, likely from the early imperial period.

EDCS-10101008.text_edition: \n Deo Optimo Magno Quintus Clodius Dardanus et Veius Aurelianus nepos ipsius donum posuerunt\n
EDCS-10101008.translation: Quintus Clodius Dardanus and Veius Aurelianus, his grandson, placed this gift to the All-Powerful Great God.
EDCS-10101008.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicating a gift to a deity, likely Sol Invictus, by Quintus Clodius Dardanus and his grandson Veius Aurelianus, probably from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10101009.text_edition: \n Dis Patris sacrum Titus Tarconius Catho posuit\n
EDCS-10101009.translation: Sacred to the Underworld Gods. Titus Tarconius Catho erected this.
EDCS-10101009.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the underworld gods by Titus Tarconius Catho, likely from the early imperial period.

EDCS-10101011.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Iulius Iovinus veteranus legionis X geminae exio sacrum ex voto solvit\n
EDCS-10101011.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Caius Iulius Iovinus, a veteran of the Legio X Gemina, fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-10101011.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caius Iulius Iovinus, a veteran of the Legio X Gemina, dedicating a dedication to Jupiter, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10101013.text_edition: \n Iovi Adflatori Iunia Iucunda solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10101013.translation: To Jupiter the Inspirer, Iunia Iucunda willingly fulfilled [her vow] as deserved.
EDCS-10101013.summary: This is a votive inscription by Iunia Iucunda dedicated to Jupiter Adflator, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10101014.text_edition: \n Iovi Augusto sacrum Caius Arrius Narcissus collegio dono dedit\n
EDCS-10101014.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Augustus. Caius Arrius Narcissus dedicated this to the college.
EDCS-10101014.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Augustus by Caius Arrius Narcissus for a college or association, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10101016.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater. Curianus [dedicated this].
EDCS-10101016.summary: This is a dedication to Liber Pater by Curianus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10101018.text_edition: \n Matribus Magnis sacrum Publius Safinius Filucinus Terentiae sacerdotis filius aram supstituit sic idem ampliavit sibi et cognationi suae permissu Cai Clodi Gracilis\n
EDCS-10101018.translation: Sacred to the Great Mothers. Publius Safinius Filucinus, son of Terentia, the priestess, replaced the altar and likewise enlarged it for himself and his kin, with the permission of Caius Clodius Gracilis.
EDCS-10101018.summary: This inscription records the replacement and enlargement of an altar to the Great Mothers by Publius Safinius Filucinus, son of a priestess, with permission from Caius Clodius Gracilis, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10101020.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum Tiberius Claudius Verus ex voto reddidit\n
EDCS-10101020.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Tiberius Claudius Verus restored [this] in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-10101020.summary: This is a votive inscription by Tiberius Claudius Verus dedicated to Silvanus Augustus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10101021.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Fabius Valentinus [dedicated this].
EDCS-10101021.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus Augustus by Fabius Valentinus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10101022.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum Laberius Rufinus deo exauditus libens posuit\n
EDCS-10101022.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Laberius Rufinus, having been heard by the god, gladly set this up.
EDCS-10101022.summary: A dedication to Silvanus Augustus, set up by Laberius Rufinus, likely a private individual, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101023.translation: Sacred to the common Silvanus. Alypus, four times, set this up.
EDCS-10101023.summary: A dedication to Silvanus, possibly indicating multiple offerings or a reference to a specific group associated with the god, set up by Alypus, likely a freedman, probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101025.text_edition: \n Cleudicia Dis Triviis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10101025.translation: Cleudicia gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow to Trivia of the Crossroads.
EDCS-10101025.summary: A vow fulfilled by Cleudicia to Trivia, a goddess associated with crossroads, likely a private dedication from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101028.text_edition: \n Veneri Victrici sacrum IRE pro muni filia votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10101028.translation: Sacred to Venus Victorious. IRE, daughter of Muni, gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-10101028.summary: A vow fulfilled by IRE, daughter of Muni, to Venus Victorious, likely a private dedication from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101030.translation: Antistius Fortunius with his family made a vow.
EDCS-10101030.summary: A record of a vow made by Antistius Fortunius and his family, likely a private dedication from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101031.text_edition: \n RI ET LE AD et Aurelius Bastianus filius votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-10101031.translation: RI ET LE AD and Aurelius Bastianus, his son, gladly fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-10101031.summary: A vow fulfilled by Aurelius Bastianus and another individual or group (RI ET LE AD), likely a private dedication from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101032.summary: A very fragmentary inscription indicating that Aratus set something up, likely a private dedication from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101033.summary: The names of Marcus Luctilius Myro, likely indicating ownership or a dedication, possibly from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101036.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus A Augustus restituit Ino viro perfectissimo praeside provinciae\n
EDCS-10101036.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Augustus restored this, under Ino, a most excellent man, governor of the province.
EDCS-10101036.summary: An imperial inscription recording the restoration of a structure by Emperor Marcus Augustus, under the provincial governor Ino, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10101038.text_edition: \n Diocletiano VIIIS Maximiano VII Auggustis consulibus Aurelio Valentiniano viro perfectissimo bis praef(ecto) \n
EDCS-10101038.translation: In the consulship of Diocletian (for the 8th time) and Maximian (for the 7th time), Augusti. To Aurelius Valentinianus, a most excellent man, twice prefect.
EDCS-10101038.summary: An inscription honoring Aurelius Valentinianus, who held the office of prefect twice, during the consulship of Diocletian and Maximian, dating to the late 3rd or early 4th century CE.

EDCS-10101040.text_edition: \n M Titus Aurelius Marcellinus IIvir aed(ilicia)? pot(estate) Caio? Fulvio Maximo viro clarissimo legato Augusti pro praetore provinciae Dalmatiae\n
EDCS-10101040.translation: To Titus Aurelius Marcellinus, duovir, in magisterial or aedilitian power, [and] to Gaius Fulvius Maximus, clarissimus vir, imperial legate pro praetore of the province of Dalmatia.
EDCS-10101040.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication by Titus Aurelius Marcellinus, a local magistrate, to Gaius Fulvius Maximus, a high-ranking imperial governor, likely in the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10101046.text_edition: \n Vibius Firmus magister et quinquennalis mensam dono dedit\n
EDCS-10101046.translation: Vibius Firmus, master and quinquennalis, gave the table as a gift.
EDCS-10101046.summary: This records a gift of a table by Vibius Firmus, who held a leadership position in a collegium or association, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10101048.text_edition: \n Maceria privata Sexti Gelli Primi cum solo suo\n
EDCS-10101048.translation: Maceria privata of Sextus Gellius Primus, with its land.
EDCS-10101048.summary: This is a property description, likely for legal or administrative purposes, identifying a walled enclosure belonging to Sextus Gellius Primus, dating possibly to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-10101049.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Valerius Augustalis speculator legionis I adiutricis infelicissimo posuit Valeria Sabina patri pientissimo pio\n
EDCS-10101049.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Valerius Augustalis, speculator of the First Adjutant Legion, to his most unfortunate self, erected [this monument]. Valeria Sabina to her most pious and dutiful father.
EDCS-10101049.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Valerius Augustalis, a soldier, erected by his daughter Valeria Sabina, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10101050.text_edition: \n Ulpius Iustinus beneficiarius consularis legionis primae Italicae defunctus annorum XXX\n
EDCS-10101050.translation: Ulpius Iustinus, beneficiarius of the consular legate of the First Italian Legion, deceased at 30 years of age.
EDCS-10101050.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ulpius Iustinus, a soldier of the First Italian Legion serving as an aide to a legate, who died at age 30, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10101052.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucius Domitius Valens veteranus legionis V Macedonicae et A AFA B Caius Offonius Domiti Caius Domi ann\n
EDCS-10101052.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucius Domitius Valens, veteran of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, and A AFA B Gaius Offonius Domitius, Gaius Domitius [son]... [length of life, burial instructions].
EDCS-10101052.summary: This is a partial funerary inscription commemorating Lucius Domitius Valens, a veteran of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, and possibly another individual, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10101053.text_edition: \n Marcus Titius Marci filius Sergia domo Isinda eques legionis VII annorum XXXV stipendiorum X ex testamento fieri iussit arbitratu Luci Gelli Marci fili Sergia\n
EDCS-10101053.translation: Marcus Titius, son of Marcus, of the Sergia tribe, from Isinda, an equestrian soldier of the Seventh Legion, aged 35, with 10 years of service, ordered to be made from his will, under the supervision of Lucius Gellius, son of Marcus, of the Sergia tribe.
EDCS-10101053.summary: This is a funerary inscription specifying that Marcus Titius, an equestrian soldier of the Seventh Legion, left instructions in his will for the monument to be erected, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10101054.text_edition: \n Lucius Arruntius domo Pasimoae sic nnrum LV veteranus? legionis VII hic situs est testamento fieri iussit \n
EDCS-10101054.translation: Lucius Arruntius, from Pasimoae, aged 55, a veteran, perhaps of the Seventh Legion, is buried here; he ordered to be made from his will.
EDCS-10101054.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Arruntius, a veteran, who specified in his will that this monument be erected, likely in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-10101055.text_edition: \n Caius Manticus Cai filius Scaptia miles legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis stipendiorum XXIIII Caius Avinnius Postumius s \n
EDCS-10101055.translation: Caius Manticus, son of Caius, of the Scaptia tribe, a soldier of the Seventh Claudian Legion, pious and faithful, with 24 years of service. Caius Avinnius Postumius [ordered this].
EDCS-10101055.summary: This inscription records Caius Manticus, a soldier of the Seventh Claudian Legion, likely with details of his service, and possibly indicates Caius Avinnius Postumius as the dedicator, probably in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10101056.text_edition: \n signifer legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis Caius Clodius Felix libertus IIIIIIvir Augustalis\n
EDCS-10101056.translation: Caius Clodius Felix, standard-bearer of the Seventh Claudian Legion, pious and faithful, freedman, and member of the college of four magistrates (Augustales).
EDCS-10101056.summary: This inscription identifies Caius Clodius Felix, a freedman holding a significant religious and civic office, and also serving as a standard-bearer in the Seventh Claudian Legion, likely in the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-10101058.text_edition: \n Lucio Barbio veterano legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis et Quintiae matri Barbia Paulla posuit et Silvinae carissimae ARANm facvinae XIII TE \n
EDCS-10101058.translation: To Lucius Barbius, veteran of the eleventh Claudian legion, pious and faithful, and to Quintia, mother. Barbia Paula erected this for him. And for Silvina, most beloved, Ar(a)n(o?)m, devoted for 13 years.
EDCS-10101058.summary: A funerary monument erected by Barbia Paula for her husband Lucius Barbius, a veteran, and her mother Quintia, and also for Silvina.

EDCS-10101062.text_edition: \n sia Sexti filius Sec Iulio Sec nnorum XXX stipendiorum XVII T IETSS L bene merenti mille C \n
EDCS-10101062.translation: To Sia, son of Sextus, Secundus, aged 30 years, with 17 years of service. To Titus Ie[t]essus, a man well deserving, 1000 [sestertii?] for his good service.
EDCS-10101062.summary: A funerary dedication to Sia Secundus, a soldier with 17 years of service, likely including a monetary bequest.

EDCS-10101064.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelia Messiana Aurelio Saturnino ex |(centurione) ex castris Batavum sic co<m=N>pari inco<m=N>parabili qui militavit annos XXVIII et mecum vixit nns XXXVII arcam posuit et sibi \n
EDCS-10101064.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aelia Messiana erected this tomb for Aurelius Saturninus, former centurion from the Batavian camp, incomparable, who served 28 years and lived with me for 37 years, and for herself.
EDCS-10101064.summary: A tomb erected by Aelia Messiana for her husband Aurelius Saturninus, a former centurion, and herself.

EDCS-10101065.text_edition: \n Publio Aelio Proculino veterano Aelia Proculina filia et Aelius Urbanus libertus et heres bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-10101065.translation: To Publius Aelius Proculinus, a veteran. Aelia Proculina, daughter, and Aelius Urbanus, freedman and heir, erected this for him, a man well deserving.
EDCS-10101065.summary: A funerary monument for Publius Aelius Proculinus, a veteran, erected by his daughter and his freedman and heir.

EDCS-10101066.translation: To Marcus Cam, from [his] home, 10 years old.
EDCS-10101066.summary: A brief funerary inscription for a young boy named Marcus Cam.

EDCS-10101067.text_edition: \n ara ulio t Quintus Ignienus Quinti filius Aniensis Ferox domo Troade\n
EDCS-10101067.translation: Altar to the gods. Quintus Ignienus, son of Quintus, of the Aniensis tribe, Ferox, from Troas.
EDCS-10101067.summary: A dedication of an altar by Quintus Ignienus Ferox.

EDCS-10101068.text_edition: \n Gaio Nertonio Sperato veterano ex decurione ex Dacia vixit annis LV Gaius Nertonius Philocyrius et Gaius Nertonius Chrysanthus liberti patrono posuerunt\n
EDCS-10101068.translation: To Gaius Nertionius Speratus, veteran, former decurion from Dacia, lived 55 years. Gaius Nertonius Philocyrius and Gaius Nertonius Chrysanthus, freedmen, erected this for their patron.
EDCS-10101068.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Nertionius Speratus, a veteran and former decurion, erected by his freedmen.

EDCS-10101069.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus ? Caerelleo sic Museo militi c<l=T>assis praetoriae Antoninianae Misenatium IIIIquadriremi Venere Victoriacare \n
EDCS-10101069.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. To Caerellius Museo, soldier of the praetorian fleet Antoniniana of Misenum, serving on a quadrireme named Victory.
EDCS-10101069.summary: A funerary dedication for Museo, a soldier of the Misenum fleet.

EDCS-10101071.text_edition: \n Marco Ulpio Marci filio Sabino equo publico decurioni coloniae Salonitanorum IIviro iure dicundo praefecto bis collegii fabrum et centonariorum patrono collegii supra scripti ob industriam adque sic singularem eius innocentiam et integritatem defensionemque ex aere collato collegium supra scriptum\n
EDCS-10101071.translation: To Marcus Ulpius Sabinus, son of Marcus, on public equestrian rank, decurion of the colony of Salona, twice chief magistrate, prefect twice of the guild of craftsmen and builders, patron of the aforementioned guild, the aforementioned guild erected this from a joint contribution for his industry and singular innocence, integrity, and defense.
EDCS-10101071.summary: A monument erected by a guild to honor Marcus Ulpius Sabinus, a prominent local official and patron of the guild, for his service and integrity.

EDCS-10101075.text_edition: \n Lucius Curius Luci libertus Sosibius IIIIIIvir Augustalis sibi et Iuliae Scepsidi uxori testamento fieri iussit arbitratu Iuliae Scepsidis in fronte pedes XX in agro pedes XXX\n
EDCS-10101075.translation: Lucius Curius Sosibius, freedman of Lucius, IVvir Augustalis, ordered this to be made for himself and for his wife Iulia Scepsis by his will, under the arbitration of Iulia Scepsis. Frontage 20 feet, depth in the field 30 feet.
EDCS-10101075.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Lucius Curius Sosibius for himself and his wife, specifying dimensions and arbitration.

EDCS-10101078.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Prota<g=C>orae L AG PP notario et opsonatori Felicissima coniugi piissimo fecit \n
EDCS-10101078.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Protagora, notary and provisioner, erected this for her most devoted wife, Felicissima.
EDCS-10101078.summary: A funerary epitaph for Felicissima, erected by her wife Protagora, who was a notary and provisioner.

EDCS-10101079.summary: This inscription likely refers to the title or occupation of an individual, possibly a funerary marker or a title on a building.

EDCS-10101080.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Primo Caesaris servo librario \n
EDCS-10101080.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Primo, a scribe, slave of Caesar.
EDCS-10101080.summary: A funerary epitaph for Primo, a scribe and slave of Caesar.

EDCS-10101081.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Renover dispensatoris Augusti vicarius defunctus annorum XXX et Aeliae Augusti libertae defunctae annorum XXVII Aelia Eutiches! liberta Augusti filiae et genero\n
EDCS-10101081.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Renover, freedman of Augustus, a vicarius (deputy) of the procurator, who died at 30 years of age, and for Aelia Augusta Liberta, who died at 27 years of age, daughter of Aelia Eutiches, freedwoman of Augustus, and her son-in-law.
EDCS-10101081.summary: A funerary epitaph for Renover and Aelia Augusta Liberta, with mentions of their familial connections and approximate ages at death.

EDCS-10101082.text_edition: \n l Augusti libertus Magnus alumnae piissimae erga se bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-10101082.translation: Magnus, freedman of Augustus, erected this for his most devoted alumna, who deserved well of him.
EDCS-10101082.summary: An epitaph recording the dedication of Magnus, a freedman of Augustus, to his devoted alumna.

EDCS-10101083.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Aelio Quintiano filio dulcissimo defuncto nnrum VIIII dierum XXXIIII mater pientissima Dexsia sic Proba\n
EDCS-10101083.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Titus Aelius Quintianus, my dearest son, who died aged 9 years and 34 days. His most devoted mother, Dexsia, wife of Probus, [erected this].
EDCS-10101083.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Aelius Quintianus, erected by his mother Dexsia, detailing his age at death.

EDCS-10101084.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aemilio Rufo et Se Rufae \n
EDCS-10101084.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Aemilius Rufus and Sevia Rufa.
EDCS-10101084.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicating the memorial to Aemilius Rufus and Sevia Rufa.

EDCS-10101086.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Armeniae Alexandriae Claudius Proculus heres bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101086.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudius Proculus, the heir, set this up for his well-deserving Armenia Alexandria.
EDCS-10101086.summary: A funerary epitaph where Claudius Proculus, the heir, placed the monument for Armenia Alexandria.

EDCS-10101087.text_edition: \n Aurelio Aeneae coniugi incomparabili natione Graeco <q=CL>ui vixit annos XXVII posuit Cassia Decorata coniux virginio suo bene merenti \n
EDCS-10101087.translation: Cassia Decorata erected this for her incomparable husband Aurelius Aeneas, a Greek by birth, who lived 27 years.
EDCS-10101087.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelius Aeneas, a Greek who lived 27 years, erected by his wife Cassia Decorata.

EDCS-10101088.text_edition: \n Aurelio Anthigono bene merenti co<ll=II>(egae saciicariorum posuerunt \n
EDCS-10101088.translation: Colleagues of the sacrificers erected this for Aurelius Anthigonus, who deserved well of them.
EDCS-10101088.summary: An inscription recording the dedication by colleagues of the sacrificers to Aurelius Anthigonus.

EDCS-10101089.text_edition: \n Aureliae Ferocillae in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XVI\n
EDCS-10101089.translation: For Aurelia Ferocilla, 12 feet in front and 16 feet in the field.
EDCS-10101089.summary: This inscription likely records the dimensions of a plot of land, possibly a tomb, belonging to Aurelia Ferocilla.

EDCS-10101090.text_edition: \n Aurelio Maximino ITLV qui vixit ter denus sic annos inplevit huic infelici hunc sar<c=T>ofacum sic posuit Aur(elius) Eutichianus sic nutritor \n
EDCS-10101090.translation: To Aurelius Maximinus, who lived thirty years, his foster father Aurelius Eutichianus set up this unhappy sarcophagus.
EDCS-10101090.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelius Maximinus, set up by his foster father, specifying his age at death and the object placed.

EDCS-10101092.summary: This inscription likely denotes a funerary plot or land belonging to Marcus Aurelius Polyvenus, measuring 20 feet.

EDCS-10101094.text_edition: \n innocenti Aurelius Titus bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101094.translation: Aurelius Titus set this up for the deserving innocent.
EDCS-10101094.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dedicated by Aurelius Titus to an 'innocent' person who was well-deserving.

EDCS-10101095.text_edition: \n Aureliae Ursinae filiae piissimae adque infelicissimae quae vixit nnos XVIII menses VIII Aurelius Ursinus et Iulia Eucarpia parentes bene merenti posuerunt et sibi\n
EDCS-10101095.translation: For Aurelia Ursina, his most dutiful and most unfortunate daughter, who lived 18 years and 8 months. Aurelius Ursinus and Iulia Eucarpia, her parents, set this up for the well-deserving, and for themselves.
EDCS-10101095.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Ursina, detailing her age and the inscription being set up by her parents, who also included themselves.

EDCS-10101097.text_edition: \n Aureliae Valentinae puellae infelicissimae quae vixit annnos plus minus XX Aelius Valentio pater filiae dulcissimae posuit\n
EDCS-10101097.translation: To Aurelia Valentina, most unfortunate girl, who lived more or less 20 years. Her father Aelius Valentio set this up for his sweetest daughter.
EDCS-10101097.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Valentina, erected by her father, stating her approximate age at death and her relationship to the dedicator.

EDCS-10101098.text_edition: \n tycho Aurelius Victorinus patronus bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101098.translation: To Tyco Aurelius Victorinus, patron, set this up for the well-deserving.
EDCS-10101098.summary: This funerary inscription records a well-deserving individual named Tyco Aurelius Victorinus, referred to as a patron, for whom someone erected the monument.

EDCS-10101099.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, for Lucius Baebidius Primitus.
EDCS-10101099.summary: This is a dedication to the chthonic deities (Dis Manibus) for Lucius Baebidius Primitus, indicating a funerary context.

EDCS-10101100.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Hilarae defunctae nnorum II mensium II Baebius Saloninus\n
EDCS-10101100.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, for Hilara, deceased, 2 years and 2 months old. Baebius Saloninus.
EDCS-10101100.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld for a child named Hilara, recorded by Baebius Saloninus and detailing her age.

EDCS-10101101.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Basidius Philumenus Saccidiae Primitivae \n
EDCS-10101101.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, for Basidius Philumenus and Saccidia Primitiva.
EDCS-10101101.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Spirits of the Underworld (Dis Manibus) for Basidius Philumenus and Saccidia Primitiva, likely a joint funerary monument.

EDCS-10101102.text_edition: \n Bellius Crescens Cerenio Grato amico carissimo bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101102.translation: Bellius Crescens set this up for his dearest friend, the well-deserving Cerenius Gratus.
EDCS-10101102.summary: A funerary or commemorative dedication by Bellius Crescens to his friend Cerenius Gratus.

EDCS-10101103.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Benniusnticus Eucarpiae matri et Valeriae Respectillae sorori et \n
EDCS-10101103.translation: To the departed spirits: Benniusnticus for Eucarpia his mother and Valeria Respectilla his sister.
EDCS-10101103.summary: A funerary dedication by Benniusnticus to his mother and sister.

EDCS-10101105.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caecili Acindyni qui in codicillis suis pon inscribi iussit cos st \n
EDCS-10101105.translation: To the departed spirits: Caecilius Acindynus, who ordered that this be inscribed in his codicils.
EDCS-10101105.summary: A funerary dedication by Caecilius Acindynus, intended to be inscribed after his death as per his will.

EDCS-10101107.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caetenio Satriano qui vixit annos XI \n
EDCS-10101107.translation: To the departed spirits of Caetenius Satrianus, who lived for 11 years.
EDCS-10101107.summary: A funerary dedication to Caetenius Satrianus, a child who died at age 11.

EDCS-10101108.text_edition: \n Cannutiae Crescentillae defunctae annorum XXXVI Vibius Anicetus coniugi incomparabili\n
EDCS-10101108.translation: Vibius Anicetus for his incomparable wife, Cannutia Crescentilla, who died at 36 years of age.
