EDCS-10101108.summary: A funerary dedication by Vibius Anicetus to his wife, Cannutia Crescentilla.

EDCS-10101110.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Casinia Hermione c heres C D \n
EDCS-10101110.translation: To the departed spirits of Casinia Hermione; her heir C D [restored].
EDCS-10101110.summary: A funerary dedication to Casinia Hermione, possibly set up by her heir.

EDCS-10101111.text_edition: \n Cassiae Flaviae defunctae annorum VI Mettia Placidia nepotiae infelicissimae I\n
EDCS-10101111.translation: Mettia Placidia for her dearest granddaughter Cassia Flavia, who died at 6 years of age.
EDCS-10101111.summary: A funerary dedication by Mettia Placidia to her granddaughter Cassia Flavia, who died young.

EDCS-10101112.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cassio Marcellino infelicissimo defuncto nnorum VII Cassius Marcellinus pater posuit\n
EDCS-10101112.translation: To the departed spirits of Cassius Marcellinus, most unfortunate, who died at 7 years of age; his father Cassius Marcellinus set this up.
EDCS-10101112.summary: A funerary dedication by Cassius Marcellinus to his son, also named Cassius Marcellinus, who died at age 7.

EDCS-10101113.text_edition: \n Chrys Chrys filius Rhod Callityche uxor \n
EDCS-10101113.translation: Chrys, son of Rhod; Callityche his wife.
EDCS-10101113.summary: A funerary dedication listing Chrys, his father Rhod (implied), and his wife Callityche.

EDCS-10101115.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Clodiae Secundinae defunctae annorum II dierum XXX Clodia Firmina et Aurelius Secundus parentes\n
EDCS-10101115.translation: To the departed spirits of Clodia Secundina, who died at 2 years and 30 days; her parents Clodia Firmina and Aurelius Secundus.
EDCS-10101115.summary: A funerary dedication by parents Clodia Firmina and Aurelius Secundus to their daughter Clodia Secundina, who died in infancy.

EDCS-10101116.text_edition: \n Coelia Arescusa Primitivae libertae bene merenti\n
EDCS-10101116.translation: To Coelia Arescusa, for her well-deserving freedwoman Primitiva.
EDCS-10101116.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Coelia Arescusa for her freedwoman Primitiva, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101118.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Corneliae Chrysidi Cornelius Didymus coniugi optumae sic cum qua vixi annis XXX\n
EDCS-10101118.translation: To the Divine Shades. Cornelius Didymus to his most excellent wife Cornelia Chrysidis, with whom I lived for 30 years.
EDCS-10101118.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Cornelius Didymus to his wife Cornelia Chrysidis, recording their 30 years of marriage, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101119.text_edition: \n <S=C>er Cornelio Clementi Petronia Secunda mater et Paulla s<o=E>ror infelix mater quem dilexi vixit annos XXXI vitae praesidium meae \n
EDCS-10101119.translation: To our Ser... Cornelius Clemens, his mother Petronia Secunda and his unfortunate sister Paulla, whom I loved, my life's protection, lived 31 years.
EDCS-10101119.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Cornelius Clemens, set up by his mother and sister, lamenting his life of 31 years and calling him their protection, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101120.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Corneliae Euhodiae Aurelius gi\n
EDCS-10101120.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aurelius... to Cornelia Euhodia.
EDCS-10101120.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Aurelius to Cornelia Euhodia, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101121.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Egnatia Quinta Egnatiae Secundae filiae \n
EDCS-10101121.translation: To the Divine Shades. Egnatia Quinta to her daughter Egnatia Secunda.
EDCS-10101121.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Egnatia Quinta to her daughter Egnatia Secunda, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101122.text_edition: \n Fabia M viva fecit sibi et Buccioni et Fabiae N et Fabiae My et Tiberio Claudio nepoti aditu in fronte pedes VI in agro pedes \n
EDCS-10101122.translation: Fabia M made this for herself and Buccioni and Fabia N and Fabia My and her grandson Tiberius Claudius, with 6 feet of access in front and [number illegible] feet in the field.
EDCS-10101122.summary: This is a funerary monument self-funded by Fabia M for herself and family members, specifying property dimensions, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101123.text_edition: \n Posphoro mensium XI Titus Flavius Alexander et Horea nepoti\n
EDCS-10101123.translation: For Posphorus, 11 months. Titus Flavius Alexander and Horea [for their] grandson.
EDCS-10101123.summary: This inscription records the infant Posphorus, who lived 11 months, dedicated by Titus Flavius Alexander and Horea, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101125.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus F Flavio Ianuario Gobo fratri karissimo \n
EDCS-10101125.translation: To the Divine Shades. Gobo to his dearest brother Flavius Ianuarius.
EDCS-10101125.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Gobo to his brother Flavius Ianuarius, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101126.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavia Septimia Flavius ci \n
EDCS-10101126.translation: To the Divine Shades. Flavius... to Flavia Septimia.
EDCS-10101126.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Flavius to Flavia Septimia, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101129.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Aprili Caius Iulius Tacitus patri bene merenti\n
EDCS-10101129.translation: To the Divine Shades. Caius Julius Tacitus to his well-deserving father Gaius Julius Aprilis.
EDCS-10101129.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Caius Julius Tacitus to his father Caius Julius Aprilis, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101130.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulius Asclepiodotus et Valeria Valentina filio Iulio annuclo mensium V posuerunt\n
EDCS-10101130.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Julius Asclepiodotus and Valeria Valentina placed this for their son Julius, aged five months.
EDCS-10101130.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of a young child, set up by his parents.

EDCS-10101131.text_edition: \n ------]IDIAI Firmo Iulia Clementina coniugi et sibi\n
EDCS-10101131.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Iulia Clementina for Firminus [---]dia and for herself.
EDCS-10101131.summary: A funerary inscription, partially restored, likely for a husband and wife, dedicated by the wife.

EDCS-10101132.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulio Crescenti Armenia Alexandria marito optimo bene merenti\n
EDCS-10101132.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Julius Crescentes, Armenia Alexandria, her best and well-deserving husband.
EDCS-10101132.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by a wife to her deceased husband, praising his virtues.

EDCS-10101133.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Iulius Dionisius sic et Caius Iulius Pamphilus II \n
EDCS-10101133.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Julius Dionisius, and Gaius Julius Pamphilus the Younger.
EDCS-10101133.summary: A funerary inscription for two individuals, likely family members, given the repeated names.

EDCS-10101134.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulius Faustinianus Iul VCO \n
EDCS-10101134.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Julius Faustinianus, Iul [---] VCO.
EDCS-10101134.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription for Julius Faustinianus, with an incomplete dedication.

EDCS-10101135.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Iulia Messa, mother.
EDCS-10101135.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to a mother by her child.

EDCS-10101136.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iuliae Nicini defunctae nnorum XIX mensium V dierum I Herculianus \n
EDCS-10101136.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Iulia Nicini, deceased, aged nineteen years, five months, and [---] days. I. Herculianus [placed this].
EDCS-10101136.summary: A funerary inscription for a young woman named Iulia Nicini, detailing her age and likely set up by an individual named Herculianus.

EDCS-10101137.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulia Panthia Veneriae ancillae \n
EDCS-10101137.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Iulia Panthia, the slave woman Veneria.
EDCS-10101137.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by a slave named Veneria to another slave named Iulia Panthia.

EDCS-10101138.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Soteridi Iulia Tertia matri bene merenti\n
EDCS-10101138.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Iulia Soteris, Iulia Tertia, her best and well-deserving mother.
EDCS-10101138.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by a daughter to her deceased mother, praising her virtues.

EDCS-10101139.text_edition: \n ELIE Caio Iulio Turboni Caio Iulio Magno filiis hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur Alexsander sic\n
EDCS-10101139.translation: For Gaius Julius Turbon, Gaius Julius Magnus, sons. Alexander [placed this]; this monument will not follow an heir.
EDCS-10101139.summary: A funerary inscription for two sons, Gaius Julius Turbon and Gaius Julius Magnus, set up by Alexander, with a clause about inheritance.

EDCS-10101140.translation: To the departed spirits of Julia Veneria.
EDCS-10101140.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Julia Veneria.

EDCS-10101141.text_edition: \n Firmus et Atticilla Publi Iulsimi si mea fata fuissent bona hic lapis in titulo qui premet hossua sic mea aequius iste lapis patri super hossua sic iaceret\n
EDCS-10101141.translation: Firmus and Atticilla to Publius Iulsimius: if my fate had been good, this stone would press down the grave mound here as is right; thus this stone would lie over my father on the grave mound.
EDCS-10101141.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the 1st century CE, records a dispute over burial placement, written by Firmus and Atticilla.

EDCS-10101142.text_edition: \n Patri dulcissimo Iunius Lupulus et Cylnia Lupula coniunx bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-10101142.translation: Iunius Lupulus and his wife Cylnia Lupula erected this for their most deserving father.
EDCS-10101142.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Iunius Lupulus and Cylnia Lupula for their father.

EDCS-10101143.text_edition: \n Marcus Licinius Marci filius Dignus et Liciniae Marci filiae Proculae Caius Licinius et Lucius Licinius patrui titulum posuerunt in fronte pedes XV in agro pedes XV aditum ad sepulchrum latum pedes IIII\n
EDCS-10101143.translation: Marcus Licinius, son of Marcus, Dignus, and Licinia Procula, daughter of Marcus, had this tombstone erected by Caius Licinius and Lucius Licinius, their paternal uncles. In front, 15 feet; in the field, 15 feet. The approach to the tomb is 4 feet wide.
EDCS-10101143.summary: This funerary inscription records the dimensions of a tomb and identifies the deceased, Marcus Licinius Dignus and Licinia Procula, with their uncles Caius and Lucius Licinius as the erectors.

EDCS-10101144.text_edition: \n Lollia Victorina Lollio Calistomeno coniugi piissimo\n
EDCS-10101144.translation: Lollia Victorina to her most devoted husband, Lollio Calistomenus.
EDCS-10101144.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Lollia Victorina to her husband, Lollio Calistomenus.

EDCS-10101145.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Longiniae Tertullae Octavius Valens maritus et Octavius Tertullinus filius bene merenti\n
EDCS-10101145.translation: To the departed spirits of Longinia Tertulla, to the most deserving, erected by Octavius Valens, her husband, and Octavius Tertullinus, her son.
EDCS-10101145.summary: This funerary monument honors Longinia Tertulla and was erected by her husband, Octavius Valens, and son, Octavius Tertullinus.

EDCS-10101146.text_edition: \n Publius Mescenius Dorus emit monimentum de Cincia Placida sibi et Statiae Epiteuxi uxori et suis in fronte pedes XIIX in agro pedes XIIX\n
EDCS-10101146.translation: Publius Mescenius Dorus bought this monument from Cincia Placidus for himself and his wife, Statia Epiteuxa, and his family. In front, 18 feet; in the field, 18 feet.
EDCS-10101146.summary: This funerary inscription records the purchase of a burial plot by Publius Mescenius Dorus for himself, his wife Statia Epiteuxa, and their family, including its dimensions.

EDCS-10101147.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Mescenio Secundino fratri et Mesceniae Postuminae sorori et Lucio Cornificio Postumino nepoti et Lucio Cornificio Parthenio adfini Mescenia Rufina\n
EDCS-10101147.translation: To the departed spirits of Publius Mescenius Secundinus, brother; and Mescenia Postumina, sister; and Lucius Cornificius Postuminus, nephew; and Lucius Cornificius Parthenius, relative; erected by Mescenia Rufina.
EDCS-10101147.summary: This funerary monument honors multiple family members and a relative, erected by Mescenia Rufina.

EDCS-10101148.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Paetronio Aerasino sic defuncto nnorum XXXI \n
EDCS-10101148.translation: To the departed spirits of Paetronius Aerasinus, who died at the age of 31 years.
EDCS-10101148.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Paetronius Aerasinus at the age of 31.

EDCS-10101149.text_edition: \n Lucio Plotio Ursioni defuncto annorum XXX mensium VII Plotia Prima filio pientissimo\n
EDCS-10101149.translation: To Lucius Plotius Ursio, deceased, aged 30 years and 7 months, erected by Plotia Prima, his most dutiful son.
EDCS-10101149.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Lucius Plotius Ursio at the age of 30 years and 7 months, erected by his son Plotia Prima.

EDCS-10101150.text_edition: \n Statio Eutico Statia Artemisia bene merenti coniugi\n
EDCS-10101150.translation: Statio Eutico to Statia Artemisia, his deserving wife.
EDCS-10101150.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Statio Eutico to his wife, Statia Artemisia.

EDCS-10101151.translation: Statia Marcellina dedicated this to Secunda.
EDCS-10101151.summary: This funerary inscription records Statia Marcellina dedicating a monument to Secunda.

EDCS-10101152.text_edition: \n Marcus Titius Verna Fortunato alumno infelicissimo set sic et sibi vivo posuit et libertis libertabus suis\n
EDCS-10101152.translation: Marcus Titius Verna dedicated this to his most unfortunate freedman Fortunatus, but nevertheless, also to himself while alive, and to his freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-10101152.summary: This funerary epitaph is set up by Marcus Titius Verna for his freedman Fortunatus and for himself and his freed dependents.

EDCS-10101153.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caius Tafius.
EDCS-10101153.summary: This is a standard funerary dedication to the gods of the underworld by Caius Tafius.

EDCS-10101154.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus oni DATNIAT Tarconio Diadumeno coniugi pientissimo\n
EDCS-10101154.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Tarconius Diadumenus, her most devoted husband.
EDCS-10101154.summary: This funerary epitaph, beginning with the standard invocation to the Manes, is dedicated to Tarconius Diadumenus by his wife.

EDCS-10101155.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Tarconius Fortunatus Tarconiae Lupercae bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101155.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Tarconius Fortunatus dedicated this to Tarconia Luperca, his deserving wife.
EDCS-10101155.summary: This funerary monument records Titus Tarconius Fortunatus dedicating it to his wife, Tarconia Luperca.

EDCS-10101156.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caeteneniae Luciferae Caetenia Vibia amica\n
EDCS-10101156.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caetenia Vibia to her friend Caetenenia Lucifera.
EDCS-10101156.summary: This funerary inscription records Caetenia Vibia dedicating a monument to her friend, Caetenenia Lucifera.

EDCS-10101157.text_edition: \n Quintus Terentius Seleucianus vivus fecit sibi in agro pedes XXV in fronte pedes et Vipsaniae sibi et INPA SIN\n
EDCS-10101157.translation: Quintus Terentius Seleucianus made this while alive for himself, 25 feet in length and 5 feet in front, and for Vipsania, for himself, and...
EDCS-10101157.summary: This is a self-funded funerary monument by Quintus Terentius Seleucianus, also intended for Vipsania and others, specifying dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-10101159.text_edition: \n ------] Caius Tossius Cai filius Tossia Cai liberta Thalassia Tossiae Primigeniae filiae carissimae \n
EDCS-10101159.translation: ... Caius Tossius, son of Caius, to Tossia Thalassia, freedwoman of Caius, daughter of Tossia, his dearest daughter.
EDCS-10101159.summary: This fragmented funerary epitaph records Caius Tossius dedicating a monument to his daughter, Tossia Thalassia.

EDCS-10101160.text_edition: \n Trosiae Celerinae filiae infelici nnorum mensium VII MA \n
EDCS-10101160.translation: To Trosia Celerina, an unfortunate daughter, 7 months old...
EDCS-10101160.summary: This fragmented funerary epitaph records the death of Trosia Celerina, who died at the age of seven months.

EDCS-10101163.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus riae Crisidi filiae quae vixit annos IIII menses dies IXX Iulia Popilia filiae bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101163.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Ria Crisida, daughter, who lived 4 years, 19 days. Iulia Popilia placed this for her deserving daughter.
EDCS-10101163.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young girl named Ria Crisida, set up by her mother, Iulia Popilia.

EDCS-10101164.summary: A simple funerary epitaph or dedication, naming Gaius Valerius Dius.

EDCS-10101165.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Derm puero annorum mensium III dierum VIII Valerius Euporistus pater et Valeria mater filio infelicissimo\n
EDCS-10101165.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Derm, a boy of 3 months and 8 days. Valerius Euporistus, father, and Valeria, mother, for their most unfortunate son.
EDCS-10101165.summary: A funerary epitaph for a very young child named Derm, erected by his grieving parents, Valerius Euporistus and Valeria.

EDCS-10101167.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vallio Primitivo def(uncto) an(norum) \n
EDCS-10101167.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Vallius Primitivus, deceased, aged [---].
EDCS-10101167.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vallius Primitivus, recording his death and incomplete age.

EDCS-10101168.summary: A funerary dedication by Publius Vibius Victor for Egnatia.

EDCS-10101169.text_edition: \n ius Manertus - Laelia uxor - CV fieri iussit\n
EDCS-10101169.translation: [For] Manertus. Laelia, his wife, ordered this to be made.
EDCS-10101169.summary: A funerary epitaph where Laelia commissioned the monument for her husband, Manertus.

EDCS-10101170.text_edition: \n ius Vindemiator coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101170.translation: [For] Vindemiator, placed this for his deserving wife.
EDCS-10101170.summary: A funerary epitaph where Vindemiator dedicated the monument to his deserving wife.

EDCS-10101171.text_edition: \n etoCalliope mater infelicissima dulcissimo et piissimo natus Salonis defuncto Monte Gargano\n
EDCS-10101171.translation: [For] Calliope, his most unfortunate mother. Born in Salonis, deceased on Monte Gargano.
EDCS-10101171.summary: A funerary epitaph for Calliope, described as a mother, originating from Salonis and dying on Monte Gargano.

EDCS-10101172.text_edition: \n Pater et mater Abascanto filio bene merito\n
EDCS-10101172.translation: Father and mother for their well-deserving son, Abascantus.
EDCS-10101172.summary: A funerary epitaph from parents for their deserving son, Abascantus.

EDCS-10101173.translation: For Alexander. Stratonice and Aglaus for their son.
EDCS-10101173.summary: A funerary epitaph by Stratonice and Aglaus for their son, Alexander.

EDCS-10101174.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Candidiano qui vixit annos III Larga mater \n
EDCS-10101174.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Candidianus, who lived for 3 years. Larga, his mother.
EDCS-10101174.summary: A tombstone dedicated by a mother to her son, Candidianus, who died at the age of three.

EDCS-10101175.text_edition: \n Capitoni qui vixit annis plus minus XXX Veneria conservo bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101175.translation: To Capito, who lived about 30 years. Veneria set this up for her deserving freedman.
EDCS-10101175.summary: A tombstone set up by Veneria for her freedman, Capito, who died around the age of thirty.

EDCS-10101176.text_edition: \n Chinnamis Luci Enni Clementis contubernalis bene merenti posuit hunc titulum accepi supremi temporis a te mandatu posui sit levis iste tibi\n
EDCS-10101176.translation: Chinnamis, companion of Lucius Ennius Clementis, set this monument up for him as deserving well; I received this inscription from you at your final moment, and I have placed it; may it lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10101176.summary: An epitaph for Chinnamis, companion of Lucius Ennius Clementis, erected by a friend or associate who was entrusted with the task at the moment of death.

EDCS-10101177.text_edition: \n Helpis Corelliae suae carissimae posuit h\n
EDCS-10101177.translation: For Helpis, his dearest wife, Corellius set this up.
EDCS-10101177.summary: A tombstone erected by Corellius for his beloved wife, Helpis.

EDCS-10101178.text_edition: \n Epetinae matri Aelia Faustina bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101178.translation: To Epetina, his mother, Aelia Faustina set this up as deserving well.
EDCS-10101178.summary: A tombstone erected by Aelia Faustina for her deserving mother, Epetina.

EDCS-10101179.text_edition: \n Felicio Camilli Arrunti Scriboniani servus annorum XL hic situs est Guttilla conserva bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101179.translation: Felicio, a slave of Camillus Arruntius Scribonianus, who lived 40 years, lies here. Guttilla, his deserving fellow slave, set this up.
EDCS-10101179.summary: An epitaph for Felicio, a slave who lived to be 40, erected by his fellow slave Guttilla.

EDCS-10101180.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Feliclae defunctae nnorum XXV Hermes conservae bene merenti\n
EDCS-10101180.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Felicula, deceased, 25 years of age. Hermes for his deserving slave.
EDCS-10101180.summary: A tombstone for Felicula, who died at 25, erected by Hermes, likely her owner or fellow slave.

EDCS-10101181.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Fortunatae defunctae annorum XX Tyche ancillae bene merenti\n
EDCS-10101181.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Fortunata, deceased, 20 years of age. Tyche for her deserving maidservant.
EDCS-10101181.summary: A tombstone for Fortunata, who died at 20, erected by Tyche, likely her owner or fellow slave.

EDCS-10101182.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Hilariano defuncto annorum IIII mensium XI dierum VII Secundinus filio infelicissimo\n
EDCS-10101182.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Hilarianus, deceased, 4 years, 11 months, and 7 days old. Secundinus for his most unhappy son.
EDCS-10101182.summary: A tombstone for Hilarianus, who died in infancy at the age of 4 years, 11 months, and 7 days, erected by his grieving father, Secundinus.

EDCS-10101183.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Hilarioni anni I mensium X Fortunata \n
EDCS-10101183.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Hilario, 1 year and 10 months old. Fortunata.
EDCS-10101183.summary: A tombstone for Hilario, who died at the age of 1 year and 10 months, erected by Fortunata, likely a parent or guardian.

EDCS-10101184.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Hilaro Iuli Festini nnorum XXIII Eutyches et Speciosa filio piissimo et sanctissimo posuerunt\n
EDCS-10101184.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Eutyches and Speciosa set up this monument to their most dutiful and holy son Hilarus, son of Julius Festinus, aged 23.
EDCS-10101184.summary: This is a tombstone for Hilarus, son of Julius Festinus, erected by his parents Eutyches and Speciosa, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101185.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Irinini defunctae nnorum XVII mensium VIII parentes et fratres infelicissimae posuerunt\n
EDCS-10101185.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Her parents and brothers set up this monument to Irinina, who died at the age of 17 years and 8 months, their most unfortunate daughter.
EDCS-10101185.summary: This is a tombstone for Irinina, erected by her parents and brothers, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101186.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcia matri pientissimae Vna filia\n
EDCS-10101186.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Vna, a daughter, set up this monument to her most dutiful mother Marcia.
EDCS-10101186.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcia, erected by her daughter Vna, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101187.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Primae defunctae nni I mensium IIII Primus pater filiae infelicissimae posuit\n
EDCS-10101187.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Primus, father, set up this monument to his most unfortunate daughter Prima, who died at the age of 1 year and 4 months.
EDCS-10101187.summary: This is a tombstone for Prima, erected by her father Primus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101188.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Primulae infelicissimae quae vixit annos plus minus XXV Quintus conlibertus posuit\n
EDCS-10101188.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Quintus, a freedman, set up this monument to Primula, his most unfortunate freedwoman, who lived about 25 years.
EDCS-10101188.summary: This is a tombstone for Primula, erected by her fellow freedman Quintus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101189.translation: To the most unfortunate parents, from Primula their daughter.
EDCS-10101189.summary: This is a dedication to parents, made by their daughter Primula, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101190.translation: Quartinus and Gemella, sons, set up this monument.
EDCS-10101190.summary: This is a dedication made by sons Quartinus and Gemella, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101192.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quinto Felicioni Lupulus et Qu \n
EDCS-10101192.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lupulus and Qu[---] set up this monument to Quintus Felicionius.
EDCS-10101192.summary: This is a tombstone fragment dedicated to Quintus Felicionius by Lupulus and another person, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101193.text_edition: \n parentes Saturae filiae infelicissimae decfunctae annorum X\n
EDCS-10101193.translation: Parents set up this monument to their most unfortunate daughter Satura, who died at the age of 10 years.
EDCS-10101193.summary: This is a tombstone for Satura, erected by her parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101194.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Secundae defunctae annorum X Marcius Muricus et Lucia Aphrodisia alumnae bene meritae\n
EDCS-10101194.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcius Muricus and Lucia Aphrodisia set up this monument to their well-deserving foster-daughter Secunda, who died at the age of 10 years.
EDCS-10101194.summary: This is a tombstone for Secunda, erected by her foster parents Marcius Muricus and Lucia Aphrodisia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101197.text_edition: \n Tritus Plati filius vivus fecet sic sibi et suis INPS\n
EDCS-10101197.translation: Tritus, son of Platius, made this while alive for himself and his family.
EDCS-10101197.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a person named Tritus who built his tomb while alive for himself and his family, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101198.text_edition: \n LIASE N MEMU Urso qui vixit annos VII VTI \n
EDCS-10101198.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the life of a young boy named Urso who died at the age of seven, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101204.text_edition: \n Hic est posita Byrgo annorum XIII NO\n
EDCS-10101204.summary: This is a funerary inscription marking the burial place of Byrgo, who died at the age of thirteen, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101205.text_edition: \n Felix vibus sic sibi posuit et Memmianae conpari suae\n
EDCS-10101205.translation: Felix, while alive, set this up for himself and for his wife Memmiana.
EDCS-10101205.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Felix established his own tomb while alive, also dedicating it to his wife Memmiana, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101207.text_edition: \n OPC annorumnia viva sibi tio Stercorio virginii vit qui vixit mecum annos XV \n
EDCS-10101207.translation: Stercorius, of the Virginii family, who lived with me for 15 years.
EDCS-10101207.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Stercorius, who lived 15 years, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101209.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-10101209.summary: This is a label or ownership mark for a strongbox belonging to Valentina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101223.text_edition: \n Diaia ex iusso sic deae Nemisi sic solvit votum libens merito\n
EDCS-10101223.translation: Diaia, by the command of the goddess Nemesis, gladly and deservedly paid her vow.
EDCS-10101223.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Diaia fulfilling a vow to the goddess Nemesis, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101224.summary: This is a dedication or honorific inscription to Secundinus, identified as a courier, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101234.text_edition: \n Pinaria Cai liberta [] patrono suo Caio Pinario Scarpo veterano legionis VII et filio \n
EDCS-10101234.translation: Pinaria, freedwoman of Caius, to her patron Caius Pinarius Scarpo, a veteran of the VIIth legion, and to his son.
EDCS-10101234.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Pinaria, a freedwoman, dedicating a monument to her patron Caius Pinarius Scarpo, a veteran, and his son, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101239.text_edition: \n Ar Uri Ar Publian qui sibi vivi fecerunt ti iu\n
EDCS-10101239.translation: To Ar [---], to Ar Publius, who made this for themselves while alive.
EDCS-10101239.summary: This is a funerary inscription where two individuals, Ar [---] and Ar Publius, constructed their tomb while alive, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101242.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Caesonia Primitiva.
EDCS-10101242.summary: A funerary dedication to Caesonia Primitiva, likely from a tombstone, date unknown.

EDCS-10101243.summary: A fragment of a funerary inscription, possibly naming Gaius Iu[..., with illegible parts, date unknown.

EDCS-10101246.summary: A funerary dedication by Ianuarius to his well-deserving wife, date unknown.

EDCS-10101248.text_edition: \n defunct-- annorum XXV Velox bene merenti\n
EDCS-10101248.translation: deceased, aged 25 years. Velox to his deserving [wife/husband/relative].
EDCS-10101248.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of a person named Velox at age 25, possibly dedicated by a loved one, date unknown.

EDCS-10101249.summary: A fragment of an inscription mentioning a deceased person and a grandson, date unknown.

EDCS-10101251.text_edition: \n Iuliae Aureliae Hilarae quae vixit annis XXVIIII mensibus VII diebus II Aurelius Hecatus coniugi castissimae et incomparabili posuit et sibi\n
EDCS-10101251.translation: To Julia Aurelia Hilara, who lived 29 years, 7 months, and 2 days. Aurelius Hecatus placed this for his most chaste and incomparable wife and for himself.
EDCS-10101251.summary: A funerary monument set up by Aurelius Hecatus for his wife, Julia Aurelia Hilara, and himself, specifying her age at death, date unknown.

EDCS-10101259.summary: A funerary epitaph for a deacon who 'lies here in peace', with a lacuna, likely from a Christian context, date unknown.

EDCS-10101268.text_edition: \n Aurelius Fortunius petitus a coniuge sua Aurelia Vernantilla sorori suae Ursae quae vixit ex caritate eorum sene sic ullo devitum sic Aurelius Fortunius concessit locum si quis autem filio meo Ver<n=M>antiano post ovitum sic eorum Ursa Vernantilla Fortunium quintum ponere voluerit davit sic ecclesiae argenti pondo V \n
EDCS-10101268.translation: Aurelius Fortunius, requested by his wife Aurelia Vernantilla, to his sister Ursa, who lived from their devoted affection, and so by death passed away. So Aurelius Fortunius granted the place. If anyone however wishes to place [his son?] Ver(n)antianus after their death, Ursa Vernantilla has given 5 pounds of silver to the church.
EDCS-10101268.summary: A complex funerary inscription where Aurelius Fortunius dedicates a burial place to his sister Ursa, mentions his wife Aurelia Vernantilla and son Ver(n)antianus, and records a donation of silver to the church, date unknown.

EDCS-10101274.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-10101274.text_edition: \n Arca Quiriacetis si quis voluerit aperire det argenti pondo quinque\n
EDCS-10101274.translation: Tomb of Quiriacetis. If anyone wishes to open it, let them pay five pounds of silver.
EDCS-10101274.summary: A funerary inscription concerning the tomb of Quiriacetis, imposing a fine of five pounds of silver for unauthorized opening, date unknown.

EDCS-10101275.summary: A fragment of a funerary inscription dedicating a monument to someone's wife, identified only by the initials LE, date unknown.

EDCS-10101504.text_edition: \n -- Marcianus SIV V et menses II dies \n
EDCS-10101504.translation: Marcianus, for 5 years and 2 months and days...
EDCS-10101504.summary: This is a funerary inscription or a record of service by Marcianus, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101590.text_edition: \n Ex voto Alexandri cot sic promisit solvi(t)\n
EDCS-10101590.translation: From a vow made by Alexander; thus he promised and paid.
EDCS-10101590.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Alexander, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101591.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Karpophorus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101592.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vivio Silvestro militi cohortis VIII voluntariorum centuria Ber annorum A CINNconiugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101592.translation: To the Divine Spirits. C. [---] placed this for his well-deserving wife Vivio Silvestra, a soldier of the VIII cohort of volunteers, of the century of Ber, aged [---] years.
EDCS-10101592.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a soldier to his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101593.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Campatiae Felicitati Geminius Felix maritus fecit piissimae\n
EDCS-10101593.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Geminius Felix made this for his most devoted Campatia Felicitas.
EDCS-10101593.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a husband for his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101594.text_edition: \n Lucius Cassius Quinti filius Severus vivus fecit sibi \n
EDCS-10101594.translation: Lucius Cassius Severus, son of Quintus, made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-10101594.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a self-dedicated tomb, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101595.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Clodiae Gratillae Aelia Germana matri pientissimae bene merenti\n
EDCS-10101595.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aelia Germana for her most dutiful mother Clodia Grattilla, well-deserving.
EDCS-10101595.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a daughter for her mother, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101596.text_edition: \n Corelliae Melete defunctae nnorum XXX uxori optimae \n
EDCS-10101596.translation: To Corellia Melete, deceased, aged 30 years, for his excellent wife.
EDCS-10101596.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Corellia Melete, specifying her age and relationship to the dedicator, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101598.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulio Veronioni Titus Flavius Quintillus \n
EDCS-10101598.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Titus Flavius Quintillus for Iulius Veronioni.
EDCS-10101598.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Titus Flavius Quintillus for Iulius Veronioni, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101599.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a tombstone or dedicatory text, bearing the name Laberia Primula, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101600.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Mariae Primae infelicissimae defunctae annorum VIIII Valeria Procula libertae posuit\n
EDCS-10101600.translation: To the spirits of the Underworld. Valeria Procula, freedwoman, set this up for Maria Prima, most unfortunate, who died at the age of nine.
EDCS-10101600.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a freedwoman for a young girl named Maria Prima, likely her daughter or ward.

EDCS-10101602.text_edition: \n L onia Syrillua marito dulcissimo posuit\n
EDCS-10101602.translation: Lonia Syrillua set this up for her dearest husband.
EDCS-10101602.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her husband.

EDCS-10101616.summary: A funerary epitaph identifying Cornelia Rustica as a freedwoman.

EDCS-10101618.translation: Gnaeus Philemo of Ahius rests here.
EDCS-10101618.summary: A funerary epitaph stating that Gnaeus Philemo rests here.

EDCS-10101619.text_edition: \n veterano legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis Neria Pieris coniugi suo et sibi\n
EDCS-10101619.translation: Neria Pieris for her husband, a veteran of the VII Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, and for herself.
EDCS-10101619.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Neria Pieris for her husband, a veteran of the VII Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, and for herself.

EDCS-10101620.text_edition: \n Primigenius Publi Ploti |(centurionis) legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis libertus annorum XXI sibi et pat \n
EDCS-10101620.translation: Primigenius, freedman of Publius Plotus, centurion of the VII Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, aged twenty-one, for himself and his father.
EDCS-10101620.summary: A funerary epitaph for Primigenius, a freedman of a centurion in the VII Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, who set it up for himself and his father.

EDCS-10101621.text_edition: \n Titi Tremeli Lepidi nnorum V hic situs est\n
EDCS-10101621.translation: Titus Tremelius Lepidus, aged five, rests here.
EDCS-10101621.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Tremelius Lepidus, who died at the age of five.

EDCS-10101622.text_edition: \n Licinius Capito miles cohortis I Belgarum centuria VI Severi S unato \n
EDCS-10101622.translation: Licinius Capito, soldier of the I Belgarum cohort, VI century of Severus Su...
EDCS-10101622.summary: A funerary epitaph for Licinius Capito, a soldier in the I Belgarum cohort.

EDCS-10101624.summary: A fragmentary inscription, likely a name, possibly Passieno.

EDCS-10101632.text_edition: \n Salvius Crescens et Scantia Prima Napini delicatae suae obitae nnorum VI\n
EDCS-10101632.translation: Salvius Crescens and Scantia Prima for their beloved Napina, who died at the age of six.
EDCS-10101632.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Salvius Crescens and Scantia Prima for their young daughter Napina.

EDCS-10101633.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Modesto Titus Aemilius Quartio discenti bene merenti\n
EDCS-10101633.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Modestus, a student who deserved well. Titus Aemilius Quartio set this up.
EDCS-10101633.summary: This is a tombstone set up by Titus Aemilius Quartio for his deserving student, Modestus.

EDCS-10101634.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Felici Caesaris nostri servo annorum VIII Antiochus fratri piissimo\n
EDCS-10101634.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Felix, an 8-year-old slave of our emperor. Antiochus set this up for his most devoted brother.
EDCS-10101634.summary: This funerary inscription is for Felix, the 8-year-old slave of the emperor, dedicated by his brother Antiochus.

EDCS-10101635.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Coylio sic Athenioni homini bono qui vixit nnos plus minus LXX Caesius Polybius collegae bono posuit\n
EDCS-10101635.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Coylius, a good man, who lived about 70 years. Caesius Polybius set this up for his good colleague.
EDCS-10101635.summary: This inscription records the death of Coylius, a good man who lived to be about 70, and was set up by his colleague Caesius Polybius.

EDCS-10101636.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Homini pagano Thelonico quendam sic retiario qui pietate populi rude liberatus est Xustus sic amicus et Pepticius sodalis\n
EDCS-10101636.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Thelonius the paganus, once a retiarius, who was freed by the pity of the people with a trident; Xustus the friend and Pepticius the comrade set this up.
EDCS-10101636.summary: This funerary inscription honors Thelonius, a retiarius gladiator who was freed, and was set up by his friend Xustus and comrade Pepticius.

EDCS-10101637.text_edition: \n Villiae Philogenis libertae Suttae Dacae Lucius Clodius Adiutor matri posuit\n
EDCS-10101637.translation: For Villia Philogenis, freedwoman of Sutta the Dacian. Lucius Clodius Adiutor set this up for his mother.
EDCS-10101637.summary: Lucius Clodius Adiutor dedicated this monument to his mother, Villia Philogenis, a freedwoman.

EDCS-10101638.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cominiae Lupae nnum XXI Quintus Petronius Epigonus vivus coniugi bene merenti posuit et Petroniae Bidusae matri\n
EDCS-10101638.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Cominia Lupa, aged 21. Quintus Petronius Epigonus set this up while alive for his deserving wife, and for Petronia Bidusa, his mother.
EDCS-10101638.summary: Quintus Petronius Epigonus erected this funerary monument during his lifetime for his wife Cominia Lupa, aged 21, and his mother Petronia Bidusa.

EDCS-10101639.text_edition: \n Lucio Cafatio Spuri filio Celeri Pomponia Philumina mater posuit\n
EDCS-10101639.translation: For Lucius Cafatius Celer, son of Spurius. Pomponia Philumina, his mother, set this up.
EDCS-10101639.summary: Pomponia Philumina erected this funerary monument for her son, Lucius Cafatius Celer.

EDCS-10101640.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Firminus.
EDCS-10101640.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription dedicated to Firminus.

EDCS-10101641.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iphigeniae Valeriae quae vixit annos XXV hunc titulum posuit infelicissima Domitia mater suo sic sic cum magno dolore etsi maerent superi fortasse et inferi multi\n
EDCS-10101641.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Iphigenia Valeria, who lived 25 years. Her most unhappy mother, Domitia, set up this inscription for her daughter with great grief, although the gods above and perhaps many below also mourn.
EDCS-10101641.summary: Domitia, the deeply grieving mother, erected this tombstone for her daughter Iphigenia Valeria, who died at 25.

EDCS-10101642.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Noeto Augusti vernae adiutori tabularii bene merenti collegae fecerunt\n
EDCS-10101642.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Noetus, an imperial attendant and assistant scribe, who deserved well. His colleagues made this.
EDCS-10101642.summary: Colleagues dedicated this tombstone to their deserving fellow worker, Noetus, an imperial attendant and assistant scribe.

EDCS-10101643.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus nae de<f=E>(unctae) annorum I mensium VII dierum XXI Mescenio de<f=I>(uncto) nnorum IIII men(sium) --I di<e=I>(rum) XXI orius nepos \n
EDCS-10101643.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Nae, deceased, aged 1 year, 7 months, 21 days. Mescenio, deceased, aged 4 years, -- months, 21 days. Orius, grandson.
EDCS-10101643.summary: A tombstone recording the ages of two deceased individuals, Nae and Mescenio, likely children, with a familial relationship indicated by 'nepos' (grandson).

EDCS-10101644.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudia Titiana P I fratribus Titiano et Cl \n
EDCS-10101644.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Claudia Titiana [places this for her] brothers Titianus and Cl...
EDCS-10101644.summary: A tombstone for Claudia Titiana, likely dedicating a monument to her brothers Titianus and another whose name is incomplete.

EDCS-10101645.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Coylio sic Ursino Publius Valerius Severus bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101645.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Valerius Severus erected this for Coylius Ursinus, who deserved well.
EDCS-10101645.summary: A tombstone erected by Publius Valerius Severus for Coylius Ursinus, indicating a memorial to a deserving individual.

EDCS-10101646.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Urs defuncto annorum AN \n
EDCS-10101646.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Publius Urs, deceased, aged AN...
EDCS-10101646.summary: A tombstone for Publius Urs, with an incomplete record of his age.

EDCS-10101647.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Varronius...
EDCS-10101647.summary: A fragmentary tombstone dedicated to the departed spirit of Varronius.

EDCS-10101648.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titiae Moschi carissimae feminae qui sic vixit annos XXXVIII Titus Horesius Saturninus coniugi inconparabili sic bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101648.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Titus Horesius Saturninus erected this for his most beloved wife, Titia Moscha, a most worthy woman, who lived 38 years, to his incomparable wife, who deserved well.
EDCS-10101648.summary: A tombstone erected by Titus Horesius Saturninus for his beloved and incomparable wife, Titia Moscha, recording her age at death.

EDCS-10101649.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Kornelia Procla Valerio Proclo filio infelicissimo qui vixit plus minus annos XVII bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101649.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Kornelia Procla erected this for her most unfortunate son, Valerius Proclus, who lived more or less 17 years, who deserved well.
EDCS-10101649.summary: A tombstone erected by Kornelia Procla for her son, Valerius Proclus, recording his age at death and expressing grief.

EDCS-10101651.text_edition: \n M VET Sym Secu die--- XXXVII F \n
EDCS-10101651.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely related to a burial, mentioning a name and an age of 37 years.

EDCS-10101652.summary: A very fragmentary inscription with no discernible meaning.

EDCS-10101655.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Alus Vic...
EDCS-10101655.summary: A fragmentary tombstone indicating a dedication to the departed spirits, possibly for an individual named Alus Vic.

EDCS-10101656.text_edition: \n Noviae Compsini Novius et <N>ovia patroni sic bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-10101656.translation: Novius and Novia, patrons, set this up for their well-deserving Novia Compsina.
EDCS-10101656.summary: A funerary monument set up by patrons for their freedwoman, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101657.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-10101657.text_edition: \n ------] suo IA supremo t officium nunc se si fungere maerens us edidit hunc titulum\n
EDCS-10101657.translation: Mourning, she performed her final duty for her [beloved] husband and erected this inscription.
EDCS-10101657.summary: A funerary inscription recording a wife's devotion to her deceased husband, with the date and full context lost.

EDCS-10101658.text_edition: \n indicet I levis ut I quaeso super eposi<t>us testeturque coniugis officium os advenae\n
EDCS-10101658.translation: I beg that this may show and attest to the duty of my wife to a stranger.
EDCS-10101658.summary: A funerary epitaph, possibly from a tomb, expressing a husband's grief and remembrance of his wife's virtue.

EDCS-10101659.text_edition: \n coniunx EIVsus filius pientissim Trophimo - Claudi Pomponiani --\n
EDCS-10101659.translation: The devoted son Eivsus, son of Claudius Pomponianus, to Trophimus...
EDCS-10101659.summary: A funerary inscription for Trophimus, set up by his devoted son, identifying the father and possibly the deceased's lineage.

EDCS-10101660.text_edition: \n IIOVE acceptam tuerat et ponere sim titulum modo \n
EDCS-10101660.translation: ...had carried Jove's gift and would place a similar inscription.
EDCS-10101660.summary: A fragment of a dedication, possibly to Jupiter, referencing a previous act of piety and the erection of an inscription.

EDCS-10101661.text_edition: \n IIIProclo menses X posuit Antonia Soteria alumno\n
EDCS-10101661.translation: Antonia Soteria set this up for Proclus for 10 months as his teacher.
EDCS-10101661.summary: A dedication for a student, Proclus, set up by his teacher Antonia Soteria, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101663.translation: Vatinia Privata to her most devoted son.
EDCS-10101663.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Vatinia Privata for her son, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101666.summary: A dedication or funerary epitaph fragment mentioning a man named Clodius Maximus.

EDCS-10101671.summary: An incomplete dedication or funerary epitaph for Gaius Carfanius Vern.

EDCS-10101672.summary: An incomplete inscription mentioning a duration of 26 years and a patron, possibly funerary or commemorative.

EDCS-10101674.translation: To Gaius P. Herm, his wife.
EDCS-10101674.summary: This is a dedication to a wife named P. Herm by her husband, Gaius.

EDCS-10101675.text_edition: \n ------] Publi- Su SAET fecit C DCI \n
EDCS-10101675.summary: This inscription records a work made by Publius Su[---] in the year 261, likely a tombstone or monument.

EDCS-10101700.summary: This fragment likely refers to a person named Gaudentius, part of whose name is lost.

EDCS-10101701.summary: This inscription fragment mentions a name ending in Ricia and a duration of 15 years, possibly referring to an age or a period of service.

EDCS-10101712.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Accio Zosimo qui vixit nnis XXIII menssibus XI diebus XVI fecit Accia Amabilis mater filio pientissimo\n
EDCS-10101712.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Accia Amabilis set this up for her son, Marcus Accius Zosimus, who lived 23 years, 11 months, and 16 days, as a most devoted son.
EDCS-10101712.summary: This tombstone honors Marcus Accius Zosimus, recording his age at death and erected by his devoted mother, Accia Amabilis.

EDCS-10101713.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aebutiae Ursinae defunctae <a=E>nno<r=P>um XXX Marcius Primitivus maritus et Cusia Victoria filia eius bene merenti posuerunt \n
EDCS-10101713.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Marcius Primitivus, her husband, and Cusia Victoria, her daughter, placed this for Aebutia Ursina, deceased, aged 30 years, deserving of remembrance.
EDCS-10101713.summary: This tombstone records the death of Aebutia Ursina at age 30 and was erected by her husband and daughter.

EDCS-10101714.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aeliae Maximinae defunctae annorum XXI posuit frater titulum Felicianus bene merenti D L S\n
EDCS-10101714.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Felicianus, her brother, placed this monument for Aelia Maximina, deceased, aged 21 years, deserving of remembrance.
EDCS-10101714.summary: This tombstone records the death of Aelia Maximina at age 21 and was erected by her brother, Felicianus.

EDCS-10101715.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Albiae Cale Albii Marina et Hermes matri piissimae\n
EDCS-10101715.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Albia Cale, from Albii Marina and Hermes, most devoted mother.
EDCS-10101715.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Albia Cale, likely by her parents Marina and Hermes.

EDCS-10101716.text_edition: \n Titus Aurelius Messianus et Clodia Maximina sibi vivi posuerunt locum concessum a Domitia Antonia\n
EDCS-10101716.translation: Titus Aurelius Messianus and Clodia Maximina placed this for themselves while alive. The place was granted by Domitia Antonia.
EDCS-10101716.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Aurelius Messianus and Clodia Maximina, who purchased their burial plot from Domitia Antonia.

EDCS-10101717.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-10101717.text_edition: \n Aurelius Saloninus concessit arcam Barroniae sic\n
EDCS-10101717.translation: Aurelius Saloninus granted the burial chamber to Barronia thus.
EDCS-10101717.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Saloninus granting a burial chamber to Barronia, likely concerning property rights for a tomb.

EDCS-10101718.translation: To Baebia Feliclsa, from her heirs.
EDCS-10101718.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Baebia Feliclsa by her heirs, likely marking a tomb or monument.

EDCS-10101719.text_edition: \n Caio Caecilio Vilielliano Cornelia Titia marito rarissimo\n
EDCS-10101719.translation: To Gaius Caecilius Viliellianus, from Cornelia Titia, his most beloved husband.
EDCS-10101719.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Gaius Caecilius Viliellianus by his wife, Cornelia Titia, likely for a tomb.

EDCS-10101720.text_edition: \n Calpurnia Pitiana arcam se viva posuit sibi et Publio Claudio coniugi suo domum aeternalem\n
EDCS-10101720.translation: Calpurnia Pitiana placed this tomb for herself and for Publius Claudius, her husband, an eternal home, while she was alive.
EDCS-10101720.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording Calpurnia Pitiana's construction of her own tomb and that of her husband, Publius Claudius, during her lifetime.

EDCS-10101721.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Camuraenae Afrodisiae annorum XXX Caius Salvius Cosmicus coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-10101721.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Camuraena Afrodisia, aged 30. Caius Salvius Cosmicus placed this for his well-deserving wife.
EDCS-10101721.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Camuraena Afrodisia by her husband, Caius Salvius Cosmicus, indicating her age at death.

EDCS-10101722.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Camureno Lupulo defuncto nnum XXVII Vibia Doxa infelicissimo coniugi posuit\n
EDCS-10101722.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Lucius Camurenus Lupus, deceased, aged 27 years. Vibia Doxa placed this for her most unhappy husband.
EDCS-10101722.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Lucius Camurenus Lupus by his wife, Vibia Doxa, noting his age at death.

EDCS-10101723.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Camurenae Luci filiae Proculae\n
EDCS-10101723.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Camurena Procula, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-10101723.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Divine Spirits of Camurena Procula, daughter of Lucius.

EDCS-10101724.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caninio Tito Caninia Syra patrono bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101724.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Caninius Titus. Caninia Syra placed this for her well-deserving patron.
EDCS-10101724.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Caninius Titus by his patron, Caninia Syra, indicating his well-deserving status.

EDCS-10101725.text_edition: \n nnosae defunctae nnorum XVII Claudia Quarta mater\n
EDCS-10101725.translation: To the deceased [name], aged 17 years. Claudia Quarta, mother.
EDCS-10101725.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph recording the death of a 17-year-old, set up by her mother, Claudia Quarta.

EDCS-10101726.text_edition: \n defuncto annorum mensium III liberto Claudius Ursus adfini bene merenti\n
EDCS-10101726.translation: [For the] deceased [name], aged [X] years, [X] months, 3 days, freedman. Claudius Ursus placed this for his well-deserving relative.
EDCS-10101726.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for a freedman, set up by his relative Claudius Ursus.

EDCS-10101727.text_edition: \n Coe Maritima mater Coeo <in=VI>felicissimo annorum XXII mensium II bene merenti \n
EDCS-10101727.translation: Coe Maritima, mother, for Coeus, her most happy [son? husband?], aged 22 years, 2 months, well-deserving.
EDCS-10101727.summary: This inscription records Coe Maritima setting up a monument for Coeus, likely her son or husband, noting his age and positive attributes.

EDCS-10101728.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Cornelio Diadumeno Caius Cornelius Venamius libertus et heres bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101728.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Cornelius Venamius, his freedman and heir, erected this for Gaius Cornelius Diadumenus, deserving of much good.
EDCS-10101728.summary: A funerary dedication by a freedman and heir to his former master, Gaius Cornelius Diadumenus.

EDCS-10101729.text_edition: \n Flavia Eucarpia Charmideti filio pientissimo annorum XVI mensium IIII dierum IIII\n
EDCS-10101729.translation: Flavia Eucarpia for Charmidetus, her most dutiful son, aged sixteen years, four months, and four days.
EDCS-10101729.summary: A funerary dedication for Flavia Eucarpia's son, Charmidetus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-10101731.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Iulius Epafroditus se vivo posuit sibi et Iuliae Agrippinae coniugi suae\n
EDCS-10101731.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Marcus Iulius Epafroditus erected this for himself and his wife, Iulia Agrippina, during his lifetime.
EDCS-10101731.summary: A self-funded funerary dedication by Marcus Iulius Epafroditus for himself and his wife, Iulia Agrippina.

EDCS-10101732.text_edition: \n Depositio si sorori et Iulio Epicteto vivo Ianuarius filius posuit\n
EDCS-10101732.translation: Burial for my sister and Iulius Epictetus, erected during their lifetime by Ianuarius, their son.
EDCS-10101732.summary: A funerary dedication by Ianuarius for his mother and Iulius Epictetus, set up during their lifetime.

EDCS-10101733.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae E<u>tychini Iulia Fortunata alumnae M \n
EDCS-10101733.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulia Fortunata for Iulia Eutychina, her foster-daughter.
EDCS-10101733.summary: A funerary dedication by Iulia Fortunata for her foster-daughter, Iulia Eutychina.

EDCS-10101734.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Feliculae amicae bonae Septueia Ingenua posuit\n
EDCS-10101734.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Septueia Ingenua erected this for Iulia Felicula, her good friend.
EDCS-10101734.summary: A funerary dedication by Septueia Ingenua for her friend, Iulia Felicula.

EDCS-10101735.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus ]] II(?)[] [[ Iulius Fronto filius Vitalianus filia Valentilla Vibia matri \n
EDCS-10101735.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Fronto, son, Vitalianus, daughter, and Valentilla for their mother.
EDCS-10101735.summary: A funerary dedication by the children Iulius Fronto, Vitalianus, and Valentilla for their mother.

EDCS-10101737.text_edition: \n coniugi bene merenti Lucius Ulpius Sera<p=\xcf\x86>io \n
EDCS-10101737.translation: For his deserving wife. Lucius Ulpius Sera[p]io
EDCS-10101737.summary: A funerary dedication to an unnamed wife by Lucius Ulpius Sera[p]io.

EDCS-10101738.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Maria Ofelima Marco Memnio Primitio suo infelicissimo annorum XVIIII\n
EDCS-10101738.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For my most unfortunate Marcus Memnius Primitivus, aged nineteen.
EDCS-10101738.summary: A funerary dedication by Maria Ofelima for Marcus Memnius Primitivus, noting his age and unfortunate end.

EDCS-10101739.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Masuriae Titiae coniugi inconparabili sic quae vixit nnos XXXI menses VI Ursus tabellarius posuit coniugi bene merenti quae vixit mecum nnos XVII menses V et sibi vivus posuit\n
EDCS-10101739.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Ursus the courier erected this for Masuria Tita, his incomparable wife, who lived with me for thirty-one years and six months, and for himself, alive.
EDCS-10101739.summary: A funerary dedication by Ursus for his wife Masuria Tita, recording their marriage duration and also for himself.

EDCS-10101740.text_edition: \n annorum VI Titus Mescenius Euprepes maritus bene merenti posuit et mater Trofime\n
EDCS-10101740.translation: Titus Mescenius Euprepes erected this for his well-deserving wife, who lived six years, and for his mother Trofime.
EDCS-10101740.summary: A funerary epitaph for a six-year-old girl, erected by her husband and mother, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101741.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Minicius Hermes pater Minicio Felici filio pientissimo defuncto nnorum XII \n
EDCS-10101741.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Minicius Hermes erected this for his most dutiful son, Minicius Felix, who died at twelve years of age.
EDCS-10101741.summary: A funerary epitaph for a twelve-year-old boy, dedicated by his father, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101742.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Plotia Fortunata VALVEN merenti\n
EDCS-10101742.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For the well-deserving Plotia Fortunata.
EDCS-10101742.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Plotia Fortunata, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101743.text_edition: \n Marco Seppieno Aurioni defuncto annorum VIIII Marcus Seppienus Eutychus pater posuit filio\n
EDCS-10101743.translation: Marcus Seppienus Eutychus erected this for his son, Marcus Seppieno Aurioni, who died at nine years of age.
EDCS-10101743.summary: A funerary epitaph for a nine-year-old boy, dedicated by his father, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101744.text_edition: \n - Seppieno Eutycheti defuncto annorum XVI Seppiena Nice mater filio infelicissimo posuit\n
EDCS-10101744.translation: Seppiena Nice erected this for her most unfortunate son, Seppieno Eutycheti, who died at sixteen years of age.
EDCS-10101744.summary: A funerary epitaph for a sixteen-year-old boy, dedicated by his mother, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101745.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Septiena Marcia coniugi carissimo pientissimo Iulio Crispino qui et Passar miles strator viri consularis bene merenti\n
EDCS-10101745.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For his most dear and dutiful wife, Septiena Marcia, [erected by] Iulius Crispinus, who was also called Passar, a soldier of the consular general, for her well-deserving.
EDCS-10101745.summary: A funerary epitaph for Septiena Marcia, wife of Iulius Crispinus, a soldier, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101746.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valeriae Valentinae coniugi rarissimae \n
EDCS-10101746.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Valeria Valentina, his most rare wife.
EDCS-10101746.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Valeria Valentina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101747.text_edition: \n tiliae Secundinae Marcus U<l=I>pius Germanus coniugi bene merenti \n
EDCS-10101747.translation: Marcus Ulpius Germanus erected this for his well-deserving wife, Titia Secundina.
EDCS-10101747.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titia Secundina, erected by her husband Marcus Ulpius Germanus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101748.text_edition: \n Crescens vixit annos IIII menses VI Prima mater infelicissima filio fecit hic situs est sit tibi terra levis\n
EDCS-10101748.translation: Crescens lived for four years and six months; his most unfortunate mother Prima made this for her son. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-10101748.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young boy named Crescens who died at four years and six months, dedicated by his mother, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101749.translation: For his most dear wife, Tritma Lebinthus.
EDCS-10101749.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Tritma, wife of Lebinthus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101750.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Restutus placed this as a friend.
EDCS-10101750.summary: This funerary inscription records a tomb set up by Restutus for a deceased friend.

EDCS-10101751.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus parentes infelicissimi posuerunt filiis numero III uno annorum XXVII altero XXVIII altero XXX\n
EDCS-10101751.translation: To the spirits of the departed: The most unhappy parents placed this for their three sons, one aged 27, another 28, and the third 30.
EDCS-10101751.summary: This funerary inscription records a tomb erected by grieving parents for their three sons, whose ages are specified.

EDCS-10101753.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valeriae Verae defunctae nnorum XXVII coniugi incomparabili Iulius Fortunatus bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101753.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Iulius Fortunatus placed this for Valeria Vera, deceased at age 27, his incomparable wife, well-deserving.
EDCS-10101753.summary: This funerary inscription records a tomb set up by Iulius Fortunatus for his wife, Valeria Vera, emphasizing her virtues and their age difference.

EDCS-10101754.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Epetinae defunctae annorum XXV Lucris filiae infelicissimae\n
EDCS-10101754.translation: To the spirits of the departed: For Epetina, deceased at age 25, his most unhappy daughter.
EDCS-10101754.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Epetina at age 25 and mentions her grieving parent.

EDCS-10101756.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Numida Apollini votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10101756.translation: Caius Iulius Numida willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Apollo.
EDCS-10101756.summary: This votive inscription records Caius Iulius Numida fulfilling a vow made to the god Apollo.

EDCS-10101760.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudio Prisco coniugi carissimo et infelicissimo \n
EDCS-10101760.translation: To the spirits of the departed: For Claudius Priscus, his dearest and most unhappy husband.
EDCS-10101760.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Claudius Priscus, mourned by his spouse.

EDCS-10101761.text_edition: \n VIV Cupinae defunctae nnorum XVIII Corellius Cassius pater\n
EDCS-10101761.translation: For Cupina, deceased at age 18: Corellius Cassius, her father, [erected this].
EDCS-10101761.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Cupina at age 18, erected by her father.

EDCS-10101762.text_edition: \n Herennius Capitolinus Aemiliae Maxximillae coniugi inconparabili sic\n
EDCS-10101762.translation: Herennius Capitolinus [erected this] for Aemilia Maxximilla, his incomparable wife.
EDCS-10101762.summary: This funerary inscription records Herennius Capitolinus setting up a monument for his wife, Aemilia Maxximilla.

EDCS-10101763.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Papinio Thr<e=I>ptiano Papinia Festiva liberta \n
EDCS-10101763.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Papinia Festiva, freedwoman, [placed this] for Papinius Thraeptianus.
EDCS-10101763.summary: This funerary inscription records Papinia Festiva setting up a memorial for Papinius Thraeptianus.

EDCS-10101764.text_edition: \n Selia Marci liberta Chia hic ego Chia cinis fatis mea forma deleta? est mors cuncta aequat m\n
EDCS-10101764.translation: Selia Chia, freedwoman of Marci, I, Chia, ashes, have been erased by fate. Does death make all equal?
EDCS-10101764.summary: This funerary inscription is a self-commemorative epitaph by Selia Chia, a freedwoman, reflecting on mortality and fate.

EDCS-10101765.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Annidi Messor conservae pientissimae bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101765.translation: To the Divine Spirits: Messor erected this for Annidia, his most devoted and deserving colleague.
EDCS-10101765.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Messor for his colleague Annidia, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-10101800.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum pro salute et reditu Luci Pomponi Valentini Lucius Pomponius Restutus cliens\n
EDCS-10101800.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus, for the health and safe return of Lucius Pomponius Valentinus. Lucius Pomponius Restutus, his client.
EDCS-10101800.summary: An altar dedicated to Silvanus Augustus by a client for the well-being of his patron, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-10101801.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelius Capito decurio municipi Salvio natus Starve qui vixit annos XLV Aelius Victor filius ipsius patri karissimo bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-10101801.translation: To the Divine Spirits: Aelius Victor erected this for his most devoted and deserving father, Aelius Capito, a decurion of the municipium of Salvia, born in Starve, who lived 45 years.
EDCS-10101801.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aelius Capito, a decurion from Salvia, set up by his son, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-10101802.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Liciniae defunctae annorum XVIIII pat(er) filiae pientissimae posuit\n
EDCS-10101802.translation: To the Divine Spirits: Her father erected this for his most devoted daughter, Licinia, who died at 19 years of age.
EDCS-10101802.summary: A funerary epitaph for Licinia, who died at 19, set up by her father, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-10101803.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vettiae Ingenuae matri pientissimae quae vixit nnos XL Vettia Tertullina posuit\n
EDCS-10101803.translation: To the Divine Spirits: Vettia Tertullina erected this for Vettia Ingenua, her most devoted mother, who lived 40 years.
EDCS-10101803.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vettia Ingenua, set up by her daughter, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-10101804.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Actiaco Marinus tatae baene merenti et Glaucus Filocrenus bene merenti\n
EDCS-10101804.translation: To the Divine Spirits: For Actiacus Marinus, most deserving, and for Glaucus Filocrenus, most deserving.
EDCS-10101804.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicating the monument to Actiacus Marinus and Glaucus Filocrenus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-10101805.text_edition: \n Lucius Antonius Niger Antoniae Telesphoridi libertae et coniugi\n
EDCS-10101805.translation: Lucius Antonius Niger to Antonia Telesphoris, his freedwoman and wife.
EDCS-10101805.summary: A funerary epitaph for Antonia Telesphoris, a freedwoman and wife, set up by Lucius Antonius Niger, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-10101806.text_edition: \n Nymphis Cato et Lupo socii votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-10101806.translation: Cato and Lupus, associates, willingly fulfilled their vow to the Nymphs, as was deserved.
EDCS-10101806.summary: A dedication to the Nymphs by two associates who fulfilled a vow, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-10101807.text_edition: \n Arrio Calationi et Minatiae Myrine parentibus bene merentibus filia fecit\n
EDCS-10101807.translation: Their daughter erected this for Arrius Calatianus and Minatia Myrine, her deserving parents.
EDCS-10101807.summary: A funerary epitaph for parents Arrius Calatianus and Minatia Myrine, set up by their daughter, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-10101808.translation: To the Divine Spirits: For Julia Saturnina...
EDCS-10101808.summary: A partial funerary epitaph for Julia Saturnina, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-10101812.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum Publius C A Abas voto posuit\n
EDCS-10101812.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus; Publius C. A. Abas set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-10101812.summary: This is a dedication to Silvanus Augustus by Publius C. A. Abas, likely a private vow, probably Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101813.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Papirius Quintio Dionisiae sic coniugi quae vixitm mecum annis XXVI et sibi posuit\n
EDCS-10101813.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Dionisia, wife of Papirius Quintio, who lived with me for 26 years, and himself, set this up.
EDCS-10101813.summary: This is a tombstone for Dionisia, wife of Papirius Quintio, who also set it up for himself, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101814.text_edition: \n Rutiliae Zonae puellae infelicissimae quae vixit nnos XXI sumptus sic eius positum et Ruti<li>ae Augustae sorori instantia Rutiliorum Zenonis et Latinae parentum Clodius Fortunatus locum concessit Arca Iuliano Pandurio\n
EDCS-10101814.translation: To Rutilia Zona, a most unfortunate girl, who lived 21 years; set up at the expense of her parents, Rutilia Augusta and Rutilius Zeno, and Latina; Clodius Fortunatus granted the burial plot, in the presence of Arca Iulianus Pandurius.
EDCS-10101814.summary: This is a funerary monument for Rutilia Zona, set up by her parents and Clodius Fortunatus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101816.text_edition: \n Turoni Statariae Turi filiae hic sitae\n
EDCS-10101816.translation: For Turonia Stataria, daughter of Turius, buried here.
EDCS-10101816.summary: This is a simple tombstone for Turonia Stataria, daughter of Turius, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101817.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-10101817.summary: This inscription likely marks a tomb or memorial for Arca Iulianus Pandurius, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101819.text_edition: \n Pomponius V<a=M>lentinus et Titia Rufina P C C P S fecerunt \n
EDCS-10101819.translation: Pomponius Valentinus and Titia Rufina, public benefactors, made this.
EDCS-10101819.summary: This is a record of a construction or dedication by Pomponius Valentinus and Titia Rufina, who are identified as public benefactors (P.C.C.P.S.), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101822.translation: GI, a member of the sodality, in honor of Pertinax!
EDCS-10101822.summary: This is a dedication by a member of a sodality (fellowship) to honor Pertinax, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101823.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record the name and title of an official, possibly a prefect, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101824.text_edition: \n Septiminae IITEMPOLA annorum XI FIIEEGIVR O annorum OV IIIOM \n
EDCS-10101824.translation: To Septimina Pola, aged 11 years, and [...], aged 3 years.
EDCS-10101824.summary: This is a tombstone for two young girls, Septimina Pola and another unnamed child, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101825.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that may contain personal names, likely from a tombstone or dedication, Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101826.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Plo Varierius, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-10101828.text_edition: \n Aureliae Stercoriae infanti infelicissimae quae vixit nnum I mensem I dies VIII Aurelius Pat et Fabia Maxima parentes filiae dulcissimae et sibi vivi\n
EDCS-10101828.translation: To Aurelia Stercoria, an unfortunate infant who lived 1 year, 1 month, and 8 days. Aurelius Paternus and Fabia Maxima, parents, for their sweetest daughter and for themselves while alive.
EDCS-10101828.summary: This is a tombstone for Aurelia Stercoria, an infant, set up by her parents, Aurelius Paternus and Fabia Maxima.

EDCS-10101829.text_edition: \n - Antonius Martialis signum Minervae et aedem vetustate consumptam restituit\n
EDCS-10101829.translation: Antonius Martialis restored the statue of Minerva and the temple, which had been consumed by age.
EDCS-10101829.summary: Antonius Martialis restored a statue of Minerva and a temple that had fallen into disrepair.

EDCS-10101830.text_edition: \n Manibus Cai Valeri Cai fili Scaptia Secundi Altino praefecti kastrorum legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis\n
EDCS-10101830.translation: To the spirits of Gaius Valerius, son of Gaius, of the Scaptian tribe, Secundus, prefect of the camp of the 11th Claudian legion, pious and faithful.
EDCS-10101830.summary: This inscription honors Gaius Valerius Secundus, a prefect of the camp of the 11th Claudian legion.

EDCS-10101832.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Billianius filius Verus beneficiarius consularis \n
EDCS-10101832.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, by Billianius Verus, son of Billianus, a beneficiarius consularis.
EDCS-10101832.summary: Billianius Verus, a beneficiarius consularis, dedicated this altar to Jupiter.

EDCS-10101833.summary: This inscription likely marks a boundary or property belonging to the century of Betu, possibly a military unit.

EDCS-10101834.translation: Public vows to the Augusti, for success.
EDCS-10101834.summary: This records public vows made for the success of the Emperors.

EDCS-10101838.text_edition: \n Tertius Baebius Cai filius Scaptia domo Florentia nnorum XXXV stipendiorum XVII miles legionis XI cornicen\n
EDCS-10101838.translation: Tertius Baebius, son of Gaius, of the Scaptian tribe, from Florence, aged 35 years, with 17 years of service, a miles (soldier) in the 11th legion, a cornicen (horn-blower).
EDCS-10101838.summary: This is a tombstone for Tertius Baebius, a cornicen (horn-blower) in the 11th legion, who died after 17 years of service.

EDCS-10101839.text_edition: \n Lucius Bodlius Luci filius Pollia Genialis Pollentia miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis centuria Iuli Secundi nnorum XXX stipendiorum X heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-10101839.translation: Lucius Bodlius, son of Lucius, of the Pollian tribe, Genialis, from Pollentia, a miles (soldier) in the 11th Claudian legion, pious and faithful, in the century of Iulius Secundus, aged 30 years, with 10 years of service, his heir arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-10101839.summary: Lucius Bodlius, a soldier in the 11th Claudian legion, set up this funerary monument, arranged by his heir.

EDCS-10101840.text_edition: \n ------] Satrius filius Camilia Rufus Ravenna miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis centuria Fabrici Veri nnorum XXXIII stipendiorum XV testamento fieri iussit Caius Muscius Maternus et Caius Asuvius Crescens heredes faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-10101840.translation: [...] Satrius Rufus, son of Satrius, of the Camillian tribe, from Ravenna, a miles (soldier) in the 11th Claudian legion, pious and faithful, in the century of Fabricius Verus, aged 33 years, with 15 years of service, ordered this to be made in his will; Gaius Muscius Maternus and Gaius Asuvius Crescens, his heirs, arranged for it to be made.
EDCS-10101840.summary: This tombstone for Satrius Rufus, a soldier in the 11th Claudian legion, was commissioned by him in his will and executed by his heirs, Gaius Muscius Maternus and Gaius Asuvius Crescens.

EDCS-10101843.text_edition: \n Sextus Valerius Sexti filius V<o>ltinia Lucinus Vianna sic miles legionis VIII Augustae centuria Terenti Iuliani vixit annis XXIX miles annis VIIII\n
EDCS-10101843.translation: Sextus Valerius Lucinus, son of Sextus, of the Voltinia tribe, from Vianna, a soldier of the 8th Augustan Legion, in the century of Terentius Iulianus, lived 29 years, served 9 years.
EDCS-10101843.summary: This is a tombstone for Sextus Valerius Lucinus, a soldier who died at the age of 29 after 9 years of service, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-10101844.text_edition: \n Caius Vaternius Cai filius Lemonia Aprio Bononia stip<e=F>(ndiorum) XIX heres faciendum curavit \n
EDCS-10101844.translation: Caius Vaternius Aprio, son of Caius, of the Lemonia tribe, from Bononia, a builder of nineteen years' experience, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-10101844.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Caius Vaternius Aprio, a craftsman from Bononia, who had 19 years of experience, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-10101845.text_edition: \n Dacnas Apsaei filius miles cohortis II Cyrrhestarum domo Berea nnorum L stipendiorum XXIV hic situs est\n
EDCS-10101845.translation: Dacnas, son of Apsaeus, a soldier of the 2nd Cohort of Cyrrhestae, from Berea, aged 50 years and serving 24 years, is buried here.
EDCS-10101845.summary: This inscription records the burial of Dacnas, a soldier from Berea serving in the 2nd Cohort of Cyrrhestae, who died at the age of 50 after 24 years of service, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101847.text_edition: \n Caius Egnatius Rogatus Silvano votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-10101847.translation: Caius Egnatius Rogatus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-10101847.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caius Egnatius Rogatus dedicating a vow to the god Silvanus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10101848.text_edition: \n Tertia Claudia <T=I>riviis votum solvit libens merito \n
