EDCS-27500126.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Pomponius Ursus, a member of the college of six who died at 45, erected by his wife Terentia Dafine, who died at 25.

EDCS-27500127.translation: Olybius Amen, freedman of Lucius, a member of the college of six.
EDCS-27500127.summary: This inscription records the name and status of Olybius Amen, freedman of Lucius and member of the college of six.

EDCS-27500128.text_edition: \n lo infanti issimo qui vixit --- dies XX Publius V IIIIIIvir pater et sibi\n
EDCS-27500128.translation: To the most infant [child] who lived 20 days. Publius V---, member of the college of six, father, and for himself.
EDCS-27500128.summary: This inscription records the death of an infant who lived 20 days, dedicated by their father, Publius V---, a member of the college of six, who also set it up for himself.

EDCS-27500130.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Atimeti Ulpi Menophanis Augusti dispensatoris marito bene merenti Aelia Nice coniux\n
EDCS-27500130.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Atimetus, husband of Ulpia Menophanis, imperial administrator, a deserving husband. Aelia Nice, wife.
EDCS-27500130.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Atimetus, husband of Ulpia Menophanis and an imperial administrator, erected by his wife Aelia Nice.

EDCS-27500131.text_edition: \n Victori Flavi Secundi conspectori Epaphroditus eiusdem\n
EDCS-27500131.translation: To Victor, attendant of Flavius Secundus.
EDCS-27500131.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Victor, who served as an attendant to Flavius Secundus.

EDCS-27500132.summary: This inscription records the name Lucius Annaeus, likely a funerary dedication or a building inscription, with an unknown date.

EDCS-27500133.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Anneae An filiae Victorinae pietatis incomparabili vixit annos XX mensem I dies XV Iulius Victor et Annea Sura parentes infelicissimi\n
EDCS-27500133.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Anneia Victorina, daughter of Anneius, of incomparable devotion. She lived 20 years, 1 month, 15 days. Julius Victor and Anneia Sura, most unhappy parents.
EDCS-27500133.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Anneia Victorina, dedicated by her parents, Julius Victor and Anneia Sura, and dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27500134.text_edition: \n N S TAIVS Anteroni L S \n
EDCS-27500134.translation: To Anteronius, son of N.S.taius. L.S.
EDCS-27500134.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a monument to Anteronius, son of N.S.taius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27500135.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Aprilla.
EDCS-27500135.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Aprilla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27500137.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Artorius F<e=F>licissimus Aemiliae Barbarae coniugi pientissimae quae vixit mecum annos LVI queius sic maculam non habui queius sic beneficio me exportavi Salona et ab omnibus meis dunc sic ---? potestatem facere \n
EDCS-27500137.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Aemilia Barbara, my most devoted wife, who lived with me for 56 years and of whom I have never had any fault, and by whose benefit I was carried away from Salona and from all my people, thus… to be able to make power.
EDCS-27500137.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Artorius Felicissimus for his wife Aemilia Barbara, lamenting her death and praising her virtues, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27500138.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Tiberius Claudius Metrodorus sibi et suis vivus fecit\n
EDCS-27500138.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Tiberius Claudius Metrodorus made this for himself and his family while alive.
EDCS-27500138.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Claudius Metrodorus, who dedicated it for himself and his family during his lifetime, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27500139.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Claudiae Engenuae sic Horatius Vivianus uxsori sic bene merenti et suis fecit\n
EDCS-27500139.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Horatius Vivianus made this for his well-deserving wife, Claudia Engenua, and his family.
EDCS-27500139.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Horatius Vivianus for his wife Claudia Engenua and their family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27500140.text_edition: \n RIOCO filio annorum VI et Hermae annorum IIII\n
EDCS-27500140.translation: To Riochus, aged 6 years, and to Herma, aged 4 years.
EDCS-27500140.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating two children, Riochus and Herma, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27500142.summary: This inscription records the name Gaius Julius and an abbreviation, possibly a title or office, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27500143.text_edition: \n Iuliae Secundinae et Iulio Maservae infelicissimis qui vixerunt annos XXIIII menses IIII dies VIIII Secunda mater filiis infelicissimis\n
EDCS-27500143.translation: To Julia Secundina and Julius Maserva, most unhappy, who lived 22 years, 4 months, 9 days. Secunda, most unhappy mother, to her children.
EDCS-27500143.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julia Secundina and Julius Maserva, dedicated by their mother Secunda, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27500144.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Lasius, with the rest of the text lost.

EDCS-27500145.text_edition: \n Lucius Lusius Severinus Curiae Proculae et Lusiae Proculae et Lucio Lusio Maximo filiis sacrum\n
EDCS-27500145.translation: Lucius Lusius Severinus, sacred to the Curia Procula, and to Lusia Procula, and to Lucius Lusius Maximus, his children.
EDCS-27500145.summary: This is a dedication made by Lucius Lusius Severinus to his children, possibly on a family tomb.

EDCS-27500146.text_edition: \n Pomponio Stercorio infanti infelicissimo non clausa in tumulo requiescunt ossa sepulcro istius nunc infantis nec corpus morte sacratum cessit ad infernos \n
EDCS-27500146.translation: To Pomponius Stercorius, most unhappy infant; his bones do not rest in this tomb, nor did his sacred body yield to death and pass to the underworld.
EDCS-27500146.summary: This funerary epitaph laments the death of an infant named Pomponius Stercorius, expressing grief over the circumstances of his burial.

EDCS-27500147.text_edition: \n Publius Publicius Publi libertus Tychicus se vivo fecit sibi et filiis suis Publio Publicio Restituto et Publio Publicio Germano \n
EDCS-27500147.translation: Publius Publicius Tychicus, freedman of Publius, made this in his own lifetime for himself and for his sons Publius Publicius Restitutus and Publius Publicius Germanus.
EDCS-27500147.summary: This is a self-funded funerary monument made by Publius Publicius Tychicus for himself and his sons.

EDCS-27500148.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Salvio Saturnino marito optimo et Probae libertae et Lusiae Publi filiae Memoriae nnorum XXXII mensium VI Claudia Probilla mater infelicissima filiae pientissimae et sibi viva fecit\n
EDCS-27500148.translation: To the Divine Manes. To Lucius Salvius Saturninus, my best husband, and to Proba, his freedwoman, and to Lusia, daughter of Publius, in memory. Aged 32 years, 6 months. Claudia Probilla, most unhappy mother, to her most dutiful daughter, and to herself in her lifetime.
EDCS-27500148.summary: This funerary inscription honors Lucius Salvius Saturninus, a freedwoman named Proba, and Lusia (daughter of Publius), and was set up by Claudia Probilla, the mother.

EDCS-27500149.text_edition: \n Publio Spedio Publi filio Viro Proculo \n
EDCS-27500149.translation: To Publius Spedius, son of Publius, Viro Proculus.
EDCS-27500149.summary: This is a dedication or memorial for Publius Spedius Viro Proculus.

EDCS-27500150.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Valerio Eutychiano pater infelicissimus qui vixit nnos V menses II dies VIII filio dulcissimo et \n
EDCS-27500150.translation: To the Divine Manes. The most unhappy father [sets this up] for Lucius Valerius Eutychianus, who lived 5 years, 2 months, and 8 days, his sweetest son, and [------
EDCS-27500150.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young boy, Lucius Valerius Eutychianus, and was erected by his grieving father.

EDCS-27500158.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-27500158.translation: Marturius made a memorial for himself.
EDCS-27500158.summary: This inscription records that Marturius commissioned a memorial for himself during his lifetime.

EDCS-27500159.text_edition: \n Cereri sanctae Caius Frisius Marinus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-27500159.translation: To holy Ceres, Gaius Frisius Marinus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27500159.summary: This is a votive inscription by Gaius Frisius Marinus dedicated to the goddess Ceres.

EDCS-27500161.text_edition: \n Veneri Victrici Augustae sacrum Caius Vesius Cerdo IIIIIIvir TRIB\n
EDCS-27500161.translation: Sacred to Victory-Bringing Augusta Venus. Gaius Vesius Cerdo, a magistrate of the six priests, a tribune.
EDCS-27500161.summary: This is a dedication to Venus Victrix Augusta by Gaius Vesius Cerdo, a local magistrate.

EDCS-27500162.text_edition: \n Lucius Riccius Luci filius Velina Pessinunte nnorum LX veteranus Marcia Maxima uxor Lucius Riccius Luci filius Valens Lucius Riccius Luci filius Gallus Riccia Luci filia Bulla hic siti sunt Lucius Atilius Luci filius Velina veteranus legionis VII avunculo suo posuit et sibi\n
EDCS-27500162.translation: Lucius Riccius Pessinus, son of Lucius, of the Velina tribe, aged 60, a veteran. Marcia Maxima, his wife; Lucius Riccius Valens, son of Lucius; Lucius Riccius Gallus, son of Lucius; Riccia Bulla, daughter of Lucius, are buried here. Lucius Atilius Pessinus, son of Lucius, a veteran of the 7th legion, erected this monument to his uncle and to himself.
EDCS-27500162.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Riccius Pessinus and his family, erected by his uncle, a veteran of the 7th legion.

EDCS-27500163.text_edition: \n Atiliae Quartae libertae karissimae sacrum Publius Atilius Cognatus\n
EDCS-27500163.translation: Sacred to Atilia Quarta, dearest freedwoman. Publius Atilius Cognatus (dedicated this).
EDCS-27500163.summary: This is a funerary dedication to a freedwoman named Atilia Quarta by Publius Atilius Cognatus.

EDCS-27500164.text_edition: \n Caius Feresius Cai filius Tromentina Venustus magister Augustalis poni curavit locus datus decreto decurionum E annorum XCX F RER LI HS CXIII DC\n
EDCS-27500164.translation: Gaius Feresius Venustus, son of Gaius, of the Tromentina tribe, the Augustalis magister, saw to its erection. The site was granted by decree of the town councillors. Aged 90 years. [details of payment].
EDCS-27500164.summary: This is a public record concerning the erection of a monument by Gaius Feresius Venustus, an Augustalis magister, with the site granted by the town councillors.

EDCS-27500165.text_edition: \n Caio Masurio Masuriano ex testamento ipsius Cassia Laetina heres\n
EDCS-27500165.translation: Cassia Laetina, the heir, (erected this) from the will of Gaius Masurius Masurianus.
EDCS-27500165.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Cassia Laetina, the heir of Gaius Masurius Masurianus, according to his will.

EDCS-27500166.text_edition: \n Tito Mevio Titi filio Tromentina Celso aedili IIIIviro Lupercus \n
EDCS-27500166.translation: To Titus Mevius Celsus, son of Titus, of the Tromentina tribe, aedile and IIIIvir. Lupercus (dedicated this).
EDCS-27500166.summary: This is a dedication to Titus Mevius Celsus, who held the offices of aedile and IIIIvir, by Lupercus.

EDCS-27500167.text_edition: \n Papiae Luci filiae Cale Caius Cominius Fuscus uxori vivos sic fecit\n
EDCS-27500167.translation: Caius Cominius Fuscus made this for his wife Papia, daughter of Lucius, of the Velina tribe, while he was alive.
EDCS-27500167.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Papia, wife of Caius Cominius Fuscus, made by her husband during his lifetime.

EDCS-27500168.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Pomponiae Messillae coniugi karissimae nnorum XXV et Lusiae Proculae coniugi et Lusio Rufino adfini Lucius Caerellius Proculus vivus fecit sibi et libertis libertabusque suis\n
EDCS-27500168.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Caerellius Proculus, while alive, dedicated this to his dearest wife Pomponia Messilla, aged 25, and to his wife Lusia Procula, and to his relative Lusius Rufinus, and to himself and his freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-27500168.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucius Caerellius Proculus for his wives, a relative, himself, and his freedpersons.

EDCS-27500169.text_edition: \n Caio Vibio Severo IIIIIIviro nnum XXV Caius Vibius Ingenuus pater IIIIIIvir Augustalis Flavialis Titialis Nervialis vivos sic fecit et sibi et Vibiae Rhodope uxori libertae vivae et Caio Vibio Primigenio IIIIIIviro liberto optimo\n
EDCS-27500169.translation: Caius Vibius Ingenuus, father, an Augustalis Flavialis Titialis Nervialis IIIIvir, while alive, made this for his son Caius Vibius Severus, aged 25, a IIIIvir, and for himself, and for his freedwoman wife Vibia Rhodope, and for his excellent freedman Caius Vibius Primigenius, a IIIIvir.
EDCS-27500169.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Vibius Severus and others, erected by his father Caius Vibius Ingenuus, a prominent local official, during his lifetime.

EDCS-27500170.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly a name from a tomb or dedication.

EDCS-27500172.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Pulchra et Ursus parentes Viperino filio pientissimo posuerunt bene merenti sibi et suis\n
EDCS-27500172.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Pulchra and Ursus, parents, erected this for their most devoted son Viperinus, who deserved well, and for themselves and their family.
EDCS-27500172.summary: This is a funerary dedication by parents, Pulchra and Ursus, to their son Viperinus.

EDCS-27500173.text_edition: \n Aurelio Veturio decurioni municipii Novensium defuncto nnorum XXX Aurelia Veturia fratri piissimo posuit\n
EDCS-27500173.translation: To Aurelius Veturio, councilor of the municipality of Novensium, who died at the age of 30, Aurelia Veturia erected this for her most devoted brother.
EDCS-27500173.summary: A tombstone for Aurelius Veturio, a councilor of Novensium, erected by his sister.

EDCS-27500174.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Viselliae Paulinae lia Marcellina matri carissimae posuit\n
EDCS-27500174.translation: To the divine shades, of Visellia Paulina. Marcellina erected this for her most cherished mother.
EDCS-27500174.summary: A funerary inscription for Visellia Paulina, dedicated by her daughter Marcellina.

EDCS-27500175.text_edition: \n Litorea praessus sic scruposae margine rupis inriguus gelido defluit amne latex cuius perspicuo per levia saxa meatu praedulcis salsam perluit unda Thetyn sic indigenis gratus preterlabentibus! almus incola delicias advena laudat opes salve Nymfa sic meos dignata invisere finis sic et celebrem cunctis conciliare locum nostra salutifero tu mactas praedia fonte Licinianus ego carmine te dominus diversum sortita capis finemque caputque Nymfa sic caput cautes terminus unda tibi est quis queat arcanum sapiens pernoscere fontis nasceris e scopulis fons moriture fretis hoc Pelagia suos fontes epigrammate donat magne tui pignus Liciniane tori\n
EDCS-27500175.translation: Near the edge of the rough cliff, a spring flows with a cold stream, whose clear water washes the salty sea over smooth rocks. Welcome to the inhabitants, and to the visitors who pass by! The honored spring, the bounty of the land, the foreigner praises its riches. Hail, Nympha, that you have deigned to visit my lands and make my estate celebrated with your health-giving spring. I, Licinianus, your master, chose you with a poem, Nympha, as the end and head of the fountain; the rocks, the boundary, the water, these are yours. Who can wisely discern the secret of the spring? You are born from rocks, you will die in the seas. Pelagia dedicates these springs to you with an epigram, a great pledge of your marriage bed, Licinianus.
EDCS-27500175.summary: A poetic dedication to a Nymph associated with a spring, possibly erected by Licinianus, celebrating the water's purity and life-giving properties.

EDCS-27500176.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Felex sic posuit titulum conpari suae Serciae Maximae Iuniae vita functae annorum XXXX coniux carissima mecum fecit nns XXI\n
EDCS-27500176.translation: To the divine shades. Aurelius Felex erected this monument for his wife Sercia Maxima, who passed away at the age of 40, his most cherished wife. They lived together for 21 years.
EDCS-27500176.summary: A tombstone for Sercia Maxima, who died at 40, erected by her husband Aurelius Felex, commemorating their 21 years of marriage.

EDCS-27500177.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Numerius Valens\n
EDCS-27500177.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest. Lucius Numerius Valens [dedicated this].
EDCS-27500177.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Numerius Valens.

EDCS-27500178.text_edition: \n sacrum Daphnus P C vilicus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-27500178.translation: Sacred. Daphnus, a freedman of Claudius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-27500178.summary: A record of a vow fulfilled by Daphnus, a freedman, possibly to a deity.

EDCS-27500179.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Licinia Prima et Licinius Gerontius Licinio Crescenti coniux et filius adhuc intrepidantis aetatis patri cui non licuit infantiam aeius sic ad suos annos perducere pio et bene merenti et omnium amatori suorum et amicorum digno hunc titulum posuerunt\n
EDCS-27500179.translation: To the divine shades. Licinia Prima and Licinius Gerontius, wife and son, erected this monument for their father, who died at an intrepid age, to their father who did not live to see his childhood reach its years, devoted and well-deserving, beloved by all his own and his friends.
EDCS-27500179.summary: A funerary inscription for a deceased father, erected by his wife and son, lamenting his premature death before experiencing childhood.

EDCS-27500180.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Allius Firminus Ursino filio Cai Septimi Carpophori delicato infelicissimo puero naufragio obito annorum XI cuius membra consum<p>sit maris sic per se quot nomen titulus praestat suisque dolorem\n
EDCS-27500180.translation: To the divine shades. For Marcus Allius Firminus, the beloved son of Ursina, daughter of Caius Septimius Carpophorus, an unfortunate boy of 11 years, who perished in a shipwreck, whose body the sea consumed, so that only his name remains for the monument and sorrow for his own.
EDCS-27500180.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Allius Firminus, an 11-year-old boy who drowned in a shipwreck, erected by his father.

EDCS-27500181.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Allia M A P ET coniugi IMET P \n
EDCS-27500181.translation: To the divine shades. Allia M A P [erected this] for her husband IMET P.
EDCS-27500181.summary: A funerary inscription for a husband, dedicated by his wife Allia M A P.

EDCS-27500182.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Messilla filio infelicissimo suo qui vixxit sic plus minus annos III Aurelia Messoria titulum posuit\n
EDCS-27500182.translation: To the divine shades. For her most unfortunate son, who lived approximately 3 years, Aurelia Messilla erected this monument. Aurelia Messoria [also dedicated it].
EDCS-27500182.summary: A tombstone for a young son who died around the age of three, erected by his mother Aurelia Messilla, with Aurelia Messoria also contributing.

EDCS-27500183.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Firminae infelici inconparabili sic uxsori sic Victor posuit bene merenti\n
EDCS-27500183.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Victor set this up for Firmina, his incomparable and unfortunate wife, his wife indeed, well deserving.
EDCS-27500183.summary: A tombstone set up by Victor for his deceased wife Firmina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27500184.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerius Maximus et Cornelia Vera cum dolum tantum haberent de Valerio delicato quam ut filio infelicissimo iuveni dulcissimo nnorum XXIIII dunc sic dolum haberit sic Cornelia Vendemia de marito Valerio et socero puero pientissimo titulum posuit\n
EDCS-27500184.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Valerius Maximus and Cornelia Vera, having only grief for Valerius, their most unfortunate and sweetest son, aged 24, thus felt grief, and Cornelia Vendemia set up this monument for her husband Valerius and for her most devoted boy.
EDCS-27500184.summary: A tombstone erected by Cornelia Vendemia for her son Valerius and her husband Valerius, lamenting their deaths and praising their devotion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27500185.text_edition: \n Aurelius Nigrinus Herculi Augusto crateram fecit\n
EDCS-27500185.translation: Aurelius Nigrinus made this crater for Hercules Augustus.
EDCS-27500185.summary: A dedication of a crater (bowl) to the god Hercules Augustus by Aurelius Nigrinus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27500186.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Victori qui vixit annos VIIII Valeria Severina et Messor Firmionis delicato in<feli>cissimo\n
EDCS-27500186.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Victor, who lived 9 years, Valeria Severina and Messor Firmionis set this up for their most unfortunate and beloved son.
EDCS-27500186.summary: A tombstone erected by Valeria Severina and Messor Firmionis for their young son Victor, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601564.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Diogeni Athicti filio Diogenes Augusti libertus tabularius provinciae Dalmatiae nepoti posuit\n
EDCS-27601564.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Diogenes, son of Athictus. Diogenes, freedman of Augustus, scribe of the province of Dalmatia, set this up for his grandson.
EDCS-27601564.summary: A tombstone set up by Diogenes, an imperial freedman and provincial scribe, for his grandson Diogenes, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601565.text_edition: \n Phrygio Auggustorum nnostrorum ab instrumentis defuncto nnorum XXXVIII Lucius Aurelius Castus et Gaius Iulius Honoratus filii patri bene merenti posuerunt et Iuliae Valeriae matri defunctae nnorum XXX\n
EDCS-27601565.translation: Lucius Aurelius Castus and Gaius Iulius Honoratus set this up for their well-deserving father, Phrygio, attendant of our Emperors, from the administrative staff, who died at 38 years of age, and also for their mother, Iulia Valeria, who died at 30 years of age.
EDCS-27601565.summary: A tombstone erected by two sons for their deceased father, Phrygio, an imperial administrator, and their mother Iulia Valeria, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601566.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quintiano vernae Augusti vilico et arcario XX hereditatium Iulia Helpis coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-27601566.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Quintianus, a household slave of Augustus, steward and treasurer of 20 estates. Iulia Helpis set this up for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-27601566.summary: A tombstone set up by Iulia Helpis for her husband Quintianus, who served as a slave and administrator for Augustus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601567.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Thaumasto Augusti conmentariensi sic aurariarum Delmatarum felicissimus dispensator titulum posuit\n
EDCS-27601567.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Thaumastus, the most fortunate supervisor of the Augustan mints of Dalmatia, he himself set up this monument.
EDCS-27601567.summary: A tombstone set up by Thaumastus, an administrator of Augustan mines in Dalmatia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601568.summary: A very fragmentary inscription that likely refers to the Roman province of Illyricum, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601569.text_edition: \n Publio Aelio Lupo beneficiario consularis et Vibiae Forti Aelia Tethis coniugi et filiae suis bene merenti et Aeliae Ursillae filiae nnorum XXV\n
EDCS-27601569.translation: For Publius Aelius Lupus, a beneficiary of the consular legate, and for Vibius Fortis. Aelia Tethis set this up for her deserving wife and daughter, and for Aelia Urscilla, her daughter, aged 25.
EDCS-27601569.summary: A tombstone set up by Aelia Tethis for her wife, daughter, and another daughter Aelia Urscilla, honoring a beneficiary of a consular legate, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601570.text_edition: \n Aelio Ursacio militi cohortis VIII voluntariorum qui vixit annis XXV mensibus VIIII marito pientissimo posuit\n
EDCS-27601570.translation: To Aelius Ursacius, soldier of the 8th volunteer cohort, who lived 25 years and 9 months, his most devoted husband placed [this].
EDCS-27601570.summary: This epitaph records the death of Aelius Ursacius, a soldier in the 8th volunteer cohort, and was set up by his husband.

EDCS-27601571.text_edition: \n Aurelio Apollinari veterano Aurelius Delmatius filius patri pientissimo\n
EDCS-27601571.translation: To Aurelius Apollinaris, a veteran, Aurelius Delmatius, his son, for his most devoted father.
EDCS-27601571.summary: This epitaph commemorates Aurelius Apollinaris, a veteran, and was set up by his son.

EDCS-27601572.text_edition: \n Tito Aurelio Apollonio fratri eius |(centurioni) cohortis I |(milliariae) defuncto Sirmi nnorum XXXIII Aurelius Aquila pater infelicissimus vivus fecit et Aureli sic <F=E>lavi sic negotiantis sic natione Suri sic defuncto annorum LV Sirmi et Aurelio Luciano defuncto annorum amico eorum merenti \n
EDCS-27601572.translation: To Titus Aurelius Apollonius, his brother, a centurion of the 1st Millarain Cohort, who died at Sirmium at the age of 33. Aurelius Aquila, his most unhappy father, made this while alive, and also for Aurelius Flavi, a merchant of Syrian nationality, who died at Sirmium at the age of 55, and for Aurelius Lucianus, their friend, who deserved well.
EDCS-27601572.summary: This epitaph records the death of Titus Aurelius Apollonius, a centurion, and two other individuals, likely set up by his father.

EDCS-27601573.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Eutichiano sic infelicissimo ex cursore pravato sic qui confecit sub die milia XCIV qui vixit annos XXV menses duos dies VIIII Aurelius Graecio fratri pientissimo hanc memoriam posuit\n
EDCS-27601573.translation: To the departed spirits of Aurelius Eutichianus, most unfortunate, a corrupted courier, who completed the work of 90,000 miles, who lived 25 years, 2 months, and 9 days. Aurelius Graecius, his most devoted brother, placed this memorial.
EDCS-27601573.summary: This epitaph commemorates Aurelius Eutichianus, a courier who died at the age of 25, and was erected by his brother.

EDCS-27601574.text_edition: \n Aurelius Martialis miles legionis I Italici sic stipendiorum XXVII et Aelia coniux aeius sic et Martiali filio eorum uno fato decepti Disdozi frater et heres eorum posuit\n
EDCS-27601574.translation: To Aurelius Martialis, soldier of the 1st Italian Legion, with 27 years of service, and Aelia, his wife, and Martialis, their son, taken by a single fate. Disdozi, their brother and heir, placed this.
EDCS-27601574.summary: This epitaph records the deaths of Aurelius Martialis, a soldier, his wife Aelia, and their son Martialis, and was erected by their brother and heir.

EDCS-27601575.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Mucatrae militi legionis I Italicae stipendiorum XIIII Aurelius Sabasianus miles legionis supra scriptae bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-27601575.translation: To the departed spirits of Aurelius Mucatra, soldier of the 1st Italian Legion, with 14 years of service. Aurelius Sabasianus, a soldier of the aforementioned legion, erected this for his well-deserving comrade.
EDCS-27601575.summary: This epitaph commemorates Aurelius Mucatra, a soldier in the 1st Italian Legion, and was erected by a fellow soldier.

EDCS-27601576.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaio Baebidio Marcello militi cohortis III Alpinorum vexillario equitum defuncto nnorum XXIII Anna alumno infelicissimo titulum posuit\n
EDCS-27601576.translation: To the departed spirits of Gaius Baebidius Marcellus, soldier of the 3rd Alpine Cohort, a cavalry standard-bearer, who died at the age of 23. Anna, his most unhappy foster-mother, placed this monument.
EDCS-27601576.summary: This epitaph records the death of Gaius Baebidius Marcellus, a cavalry standard-bearer, and was set up by his foster-mother.

EDCS-27601577.text_edition: \n Marcus Blaesius Tertullus miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis Papiriae Pyrallidi bene merenti hospitae suae annorum XXV de suo fecit\n
EDCS-27601577.translation: Marcus Blaesius Tertullus, soldier of the 11th Claudian Legion, pious and loyal, for Papiria Pyrallis, his deserving host, who lived 25 years, made this from his own resources.
EDCS-27601577.summary: This epitaph records Marcus Blaesius Tertullus, a soldier, who erected a monument for his deserving hostess, Papiria Pyrallis.

EDCS-27601578.text_edition: \n Lucius Caesius Luci filius Camilia Bassus domo Pisauri veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis annorum LIII stipendiorum XXXII hic situs est testamento fieri iussit heres posuit in fronte pedes VI in agro pedes X\n
EDCS-27601578.translation: Lucius Caesius Bassus, son of Lucius, of the Camilia tribe, from Pisaurum, a veteran of the 7th Claudian Legion, pious and loyal, aged 53 years with 32 years of service, lies here; he ordered this to be made in his will, his heir placed it. Six feet in front, ten feet in the field.
EDCS-27601578.summary: This epitaph records the burial of Lucius Caesius Bassus, a veteran of the 7th Claudian Legion, who stipulated its construction in his will.

EDCS-27601579.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tiberio Claudio Ianuario speculatori legionis XIIII geminae commentariensi consularis provinciae Delmatiae stipendiorum XIII Aebutia Ianuaria filio piissimo et Visellia Iulia marito\n
EDCS-27601579.translation: To the departed spirits of Tiberius Claudius Ianuarius, a scout of the 14th Gemina Legion, a scribe of the governor of the province of Dalmatia, with 13 years of service. Aebutia Ianuaria for her most devoted son and Visellia Iulia for her husband.
EDCS-27601579.summary: This epitaph commemorates Tiberius Claudius Ianuarius, a legionary scout and provincial scribe, and was erected by his mother and wife.

EDCS-27601580.text_edition: \n Cloutius Clutami filius duplicarius alae nnum Susarrus domo Curunniace annorum XXXV stipendiorum XI- hic situs est posuit CA \n
EDCS-27601580.translation: Cloutius, son of Clutamus, a duplicarius of the ala Augusta, from Curunniace, aged 35, with 11 years of service, lies here. CA [placed this].
EDCS-27601580.summary: A funerary inscription for Cloutius, a duplicarius in the ala Augusta, recorded with his age, service length, and place of origin, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-27601581.text_edition: \n Lucius Cornelius Luci <f=E>(ilius) Maecia Pelagonia ve<t=R>(eranus) legionis XI nnorum XLV stipendiorum XXV hic situs est filii posuerunt \n
EDCS-27601581.translation: Lucius Cornelius Pelagonius, son of Lucius, of the Maecia tribe, a veteran of the 11th legion, aged 45, with 25 years of service, lies here. His sons erected this.
EDCS-27601581.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Cornelius Pelagonius, a veteran of the 11th legion, detailing his age, service length, tribal affiliation, and stating it was erected by his sons, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-27601583.text_edition: \n Caius Curiatius Titi filius Sergia Secundus domo Augusta Troade veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis scriba Salonis nnum LXV hic situs est in fronte pedes in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-27601583.translation: Caius Curiatius Secundus, son of Titus, of the Sergia tribe, from Augusta Troade, a veteran of the 7th Claudian legion, pious and loyal, scribe of Salona, aged 65, lies here. In front [of the monument] 20 feet in the ground.
EDCS-27601583.summary: A funerary inscription for Caius Curiatius Secundus, a veteran scribe of the 7th Claudian legion, providing his age, tribal affiliation, origin, and dimensions of the plot, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27601584.text_edition: \n Lucius Fabius Luci filius Saturninus veteranus legionis VII pia Claudiae fidelis vivus fecit sibi et Claudiae Tiberi Claudi Alypi liberti Caesaris libertae Euche hic siti sunt locum donavit Caius Titius Restitutus amicus hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-27601584.translation: Lucius Fabius Saturninus, son of Lucius, a veteran of the 7th Claudian legion, pious and loyal, made this while alive for himself and for Euchē, freedwoman of Lucius Claudius Alypus, freedman of the emperor; they lie here. The place was granted by Caius Titius Restitutus, a friend. This monument will not bind the heir.
EDCS-27601584.summary: A funerary monument built during the lifetime of Lucius Fabius Saturninus, a veteran of the 7th Claudian legion, for himself and his freedwoman, with the plot donated by a friend and a clause concerning inheritance, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-27601585.text_edition: \n Tito Flavio Titi filio Tromentina Agricolae decurioni coloniae Salonitanae aedili IIviro iure dicundo decurioni coloniae Aequitatis IIviro quinquennali dispunctori municipii Riditarum praefecto et patrono collegii fabrum ob merita eius collegium fabrum ex aere conlato curatori rei publicae Splonisstarum tribuno legionis X geminae piae fidelis Tito Vettio Augustali decurioni coloniae Salonitanae quaestori aedili IIviro iure dicundo praefecto et patrono collegii fabrum ob merita eius collegium fabrum ex aere conlato\n
EDCS-27601585.translation: To Titus Flavius Agricola, son of Titus, of the Tromentina tribe, decurion of the colony of Salona, aedile, duovir with judicial power, decurion of the colony of Aequitas, quinquennial duovir, dispunctor of the municipality of Riditae, prefect and patron of the guild of builders, for his merits the guild of builders, from a money contribution. Curator of the public affairs of the Splonestars, tribune of the 10th Gemina legion, pious and loyal. To Titus Vettius Augustalis, decurion of the colony of Salona, quaestor, aedile, duovir with judicial power, prefect and patron of the guild of builders, for his merits the guild of builders, from a money contribution.
EDCS-27601585.summary: A dedication honoring two prominent civic and military officials, Titus Flavius Agricola and Titus Vettius Augustalis, detailing their numerous public offices and military careers, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27601586.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavio Caroso veterano Attigia Ursacia compatri benignissimo\n
EDCS-27601586.translation: To the departed spirits. For Flavius Carosus, a most kind veteran, and Attigia Ursacia, his fellow countrywoman.
EDCS-27601586.summary: A funerary dedication to Flavius Carosus, a veteran, and Attigia Ursacia, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27601587.text_edition: \n Lucio Praecilio Luci filio Clementi Iuliano pontifici nni designato flamini patrono coloniae primipilari legionis V Macedonicae praefecto castrorum legionis eiusdem veterani qui militaverunt sub Publio Memmio Regulo legato Augusti et missi sunt Quinto <F=E>utetio Lusio Saturnino Marco Seio Verano consulibus curam gerentibus Tiberio Claudio Celso Tiberi Augusti COMBIO Marco Tauritio Marcellino locus datus publice \n
EDCS-27601587.translation: To Lucius Praecilius Clemens Julianus, son of Lucius, designated pontiff, flamen, patron of the colony, primipilaris of the 5th Macedonian legion, camp prefect of the same legion, veterans who served under Publius Memmius Regulus, legate of the emperor, and were dismissed under Quintus Futetius Lusius Saturninus and Marcus Seius Veranus, consuls, during the administration of Tiberius Claudius Celsus, son of Tiberius Augustus, and Marcus Tauritius Marcellinus. A plot of land was publicly granted.
EDCS-27601587.summary: A dedication for Lucius Praecilius Clemens Julianus, a high-ranking military officer and local dignitary, detailing his career and honors, and mentioning the public grant of land, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-27601588.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Flavio Pomponiano |(centurioni) legionis II Traianae fortis |(centurioni) legionis IIII Flaviae |(centurioni) legionis XII fulminatae |(centurioni) legionis XVI Flaviae |(centurioni) legionis XIIII geminae Martiae |(centurioni) legionis II Tr<a=O>(ianae) fortis heredes \n
EDCS-27601588.translation: To the departed spirits. For Titus Flavius Pomponianus, centurion of the 2nd Trajana Fortis legion, centurion of the 4th Flavia legion, centurion of the 12th Fulminata legion, centurion of the 16th Flavia legion, centurion of the 12th Gemina Martia legion, centurion of the 2nd Trajana Fortis legion. His heirs [dedicated this].
EDCS-27601588.summary: A funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Pomponianus, a career centurion who served in multiple legions, with his heirs dedicating the monument, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-27601589.translation: RM Herennius of the 1st Italic legion.
EDCS-27601589.summary: A fragment mentioning Herennius of the 1st Italic legion, likely part of a larger inscription, dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-27601590.text_edition: \n Quinto Hortensio Quinti filio veterano legionis VII hic situs est Hortensia Quinti filia Quarta et Lucius Corellius Iucundus patri posuere\n
EDCS-27601590.translation: To Quintus Hortensius, son of Quintus, a veteran of the 7th legion. Hortensia Quarta, daughter of Quintus, and Lucius Corellius Iucundus placed this for their father.
EDCS-27601590.summary: A funerary inscription for Quintus Hortensius, a veteran of the 7th legion, erected by his daughter and son-in-law, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-27601591.text_edition: \n Diophantus Alexandri filius de liburna Murena vixit annos XXXVIII militavit annos XVI hic situs\n
EDCS-27601591.translation: Diophantus, son of Alexandrus, from the warship Murena, lived for 38 years, served for 16 years; he is buried here.
EDCS-27601591.summary: This is a tombstone for Diophantus, a soldier who served on the warship Murena, who died at the age of 38.

EDCS-27601592.text_edition: \n Marcus Iulius Marci filius Voltinia Paternus Aquis Sextiis miles legionis VI victricis |(centurio) legionis VIII Augustae |(centurio) legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis |(centurio) legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis testamento fieri iussit ex HS Xdecem milibus Iulia Titi filia Maxima uxor et Marcus Iulius Marci libertus Docimus heredes faciendum curaverunt\n
EDCS-27601592.translation: Marcus Iulius Paternus, son of Marcus, of the Voltinia tribe, from Aquae Sextiae, a soldier of the VI Victrix legion, then a centurion of the VIII Augusta legion, then a centurion of the XIV Gemina Martia Victrix legion, then a centurion of the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, ordered this to be made by his will from 10,000 sesterces; Iulia Maxima, daughter of Titus, his wife, and his freedman Marcus Iulius Docimus, his heirs, saw to its construction.
EDCS-27601592.summary: This is a funerary monument for Marcus Iulius Paternus, a career centurion in several legions, funded by his will and completed by his wife and freedman heir.

EDCS-27601593.text_edition: \n Tito Laelio Titi filio Severo |(centurioni) legionis III Cyrenaicae\n
EDCS-27601593.translation: To Titus Laelius Severus, son of Titus, a centurion of the III Cyrenaica legion.
EDCS-27601593.summary: This is an epitaph for Titus Laelius Severus, a centurion in the III Cyrenaica legion.

EDCS-27601594.text_edition: \n Caius Lucretius signifer legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis domo Verona annorum XLV stipendiorum XXVI de suo peculio faciendum iussit sibi et Primo suo curam egerunt frater et Chrestus libertus in fronte pedes X in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-27601594.translation: Gaius Lucretius, a standard-bearer of the VII Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, from Verona, aged 45, with 26 years of service, ordered this to be made from his own peculium for himself and his beloved Primo; his brother and his freedman Chrestus managed the work; 10 feet in front, 20 feet in the field.
EDCS-27601594.summary: This is a funerary monument funded by Gaius Lucretius, a standard-bearer of the VII Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, for himself and his companion Primo, detailing plot dimensions.

EDCS-27601595.text_edition: \n Caius Lucretius veteranus legionis VII Claudiae piae fidelis domo Verona vivus fecit sibi et Provinciali filio et Firmillae et Tyche libertae suisque omnibus in fronte pedes X in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-27601595.translation: Gaius Lucretius, a veteran of the VII Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, from Verona, made this for himself while alive, and for his son Provincialis, and Firmilla, and his freedwoman Tyche, and all his own people; 10 feet in front, 20 feet in the field.
EDCS-27601595.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Gaius Lucretius, a veteran of the VII Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, for himself and his family and freedmen, detailing plot dimensions.

EDCS-27601596.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Masclinio Romulo veterano integerrimo GERENTI AVTORITE nnorum \n
EDCS-27601596.translation: To the departed spirits: Gaius Masclinius Romulus, most upright veteran, [holding authority] for [many] years.
EDCS-27601596.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Gaius Masclinius Romulus, an honorable veteran, commemorating his authority.

EDCS-27601597.text_edition: \n XII bixit sic annos XXX Caius Memmius Hierax optio fratri bene merente sic posuit\n
EDCS-27601597.translation: Lived 30 years. Gaius Memmius Hierax, optio, erected this for his well-deserving brother.
EDCS-27601597.summary: This is an epitaph for an unnamed man who lived 30 years, set up by his brother Gaius Memmius Hierax.

EDCS-27601598.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio P Ver militi cohortis VIII voluntariorum annorum XVIIII Helius domino bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-27601598.translation: To the departed spirits: Gaius P. Ver, a soldier of the VIII cohort of volunteers, aged 19. Helius erected this for his deserving master.
EDCS-27601598.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Gaius P. Ver, a young soldier in the VIII cohort of volunteers, erected by his master Helius.

EDCS-27601599.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Sabinio Niciati equiti ex singularibus homini verecundissimo stipendiorum XXV Flavia Tertia coniugi inconparabili sic cum quo vixit nnos XXV bene merenti\n
EDCS-27601599.translation: To the departed spirits: Gaius Sabinius Niciatus, an equestrian from the singulares, a most modest man, with 25 years of service. Flavia Tertia, his incomparable wife, with whom he lived for 25 years, erected this for him, his deserving husband.
EDCS-27601599.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Sabinius Niciatus, a modest equestrian soldier with 25 years of service, erected by his wife Flavia Tertia.

EDCS-27601600.text_edition: \n Titus Sabinius Titi filius Fabia domo Sebaste veteranus legionis VII nnorum LVI stipendiorum XXXII hic situs est\n
EDCS-27601600.translation: Titus Sabinius, son of Titus, of the Fabia tribe, from Sebaste, a veteran of the VII legion, aged 56, with 32 years of service, is buried here.
EDCS-27601600.summary: This is a tombstone for Titus Sabinius, a veteran of the VII legion, detailing his age, service length, and place of burial.

EDCS-27601601.text_edition: \n Quinto Servilio Marci filio Palatina Pacuviano aedili praefecto cohortis I Morinorum Marcus Servilius Copiensis Pacuvianus pater fecit et sibi\n
EDCS-27601601.translation: To Quintus Servilius Pacuvianus, son of Marcus, of the Palatine tribe, aedile and prefect of the I Cohort of the Morini, Marcus Servilius Copiensis Pacuvianus, his father, made this, and for himself.
EDCS-27601601.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by a father to his son, Quintus Servilius Pacuvianus, who held military and civic offices, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-27601602.text_edition: \n Tito Statilio Maximo militi cohortis VIII voluntariorum adiutori cornicularii consularis defuncto annorum XXV Iulia Fructula hospita et sibi\n
EDCS-27601602.translation: To Titus Statilius Maximus, soldier of the VIII Cohort of Volunteers, assistant to the consular cornicularius, who died at 25 years of age, Iulia Fructula, his hospita (housemate/lodger), made this, and for herself.
EDCS-27601602.summary: This is a funerary monument for Titus Statilius Maximus, a soldier who died young, set up by his hospita, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-27601603.text_edition: \n Titi Titiseni Convictoriani decurionis coloniae Salonitanae sacerdotis Titus Titisenus Valentinus |(centurio) legionis filius\n
EDCS-27601603.translation: To Titus Titisenus Convictorianus, decurion of the colony of Salona, priest, Titus Titisenus Valentinus, son of a centurion of the legion, [set this up].
EDCS-27601603.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Titisenus Convictorianus, a local official and priest in Salona, erected by his son, a centurion, probably from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-27601604.text_edition: \n Caio Valerio veterano legionis XI pia fidelis Valerius I patrono \n
EDCS-27601604.translation: To Gaius Valerius, veteran of the XI Legion Pia Fidelis. Valerius [made this for] his patron.
EDCS-27601604.summary: This is a dedication from a patron to his former soldier, a veteran of the XI Legion, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-27601605.text_edition: \n Gaio Valerio Gai filio Claudia Marcello III militi cohortis \n
EDCS-27601605.translation: To Gaius Valerius, son of Gaius, [serving as] III soldier of the cohort, Claudia Marcello.
EDCS-27601605.summary: This inscription records Gaius Valerius, a soldier serving in the third cohort, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-27601606.text_edition: \n Lucio Vario Luci filio Sergia domo Augusta Praetoria legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis |(centurio) cohortis VI vigilum Lucius Varius Laetus Pu et Benniae Sabinae vivae in fronte pedes [in agro pedes ------\n
EDCS-27601606.translation: To Lucius Varus, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, from Augusta Praetoria, a centurion of the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis Legion, [and] of the VI Cohort of Vigiles. Lucius Varus Laetus [made this] for himself and for Bennia Sabina, while alive. In front, X feet; in the field, X feet.
EDCS-27601606.summary: This is a funerary monument for Lucius Varus, a centurion in both a legion and a cohort of vigiles, likely from the late 1st or 2nd century CE, specifying plot dimensions.

EDCS-27601607.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus V<a>rronius Maro frumen<t=I>arius legionis III Quirenaricae sic qui cucurrit <f=T>rumentarius nnos XL et centurio frumentariorum factus modo Firminus libertus eius posuit locus concessus \n
EDCS-27601607.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Titus Varrronius Maro, grain merchant of the III Quirenca Legion, who served as a grain merchant for 40 years and had just been made a centurion of grain merchants, was placed here. Firminus, his freedman, erected this. The plot was granted.
EDCS-27601607.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Varrronius Maro, a high-ranking civilian administrator (grain merchant and centurion of grain merchants) in the III Quirenca Legion, set up by his freedman, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-27601608.text_edition: \n Virdomarus Thartontis filius domo Biturix missicius alae Claudiae novae testamento fieri iussit et Pamae sorori\n
EDCS-27601608.translation: Virdomarus, son of Thartontis, from the Bituriges, a discharged soldier of the ala Claudia Nova, ordered this to be made in his will, and for Pamae, his sister.
EDCS-27601608.summary: This funerary monument, established by will, is for Virdomarus, a discharged cavalry soldier from the ala Claudia Nova, and his sister, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-27601609.text_edition: \n Marco Uttedio Sallubiano Caio Petilio Amando domo Iguvio veterano legionis XIIII geminae decurioni coloniae Salonitanae quaestori pontifici Petilia Secundina coniux\n
EDCS-27601609.translation: To Marcus Uttedius Sallubianus, Gaius Petilius Amandus, from Iguvium, veteran of the XIIII Gemina Legion, decurion of the colony of Salona, quaestor, pontifex. Petilia Secundina, his wife, [set this up].
EDCS-27601609.summary: This is a funerary monument for Marcus Uttedius Sallubianus, a veteran of the XIV Legion and a prominent local official in Salona, erected by his wife, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-27601610.text_edition: \n Zosime Statilius Pulcher miles cohortis I Belgarum ex stratore consularis uxori bene merenti duplicarius\n
EDCS-27601610.translation: To Zosime, wife of Statilius Pulcher, soldier of the I Cohort of Belgae, former strator consularis, for his well-deserving wife, a duplicarius.
EDCS-27601610.summary: This funerary monument is dedicated by Statilius Pulcher, a soldier of the I Cohort of Belgae and former strator consularis, to his deserving wife Zosime, likely from the late 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-27601611.text_edition: \n tribuno militum legionis XIII geminae flamini Flaviali \n
EDCS-27601611.translation: To the tribune of soldiers of the thirteenth legion Gemina, Flamen Flavialis.
EDCS-27601611.summary: This inscription likely records the name and offices of a military tribune and priest, possibly as part of a larger monument.

EDCS-27601612.text_edition: \n Pomptina Arretio miles legionis VII anniorum VM stipendiorum IX hic situs\n
EDCS-27601612.translation: Arretio of the Pomptina tribe, soldier of the seventh legion, aged 45 years, with 9 years of service, lies here.
EDCS-27601612.summary: This is a tombstone for Arretio, a soldier of the seventh legion, noting his age and years of service.

EDCS-27601614.text_edition: \n Agriae Ianuariae defunctae annis XXXXI filiae verginiae sic et coniugi incomparabili cum qua concorditer egit sine querela per annos XXVI Marcus Iulius Felix ab sic ratione fisci vivus posuit vivas qui dixeris vivit Elysiis\n
EDCS-27601614.translation: To Agria Ianuaria, deceased at 41 years, a virgin daughter, and also to an incomparable wife, with whom he lived harmoniously without complaint for 26 years, Marcus Iulius Felix, acting as tax official, erected this monument while alive, declaring 'she lives' so that he who says it lives in Elysium.
EDCS-27601614.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Marcus Iulius Felix for his wife Agria Ianuaria, detailing their married life and his wish for her eternal peace.

EDCS-27601615.text_edition: \n Marcus Ulpius Alexander Augusti libertus ab auctoritatibus Marcus Ulpius Asiaticus filius Marcus Ulpius Alexander Marcus Ulpius Theodorus liberti Ulpia Procla vernacula \n
EDCS-27601615.translation: Marcus Ulpius Alexander, freedman of Augustus, by his patrons Marcus Ulpius Asiaticus, his son Marcus Ulpius Alexander, Marcus Ulpius Theodorus, and his freedwoman Ulpia Procla.
EDCS-27601615.summary: This inscription lists members of a family and their freedpersons, likely indicating ownership or dedication of a property or monument.

EDCS-27601616.text_edition: \n Marcus Antonius Marci filius Sergia Surus veteranus legionis IIII Flaviae felicis testamento fieri iussit sibi et Pasiae Primae uxori et libertis libertabusque et Hermeti in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XIX hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-27601616.translation: Marcus Antonius Surus, son of Marcus, of the Sergia tribe, a veteran of the fourth legion Flavia Felix, ordered by his will that this monument be made for himself and for his wife Pasia Prima, and for their freedmen and freedwomen, and for Hermetus; 12 feet in front and 19 feet in depth; this monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-27601616.summary: This is a funerary monument commissioned by a veteran, Marcus Antonius Surus, for himself and his family, specifying dimensions and inheritance exclusion.

EDCS-27601617.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Po<n=M>tianus sic miles legionis primae Italicae stipendiorum XXVI <q=O>ui vixit annos L Aur<e=I>lius <Q=O>uintianus titulum patri pientissimo posuit \n
EDCS-27601617.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Aurelius Pontianus, soldier of the first legion Italica, with 26 years of service, who lived 50 years. Aurelius Quintianus placed this inscription for his most devoted father.
EDCS-27601617.summary: This is a tombstone for Aurelius Pontianus, a soldier of the first legion Italica, erected by his son Aurelius Quintianus.

EDCS-27601618.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Dionysius Firmus veteranus ex classe praetoria Rannatis Marcus Dionysius Firmus filius patri piissimo\n
EDCS-27601618.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Marcus Dionysius Firmus, veteran from the praetorian fleet of Rannatis. Marcus Dionysius Firmus, his son, for his most devoted father.
EDCS-27601618.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Dionysius Firmus, a veteran of the praetorian fleet, erected by his son of the same name.

EDCS-27601619.text_edition: \n Marcus Eupunius Victor Aequitinae Tertiae coniugi et Lucio Mario Fortunato fratri militi legionis IIII Flaviae felicis hic siti sunt\n
EDCS-27601619.translation: Marcus Eupunius Victor of the Aequitina Tertia tribe and Lucius Marius Fortunatus, his brother, a soldier of the fourth legion Flavia Felix, are buried here.
EDCS-27601619.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial place of Marcus Eupunius Victor and his brother, a soldier in the fourth legion Flavia Felix.

EDCS-27601620.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Fideli Saturionis miles cohortis I Flaviae Brittonum stipendiorum XVIII\n
EDCS-27601620.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Fidelius, soldier of the first cohort Flavia Brittonum, with 18 years of service.
EDCS-27601620.summary: This is a tombstone for Fidelius, a soldier who served 18 years in the first cohort Flavia Brittonum.

EDCS-27601621.text_edition: \n Titus Fuficius Cai filius Pollia veteranus legionis XX Titus Fuficius Titi libertus Privatus Fuficia |(mulieris) liberta Prisca Fuficia Titi liberta Prima\n
EDCS-27601621.translation: Titus Fuficius Privatus, freedman of Titus Fuficius, a veteran of the twentieth legion, Titus Fuficius Caius Pollia, his freedwoman Prisca, and his freedwoman Prima.
EDCS-27601621.summary: This inscription lists a veteran, Titus Fuficius, and several freedpersons associated with him, likely marking a property or family plot.

EDCS-27601622.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iuliae Cre<s>centinae coniugi nnorum XXX et Iuliae Marciae filiae pientissimae nnorum VIII a Iulio Marciano milite classis praetoriae Misenatium datum ded(icatum\n
EDCS-27601622.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Julia Crescentina, wife, aged 30 years, and to Julia Marcia, her most devoted daughter, aged 8 years. Given and dedicated by Julius Marcianus, a soldier of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum.
EDCS-27601622.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a soldier to his wife and daughter, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601623.text_edition: \n Quartus Iuventius Titi filius missicius legionis XI nnorum XXXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-27601623.translation: Quartus Iuventius, son of Titus, a discharged soldier of the 11th Legion, aged 35 years, lies buried here.
EDCS-27601623.summary: This is a tombstone for a discharged soldier of the 11th Legion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601624.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Licinius miles cohortis VIII voluntariorum nepotiae Filenidi defunctae \n
EDCS-27601624.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Filenis, granddaughter of Licinius, a soldier of the 8th Cohort of Volunteers.
EDCS-27601624.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Filenis, granddaughter of a soldier in the 8th Cohort of Volunteers, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601625.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-27601625.text_edition: \n Maurentius fabricensis una cum Concordia uxore sua sarcofacum sic vivi sibi ordinaverunt\n
EDCS-27601625.translation: Maurentius, a builder, together with his wife Concordia, ordered this sarcophagus for themselves while still alive.
EDCS-27601625.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the self-funded commission of a sarcophagus by a builder and his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601626.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Sextilio Rufo veterano ex classe praetoria Misenatium et Papiriae Helpidi parentibus et Sextiliae Valeriae Sextilia Rufina viva fecit\n
EDCS-27601626.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Sextilia Rufina, while alive, made this for her parents, Lucius Sextilius Rufus, a veteran of the Praetorian fleet at Misenum, and Papiria Helpidi, and for Sextilia Valeria.
EDCS-27601626.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Sextilia Rufina for her parents, a veteran of the Praetorian fleet, and Sextilia Valeria, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601627.text_edition: \n Tarcho Tarbunnis filio militi cohortis Aquitanorum nnorum XLV stipendiorum XXV domo Camu<l=I>(oduno TARPOLIE \n
EDCS-27601627.translation: To Tarcho, son of Tarbunnis, a soldier of the Cohort of Aquitanians, aged 45 years, with 25 years of service, from Camulodunum.
EDCS-27601627.summary: This is a tombstone for Tarcho, a soldier of the Cohort of Aquitanians, who served for 25 years, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601628.text_edition: \n Bennia Callityche viva fecit sibi et Valerio Proculo veterano coniugi bene merito\n
EDCS-27601628.translation: Bennia Callityche, while alive, made this for herself and for her well-deserving husband, Valerius Proculus, a veteran.
EDCS-27601628.summary: This tomb inscription records the self-funded commission of a burial place by a woman for herself and her veteran husband, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601629.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerio Valentino veterano ex equitum singularium Simpronia sic Marcellina coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-27601629.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Valerius Valentinus, a veteran from the Praetorian Equites Singulares, and to his well-deserving wife, Simpronia Marcellina.
EDCS-27601629.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a veteran of the Praetorian Equites Singulares and his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601630.text_edition: \n Locho Augusti dispensatori De<l=I>matiae Himer Augusti dispensator Moesiae patri piissimo \n
EDCS-27601630.translation: To Locho, freedman of Augustus, an accountant, and to his most devoted father, Himero, freedman of Augustus, an accountant in Moesia.
EDCS-27601630.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Locho, a freedman and accountant, and his father Himero, also a freedman and accountant, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601632.text_edition: \n Faniae Maximae innocentissimae puellae quae vixit nnos II menses VIII Aelius Martinus beneficiarius consularis legionis I Italicae dulcissimae\n
EDCS-27601632.translation: To Fania Maxima, a most innocent girl, who lived 2 years and 8 months. Aelius Martinus, an assistant to a consular legate of the 1st Italian Legion, [dedicated this] to his sweetest [daughter].
EDCS-27601632.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Fania Maxima, a young girl, dedicated by her father, a soldier in the 1st Italian Legion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27601633.translation: Firmus, son of Julius, from Ar...
EDCS-27601633.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Firmus, son of Julius, with his place of origin partially preserved.

EDCS-27601634.text_edition: \n S<e=F>sti Nepotis vet<e=F>rani ex signifero Aurelia Calliste marito p<ii=E>ssimo sibi \n
EDCS-27601634.translation: Aurelia Calliste to her most loving husband Nepos Festus, veteran standard-bearer, for herself.
EDCS-27601634.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Aurelia Calliste for her husband Nepos Festus, a veteran standard-bearer.

EDCS-27601635.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Anthistia Anterotis Publio Domitio Potenti coniugi bene merenti posuit veterano cohortis III Alpinorum in suo\n
EDCS-27601635.translation: To the Divine Shades. Anthistia Anterotis set this up for Publius Domitius Potens, her deserving husband, a veteran of the III Alpine cohort, on his own land.
EDCS-27601635.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Anthistia Anterotis for her deserving husband, Publius Domitius Potens, a veteran of the III Alpine cohort.

EDCS-27601636.text_edition: \n Aurelius Priscus optio equitum singularium Aurelio Prisco filio infelicissimo qui vixit nnos II menses VIII\n
EDCS-27601636.translation: Aurelius Priscus, optio of the imperial horseguards, for his most unfortunate son Aurelius Priscus, who lived 2 years and 8 months.
EDCS-27601636.summary: This inscription records the death of Aurelius Priscus, son of Aurelius Priscus, an optio of the imperial horseguards, who died at a very young age.

EDCS-27601637.text_edition: \n <Co=QV>eliae Calpurniae quae vixit annos XXVI Aurelius Vitalis centurio legionis V Macedonicae maritus bene merenti posuit Coeliae Calpurniae sorori in hac etiam Aeliae Pomponiae locum concessit Iulia Vettia amica \n
EDCS-27601637.translation: For Coelia Calpurnia, who lived 26 years. Aurelius Vitalis, centurion of the V Macedonian Legion, set this up for his deserving wife. Iulia Vettia, a friend, also granted a place here for Coelia Calpurnia, her sister.
EDCS-27601637.summary: This funerary monument commemorates Coelia Calpurnia, wife of Aurelius Vitalis, a centurion, and also allocates space for her sister, Iulia Vettia.

EDCS-27601638.text_edition: \n Caius Valerius Marcellinus eques cohortis III Alpinorum turmae \n
EDCS-27601638.translation: Gaius Valerius Marcellinus, a cavalryman of the III Alpine cohort, of the troop...
EDCS-27601638.summary: This inscription identifies Gaius Valerius Marcellinus as a cavalryman in a specific troop of the III Alpine cohort.

EDCS-27601639.text_edition: \n Lucius Tettennius Titi filius S<t=I>ellatina Pansa veteranus legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis hic situs est \n
EDCS-27601639.translation: Lucius Tettennius Pansa, son of Titus, from Stellatina, a pious and faithful veteran of the XI Claudian Legion, lies here.
EDCS-27601639.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Tettennius Pansa, a veteran of the XI Claudian Legion.

EDCS-27601640.text_edition: \n Marcus Valerius -- Rufus veteranus militavit SP testamento posuit heredes faciendum curaverunt in fronte pedes X? in agro pedes \n
EDCS-27601640.translation: Marcus Valerius Rufus, veteran, served... He set this up by his will; his heirs had it made. 10 feet in front? ... acres in the field...
EDCS-27601640.summary: This funerary inscription, set up by will and completed by heirs, records the service and burial plot of the veteran Marcus Valerius Rufus.

EDCS-27700003.text_edition: \n Lucio Aemilio Martialis sic Aemilia Bauce posuit bene merenti\n
EDCS-27700003.translation: For Lucius Aemilius Martialis. Thus Aemilia Bauce set this up for her deserving [husband].
EDCS-27700003.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by Aemilia Bauce for her deserving husband, Lucius Aemilius Martialis.

EDCS-27700004.text_edition: \n Caius Aemilius Theophilus vivos sic fecit sibi et Flaviae Hecyoni concubinae defunctae nnum XX Caius Aemilius Theophilus posuit bene merenti\n
EDCS-27700004.translation: Gaius Aemilius Theophilus made this while alive for himself and for Flavia Hecyon, his deceased concubine, [who lived] 20 years. Gaius Aemilius Theophilus set this up for [her], deserving.
EDCS-27700004.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Gaius Aemilius Theophilus during his lifetime for himself and his deceased concubine, Flavia Hecyon.

EDCS-27700005.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quintus Aeronius A<d>iutor et Aeronia Delicia Quinti liberta fecerunt se vivi posterisque suis hoc monumentum heredem non <s>(equetur)\n
EDCS-27700005.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Quintus Aeronius Adiutor and Aeronia Delicia, freedwoman of Quintus, made this monument for themselves while alive and for their descendants; an heir shall not claim it.
EDCS-27700005.summary: A funerary monument set up by Quintus Aeronius Adiutor and his freedwoman Aeronia Delicia for themselves and their descendants.

EDCS-27700006.text_edition: \n L D D Marci Albini Raspaleti domum perpetuam hanc sibi et suis posteris anno XXVIII aetatis suae\n
EDCS-27700006.translation: To the Gods Below. Marcus Albinus Raspaletus [made] this eternal home for himself and his descendants in the 28th year of his age.
EDCS-27700006.summary: An inscription recording the construction of a tomb by Marcus Albinus Raspaletus for himself and his descendants at the age of 28.

EDCS-27700007.text_edition: \n Caio Albucio Trophimo Caius Albucius Vitalis collibertus bene merenti\n
EDCS-27700007.translation: To Caius Alucius Trophimus, a fellow freedman of Caius Alucius Vitalis, for a well-deserving recipient.
EDCS-27700007.summary: A dedication by Caius Alucius Vitalis to his fellow freedman Caius Alucius Trophimus.

EDCS-27700008.summary: A simple inscription, likely a name and epithet, possibly for a tomb or a statue.

EDCS-27700009.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Anchario Abellioni defuncto annorum XV Iulia Marcina mater filio infelicissimo\n
EDCS-27700009.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Titus Ancharius Abellio, deceased at 15 years of age, from his most unfortunate mother, Iulia Marcina.
EDCS-27700009.summary: A funerary inscription by Iulia Marcina for her son Titus Ancharius Abellio, who died at age 15.

EDCS-27700010.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Anniae defunctae annorum XXV dominus bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-27700010.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Annia, deceased at 25 years of age, placed by her master for a well-deserving [person].
EDCS-27700010.summary: A funerary inscription by a master for his deceased slave Annia, who died at age 25.

EDCS-27700011.text_edition: \n Publius Anteius Secundinus et Titia Artemisia eius vivi sibi posuerunt\n
EDCS-27700011.translation: Publius Anteius Secundinus and his wife Titia Artemisia set this up for themselves while alive.
EDCS-27700011.summary: A funerary monument set up by Publius Anteius Secundinus and Titia Artemisia for themselves.

EDCS-27700013.text_edition: \n Antonius Anicetus et Verginia Marcellina parentes infelicissimi Antoniae \n
EDCS-27700013.translation: Antonius Anicetus and Verginia Marcellina, most unfortunate parents, [for] Antonia [Primitiva?].
EDCS-27700013.summary: A funerary inscription by parents, Antonius Anicetus and Verginia Marcellina, for their child Antonia.

EDCS-27700014.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Antonia Primitiva et Antonia Eutychia et Publio Antonio Restituto bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-27700014.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Antonia Primitiva, Antonia Eutychia, and Publius Antonius Restitutus set this up for a well-deserving [person].
EDCS-27700014.summary: A funerary dedication by Antonia Primitiva, Antonia Eutychia, and Publius Antonius Restitutus.

EDCS-27700015.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Antoniae De filiae Severae\n
EDCS-27700015.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Antonia De, daughter of Severa.
EDCS-27700015.summary: A funerary inscription for Antonia De, daughter of Severa.

EDCS-27700016.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aprofennio Magno defuncto nnorum XXIII Aprofennia Flora liberta bene merenti\n
EDCS-27700016.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aprofennius Magnus, who died at the age of 23 years. Aprofennia Flora, his freedwoman, placed this for him, who deserved well.
EDCS-27700016.summary: A tombstone set up by a freedwoman for her deceased patron, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700017.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aquesillae annorum III Melissa mater posuit\n
EDCS-27700017.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aquesilla, who died at the age of 3 years. Her mother Melissa placed this.
EDCS-27700017.summary: A tombstone set up by a mother for her young daughter, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700018.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Arethusae nnorum II mensium VIII dierum IX\n
EDCS-27700018.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Arethusa, aged 2 years, 8 months, and 9 days.
EDCS-27700018.summary: A tombstone recording the early death of a child named Arethusa, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700019.text_edition: \n Lucius Arrius Saecular<i=E>s Protiani fratri pientissimo bene merenti posuit \n
EDCS-27700019.translation: Lucius Arrius Saecularis placed this for his most dutiful brother, Protianus, who deserved well.
EDCS-27700019.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a brother to his deceased sibling, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700020.text_edition: \n Atilius Marcellus Atilio Luciano filio infelicissimo qui vixit nnos II menses II posuit\n
EDCS-27700020.translation: Atilius Marcellus placed this for his most unfortunate son, Atilius Lucianus, who lived for 2 years and 2 months.
EDCS-27700020.summary: A tombstone erected by a father for his young son, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700021.text_edition: \n Atiliae Quintinae coniugi pientissimae quae vixit nnos XXVIII menses X dies X Clodius Salutaris maritus bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-27700021.translation: For his most dutiful wife, Atilia Quintina, who lived for 28 years, 10 months, and 10 days. Clodius Salutaris, her husband, placed this for her, who deserved well.
EDCS-27700021.summary: A tombstone set up by a husband for his deceased wife, including details of her age at death, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700023.text_edition: \n Marcus Attius Marci libertus Faustus vivus fecit sibi et Attiae Marci libertae Secundae coniugi sanctissimae et Marco Attio Marci filio Crispo et Marco Attio Marci filio Secundo coniugis amissae lacrumas satis esse putavi me fudisse meae vimque tulisse deum et tuleram graviter sed nunc et solus et orbus amissis natis maxima damna fleo et testor superos patri quae ponere nati debuerant natis ponere dona patrem Attia Marci liberta Ampliata nnum XXIII hic sita est florente aetate depressere veneficiae mensesque quinque et annum cum aegrotaverit abreptam aetate in inferi Ditis specus hunc titulum posuit Faustus libertae suae\n
EDCS-27700023.translation: Marcus Attius Faustus, freedman of Marcus, made this while alive for himself and for Attia Secunda, freedwoman of Marcus, his most chaste wife, and for Marcus Attius Crispus, son of Marcus, and for Marcus Attius Secundus, son of Marcus. I thought that the tears of a lost wife were enough, that I had shed the strength of my grief and borne it heavily. But now, alone and bereft, with my children lost, I weep for the greatest losses and call the gods above to witness that what sons ought to have placed for their father, a father placed for his children. Attia Ampliata, freedwoman of Marcus, aged 23 years, lies here. In the flower of her age, witchcraft depressed her. And five months and a year she had been ill, when she was snatched away in age into the caves of the underworld. Faustus placed this inscription for his freedwoman.
EDCS-27700023.summary: A complex tombstone set up by Faustus for himself, his wife, and two sons, lamenting the death of his wife Ampliata at a young age due to witchcraft, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700024.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Attiae Gratillae coniugi incomparabili quondam nnorum plus minus XL Iulius Timotheus maritus cum quo nnos XVIIII bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-27700024.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Attia Gratilla, his incomparable wife, who lived somewhat more than 40 years. Iulius Timotheus, her husband, placed this for her, with whom she lived for 19 years, and who deserved well.
EDCS-27700024.summary: A tombstone set up by a husband for his wife, indicating their years of marriage and her approximate age at death, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700025.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aufidiae Quartae Caesienus Achilleus coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-27700025.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aufidia Quarta. Caesienus Achilleus placed this for his wife, who deserved well.
EDCS-27700025.summary: A tombstone erected by a husband for his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700026.text_edition: \n Avidiae Titi libertae Ingenuae matri et Caeciliae Cai filiae Tertullae sorori Avidia Titi filia Luculla posuit\n
EDCS-27700026.translation: Avidia Luculla, daughter of Titus, placed this for her mother, Avidia Ingenua, freedwoman of Titus, and for her sister, Caecilia Tertulla, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-27700026.summary: A tombstone set up by a daughter for her mother and sister, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700027.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Anniano iuveni qui vixit annos XXVI\n
EDCS-27700027.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Aurelius Annianus the young man, who lived 26 years.
EDCS-27700027.summary: This is a tombstone for Aurelius Annianus, a young man who died at the age of 26, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700028.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-27700028.text_edition: \n Aurelius Aprilianus se vivo donavet sic arcam Aurelio Amuro et Aureliae Quintae quae est in horto Metrodori vivi sibi posuerunt IIarnum quod si quis post mortem eorum super eos alia corpora velet sic inponere inferet! solidos\n
EDCS-27700028.translation: Aurelius Aprilianus dedicated this while alive for himself, and placed this tomb for Aurelius Amurus and Aurelia Quinti, who are in the garden of Metrodorus. Whoever, after their death, wishes to place other bodies upon them, shall pay [a fine of] X solidi.
EDCS-27700028.summary: This funerary monument, dedicated by Aurelius Aprilianus for himself and others, specifies a penalty for unauthorized burials, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700029.text_edition: \n Aurelius Burius pos<v=I><i=V>t sibi et matri suae piae Horaniae sic qui sibi vivi <p=R>osuerunt quot sic si quis voluerit sene sic iussione nostra aperire inferet \n
EDCS-27700029.translation: Aurelius Burius placed this for himself and for his pious mother, Horania. He placed this for himself while alive. Whoever wishes to open it after our instruction shall pay [a fine].
EDCS-27700029.summary: This monument, dedicated by Aurelius Burius for himself and his mother Horania, includes a penalty for unauthorized opening, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700030.text_edition: \n VIV nno--- L infelicissimo Aurelius Cassius patronus \n
EDCS-27700030.translation: To the Divine Shades. [For] L[ucius?], most unhappy in life. Aurelius Cassius, patron.
EDCS-27700030.summary: This fragmentary inscription honors a deceased individual, likely Aurelius Cassius, with the epithet 'most unhappy in life', from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700031.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Deucalioni Au<r=B>elia Ursina patri \n
EDCS-27700031.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aurelia Ursina to her father, Aurelius Deucalion.
EDCS-27700031.summary: This is a dedication from Aurelia Ursina to her deceased father, Aurelius Deucalion, a tombstone from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700033.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Emeritianus vixit annis XXIIII Flavia Valentina mater filio carissimo posuit locus emptus cum vaso a Caesonio Primo\n
EDCS-27700033.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aurelius Emeritianus, who lived 24 years. Flavia Valentina, mother, placed this for her dearest son. The place was purchased with the vase from Caesonius Primus.
EDCS-27700033.summary: This tombstone records the death of Aurelius Emeritianus at 24 years old and was set up by his mother Flavia Valentina, noting the purchase of the burial plot, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700034.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Aurelio Fortunio defuncto annorum XXX Aurelia Panthera mater et patrona filio infelicissimo posuit\n
EDCS-27700034.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Titus Aurelius Fortunius, deceased, 30 years old. Aurelia Panthera, mother and patroness, placed this for her most unhappy son.
EDCS-27700034.summary: This tombstone commemorates Titus Aurelius Fortunius, who died at 30, and was erected by his mother and patroness, Aurelia Panthera, during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700035.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Hermeti Filia marito bene merenti\n
EDCS-27700035.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Aurelius Hermes. From his daughter, to her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-27700035.summary: This inscription is a dedication by a daughter to her deceased husband, Aurelius Hermes, honoring his merit, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700036.text_edition: \n Aurelio Iulio Titi Aureli Nepos sic Nepos posuit\n
EDCS-27700036.translation: To Aurelius Julius. His nephew, Titus Aurelius Nepos, placed this for his nephew.
EDCS-27700036.summary: This monument was erected by Titus Aurelius Nepos for Aurelius Julius, likely a relative, during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700037.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Maximino defuncto annorum III dierum VIII parentes posuerunt filio infelicissimo\n
EDCS-27700037.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Aurelius Maximus, deceased, 3 years and 8 days old. His parents placed this for their most unhappy son.
EDCS-27700037.summary: This tombstone records the death of Aurelius Maximus at the age of three years and eight days, erected by his parents, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27700038.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Veneriano qui vixit annos CVIII Aurelius Polensis nepos\n
EDCS-27700038.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Aurelius Venerianus, who lived 108 years. Aurelius Polensis, his grandson.
EDCS-27700038.summary: A tombstone erected by a grandson for his grandfather Aurelius Venerianus, who lived to an exceptional age.

EDCS-27700039.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Victorinus Aeliae Maximillae carae coniugi posuit titulum memoriens qui sic convixxit sic mecum annus sic quinque et dies viginti quinque et moritur plus minus anno<r=p>om sic numero XXXV \n
EDCS-27700039.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aurelius Victorinus erected this monument to his dear wife Aelia Maximilla, who lived with him for so many years, five and twenty days, and died in her thirty-fifth year, more or less.
EDCS-27700039.summary: A funerary monument set up by Aurelius Victorinus for his wife Aelia Maximilla, recording their shared life and her age at death.

EDCS-27700040.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Crescentillae coniugi quae vixit nnos XXV Iulius Fortunius maritus et sibi\n
EDCS-27700040.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Aurelia Crescentilla, wife, who lived 25 years. Iulius Fortunius, her husband, and for himself.
EDCS-27700040.summary: A tombstone set up by Iulius Fortunius for his wife Aurelia Crescentilla and also for himself, noting her age at death.

EDCS-27700041.text_edition: \n M Aurelius H et Aurelia Eulogia filiis infelicissimis\n
EDCS-27700041.translation: M. Aurelius H. and Aurelia Eulogia to their most unfortunate sons.
EDCS-27700041.summary: A dedication by M. Aurelius H. and Aurelia Eulogia to their deceased sons.

EDCS-27700042.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
