EDCS-28300055.translation: Sacred to Liber Augustus. Lucius Aebutius, son of Lucius, Sergia Celer, aedile and duumvir for dispensing justice, from public funds.
EDCS-28300055.summary: This is a dedication of a sacred building or object to Liber Augustus by Lucius Aebutius Celer, an official who funded the work, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300057.text_edition: \n Cnaeo Iulio Cnaei filio Vero consuli designato II auguri legato Augusti pro praetore provinciae Syriae legato Augusti pro praetore provinciae Brittanniae legato Augusti pro praetore provinciae Germaniae inferioris praefecto aerarii Saturni legato legionis XXX Ulpiae praetori tribuno plebis quaestori Augusti tribuno laticlavio legionis X fretensis triumviro aere argento auro flando feriundo Aequenses municipes\n
EDCS-28300057.translation: To Gnaeus Julius Verus, son of Gnaeus, consul-designate, augur, legate of the Emperor with proconsular power of Syria, legate of the Emperor with proconsular power of Britannia, legate of the Emperor with proconsular power of Lower Germania, prefect of the Saturnian treasury, legionary legate of the XXX Ulpian legion, praetor, tribune of the plebs, quaestor of the Emperor, wide-striped tribune of the Tenth Fretensis legion, commissioner for minting bronze, silver, and gold; the people of Aequum and the municipes.
EDCS-28300057.summary: This is a dedication by the people of Aequum and its municipes to Gnaeus Julius Verus, listing his extensive career and offices, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28300059.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Cai libertus Crescens IIIIIIvir vivus fecit sibi et Iuliae Anthusae coniugi Caio Iulio Maximo filio\n
EDCS-28300059.translation: Gaius Julius Crescens, freedman of Gaius, a municipal official (IIIIvir), made this while alive for himself and for his wife Julia Anthusa and his son Gaius Julius Maximus.
EDCS-28300059.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Gaius Julius Crescens for himself and his family, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28300060.text_edition: \n Publius Aelius Verus Aeliae Trioni matri pientissimae defunctae annorum LXX et sibi posuit\n
EDCS-28300060.translation: Publius Aelius Verus placed this for Aelia Trion, his most devoted mother, who died at the age of 70, and for himself.
EDCS-28300060.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Publius Aelius Verus for his mother and himself, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28300061.text_edition: \n omnibus Publii nnii Nepos et Surus \n
EDCS-28300061.translation: To all the gods. Nepos and Surus, sons of Publius.
EDCS-28300061.summary: This is a dedication to all the gods by Nepos and Surus, sons of Publius, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28300062.text_edition: \n Cornelia Ferocilla Corneliae Ferocillae obsequentissimae filiae mater et matertera deposuerunt\n
EDCS-28300062.translation: Cornelia Ferocilla's mother and aunt placed [this] for Cornelia Ferocilla, their most obedient daughter.
EDCS-28300062.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a mother and aunt for their deceased daughter and niece, Cornelia Ferocilla, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28300063.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cornelia Ferocilla et Coelia Oclatia Coeliae Marcellae defunctae nnorum XXXV mater et matertera filiae infelicissimae et obsequentissimae posuerunt\n
EDCS-28300063.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Cornelia Ferocilla and Coelia Oclatia placed [this] for Coelia Marcella, deceased at 35 years of age, their most unhappy and obedient daughter.
EDCS-28300063.summary: This funerary monument was erected by the mother and aunt for their deceased daughter and niece, Coelia Marcella, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28300064.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus virginius Secundinus defunctus annorum XL St<e>rcorina annorum II Tertulla incomparabilibus RAVENVR\n
EDCS-28300064.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Virginius Secundinus, deceased at 40 years of age, [and] Stercorina, aged 2, [and] Tertulla, incomparable; RAVENVR.
EDCS-28300064.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Virginius Secundinus, Stercorina, and Tertulla, with the word 'RAVENVR' possibly indicating a place or a posthumous designation, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28300065.text_edition: \n Vettia Cai filia Tertullina viva fecit sibi et Sexto Iulio Nostro marito optimo hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-28300065.translation: Vettia Tertullina, daughter of Caius, made this monument while alive for herself and for her most excellent husband, Sextus Julius Noster; she will not be followed by an heir.
EDCS-28300065.summary: This is a funerary monument created by Vettia Tertullina for herself and her husband, specifying that no heir will inherit the plot, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28300066.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vipsanius Terentius Aureliae Victorinae coniugi carissimae virginiae posuit et sibi quae vixit mecum annos VIIII menses V defunctae annorum XXIII\n
EDCS-28300066.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Vipsanius Terentius placed [this] for Aurelia Victorina, his dearest wife, who lived with him for 9 years and 5 months, deceased at 23 years of age, and for himself.
EDCS-28300066.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Vipsanius Terentius for his late wife Aurelia Victorina and for himself, providing details of her age and length of marriage, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28300068.text_edition: \n Castori et Polluci sacerum Fabericia Pieris votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28300068.translation: To Castor and Pollux. Fabricia Pieris fulfilled her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-28300068.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Dioscuri (Castor and Pollux) by Fabricia Pieris, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28300069.text_edition: \n Turranius Firmus vexillarius cohortis I Belgarum Mercurio votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28300069.translation: Turranius Firmus, standard-bearer of the I Belgarum cohort, willingly and deservedly paid his vow to Mercury.
EDCS-28300069.summary: This is a votive inscription by a military standard-bearer to the god Mercury, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28300070.text_edition: \n Lucio Terentio Luci filio Claudia Severo Celeia militi cohortis VIII vexillario nnorum XXX stipendiorum XIV Lucius Aebutius Felix posuit\n
EDCS-28300070.translation: Lucius Aebutius Felix erected this for Lucius Terentius Severus, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, a soldier and standard-bearer of the VIII cohort, aged 30, with 14 years of service.
EDCS-28300070.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Lucius Aebutius Felix for Lucius Terentius Severus, a soldier of the VIII cohort, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28300071.text_edition: \n Marco Valerio Donico natus domo Celeiae |(centurioni) cohortis III Alpinorum fecit Herennia Pudentilla coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-28300071.translation: Herennia Pudentilla made this for her well-deserving husband, Marcus Valerius Donicus, born in Celeia, a centurion of the III Alpinorum cohort.
EDCS-28300071.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a wife to her husband, a centurion, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28300072.text_edition: \n Iulia Aelia rus vetustate conlapsam sic suo aere restituit\n
EDCS-28300072.translation: Iulia Aelia restored this house, collapsed from age, at her own expense.
EDCS-28300072.summary: This is an inscription recording the restoration of a house by Iulia Aelia, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28300073.text_edition: \n Aurelio Maximo militi cohortis III A<l=T>pinorum defuncto nnorum XXVII Aurelia Valentina uxor posuit \n
EDCS-28300073.translation: Aurelia Valentina, wife, placed this for her well-deserving husband Aurelius Maximus, a soldier of the III Alpinorum cohort, who died at the age of 27.
EDCS-28300073.summary: This is a funerary inscription placed by a wife for her deceased husband, a soldier, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28300074.text_edition: \n Batonis sic Baracionis Tato filius bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-28300074.translation: Tato, son of Batonis (called Baracionis), erected this for him, well-deserving.
EDCS-28300074.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Tato for his father, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28300075.text_edition: \n Panes fecit sibi et suis se vivo fecit\n
EDCS-28300075.translation: Panes made this for himself and his family while he was alive.
EDCS-28300075.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Panes for himself and his family during his lifetime, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28300076.text_edition: \n Platori Varronis Panes Titi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-28300076.translation: Panes, son of Titius Varro, erected this for his well-deserving son.
EDCS-28300076.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by Panes for his well-deserving son, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28300077.text_edition: \n fili-- et Varroni Pantoni marito suo posuit viva \n
EDCS-28300077.translation: While alive, she erected this for her son(s) and for Varro Pantonus, her husband.
EDCS-28300077.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by a wife for her husband and son(s) during her lifetime, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28300078.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Surus Panenti Muteli fecit sibi et Buomi coniugi suae posuit\n
EDCS-28300078.translation: To the Divine Manes. Surus Panentis, son of Mutelus, made this for himself and for his wife Buomia, while alive.
EDCS-28300078.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the Divine Manes by Surus Panentis for himself and his wife, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28300079.text_edition: \n Turo et Sextoni parentibus bene merentibus\n
EDCS-28300079.translation: To Turo and Sexto, parents who deserved well.
EDCS-28300079.summary: This dedication is made to parents Turo and Sexto, likely from their child.

EDCS-28300080.text_edition: \n MAT D D Vitali et Maximus frater\n
EDCS-28300080.translation: Vitali and Maximus, brothers, as a parental dedication.
EDCS-28300080.summary: This inscription records a parental dedication made by the brothers Vitali and Maximus.

EDCS-28300081.text_edition: \n Zanosia Lisenonis nepotie suae annorum VIIII et sibi inreparabile possuit\n
EDCS-28300081.translation: Zanosia acquired this for Lisenonis, her granddaughter aged nine, and for herself.
EDCS-28300081.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the acquisition of burial space by Zanosia for her granddaughter and herself.

EDCS-28300082.text_edition: \n Titus Aurelius Panes Ciasiicianus Titoni et Sextoni sororibus bene merentibus posuit\n
EDCS-28300082.translation: Titus Aurelius Panes Ciasiicianus set this up for his sisters Titoni and Sextoni, who deserved well.
EDCS-28300082.summary: This funerary monument was erected by Titus Aurelius Panes Ciasiicianus for his sisters Titoni and Sextoni.

EDCS-28300083.summary: This fragmentary inscription is too incomplete to determine its purpose or subjects.

EDCS-28300084.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Iunius Fadenus decurio equitum cohortis III Alpinorum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28300084.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Iunius Fadenus, decurion of cavalry of the 3rd Alpine Cohort, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-28300084.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Iunius Fadenus, a decurion in the military, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-28300085.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus VVO Iucundo mer<e=I>n<t>i LPSVVLI Valentinus socro suo \n
EDCS-28300085.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Valentinus set this up for his well-deserving father-in-law, Iucundus.
EDCS-28300085.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Valentinus to his father-in-law Iucundus.

EDCS-28300086.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aelius Titus ex protectore votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-28300086.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Aelius Titus, formerly a guard, willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-28300086.summary: This votive inscription is from Aelius Titus, a former member of the guard, to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-28300087.text_edition: \n Ilarus Gra--- f(ilius) posuit sepulcrum Lavoni filiae defunctae annorum septem sibi et suis\n
EDCS-28300087.translation: Ilarus son of Gra[---] set up this tomb for Lavonia, his deceased daughter aged seven, and for himself and his family.
EDCS-28300087.summary: This funerary inscription records the erection of a tomb by Ilarus for his young daughter Lavonia and for his family.

EDCS-28300088.text_edition: \n S Batun sic mihi et meis\n
EDCS-28300088.translation: S. Batun. May it be thus for me and mine.
EDCS-28300088.summary: This short, possibly funerary or protective, inscription expresses a wish for the well-being of the author and their family.

EDCS-28300089.text_edition: \n Marcus Antonius Dis Manibus Aurelius Licinianus vivus sibi et memoriam fe[cit ------\n
EDCS-28300089.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Aurelius Licinianus, while alive, made this memorial for himself and [---].
EDCS-28300089.summary: A funerary monument set up by Marcus Aurelius Licinianus for himself and others.

EDCS-28300090.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Firmus Surionis vixit annos XXX posuit frater et posteris \n
EDCS-28300090.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Firmus Surionis lived 30 years; his brother placed this for him and his descendants.
EDCS-28300090.summary: A funerary inscription for Firmus Surionis, set up by his brother.

EDCS-28300091.text_edition: \n Quartiniano caro coniugi Elpis titulum posuit qui Salona in fata ruit\n
EDCS-28300091.translation: Elpis placed this monument for her dear husband Quartinianus, who died in Salona.
EDCS-28300091.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Elpis for her husband Quartinianus.

EDCS-28300092.text_edition: \n In locis peregrinis monumentum fecerunt infelices parentes vivi Valentio iini\n
EDCS-28300092.translation: In foreign lands, his unhappy parents, while alive, made this monument for Valentinus.
EDCS-28300092.summary: A funerary monument set up by the parents for their son Valentinus.

EDCS-28300093.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Tonitratori Titus Aurelius Maximus VI Idus Auggustas\n
EDCS-28300093.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Thunderer. Titus Aurelius Maximus on the 6th day before the Ides of August.
EDCS-28300093.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by Titus Aurelius Maximus on August 8th.

EDCS-28300094.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Claudio Maximo decurioni coloniae Ris defuncto an(norum) LV T \n
EDCS-28300094.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Titus Claudius Maximus, decurion of the colony Ris, died aged 55 years.
EDCS-28300094.summary: A funerary inscription for Titus Claudius Maximus, a decurion, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-28300095.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aelius Maximus et Aelia Tatta parentes Aeliae Marcellae filiae defunctae annorum XX vivi sepulcrum fecerunt et sibi et suis\n
EDCS-28300095.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aelius Maximus and Aelia Tatta, parents, made this tomb for their deceased daughter Aelia Marcella, aged 20, while alive, and for themselves and their kin.
EDCS-28300095.summary: A funerary inscription for Aelia Marcella, set up by her parents, who also reserved space for themselves and their family.

EDCS-28300096.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Arrius Agaevivus fecit\n
EDCS-28300096.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Gaius Arrius Agaevivus made this.
EDCS-28300096.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by Gaius Arrius Agaevivus.

EDCS-28300097.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Iettus Dasantis\n
EDCS-28300097.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Iettus Dasantis made this.
EDCS-28300097.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus made by Iettus Dasantis.

EDCS-28300098.summary: A dedication to the Nymphs made by Maxima Turi.

EDCS-28300099.text_edition: \n Veneri Victrici Parthicae Augustae sacrum in memoriam Antoniae Decimi filiae Paulae Decimus Antonius Proculus decurio Salonae et Coponia parentes locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28300099.translation: Sacred to Victory-bringing Parthian Augusta Venus, in memory of Antonia Paula, daughter of Decimus. Decimus Antonius Proculus, decurion of Salona, and her parents. The site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28300099.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by parents and a decurion to Antonia Paula, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE based on the cult of 'Veneri Victrici Parthicae Augustae'.

EDCS-28300100.text_edition: \n Virtute et felicitate omnes retro principes supergresso domino nostro Flavio Iulio Constanti victori ac triumfatori semper Augusto Flavius Iulius Rufinus Sarmentius vir clarissimus praesis sic provinciae Dalmatiae devotus numini maiestatique <e=I>ius \n
EDCS-28300100.translation: To our lord Flavius Julius Constantius, victor and triumphant ever-Augustus, surpassing all previous rulers in valor and fortune. Flavius Julius Rufinus Sarmentius, vir clarissimus, praeses of the province of Dalmatia, devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-28300100.summary: An honorific inscription praising Emperor Constantius, erected by the provincial governor Flavius Julius Rufinus Sarmentius, dating to his reign (337-361 CE).

EDCS-28300101.text_edition: \n Quinto Petronio Valenti militiavit legione VII <C=G>(laudia) pia fideli hic situs est \n
EDCS-28300101.translation: Quinto Petronio Valenti served in the VII Claudia Pia Fidelis legion; he lies buried here.
EDCS-28300101.summary: A tombstone recording the service of Quinto Petronio Valenti in a Roman legion, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28300102.text_edition: \n Pantoni Lavinciae Platoris filiae Apludi Statici decurionis uxori hic est sita annorum XLV\n
EDCS-28300102.translation: Here lies Pantoni Lavincia, wife of Apludus Staticus, decurion, daughter of Plator, aged 45.
EDCS-28300102.summary: A tombstone for Pantoni Lavincia, wife of a decurion, with her age specified, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28300104.text_edition: \n NOI Scenobaro Tizio decurioni patri suo fecit bene merito\n
EDCS-28300104.translation: Tizio Scenobar, decurion, erected this for his father, well-deserving.
EDCS-28300104.summary: A funerary dedication by a son, Tizio Scenobar, to his deserving father, also a decurion, likely from the 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28300105.text_edition: \n principi Delmatarum nnorum XXII Claudia Tiberi filia \n
EDCS-28300105.translation: Claudia, daughter of Tiberius, leader of the Delmatae, aged 22.
EDCS-28300105.summary: An epitaph for Claudia, daughter of Tiberius, identified as a 'principi Delmatarum' (leader of the Delmatae), aged 22, likely from the Roman period of Dalmatia.

EDCS-28300106.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aeliis Feliculae et Malcioni filiis Iulius Cosmus bene merenti\n
EDCS-28300106.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Aelia Felicula and Malcion, sons. Julius Cosmus, for them well-deserving.
EDCS-28300106.summary: A funerary dedication to two individuals, Aelia Felicula and Malcion, by Julius Cosmus, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28300107.text_edition: \n Aplis Ledietis Sini filius annorum VIII\n
EDCS-28300107.translation: Aplis, son of Sinius Ledietis, aged 8.
EDCS-28300107.summary: A tombstone for a child named Aplis, son of Sinius Ledietis, aged 8, likely from the Roman period in Dalmatia.

EDCS-28300108.text_edition: \n Aplo Darmoca Turi filia nnorum XXV hic sita est\n
EDCS-28300108.translation: Aplo Darmoca, daughter of Turius, aged 25, lies buried here.
EDCS-28300108.summary: A tombstone for Aplo Darmoca, daughter of Turius, aged 25, likely from the Roman period in Dalmatia.

EDCS-28300109.text_edition: \n Aurelia Trita N CVNTINI MIL ENO ARP PA\n
EDCS-28300109.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription for Aurelia Trita, the rest of which is illegible, likely from the Roman period in Dalmatia.

EDCS-28300110.text_edition: \n Baezo Cursulavia Lavi filia annorum LXV \n
EDCS-28300110.translation: Baezo, daughter of Cursulavia Lavi, aged 65.
EDCS-28300110.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name, parentage, and age of the deceased Baezo.

EDCS-28300111.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary epitaph, mentioning the name Bizo and recording that they lived.

EDCS-28300113.text_edition: \n Neriae Helpidi Eutychus coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-28300113.translation: Eutychus to his well-deserving wife Neria Helpidi.
EDCS-28300113.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Eutychus to his deserving wife Neria Helpidi.

EDCS-28300114.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Nomedito Benni posuit viva hic - def(uncto MA dat\n
EDCS-28300114.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Nomedito Benni, while alive, erected this for [her deceased husband] MA, [who is] dead.
EDCS-28300114.summary: This funerary epitaph, set up by Nomedito Benni for her deceased husband, is dedicated to the underworld deities.

EDCS-28300115.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary epitaph, mentioning the name Panto and his parentage.

EDCS-28300116.text_edition: \n Pladomenus Sera Turi filius annorum LXXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-28300116.translation: Pladomenus, son of Sera Turi, aged 75, is buried here.
EDCS-28300116.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the burial of Pladomenus, son of Sera Turi, and his age.

EDCS-28300117.text_edition: \n Platino Platoris Tizi filia viva fecit sibi\n
EDCS-28300117.translation: Platino, daughter of Plator Tizi, made this for herself while alive.
EDCS-28300117.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where Platino, daughter of Plator Tizi, recorded that she dedicated the monument for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-28300118.text_edition: \n cis viva et Quartae et Quintae filiabus defunctis\n
EDCS-28300118.translation: [she lived] alive with Quartae and Quintae, her daughters, [now] deceased.
EDCS-28300118.summary: This is a funerary inscription fragment that mentions a woman living with her deceased daughters, Quartae and Quintae.

EDCS-28300119.text_edition: \n Sextus Statinius Aplinis filius nnrum LXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-28300119.translation: Sextus Statinius Aplinis, aged 70, is buried here.
EDCS-28300119.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Sextus Statinius Aplinis and his age.

EDCS-28300120.text_edition: \n VGAEG et lea filiae riae infelicissimae posuerunt anorum plus minus II\n
EDCS-28300120.translation: VGAEG and lea, her most unhappy daughters, placed [this] for [their mother] aged more or less 2.
EDCS-28300120.summary: This funerary epitaph, set up by VGAEG and lea for their mother, laments her passing and possibly records her age at death.

EDCS-28300121.text_edition: \n Tritanoni Lavi filiae annorum LX et Aploni Tritanonis annorum XII Tritano Acali matri et filiae fecit\n
EDCS-28300121.translation: To Tritano, son of Lavius, aged 60, and to Aplonius, aged 12, Tritano Acalis made this for his mother and daughter.
EDCS-28300121.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by Tritano Acalis to his mother and daughter, Tritano and Aplonius, and likely his father Lavius, with approximate ages provided.

EDCS-28300123.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely a funerary monument, mentioning the name Turo, with the rest of the text illegible.

EDCS-28300124.text_edition: \n Turo Statica Aplinis filia annorum XXI <h=II>(ic) sita est \n
EDCS-28300124.translation: Turo Statica, daughter of Aplinis, aged 21, lies here.
EDCS-28300124.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Turo Statica, daughter of Aplinis, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-28300125.text_edition: \n Vendo Tritaneria Pinsi filia nnorum XXX hic sita est\n
EDCS-28300125.translation: Vendo Tritaneria, daughter of Pinsi, aged 30, lies here.
EDCS-28300125.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vendo Tritaneria, daughter of Pinsi, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-28300126.text_edition: \n Vendo Tudania Pladomeni filia annorum XXI hic sita est\n
EDCS-28300126.translation: Vendo Tudania, daughter of Pladomenus, aged 21, lies here.
EDCS-28300126.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Vendo Tudania, daughter of Pladomenus, who died at the age of 21.

EDCS-28300127.text_edition: \n CICDETDMRIIARO R sibi et compari et heredibus suis vivis vivus sibi vivus posuit et Tertullinae nepotiae annorum XII mensium II\n
EDCS-28300127.translation: For himself and his partner and his heirs, while living, he set this up while alive for himself, and for Tertullina, niece, aged 12 years and 2 months.
EDCS-28300127.summary: This is a self-dedicated monument, set up by a living person for himself, his partner, heirs, and a niece named Tertullina, also noting her age.

EDCS-28300128.text_edition: \n Infelicissimo infanti Cornelio Urso qui vixit annos II menses II dies XX Birrius Fortunius et Iulia Firmina filio incomparabili\n
EDCS-28300128.translation: To the most unhappy infant Cornelius Urso, who lived 2 years, 2 months, 20 days. Birrius Fortunius and Iulia Firmina to their incomparable son.
EDCS-28300128.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young child, Cornelius Urso, detailing his lifespan and dedicated by his parents, Birrius Fortunius and Iulia Firmina.

EDCS-28300129.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Surae defunctae plus minus nnorum LX Verna filius pientissimus bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-28300129.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Sura, deceased, about 60 years of age. Verna, her most devoted son, set this up for her well-deserving.
EDCS-28300129.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Verna to his mother Sura, who died around the age of 60.

EDCS-28300130.text_edition: \n Caius I R Dianae sacrum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28300130.translation: To Diana, Gaius I[---] R[---] freely and deservedly paid a sacred vow.
EDCS-28300130.summary: This is a votive inscription recording that Gaius I[---] R[---] fulfilled a vow to the goddess Diana.

EDCS-28300131.text_edition: \n Genio municipii Flavi Scardonae Caius Petronius Firmus ob honorem auguratus locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28300131.translation: To the Genius of the municipality of Flavia Scardona. Gaius Petronius Firmus, by decree of the decurions, was granted this place for the honor of his augurship.
EDCS-28300131.summary: This inscription records the granting of a public space to Gaius Petronius Firmus by the town council for his distinguished honor as an augur in Flavia Scardona.

EDCS-28300133.text_edition: \n Iovi Taranuco Arria Successa votum solvit\n
EDCS-28300133.translation: Arria Successa willingly fulfilled her vow to Jupiter Taranucus.
EDCS-28300133.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Arria Successa to the god Jupiter Taranucus.

EDCS-28300134.text_edition: \n Veneri Victrici sacrum solvit laet- libens T\n
EDCS-28300134.translation: Sacred to Venus Victrix; the donor fulfilled [her/his] vow gladly and willingly.
EDCS-28300134.summary: This inscription records a sacred offering to Venus Victrix, with the donor willingly fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-28300135.text_edition: \n Mutilia Publi filia Nertilla votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28300135.translation: Nertilla, daughter of Publius, willingly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-28300135.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by Nertilla, daughter of Publius.

EDCS-28300136.text_edition: \n Marcus Aurelius Claudi filius Postumus Iupiter ex imperio libens merito\n
EDCS-28300136.translation: Marcus Aurelius Postumus, son of Claudius, willingly and deservedly [erected this] to Jupiter by order.
EDCS-28300136.summary: This inscription records an offering made by Marcus Aurelius Postumus to Jupiter, apparently by divine command.

EDCS-28300137.text_edition: \n Neroni Caesari Germanici filio Tiberi Augusti nepoti divi Augusti pronepoti flamini Augustali civitates Liburniae\n
EDCS-28300137.translation: The communities of Liburnia [dedicated this] to Nero Caesar, son of Germanicus, grandson of Tiberius Augustus, great-grandson of the deified Augustus, as Augustalis priest.
EDCS-28300137.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the communities of Liburnia to Emperor Nero, highlighting his lineage and priestly role.

EDCS-28300139.text_edition: \n Tito Turranio Titi filio Sergia Sedato decurioni IIviro Scardonis sacerdoti ad aram Augusti Liburnorum huic ordo Scardonitanorum statuam decrevit Iulia Sexti filia Maxima inpensa remissa de sua pecunia fecit loco dato decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28300139.translation: The Senate of Scardona decreed a statue to Titus Turranius Sedatus, son of Titus, of the Sergia tribe, decurion and IIvir of Scardona, priest at the altar of Augustus of the Liburnians; Julia Maxima, daughter of Sextus, made it with her own funds, with the cost remitted, on a site granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28300139.summary: This inscription records the decree of a statue by the Scardona town council to Titus Turranius Sedatus, funded by Julia Maxima, for his service as a local official and priest.

EDCS-28300140.text_edition: \n Titus Septumius sic Titi Luci libertus Charito qulinam sic de sua pecunia fecit\n
EDCS-28300140.translation: Titus Septumius Charito, freedman of Titus and Lucius, made this cookshop at his own expense.
EDCS-28300140.summary: This inscription records the construction of a cookshop by Titus Septumius Charito, a freedman.

EDCS-28300141.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Dracontius compari Aureliae Sabinillae coniugi dulcissimae et obsequentissimae quae vixit mecum annos XI menses VIII utrisque hoc sepulcrum fieri voluit\n
EDCS-28300141.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Dracontius wished this tomb to be made for himself and for his dearest and most obedient wife Aurelia Sabinilla, who lived with him for 11 years and 8 months.
EDCS-28300141.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Dracontius's wish for a shared tomb for himself and his wife Aurelia Sabinilla, who lived 11 years and 8 months.

EDCS-28300142.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Oppiae Martiae Oppius Cosmus filius matri bene merenti\n
EDCS-28300142.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Oppius Cosmus [erected this] to his deserving mother, Oppia Martia.
EDCS-28300142.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument erected by Oppius Cosmus to his mother, Oppia Martia.

EDCS-28300143.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Retinia Queta Retinio Felicissimo fratri carissimo qui vixit plus minus annos XXV\n
EDCS-28300143.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Retinius Festissimus, dearest brother, who lived about 25 years.
EDCS-28300143.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Retinius Festissimus, brother of the deceased, who lived approximately 25 years.

EDCS-28300144.text_edition: \n Titus Plotius Clemens Libero Patri aram votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28300144.translation: Titus Plotius Clemens gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow by dedicating an altar to Father Liber.
EDCS-28300144.summary: This inscription records Titus Plotius Clemens dedicating an altar to Father Liber, likely in fulfillment of a vow, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300145.text_edition: \n Caius Turranius Cai filius Severus evocatus Augusti Latrae aram posuit\n
EDCS-28300145.translation: Gaius Turranius Severus, son of Caius, a conscripted soldier of Augustus, dedicated an altar at Latrae.
EDCS-28300145.summary: This inscription records Gaius Turranius Severus, a soldier conscripted by Augustus, dedicating an altar at a place called Latrae, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300146.text_edition: \n Appinius Pollia Faventia Quadratus |(centurio) veteranorum legionis IIII Macedonicae annos natus XL bis huc pietas ossa bene qui<es>cant\n
EDCS-28300146.translation: Appinius Pollia Faventia Quadratus, a centurion of the veterans of the Legio IV Macedonica, aged 40, here his piety brings his bones to rest in peace.
EDCS-28300146.summary: This inscription records the burial or memorial of Appinius Pollia Faventia Quadratus, a 40-year-old veteran centurion of Legio IV Macedonica, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300147.text_edition: \n Titus Cillius Titi filius Fabia domo Laranda veteranus legionis XI nnorum LXX stipendiorum XXXIIX E\n
EDCS-28300147.translation: Titus Cillius, son of Titus, of the Fabia tribe, from Laranda, a veteran of Legio XI, aged 70, with 38 years of service.
EDCS-28300147.summary: This inscription records Titus Cillius, a 70-year-old veteran of Legio XI with 38 years of service, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300148.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus SMEITIOM annorum LVIIII V Maria coniux posuit\n
EDCS-28300148.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Maria, his wife, dedicated this for Smeitiom, aged 59.
EDCS-28300148.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument dedicated by Maria to her husband Smeitiom, aged 59, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300149.text_edition: \n Iovi votum solvit Gaius Calius libens laetus merito\n
EDCS-28300149.translation: Gaius Calius gladly, joyfully, and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter.
EDCS-28300149.summary: This inscription records Gaius Calius fulfilling a vow to Jupiter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300150.text_edition: \n Caius Helvius Maximinus Iovi Optimo Maximo ex viso aram posuit\n
EDCS-28300150.translation: Gaius Helvius Maximinus dedicated an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus after seeing a vision.
EDCS-28300150.summary: This inscription records Gaius Helvius Maximinus dedicating an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus after a divine vision, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300151.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Tertius Alexander \n
EDCS-28300151.translation: Lucius Tertius Alexander [dedicated an altar] to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-28300151.summary: This inscription records Lucius Tertius Alexander dedicating an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300152.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Caius Vibius Iulianus miles legionis I adiutricis frumentarius\n
EDCS-28300152.translation: Gaius Vibius Iulianus, a soldier of the Legio I Adiutrix, a quartermaster, [dedicated an altar] to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-28300152.summary: This inscription records Gaius Vibius Iulianus, a quartermaster soldier of Legio I Adiutrix, dedicating an altar to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300153.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Publius Aelius Andes Barcini votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28300153.translation: Publius Aelius Andes Barcini gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Protector.
EDCS-28300153.summary: This inscription records Publius Aelius Andes Barcini fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Conservator, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300154.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Conservatori Lucius S P posuit\n
EDCS-28300154.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, the Protector, Lucius S.P. erected this.
EDCS-28300154.summary: A dedication to Jupiter, likely erected by Lucius, though his full name and the type of monument are lost.

EDCS-28300155.text_edition: \n Mercurio Augusto sacrum Quintus B R F N V S votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-28300155.translation: Sacred to Mercury Augustus; Quintus B.R.F.N.V.S. willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28300155.summary: A votive inscription to Mercury Augustus, dedicated by Quintus who willingly paid his vow.

EDCS-28300156.text_edition: \n Neptuno sacrum Lucius Lipo votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-28300156.translation: Sacred to Neptune; Lucius Lipo willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28300156.summary: A votive inscription to Neptune, dedicated by Lucius Lipo.

EDCS-28300157.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate II consuli II patri patriae decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28300157.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the deified Trajan the Parthian, grandson of the deified Nerva, Trajan Hadrian Augustus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the second time, consul for the second time, father of the country, by decree of the town council.
EDCS-28300157.summary: An honorific inscription for Emperor Hadrian, detailing his titles and issued by the town council.

EDCS-28300158.summary: A formal declaration or title associated with Emperor Constans.

EDCS-28300159.text_edition: \n Cnaeo Minicio Faustino Sex(to) Iulio Cai? filio Sergia Severo seviro turmae V equitum Romanorum IIIIviro viarum curandarum XVviro sacris faciundis tribuno militum legionis XIII geminae quaestori provinciae Macedoniae candidato divi Traiani Parthici tribuno plebis candidato eiusdem praetori legato legionis XIIII geminae legato pro praetore Imperatoris Traiani Hadriani Augusti provinciae Daciae consuli legato pro praetore provinciae Moesiae inferioris legato pro praetore provinciae Brittanniae legato pro praetore provinciae Iudaeae legato pro praetore provinciae S<y=V>riae huic senatus auctore Imperatore Caesare Traiano Hadriano Augusto ornamenta triumphalia decrevit ob res in Iudaea prospere gestas decreto decurionum \n
EDCS-28300159.translation: To Gnaeus Minicius Faustinus Sextus Iulius Severus, son of Caius? Sergia, Seviri of the Vth troop of Roman Knights, commissioner of roads, member of the XVviri for sacred rites, military tribune of the XIII Gemina legion, quaestor of the province of Macedonia, candidate of the deified Trajan Parthicus, tribune of the plebs, candidate of the same, praetor, legate of the XIIII Gemina legion, legate pro praetore of Emperor Trajan Hadrian Augustus in the province of Dacia, consul, legate pro praetore in the province of Lower Moesia, legate pro praetore in the province of Britannia, legate pro praetore in the province of Judea, legate pro praetore in the province of Syria; with the approval of the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrian Augustus, the Senate decreed triumphal honors to him for his successful deeds in Judea, by decree of the town council.
EDCS-28300159.summary: A lengthy cursus honorum detailing the extensive career of Gnaeus Minicius Faustinus, who was honored by the Senate with triumphal decorations for his service in Judea.

EDCS-28300161.text_edition: \n CI AVI INI legionis XI nnorum XLV stipendiorum XXI Caius Aemilius Cai filius Marcellus nnorum XIIX pater et filius hic situs! est sic\n
EDCS-28300161.translation: Caius Aemilius Marcellus, son of Caius, of the legion XI, aged 45, with 21 years of service, aged 18, lies here, father and son. Thus...
EDCS-28300161.summary: A tombstone inscription for Caius Aemilius Marcellus, a soldier of the XI Legion, noting his age and service, and mentioning his son.

EDCS-28300162.text_edition: \n Lucius Cassius Luci filius Tromentina Martialis Aquae Statiellae miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis centuria Pulpidi Silvestris stipendiorum XII annorum XXXV titulum fieri iussit heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-28300162.translation: Lucius Cassius Martialis, son of Lucius, of the Tromentina tribe, from Aquae Statiellae, a soldier of the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, in the centuria of Pulpidus Silvestris, with 12 years of service, aged 35. His heir ordered this tombstone to be made and saw to its construction.
EDCS-28300162.summary: A tombstone inscription for Lucius Cassius Martialis, a soldier of the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, whose heir commissioned the monument.

EDCS-28300163.text_edition: \n D(is) M(anibus) Aulus? ubius Auli f(ilius) Celer |(centurio) legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis domo Verona nnorum LXIII stipendiorum XXXXIII titulum fieri iussit Panthagathus libertus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-28300163.translation: To the departed spirits. Aulus? Celer, son of Aulus, centurion of the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, from Verona, aged 63, with 43 years of service. His freedman Panthagathus ordered this tombstone to be made and saw to its construction.
EDCS-28300163.summary: A tombstone inscription for Aulus? Celer, a centurion of the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis legion from Verona, commissioned by his freedman.

EDCS-28300164.text_edition: \n Titus Cominius Cai filius Romilia Ateste miles legionis XI annorum XL stipendiorum XVI hic situs est frater fratri posuit vixsi sic quoad potui semper bene pauper honeste fraudavi nullum nunc iuvat ossa mea\n
EDCS-28300164.translation: Titus Cominius, son of Caius, of the Romilia tribe, from Ateste, a soldier of the XI legion, aged 40, with 16 years of service, lies here. His brother set this up for his brother. 'I lived as best I could, always poor, honest, and without deceit; now it helps no one to disturb my bones.'
EDCS-28300164.summary: A tombstone inscription for Titus Cominius, a soldier of the XI legion, set up by his brother with a poignant epitaph reflecting on his life.

EDCS-28300165.text_edition: \n Salvius Frebranus Titi filius Quirina Baculus hastatus prior legionis XX\n
EDCS-28300165.translation: Salvius Frebranus Baculus, son of Titus, of the Voltinia tribe, prior hastatus of the 20th Legion.
EDCS-28300165.summary: This is a military inscription naming a soldier, Salvius Frebranus Baculus, of the 20th Legion, likely from the late 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28300167.text_edition: \n IMA NAEV Quinti liberti Primi Quintus Naevius Cai filius Voltinia Ponticus signifer\n
EDCS-28300167.translation: Quintus Naevius Ponticus, son of Caius, of the Voltinia tribe, signifer (standard-bearer), freedman of Primus, freedman of Quintus Naevius.
EDCS-28300167.summary: This inscription records Quintus Naevius Ponticus, a soldier serving as a signifer, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28300168.text_edition: \n Quintus Valerius Quinti filius Aniensis Niger domo Foro Iulii veteranus legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis nnorum XLV stipendiorum XXIIII hic situs est testamento fieri iussit Caius Pontius et Quintus Gussius heredes fecerunt\n
EDCS-28300168.translation: Quintus Valerius Niger, son of Quintus, of the Aniensis tribe, from Forum Julii, a veteran of the 11th Claudian Pia Fidelis Legion, aged 45, with 24 years of service, lies buried here; he ordered this to be made by will, Caius Pontius and Quintus Gussius, his heirs, made it.
EDCS-28300168.summary: This is a tombstone for Quintus Valerius Niger, a veteran of the 11th Legion, who died at age 45 after 24 years of service, with his heirs commissioning the monument.

EDCS-28300169.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Cai Maeloni Cai liberto Celeris domo Arretio annorum LXX Maelonius Erastus patrono posuit\n
EDCS-28300169.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Maelonius Erastus dedicated this to his patron Celer, freedman of Caius, son of Caius, from Arretium, aged 70.
EDCS-28300169.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Maelonius Erastus to his patron, Celer, a freedman from Arretium, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28300170.text_edition: \n Folnia Auli filia Spes Annia Spes Aulus Folnius parentes posuerunt\n
EDCS-28300170.translation: Folnia Spes and Aulus Folnius, parents, dedicated this to their parents.
EDCS-28300170.summary: This inscription records a dedication by children to their parents, likely a funerary monument from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28300171.text_edition: \n <D=L>(is) Manibus Caecili-- Prae Q D M S P II T <s=R>ibi et coniugi \n
EDCS-28300171.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Caecilius [---] Prae [---] for himself and his wife.
EDCS-28300171.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to Caecilius and his wife, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28300173.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Traiani Parthici filio divi Nervae nepoti Traiano Hadriano Augusto pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli III decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28300173.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Trajan Hadrianus Augustus, son of the divine Trajan Parthicus, grandson of the divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, consul for the third time, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28300173.summary: This is an official dedication to Emperor Hadrian, likely commemorating a public work or honor, dating to his reign (117-138 CE).

EDCS-28300174.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Lucio Aurelio Vero Augusto divi Antonini Pii filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti divi Nervae abnepoti pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate III consuli II decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28300174.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Aurelius Verus Augustus, son of the divine Antoninus Pius, grandson of the divine Hadrian, great-grandson of the divine Trajan Parthicus, great-great-grandson of the divine Nerva, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power for the third time, consul for the second time, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28300174.summary: This is an official dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius (as co-emperor Verus), likely commemorating a public work or honor, dating to his reign (161-169 CE).

EDCS-28300175.text_edition: \n Quinto Cestio Quinti filio Proculo annorum XXXX IIviro bis Cestia Quinti filia Quintina patri pientissimo\n
EDCS-28300175.translation: To Quintus Cestius Proculus, son of Quintus, aged 42, twice duovir (chief magistrate); Cestia Quintina, daughter of Quintus, dedicated this to her most devoted father.
EDCS-28300175.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Cestia Quintina to her father, Quintus Cestius Proculus, a former duovir, likely from a Roman colony in the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28300176.text_edition: \n Memoriai sic Dentonis patris Sextus Thorius Capito fecit sibi et suis\n
EDCS-28300176.translation: Sextus Thorius Capito made this memorial for his father Denton and for himself and his family.
EDCS-28300176.summary: This inscription records Sextus Thorius Capito erecting a memorial for his father Denton and for his own family, likely a funerary monument from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28300177.text_edition: \n Titius Caper Silvano solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28300177.translation: Titius Caper willingly paid his due to Silvanus.
EDCS-28300177.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titius Caper to the god Silvanus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300179.text_edition: \n Tito Iulio Clementi decurioni Asseriae Iulia Titi filia Proclina fratri posuit\n
EDCS-28300179.translation: Iulia Proclina, daughter of Titus, dedicated this to her brother Tito Iulio Clemente, a decurion of Asseria.
EDCS-28300179.summary: This tombstone was erected by Iulia Proclina for her brother Tito Iulio Clemente, a local councillor, probably in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300180.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Cornelio Eutychiano Cornelia Paulina patri pientissimo fecit\n
EDCS-28300180.translation: Cornelia Paulina dedicated this to the Divine Spirits for her most devoted father, Marcus Cornelius Eutychianus.
EDCS-28300180.summary: This funerary inscription was set up by Cornelia Paulina for her father, Marcus Cornelius Eutychianus, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300181.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Laetiliae Aprillae Laetilia Fructa matri <f=E>ecit \n
EDCS-28300181.translation: Laetilia Fructa dedicated this to the Divine Spirits for her mother, Laetilia Priscilla.
EDCS-28300181.summary: This tombstone was erected by Laetilia Fructa for her mother, Laetilia Priscilla, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300182.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicating a monument to Publius Papirius, likely funerary and from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300185.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valeriae Victorinae coniugi incomparabili bene merenti Vibullius Doryphorus maritus\n
EDCS-28300185.translation: Vibullius Doryphorus, her husband, dedicated this to the Divine Spirits for his incomparable and deserving wife, Valeria Victorina.
EDCS-28300185.summary: This funerary inscription was dedicated by Vibullius Doryphorus for his wife Valeria Victorina, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300186.text_edition: \n Calpurnia Cai filia Ceuna Latrae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28300186.translation: Calpurnia Ceuna, daughter of Caius, willingly paid her due to Latra.
EDCS-28300186.summary: This is a votive inscription by Calpurnia Ceuna to the goddess Latra, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300187.text_edition: \n Pupillorum Moicorum liberta Dumma Latrae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28300187.translation: Dumma, freedwoman of the Pupilli Moici, willingly paid her due to Latra.
EDCS-28300187.summary: This is a votive inscription by Dumma, a freedwoman, to the goddess Latra, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28300188.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Picusi filius Ceunus Latrae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28300188.translation: Caius Iulius Ceunus, son of Picus, willingly paid his due to Latra.
EDCS-28300188.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caius Iulius Ceunus to the goddess Latra, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400114.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Imperatoris Caesaris Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti Arabici Adiabenici Parthici maximi filio decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28400114.translation: By decree of the decurions, to Imperator Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, son of Imperator Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus Arabicus Adiabenicus Parthicus Maximus.
EDCS-28400114.summary: This is an official dedication, likely to a public monument, honoring Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus (Caracalla) and commissioned by local councillors, probably dating to the early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400115.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Publio Licinio Valeriano Pio Felici Augusto pontifici maximo tribuniciae potestatis consuli III patri patriae decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28400115.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Valerianus Pius Felix Augustus, chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the third time, consul, father of the country, by decree of the local council.
EDCS-28400115.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Valerian, likely erected by the local council of a city in the mid-3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400116.text_edition: \n Caio Octavio Tidio Tossiaano Lucio Iavoleno Prisco legato legionis IV Flaviae legato legionis III Augustae iuridico provinciae Brittanniae legato consuari provinciae Germaniae superioris legato consulari provinciae Syriae proconsuli provinciae Africae pontifici Publius Mutilius Publi filius Claudia Crispinus testamento poni iussit amico carissimo\n
EDCS-28400116.translation: Publius Mutilius Crispinus, son of Publius, of the Claudia tribe, by his will ordered this to be set up to Gaius Octavius Tidianus Iavolenus Priscus, former legate of the IV Flavian Legion, legate of the III Augusta Legion, legal official of Britain, consular legate of Germania Superior, consular legate of Syria, proconsul of Africa, and priest, his dearest friend.
EDCS-28400116.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by will to Gaius Octavius Iavolenus Priscus, a distinguished senator and former governor, likely in the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400117.text_edition: \n veteranus legionis VIII Lucio Octavio filio Claudia Frontino fratri\n
EDCS-28400117.translation: To Lucius Octavius Frontinus, veteran of the VIII Legion, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, his brother.
EDCS-28400117.summary: This is a funerary dedication for a veteran of the VIII Legion, Lucius Octavius Frontinus, erected by his brother, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400118.text_edition: \n Cocceio Umbriano decurioni auguri et pontifici civitatis Paralissensium sic provinciae Daciae Cocceius Severus filius patri pientissimo\n
EDCS-28400118.translation: To Cocceius Umbranus, decurion, augur, and priest of the city of Paralissum. Cocceius Severus, son of Cocceius, of the Claudia tribe, set this up to his most devoted father.
EDCS-28400118.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated to Cocceius Umbranus, a local official and priest, by his son, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400119.text_edition: \n Marco Fulvio filio Claudia Rufo aedili\n
EDCS-28400119.translation: To Marcus Fulvius Rufus, son of Marcus, of the Claudia tribe, the aedile.
EDCS-28400119.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Fulvius Rufus, likely for his service as an aedile, possibly in a Roman colony or municipality, dated to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400120.text_edition: \n Caio Lartio Sabiniano officiali Naeditarum sic rarissimo vitae incorruptae nnorum XXXII mensium III patri filiorum II Magia Maximina coniugi incomparabili cum quo vixit nnos XV virginio infelicissimo mater posuit\n
EDCS-28400120.translation: To Gaius Lartius Sabinianus, an official in the treasury, of most distinguished character, of incorrupt life, aged 32 years and 3 months, with 2 sons. Magia Maximina erected this to her incomparable wife, with whom he lived for 15 years, for her most unhappy virginal life.
EDCS-28400120.summary: This is a funerary monument for Gaius Lartius Sabinianus, an official, erected by his wife, dating to the late 1st to early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400121.text_edition: \n Sextus Octavius Claudia Constans IIvir pontifex testamento fieri iussit e<p=R>uloque dedicari \n
EDCS-28400121.translation: Sextus Octavius, *duovir* and priest, son of Sextus, of the Claudia tribe, by his will ordered this to be made and dedicated.
EDCS-28400121.summary: This records a posthumous dedication ordered by Sextus Octavius, a *duovir* and priest, likely in a Roman municipality, dated to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400122.text_edition: \n Octavia Quinti filia Secunda viva fecit sibi et Octaviae Titi filiae Gracillae filiae Octaviae Sexti filiae Celsinae nepti Quinto Octavio patri aedili IIviro III Quintiae Voltisae matri Tito Octavio Sexti filio Gracili viro aedili IIviro pontifici Caio Octavio Rufo fratri aedili IIviro pontifici Sexto Octavio Sexti filio Celso genero\n
EDCS-28400122.translation: Octavia Secunda, daughter of Quintus, of the Claudia tribe, alive, made this for herself and for her daughter Octavia Gracilla, daughter of Titus, daughter of Octavia Celsina, granddaughter; for her father Quintus, aedile and *duovir* for the third time; for Quintia Voltisa, her mother; for Titus Octavius Gracilis, son of Sextus, her husband, aedile and *duovir*, priest; for Gaius Octavius Rufus, her brother, aedile and *duovir*, priest; for Sextus Octavius Celsus, her son-in-law.
EDCS-28400122.summary: This is a family funerary monument erected by Octavia Secunda, listing herself, deceased family members, and relatives who held civic and religious offices, likely in the late 1st to 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400123.text_edition: \n Titus Turranius Luci filius Claudia Rufus ob honorem aedilitatis porticum longam pedes C latam pedes XX item clatra cum Lucio Turranio Proculo et Tito Turannio Frontone filiis fecit\n
EDCS-28400123.translation: Titus Turranius Rufus, son of Titus, of the Claudia tribe, for the honor of his aedileship, built a portico 100 feet long and 20 feet wide, and also a cloisters, with his sons Lucius Turranius Proculus and Titus Turranius Fronton.
EDCS-28400123.summary: This is a public works dedication by Titus Turranius Rufus, celebrating his aedileship by funding the construction of a portico and cloisters, likely in the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400124.text_edition: \n Octaviae Cai filiae Quintilliae Iulia Quinti filia Paula mater fecit\n
EDCS-28400124.translation: Iulia Paula, daughter of Quintus, mother, made this for Octavia Quintillia, daughter of Gaius.
EDCS-28400124.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Octavia Quintillia, erected by her mother Iulia Paula, likely in the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400125.text_edition: \n a Cai filia Maxima Titi Iulii \n
EDCS-28400125.translation: To Caius, son of Maxima, from Titus Julius.
EDCS-28400125.summary: This inscription likely records a funerary monument, possibly an epitaph, dedicated to Caius by Titus Julius, and appears to be a family or personal dedication.

EDCS-28400126.translation: To Maximo Octavio Frontono, son of Marcus.
EDCS-28400126.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription for Maximo Octavio Frontono, son of Marcus.

EDCS-28400127.text_edition: \n Opiniae Marci filiae Neptillae nnum XIIII virgini desperatae prope diem nuptiarum defunctae Marcus Opinius Rufus et Gellia Neptilla parentes\n
EDCS-28400127.translation: To Neptilla, daughter of Marcus, aged 14, a virgin who died in despair shortly before her wedding. Marcus Opinius Rufus and Gellia Neptilla, parents, [erected this].
EDCS-28400127.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Neptilla, a 14-year-old virgin who died shortly before her wedding, erected by her parents Marcus Opinius Rufus and Gellia Neptilla.

EDCS-28400128.text_edition: \n Marcus Papisius Marci filius Claudia Maximus vivus fecit sibi et suis Marco Papisio Marci filio Claudia Nigro Aemiliae Volsci filiae Pietati parentibus hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-28400128.translation: Marcus Papisius Maximus, son of Marcus, of the Claudia tribe, made this monument while alive for himself and his [family], for Marcus Papisius Niger, son of Marcus, of the Claudia tribe, and for Pietas, daughter of Volscus, his parents; this monument will not be inherited by his heir.
EDCS-28400128.summary: This is an ownership inscription for a monument created by Marcus Papisius Maximus for himself, his parents, and other family members, stipulating that it cannot be inherited.

EDCS-28400129.text_edition: \n Sentia Cai filia Prima viva fecit sibi et Caio Tario Cai filio Sextioni viro Laecaniae Cai filiae Maxumae matri Marco Tario Cai filio Clementi filio Publio Tario Cai filio Rufo et Lucio Tario Cai filio Celso filio\n
EDCS-28400129.translation: Sentia Prima, daughter of Caius, while alive, made this for herself, and for her husband Caius Tarius Sextio, son of Caius; for her mother Laecania Maxuma, daughter of Caius; for her son Clementes, son of Caius; and for her son Rufus, son of Caius; and for her son Celsus, son of Caius.
EDCS-28400129.summary: This is a private funerary monument inscription made by Sentia Prima for herself and several family members, including her husband, mother, and sons.

EDCS-28400130.text_edition: \n Marcus Tarius Cai filius Triera Tariae Sexto Tario Cai filio fratri Tariae Cai filiae Tertiae sorori Quinto Tario Cai filio fratri vivos sic fecit et suis filiis\n
EDCS-28400130.translation: Marcus Tarius, son of Caius, made this while alive for himself and his sons, and for his brother Sextus Tarius, son of Caius; for his sister Taria Tertia, daughter of Caius; and for his brother Quintus Tarius, son of Caius.
EDCS-28400130.summary: This is a private funerary monument inscription made by Marcus Tarius for himself, his sons, and his siblings Sextus, Taria Tertia, and Quintus.

EDCS-28400131.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus piissimo dies Severus parentes posuerunt\n
EDCS-28400131.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. His most devoted parents erected this for Severus.
EDCS-28400131.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Severus by his parents.

EDCS-28400133.text_edition: \n Iano Patri Quintus Ennius filius Agrippa votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28400133.translation: To Janus the Father, Quintus Ennius Agrippa, son, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400133.summary: This is a votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow to the god Janus by Quintus Ennius Agrippa.

EDCS-28400136.text_edition: \n Titus Caninius Titi filius Sergia Maximus veteranus cohortis VI praetoriae sibi testamento poni iussit Marcus Maesius Paulus heres ponendum curavit\n
EDCS-28400136.translation: Titus Caninius Maximus, son of Titus, a veteran of the 6th Praetorian cohort, ordered this to be set up for himself in his will; Marcus Maesius Paulus, his heir, took care to have it erected.
EDCS-28400136.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Titus Caninius Maximus, a veteran of the Praetorian Guard, who arranged for it in his will and was honored by his heir Marcus Maesius Paulus.

EDCS-28400137.text_edition: \n Caio Iulio Cai filio Sergia Agrippae militi legionis VII piae fidelis centuria Agrippae p Avita marito Agrippina patri\n
EDCS-28400137.translation: To Gaius Julius Agrippa, soldier of the 7th Legion, loyal and faithful, of Agrippa's century, [erected by] Avita. To Agrippina, [his] father.
EDCS-28400137.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Agrippa, a soldier in the 7th Legion, likely set up by his wife Avita, and also possibly mentioning his father, Agrippina.

EDCS-28400138.text_edition: \n Marco Iulio Severo militi cohortis VII urbanae Calpurnia Volaesa mater posuit\n
EDCS-28400138.translation: Calpurnia Volaesa erected this for her son, Marcus Julius Severus, a soldier of the VII Urban Cohort.
EDCS-28400138.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a mother for her son, a soldier in the VII Urban Cohort.

EDCS-28400141.text_edition: \n Aprilis Gemellae bene merenti conservae posuit Dis Manibus\n
EDCS-28400141.translation: To Aprilis, his well-deserving slave, erected this. To the spirits of the departed.
EDCS-28400141.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to a deserving slave named Aprilis.

EDCS-28400142.text_edition: \n Baebiae Luci filiae Tertullinae nnorum XXIX Calpurnia Tertulla mater viva posuit\n
EDCS-28400142.translation: Calpurnia Tertulla, her mother, erected this while alive for her daughter, Baebia Tertullina, daughter of Lucius, aged 29.
EDCS-28400142.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a mother for her deceased daughter, aged 29.

EDCS-28400143.text_edition: \n Calpurnia Cai filia Ceuna viva fecit sibi et Oppiae filiae Secundae matri et Oppiae Oepli filiae Voltisae amitae et Calpurniae Cai filiae Oeplae sorori\n
EDCS-28400143.translation: Calpurnia Ceuna, daughter of Caius, made this while alive for herself, and for her mother Oppia Secunda, daughter of Oppius, and for her aunt Voltisa, daughter of Oppius, and for her sister Calpurnia Oepla, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-28400143.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a woman for herself and several female relatives, including her mother, aunt, and sister.

EDCS-28400145.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Dion<y=V>siae infelicissimae defunctae annorum III mensium X Atticilla alumnae posuit \n
EDCS-28400145.translation: Atticilla, her foster mother, erected this for Dionysia, her most unhappy departed one, aged 3 years and 10 months. To the spirits of the departed.
EDCS-28400145.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a foster mother for a deceased child.

EDCS-28400146.text_edition: \n Marco Iulio Titi filio Proculo patri Lucio Iulio Proculo fratri Marcus Iulius Severus\n
EDCS-28400146.translation: Marcus Julius Severus [erected this] for his father, Marcus Julius Proculus, son of Titus, and for his brother, Lucius Julius Proculus.
EDCS-28400146.summary: This monument is dedicated by a son to his deceased father and brother.

EDCS-28400147.text_edition: \n Iuliae Cai filiae Oiae Iulia filia Patrinia matri posuit\n
EDCS-28400147.translation: Iulia Patrinia erected this for her mother, Iulia Oia, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-28400147.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a daughter for her mother.

EDCS-28400148.translation: To Iulia Secunda, daughter of Iaetus.
EDCS-28400148.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Iulia Secunda.

EDCS-28400149.text_edition: \n Iulia Valentilla sibi et Iuliae Virnoni patronae viva fecit\n
EDCS-28400149.translation: Iulia Valentilla made this while alive for herself and for her patroness, Iulia Virnoni.
EDCS-28400149.summary: This monument was set up by a woman for herself and her patroness.

EDCS-28400150.text_edition: \n Octavia Titi filia Tertulla viva sibi et suis fecit in fronte pedes XX in agro pedes XXX\n
EDCS-28400150.translation: Octavia Tertulla, daughter of Titus, made this while alive for herself and her family; 20 feet in front, 30 feet in the field.
EDCS-28400150.summary: This monument, set up by a woman for herself and her family, includes dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-28400151.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus s(acrum) Rutilio Amoeno conservo Parthenopeus bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-28400151.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Parthenopeus erected this for his deserving fellow slave, Rutilius Amoenus.
EDCS-28400151.summary: A funerary dedication by Parthenopeus to his fellow slave Rutilius Amoenus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400152.text_edition: \n Tullia Oepli filia Voltisa sibi et Caio Iulio Ceuni filio Acirrio filio et Orpiae Quinti filiae Opiavae matri viva fecit in fronte pedes V |(triens) in agro pedes X |(dextans)\n
EDCS-28400152.translation: Tullia Oepli, daughter of Voltisa, made this while alive for herself, for Gaius Julius Ceuni, son of Acirrius, and for Opiava, daughter of Quinti, her mother. In the front five feet; in the land ten feet.
EDCS-28400152.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Tullia Oepli for herself and family members, including land and front measurements, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400154.text_edition: \n Apollini Lycio Marcus Publicius Campanus aedituus iussu ipsius dedit dedicavit\n
EDCS-28400154.translation: Marcus Publicius Campanus, the temple attendant, dedicated and consecrated this to Apollo Lycius by his command.
EDCS-28400154.summary: A dedication to Apollo Lycius by the temple attendant Marcus Publicius Campanus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400157.text_edition: \n Caius Egnatius Secundus ara Minervae libens\n
EDCS-28400157.translation: Gaius Egnatius Secundus willingly (dedicated this) altar to Minerva.
EDCS-28400157.summary: A dedication of an altar to Minerva by Gaius Egnatius Secundus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400158.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Divi filius Augustus parens coloniae murum et turris dedit Titus Iulius Optatus turris vetustate consumptas inpensa sua restituit\n
EDCS-28400158.translation: The Emperor Caesar Augustus, son of the Divine, father of the colony, gave walls and towers; Titus Julius Optatus restored towers consumed by age at his own expense.
EDCS-28400158.summary: A record of public works where Emperor Augustus provided walls and towers, and Titus Julius Optatus restored existing ones, likely from the late Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-28400159.text_edition: \n Tiberius Caesar divi Augusti filius Augustus imperator pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XX consul III legio VII legio XI Publio Cornelio Dolabella legato pro praetore\n
EDCS-28400159.translation: Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the Divine Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 20th time, consul for the 3rd time (dedicates this to) Publius Cornelius Dolabella, governor with proconsular authority, of the VII and XI legions.
EDCS-28400159.summary: An inscription likely recording a military honor or dedication by Tiberius Caesar to the governor Publius Cornelius Dolabella, dated to Tiberius's reign (AD 14-37).

EDCS-28400161.text_edition: \n Caius Allius speculator legionis [[]] vivus fecit sibi et in fronte pedes in agro pedes\n
EDCS-28400161.translation: Gaius Allius, a scout of the [ ] legion, made this while alive for himself. In the front [ ] feet; in the land [ ] feet.
EDCS-28400161.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Gaius Allius, a military scout, for himself, with incomplete measurements, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400162.text_edition: \n Caio Allio Titi filio Fabia Luco veterano legionis XX Caio Allio Prisco filio nnorum X Allia Cai liberta Mete patrono et filio viva bene merenti in fronte pedes X in agro pedes XVI et Caio Allio Vitali filio\n
EDCS-28400162.translation: For Gaius Allius Luco, son of Titus of the Fabia tribe, a veteran of the XX legion, and for his son Gaius Allius Priscus, aged 10, and for Allia Mete, freedwoman of Gaius, as patron and son, alive, well-deserving. In the front ten feet; in the land sixteen feet; and for Gaius Allius Vitalis, son.
EDCS-28400162.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Allia Mete for her husband Gaius Allius Luco, their son Gaius Allius Priscus, and another son Gaius Allius Vitalis, including land and front measurements, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400163.text_edition: \n Manius Antestius Auctus veteranus vivus fecit sibi et suis in fronte pedes XX in agro pedes XVI et ustrina in fronte pedes XX\n
EDCS-28400163.translation: Manius Antestius Auctus, a veteran, made this while alive for himself and his family. In the front twenty feet; in the land sixteen feet; and a crematorium (ustrina) in the front twenty feet.
EDCS-28400163.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by the veteran Manius Antestius Auctus for himself and his family, including land and ustrina measurements, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400164.text_edition: \n Sextus Atilius Sexti filius Papiria veteranus legionis VII domo Ticino hic situs est testamento fieri iussit in fronte pedes XV in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-28400164.translation: Sextus Atilius, son of Sextus of the Papiria tribe, a veteran of the VII legion, from Ticinum, lies here. He ordered this to be made by his will. In the front fifteen feet; in the land twenty feet.
EDCS-28400164.summary: A funerary monument for Sextus Atilius, a veteran of the VII legion from Ticinum, erected according to his will, including land and front measurements, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400165.text_edition: \n Tito Helvio Scaptia Marino IIIIviro Altini Titus Helvius Titi filius Salinator |(centurio) legionis VII patri in fronte pedes XXX in agro pedes XXX hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-28400165.translation: Titus Helvius Salinator, son of Titus Helvius, from the tribe Scaptia, a *quattuorvir*, erected this monument for his father, Titus Helvius Marino, a *quattuorvir*. It measures 30 feet in front and 30 feet in depth, and this monument will not pass to heirs.
EDCS-28400165.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Titus Helvius Salinator for his father, Titus Helvius Marino, who was a *quattuorvir*, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28400166.text_edition: \n Lucius Fadienus Luci filius Pomptina Fuscus domo Dertona veteranus speculator vivus fecit sibi et Camuriae Pollae uxori Lucio Fadieno patri Vibiae Primae matri Fadienae Pollae sorori Fadieno Tertio fratri signifero legionis X<I=V>III geminae suisque omnibus ossibus inferre in fronte pedes XV in agro pedes XXXI \n
EDCS-28400166.translation: Lucius Fadienus Fuscus, son of Lucius, from the tribe Pomptina, originally from Dertona, a veteran *speculator*, made this tomb while alive for himself and his wife Camuria Polla, his father Lucius Fadienus, his mother Vibia Prima, his sister Fadiena Polla, his brother Fadienus Tertius, a standard-bearer of the Legio XIIII Gemina, and all his kin. It measures 15 feet in front and 31 feet in depth.
EDCS-28400166.summary: This is a family tomb built by Lucius Fadienus Fuscus, a veteran *speculator*, for himself and his relatives, likely dating to the late 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28400167.text_edition: \n Lucio Geminio Cai filio Tromentina Montano praefecto cohortis I Sebastenorum Publius Aufidius Postimus amico\n
EDCS-28400167.translation: To Lucius Geminius Montanus, son of Caius, from the tribe Tromentina, prefect of the I Sebastenorum cohort, from Publius Aufidius Postimus, his friend.
EDCS-28400167.summary: This is a dedication by Publius Aufidius Postimus to his friend Lucius Geminius Montanus, a cohort prefect, probably dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28400168.text_edition: \n Quinto Raecio Quinti filio Claudia Rufo primo pilo legionis XII fulminatae trecenario donis donato ab Imperatore Vespasiano et Tito Imperatore bello Iudaico ab imperatore Traiano bello Dacico principi praetorii Trebia Marci filia Procula marito testamento poni iussit\n
EDCS-28400168.translation: Trebia Procula, daughter of Marcus, wife of Quintus Raecius Rufus, son of Quintus, from the tribe Claudia, a *primus pilus* of the Legio XII Fulminata, who was honored with gifts by Emperor Vespasian and Emperor Titus in the Jewish War and by Emperor Trajan in the Dacian War, and was *princeps praetorii*, ordered this monument to be set up to her husband by her will.
EDCS-28400168.summary: This funerary monument, erected by Trebia Procula in memory of her husband Quintus Raecius Rufus, a distinguished centurion, honors his military achievements under Vespasian, Titus, and Trajan, likely dating to the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28400169.text_edition: \n Caius Trebius Cai filius Firmus veteranus legionis XI stipendiorum XXV nnum LXXX A O V F F D S E M Q M C S\n
EDCS-28400169.translation: Caius Trebius Firmus, son of Caius, from the tribe Tromentina, a veteran of the Legio XI, with 25 years of service, 80 years old, alive and well, freely dedicating this tomb.
EDCS-28400169.summary: This is a tomb built by Caius Trebius Firmus, a veteran of Legio XI with 25 years of service, indicating he died at the age of 80, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28400170.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-28400170.text_edition: \n Caius Arrenus Cai filius Tromentina Maximus aedilis honoratus ab ordine Iadestino loco libis in funus statua pedestri testamento fieri iussit arbitratu et cura Publi Helvi Corinthi et Cai Arreni Dionysi\n
EDCS-28400170.translation: Caius Arrenus Maximus, son of Caius, from the tribe Tromentina, an honored *aedile* by the council of Iadestina, ordered by his will that a statue of a standing man be made for his funeral and burial, by the arbitration and care of Publius Helvius Corinthus and Caius Arrenus Dionysius.
EDCS-28400170.summary: This inscription records the posthumous commission of a statue for the funeral of Caius Arrenus Maximus, an honored *aedile*, by his will, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28400171.text_edition: \n Quinto Asisieno Quinti filio Tromentina Agrippae aedili IIviro pontifici ex aere conlato decuriones et plebs\n
EDCS-28400171.translation: To Quintus Asisienus Agrippa, son of Quintus, from the tribe Tromentina, *aedile*, *duovir*, *pontifex*, by the decurions and the plebs from collected bronze.
EDCS-28400171.summary: This monument was erected by the local council and populace in honor of Quintus Asisienus Agrippa, who held the offices of *aedile*, *duovir*, and *pontifex*, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28400172.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Barbunteio Demetrio archigallo Salonitano qui annis XVII usque ad nnum LXXV integre sacra confecit Barbunteia Thallusa Callistera patrono pientissimo posuit\n
EDCS-28400172.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Barbunteia Thallusa Callistera placed this for her most devoted patron, Lucius Barbunteius Demetrius, chief priest of the Salonitans, who performed sacred rites faithfully for 17 years until the age of 75.
EDCS-28400172.summary: This funerary dedication by Barbunteia Thallusa Callistera honors her patron, Lucius Barbunteius Demetrius, the chief priest of the Salonitans, who served for 17 years and lived to 75, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28400173.text_edition: \n Quintus Dellius Quinti libertus Fuscus VIvir Augustalis vivus fecit sibi et suis in fronte pedes XX in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-28400173.translation: Quintus Dellius Fuscus, freedman of Quintus, a *sextumvir Augustalis*, made this tomb while alive for himself and his family. It measures 20 feet in front and 20 feet in depth.
EDCS-28400173.summary: This is a tomb built by Quintus Dellius Fuscus, a freedman and *sextumvir Augustalis*, for himself and his family, likely dating to the late 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28400174.text_edition: \n Melia Anniana in memoriam Quinti Laepici Quinti fili Sergia Bassi mariti sui emporium sterni et arcum fieri et statuas superponi testamento iussit ex HS DCsescentis milibus deducta XXvicesima populi Romani\n
EDCS-28400174.translation: Melia Anniana, by her will, ordered the market-place to be paved, an arch to be built, and statues to be placed on top, in memory of her husband Quintus Laepicius Bassus, son of Quintus, from the tribe Sergia, using 600,000 sesterces, after deducting the Roman people's 20% tax.
EDCS-28400174.summary: This inscription records Melia Anniana's posthumous public works funded by her will in memory of her husband Quintus Laepicius Bassus, including paving, an arch, and statues, likely dating to the late 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-28400175.text_edition: \n Feresiae Quinti filiae Tertullae nnorum XXIIII Quinto Feresio Quinti liberto Spiculo Augustali Vipsania Marci filia Silana fecit filiae viro et sibi\n
EDCS-28400175.translation: To Tertulla, daughter of Quintus Feresius, aged 24. Silana, daughter of Marcus Vipsanius, made this for her daughter, husband, and herself.
EDCS-28400175.summary: A funerary monument set up by Silana for her daughter Tertulla and husband Spiculus, an Augustalis and freedman of Quintus Feresius, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-28400176.text_edition: \n Meviae Feliculae annorum XII Marco Catonio Varo decurionalibus ornamentis honorato Mevia Arcas fecit filiae viro et sibi\n
EDCS-28400176.translation: To Mevia Felicula, aged 12. Mevia Arcas made this for her daughter, who was honored with decurional ornaments, and her husband, and herself.
EDCS-28400176.summary: A funerary monument erected by Mevia Arcas for her daughter Mevia Felicula and husband Marcus Catonius Varus, who held civic office, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400178.text_edition: \n Mariae Quinti filiae Paullae donum dedit\n
EDCS-28400178.translation: He gave as a gift to Paula, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-28400178.summary: A dedication, possibly for a statue or an object, to Paula, daughter of Quintus.

EDCS-28400180.text_edition: \n Publius Pomponius Publi libertus Iucundus sexvir\n
EDCS-28400180.translation: Publius Pomponius Iucundus, freedman of Publius, a sexvir.
EDCS-28400180.summary: An epitaph or dedication naming Publius Pomponius Iucundus, a freedman and a member of the college of six municipal officials (sexvir).

EDCS-28400181.text_edition: \n Quintus Quinctilius Optatus IIIIIIvir vivos sic fecit sibi et Quinctiliae Arbusculae matri et Numisiae Pusillae latum pedes XX longum ab unda ad maceriam\n
EDCS-28400181.translation: Quintus Quinctilius Optatus, a member of the college of six priests, made this for himself while alive, and for his mother Quinctilia Arbuscula, and for Numisia Pusilla, 20 feet wide from the water source to the wall.
EDCS-28400181.summary: A tomb inscription by Quintus Quinctilius Optatus, a high-ranking municipal priest, detailing dimensions and naming his mother and another individual, likely from the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-28400182.text_edition: \n Marco Titio Marci filio Sergia Marino decurioni testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-28400182.translation: He ordered this to be made by testament for Marcus Titius Sergia's son Marinus, a decurion.
EDCS-28400182.summary: A funerary monument ordered by testament for Marcus Titius Marinus, a decurion and son of Sergia.

EDCS-28400183.text_edition: \n Marco Trebio Proculo equom sic publicum habenti sacerdoti Liberi IIviro aedili Arba Trebia Marci filia Procula patri testamento poni iussit\n
EDCS-28400183.translation: Arba Trebia Procula, daughter of Marcus, ordered this to be placed by testament for her father, Marcus Trebius Proculus, who held the public horse, was a priest of Liber, a duovir, and an aedile.
