EDCS-28400183.summary: A funerary monument erected by testament by Arba Trebia Procula for her father Marcus Trebius Proculus, a prominent local magistrate and priest, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400184.text_edition: \n Caio Vettidio Cai filio Tromentina Maximo equom sic publicum habenti pontifici IIviro IIviro quinquennali patrono coloniae pater\n
EDCS-28400184.translation: For Gaius Vettidius Maximus, son of Gaius, from the Tromentina tribe, who held the public horse, was a pontiff, a duovir, a quinquennial duovir, and patron of the colony; his father [made this].
EDCS-28400184.summary: A funerary monument erected by a father for his son, Gaius Vettidius Maximus, a distinguished local official and priest, likely from the 2nd-3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400186.text_edition: \n nium Q CO Titi filia Tertulla \n
EDCS-28400186.summary: An incomplete inscription likely naming Tertulla, daughter of Titus.

EDCS-28400187.text_edition: \n Attico Publi Aufidi Longi servo dispensatori et Safiniae Procni Publius Aufidius Eros posuit fratri et coniugi\n
EDCS-28400187.translation: Publius Aufidius Eros dedicated this for his brother Atticus, slave of Publius Aufidius Longus and dispenser, and for Safinia Procne, his wife.
EDCS-28400187.summary: A funerary monument set up by Publius Aufidius Eros for his brother Atticus, an estate manager, and his wife Safinia Procne.

EDCS-28400188.text_edition: \n Manius Cornelius Hiero vivus fecit sibi et Corneliae Heroidi uxori carissimae et Manio Cornelio Carpo negotiatori oleario et Manio Cornelio Carpo iuris studioso et libertis libertabusque\n
EDCS-28400188.translation: Manius Cornelius Hiero made this while alive for himself and his dearest wife Cornelia Heroidis, and for Manius Cornelius Carpus, an oil merchant, and for Manius Cornelius Carpus, a student of law, and for their freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-28400188.summary: This is a funerary monument for Manius Cornelius Hiero, his wife, and other family members, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400189.text_edition: \n Caius Modius Cai filius Publilia Primus Verona nnorum XX hic situs est\n
EDCS-28400189.translation: Gaius Modius Primus, son of Gaius, of the Publilia tribe, aged 20 years, is buried here.
EDCS-28400189.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Modius Primus, a young man, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400190.text_edition: \n Abudia Maxima filio viro infelicissimo posuit\n
EDCS-28400190.translation: Abudia Maxima dedicated this for her most unfortunate son and husband.
EDCS-28400190.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, by a grieving woman for her son and husband, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400191.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Antonio Anbrosiano sic annorum XXI Lucius Antonius Stephanus et Cordia Pretiosa parentes bene merenti\n
EDCS-28400191.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Antonius Ambrosianus, aged 21 years. Lucius Antonius Stephanus and Cordia Pretiosa, his parents, made this for their well-deserving son.
EDCS-28400191.summary: This is a tombstone set up by parents for their deceased son, Lucius Antonius Ambrosianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400192.text_edition: \n Appuleia Marci filia Quinta libertis libertabusque suis et filiis V posterisque eorum quive ab eis liberabuntur\n
EDCS-28400192.translation: Appuleia Quinta, daughter of Marcius, for her freedmen and freedwomen, and for her children V and their descendants, and for those who will be freed by them.
EDCS-28400192.summary: This inscription records Appuleia Quinta's provision for her freedpeople and descendants, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400193.text_edition: \n Aurelio Aquilino liberto qui vixit nnos XXIIII Iulia Iulitta marito bene merenti et sibi\n
EDCS-28400193.translation: Iulia Iulitta for her freedman Aurelius Aquilinus, who lived 24 years, her well-deserving husband, and for herself.
EDCS-28400193.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a woman for her husband, who was also her freedman, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400194.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Tiberius Claudius, likely part of a longer dedication or epitaph from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400195.text_edition: \n Cornelia Restuta Corneliae Quartae patronae bene merenti\n
EDCS-28400195.translation: Cornelia Restuta for her patroness Cornelia Quarta, well-deserving, and for herself.
EDCS-28400195.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a freedwoman for her patroness, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400196.text_edition: \n Marco Curiatio Clementi Curiatius Vitalis patrono sibique et suis et Turpiliae Marci filiae madi coniugi vivus fecit\n
EDCS-28400196.translation: Curiatius Vitalis made this while alive for Marcus Curiatius Clementis, his patron, and for himself and his family, and for Turpilia, daughter of Marcius, his wife.
EDCS-28400196.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Curiatius Vitalis for his patron, himself, his family, and his wife, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400197.text_edition: \n Egnatia Spendusa Iuliae Honoratae mater misera fecit annorum XXXIIII\n
EDCS-28400197.translation: Egnatia Spendusa, a miserable mother, made this for Iulia Honorata, aged 34 years.
EDCS-28400197.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a grieving mother for her daughter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400198.text_edition: \n Entella Ianuari fili- pientissim- nnorum XXX\n
EDCS-28400198.translation: To Entella, daughter of Ianuarius, most dutiful, 30 years old.
EDCS-28400198.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Entella, a woman of 30, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-28400199.text_edition: \n Feresia |(mulieris) liberta Nebris viva fecit sibi et Caio Sempronio Corinthio viro suo loco sibi dato a Sempronia Tertulla viva\n
EDCS-28400199.translation: Feresia, freedwoman of Nebris, made this while alive for herself and for Gaius Sempronius Corinthius, her husband, in a plot given to her by Sempronia Tertulla, alive.
EDCS-28400199.summary: This funerary epitaph records a burial plot purchased by Feresia, a freedwoman, for herself and her husband, with the plot granted by Sempronia Tertulla.

EDCS-28400200.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flaviae Myrtalae Marius Phoebus coniugi bene merenti vivos sic fecit\n
EDCS-28400200.translation: To the divine spirits of Flavia Myrtala, Marius Phoebus made this while alive for his deserving wife.
EDCS-28400200.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Flavia Myrtala by her husband Marius Phoebus, who prepared the tomb while alive.

EDCS-28400202.text_edition: \n Helvia Veneria viva fecit sibi et Quinto Petronio Capitoni viro suo et Quinto Petronio Aphrodisio et suis libertis libertabusque posterisque eorum\n
EDCS-28400202.translation: Helvia Veneria made this while alive for herself and for Quintus Petronius Capito, her husband, and for Quintus Petronius Aphrodisius and their descendants, freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-28400202.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commissioned by Helvia Veneria for herself, her husband, and their descendants, including freedmen and freedwomen.

EDCS-28400203.text_edition: \n AMERA sicsaIco Septumi sic filio fratri suo Ravonia Maxuma sic annorum -XXXII Cornelia Publi filia Secunda annorum XII vivae fecerunt sibi et suis\n
EDCS-28400203.translation: Amera, daughter of Amera, for Septumius, her son, brother, Ravonia Maxuma, 32 years old, and Cornelia Publia's daughter Secunda, 12 years old, made this while alive for themselves and their families.
EDCS-28400203.summary: This funerary inscription records the commission of a tomb by Amera and Ravonia Maxuma for themselves and their deceased relatives, including a son and daughter.

EDCS-28400204.text_edition: \n Caio Iulio Quinti f(ilio) Hermae IVRI \n
EDCS-28400204.translation: To Gaius Julius Hermes, son of Quintus.
EDCS-28400204.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription likely dedicating a monument or tomb to Gaius Julius Hermes.

EDCS-28400205.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription beginning with the name Marcus Julius.

EDCS-28400206.text_edition: \n Marco Lollio Marci filio Paterno mensium II dierum IIII hic situs Callistus\n
EDCS-28400206.translation: Here lies Marcus Loll Thus, son of Marcus, aged 2 months and 4 days, Callistus.
EDCS-28400206.summary: This funerary epitaph records the burial of Marcus Loll Thus, an infant, and is noted by Callistus.

EDCS-28400207.text_edition: \n Publio Munnio Quieto patri ex testamento Cai Munni Quieti fili Lucius Pomponius Pudens\n
EDCS-28400207.translation: Lucius Pomponius Pudens for his father Publius Munnus Quietus, according to the will of Gaius Munnius Quietus.
EDCS-28400207.summary: This inscription records Lucius Pomponius Pudens fulfilling a bequest in the will of Gaius Munnius Quietus to honor his father, Publius Munnus Quietus.

EDCS-28400208.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publiciae Maximae Maximinus feminae obsequentissimae et sibi vixit annos -XVI menses VI\n
EDCS-28400208.translation: To the divine spirits of Publicia Maxima, the most obedient woman, who lived 16 years and 6 months; Maximinus also for himself.
EDCS-28400208.summary: This funerary epitaph is dedicated to Publicia Maxima, who died at 16 years and 6 months, and was commissioned by Maximinus for himself as well.

EDCS-28400209.text_edition: \n Lucius Roscius Luci filius Sergia Genialis F S\n
EDCS-28400209.translation: Lucius Roscius Genialis, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, made this.
EDCS-28400209.summary: A funerary monument set up by Lucius Roscius Genialis, son of Lucius, identifying his tribal affiliation and stating he made it.

EDCS-28400210.text_edition: \n Sabinius Titi filius Lesbius vivo se posuit libertis libertabusque suis omnibus\n
EDCS-28400210.translation: Sabinius Lesbius, son of Titus, set this up for himself while alive, and for all his freedmen and freedwomen.
EDCS-28400210.summary: A funerary monument erected by Sabinius Lesbius for himself and his freed dependents, indicating it was made during his lifetime.

EDCS-28400211.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tettenio Euangelo coniugi carissimo Stratonia et sibi viva posuit\n
EDCS-28400211.translation: To the departed spirits. Stratonia set this up for her dearest husband Tettenius Euangelus and for herself while alive.
EDCS-28400211.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Tettenius Euangelus by his wife Stratonia, who also prepared it for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-28400212.text_edition: \n Valeria Titi filia Secunda viva fecit sibi et Marco Iulio Titi filio Sergia Asprenati viro et Lucio Iulio Titi filio Sergia Macro et Iuliae Marci filiae Maximillae filiae et Lucio Iulio \n
EDCS-28400212.translation: Valeria Secunda, daughter of Titus, made this while alive for herself, and for her husband Marcus Iulius Macro, son of Titus and Asprenas, and for her daughter Iulia Maximilla, daughter of Lucius Iulius.
EDCS-28400212.summary: A funerary monument established by Valeria Secunda for herself and various family members, including her husband and children.

EDCS-28400213.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Vettio Maritimo Publi filio Annia \n
EDCS-28400213.translation: To the departed spirits. For Publius Vettius Maritimus, son of Publius, of the Annia tribe.
EDCS-28400213.summary: A funerary inscription honouring Publius Vettius Maritimus, son of Publius, identifying his tribal affiliation.

EDCS-28400214.text_edition: \n Vettia Cai filia Rufa viva fecit sibi et Caio Vettio Rufo patri et Iuliae Maximae matri et suis in fronte pedes XV in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-28400214.translation: Vettia Rufa, daughter of Caius, made this while alive for herself, and for her father Caius Vettius Rufus, and for her mother Iulia Maxima, and for her family; 15 feet in front and 20 feet in depth.
EDCS-28400214.summary: A funerary monument erected by Vettia Rufa for herself and her parents, specifying the dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-28400215.text_edition: \n Manibus gentis suae libertis libertabusque ossibus ponendis Vinediae Cai filiae Iucundae uxori hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur in fronte pedes XII in agro pedes XVI\n
EDCS-28400215.translation: For the departed spirits of his family, for his freedmen and freedwomen. This monument for Vinedia Iucunda, daughter of Caius, his wife, will not pass to an heir; 12 feet in front and 16 feet in depth.
EDCS-28400215.summary: A funerary monument intended for the departed spirits of a family, including freedpersons, specifically for the wife Vinedia Iucunda, with specified dimensions and a stipulation against inheritance.

EDCS-28400216.text_edition: \n a Quinti filia sibi et filio Rufo filio d Cai filiae Verae socero cundae Secundae\n
EDCS-28400216.translation: daughter of Quintus, for herself and her son Rufus, son of [---]; for her daughter Vera; for her father-in-law Cundus Secundus.
EDCS-28400216.summary: A funerary monument set up for multiple individuals, including the dedicator (a daughter of Quintus), her son, her daughter, and her father-in-law.

EDCS-28400218.text_edition: \n Aulo Caecinae Aulo Caecin<ae=O> Auli <f=P>(ilio) parenti \n
EDCS-28400218.translation: For Aulus Caecina, son of Aulus Caecina, as a parent.
EDCS-28400218.summary: A funerary inscription dedicating a monument to Aulus Caecina, son of Aulus Caecina, who is identified as a parent.

EDCS-28400219.text_edition: \n Fermus tribunus et Surnia soror eius et Dacia coniuge sic eius\n
EDCS-28400219.translation: Fermus the tribune, and his sister Surnia, and his wife Dacia.
EDCS-28400219.summary: A funerary monument established by Fermus, identified as a tribune, along with his sister Surnia and his wife Dacia.

EDCS-28400220.text_edition: \n quinque viginti quinque Senea Romia mater filio pientissimo vivens fecit\n
EDCS-28400220.translation: Senea Romia, mother, made this for her most dutiful son while she was alive.
EDCS-28400220.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by a mother for her son.

EDCS-28400221.translation: To the gods of the departed, for Marcus Aurelius Diatratus.
EDCS-28400221.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Aurelius Diatratus.

EDCS-28400222.translation: Actia Eutichia, in accordance with Actia.
EDCS-28400222.summary: This is a funerary inscription, possibly for Actia Eutichia, referencing Actia.

EDCS-28400223.text_edition: \n VNESIC centuria Octavi F Volso et F Octavio - filio Balbo \n
EDCS-28400223.translation: From the century of Octavius F. Volso and F. Octavius, to the son Balbus.
EDCS-28400223.summary: This inscription likely marks a dedication or memorial from a centuria to a son named Balbus.

EDCS-28400224.text_edition: \n Paula Lucii filia annorum septem Iulia Caii filia vivens \n
EDCS-28400224.translation: Paula, daughter of Lucius, aged seven; Julia, daughter of Gaius, alive.
EDCS-28400224.summary: This inscription records two young girls, Paula and Julia, possibly commemorating their lives or deaths.

EDCS-28400225.text_edition: \n Iano Augusto sacrum Cinius Genialis pro salute ordinis sui et civium suorum simulacrum <e=T>i reformavit a<t=D>que restitui<t=D> \n
EDCS-28400225.translation: Sacred to Janus Augustus. Cinius Genialis restored the statue and had it replaced for the well-being of his board and his fellow citizens.
EDCS-28400225.summary: This is a dedication to Janus Augustus, recording Cinius Genialis' restoration of a statue for the welfare of his colleagues and citizens.

EDCS-28400226.text_edition: \n Neptuno Dianae propter maiestatem Lucius Cincius Trophimus ex viso posuit quod Halis vidit\n
EDCS-28400226.translation: Lucius Cincius Trophimus set up this statue to Neptune and Diana, because of their majesty, after seeing it in a vision at Halis.
EDCS-28400226.summary: This inscription records Lucius Cincius Trophimus dedicating a statue to Neptune and Diana based on a vision.

EDCS-28400227.text_edition: \n Veneri Augustae sacrum Egnatia Cai filia Paullina solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28400227.translation: Sacred to Venus Augusta. Egnatia, daughter of Caius, Paullina, willingly paid her vow as deserved.
EDCS-28400227.summary: This is a dedication to Venus Augusta, where Egnatia Paullina willingly fulfilled a vow.

EDCS-28400228.text_edition: \n Tiberius Caesar divi Augusti filius Augustus pontifex maximus imperator tribunicia potestate XVIII consul designatus tertius\n
EDCS-28400228.translation: Tiberius Caesar, son of the deified Augustus, Augustus, chief priest, emperor, holding tribunician power for the 18th time, designated consul for the third time.
EDCS-28400228.summary: This is a formal titulature of Emperor Tiberius, listing his honors and powers.

EDCS-28400229.text_edition: \n Publio Silio Publi filio proconsuli patrono decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28400229.translation: To Publius Silius, son of Publius, proconsul, patron, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400229.summary: This inscription records a public honor bestowed upon Publius Silius, proconsul and patron, by the local council.

EDCS-28400233.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Appuleio Publi filio Frontoni aedili IIviro quinquennali pontifici Saenia Maxima marito de se bene merito\n
EDCS-28400233.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld. To Appuleius Fronton, son of Publius, who held the office of aedile twice, quinquennial duovir, and pontiff, a monument from Saenia Maxima, his wife, for her husband who deserved well of her.
EDCS-28400233.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by a wife to her husband, who held several prominent local magistracies, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400234.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caio Tullio Ursioni IIIIIIviro Augustali Tullia Victorina marito optimo\n
EDCS-28400234.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld. To Gaius Tullius Ursio, a six-time Augustal duovir, a monument from Tullia Victorina, his wife, for her most excellent husband.
EDCS-28400234.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated by a wife to her husband, an Augustal duovir, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400235.text_edition: \n Lucius Barbius Crapus et Quintus Barbius Rufus sibi et suis vivi fecerunt in fronte pedes XXXV in agro pedes X\n
EDCS-28400235.translation: Lucius Barbius Crapus and Quintus Barbius Rufus made this for themselves and their families while alive. Frontage 35 feet, depth 10 feet.
EDCS-28400235.summary: A self-funded funerary monument built by two individuals for their families, specifying dimensions, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400236.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld, for Caecianus.
EDCS-28400236.summary: A very brief funerary inscription dedicated to an individual named Caecianus, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400237.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum i<n=M> memoriam Iuli Frontoni qui tulit annos XXXXVII Secundina coniugi bene merenti \n
EDCS-28400237.translation: Dedicated to the Spirits of the Underworld in memory of Iulius Fronton, who lived 47 years, a monument from Secundina, his wife, for her deserving husband.
EDCS-28400237.summary: A funerary inscription recording the age at death of Iulius Fronton and dedicated by his wife, Secundina, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400238.summary: A very brief inscription recording a name, possibly a funerary dedication or a name on a building, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400239.text_edition: \n Titus Oc<t=I>avius Capito vivus fecit sibi TAVIO Octavi- T filiae patri SERT II VI L O I S VII D M \n
EDCS-28400239.translation: Titus Octavius Capito made this for himself while alive, for his daughter Tavia, daughter of Octavius, father Sertorio VI L O I S VII D M.
EDCS-28400239.summary: A complex funerary inscription indicating a father building for himself and his daughter, with a partial and difficult to interpret dedication at the end, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400240.summary: An inscription recording the restoration of something by Lucius Septumius Frunitus, the nature of the object restored is unclear, likely from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400241.text_edition: \n Vetillius N Volsi Potisus VI L C\n
EDCS-28400241.translation: Vetillius Potisus, son of Nvolus, VI L C.
EDCS-28400241.summary: A fragmented inscription recording a name and filiation, with unclear abbreviations, likely part of a funerary or public record, from the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400242.text_edition: \n Millae uxori III et suis viv- fec--- in fronte pedes XX in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-28400242.translation: Milla made this for his wife and their family while alive. Frontage 20 feet, depth 20 feet.
EDCS-28400242.summary: A self-funded funerary monument built by Milla for his wife and family, specifying dimensions, likely dating to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-28400244.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription, likely for a woman named Iousa, with an incomplete dedication.

EDCS-28400245.translation: To his unfortunate wife, who deserved well.
EDCS-28400245.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a tombstone, dedicated by a husband to his deceased wife, praising her merit.

EDCS-28400248.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Anius Rufinus sibi et Gratulae uxori suae et Aelio Scorpioni filio defuncto annorum XXVIII vivus posuit\n
EDCS-28400248.translation: To the Divine Shades. Anius Rufinus, alive, erected this for himself, for his wife Gratula, and for his deceased son Aelius Scorpius, aged 28.
EDCS-28400248.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Anius Rufinus, who set up the monument for himself, his wife Gratula, and his son Aelius Scorpius, who died at 28.

EDCS-28400249.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Quinto annorum XXX veterano Flavia Q<u>ie<t=I>a V marito \n
EDCS-28400249.translation: To the Divine Shades. Flavia Quieta, his wife, set this up for Aurelius Quintus, a veteran aged 30.
EDCS-28400249.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Flavia Quieta for her husband, Aurelius Quintus, a 30-year-old veteran.

EDCS-28400250.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Herennia Rufina POIA SVVE annorum LXX\n
EDCS-28400250.translation: To the Divine Shades. Herennia Rufina, aged 70.
EDCS-28400250.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Herennia Rufina, who died at the age of 70.

EDCS-28400252.text_edition: \n D(is) Manibus IIVISEI sibi et Catae coniugi MAEVI posuerunt nnrum LXXVIII\n
EDCS-28400252.translation: To the Divine Shades. IIVISEI and Maeui, alive, erected this for themselves, for their wife Cata, aged 78.
EDCS-28400252.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by two individuals, IIVISEI and Maeui, for themselves and their wife Cata, who died at 78.

EDCS-28400253.text_edition: \n Publius Aelius vivus sibi et Aeliae Iuliae nnorum XLV uxori pientissimae posuit\n
EDCS-28400253.translation: Publius Aelius, alive, erected this for himself and for his most devoted wife Aelia Julia, aged 45.
EDCS-28400253.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Publius Aelius for himself and his devoted wife Aelia Julia, who died at 45.

EDCS-28400255.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Priscus annorum LX vivus fecit sibi et Aeliae Mellitoni uxori pientissimae annorum LX et Aelio Frontoni filio et suis\n
EDCS-28400255.translation: To the Divine Shades. Publius Aelius Priscus, aged 60, made this while alive for himself, for his most devoted wife Aelia Mellitona, aged 60, and for his son Aelius Fronto, and for his family.
EDCS-28400255.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Publius Aelius Priscus for himself, his wife Aelia Mellitona, his son Aelius Fronto, and his family.

EDCS-28400256.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vit Stennas sibi et Stennatoni coniugi quae vixit annos LX vivi posuerunt\n
EDCS-28400256.translation: To the Divine Shades. Vit and Stennas, alive, erected this for themselves and for their spouse Stennatona, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-28400256.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Vit and Stennas for themselves and their spouse Stennatona, who lived to 60.

EDCS-28400257.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Andes et Cleus vivi posuerunt sibi annorum LXX Caius MON\n
EDCS-28400257.translation: To the Divine Shades. Andes and Cleus, alive, erected this for themselves, aged 70. Caius...
EDCS-28400257.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Andes and Cleus for themselves, both aged 70, with an incomplete additional mention of Caius.

EDCS-28400260.text_edition: \n Florentino plus quam delicias Verae moestae matris tuetur manenti corpus VS pater infelix a repositione monumenti vice inscribendum Vatque ipso CEPE COL Ursinus unquam sic Volcenius Nepos quattuorvir iure dicundo quinquennalis VC\n
EDCS-28400260.translation: A grieving father protects the body of Florentinus, more than Vera's beloved, and the monument erected by him. Ursinus, formerly a four-man magistrate with judicial power, quinquennial censor, also Volcenius Nepos, always.
EDCS-28400260.summary: This funerary inscription records the grief of a father and the dedication of a monument, mentioning local magistrates and likely dating to the early imperial period.

EDCS-28400261.text_edition: \n Titus Auius AV NI IT XXXX viv- fecit et I P P P M\n
EDCS-28400261.translation: Titus Auius, aged 40, made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-28400261.summary: This brief inscription records a man named Titus Auius who built something for himself and likely dates to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400262.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Nervae Augusto patri patriae consuli III decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28400262.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Nerva Augustus, father of the fatherland, consul for the third time, by decree of the town council.
EDCS-28400262.summary: This is an official dedication to Emperor Nerva, likely from a town council, and dates to his reign (96-98 AD).

EDCS-28400263.text_edition: \n Marco Aelio Aurelio Vero Caesari Imperatoris Titi Aeli Caesaris Hadriani Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti divi Nervae abnepoti consuli II decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28400263.translation: To Marcus Aelius Aurelius Verus Caesar, son of Emperor Titus Aelius Caesar Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, grandson of the deified Hadrian, great-grandson of the deified Trajan Parthicus, descendant of the deified Nerva, consul for the second time, by decree of the town council.
EDCS-28400263.summary: This is an official dedication to Marcus Aurelius (as Caesar), listing his imperial lineage and consulship, likely dating to his co-rule with Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-28400266.text_edition: \n Tiberius Claudius Domitius titulum IVS fecit sibi vivus FANO EINESVO CLVAIA et coniugi carissimae suae castae bene merentique quae vixit annos LXXIIII\n
EDCS-28400266.translation: Tiberius Claudius Domitius erected this monument while alive for himself, and for his most beloved, chaste, and deserving wife, who lived 74 years.
EDCS-28400266.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tiberius Claudius Domitius and his wife, dedicated by himself while alive, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400267.text_edition: \n Caio Iulio Rufino filio patri A\n
EDCS-28400267.translation: To Gaius Julius Rufus, son, father.
EDCS-28400267.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription that appears to be a dedication or memorial to a man named Gaius Julius Rufus.

EDCS-28400268.text_edition: \n Marcus Livius Lucius C R O\n
EDCS-28400268.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely just the name of an individual, Marcus Livius Lucius.

EDCS-28400271.text_edition: \n Iuliae Sexti filiae Paullae filiae Appuleiae Cai filiae Marcellae nepti Iulia Cai filia Tertia Toruca viva fecit\n
EDCS-28400271.translation: Iulia Tertia Toruca, daughter of Gaius, made this while alive for her daughter Iulia Paulla, daughter of Sextus, granddaughter of Appuleius, and Marcella.
EDCS-28400271.summary: This is a funerary inscription where a mother, Iulia Tertia Toruca, dedicated a monument to her deceased daughter and granddaughter.

EDCS-28400272.text_edition: \n Luci Aurelio Luci liberto Victori domo Aequo Augustali et Seniae sacerdoti primo corporis Augustalium ornato ornamenis decurionalibus plebs Seniensium aere conlato curante Aurelio Secundo\n
EDCS-28400272.translation: To Lucius Aurelius Victor, freedman of Lucius, an Augustalis from Aequum, and to Senia, priestess, first of the College of Augustales, with decurional honors, the plebs of the people of Senis contributed money, under the care of Aurelius Secundus.
EDCS-28400272.summary: This inscription records the honoring of a freedman and a priestess by the common people of the town of Senis with money and official honors, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400273.text_edition: \n Lucio Valerio Agathopo Augustali huic ordo Seniensium ornamenta decurionalia primo decrevit Valeria Montana coniugi optimo locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28400273.translation: To Lucius Valerius Agathopus, an Augustalis, to whom the order of the people of Senis first decreed decurional honors; a place was granted to Valeria Montana, his excellent wife, by decree of the town council.
EDCS-28400273.summary: This inscription records the bestowal of decurional honors upon an Augustalis and his wife by the town council of Senis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400274.text_edition: \n Flavia Procula Iusto coniugi suo Neapolitano s<e=F> viva titulum posuit \n
EDCS-28400274.translation: Flavia Procula erected this monument during her lifetime to her husband Iustus, a citizen of Neapolis.
EDCS-28400274.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her husband, dated by the mention of Neapolis, suggesting a likely 2nd or 3rd century CE date.

EDCS-28400276.text_edition: \n Marcus Aurelius Aurelianus ex <f=P>ru<m=V>(entario) beneficiarius consularis et Cnaeus SAV ROB CIVM D I M POBI M A MVNC FILI D \n
EDCS-28400276.translation: Marcus Aurelius Aurelianus, a *beneficiarius consularis* (an officer attached to a consular governor's staff), and Gnaeus SAV ROB CIVM D I M POBI M A MVNC FILI D.
EDCS-28400276.summary: Part of a dedication possibly made by a *beneficiarius consularis* and another individual, with the names and full context unclear due to damage, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28400277.text_edition: \n Numini maiestatique domini nostri Gordiani Augusti et Genio loci Aurelius Valerianus speculator legionis XI Claudiae referens gratiam votum solvit\n
EDCS-28400277.translation: Aurelius Valerianus, a *speculator* (military messenger/bodyguard) of the XI Claudia legion, fulfilled his vow to the divine majesty of our lord Gordian Augustus and the genius of the place, returning thanks.
EDCS-28400277.summary: A votive inscription dedicated by a soldier of the XI Claudia legion to the emperor Gordian and the local deity, indicating the reign of Gordian III (238-244 CE).

EDCS-28400278.text_edition: \n Corneliae Saloninae Augustae coniugi domini nostri Gallieni Augusti decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28400278.translation: To Cornelia Salonina Augusta, wife of our lord Gallienus Augustus, by decree of the town council.
EDCS-28400278.summary: A public dedication to Empress Cornelia Salonina, wife of Emperor Gallienus, ordered by the local senate, likely dating to Gallienus' reign (253-268 CE).

EDCS-28400279.summary: A fragmentary inscription indicating a dedication or memorial to two individuals named Aurelius Niger and Aurelia, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28400281.summary: A very fragmentary epitaph or dedication mentioning a woman named Mula and her father, ODI, with the original purpose and date unclear.

EDCS-28400282.text_edition: \n Sentonae Eutychus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28400282.translation: Sentona: Eutychus willingly fulfilled his vow for a just cause.
EDCS-28400282.summary: A votive inscription by Eutychus to Sentona, a deity or person, who willingly fulfilled a vow, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28400283.text_edition: \n Caio Livio Cai filio Sergia Clementi militi cohortis VIII praetoriae centuria Cai Marci Gemellini Livius Obsequens libertus vivus fecit\n
EDCS-28400283.translation: Livius Obsequens, freedman, erected this while alive for Gaius Livius Clementi, son of Gaius, a soldier of the VIII Praetorian cohort, in the century of Gaius Marcius Gemellinus.
EDCS-28400283.summary: A funerary monument erected by a freedman for a Praetorian guardsman, identified by the soldier's cohort and centurion, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28400284.text_edition: \n Caio Notario Vettidiano Secundo IIviro iure dicundo sacerdoti Augusti fratri Titus Vettidius Nepos testamento fieri iussit locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28400284.translation: Titus Vettidius Nepos ordered this to be made in his will for Gaius Notarius Vettidianus Secundus, *IIvir* (a high local magistrate) with judicial authority, priest of Augustus, and brother; the site was granted by decree of the town council.
EDCS-28400284.summary: A posthumous dedication by will for a prominent local magistrate and priest, ordered by his brother and approved by the town council, likely dating to the 1st-3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-28400285.text_edition: \n Vettidio Nepoti patri IIviro iure dicundo Titus Vettidius Nepos testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-28400285.translation: Titus Vettidius Nepos ordered this to be made in his will for his father, Vettidius Nepos, *IIvir* with judicial authority.
EDCS-28400285.summary: A posthumous dedication by will by Titus Vettidius Nepos for his father, a former *IIvir* (high local magistrate), likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28400287.text_edition: \n Marcus Vipsanus Marci libertus Faustus Icae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28400287.translation: Marcus Vipsanus Faustus, freedman of Marcus, gladly fulfilled his vow to Ica, as was deserved.
EDCS-28400287.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Marcus Vipsanus Faustus, a freedman, to a deity named Ica, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400288.text_edition: \n Iriae Augustae in memoriam Vibiae Portiae matris Aquilia Quinti filia Colatina donum dedit\n
EDCS-28400288.translation: Aquilia Colatina, daughter of Quintus, gave this offering to Augusta Iria in memory of her mother, Vibia Portia.
EDCS-28400288.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by Aquilia Colatina to Augusta Iria in memory of her mother, Vibia Portia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400289.text_edition: \n Iriae Veneri Caia Valeria Optati <f=E>(ilia) Felicula votum solvit libens merito \n
EDCS-28400289.translation: Caia Valeria Felicula, daughter of Optatus, gladly fulfilled her vow to Iria Venus, as was deserved.
EDCS-28400289.summary: This inscription records a vow fulfilled by Caia Valeria Felicula to Iria Venus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400290.translation: Lucius V. F. to Silvanus Augustus.
EDCS-28400290.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Silvanus Augustus by Lucius V. F., likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400291.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Nobilis, identified as a steward, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400292.text_edition: \n Acilia Luci filia Quincta viva fecit sibi et Sexto Aquillio viro suo heredi et Lucio Aquillio Sexti filio Claudia Longo filio decurioni\n
EDCS-28400292.translation: Acilia Quincta, daughter of Lucius, made this while alive for herself, for her husband Sextus Aquillius, her heir, and for her son Lucius Aquillius Rufus, son of Sextus, a decurion.
EDCS-28400292.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a self-funded dedication by Acilia Quincta for herself and her family, including her husband Sextus Aquillius and son Lucius Aquillius Rufus, a decurion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400293.text_edition: \n Sexto Aquillio Sexti filio Lucio Aquillio Sexti filio Rufo \n
EDCS-28400293.translation: To Sextus Aquillius, son of Sextus, and to Lucius Aquillius Rufus, son of Sextus.
EDCS-28400293.summary: This inscription appears to be an honorary or funerary dedication to Sextus Aquillius and his son Lucius Aquillius Rufus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400294.text_edition: \n Avita Suioca Vesclevesis filia viva fecit sibi et V Volsounae Suiocae Vesclevesis filiae Avita Aquillia Luci filia viva fecit sibi et Vol \n
EDCS-28400294.translation: Avita, daughter of Suioca Vesclevesis, made this while alive for herself and for her daughter Voolsouna, daughter of Suioca Vesclevesis; Avita Aquillia, daughter of Lucius, made this while alive for herself and for Vol-
EDCS-28400294.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a self-funded dedication by two women, Avita and Avita Aquillia, for themselves and their daughters, likely from the Roman Imperial period and possibly of Gallic origin given the names.

EDCS-28400295.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that preserves the name Caius Lanius, likely part of a larger dedication or funerary monument from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400296.summary: This inscription records the name Volso Graesimus, likely indicating the person responsible for an erection or dedication, probably from the Roman Imperial period and possibly of Gallic origin.

EDCS-28400297.text_edition: \n Flaviae Titiae coniugi incomparabili Flavius Felicissimus fecit\n
EDCS-28400297.translation: Flavius Felicissimus made this for his incomparable wife, Flavia Titia.
EDCS-28400297.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Flavius Felicissimus for his wife, Flavia Titia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400300.summary: This is a short memorial inscription for Lucius Thoris, probably Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400301.text_edition: \n uxori C Cai filia Maxuma Sex Cai filia CaP Turrania \n
EDCS-28400301.translation: To his wife, Maxuma, daughter of C. Cai, and to Ca[...?], daughter of Sex. Cai and Turrania.
EDCS-28400301.summary: This inscription records a dedication or memorial to two women, Maxuma and another individual, with complex patronymic and filiation details, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400302.text_edition: \n Libero Patri Augusto sacrum Lucius Volumnius Pudens votum solvit libens merito fecit D\n
EDCS-28400302.translation: Sacred to Liber Pater Augustus; Lucius Volumnius Pudens gladly fulfilled his vow, rightly making this.
EDCS-28400302.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Liber Pater Augustus by Lucius Volumnius Pudens, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400303.text_edition: \n Nymphis Augustis sacrum ex voto suscepto pro salute municipii balineo effecto Tiberius Gavillius Cai filius Claudia Lambicus aedilis IIvir posuit\n
EDCS-28400303.translation: Sacred to the August Nymphs, in fulfillment of a vow undertaken for the well-being of the municipality; Tiberius Gavillius, son of Caius, of the Claudia tribe, Lambicus, aedile and duovir, erected this bathhouse.
EDCS-28400303.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a bathhouse to the August Nymphs by Tiberius Gavillius, an aedile and duovir, for the welfare of the municipality, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400305.text_edition: \n Marco Iulio Severo Filippo sic nobilissimo Caesari nobilissimo principi iuventutis res publica Albonessium sic\n
EDCS-28400305.translation: To Marcus Julius Severus Philippus, most noble Caesar, most noble prince of youth, the state of Albonessium...
EDCS-28400305.summary: This is a dedication to Marcus Julius Severus Philippus, a Roman emperor, by the community of Albonessium, likely dating to his reign (mid-3rd century CE).

EDCS-28400308.text_edition: \n VIIII Lucius Veratius Luci filius Claudia Otho miles legionis XI annorum XX dedicavit\n
EDCS-28400308.translation: Lucius Veratius, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, Otho, a soldier of the 11th legion, aged 20, dedicated this.
EDCS-28400308.summary: This is a dedication by Lucius Veratius Otho, a 20-year-old soldier of the 11th legion, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400309.text_edition: \n us Hostiduces filius Volso hic LES in S<y=V>ria D Titius fratri fecit \n
EDCS-28400309.translation: Hostiduces, son of us [or Hostiduces, son of Hostius?], Volso, here in Syria; D. Titius made this for his brother.
EDCS-28400309.summary: This is a funerary dedication by D. Titius for his brother, Hostiduces Volso, likely a soldier serving in Syria during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400310.text_edition: \n Sexto Gavilio Sexti filio Claudia Germo aedili IIviro Gavilia Sexti filia Maxima filia fecit huic ob merita locus sepulturae datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28400310.translation: To Sextus Gavillius, son of Sextus, of the Claudia tribe, Germo, aedile and duovir; his daughter Gavilia Maxima, daughter of Sextus, made this for him; a burial plot was granted to him for his merits by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400310.summary: This inscription records a funerary dedication to Sextus Gavillius, an aedile and duovir, by his daughter, and notes that a burial plot was granted by decree, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400311.text_edition: \n Publio Gavillio Sexti filio Claudia Prisco aedili IIviro patri huic decreto decurionum <d>(atum) est funus publicum Taeliae Volsetis filiae Quartae matri Titiae Luci filiae Proculae uxori Publius Gavillius Publi filius Claudia Maximus aedilis IIvir fecit\n
EDCS-28400311.translation: To Publius Gavillius Priscus, son of Sextus, of the Claudia tribe, aedile and duovir, his father; a public funeral was granted to his mother, Taelia Quartilla, daughter of Volsetis, and to his wife, Procula, daughter of Lucius, by decree of the decurions; Publius Gavillius Maximus, son of Publius, of the Claudia tribe, aedile and duovir, made this.
EDCS-28400311.summary: This complex funerary inscription honors Publius Gavillius Priscus and his deceased family members, with a public funeral granted by decree of the decurions, likely Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400313.text_edition: \n ER AN CONSVI RAT DEIA TATM L MAR\n
EDCS-28400313.translation: The augurs consulted concerning the goddess...
EDCS-28400313.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription, likely recording a religious consultation by augurs concerning a deity, with an unknown date and context.

EDCS-28400314.text_edition: \n Vesclevesi Petronio Triti filio is in provincia defunctus est iturus\n
EDCS-28400314.translation: Vesclevesi to his son Petronius, died in the province while on his way...
EDCS-28400314.summary: This epitaph commemorates Petronius, son of Vesclevesi, who died in a province while traveling, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28400315.text_edition: \n Sextus Ceionius Voltimesis filius Claudia Loiscus annorum LXXX\n
EDCS-28400315.translation: Sextus Ceionius Voltimesis, son of Claudia Loiscus, aged 80.
EDCS-28400315.summary: This inscription records the name and age of Sextus Ceionius Voltimesis, identified by his father, and is likely part of a tombstone from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28400316.text_edition: \n Lucius Ceionius Luci libertus Claudia Priamus vivus fecit sibi et Tariae libertae Primigeniae uxori carissimae\n
EDCS-28400316.translation: Lucius Ceionius, freedman of Lucius, made this while alive for himself and for Taria Primigenia, his freedwoman wife, most beloved.
EDCS-28400316.summary: This funerary inscription records that Lucius Ceionius, a freedman, built his tomb for himself and his wife Taria Primigenia, a freedwoman, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28400317.text_edition: \n Locus sepulturae Sexti Gavilli Titi fili in fronte pedes XXV in agro pedes XXX\n
EDCS-28400317.translation: Burial place of Sextus Gavillius, son of Titus, 25 feet in front, 30 feet in the field.
EDCS-28400317.summary: This inscription describes the dimensions of a burial plot belonging to Sextus Gavillius, son of Titus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28400318.text_edition: \n Lucius Vibius Luci libertus Crescens Aur- V \n
EDCS-28400318.translation: Lucius Vibius Crescens, freedman of Lucius...
EDCS-28400318.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription identifying Lucius Vibius Crescens, a freedman of Lucius, possibly part of a tombstone or dedication, with an unknown date.

EDCS-28400319.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Volumniae Luci filiae Proculae miseri parentes qui in spem tollunt filias si spes decepit cum viderunt mortuas\n
EDCS-28400319.translation: To the spirits of the departed, miserable parents for their daughter Volumnia Procula, daughter of Lucius, who raise daughters in hope, if hope deceived them when they saw them dead.
EDCS-28400319.summary: This funerary inscription is a lament by parents for their deceased daughter Volumnia Procula, expressing their grief and dashed hopes, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28400320.text_edition: \n Dicanilla Maritimo coniugi pientissimo et Coelia Maritimo patri dulcissimo et Coeli \n
EDCS-28400320.translation: To Dicanilla for her most dutiful husband Maritimus, and to Coelia for her dearest father Maritimus, and to Coelius...
EDCS-28400320.summary: This funerary inscription honors Maritimus, husband of Dicanilla and father of Coelia, also possibly mentioning a Coelius, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28400321.text_edition: \n Libero Patri Torclesi fecit P Saturninus ex voto pro P Clemente filio suo ex suo cum suis\n
EDCS-28400321.translation: P. Saturninus made this for Father Liber Torclesi, fulfilling a vow for his son P. Clemens, with his own property and his own people.
EDCS-28400321.summary: This inscription records a votive offering made by P. Saturninus to the god Liber Torclesi for his son P. Clemens, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28400322.text_edition: \n Signia Ursa Signi Symphori filia templum Veneri Pelagiae a solo fecit et signum ipsius deae posuit sacerdote Lucio Cornificio Secundo Kalendis Maiis\n
EDCS-28400322.translation: Signia Ursa, daughter of Signus Symphorus, built the temple to Venus Pelagia from the ground up and placed the statue of the goddess herself, with Lucius Cornificius Secundus as priest, on the Kalends of May.
EDCS-28400322.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a temple and statue to Venus Pelagia by Signia Ursa, daughter of Signus Symphorus, with Lucius Cornificius Secundus as priest, on May 1st, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-28400323.translation: Rufinus, freedman of Lucius, [member of the college of] the six(?) [priests...].
EDCS-28400323.summary: This inscription, likely from a tomb or a public monument, records Rufinus, a freedman, and his affiliation with a priestly college, dating perhaps to the Imperial period.

EDCS-28400324.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Coelia Crescentino patri pientissimo filia cum matre in memoriam\n
EDCS-28400324.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Coelia placed [this] in memory of her most dutiful father, Crescentinus, along with [her] mother.
EDCS-28400324.summary: This funerary inscription records Coelia setting up a memorial for her pious father, Crescentinus, with her mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400325.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Pardalati posuit Queta quonserva sic qui vicxit sic annos XL\n
EDCS-28400325.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Queta placed [this] for Pardalatus, who lived thus for 40 years.
EDCS-28400325.summary: This funerary inscription records Queta dedicating a monument to Pardalatus, who lived for 40 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400326.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Urbana -- fratri su<o=Q> Martiali bene merenti posuit \n
EDCS-28400326.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulia Urbana placed [this] for her brother Martialis, who deserved well.
EDCS-28400326.summary: This funerary inscription records Iulia Urbana dedicating a monument to her deserving brother, Martialis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400327.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Ursinus et Eutychia parentes infelices Ursino filio pientissimo posuerunt annorum XXV\n
EDCS-28400327.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Ursinus and Eutychia, unhappy parents, placed [this] for their most dutiful son Ursinus, aged 25.
EDCS-28400327.summary: This funerary inscription records the grieving parents Ursinus and Eutychia dedicating a monument to their pious son Ursinus, who died at age 25, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400329.summary: This fragmented inscription likely records names and possibly a dedication or funerary notice, with the date uninferable due to significant damage.

EDCS-28400330.text_edition: \n Quintus Blassius Firmus Dianae conservatrici posuit libens merito\n
EDCS-28400330.translation: Quintus Blassius Firmus willingly erected [this] for Diana the preserver, as he deserved.
EDCS-28400330.summary: This dedication records Quintus Blassius Firmus erecting a monument to the goddess Diana Conservatrix, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400331.text_edition: \n Sacerdos Quintus Baronius Quinti filius templum et aras Iovi Herculi de <s=I>(ua pecunia facienda curavit \n
EDCS-28400331.translation: The priest Quintus Baronius, son of Quintus, had the temple and altars made for Jupiter and Hercules with his own money.
EDCS-28400331.summary: This inscription records the priest Quintus Baronius commissioning the construction of a temple and altars for Jupiter and Hercules using his own funds, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400334.text_edition: \n Lucius Serrvilius Publi filius Sergia Dalmasius S F --\n
EDCS-28400334.translation: Lucius Serrvilius, son of Publius, Sergia Dalmasius, daughter of S(extus)...
EDCS-28400334.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary or commemorative inscription, likely recording names and parentage, with the date uninferable due to its incompleteness.

EDCS-28400336.text_edition: \n Caius Fadius Cai Fadi Cerdonis libertus Epictetus vivos sic fecit sibi et patrono et suis omnibus\n
EDCS-28400336.translation: Caius Fadius Epictetus, freedman of Caius Fadius Cerdon, made [this] while alive for himself, his patron, and all his family.
EDCS-28400336.summary: This funerary inscription records Caius Fadius Epictetus, a freedman, dedicating a monument for himself, his patron, and his household, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400337.summary: This is a dedication to Numonia Maxima by her daughters.

EDCS-28400338.text_edition: \n Gaius Octavius Marcus Octavius Marcus Octavius Gaius Octavius N et fratribus faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-28400338.translation: Gaius Octavius, Marcus Octavius, Marcus Octavius, and Gaius Octavius, along with their brothers, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-28400338.summary: This records the construction of a monument arranged by Gaius Octavius, Marcus Octavius, Marcus Octavius, and Gaius Octavius, and their brothers.

EDCS-28400339.text_edition: \n Clodiae Severae Clodius Gallicanus et Vipsana Surilla filiae locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28400339.translation: To Clodia Severa, Clodius Gallicanus and Vipsana Surilla granted a plot of land for their daughter by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400339.summary: This inscription records the granting of a burial plot by decree of the town council to Clodia Severa, provided by Clodius Gallicanus and Vipsana Surilla.

EDCS-28400340.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caetenniae Hermioni Caetennius V patronus posuit\n
EDCS-28400340.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Caetennius V erected this for Caetennia Hermione, his patron.
EDCS-28400340.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Caetennia Hermione by her patron, Caetennius V.

EDCS-28400341.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Flavio Vementi annorum XXV bene merenti liberta posuit\n
EDCS-28400341.translation: To the Divine Spirits. His freedwoman erected this for Lucius Flavius Vementus, aged 25, who deserved well.
EDCS-28400341.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Lucius Flavius Vementus by his freedwoman.

EDCS-28400342.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gloriam sic annorum V Ursu<l=I>a pientissima <ma>ter posuit \n
EDCS-28400342.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gloria, aged 5. Urusula, most devoted mother, erected this.
EDCS-28400342.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Gloria, aged 5, by her devoted mother, Urusula.

EDCS-28400343.text_edition: \n Dibus Manibus Lucius Statius Marcellus Lucio Statio Marcellino patri piientissimo Laelia Chrisis sic Lucio Statio Marcellino coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-28400343.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Statius Marcellus for his most devoted father, Lucius Statius Marcellinus; and for his well-deserving spouse, Laelia Chrisis.
EDCS-28400343.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Lucius Statius Marcellus to his father, Lucius Statius Marcellinus, and his spouse, Laelia Chrisis.

EDCS-28400344.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titiae Nevillae defunctae nnorum L Flavii Florentini filius et pater matri dulcissimae et coniugi posuerunt bene merenti\n
EDCS-28400344.translation: To the Divine Spirits. The son of the Flavii Florentini and their father erected this for their most sweet mother and spouse, Titiis Nevilla, who deserved well.
EDCS-28400344.summary: This is a funerary dedication by the son and father of Titiis Nevilla for her, noting her virtues.

EDCS-28400345.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Saloniae filiae annorum mater \n
EDCS-28400345.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Salonia, daughter, aged [---]. Her mother erected this.
EDCS-28400345.summary: This is a funerary dedication to Salonia by her mother.

EDCS-28400346.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto sacrum Sal<v=N>ius Obultronius Dexter \n
EDCS-28400346.translation: Sacred to Hercules Augustus. Salvius Obultronius Dexter dedicated this.
EDCS-28400346.summary: This is a dedication to the god Hercules Augustus by Salvius Obultronius Dexter.

EDCS-28400347.text_edition: \n Vesellia sic Felicetas sic Libero Magno Patri Torculensi ex voto\n
EDCS-28400347.translation: Vesellia, called Felicetas, to Liber Magnus Pater Torculensis, according to her vow.
EDCS-28400347.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated by Vesellia Felicetas to the god Liber Pater, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400348.text_edition: \n Libero Patri Aristio votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-28400348.translation: Aristio gladly fulfilled his vow to Liber Pater.
EDCS-28400348.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aristio to Liber Pater, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400349.translation: Julius Bubalus to Sol Invictus Mithras.
EDCS-28400349.summary: This is a dedication to the syncretic deity Sol Invictus Mithras by Julius Bubalus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400350.text_edition: \n Nymphis sacrum Quintus Silvius Speratus centurio cohortis I Belgarum curagens theatri donum dedit\n
EDCS-28400350.translation: Quintus Silvius Speratus, a centurion of the 1st Cohort of Belgae, acting as curator, gave this gift of the theater to the Nymphs.
EDCS-28400350.summary: This inscription records a donation to the Nymphs by Quintus Silvius Speratus, a centurion, likely from the Roman Imperial period, related to a theater.

EDCS-28400351.text_edition: \n TRO ?A3 G3 S3 Quintus Corellius Threptus locum constituit\n
EDCS-28400351.translation: Quintus Corellius Threptus established the place.
EDCS-28400351.summary: This inscription records the establishment of a place by Quintus Corellius Threptus, with an unclear dedication, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400353.text_edition: \n Arriae Florae matri Arriae Tertullae sorori\n
EDCS-28400353.translation: To Arria Flora, my mother; to Arria Tertulla, my sister.
EDCS-28400353.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating two women, Arria Flora (mother) and Arria Tertulla (sister), likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400354.text_edition: \n Ianuario Ianuari liberto filio ann(orum) XL\n
EDCS-28400354.translation: To Ianuarius, son and freedman of Ianuarius, aged 40.
EDCS-28400354.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ianuarius, son and freedman of another Ianuarius, who died at the age of 40, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400356.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureli Nigellionis nnorum XXXX Petilia Secunda bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-28400356.translation: To the divine shades: Petilia Secunda placed this monument for Aurelius Nigellionis, deserving of remembrance, aged 40.
EDCS-28400356.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Petilia Secunda for her deserving husband Aurelius Nigellionis, who died at age 40, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400357.text_edition: \n Pomponio Serapioni carissimo Lucilia Aufidia mater bene merenti L posuit\n
EDCS-28400357.translation: Lucilia Aufidia, my dearest mother, placed this monument for Pomponius Serapio, deserving of remembrance, aged 50.
EDCS-28400357.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Lucilia Aufidia for her dear son Pomponius Serapio, who died at age 50, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400359.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Victor Messillae conpari infelici quae vicxit sic annos plus <m=N>(inus) XXX titulum posuit \n
EDCS-28400359.translation: To the divine shades: Aurelius Victor placed this monument for his unfortunate partner in life, Messilla, who lived more than 30 years.
EDCS-28400359.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Aurelius Victor for his unfortunate wife Messilla, who lived over 30 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400361.text_edition: \n D D infelicissimis infantibus <H=ET>ilarioni et Revocatae fratribus qui vixerunt puella annos plus minus VIII puer annos plus minus IIII qui in sic se sic i<n=IV> mare perierunt Maximus pater posuit \n
EDCS-28400361.translation: To the most unfortunate infants Ilarion and Revocata, brother and sister, who lived, the girl for about 8 years, the boy for about 4 years, and who perished in the sea in this way. Maximus, their father, erected this.
EDCS-28400361.summary: A funerary epitaph for two young siblings, Ilarion and Revocata, who drowned, erected by their father Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400363.text_edition: \n Aurelius Kamines natione Armenius qui vixit annos XXVIII\n
EDCS-28400363.translation: Aurelius Kamines, by birth an Armenian, who lived 28 years.
EDCS-28400363.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name, ethnic origin, and age at death of Aurelius Kamines, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400365.text_edition: \n Marcus Octavius Pullus hic situs est\n
EDCS-28400365.translation: Marcus Octavius Pullus is laid here.
EDCS-28400365.summary: A simple tomb inscription for Marcus Octavius Pullus, likely from the Roman Republican or early Imperial period.

EDCS-28400366.text_edition: \n Helviae Saturninae coniugi Publius Titienus Nepos\n
EDCS-28400366.translation: To Helvia Saturnina, his wife, Publius Titienus Nepos.
EDCS-28400366.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Publius Titienus Nepos to his wife Helvia Saturnina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400367.text_edition: \n Divo Augusto sacrum Lucius Quinctius Cai filius Gallus testamento poni iussit\n
EDCS-28400367.translation: Sacred to the Divine Augustus. Lucius Quinctius Gallus, son of Caius, ordered this to be set up by his will.
EDCS-28400367.summary: A dedication to the deified Augustus, commissioned by Lucius Quinctius Gallus through his will, likely from the early Imperial period.

EDCS-28400368.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Sextus Quinctilius Quinti filius Seneca veteranus cohortis IIII praetoriae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-28400368.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Sextus Quinctilius Seneca, son of Quinctilius, a veteran of the IV Praetorian cohort, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-28400368.summary: A votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Sextus Quinctilius Seneca, a veteran of the Praetorian Guard, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400369.text_edition: \n Matri Deum porticus i<n=M> memoriam su<o=P><r=E>um Titus Prus<i=T>us Titi filius Se<r=D>(gia) Optatus testamento poni iussit <p=B>e<r=T> Babria<m=N> Titi filiam Tertullam sororem heredemque \n
EDCS-28400369.translation: To the Mother of the Gods, Titus Prusius Optatus, son of Titus, of the tribe Sergia, ordered this portico to be set up by his will, to his own memory, with Babria Tertulla, daughter of Titus, his sister, as his heir.
EDCS-28400369.summary: A funerary monument to the Mother of the Gods, commissioned by Titus Prusius Optatus through his will, with his sister Babria Tertulla as heir, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400370.text_edition: \n Nymphis Augustis sacrum Caius Raecius Leo aquam quam nullus antiquorum in civitate fuisse meminerit inventam impendio ex volu<nt=TN>(ate) Cai Raeci Rufi clarissimi viri patroni sui dedicavit Severo et Pompeiano II consulibus VI Idus Novembres \n
EDCS-28400370.translation: Sacred to the August Nymphs. Gaius Raecius Leo dedicated this water, which no one before him in the city remembered to have existed, using funds from the generosity of Gaius Raecius Rufus, his most distinguished patron, Severus and Pompeianus being consuls, on the sixth day before the Ides of November.
EDCS-28400370.summary: A dedication of a newly discovered water source to the Nymphs by Gaius Raecius Leo, honoring his patron Gaius Raecius Rufus, dated to 207 AD.

EDCS-28400372.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Antonini filio divi Hadriani nepoti divi Traiani Parthici pronepoti divi Nervae abnepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Armeniaco tribunicia potestate XVIIII consuli III imperatori III decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28400372.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the Divine Antoninus, grandson of the Divine Hadrian, great-grandson of the Divine Trajan the Parthian, great-great-grandson of the Divine Nerva, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Armeniacus, holding tribunician power for the 19th time, consul for the 3rd time, Emperor for the 3rd time, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400372.summary: A dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus (Caracalla), honoring his imperial titles and dignities, enacted by local councilors (decurions), likely dating to 212-217 AD.

EDCS-28400373.text_edition: \n Iuliae Dominae Augustae matri castrorum decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28400373.translation: To Julia Domna, Augusta, mother of the camps, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400373.summary: A dedication to Julia Domna, mother of the camps, by local councilors (decurions), likely dating to the reign of Caracalla (193-217 AD).

EDCS-28400374.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Luci Septimi Severi Pii Pertinacis Augusti filio Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto Parthico \n
EDCS-28400374.translation: To Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax Augustus, son of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus Parthicus.
EDCS-28400374.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Caracalla (Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus), son of Septimius Severus, likely part of a larger monument.

EDCS-28400375.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Severo Alexandro Augusto divi Magni Antonini filio divi Severi nepoti pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate consuli III patri patriae decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28400375.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Severus Alexander Augustus, son of the deified Magnus Antoninus, grandson of the deified Severus, pontifex maximus, holding tribunician power, consul for the third time, father of the fatherland, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400375.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Emperor Severus Alexander by the town council, highlighting his titles and status.

EDCS-28400376.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Caio Vibio Treboniano Gallo Pio Felici Augusto decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28400376.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Vibius Trebonianus Gallus Pius Felix Augustus, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400376.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Trebonianus Gallus, decreed by the local town council.

EDCS-28400378.text_edition: \n Magia Lugulla nnum XVIIII hic sita est\n
EDCS-28400378.translation: Magia Lugulla, aged 19, is buried here.
EDCS-28400378.summary: This is a tombstone inscription recording the burial of Magia Lugulla, who died at the age of 19.

EDCS-28400379.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Remmiae Luci filiae Maximae anniorum XXVII mensium XI dierum XXVII mater infelicissima\n
EDCS-28400379.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Remmia Maxima, daughter of Lucius, aged 27 years, 11 months, and 27 days. Her most unhappy mother [dedicated this].
EDCS-28400379.summary: This is a tombstone inscription dedicated to Remmia Maxima by her grieving mother, detailing her age.

EDCS-28400380.text_edition: \n ILII XI urbanae tribuno cohortis VI praetoriae et protectori Auggustorum nnnostrum patroni splendidissimae civitatis Curictarum ob insignem eius benevolentiam statuam poni sanxerunt feliciter\n
EDCS-28400380.translation: The patrons of the most splendid city of the Curictae decreed that a statue be set up for [him], a tribune of the sixth praetorian cohort and protector of our Emperors, because of his outstanding benevolence. With good fortune.
EDCS-28400380.summary: This inscription records the decree by the patrons of Curictae to erect a statue for a praetorian tribune in recognition of his benevolence.

EDCS-28400381.text_edition: \n Lucius Trebius Paulus veteranus legionis VIII Augustae testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-28400381.translation: Lucius Trebius Paulus, veteran of the Eighth Augustan Legion, ordered this to be made in his will.
EDCS-28400381.summary: This inscription states that Lucius Trebius Paulus, a veteran of the Eighth Legion, willed this monument to be constructed.

EDCS-28400382.text_edition: \n Publius Pitius Publi filius Marullus decurionum decreto publice elatus sepultusque est\n
EDCS-28400382.translation: Publius Pitius Marullus, son of Publius, was publicly honored and buried by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400382.summary: This inscription records that Publius Pitius Marullus was granted a public funeral and burial by a decree of the town council.

EDCS-28400383.text_edition: \n Raeciae Cai filiae Pollae decurionum decreto publice\n
EDCS-28400383.translation: To Raecia Polla, daughter of Caius, [honored] by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400383.summary: This incomplete inscription records that Raecia Polla was honored by decree of the decurions.

EDCS-28400385.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Oppiae Luci filiae Proclae Aurelia Publi filia Maxima matri pientissimae viva fecit\n
EDCS-28400385.translation: To Oppia Procla, daughter of Lucius. Aurelia Maxima, daughter of Publius, made [this] for her most devoted mother while alive.
EDCS-28400385.summary: This is a tombstone inscription where Aurelia Maxima dedicated a monument to her mother, Oppia Procla, during her lifetime.

EDCS-28400386.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Primulae Eutyches coniugi merenti\n
EDCS-28400386.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Eutyches to his deserving wife Prímula.
EDCS-28400386.summary: A funerary dedication by Eutyches to his wife Prímula, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-28400387.text_edition: \n Quinto Kapio Maximo Kapiae Kapiae Maximae Kapiae Postumae et \n
EDCS-28400387.translation: To Quintus Capio Maximus, from Capia Maxima, Capia Postuma, and...
EDCS-28400387.summary: A dedication to Quintus Capius Maximus, with the dedicants named as Capia Maxima and Capia Postuma, likely a private monument.

EDCS-28400388.text_edition: \n Turi fili- sibi et Volsoni viro is filio Turo patri Volsonis filiae Fatae matri us faciundum curavit\n
EDCS-28400388.translation: Turius, to himself and to his son Volsonius, to his father Turo, and to his mother Fata, daughter of Volsonius, arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-28400388.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Turius for himself and his family members, including his son Volsonius and parents Turo and Fata.

EDCS-28400389.text_edition: \n II Tullia Publi filia Maxima matri locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-28400389.translation: To Tullia Maxima, daughter of Publius, for her mother. The burial place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-28400389.summary: A record of a burial plot granted by local authorities to Tullia Maxima for her mother, indicating civic recognition.

EDCS-28400390.text_edition: \n Artificibus Minervae Babullia Sexti filia Maxima votum solvit\n
EDCS-28400390.translation: Babullia Maxima, daughter of Sextus, fulfilled her vow to Minerva, patron of artisans.
EDCS-28400390.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to Minerva by Babullia Maxima, likely honoring the goddess of crafts.

EDCS-28400391.text_edition: \n Lartiae Titi filiae Maximae Luci Magii Nei huic decreto decurionum ob merita patris funus publice datum est Magiae Luci filiae\n
EDCS-28400391.translation: To Lartia Maxima, daughter of Titus. A funeral with public honors was granted by decree of the decurions to Luci Magius Neius for his merits, and to his daughter Magia, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-28400391.summary: A decree by the decurions granting public funeral honors to Luci Magius Neius and his daughter Magia, recognizing his merits.

EDCS-28400392.text_edition: \n Valerio Oclatino aedili aedili IIviro quinquennali Valeria Procilla filia patri viva fecit\n
EDCS-28400392.translation: Valeria Procilla, daughter of Valerius Oclatinus, erected this for her father, who was aedile and twice duovir quinquennalis, while he was alive.
EDCS-28400392.summary: A dedication by Valeria Procilla to her father, Valerius Oclatinus, who held important local magistracies, erected during his lifetime.

EDCS-28400393.summary: A fragment of an inscription mentioning an aedile named Opius, likely from a public monument.

EDCS-28400394.text_edition: \n Caio Hostilio Luci filio Claudia Celeri Iulia Titi filia Procula mater\n
EDCS-28400394.translation: To Gaius Hostilius, son of Lucius. Claudia Celer and Iulia Procula, mother of Titus, [erected this].
EDCS-28400394.summary: A dedication to Gaius Hostilius, son of Lucius, likely erected by his wife Claudia Celer and mother Iulia Procula.

EDCS-28400395.text_edition: \n Sexti Iuli Agathopi Phoebus annorum VII te terra optestor leviter super ossa quiescas et tenerae aetati ne gravis esse velis\n
EDCS-28400395.translation: Phoebus, son of Sextus Julius Agathopus, seven years old. I swear by the earth, may you rest lightly upon the bones and not wish to be heavy on your tender age.
EDCS-28400395.summary: A child's epitaph for Phoebus, aged seven, expressing a wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-28400396.text_edition: \n Caio Lucretio Sexti filio annorum IX hic situs Catellia Titi filia Secunda fecit\n
EDCS-28400396.translation: To Gaius Lucretius, son of Sextus, aged nine years, buried here; Catellia Secunda, daughter of Titus, made this.
EDCS-28400396.summary: A funerary monument for Gaius Lucretius, a boy of nine, erected by Catellia Secunda, likely his mother or close relative.

EDCS-28400397.text_edition: \n Simpliciae Ianuariae filiae dulcissimae quae vixit annis XII <d=B>ies CCFII castissim a Simplicia amantissim \n
EDCS-28400397.translation: To Simplicia Ianuaria, dearest daughter, who lived 12 years and 211 days, most chaste; made by Simplicia, most loving.
EDCS-28400397.summary: A funerary epitaph for Simplicia Ianuaria, a girl who died at 12 years and 211 days, erected by her loving mother, also named Simplicia.

EDCS-28400398.text_edition: \n Lucius Sulpicius Publi filius Varus vivus fecit sibi et Mendae Opiae filiae matri suae et suis omnibus\n
EDCS-28400398.translation: Lucius Sulpicius Varus, son of Publius, made this while alive for himself, and for his mother Menda Opia, daughter of Opia, and for all his kin.
EDCS-28400398.summary: A dedication made by Lucius Sulpicius Varus for himself, his mother, and his family, indicating provision for family burial.

EDCS-28400399.text_edition: \n Veneriae Cnaei filiae Primae Crispus Hilari Sulpici fecit\n
EDCS-28400399.translation: Crispus Hilari Sulpici made this for Veneria Prima, daughter of Cnaeus.
EDCS-28400399.summary: A funerary dedication made by Crispus Hilari Sulpici for Veneria Prima, daughter of Cnaeus.

EDCS-28400400.text_edition: \n Felix haec visa est nascendi lege puella quot non est miseros tum sortita Lares sed legem fatis Parcae dixere cruentam primus natalis condat ut ossa sua cognomen pater huic fuerat natale daturus abstulit atra dies una cum corpore nomen\n
EDCS-28400400.translation: This girl seemed lucky by the law of birth, who did not receive wretched household gods; but the Fates decreed a cruel law, that her first birthday should bury her bones; her father had given her a family name, but the dark day took away her name together with her body.
EDCS-28400400.summary: A poetic epitaph lamenting the premature death of a girl on her first birthday, whose life was tragically cut short before she could fully receive her name and place in the family.

EDCS-28400401.text_edition: \n Sextus Iulius Cai filius Niger aedilis IIvir vivus fecit sibi et IuliETFR in fronte pedes a tergo pedes M\n
EDCS-28400401.translation: Sextus Iulius Niger, son of Caius, aedile, duovir, made this while alive for himself. In front X feet, behind X feet.
EDCS-28400401.summary: A self-dedicated monument by Sextus Iulius Niger, who held the offices of aedile and duovir, indicating the dimensions of the funerary plot.

EDCS-28500007.summary: A simple ownership mark or dedication indicating property belonging to Tiberius.

EDCS-28500008.summary: A simple ownership mark or dedication indicating property belonging to Tiberius.

EDCS-28500009.summary: A simple ownership mark or dedication indicating property belonging to Caesar.

EDCS-28500016.summary: A simple ownership mark or dedication indicating property belonging to Tiberius.

EDCS-28500044.summary: This inscription, likely from the reign of Nero, records a dedication related to a structure or place called Pansiona.

EDCS-28500050.summary: This inscription, likely from the reign of Claudius, records a dedication related to a structure or place called Pansiona.

EDCS-28500052.summary: This inscription, likely from the reign of Caligula, records a dedication related to a structure or place called Pansiona.

EDCS-28500058.summary: This inscription, likely from the reign of Caligula, records a dedication related to a structure or place called Pansiona.

EDCS-28500064.summary: This inscription, likely from the reign of Vespasian, records a dedication related to a structure or place called Pansiona.

EDCS-28500424.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Antonini Pii filio divi Hadriani nepoti Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto \n
EDCS-28500424.translation: To the Emperor Caesar, son of the divine Antoninus Pius, grandson of the divine Hadrian, Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus.
EDCS-28500424.summary: This is a titulature inscription for Emperor Marcus Aurelius, dating to his reign (AD 161-180).

EDCS-28600106.text_edition: \n Tiberio Caesare Augusti filio Augusto pontifici maximo Caius Aemilius Volsoni filius Oclatinus Lucius Fonteius Quinti filius Rufus IIviri porticum curiam decreto decurionum faciundum curavere idemque probavere\n
EDCS-28600106.translation: Gaius Aemilius Volsoni son Oclatinus and Lucius Fonteius Quintus son Rufus, the two officials, by decree of the decurions, saw to the construction of the portico and curia and also approved it; Tiberius Caesar Augustus' son Augustus, chief priest.
EDCS-28600106.summary: This inscription records the construction of a portico and curia in a municipium by two local magistrates, likely in the early imperial period, and includes the titles of Emperor Augustus and a Pontifex Maximus.

EDCS-28600107.text_edition: \n Quintus Fonteius Raeci filius vivus fecit sibi et Volsounae Oplicae Plaetoris filiae coniugi suae\n
EDCS-28600107.translation: Quintus Fonteius Raeci's son, while alive, made this for himself and for Volsouna Oplicae, Plaetoris' daughter, his wife.
EDCS-28600107.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Quintus Fonteius Raeci's son for himself and his wife, likely dating to the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-28600108.text_edition: \n Laeliae Pyramidi contubernali suae et Laeli? \n
EDCS-28600108.translation: To Laelia, his companion in the barracks, and to Laelius?
EDCS-28600108.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary or commemorative, to a woman named Laelia and possibly a Laelius, possibly from a soldier.

EDCS-28600109.text_edition: \n Quintus Nigidius Turi filius vivus fecit sibi et Avitae Nigidiae Volsouni filiae uxori\n
EDCS-28600109.translation: Quintus Nigidius Turi's son, while alive, made this for himself and for Avita Nigidia, Volsoun's daughter, his wife.
EDCS-28600109.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Quintus Nigidius Turi's son for himself and his wife, likely dating to the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-28600110.translation: To Lucius, son of Lucius, and to Paula.
EDCS-28600110.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius and Paula, likely a family monument.

EDCS-28600111.text_edition: \n Primitivo filio nnorum XXVI mensium V Priscilla et Successus parentes infelicissimi\n
EDCS-28600111.translation: To Primitivus, son of Primitivus, aged 26 years and 5 months. Priscilla and Successus, his most unhappy parents.
EDCS-28600111.summary: A funerary epitaph for Primitivus, set up by his grieving parents, Priscilla and Successus.

EDCS-28600112.text_edition: \n Vinioca Spuri filia Maximilla sibi et Marco Vinioco Primigenio viro libertis libertabusque viva fecit\n
EDCS-28600112.translation: Maximilla, daughter of Spurius Vinioca, made this for herself and for Marcus Viniocus Primigenius, her husband, and for her freedmen and freedwomen, while alive.
EDCS-28600112.summary: A funerary monument set up during her lifetime by Maximilla for herself, her husband Marcus Viniocus Primigenius, and their freed dependants.

EDCS-28600116.summary: A dedication recording the fulfillment of a vow by Maximianus.

EDCS-28600117.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto sacrum Valerius Valens vetereranus ex |(centurione) limitem publicum praeclusum ob decessum Aureli Galli legati suo inpendio aperuit Imperatore Commodo II et Martio Vero II consulibus VI Kalendas Maias\n
EDCS-28600117.translation: To Hercules Augustus, sacred. Valerius Valens, a veteran ex-centurion, opened a public road which had been blocked due to the death of the legate Aurelius Gallus, at his own expense, on the 6th day before the Kalends of May, during the consulships of Emperor Commodus II and Martius Verus II.
EDCS-28600117.summary: A dedication to Hercules Augustus by Valerius Valens, a veteran, recording his clearing of a blocked road following the death of legate Aurelius Gallus, dated to April 26, 179 AD.

EDCS-28600118.text_edition: \n Iano Patri Augusto sacrum Caius Iulius Cai filius Sergia Aetor aedilis donatus ab Tiberio Caesare Augusti filio Augusto torque maiore bello Delmatico ob honorem IIviratus cum liberis suis posuit\n
EDCS-28600118.translation: To Janus Father Augustus, sacred. Caius Iulius Aetor, son of Caius of the Sergia tribe, aedile, honored by Tiberius Caesar Augustus' son Augustus with a larger torque during the Delmatic War, for his honor of holding the office of duovir twice, set this up with his children.
EDCS-28600118.summary: A dedication to Janus Augustus by Caius Iulius Aetor, an aedile, commemorating an honor received from Tiberius Caesar and his own second tenure as duovir, likely dating to the late 1st century BC or early 1st century AD.
