EDCS-29100372.translation: To the Divine Manes of Aurelius Epictetus, deceased. His daughter placed this for her most devoted and well-deserving father.
EDCS-29100372.summary: This funerary epitaph records a daughter dedicating a monument to her devoted father, Aurelius Epictetus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100373.text_edition: \n O viva dierum XX Aureli-- Eutych3 Aureli-- Eutychi\n
EDCS-29100373.translation: Living for 20 days. Aureli-- Eutych3. Aureli-- Eutychi.
EDCS-29100373.summary: This fragmentary funerary epitaph records the very short life of a child named Eutychi, possibly two children of the same name, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100374.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that appears to record a dedication or epitaph related to Aurelius Germanus and possibly Caius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29100375.text_edition: \n Marco Aurelio Augusti liberto Hermeroti vixit annos X menses VI dies V filio dulcissimo et pientissimo et Germanio et Stefanae parentes infelicissimi\n
EDCS-29100375.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Hermerotus, freedman of the Emperor, who lived 10 years, 6 months, and 5 days; his most beloved and devoted parents and Germanus and Stephana, most unhappy parents, [set this up].
EDCS-29100375.summary: This is a tombstone commemorating Marcus Aurelius Hermerotus, a freedman of the emperor, who died young, set up by his parents and two other individuals.

EDCS-29100376.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a tombstone, mentioning the name Aurelius and possibly a gentilicium or cognomen.

EDCS-29100377.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a dedication or a tombstone, for Aurelia Macedona.

EDCS-29100379.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Maximus vixit annos plus minus IIII dies Aurelius VISanus et Roscia Maximina <p=R>arentes filio in<f=E>elicissimo posuerunt \n
EDCS-29100379.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aurelius Maximus, who lived about 4 years, [and his brother?] Aurelius Visanus and Roscia Maximina, parents, set this up for their most unhappy son.
EDCS-29100379.summary: This is a tombstone for Aurelius Maximus, who died very young, set up by his parents.

EDCS-29100380.text_edition: \n ------] Aurelius Q defunctus annorum Messia bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-29100380.translation: [--- Aurelius Q, deceased, aged --- years. Messia set this up for the well-deserving.]
EDCS-29100380.summary: This is a fragmentary tombstone commemorating a deceased Aurelius, set up by Messia.

EDCS-29100381.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely mentioning a person with the name Aurelius.

EDCS-29100382.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Satrio qui vixit annos VIIII menses IIII et Aureliae Maximae quae vixit nnos VI menses III filiis piissimis Aurelii Flavus et Ceres parentes et is sic Vic Ursa con sic alumnis suis P Hipetiu sic et\n
EDCS-29100382.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Aurelius Satrius, who lived 9 years, 4 months, and for Aurelia Maxima, who lived 6 years, 3 months, their most devoted children. Aurelii Flavus and Ceres, parents, and they themselves, Ursa, their pupils, and Hipetius [set this up].
EDCS-29100382.summary: This is a tombstone commemorating two children, Aurelius Satrius and Aurelia Maxima, set up by their parents and others.

EDCS-29100383.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Saturnino Auirelia Messorina uxor et sibi fecit\n
EDCS-29100383.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aurelia Messorina, his wife, made this for Aurelius Saturninus and for herself.
EDCS-29100383.summary: This is a tombstone for Aurelius Saturninus, made by his wife Aurelia Messorina, who also included herself.

EDCS-29100384.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Sextilio filio innocentissimo et infelicissimo qui vixit anno sic uno! <e=F>t menses sex et dies VIIII Aurelii Lupus et Privata parentes posuerunt \n
EDCS-29100384.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Aurelius Sextilius, the most innocent and most unhappy son, who lived one year, six months, and 9 days. Aurelii Lupus and Privata, parents, set this up.
EDCS-29100384.summary: This is a tombstone for Aurelius Sextilius, who died in infancy, set up by his parents.

EDCS-29100385.text_edition: \n Aurelius Silvinus et Quadratia Urbana Aureliae Felicissimae filiae infelicissimae quae vixit annos XXI menses V posuerunt\n
EDCS-29100385.translation: Aurelius Silvinus and Quadratia Urbana set this up for their most unhappy daughter, Aurelia Felicissima, who lived 21 years, 5 months.
EDCS-29100385.summary: This is a tombstone commemorating Aurelia Felicissima, who died in young adulthood, set up by her parents.

EDCS-29100386.text_edition: \n Marco Aurelio Sodali Aurelius Corinthus bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-29100386.translation: Aurelius Corinthus placed this for Marcus Aurelius Sodali, who deserved well.
EDCS-29100386.summary: This is a dedication from Aurelius Corinthus to Marcus Aurelius Sodali, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-29100387.text_edition: \n ------ infan]ti in<f=E>elicissimo vixit annu<m=N> menses VIIII Aurelius Tiberianus pater et Basilia <m=N>ater filio fecerunt \n
EDCS-29100387.translation: For the most unfortunate infant, he lived 9 months. Aurelius Tiberianus, father, and Basilia, mother, made this for their son.
EDCS-29100387.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an infant, set up by his parents Aurelius Tiberianus and Basilia.

EDCS-29100388.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus - Aurelio Valentino Aelia Valentine mater bene merenti \n
EDCS-29100388.translation: To the departed spirits. Aelia Valentine placed this for her well-deserving Aurelius Valentino.
EDCS-29100388.summary: This is a funerary dedication from Aelia Valentine to Aurelius Valentino.

EDCS-29100390.summary: The inscription is too fragmentary to determine its purpose or date.

EDCS-29100391.text_edition: \n Aurel MA V Maximannum I menses V Plat\n
EDCS-29100391.translation: [...] Aurel MA V Maximannus 5 months. Plat [made this].
EDCS-29100391.summary: This inscription likely records the lifespan of a person named Maximannus, possibly an infant, with Plat as the dedicator.

EDCS-29100392.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Crescentillae coniugi quae vixit annos XXV Iulius \n
EDCS-29100392.translation: To the departed spirits. For Aurelia Crescentilla, wife, who lived 25 years. Iulius [placed this].
EDCS-29100392.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Crescentilla, set up by her husband Iulius.

EDCS-29100393.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Eutychis Aurelio Callistioni alumno bene merenti de suo posuit annorum XXX\n
EDCS-29100393.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelia Eutychis placed this from her own means for her well-deserving pupil Aurelius Callistio, aged 30.
EDCS-29100393.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Aurelia Eutychis for her well-deserving pupil Aurelius Callistio.

EDCS-29100394.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Marcella Aureliae Vinniae libertae bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-29100394.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelia Marcella placed this for her well-deserving freedwoman Aurelia Vinnia.
EDCS-29100394.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Aurelia Marcella for her freedwoman Aurelia Vinnia.

EDCS-29100395.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Messia filio defuncto raptus esst posit(um) arbitratu Quinti Pr Urani Criscentioni sic infanti annorum XI\n
EDCS-29100395.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelia Messia for her deceased son, who was snatched away. Placed by the decision of Quintus Pr. Uranius Criscentio, thus for an infant, aged 11.
EDCS-29100395.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Messia's son, an 11-year-old infant, arranged by Quitus Pr. Uranius Criscentio.

EDCS-29100396.text_edition: \n Aurelia Parasceue hic est posita vixit nnos LX <c=Q>uius memoriam fabric(avit) vivus maritus eius Aurelius Isidorus qui et Edasterius \n
EDCS-29100396.translation: Aurelia Parasceue is placed here. She lived 60 years. Her husband Aurelius Isidorus, also called Edasterius, had this memorial built during his lifetime.
EDCS-29100396.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Parasceue, erected by her husband Aurelius Isidorus (also known as Edasterius) during his lifetime.

EDCS-29100397.text_edition: \n Aureliae Rufinae defunctae annorum plus minus LXX Publius Maximus matri posuit\n
EDCS-29100397.translation: Publius Maximus erected this for Aurelia Rufina, his mother, who died at about 70 years of age.
EDCS-29100397.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Publius Maximus for his mother, Aurelia Rufina.

EDCS-29100398.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Veneriae quae vixit annos LX menses quinque Aurelius Secundus <fi=EL>lius <p=R>osuit \n
EDCS-29100398.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelius Secundus, her son, set this up for Aurelia Veneria, who lived for 60 years and 5 months.
EDCS-29100398.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Aurelius Secundus for his mother, Aurelia Veneria.

EDCS-29100399.text_edition: \n Manlius sibi et Aureliae Urbanae coniugi infelicissimae defunctae cum qua concorditer vixit\n
EDCS-29100399.translation: Manlius for himself and for Aurelia Urbana, his most unfortunate wife, with whom he lived harmoniously.
EDCS-29100399.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Manlius for himself and his deceased wife, Aurelia Urbana.

EDCS-29100400.summary: This is a funerary epitaph indicating Axilius Primus dedicated a monument to Marcellinus.

EDCS-29100401.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Baebilio Epaphrodito Baebilii Eutyches et Ianuarius liberti\n
EDCS-29100401.translation: To the departed spirits. Baebilius Eutyches and Ianuarius, his freedmen, [set this up] for Lucius Baebilius Epaphroditus.
EDCS-29100401.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by the freedmen Baebilius Eutyches and Ianuarius for their patron, Lucius Baebilius Epaphroditus.

EDCS-29100402.text_edition: \n Baebiliae Charidi defunctae annorum VIIII mensium IIII dierum V Baebilii Satyrus et Prepusa alumnae karissimae\n
EDCS-29100402.translation: Baebilius Satyrus and Prepusa, her cherished alumnae, set this up for Baebilia Charis, who died at 9 years, 4 months, and 5 days of age.
EDCS-29100402.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Baebilius Satyrus and Prepusa for their beloved ward, Baebilia Charis.

EDCS-29100403.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Baebiliae iarini defunctae nnorum plus minus III \n
EDCS-29100403.translation: To the departed spirits. [This is for] Baebilia Iarina, who died at about 3 years of age.
EDCS-29100403.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Baebilia Iarina, who died in childhood.

EDCS-29100404.text_edition: \n Baebio Alexanddri Baebio to Caius Baebius Sabinus Baebi-- He conlibertis \n
EDCS-29100404.translation: Baebius Alexander, Baebius [son of] Caius, and Baebius Sabinus, [from] their fellow freedmen.
EDCS-29100404.summary: This epitaph records Baebius Alexander, Baebius (son of Caius), and Baebius Sabinus as fellow freedmen.

EDCS-29100405.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Baebio Quartioni et ? Baebio Epicteto colliberto optimo eius Valeria Euphrosyne coniugi piissimo bene merito\n
EDCS-29100405.translation: To the departed spirits. For Baebius Quartio and his best fellow freedman, also Baebius Epictetus. Valeria Euphrosyne [erected this] for her most devoted and deserving husband.
EDCS-29100405.summary: This funerary epitaph records dedications to Baebius Quartio and Baebius Epictetus, with Valeria Euphrosyne honoring her husband, Baebius Quartio.

EDCS-29100433.text_edition: \n Iovi Conservatori Dolicheno sacrum Octavius Euphemus\n
EDCS-29100433.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Conservator Dolichenus. Octavius Euphemus [dedicated this].
EDCS-29100433.summary: This is a dedication to the god Jupiter Dolichenus by Octavius Euphemus.

EDCS-29200199.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Baebiliae Luci filiae Proculae defunctae nnorum III mensium VIII\n
EDCS-29200199.translation: To the departed spirits, for Baebilia Procula, daughter of Lucius, who died at 3 years, 8 months.
EDCS-29200199.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring a young girl named Baebilia Procula who died in infancy.

EDCS-29200200.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Barbaroni defuncto anni I mensium III Barbara mater\n
EDCS-29200200.translation: To the departed spirits, for Barbaro, who died at 1 year, 3 months. Barbara, his mother.
EDCS-29200200.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring a young child named Barbaro, erected by his mother, Barbara.

EDCS-29200201.summary: This is a brief funerary epitaph for an unnamed boy.

EDCS-29200203.summary: This inscription appears to record the name of an individual, possibly Publius B--- Priscianus, with an unclear designation or context.

EDCS-29200204.text_edition: \n Baebidiae Salvianeti quae vixit nnos III menses V dies XIIII Tertius et Donata parentes filiae infelicisimae posuerunt\n
EDCS-29200204.translation: Tertius and Donata set this up for their very unhappy daughter, Baebidia Salvianeta, who lived 3 years, 5 months, and 14 days.
EDCS-29200204.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by parents, Tertius and Donata, for their deceased daughter, Baebidia Salvianeta.

EDCS-29200205.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marcus Bibbius Herma et Licinia Ianuaria Marco Bibbio Primigenio liberto bene merito posuerunt defuncto annorum XII mensium VII\n
EDCS-29200205.translation: To the departed spirits. Marcus Bibbius Herma and Licinia Ianuaria set this up for their freedman, Marcus Bibbius Primigenius, well deserving, who died at 12 years, 7 months.
EDCS-29200205.summary: This funerary inscription honors a freedman, Marcus Bibbius Primigenius, erected by his former masters.

EDCS-29200206.text_edition: \n C Acrestina Valerio Var coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-29200206.translation: To Acrestina, her husband Valerius Var, a deserving husband.
EDCS-29200206.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Acrestina, honoring her husband Valerius Var.

EDCS-29200207.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Ca<l=E>petanus Martialis vivus fe[cit ------ \n
EDCS-29200207.translation: To the departed spirits. Gaius Capetanus Martialis, while alive, made [this monument] for...
EDCS-29200207.summary: This is a funerary inscription begun by Gaius Capetanus Martialis for himself or another, which remains incomplete.

EDCS-29200208.summary: This fragmented inscription records an age of 15 years and the name Aesidius, likely part of a funerary monument.

EDCS-29200209.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caesidiae Nicadiae Caesidia Tertia patronae \n
EDCS-29200209.translation: To the departed spirits. Caesidia Tertia [set this up] for her patroness, Caesidia Nicadia.
EDCS-29200209.summary: This funerary inscription honors a patroness, Caesidia Nicadia, erected by her client, Caesidia Tertia.

EDCS-29200210.text_edition: \n Caesonius Ianuarius Valeriae Victorinae quae vixit annos LXV\n
EDCS-29200210.translation: To Caesonius Ianuarius, for Valeria Victorina, who lived 65 years.
EDCS-29200210.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Valeria Victorina.

EDCS-29200211.text_edition: \n Publius Caetennius Publi libertus Ch Caius Fuficius Cai libertus Felicianus COX\n
EDCS-29200211.translation: To Publius Caetennius Ch, son of Publius, freedman of Caius Fuficius Felicianus, freedman of Caius.
EDCS-29200211.summary: This inscription lists the names and patron-freedman relationships of several individuals.

EDCS-29200212.text_edition: \n Marcus Caetennius Mar Satriae Rhodepeni matri piae fecit\n
EDCS-29200212.translation: Marcus Caetennius made this for his devoted mother, Satriae Rhodepeni.
EDCS-29200212.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Marcus Caetennius for his mother, Satriae Rhodepeni.

EDCS-29200213.summary: This fragmented inscription likely records the name of Publius Caetennius and possibly his filiation or cognomen.

EDCS-29200214.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lucio Caltilio Luci filio Myroni Aemilia Nebris coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-29200214.translation: Dedicated to the spirits of the underworld. Aemilia Nebris to her deserving husband, Lucio Caltilio Myron, son of Lucius.
EDCS-29200214.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Aemilia Nebris for her well-deserving husband, Lucio Caltilio Myron.

EDCS-29200216.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that possibly records a dedication to someone named Campestri and concludes with the name Celadus.

EDCS-29200217.text_edition: \n Canuleia Cai filia Procula Publio Petio Marci filio Mariti[mo ------\n
EDCS-29200217.translation: Canuleia Procula, daughter of Cai, to Publius Petius, son of Marcus, her husband ------
EDCS-29200217.summary: This inscription records a dedication from Canuleia Procula to her husband, Publius Petius.

EDCS-29200218.text_edition: \n Cassio bene merenti pientissimo posuit Tigris coniunx eius\n
EDCS-29200218.translation: His wife Tigris placed this for Cassius, well-deserving and most devoted.
EDCS-29200218.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by Tigris for her deserving and devoted husband, Cassius.

EDCS-29200219.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Quinto Cassio Caeionio qui vixit nnos III menses X dies XV filio suo caro posuit Quintus Cassius Silvester et Caeionia Sexta mater\n
EDCS-29200219.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Quintus Cassius Silvester and his mother Caeionia Sexta placed this for their dear son, Quintus Cassius Caionius, who lived 3 years, 10 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-29200219.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a child, Quintus Cassius Caionius, and is dedicated by his father and mother.

EDCS-29200220.text_edition: \n Quinto Cassio Quinti liberto Eumeneti Quintus Cassius Firmus et Cassia Maecenatia et Cassia Constantilla patri\n
EDCS-29200220.translation: Quintus Cassius Firmus, Cassia Maecenatia, and Cassia Constantilla to their father, Quintus Cassius Eumenetus, freedman of Quintus.
EDCS-29200220.summary: This inscription records the dedication by children to their father, Quintus Cassius Eumenetus, who was a freedman.

EDCS-29200221.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Cassio Firmino defuncto annorum XVIIII parentes infelicissimi\n
EDCS-29200221.translation: To the spirits of the departed: For Lucius Cassius Firmus, who died at nineteen years of age, his most unhappy parents.
EDCS-29200221.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the death of Lucius Cassius Firmus at age nineteen, set up by his parents.

EDCS-29200222.text_edition: \n ------] Cassio Iuniano et Iuliae Veneriae parentibus Cassi Pa\n
EDCS-29200222.translation: For Cassius Iunianus and Iulia Veneria, parents of Cassius Pa[...]
EDCS-29200222.summary: A funerary epitaph, mostly lost, mentioning Cassius Iunianus and Iulia Veneria as parents.

EDCS-29200223.text_edition: \n Lucius Cassius Theodorus vivus fecit sibi et Papiniae Italicae uxori et Cassio Italico et Cassiae Tertullinae filiis in fronte pedes XX in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-29200223.translation: Lucius Cassius Theodorus, while alive, made this for himself, his wife Papinia Italica, and his children Cassius Italicus and Cassia Tertullina. Twenty feet in front, twenty feet in the land.
EDCS-29200223.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Lucius Cassius Theodorus for himself and his family, specifying the dimensions of the plot.

EDCS-29200224.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cassiae Secundinae defunctae annorum III mensium III Cassianus et Maxssimuna sic parentes posuaeru<n=M>(t) sic \n
EDCS-29200224.translation: To the spirits of the departed: For Cassia Secundina, who died at three years and three months of age, Cassianus and Maxssimuna, her parents, set this up.
EDCS-29200224.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cassia Secundina, who died in early childhood, erected by her parents Cassianus and Maxssimuna.

EDCS-29200225.text_edition: \n ------] CPAOTRAE Catia Iu et Caius Cat[ius ------\n
EDCS-29200225.translation: For Catia Iu[lia?] and Gaius Cat[tius ...] to [...]paotra.
EDCS-29200225.summary: A fragment of an inscription that names Catia and Caius Cattius, possibly family members.

EDCS-29200226.summary: A very fragmentary inscription that begins with the name Cattius.

EDCS-29200227.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Catttio Terentio Iulia Nepotilla marito posuit et sibi\n
EDCS-29200227.translation: To the spirits of the departed: Iulia Nepotilla erected this for her husband Lucius Catttius Terentius, and for herself.
EDCS-29200227.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Iulia Nepotilla to her husband Lucius Catttius Terentius, and also for herself.

EDCS-29200228.translation: To the spirits of the departed: For Cattia Baebilia, deceased.
EDCS-29200228.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cattia Baebilia.

EDCS-29200229.text_edition: \n Ceionia Ferocilla Ceioniae Hilarae libertae et collactanaeae sic in[felicissimae ------\n
EDCS-29200229.translation: Ceionia Ferocilla [made this] for Ceionia Hilara, her freedwoman and foster-sister, her most unhappy [...].
EDCS-29200229.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ceionia Hilara, identified as a freedwoman and foster-sister of Ceionia Ferocilla.

EDCS-29200230.summary: A funerary epitaph, partially restored, recording Celer as the brother of the deceased.

EDCS-29200231.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus rtiae Celerinae uxori et Quinto o Cele[rino ------\n
EDCS-29200231.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for his wife, Fortia Celina, and for Quintus Cele[rinus...]
EDCS-29200231.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Fortia Celina and Quintus Celernus, likely by a husband or relative.

EDCS-29200232.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus nte pia I defunct-- Celsinus Celsi Maela Netis bene maerenti sic posuerunt\n
EDCS-29200232.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Antepia Pia, deceased. Celsinus, son of Celsus Maela, and Netis, his mother, placed this for their grieving [relative].
EDCS-29200232.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Celsinus and his mother Netis for Antepia Pia, recording her death.

EDCS-29200233.translation: Caenia Quarta placed this out of duty.
EDCS-29200233.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Caenia Quarta for an unnamed individual.

EDCS-29200234.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ceriali defuncto annorum XVIII mensium VI dierum XVII Genialis filius et Deutaer sic mater posuerunt\n
EDCS-29200234.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Cerialis, deceased, aged 18 years, 6 months, and 17 days. His son Genialis and his mother Deutaer placed this.
EDCS-29200234.summary: A funerary inscription recording the death of Cerialis at the age of 18 and erected by his son and mother.

EDCS-29200235.summary: A fragment of a funerary inscription likely honoring an individual named Chari.

EDCS-29200236.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Charitoni filio infelicissimo qui vixit annis XVIII Aurelius Maximus et Aurelia Gemella parentes posuerunt\n
EDCS-29200236.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for his son Chariton, most unfortunate, who lived 18 years. Aurelius Maximus and Aurelia Gemella, his parents, placed this.
EDCS-29200236.summary: A funerary epitaph for Chariton, who died at 18, erected by his parents Aurelius Maximus and Aurelia Gemella.

EDCS-29200237.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Chresimo defuncto annorum VII Cornelia Tertulla filio \n
EDCS-29200237.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Chresimus, deceased, aged 7 years. Cornelia Tertulla placed this for her son.
EDCS-29200237.summary: A funerary inscription for Chresimus, who died at age 7, erected by his mother Cornelia Tertulla.

EDCS-29200238.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning Vivius Chrestus, possibly related to a dedication or property boundary.

EDCS-29200239.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cinciae Campanae posuit memoria Statius Victorinus ma<t=I>ri du<l=I>cissimae \n
EDCS-29200239.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Cincia Campana. Statius Victorinus placed this for his most beloved mother.
EDCS-29200239.summary: A funerary inscription set up by Statius Victorinus for his mother, Cincia Campana.

EDCS-29200240.translation: Castricius Victor for his excellent sister...
EDCS-29200240.summary: A fragmentary inscription by Castricius Victor for his sister, likely a funerary monument.

EDCS-29200241.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a vow made by Claudius, dated by its fragmentary nature to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200243.text_edition: \n Claudio Dalmatio homini incomparabili qui vicxit sic annos plus minus L lia Ilara marito pientissimo posuit et sibi\n
EDCS-29200243.translation: Lia Ilara set this up for her most devoted husband, Claudio Dalmatio, an incomparable man who lived about 50 years, and for herself.
EDCS-29200243.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Lia Ilara for her husband Claudio Dalmatio, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200245.text_edition: \n Claudi-- H et Iulia I Iulio \n
EDCS-29200245.translation: Claudius H. and Julia I. for Julius.
EDCS-29200245.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or funerary marker, for a Julius, dated to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200246.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudius Musculos, who died at the age of 14, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200247.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudio Superstiti iuveni infelicissimo qui vixit nnos XXX menses X dies XIIII Claudius Super et Victrix parentes\n
EDCS-29200247.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Claudius Super and Victrix, parents, set this up for their most unfortunate son, Claudius Superstis, the youth, who lived 30 years, 10 months, and 14 days.
EDCS-29200247.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young man named Claudius Superstis, set up by his parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200249.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudiae Dione Antonius Themistocles pietatis [causa ------\n
EDCS-29200249.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Antonius Themistocles, out of piety, for Claudia Dione...
EDCS-29200249.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Claudia Dione by Antonius Themistocles, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200251.text_edition: \n s Claudiae Maximinae quae vixit annos XXX coniugi rarissimae bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-29200251.translation: ...for Claudia Maxima, a most rare wife, who lived 30 years, they set this up as a mark of well-deserved affection.
EDCS-29200251.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Claudia Maxima, set up by her husband or family, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200253.text_edition: \n AV Clodio Charino marito bene merenti sibi et IISI fecit \n
EDCS-29200253.translation: For her deserving husband, Clodius Charinus, she made this for herself and them.
EDCS-29200253.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Clodius Charinus, set up by his wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200254.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Clodia Nice, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200255.text_edition: \n Gaius Clodius Popilianus qui Cursor coniugi dulcissimae quae vixit con sic coniuge suo annos XII defunctae annorum XXII bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-29200255.translation: Gaius Clodius Popilianus, called 'the Runner', set this up for his most sweet wife, who lived with her husband for 12 years and died at the age of 22, as a mark of well-deserved affection.
EDCS-29200255.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Gaius Clodius Popilianus for his deceased wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200256.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Clodio Zoillo filio karissimo defuncto nnorum XXXII C<l=I>odia Zotice mater infelicissima et Obultronia Fortunata fratri dulcissimo posuerunt \n
EDCS-29200256.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For their dearest son, Clodius Zoilus, who died at the age of 32. Clodia Zotice, his most unhappy mother, and Obultronia Fortunata set this up for their most beloved brother.
EDCS-29200256.summary: A funerary epitaph for Clodius Zoilus, erected by his mother and sister, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200257.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Clodiae Partenopini Gaius coniugi\n
EDCS-29200257.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Clodia Parthenope, his wife.
EDCS-29200257.summary: A funerary epitaph for Clodia Parthenope, dedicated by her husband, Gaius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200258.text_edition: \n s Luci filio Tromentina sibi et Lucio Co et Aponiae tri et AV\n
EDCS-29200258.translation: [Epitaph] for Lucius, son of Lucius, from the tribe Tromentina, for himself and Lucio Co and Aponia tri and AV.
EDCS-29200258.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription likely recording a self-dedication and dedication for family members, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200260.text_edition: \n NI Tromentina IQ CO defuncto annorum pientissimo co[niugi ------\n
EDCS-29200260.translation: [From the tribe] Tromentina, IQ CO for his most dutiful husband [who died...] a most dutiful husband.
EDCS-29200260.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription commemorating a husband, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200262.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Coelius Gaudentius, an most unhappy infant.
EDCS-29200262.summary: A funerary epitaph for an infant named Coelius Gaudentius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200263.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Coeliae Secundae defunctae annorum XXIIII Ulpia Saturnina ma<t=I>er filiae <i=L>nfelici et orius sorori pientissimae posuerunt \n
EDCS-29200263.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Coelia Secunda, who died at the age of 24. Ulpia Saturnina, her mother, set this up for her unhappy daughter, and for [her sister?] Orius, most dutiful.
EDCS-29200263.summary: A funerary epitaph for Coelia Secunda, erected by her mother and in memory of her sister, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200265.text_edition: \n Constans patri posuit et Auxesis coniunx\n
EDCS-29200265.translation: Constans set this up for his father, and Auxesis his wife.
EDCS-29200265.summary: A dedication by Constans for his father and by his wife Auxesis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200266.text_edition: \n ca Cornelio Trofimo coniugi bene merenti \n
EDCS-29200266.translation: [Epitaph] for Cornelius Trofimus, his deserving husband.
EDCS-29200266.summary: A funerary epitaph for Cornelius Trofimus, dedicated by his husband, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200267.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Cornelio Iullo defuncto nnorum VII Caius Cornelius Staphylus pater\n
EDCS-29200267.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Gaius Cornelius Iullus, who died at the age of 7. Gaius Cornelius Staphylus, his father.
EDCS-29200267.summary: A funerary epitaph for Gaius Cornelius Iullus, erected by his father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200268.summary: A very fragmentary inscription, possibly part of a funerary monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29200269.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cornelio Maximo bene merenti Coelia Gemella uxor et sibi viva fecet sic\n
EDCS-29200269.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Coelia Gemella, his wife, and she herself, made this while alive for Cornelius Maximus, who deserved well.
EDCS-29200269.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Cornelius Maximus, set up by his wife, Coelia Gemella.

EDCS-29200270.text_edition: \n REI TONI Cornelius Symphorus filius et heres? bene merenti\n
EDCS-29200270.translation: To Reunion. Cornelius Symphorus, son and heir (?), for his well-deserving father.
EDCS-29200270.summary: This is a funerary epitaph likely for Cornelius Symphorus' father, set up by his son and heir.

EDCS-29200272.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Crescentillae defunctae nnorum XI mensium V dierum II Aurelius Maximinus alumnae\n
EDCS-29200272.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Crescentilla, who died at the age of eleven years, five months, and two days. Aurelius Maximinus made this for his foster-daughter.
EDCS-29200272.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Crescentilla, who died young, set up by her foster father, Aurelius Maximinus.

EDCS-29200273.text_edition: \n VIR SIRIIAus Aliae Crescentillae coniugi bene merenti con sic <q=C>(ua) vixsi sic annos XXX posuit \n
EDCS-29200273.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Alius Crescentillus for his wife Alia Crescentilla, who deserved well. He lived thus for 30 years. He set this up.
EDCS-29200273.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Alia Crescentilla, set up by her husband, Alius Crescentillus, who also records his lifespan.

EDCS-29200275.text_edition: \n Quintus Crispuleius Hermes Quinto Crispuleio Maritimo patrono optime merito\n
EDCS-29200275.translation: Quintus Crispuleius Hermes for Quintus Crispuleius Maritimus, his highly deserving patron.
EDCS-29200275.summary: This is an honorary inscription by Quintus Crispuleius Hermes dedicating something to his patron, Quintus Crispuleius Maritimus.

EDCS-29200276.text_edition: \n Dazomenus <et=F> Panto filio suo Fuscino memoriam DI \n
EDCS-29200276.translation: Dazomenus and Panto for their son Fuscinus, in memory.
EDCS-29200276.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fuscinus, set up by his parents, Dazomenus and Panto.

EDCS-29200277.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Decidio Germano CRESINI CF \n
EDCS-29200277.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Decidius Germanus, son of Cresinius.
EDCS-29200277.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Decidius Germanus, son of Cresinius.

EDCS-29200278.text_edition: \n uminae alumnae suae nnorum dierum L\n
EDCS-29200278.translation: for his foster-daughter Lumina, aged fifty days.
EDCS-29200278.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph for a foster-daughter named Lumina, possibly set up by her foster parent.

EDCS-29200280.text_edition: \n Aga D Desidienae Profuturae coniugi incomparabili Suellius Septiminus maritus bene merenti posuit et Suellio Septimino filio dulcissimo posuit\n
EDCS-29200280.translation: Suellius Septiminus, her husband, set this up for Desidiena Profutura, his incomparable wife, who deserved well, and for Suellius Septiminus, his most beloved son.
EDCS-29200280.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Suellius Septiminus for his wife Desidiena Profutura and their son Suellius Septiminus.

EDCS-29200281.text_edition: \n <D>(is) Manibus Dexter frater sorori Sosemini annorum IIII bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-29200281.translation: To the Divine Spirits. For Dexter, his brother, for his sister Sosemina, who deserved well, aged four years. He set this up.
EDCS-29200281.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a brother for his sister Sosemina, who died at age four.

EDCS-29200282.text_edition: \n Dirutia Restuta testamento fieri iussit sibi et Lucio Dirutio Phoebo fratri et Dirutiae Damale sorori Dirutia Ianilla heres faciundum curavit et Dirutiae Threpte matri\n
EDCS-29200282.translation: Restuta Dirutia ordered this to be made in her will for herself, her brother Lucius Dirutius Phoebus, and her sister Dirutia Damala; her heir Dirutia Ianilla saw to its construction, and also for her mother Dirutia Threpte.
EDCS-29200282.summary: A funerary monument ordered by Restuta Dirutia and completed by her heir Dirutia Ianilla for herself and her family members, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29200283.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Diteio mater filio pientissimo defuncto nnorum plus minus XVIII bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-29200283.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. A mother placed this for her most devoted son, Diteius, who died at about 18 years of age, well deserving.
EDCS-29200283.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a mother for her deceased son, Diteius, who died as a young adult, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29200285.text_edition: \n Lucio Domli qui et I et Magiae V defunctae \n
EDCS-29200285.translation: For Lucius Domlius, also called Iustus, and for Magia V[---], deceased.
EDCS-29200285.summary: A fragmentary epitaph recording the names of deceased individuals, Lucius Domlius and Magia V[---], probably from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29200286.summary: A very fragmentary inscription mentioning a woman named Rufina, likely a funerary dedication from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29200287.translation: Publius Domitius Marcellinus arranged for this to be set up.
EDCS-29200287.summary: A dedication or funerary monument arranged by Publius Domitius Marcellinus, likely dating from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29200289.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Domitio Saturnino Domitius Flavianus filius patri posuit bene merenti\n
EDCS-29200289.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Domitius Flavianus placed this for his deserving father, Domitius Saturninus.
EDCS-29200289.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a son, Domitius Flavianus, to his deceased father, Domitius Saturninus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29200290.summary: A fragmentary inscription mentioning the names Domitius Secundus and Aurelius, possibly related to ownership or dedication, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29200291.text_edition: \n a et Dom Valenti et Ulpio Luci filio? parieti inscriberentur \n
EDCS-29200291.translation: [...Aurelia?] and to Domitius Valens and his son Ulpius Lucius (?) the wall inscription was to be inscribed.
EDCS-29200291.summary: A fragment referencing an inscription on a wall, possibly a dedication or property marker, involving individuals named Domitius Valens and Ulpius Lucius, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29200292.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, for Domitia Arethusa, his wife.
EDCS-29200292.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to Domitia Arethusa, described as the wife of the dedicator, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29200293.text_edition: \n et Domitiae Ianuariae filiae defunctae nnum XVIII Caius Domitius Nicias pater \n
EDCS-29200293.translation: ...and for Domitia Ianuaria, his deceased daughter, 18 years of age. Caius Domitius Nicias, her father [placed this].
EDCS-29200293.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Caius Domitius Nicias for his deceased daughter, Domitia Ianuaria, who died at age 18, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-29200294.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld for Domitia Lacena.
EDCS-29200294.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Domitia Lacena, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-29200295.text_edition: \n TA ETI Linfelicissimae defunctae nnorum XV mensium VI Egnatius Afrodisius pater posuit\n
EDCS-29200295.translation: Egnatius Afrodisius placed this for his most unfortunate wife, aged fifteen years and six months.
EDCS-29200295.summary: A funerary inscription by Egnatius Afrodisius for his wife, who died at a young age.

EDCS-29200297.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ephebo et Synerusae bene merentibus S F P\n
EDCS-29200297.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld for Ephebus and Synerusa, well deserving; S.F.P. [possibly 'Suam Fecerunt Posuerunt' - They Made and Placed For Themselves]
EDCS-29200297.summary: A funerary dedication to Ephebus and Synerusa, possibly by themselves or for them.

EDCS-29200298.text_edition: \n Epictesi Hymnus conservae nnorum XX hic sita est\n
EDCS-29200298.translation: Epictesis, a fellow slave, aged twenty years, is buried here.
EDCS-29200298.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Epictesis, a fellow slave, at the age of twenty.

EDCS-29200299.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription for a woman named Leppia, wife of someone whose name is incomplete.

EDCS-29200300.summary: A very fragmented inscription that likely records a filiation and a gentilicium.

EDCS-29200302.text_edition: \n Quinto Etuvio Quinti filio Tromentina Capriolo nnum XIIII Quintus Etuvius Erastus et Etuvia Hediste filio piissimo et Etuviae Quintae filiae\n
EDCS-29200302.translation: To Quintus Etuvius Capriolus, son of Quintus, of the Tromentina tribe, aged fourteen years; Quintus Etuvius Erastus and Etuvia Hediste placed this for their most dutiful son, and for their daughter Etuvia Quinta.
EDCS-29200302.summary: A funerary inscription by Quintus Etuvius Erastus and Etuvia Hediste for their son Quintus Etuvius Capriolus and daughter Etuvia Quinta.

EDCS-29200304.text_edition: \n Euplo nnorum XXV occiso a viatoribus\n
EDCS-29200304.translation: Euplus, aged twenty-five years, killed by highwaymen.
EDCS-29200304.summary: A tombstone recording the death of Euplus, aged 25, who was killed by robbers.

EDCS-29200305.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld for Eutyches B.
EDCS-29200305.summary: A fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to someone named Eutyches.

EDCS-29200306.text_edition: \n LIaus Eutichus Latoni coniugi Titiae Verzoni\n
EDCS-29200306.translation: Liaus Eutychus for Latonia, wife of Titia Verzo...
EDCS-29200306.summary: A funerary inscription by Liaus Eutychus for his wife Latonia, daughter of Titia Verzo...

EDCS-29200307.text_edition: \n Marco Fabio Firmo annorum XVIII Fabiae Marcellae sororis filiis\n
EDCS-29200307.translation: To Marcus Fabius Firmus, aged 18, for his sister Fabia Marcella's son.
EDCS-29200307.summary: This inscription is a dedication for a young man named Marcus Fabius Firmus, dated by the mention of his sister.

EDCS-29200309.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that seems to record the names of two individuals, Fadius and Caius, possibly as part of a larger dedication or list.

EDCS-29200313.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus <F=E>elicissimo defuncto nnorum XXVIIII Fortunius cognatus bene merenti posuit \n
EDCS-29200313.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. To Felicissimus, who died aged 29. His kinsman Fortunius placed this for him, who deserved well.
EDCS-29200313.summary: This epitaph records the death of Felicissimus, aged 29, with a dedication by his kinsman Fortunius.

EDCS-29200316.text_edition: \n illae ann--- XXX Felix coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-29200316.translation: To her... aged 30. Felix placed this for his wife, who deserved well.
EDCS-29200316.summary: This epitaph records the death of a woman aged 30, with a dedication by her husband Felix.

EDCS-29200317.translation: Felix placed this for his relative, who deserved well.
EDCS-29200317.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Felix for a deserving relative.

EDCS-29200318.text_edition: \n miseri parentes filio infelicissimo Felici qui vixit nnos XIIII bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-29200318.translation: His unfortunate parents placed this for their most unhappy son, Felix, who lived 14 years, for him who deserved well.
EDCS-29200318.summary: This epitaph records the death of Felix at age 14, with a poignant dedication by his grieving parents.

EDCS-29200322.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Pedio Felici Aelia Telesphoris patri bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-29200322.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Aelia Telesphoris made this for her deserving father, Pedius Felix.
EDCS-29200322.summary: This epitaph records that Aelia Telesphoris erected a monument for her deserving father, Pedius Felix.

EDCS-29200323.text_edition: \n OR A ndus PAT tilius Felix \n
EDCS-29200323.summary: This inscription appears to record the name of an individual, likely Aelius Andreas Patilius Felix, but is incomplete.

EDCS-29200324.text_edition: \n FEL Titus Flavius Maximus Laeva amico \n
EDCS-29200324.translation: To his friend Titus Flavius Maximus Laevus...
EDCS-29200324.summary: This inscription is a dedication from Titus Flavius Maximus to his friend, but is incomplete.

EDCS-29200325.text_edition: \n dulcissimae et pientissimae A lii Felix Quintus infelicissima mater bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-29200325.translation: For the most sweet and most dutiful Aelia Felix. Her most unhappy mother placed this for her, who deserved well.
EDCS-29200325.summary: This epitaph records the death of Aelia Felix, with a dedication by her grieving mother.

EDCS-29200326.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Sextus Festivus by his beloved Venustus.

EDCS-29300103.text_edition: \n Flavi sorori defunctae annorum mater infelicissima \n
EDCS-29300103.translation: To his departed sister, her most unhappy mother [erected this].
EDCS-29300103.summary: This epitaph records the grief of a mother for her deceased daughter.

EDCS-29300106.text_edition: \n Marcus Flavius Eutychus se vivo posuit\n
EDCS-29300106.translation: Marcus Flavius Eutychus set this up during his lifetime.
EDCS-29300106.summary: This is a self-made funerary monument by Marcus Flavius Eutychus.

EDCS-29300108.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Aulus Flavius Marcellinus.

EDCS-29300111.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, Flavius TaM.
EDCS-29300111.summary: This epitaph is a dedication to the departed spirit of Flavius TaM.

EDCS-29300112.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, Flavius Valens.
EDCS-29300112.summary: This epitaph is a dedication to the departed spirit of Flavius Valens.

EDCS-29300113.summary: This inscription is a dedication, possibly to Flavia Valeriana.

EDCS-29300114.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavia Apra Gemellino Crescenti nnorum plus minus XIIII infelicissimo ob memoriam posuit\n
EDCS-29300114.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, Flavia Apra set this up in memory of Gemellinus Crescens, who lived about fourteen years, her most unhappy [son/relative].
EDCS-29300114.summary: This epitaph records the erection of a memorial by Flavia Apra for Gemellinus Crescens, a boy of about fourteen years.

EDCS-29300116.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flaviae Nice filiae dulcissimae quae vixit annos II menses IIII Flavia Epictus mater fecet sic\n
EDCS-29300116.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, Flavia Epictus, the mother, made this for her sweetest daughter, who lived 2 years and 4 months.
EDCS-29300116.summary: This epitaph records the mother, Flavia Epictus, setting up a memorial for her daughter who died at the age of 2 years and 4 months.

EDCS-29300117.text_edition: \n Flaviae Rhopeni defunctae annorum XIII Flavius Charito et Eutych \n
EDCS-29300117.translation: To the departed Flavia Rhopeni, aged thirteen, Flavius Charito and Eutychus [set this up].
EDCS-29300117.summary: This epitaph records Flavius Charito and Eutychus setting up a memorial for their departed daughter Flavia Rhopeni, aged thirteen.

EDCS-29300118.text_edition: \n Lucius Folnius Favor vivus fecit sibi et Lolliae Basiliae coniugi et Iuliae Noemae et Tiberio Iulio Stephano et Attiae Soteridi Lucius Iulius Luci libertus Herma vivus fecit sibi et Terentiae Haidi coniugi\n
EDCS-29300118.translation: Lucius Folnius Favor, while alive, made this for himself, Lollia Basilia his wife, and Iulia Noema and Tiberius Julius Stephanus and Attia Soteris. Lucius Julius Herma, freedman of Lucius, while alive, made this for himself and Terentia Haidi his wife.
EDCS-29300118.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording two men, Lucius Folnius Favor and Lucius Julius Herma, dedicating plots for themselves and their families.

EDCS-29300121.text_edition: \n defunct- annorum X mensium XI Claudia Fortunata frat(ri) b(ene)? m(erenti)\n
EDCS-29300121.translation: Deceased at 10 years, 11 months. Claudia Fortunata to her well-deserving brother.
EDCS-29300121.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child, recording her age at death and the dedicant, her sister Claudia Fortunata.

EDCS-29300122.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Fortunatae Quintus conservae bene merenti\n
EDCS-29300122.translation: To the Divine Manes. Quintus to Fortunata, his fellow slave, well-deserving.
EDCS-29300122.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Quintus to Fortunata, his fellow slave.

EDCS-29300123.translation: She placed this for Fortunata Ridi, most dutiful.
EDCS-29300123.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating a monument to Fortunata Ridi by an unnamed individual.

EDCS-29300124.text_edition: \n Apronio Fronton? ex HS CLmilibus nummum\n
EDCS-29300124.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording a sum of money related to Apronius Fronto, likely for a tomb or endowment.

EDCS-29300125.inscription_type: carmina, tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-29300125.text_edition: \n Fulloniae Amaryllidi aequius hunc fuerat titulum me ponere matri quem miserae mater illa mihi posuit bis ternos denos complevi mensibus annos Parcae crudeles nimium properastis rumpere fata mea\n
EDCS-29300125.translation: To Fullonia Amaryllis. It would have been more just for me to place this tombstone for my mother, which that poor mother placed for me. I completed twice three times ten months and years. Cruel Fates, you hurried too much to break my destiny.
EDCS-29300125.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fullonia Amaryllis, written by her child who laments her early death and notes that Amaryllis had previously erected a tomb for her child.

EDCS-29300127.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Gavio Benedicto et Statiae Hilarae Gavius Amabilis parentibus bene merentibus\n
EDCS-29300127.translation: To the Divine Manes. Gavius Amabilis to his well-deserving parents, Titus Gavius Benedictus and Statia Hilara.
EDCS-29300127.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Gavius Amabilis dedicated to his parents, Titus Gavius Benedictus and Statia Hilara.

EDCS-29300130.text_edition: \n ? Satrius Genneus coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-29300130.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely from a husband to his wife, with the dedicant's name partially lost.

EDCS-29300131.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording Glycera dedicating something to her sons.

EDCS-29300139.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Helviae Feliculae Caius Iulius Diadochus coniugi\n
EDCS-29300139.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caius Julius Diadochus to his wife Helvia Felicula.
EDCS-29300139.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Caius Julius Diadochus dedicated to his wife Helvia Felicula.

EDCS-29300140.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Hermeroti D D innocenti et in<f=I>elici nnorum XXV Baebilia Pri<ma> mater posuit \n
EDCS-29300140.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Hermerotus, devoted and innocent and loyal, aged twenty-five. His mother Baebilia Prima placed this.
EDCS-29300140.summary: A funerary epitaph for Hermerotus, set up by his mother, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29300142.text_edition: \n E Gaius Porcius Hermes coniugi rarissimae cum qua convixit annis XV \n
EDCS-29300142.translation: To Gaius Porcius Hermes, for his most exceptional wife, with whom he lived for fifteen years.
EDCS-29300142.summary: A funerary epitaph for an unnamed wife, dedicated by her husband Gaius Porcius Hermes, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29300144.translation: To Hermes Norbanus Symphorus, his freedmen.
EDCS-29300144.summary: An epitaph recording the dedication to Hermes Norbanus Symphorus by his freedmen, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29300150.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Hy<g=C>iae defunctae nnorum VI mensium III dierum XXI Ancharius Themiso alumnae \n
EDCS-29300150.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Hygia, deceased, aged six years, three months, and twenty-one days. Her foster father Ancharius Themiso placed this.
EDCS-29300150.summary: A funerary epitaph for Hygia, a child, set up by her foster father, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29300151.summary: A funerary epitaph for a father named Ianuarius, set up by an unnamed person, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29300152.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ianuariae conlibertae Polus posuit bene merenti\n
EDCS-29300152.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Ianuaria, his fellow freedwoman. Polus placed this for her, well-deserving.
EDCS-29300152.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ianuaria, a fellow freedwoman, set up by Polus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29300153.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iasoni Grapthe sic et Agathangelis sorores fratri bene merenti fecerunt defuncto annorum XVI\n
EDCS-29300153.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Iason, and Grapthe, and Agathangelus, sisters, made this for their brother, well-deserving, deceased at sixteen years of age.
EDCS-29300153.summary: A funerary epitaph for a deceased youth named Iason, set up by his sisters Grapthe and Agathangelus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29300154.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iaso, set up by an unnamed person, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29300160.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus nicius Philetus vivus fecit sibi et Osulniae Iucundae coniugi et libertis libertabusque in fronte pedes X in agro pedes \n
EDCS-29300160.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Nicisius Philetus, who made this while alive for himself and for Osulnia Iucunda, his wife, and for his freedmen and freedwomen. Ten feet in front, in the field [further dimensions missing].
EDCS-29300160.summary: A funerary epitaph for Nicisius Philetus, his wife Osulnia Iucunda, and their freedpersons, set up by Philetus himself, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29300161.summary: A fragmentary inscription likely recording a dedication or funerary notice, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-29300163.summary: This is a funerary epitaph, likely on a tombstone, recording a dedication by Julius to his deceased brother.

EDCS-29300165.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulio Aristonico patri qui vixit annos LXV Iulius Aristonicus filius Iulia Viratia soror Viratia Hilara bene merenti \n
EDCS-29300165.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Iulius Aristoncus, father, who lived 65 years. Julius Aristoncus his son, Julia Viratia, and Viratia Hilara his sister, to their deserving father.
EDCS-29300165.summary: This funerary inscription honors Iulius Aristoncus, a father who lived to 65, with dedications from his son and daughters.

EDCS-29300166.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulius B<e=F>uzas Maxsimae sic Valeri matri pientissimae \n
EDCS-29300166.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Julius Beuzas to Maxsima, his most dutiful mother, wife of Valerius.
EDCS-29300166.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicating an epitaph to Maxsima, the devoted mother of Julius Beuzas and wife of Valerius.

EDCS-29300167.text_edition: \n VIN posuit sibi coniugi suae Iuli-- Calpurni \n
EDCS-29300167.translation: VIN erected for himself and for his wife Iulia Calpurnia.
EDCS-29300167.summary: This is an epitaph, likely for a tomb, indicating that VIN commissioned it for himself and his wife Iulia Calpurnia.

EDCS-29300168.summary: This fragmented inscription appears to be a dedication or a funerary record mentioning Ederna and possibly a place name.

EDCS-29300170.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulio Fortunato Helvius Sabinus fratri carissimo bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-29300170.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Helvius Sabinus erected this for his dearest brother Iulio Fortunatus, his deserving brother.
EDCS-29300170.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of a monument by Helvius Sabinus to his much-loved and deserving brother, Iulio Fortunatus.

EDCS-29300171.text_edition: \n Iulius Cai filius Longinus sibi et Munatiae Maxsumae sic uxori suae et Iulio Cai filio suo et Iuliae Secundae filiae suae et Iulio Cai filio filio suo et Iuliae et Tertiae filiae suae vivus fecit\n
EDCS-29300171.translation: Julius Cai, son Longinus, made this while living for himself, and for his wife Munatia Maxsima, and for his son Iulius Cai, and for his daughter Iulia Secunda, and for his son Iulius Cai, and for his daughters Iulia and Tertia.
EDCS-29300171.summary: This is a funerary epitaph commissioned during the lifetime of Julius Cai Longinus, for himself and his wife, sons, and daughters.

EDCS-29300173.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Gaius Iulius Mercurialis Cantiae Surae obitae pientissimae M CON \n
EDCS-29300173.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Gaius Julius Mercurialis to Cantia Sura, his most dutiful wife, deceased.
EDCS-29300173.summary: This funerary inscription honors the deceased Cantia Sura, the most dutiful wife of Gaius Julius Mercurialis.

EDCS-29300175.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Tyrheno puero studiosissimo qui vixit annis XIII Caius tenius I Fortunat? \n
EDCS-29300175.translation: To the Divine Spirits. To Gaius Julius Tyrhenus, a most studious boy, who lived 13 years. Caius Iulius Fortunatus.
EDCS-29300175.summary: This funerary epitaph honors Gaius Julius Tyrhenus, a very studious boy of 13, and is dedicated by Caius Iulius Fortunatus.

EDCS-29300176.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, to Gaius Julius Zophorus.

EDCS-29300177.translation: To the divine spirits of the most dutiful Iulius.
EDCS-29300177.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription likely dedicated to a man named Iulius, whose epithet suggests piety.

EDCS-29300178.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly the beginning of a funerary dedication for a woman named Julia.

EDCS-29300180.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Antigone Iulius Florentinus \n
EDCS-29300180.translation: To the divine spirits of Julia Antigone. Iulius Florentinus [dedicated this].
EDCS-29300180.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Iulius Florentinus for his wife, Julia Antigone.

EDCS-29300181.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Fabriciae Stenius Lupulus coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-29300181.translation: To the divine spirits of Julia Fabricia. Stenius Lupulus placed [this] for his deserving wife.
EDCS-29300181.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Stenius Lupulus for his well-deserving wife, Julia Fabricia.

EDCS-29300182.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Felicissimae co(n)iugi incomparabili m Niceta maritus\n
EDCS-29300182.translation: To the divine spirits of Julia Felicissima. Her husband Niceta dedicated this for his incomparable wife.
EDCS-29300182.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Niceta for his incomparable wife, Julia Felicissima.

EDCS-29300183.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulia Felicia posuit titulum Iulio Liberto annorum V mensium VII dierum V bene merenti\n
EDCS-29300183.translation: To the divine spirits of Iulia Felicia. [This monument] was placed by Iulius Libertus for [his daughter?] Iulia Felicia, aged 5 years, 7 months, and 5 days, a deserving [child].
EDCS-29300183.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young girl, Iulia Felicia, who died at the age of 5 years, 7 months, and 5 days, with the monument erected by Iulius Libertus.

EDCS-29300184.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Feliculae Vettius Hermes coniugi\n
EDCS-29300184.translation: To the divine spirits of Iulia Felicula. Vettius Hermes for his wife.
EDCS-29300184.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Vettius Hermes for his wife, Iulia Felicula.

EDCS-29300186.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulia Pudentilla et Iulia Ania matri dulcissimae\n
EDCS-29300186.translation: To the divine spirits of Iulia Pudentilla and Iulia Ania, for their most beloved mother.
EDCS-29300186.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Iulia Pudentilla and Iulia Ania to their dearly loved mother.

EDCS-29300187.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Secundae coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-29300187.translation: To the divine spirits of Julia Secunda, for her deserving wife.
EDCS-29300187.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by a husband for his well-deserving wife, Julia Secunda.

EDCS-29300188.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Silvinae nnorum XXIIII coniugi sanctissimae quae vixit viro suo annos XII Pomponius Afenodorus sic coniugi karissimae\n
EDCS-29300188.translation: To the divine spirits of Julia Silvina, aged 24 years, a most chaste wife who lived with her husband for 12 years. Pomponius Afenodorus for his most beloved wife.
EDCS-29300188.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Julia Silvina at age 24, after 12 years of marriage, and was dedicated by her husband Pomponius Afenodorus.

EDCS-29300189.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Ursinae matri pientissimae defunctae \n
EDCS-29300189.translation: To the Divine Manes of Julia Ursinia, her most devoted mother, who has departed.
EDCS-29300189.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Julia Ursinia by her children, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300190.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae Zosimini matri MV EME \n
EDCS-29300190.translation: To the Divine Manes of Julia Zosimina, her mother. [Text incomplete].
EDCS-29300190.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Julia Zosimina, mentioning her mother, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300194.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Iunio Clementiano Galliena Clementilla patri et Galliena Selene coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-29300194.translation: To the Divine Manes, for Marcus Junius Clementianus, Galliena Clementilla dedicates this to her father and husband well-deserving, and Galliena Selene to her wife well-deserving.
EDCS-29300194.summary: This funerary inscription honors Marcus Junius Clementianus, dedicated by his family members, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300195.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a name on a tombstone, of Junius Terpnus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300196.text_edition: \n et Iunia parentibus infelicissimis et Iuniinfelicissim\n
EDCS-29300196.translation: And Iunia to [her] most unfortunate parents and [---] most unfortunate.
EDCS-29300196.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription likely dedicated by Iunia to her parents, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300197.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuniae Epagathinari Iunilla et Martialis matri piissimae\n
EDCS-29300197.translation: To the Divine Manes of Iunia Epagathinari, Iunilla and Martialis to [her] most devoted mother.
EDCS-29300197.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Iunilla and Martialis to their most devoted mother, Iunia Epagathinari, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300198.text_edition: \n o et Iuniae Romulae parentibus pientissimis posu---\n
EDCS-29300198.translation: [This monument is for] and Iunia Romula, to [her] most devoted parents, set up [---].
EDCS-29300198.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription dedicated to Iunia Romula and her parents, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300199.translation: To the Divine Manes of VN Tertull[us? - ---] Valeri? V[---].
EDCS-29300199.summary: This is a damaged funerary inscription mentioning VN Tertullus and possibly Valerius, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300200.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus matri Iustae filius eius incomparabili posuit\n
EDCS-29300200.translation: To the Divine Manes of Iusta, her incomparable son placed this.
EDCS-29300200.summary: This funerary inscription records a son setting up a monument for his incomparable mother, Iusta, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300201.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus NIIINT VIAEDVS C VRAV M V bene merenti posu---\n
EDCS-29300201.translation: To the Divine Manes of NIIINT VIAEDVS C VRAV M V, well-deserving, placed [---].
EDCS-29300201.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription mentioning VIAEDVS and possibly CVRVMV, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300203.translation: Son of [..], of the Sergia tribe, [..] Aeto[..]
EDCS-29300203.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records a filiation and tribal affiliation, possibly part of a funerary monument or dedication, with an incomplete name.

EDCS-29300204.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Leoni conservo qui vixit nnos plus minus XXXX Maritima coniugi posuit\n
EDCS-29300204.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Maritima placed this for her freedman Leon, who lived about forty years.
EDCS-29300204.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Maritima to her freedman Leon, who died around the age of forty.

EDCS-29300205.text_edition: \n Lucius Calenius Luci filius Libo Caius FITICETEA IIIIIX N\n
EDCS-29300205.translation: Lucius Calenius Libo, son of Lucius, of the Fabia tribe, [lived] fifty-eight years.
EDCS-29300205.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the name, parentage, tribal affiliation, and age at death of Lucius Calenius Libo.

EDCS-29300206.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely a name on a tombstone or other monument, for Publius Licinius Erastus.

EDCS-29300208.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Licinia Etnio Cereali marito infelicissimo\n
EDCS-29300208.translation: To the spirits of the underworld: Licinia [placed this] for her most unhappy husband, Etnio Cereali.
EDCS-29300208.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where Licinia dedicates a memorial to her "most unhappy" husband, Etnio Cereali.

EDCS-29300209.translation: Tarconius V [dedicated this] to Licinia Stephana.
EDCS-29300209.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication by Tarconius to Licinia Stephana, possibly on a tomb or as a gift.

EDCS-29300212.text_edition: \n Lollio Pastori Titia Donata coniugi pient[issimo ------\n
EDCS-29300212.translation: Titia Donata [placed this] for her most devoted husband, Lollio Pastor.
EDCS-29300212.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where Titia Donata dedicates a monument to her "most devoted" husband, Lollio Pastor.

EDCS-29300213.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Longinia Victoria coniugi bene merenti posuit Publio Censorio Civili\n
EDCS-29300213.translation: Longinia Victoria placed this for her well-deserving husband, Publius Censorius Civilis, to the spirits of the underworld.
EDCS-29300213.summary: This is a funerary epitaph where Longinia Victoria dedicates a monument to her "well-deserving" husband, Publius Censorius Civilis.

EDCS-29300214.text_edition: \n VX annis Lucius Lucilius V Cornelia Pa parentes filio \n
EDCS-29300214.translation: Lucius Lucilius [and] Cornelia Pa, parents, [for their son] who lived X years.
EDCS-29300214.summary: This fragmentary funerary epitaph records the parents, Lucius Lucilius and Cornelia Pa, dedicating a monument to their son who died at an unspecified age marked by 'X'.

EDCS-29300215.text_edition: \n Frugi Caio Lucilio iae Cai filiae Secundae anio Quinti filio Gal \n
EDCS-29300215.translation: To Frugi, Gaius Lucilius, son of Gaius, daughter Secunda, son Quintus Gal[lius]...
EDCS-29300215.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph listing several names, likely family members, including Gaius Lucilius, his daughter Secunda, and son Quintus Gallius, with one name possibly referring to an ancestor or relation.

EDCS-29300218.summary: This inscription likely records the mother of a person named Lupercus, possibly as part of a larger monument or epitaph.

EDCS-29300219.summary: This is a votive inscription to the Dis Manes, possibly a dedication or a tombstone fragment mentioning Lupercia.

EDCS-29300220.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lusia Victoria posuit karo coniugi Tito Fabio Euticheti sic qui fuit annorum XXX\n
EDCS-29300220.translation: To the departed spirits. Lusia Victoria placed this for her dear husband Tito Fabio Eutiches, who lived for 30 years.
EDCS-29300220.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Lusia Victoria for her husband Tito Fabio Eutiches, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-29300221.translation: To the genius of Lucius Lutatius Restutus.
EDCS-29300221.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the genius of Lucius Lutatius Restutus, likely a shrine or dedication.

EDCS-29300226.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that likely mentions a person named Marcellus, but the context and purpose are unclear.

EDCS-29300227.summary: This inscription records the death of Marcellinus at the age of 10, erected by his brother.

EDCS-29300228.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription indicating that someone placed something for their heir, but details are missing.

EDCS-29300230.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Mariae Secundinae defunctae annorum VII mensium V Marius Secundus alumnae infelicissimae \n
EDCS-29300230.translation: To the departed spirits. Marius Secundus placed this for his beloved foster-daughter Maria Secundina, who died at the age of 7 years and 5 months.
EDCS-29300230.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Marius Secundus for his foster-daughter Maria Secundina, who died at a young age.

EDCS-29300236.text_edition: \n Maximinianus coniugi suae ne bene merenti memoria posuit locum concessum Artoriam <F=E>ronti<n=M>am \n
EDCS-29300236.translation: Maximinianus placed this memorial for his deserving wife, in the place granted to Artoria Frontona.
EDCS-29300236.summary: This inscription records Maximinianus dedicating a memorial for his wife in a plot of land granted to Artoria Frontona.

EDCS-29300237.text_edition: \n Memmiae Rufinae filiae annorum Memmia mater posuit\n
EDCS-29300237.translation: Memmia the mother placed this for her daughter Memmia Rufina, aged...
EDCS-29300237.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Memmia for her daughter Memmia Rufina, with the age of the daughter missing.

EDCS-29300238.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Messorinae filiae incompa<ra>vili sic defunctae nnorum VII i<n=M>felicissimi parentes pos<u=I>erunt \n
EDCS-29300238.translation: To the spirits of the departed, her parents erected this for Messorina, their incomparable daughter, who died at the age of seven, most unfortunate.
EDCS-29300238.summary: A tombstone for a young girl named Messorina, erected by her parents, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300240.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Narcissus Septimae conservae defunctae nnorum plus minus XXX bene merenti\n
EDCS-29300240.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Narcissus, a well-deserving fellow slave of Septima, who died at approximately 30 years of age.
EDCS-29300240.summary: A tombstone for Narcissus, a fellow slave of Septima, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300243.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Nicodromus Lupulae coniugi infelicissimae \n
EDCS-29300243.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Nicodromus, husband of Lupula, most unfortunate.
EDCS-29300243.summary: A tombstone for Nicodromus, husband of Lupula, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300246.text_edition: \n Nonia Felete sic mater et Hylla pater Annio Taurioni filio nnrum VI infelicis sic locus datus a Fabricia Ianuaria\n
EDCS-29300246.translation: Nonia Felete, his mother, and Hylla, his father, and their son Annio Taurioni, aged six, most unfortunate; this plot was given by Fabricia Ianuaria.
EDCS-29300246.summary: A tombstone for a young boy named Annio Taurioni, erected by his parents, with a note about the plot being granted by Fabricia Ianuaria, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300247.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus l Nonnae VAE et Fortunatae TI \n
EDCS-29300247.translation: To the spirits of the departed, for Nonna VAE and Fortunata TI.
EDCS-29300247.summary: A tombstone for two individuals, Nonna VAE and Fortunata TI, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300248.text_edition: \n Publio Novio Platoni Menandri liberto Philippus Balbini amico bene merenti\n
EDCS-29300248.translation: For Publius Novius Platon, freedman of Menander, erected by Philippus, a well-deserving friend of Balbinus.
EDCS-29300248.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to Publius Novius Platon, freedman of Menander, by his friend Philippus, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300249.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli possessionis, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-29300249.text_edition: \n Numeriae Irenini matri dulcissimae Coelius Euhippius posuit\n
EDCS-29300249.translation: Coelius Euhippius erected this for Numeria Irenis, his dearest mother.
EDCS-29300249.summary: A tombstone erected by Coelius Euhippius for his mother, Numeria Irenis, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300251.text_edition: \n NFIDIO S qui vixit annos III parentes filio posuerunt\n
EDCS-29300251.translation: Parents erected this for their son who lived three years.
EDCS-29300251.summary: A tombstone for a child who died at the age of three, erected by his parents, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300253.translation: To CI APE, for Titus Octavius.
EDCS-29300253.summary: A funerary dedication mentioning CI APE and Titus Octavius, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300254.text_edition: \n Lucius Oct(avius) Onesimus Iuliae Primae coniugi bene merenti se vivo posuit\n
EDCS-29300254.translation: Lucius Octavius Onesimus erected this for his wife, Julia Prima, a well-deserving woman, during his lifetime.
EDCS-29300254.summary: A tombstone erected by Lucius Octavius Onesimus for his wife, Julia Prima, during his own lifetime, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-29300255.translation: Caius Octavius Rufus, son of Caius.
EDCS-29300255.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Octavius Rufus, identified as a freedman.

EDCS-29300256.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Octaviae Venustae Aninius Lucifer uxori et Aninius Felicissimus matri bene merenti\n
EDCS-29300256.translation: To the Divine Shades of Octavia Venusta. Aninius Lucifer, to his wife, and Aninius Felicissimus, to his mother, who deserved well.
EDCS-29300256.summary: This funerary epitaph records the dedication by Aninius Lucifer to his wife Octavia Venusta and his mother.

EDCS-29300257.translation: To the Divine Shades of Onesimus. Eutychus [set this up].
EDCS-29300257.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated to Onesimus by Eutychus.

EDCS-29300260.text_edition: \n Pacuviae Quartillae Pacuvius Epafroditus sibi vivo et coniugi posuit bene merenti\n
EDCS-29300260.translation: Pacuvius Epafroditus placed this for Pacuvia Quartilla, for himself while alive, and for his deserving wife.
EDCS-29300260.summary: This funerary epitaph records Pacuvius Epafroditus setting up a monument for his wife Pacuvia Quartilla and himself.

EDCS-29300261.text_edition: \n vixit annos plus minus L Palladius et Se undus et SeP \n
EDCS-29300261.translation: Lived more or less 50 years. Palladius and Secundus and SeP [set this up].
EDCS-29300261.summary: This inscription records the age at death of at least two individuals, Palladius and Secundus, with an unclear third name.

EDCS-29300262.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Pantiae annorum XXIIII Placida mater filiae infelicissimae \n
EDCS-29300262.translation: To the Divine Shades of Pantia, 24 years old. Placidus, her most unhappy mother, [set this up].
EDCS-29300262.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Pantia at age 24, set up by her grieving mother, Placidus.

EDCS-29300264.translation: To the Divine Shades of Papirius Trophimus.
EDCS-29300264.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Papirius Trophimus.

EDCS-29300265.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Papiniae Euhodiae L Eutactus \n
EDCS-29300265.translation: To the Divine Shades of Papinia Euhodia. L. Eutactus [set this up].
EDCS-29300265.summary: This funerary epitaph records the dedication by L. Eutactus to Papinia Euhodia.

EDCS-29400079.text_edition: \n OQanno--- XVIIIR TI Iulianus pater posuit\n
EDCS-29400079.translation: lived 17 years. Ti. Iulianus, father, placed [this].
EDCS-29400079.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of an individual at age 17, set up by their father, Ti. Iulianus.

EDCS-29400081.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, likely funerary, mentioning individuals with the names -icio and Iuliano.

EDCS-29400082.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication to a person named Julianus, with some legible but incomplete names.

EDCS-29500189.summary: This inscription records that parents placed a monument for Paternus.

EDCS-29500190.text_edition: \n Publius Decimius Gratus vivus fecitsibi et libertis libertabusq(ue) suis loco empto in fronte pedes XXII in agro pedes XVIII hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-29500190.translation: Publius Decimius Gratus, alive, made this monument for himself and his freedmen and freedwomen, on land bought, measuring twenty-two feet in front and eighteen feet in depth; this monument will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-29500190.summary: This funerary monument records its owner, Publius Decimius Gratus, who built it for himself and his freed dependents on purchased land, stating it is not hereditary.

EDCS-29500191.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Peculiari defuncto nnorum XXV conservus bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-29500191.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. A fellow slave placed [this] for Peculiaris, deceased at twenty-five years, a well-deserving slave.
EDCS-29500191.summary: This funerary inscription records a fellow slave dedicating a monument to Peculiaris, who died at 25 years of age.

EDCS-29500193.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, beginning with the name Marcus Petronius.

EDCS-29500194.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Petroniae vixit annis III mensibus V diebus III Satrenius Eutiches sic vernacu<l=I>ae posuit \n
EDCS-29500194.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Petronia lived for 3 years, 5 months, and 3 days. Satrenius Eutiches placed [this] for his native-born slave.
EDCS-29500194.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Petronia at a young age (3 years, 5 months, 3 days) and was placed by Satrenius Eutiches for his enslaved person.

EDCS-29500195.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus ? Philadelpho ? ann XXX VNO DE \n
EDCS-29500195.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Philadelphus, deceased, 35 years old. One of...
EDCS-29500195.summary: This fragmentary funerary inscription is dedicated to Philadelphus, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-29500196.text_edition: \n Phileto Salvia Sollemnis marito bene merenti\n
EDCS-29500196.translation: For Philetus, her deserving husband, Salvia Sollemnis placed [this].
EDCS-29500196.summary: This inscription records Salvia Sollemnis dedicating a monument to her deserving husband, Philetus.

EDCS-29500199.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Philumeno defuncto annorum XXV\n
EDCS-29500199.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Philumenus, deceased, 25 years old.
EDCS-29500199.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Philumenus at the age of 25.

EDCS-29500202.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Pinnio Ianuario defuncto nnorum XXX Papiria Musa marito piissimo bene merenti\n
EDCS-29500202.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Titus Pinnius Ianuarius, deceased, 30 years old. Papiria Musa placed [this] for her most dutiful and deserving husband.
EDCS-29500202.summary: This funerary inscription records Papiria Musa dedicating a monument to her husband, Titus Pinnius Ianuarius, who died at 30 years of age.

EDCS-29500205.text_edition: \n Lucius Plaetorius Victorinus patri bene merenti\n
EDCS-29500205.translation: Lucius Plaetorius Victorinus to his deserving father.
EDCS-29500205.summary: This is a dedication to a father by his son, Lucius Plaetorius Victorinus.

EDCS-29500206.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Plaetoria Ursina vixit annos \n
EDCS-29500206.translation: To the departed spirits. Plaetoria Ursina lived for...
EDCS-29500206.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Plaetoria Ursina, recording her death.

EDCS-29500208.text_edition: \n OOO SI Pompeia Maxima coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-29500208.translation: To his deserving wife, Pompeia Maxima.
EDCS-29500208.summary: This is a dedication to a wife, Pompeia Maxima, likely by her husband.

EDCS-29500209.summary: This is a funerary inscription stating that Marcus Pomponius Ursinus erected the monument.

EDCS-29500210.summary: This inscription identifies Pomponia Lupa as the daughter of Idaeus.

EDCS-29500211.text_edition: \n Pomponiae IIE Pomponiae Semne et Quarta matri et Pomponiusus \n
EDCS-29500211.translation: To Semne and Quarta, mothers, and to Pomponius...
EDCS-29500211.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication to mothers, possibly two named Semne and Quarta, and Pomponius.

EDCS-29500212.text_edition: \n O - Popilius Philus coniugi annorum XXIV\n
