EDCS-30101165.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Aeta, daughter of Turius Clodius, for herself, her husband Titus Safinius Rufus, and their children, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30101166.text_edition: \n nae PCLI SPVBLVS filius vivus fecit M\n
EDCS-30101166.translation: [...]na, son of Publius, made [this] while alive.
EDCS-30101166.summary: A funerary inscription, possibly for a man named Publius, commissioned by his son while alive, the beginning of the name lost.

EDCS-30101167.text_edition: \n Sexto Livio Maximo Iulia Maximilla posuit\n
EDCS-30101167.translation: Iulia Maximilla placed [this] for Sextus Livius Maximus.
EDCS-30101167.summary: A funerary dedication by Iulia Maximilla for Sextus Livius Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30101168.summary: A brief dedication or memorial for Quintus Trosius, potentially on a tombstone or honorary monument.

EDCS-30101169.text_edition: \n Trosiae Cai filiae Secundillae Veratia Luci filia Maximilla mater infelicissima posuit\n
EDCS-30101169.translation: Veratia Maximilla, mother, most unhappy, placed [this] for her daughter Trosia Secundilla, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-30101169.summary: A funerary dedication by Veratia Maximilla for her daughter Trosia Secundilla, expressing maternal grief, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30101170.text_edition: \n Caeliae Facundae Caelius Facundus pater filiae infelicissimae posuit\n
EDCS-30101170.translation: Caelius Facundus, father, placed [this] for his daughter Caelius Facunda, most unhappy.
EDCS-30101170.summary: A funerary dedication by Caelius Facundus for his daughter Caelius Facunda, expressing paternal grief, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30101171.text_edition: \n ae inae Caesia Tertulla mater infelicissima posuit\n
EDCS-30101171.summary: A funerary dedication by an unnamed mother expressing grief, the recipient's name lost.

EDCS-30101172.text_edition: \n fuius Titi filius Claudia uter testamento fieri iussit io sibi et suis\n
EDCS-30101172.translation: [...]ius, son of Titus, Claudia ordered [this] to be made in her will for herself and her family.
EDCS-30101172.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by Claudia, daughter of Titus, through her will for herself and her family, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30101173.translation: To our Lords Flavius Constantinus Augustus.
EDCS-30101173.summary: A dedication to the Emperors Flavius Constantinus (Constantine I), likely from the Tetrarchic or Constantinian period.

EDCS-30200122.text_edition: \n Caius Seccius Cnaei filius Sergia Marcellus decurio IIvir iure dicundo Caius Seccius Cai filius Sergia Aper decurio IIvir iure dicundo ex pecunia honoraria IIviratus porticus et trichlinam sic dederunt locus datus publice decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-30200122.translation: Gaius Seccius Marcellus, son of Gnaeus, of the Sergia tribe, a decurion and duovir with judicial power, and Gaius Seccius Aper, son of Gaius, of the Sergia tribe, a decurion and duovir with judicial power, have dedicated the portico and dining room from the honorarium fund for the duovirate. The site was granted publicly by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-30200122.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a portico and dining room by two duovirs, likely in a local community, using funds intended for their office.

EDCS-30200125.text_edition: \n defuncta annorum Aemilius Secundinus maritus qui vixit cum aea sic annos XXX CONIRA IP \n
EDCS-30200125.translation: To his deceased wife, Aemilia Secundina, who lived with him for 30 years. I believe.
EDCS-30200125.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aemilia Secundina, recording her death and the duration of her marriage to her husband.

EDCS-30200126.text_edition: \n Cornelio Claudia no Cornelia patrono testamento fieri iussit Cornelii Verecundus Pudens liberti et heredes Publi Quinti Liciniani hoc testamento reliquit coloniae Claudiae Aequensium HS nummum LXmilia ex cuius reditu \n
EDCS-30200126.translation: Cornelia, daughter of Cornelius Claudia, ordered this to be made in her will for her patron. Verecundus Pudens, freedman of Cornelii, and his heirs left 60,000 sesterces to the colony of Claudia Aequensium from this will, from whose income...
EDCS-30200126.summary: This is a funerary inscription detailing a bequest of 60 million sesterces from Cornelia to the colony of Claudia Aequensium, as stipulated in her will.

EDCS-30200127.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus D V Naevenae Salviae defunctae nnorum XXXII Dieus Ursus coniugi piissimae posuit\n
EDCS-30200127.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Naevena Salvia, deceased at 32 years of age. Dieus Ursus dedicated this to his most devoted wife.
EDCS-30200127.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Dieus Ursus to his wife, Naevena Salvia, recording her age at death.

EDCS-30200128.translation: Site assigned by Lucius Iulius C[...
EDCS-30200128.summary: This fragmentary inscription marks a site that was assigned by Lucius Iulius C[...].

EDCS-30200131.text_edition: \n Cnaeo Seccio Aucto Cnaeo Seccio Felici Cnaeo Seccio Crescenti Cnaeo Seccio Diadumeno Cnaeo Seccio Mysti Secciae\n
EDCS-30200131.translation: To Gnaeus Seccius Auctus, Gnaeus Seccius Felix, Gnaeus Seccius Crescens, Gnaeus Seccius Diadumenus, Gnaeus Seccius Mystes, and Seccia...
EDCS-30200131.summary: This is a dedication or memorial listing several individuals with the cognomen Seccius and one Seccia, possibly family members.

EDCS-30200132.translation: To her patrons, Seccia Silvia, a freedwoman.
EDCS-30200132.summary: This is a dedication by a freedwoman named Seccia Silvia to her patrons.

EDCS-30200133.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Secundino iuveni infelicissimo qui vixit annos XXVII Vi<t=I>alini sorori eius posuit T N vixit annos XVIIII parentes posuerunt pro pietate \n
EDCS-30200133.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Secundinus the youth, most unfortunate, who lived 27 years. His sister Vitalini erected this for him. He lived 19 years. His parents erected this for him out of piety.
EDCS-30200133.summary: This inscription is a double epitaph: one for Secundinus, erected by his sister, and another for an unnamed youth (likely also Secundinus), erected by his parents, recording their ages at death.

EDCS-30200134.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vivius Maximus Viviae Primae Maximi filiae annorum XII infelicissimae\n
EDCS-30200134.translation: To the Divine Manes. For Vivia Prima, daughter of Vivius Maximus, aged 12 years, most unfortunate.
EDCS-30200134.summary: A funerary epitaph for Vivia Prima, daughter of Vivius Maximus, recording her age at death.

EDCS-30200136.text_edition: \n Servius Ennius Servi filius Claudia Fuscus domo Cemeneli miles cohortis VIII voluntariorum stipendiorum XVIIII nnorum XXXIIX Fulvia Vitalis viva fecit sibi et coniugi bene merenti f(ecit) hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-30200136.translation: Servius Ennius, son of Servius, from Claudia Fuscus, from Cemeneli, a soldier of the 8th cohort of volunteers, with 19 years of service, aged 38 years. Fulvia Vitalis made this while alive for herself and for her deserving husband. She made this monument; she will not follow an heir.
EDCS-30200136.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Fulvia Vitalis for herself and her husband, Servius Ennius, a soldier with 19 years of service.

EDCS-30200138.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or tombstone, with the beginning of a name and title.

EDCS-30200139.text_edition: \n ------ miles legionis eiusd]em frater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-30200139.translation: A brother of a soldier of the same legion saw to its construction.
EDCS-30200139.summary: This fragment records a funerary monument erected by a soldier's brother from the same legion.

EDCS-30200140.text_edition: \n QV LXIII SI annorum XIIII pater et mater posuerunt\n
EDCS-30200140.translation: His father and mother placed this for the 63-year-old, aged 14.
EDCS-30200140.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by parents for their son who died at age 14, possibly mentioning his age at death as 63, which could be a typo or misreading.

EDCS-30200144.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum Publius Aelius Aper\n
EDCS-30200144.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus, Publius Aelius Aper [dedicated this].
EDCS-30200144.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus Augustus by Publius Aelius Aper.

EDCS-30200145.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum Agricola Iuli servus posuit libens merito\n
EDCS-30200145.translation: Agricola, the slave of Iulius, willingly set this up to Silvanus Augustus as deserved.
EDCS-30200145.summary: This inscription records a willing dedication to Silvanus Augustus by Agricola, a slave of Iulius.

EDCS-30200146.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum Marcus Iulius Tigrinus votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-30200146.translation: Marcus Iulius Tigrinus willingly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus Augustus.
EDCS-30200146.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Silvanus Augustus by Marcus Iulius Tigrinus.

EDCS-30200148.translation: Saturninus placed [this] in accordance with a vow.
EDCS-30200148.summary: This is a dedication made in fulfillment of a vow by Saturninus.

EDCS-30200149.text_edition: \n Vercaius Mendi filius eques ala nova Claudia t(urma)? Licini domo Varcianus annorum stupendiorum sic XX hic situs est posuit Maximus Regini filius\n
EDCS-30200149.translation: Vercaius, son of Mendi, a cavalryman of the new wing Claudia, belonging to the turma(?) of Licinius, from Varcianus, aged twenty outstanding years, lies here; Maximus, son of Reginus, placed [this].
EDCS-30200149.summary: This tombstone records the burial of Vercaius, a 20-year-old cavalryman of the ala Nova Claudia, with Maximus identified as the one who placed it.

EDCS-30200150.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a name or title.

EDCS-30200151.text_edition: \n M ARr Vitalis sibi ci coniugi suae\n
EDCS-30200151.translation: M. Arrius Vitalis [dedicated this] for himself and his wife.
EDCS-30200151.summary: This inscription records a dedication made by M. Arrius Vitalis for himself and his wife.

EDCS-30200155.translation: To Juno the Protector Queen, he erected this.
EDCS-30200155.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument to Juno Regina by an unnamed dedicant, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30200158.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Aurelius T votum solvit <l=I>(ibens) \n
EDCS-30200158.translation: Titus Aurelius T gladly paid his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-30200158.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Titus Aurelius T, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30200159.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Panes Slator Ciassici votum solvit\n
EDCS-30200159.translation: Panes Slator Ciassicus gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-30200159.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Panes Slator Ciassicus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30200160.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo libens votum solvit Marcus P C\n
EDCS-30200160.translation: Marcus P C gladly paid his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-30200160.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Marcus P C, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30200161.translation: To Father Aurelius ATAR, he erected this.
EDCS-30200161.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by an unnamed dedicant to their father, referred to as Aurelius ATAR, possibly from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30200162.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto Titus Aurelius Quintus ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-30200162.translation: Titus Aurelius Quintus erected this sacred offering to Silvanus Augustus in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-30200162.summary: This inscription records a votive offering to Silvanus Augustus by Titus Aurelius Quintus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30200163.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum Titus Candalio posuit\n
EDCS-30200163.translation: Titus Candalio erected this sacred offering to Silvanus Augustus.
EDCS-30200163.summary: This inscription records the erection of a sacred offering to Silvanus Augustus by Titus Candalio, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30200164.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum Valerius Firmus votum solvit\n
EDCS-30200164.translation: Valerius Firmus gladly paid his vow to Silvanus Augustus.
EDCS-30200164.summary: This inscription records the fulfillment of a vow to Silvanus Augustus by Valerius Firmus, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30200165.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri Augusto posuit Aurelius T\n
EDCS-30200165.translation: Aurelius T erected this to Silvanus Silvestris Augustus.
EDCS-30200165.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument to Silvanus Silvestris Augustus by Aurelius T, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30200167.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by Alexander and possibly Aeternalis, with the precise nature and recipients unclear due to the lacuna, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30200168.text_edition: \n oni DEEI Aurelius ovius SEP et quia fil-- parent--- \n
EDCS-30200168.translation: To the divine gods, Aurelius Ovius and his children, parents...
EDCS-30200168.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the dedication by Aurelius Ovius and his children to his parents, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-30200169.text_edition: \n I(ovi) O(ptimo) M(aximo) Aurelius Sextus Ledri restituit libens\n
EDCS-30200169.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Aurelius Sextus Ledri restored this willingly.
EDCS-30200169.summary: This inscription records the restoration of something dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelius Sextus Ledri, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30200170.text_edition: \n Tito Aurelio Terenti patri bene merito filii posuerunt\n
EDCS-30200170.translation: To Titus Aurelius Terentius, a father well-deserving, his sons erected this.
EDCS-30200170.summary: This funerary inscription records the sons dedicating a monument to their deserving father, Titus Aurelius Terentius, probably from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30200171.translation: ...for Plator, a husband well-deserving, she erected this.
EDCS-30200171.summary: This fragmented funerary inscription records a woman dedicating a monument to her deserving husband, Plator, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30200172.text_edition: \n Titia Ursa puella infelicissima quae vixit nnos plus minus XXV parentes filiae bene merenti et sibi posuerunt\n
EDCS-30200172.translation: Titia Ursa, a most unfortunate girl, who lived about 25 years; her parents erected this for a well-deserving daughter and for themselves.
EDCS-30200172.summary: This funerary inscription records the parents' dedication for their deceased daughter, Titia Ursa, who died at approximately 25 years of age, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30200180.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum Verna Veturiani votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30200180.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus; Verna, daughter of Veturianus, paid her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-30200180.summary: This inscription records Verna, daughter of Veturianus, fulfilling a vow to Silvanus Augustus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30200377.text_edition: \n Quintus Murrius Quinti filius Sergia Augusta miles legionis VII annorum XXXV stipendiorum VI hic situs est\n
EDCS-30200377.translation: Quintus Murrius, son of Quintus, of the Sergia tribe, a soldier of the 7th legion, 35 years old, with 6 years of service, lies here.
EDCS-30200377.summary: This is a military tombstone recording the details of Quintus Murrius, a soldier of the 7th legion, who died at age 35 with 6 years of service, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30200390.text_edition: \n Iovi Cohortali Titus Aurelius Proculus libens posuit\n
EDCS-30200390.translation: To Jupiter of the Cohort, Titus Aurelius Proculus erected this willingly.
EDCS-30200390.summary: This inscription records Titus Aurelius Proculus dedicating a monument to Jupiter Cohors, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30200392.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Aelius Maximus vixit annos XXX hic situs est Aeli Vigor Maximilla Cinthena procurante Aurelia Maximilla\n
EDCS-30200392.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead; Titus Aelius Maximus lived 30 years and lies here. Aelius Vigor, Maximilla, and Cinthena, with Aurelia Maximilla managing the estate.
EDCS-30200392.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Titus Aelius Maximus at age 30, with his burial arrangements managed by Aelia Vigor, Maximilla, Cinthena, and Aurelia Maximilla, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30200393.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Parthino Aurelius Dasius IIvir votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30200393.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, the Parthian, Aurelius Dasius, as duovir, paid his vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-30200393.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Dasius, serving as duovir, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Parthinus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30200394.text_edition: \n Deo Soli Invicto Methri sic Aurelius Maximinus Flavius Marcellinus Flavius Marcellus Rumanus sic Marcianus IV pinus II ficus X\n
EDCS-30200394.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God Methras, thus Aurelius Maximinus, Flavius Marcellinus, Flavius Marcellus Rumanus, thus Marcianus, with four pines and ten fig trees.
EDCS-30200394.summary: This is a dedication to the god Sol Invictus Methras, possibly by a group including Aurelius Maximinus, mentioning plantings, likely for a shrine or garden, from an uncertain date.

EDCS-30200398.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Aelio Censorino optioni classis praetoriae Ravennatis natione nnnico vixit annos XLI militavit annos XXI \n
EDCS-30200398.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Gaius Aelius Censorinus, optio of the Praetorian fleet at Ravenna, of Nicomedia by nation, lived 41 years, served 21 years.
EDCS-30200398.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Gaius Aelius Censorinus, an optio in the Ravenna fleet, detailing his age and service length, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-30200400.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Alogio <q=C>ui et Saxxonio qui vixit annos XXVI posuit Urbica coniux cum quo vigxit sic concorditer annos sex bene merenti posuuit sic \n
EDCS-30200400.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Alogius and Saxxonius, who lived 26 years. His wife Urbica placed this for him with whom she lived harmoniously for six years, placed for the deserving.
EDCS-30200400.summary: This tomb inscription honors Alogius and Saxxonius, recording their lifespan and the duration of their marriage, with the wife Urbica as the dedicator, probably from the 3rd century AD.

EDCS-30200403.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Eutycheti Flavia Frontina marito incomparabili qui mecum vixit annis XVIII bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-30200403.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Aurelius Eutyches, an incomparable husband. Flavia Frontina placed this for him, who lived with me for 18 years, placed for the deserving.
EDCS-30200403.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Flavia Frontina for her beloved husband Aurelius Eutyches, mentioning their 18 years of marriage, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30200404.text_edition: \n Aulus Cardius Festus Iuliae Maxu<m=N>ae sic coniugi fecit suae \n
EDCS-30200404.translation: Aulus Cardius Festus made this for his wife Julia Maxuma.
EDCS-30200404.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Aulus Cardius Festus for his wife Julia Maxuma, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30200405.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Felici Augusti liberto IONII \n
EDCS-30200405.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. To Felix, freedman of Augustus.
EDCS-30200405.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Felix, a freedman of an emperor named Augustus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30200406.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flavio Trophimo Flavius Trophimus filius\n
EDCS-30200406.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Flavius Trophimus, from his son Flavius Trophimus.
EDCS-30200406.summary: This is a funerary inscription from a son, Flavius Trophimus, to his deceased father of the same name, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30200410.text_edition: \n et Sentia Primitiva heredes bene merenti\n
EDCS-30200410.translation: And Sentia Primitiva, his heirs, for the deserving.
EDCS-30200410.summary: This is a fragmentary continuation of a funerary inscription, indicating that the heirs Sentia Primitiva and others placed the monument for the deceased, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30200411.text_edition: \n Ulpiae Gaudentiae inconparabili sic filiae mensium II dierum VIIII Ulpius Parthenopeus et Iulia Sabina parentis sic posueru<nt=M> \n
EDCS-30200411.translation: To Ulpia Gaudentia, an incomparable daughter, 2 months and 9 days old. Ulpius Parthenopeus and Julia Sabina, her parents, placed this.
EDCS-30200411.summary: This is a tombstone for a young child, Ulpia Gaudentia, detailing her age at death and erected by her parents, Ulpius Parthenopeus and Julia Sabina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30200415.text_edition: \n Depositus Primus episcopus XII Kalendas Februarias nepos Domniones sic martores sic\n
EDCS-30200415.translation: Primus, the bishop, was buried on the twelfth day before the Kalends of February. His grandson Domniones and the martyrs.
EDCS-30200415.summary: This inscription records the burial date of Primus, a bishop, and mentions his grandson Domniones and martyrs, likely from late antiquity.

EDCS-30200418.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-30200418.text_edition: \n Arca Iohanni satori et iae iu<g=C>ali eius a cum angelis \n
EDCS-30200418.translation: To John the sower and his yoke-mate Iae, together with the angels.
EDCS-30200418.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription to John and Iae, likely on a tomb or memorial, mentioning their association with angels, possibly from the late Roman or early Christian period.

EDCS-30200431.text_edition: \n Caius Lartinius Cai filius Pollia domo Foro Corneli miles legionis VII nnorum XXXIV stipendiorum XVI hic situs est heredes posuerunt\n
EDCS-30200431.translation: Gaius Lartinius, son of Gaius, of the Pollia tribe, from Forum Corneli, a soldier of the 7th legion, aged 34, with 16 years of service, lies here; his heirs erected this.
EDCS-30200431.summary: This is a soldier's tombstone, recording the name, origin, legion, age, and service length of Gaius Lartinius, erected by his heirs.

EDCS-30200432.text_edition: \n Marcus Pytha Segni filius miles cohortis II Chyrr<h>esticorum domo Berea annorum LX stipendiorum XXXV testamento fieri iussit sibi Felici liberto \n
EDCS-30200432.translation: Marcus Pytha, son of Segnus, a soldier of the 2nd Cohort of Chyrrestici, from Berea, aged 60, with 35 years of service, ordered this to be made in his will for himself and for Felicis, his freedman.
EDCS-30200432.summary: This is a soldier's tomb inscription, detailing Marcus Pytha's origin, military unit, age, service, and his testamentary disposition for himself and his freedman.

EDCS-30200433.translation: Tytulius Panentis set this up for Silvanus Domestius.
EDCS-30200433.summary: This is a dedication of a monument or object by Tytulius Panentis to Silvanus Domestius.

EDCS-30200434.text_edition: \n Marcus Domitius Marci filius Fabia Severus Brixsia sic miles legionis XI nnorum XXX stipendiorum VIII hic situs est heres posuit\n
EDCS-30200434.translation: Marcus Domitius Severus, son of Marcus, of the Fabia tribe, from Brixia, a soldier of the 11th legion, aged 30, with 8 years of service, lies here; his heir erected this.
EDCS-30200434.summary: This is a soldier's tombstone, recording the name, origin, legion, age, and service length of Marcus Domitius Severus, erected by his heir.

EDCS-30200435.text_edition: \n Remmo Saeconis filius miles cohortis I Montanorum nnorum \n
EDCS-30200435.translation: Remmo, son of Saeco, a soldier of the 1st Cohort of Montani, aged...
EDCS-30200435.summary: This is a partial tombstone inscription for Remmo, a soldier of the 1st Cohort of Montani, recording his name and military service but lacking age and completion.

EDCS-30200436.text_edition: \n L(uci)? Viri Luci fili Poblilia Novici Verona militis legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis centuria Aquili Frontonis stipendiorum XXII heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-30200436.translation: Of Lucius Viri, son of Lucius, of the Poblilia tribe, Novicius from Verona, soldier of the 11th Claudian Legion, pious and faithful, in the century of Aquilius Frontonis, with 22 years of service; his heir arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-30200436.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for a soldier named Novicius, likely of equestrian rank, detailing his legion, centurion, service length, and origin, with his heir responsible for its erection.

EDCS-30200437.text_edition: \n Caius Oppius de sua pecunia Tiberius C Caius Oppius --- trib(unus) militum legionis Libra S I libra sextarius frumentum hemina sextarius\n
EDCS-30200437.translation: Gaius Oppius, at his own expense, Tiberius C. Gaius Oppius, military tribune of the Libra Legion... sextarius of wheat, hemina, sextarius.
EDCS-30200437.summary: This inscription records a donation of grain by Gaius Oppius, who also held the position of military tribune, possibly related to legionary rations or a public benefaction.

EDCS-30200439.text_edition: \n Oppia Cai filia Aequina votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30200439.translation: Oppia, daughter of Gaius, Aequina, fulfilled her vow willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-30200439.summary: This is a votive inscription recording Oppia Aequina's fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30200441.text_edition: \n Sestus <P=L>laturius TrIti filius vivus fecit sibi et coniugi annorum Dis Manibus \n
EDCS-30200441.translation: Sextus Platurius, son of Triti, made this while alive for himself and his wife, aged [---]. To the departed spirits.
EDCS-30200441.summary: This is a tomb inscription for Sextus Platurius, made by himself during his lifetime for himself and his wife, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30200442.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Flavius Marcellus seplasiarius annorum LXX sibi vivus fecit\n
EDCS-30200442.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Titus Flavius Marcellus, a plasterer, aged 70, made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-30200442.summary: A funerary inscription for Titus Flavius Marcellus, a plasterer, who erected his own tomb at the age of 70.

EDCS-30200795.text_edition: \n Cassiae Cai filiae Annae Caius Cassius Longinus contubernali suae bene merenti fecit et sibi et suis\n
EDCS-30200795.translation: To Cassia Anna, daughter of Cassius, his beloved companion; Gaius Cassius Longinus made this for her, and for himself and his family.
EDCS-30200795.summary: A funerary monument erected by Gaius Cassius Longinus for his companion Cassia Anna, and also for himself and his family.

EDCS-30200796.text_edition: \n Lucius Plaetorius Valens Luciae Plaetoriae filiae loricam fecit quae vixit annos X et sibi et suis fecit\n
EDCS-30200796.translation: Lucius Plaetorius Valens made this breastplate for his daughter Lucia Plaetoria, who lived for 10 years, and for himself and his family.
EDCS-30200796.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by Lucius Plaetorius Valens for his daughter Lucia Plaetoria, who died at age 10, and also for himself and his family.

EDCS-30301342.text_edition: \n Flavos sic Bouti filius miles cohortis I Lucensium nnorum XXXI stipendiorum X domo Luco Augusti heres faciendum curavit hic situs est\n
EDCS-30301342.translation: Flavus, son of Boutus, a soldier of the 1st Cohort of Lucenses, aged 31, with 10 years of service, from Luco Augusti, his heir took care to have this set up. He is buried here.
EDCS-30301342.summary: A funerary inscription for Flavus, a soldier of the 1st Cohort of Lucenses, noting his age, service, origin, and that his heir arranged for his burial.

EDCS-30301343.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valerio Va<l=I>enti protectori defuncto bello civile in Italia nnorum L \n
EDCS-30301343.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Valerius Valens, a protector, who died in the civil war in Italy, aged 50.
EDCS-30301343.summary: A funerary inscription for Valerius Valens, a protector, who died at age 50 during the civil war in Italy.

EDCS-30301344.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Stercoriae filiae defunctae \n
EDCS-30301344.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Aurelia Stercoria, daughter of Aurelius.
EDCS-30301344.summary: A funerary inscription for Aurelia Stercoria.

EDCS-30301346.translation: Paia, daughter of Carpius Plator, aged [---].
EDCS-30301346.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription for Paia, daughter of Carpius Plator.

EDCS-30301347.text_edition: \n Semproniae Apuleiae quae vixit mecum annos XXII Aurelius Eutichius sic maritus bene merenti posuit Monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-30301347.translation: To Sempronia Apuleia, who lived with me for 22 years. Aurelius Eutychius, her husband, placed this for his well-deserving wife. It will not pass to an heir.
EDCS-30301347.summary: A funerary monument erected by Aurelius Eutychius for his wife Sempronia Apuleia, whom he commemorates after 22 years of marriage, specifying the monument is not inheritable.

EDCS-30301348.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vitalioni dulcissimo coniugi qui vixit annos XI et Crescentioni infelicissimo \n
EDCS-30301348.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Vitalio, my sweetest spouse, who lived 11 years, and for Crescentio, most unfortunate.
EDCS-30301348.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Vitalio, a spouse who lived 11 years, and Crescentio, with an expression of grief.

EDCS-30301352.summary: An incomplete funerary inscription mentioning a possible name and the placer, with an illegible lacuna.

EDCS-30301358.text_edition: \n Marcus Nasidius Secundus decurio municipii veteranus beneficiarius consularis legionis X geminae vivus fecit sibi Nasidio Rufino filio annorum XX et \n
EDCS-30301358.translation: Marcus Nasidius Secundus, decurion of the municipium, a veteran, beneficiary of a consular legion (Legio X Gemina), made this while alive for himself and for his son Nasidius Rufinus, aged 20.
EDCS-30301358.summary: This funerary inscription records a self-funded monument for a decurion and his son, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-30301359.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Proclianus VERI-V NANI decurio municipii et Aeliae Honoratae coniugi carissimae viv sibi posu[erunt ------\n
EDCS-30301359.translation: To the Divine Manes. Publius Aelius Proclianus, decurion of the municipium, and Aelia Honorata, his dearest wife, set this up while alive for themselves.
EDCS-30301359.summary: This funerary inscription records a self-funded monument for a decurion and his wife, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-30301361.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelia Paiio nepotes vixit annos LXXX posuerunt\n
EDCS-30301361.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aelia Paiio, granddaughter, lived 80 years. They set this up.
EDCS-30301361.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, records the lifespan of Aelia Paiio and is set up by unnamed relatives.

EDCS-30301362.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Plator S socrui bene merenti\n
EDCS-30301362.translation: To the Divine Manes. Aurelius Plator for his well-deserving mother-in-law.
EDCS-30301362.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication by Aurelius Plator to his mother-in-law, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30301367.text_edition: \n Titus filio suo Tito annorum novem\n
EDCS-30301367.translation: To Titus, his son Titus, aged nine.
EDCS-30301367.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication to a deceased child named Titus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30301368.summary: This is a very brief inscription recording that Flavius Maximus was the maker of the monument, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30301369.translation: Udianus Plator set this up for his father.
EDCS-30301369.summary: This inscription records that Udianus Plator erected a monument for his father, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30301370.text_edition: \n iudex datus a Flavio Valerio Constantio viro clarissimo praeside provinciae Delmatiae sic fines inter Salviatas et Stridonenses detrminavit\n
EDCS-30301370.translation: As judge, appointed by Flavius Valerius Constantius, most distinguished man, governor of the province of Dalmatia, he determined the boundaries between the Salviatas and the Stridonenses.
EDCS-30301370.summary: This inscription records a judicial decision by a governor of Dalmatia concerning boundary demarcation between two groups, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-30301371.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Gaius Iulius Rogatus beneficiarius consularis legionis XI Claudiae et Gaius Iulius Rogatus Iunior voto libentes posuerunt\n
EDCS-30301371.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Gaius Julius Rogatus, beneficiary of a consular legion (Legio XI Claudia), and Gaius Julius Rogatus the Younger willingly set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-30301371.summary: This dedication to Jupiter records a vow fulfilled by two individuals named Gaius Julius Rogatus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30301372.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Victoris SINN Victoris SCI ntissimi et Aefetur OOII \n
EDCS-30301372.translation: To the Divine Manes. Of Victor, son of Sinn Victor, most learned, and Aefetur [and]...
EDCS-30301372.summary: This fragmented funerary inscription commemorates Victor and possibly others, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30301373.text_edition: \n Aelio Proculo basshn qo et Aelio Aelius Iotus Quartus s pientissimis posuerunt\n
EDCS-30301373.translation: Aelius Iotus Quartus set up (this) for the most dutiful Aelius Proculus.
EDCS-30301373.summary: This is a funerary inscription likely from the Roman Republic or early Empire, recording a dedication by Aelius Iotus Quartus to Aelius Proculus.

EDCS-30301374.text_edition: \n Lucius Arruntius Camillus Scribonianus legatus pro praetore Cai Caesaris Augusti Germanici iudicem dedit Manium Coelium |(centurionem) legionis VII inter Sapuates et Lamatinos ut fines regeret et termin<o=V>s poneret \n
EDCS-30301374.translation: Lucius Arruntius Camillus Scribonianus, as governor with praetorian power for Gaius Caesar Augustus Germanicus, appointed Manius Coelius, a centurion of the VII legion, as judge between the Sapuates and Lamatinos to define the borders and set boundary stones.
EDCS-30301374.summary: This inscription from the imperial period records a judicial appointment made by the governor Lucius Arruntius Camillus Scribonianus concerning a border dispute between two tribal groups.

EDCS-30301375.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum Aurelius Plator \n
EDCS-30301375.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest, Aurelius Plator.
EDCS-30301375.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter, likely from the Roman Imperial period, made by Aurelius Plator.

EDCS-30301376.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestri Messori Plator libens posuit\n
EDCS-30301376.translation: Messorius Plator willingly set this up for Silvanus Silvestris.
EDCS-30301376.summary: This is a dedication, likely from the Roman Imperial period, to the sylvan deity Silvanus, set up by Messorius Plator.

EDCS-30301377.translation: T. set up an altar for Silvanus.
EDCS-30301377.summary: This is a brief dedication, likely from the Roman Imperial period, to the god Silvanus, with the dedicator identified only by the initial T.

EDCS-30301378.text_edition: \n Aulus Larcius Clemens Triviis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30301378.translation: Aulus Larcius Clemens willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Triviis.
EDCS-30301378.summary: This is a votive inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording Aulus Larcius Clemens fulfilling a vow to the household deities of the crossroads (Triviis).

EDCS-30301379.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus V filius miles cohortisRE centuria Valentis parentes bene merenti fecerunt\n
EDCS-30301379.translation: V's son, a soldier of the cohort, from the century of Valentis, made (this) for deserving parents.
EDCS-30301379.summary: This funerary inscription from the Roman Imperial period records a soldier setting up a monument for his well-deserving parents.

EDCS-30301380.text_edition: \n Navina A Patavini filia annorum XXXX\n
EDCS-30301380.translation: Navina, daughter of A. Patavinus, aged 40.
EDCS-30301380.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, recording the death of Navina at the age of 40.

EDCS-30301381.text_edition: \n Panto Ausulinis Cremetis verna nnorum X\n
EDCS-30301381.translation: Panto, son of Ausulinus Cremetis, a freedman, aged 10.
EDCS-30301381.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, records the death of Panto, a freedman, at the age of 10.

EDCS-30301382.text_edition: \n ------ Proc]culo Plahetera Triti filia annorum LXXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-30301382.translation: Proc]ulus, son of Plaheterus Tritus, aged 80, is buried here.
EDCS-30301382.summary: This funerary inscription, likely from the Roman Imperial period, records the burial of Proculus at the age of 80.

EDCS-30301383.text_edition: \n Plaes Ctius Macri filius annorum V\n
EDCS-30301383.translation: Plaes Ctius, son of Macr, aged 5 years.
EDCS-30301383.summary: This is a tombstone for a young boy named Plaes Ctius, who died at the age of five.

EDCS-30301384.summary: This fragmented inscription likely records a patron (PRA?) and a master or owner (domi/nus) with the initial D.

EDCS-30301385.summary: This is a tombstone for Sextus Labricus, identified as the daughter of Sestus.

EDCS-30301386.text_edition: \n Trito Montani defuncto annorum XL et Maximae matri eius Maximus filius patri et aviae bene merenti fecit\n
EDCS-30301386.translation: Maximus, son of Trito Montanus, made this for his deceased father, aged 40, and for his mother Maxima, to his father and grandmother, who deserved well.
EDCS-30301386.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Maximus for his deceased father Trito Montanus and his grandmother Maxima.

EDCS-30301387.text_edition: \n Tudanio Maximo Panto mater an<A=I>(norum) XLV \n
EDCS-30301387.translation: His mother [made this for] Tudanius Maximus Panto, aged 45 years.
EDCS-30301387.summary: This tombstone records Tudanius Maximus Panto, who died at the age of 45, and was honored by his mother.

EDCS-30301388.text_edition: \n ae Augustae ia Apli filia unda pro se et suis voto suscepto porticum fecit\n
EDCS-30301388.translation: Iunda, daughter of Aplus, for herself and her family, having undertaken a vow, made this portico to the August Divine [Empress].
EDCS-30301388.summary: This inscription records the construction of a portico by Iunda, daughter of Aplus, dedicated to an Empress, likely in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30301389.text_edition: \n Lucius Aeronius Silvano votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30301389.translation: Lucius Aeronius paid his vow to Silvanus, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-30301389.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus by Lucius Aeronius, fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-30301392.text_edition: \n Marcus Fraxsanius sic Sexti filius Pollia domo Regio Lepido veteranus legionis XI eques annorum XLIIII stipendiorum XXV donatus phaleris torquibus armillis hic situs est Primus libertus pro meritis\n
EDCS-30301392.translation: Marcus Fraxsanius, son of Sextus, of Pollia tribe, from Regium Lepidum, a veteran of the 11th legion, an eques, aged 44 years and 25 years of service, honored with phalerae, torques, and armillae, is buried here. Primus, his freedman, [erected this] for his merits.
EDCS-30301392.summary: This is a tombstone for Marcus Fraxsanius, a veteran of the 11th legion, who died at 44, erected by his freedman Primus.

EDCS-30301395.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Iulio Eutycheti et Marco Iulio Iuliano Moschis coniugi et filio\n
EDCS-30301395.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, for Marcus Julius Eutychetes and for his wife and son, Marcus Julius Iulianus Moschis.
EDCS-30301395.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Julius Eutychetes and his family, specifically mentioning his son Marcus Julius Iulianus Moschis and Moschis's wife.

EDCS-30301396.text_edition: \n Plaetoriae Titi filiae Maximillae annorum XXIIII Titus Plaetorius Victor pater infelix fecit\n
EDCS-30301396.translation: For Plaetoria Maximilla, daughter of Titus, aged 24 years. Titus Plaetorius Victor, her unhappy father, made this.
EDCS-30301396.summary: This is a tombstone for Plaetoria Maximilla, who died at 24, erected by her father Titus Plaetorius Victor.

EDCS-30301397.text_edition: \n Veronia Prochne mater infelicissima sibi et filio nnum XX Veronio Sementivo\n
EDCS-30301397.translation: Veronia Prochne, most unhappy mother, [dedicated this] for herself and her son Veronio Sementivo, aged 20 years.
EDCS-30301397.summary: A funerary epitaph for a mother and her son, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30301398.summary: A votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Coranus Iovile, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30301399.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Fufisius signifer votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30301399.translation: Titus Fufisius, standard-bearer, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-30301399.summary: A votive inscription by Titus Fufisius, a standard-bearer, dedicated to Jupiter, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30301400.translation: Gaius Voconius, son of Marcus, [dedicated this] to Jupiter.
EDCS-30301400.summary: A dedication to Jupiter by Gaius Voconius, son of Marcus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30301402.translation: Gaius Atilius Macedo fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30301402.summary: A votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Gaius Atilius Macedo, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30301403.text_edition: \n Titus Lollius Titi filius Claudia Tarviso miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis hic situs est\n
EDCS-30301403.translation: Titus Lollius, son of Titus, a soldier of the 11th Claudian Pia Fidelis legion, is buried here.
EDCS-30301403.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus Lollius, a soldier of the 11th Claudian legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30301404.text_edition: \n Caius Semius Cai filius Tromentina Seus Aquis Statiellis legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis signifer \n
EDCS-30301404.translation: Gaius Semius, son of Gaius, from the Tromentina tribe, a standard-bearer of the 11th Claudian Pia Fidelis legion, [dedicated this] in Seus Aquis Statiellis.
EDCS-30301404.summary: An inscription likely recording a dedication or marking the presence of Gaius Semius, a standard-bearer of the 11th Claudian legion, in Seus Aquis Statiellis, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30301405.text_edition: \n ------ T]ertullius Novaria miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis centuria Cai Verecundi annorum XXIIX stipendiorum XXII testamento poni i(usst)\n
EDCS-30301405.translation: [Tertullius Novaria, a soldier of the 11th Claudian Pia Fidelis legion, in the century of Gaius Verecundus, aged 28 years and 22 years of service, ordered this to be set up by his will].
EDCS-30301405.summary: A funerary inscription for Tertullius Novaria, a soldier of the 11th Claudian legion, specifying his age, service years, and that the monument was erected by his will, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30301408.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph recording the short life of Aelia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30301409.text_edition: \n RX II ROM heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-30301409.translation: [... the heir arranged for this to be made].
EDCS-30301409.summary: A fragmentary inscription indicating that an heir arranged for the monument's construction, likely a funerary epitaph from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30301410.translation: Licinus Macr, after ten years of service...
EDCS-30301410.summary: This inscription, likely a tombstone, records Licinus Macr's military service of ten years.

EDCS-30301412.text_edition: \n ------ defunct-- nnorum ---] et Claudi-- defunct-- nnorum Claudius Ursus et Aelia Marciana parentes\n
EDCS-30301412.translation: ...died... years old and Claudius Ursus and Aelia Marciana, parents, died... years old.
EDCS-30301412.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to be a funerary monument commemorating Claudius Ursus and Aelia Marciana, identified as parents.

EDCS-30301413.text_edition: \n pidiae pientissimae quae vixit annos? ---XX NE\n
EDCS-30301413.translation: To Pidias, most dutiful, who lived for twenty years...
EDCS-30301413.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pidias, who lived for approximately twenty years.

EDCS-30301414.text_edition: \n Orcleca Daphne Caio Orcleco Scaptia Felici \n
EDCS-30301414.translation: Orcleca Daphne to Gaius Orclecus and Scaptia Felici.
EDCS-30301414.summary: This inscription records a dedication or bequest from Orcleca Daphne to Gaius Orclecus and Scaptia Felici.

EDCS-30301416.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Valeria Trophime, likely for a deceased relative or loved one.

EDCS-30301425.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by an individual or group to honor a patron who served them well.

EDCS-30301430.text_edition: \n Quint-- Valeri-- Q(uinti)? f(ili--)? Tromentina Sta |(centurio) legionis \n
EDCS-30301430.translation: Quintus Valerius, son of Quintus, from the Tromentina tribe, a centurion of the legion...
EDCS-30301430.summary: This inscription identifies Quintus Valerius, a centurion in a Roman legion, likely belonging to the Tromentina tribe.

EDCS-30301431.text_edition: \n Verus Vecae <f=I>(ilius) domo Bodiontius miles cohortis III Alpinorum a annorum stip[endiorum ------ \n
EDCS-30301431.translation: Verus, son of Vecca, from Bodiontius, a soldier of the III Alpinorum cohort, aged... years, after... years of service...
EDCS-30301431.summary: This inscription describes Verus, a soldier from the III Alpinorum cohort, detailing his age and length of service.

EDCS-30301432.text_edition: \n Publio Cloelio Publi liberto Hilaro et Cloeliae Publi libertae Fuscae optume! de se meritis patronus posuit\n
EDCS-30301432.translation: A patron erected this to Publius Cloelius Hilarus, his freedman, and to Cloelia Fusca, his freedwoman, who deserved exceptionally well of him.
EDCS-30301432.summary: This inscription records a patron dedicating a monument to his deserving freedman, Hilarus, and freedwoman, Fusca.

EDCS-30301436.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Helladio diem functo annorum Septimia Exuperia dulcissimo coniugi et sibi viva posuit\n
EDCS-30301436.translation: To the Divine Shades. Septimia Exuperia erected this to Helladius, who died on this day, aged... years, her dearest husband, and to herself while alive.
EDCS-30301436.summary: This funerary inscription records Septimia Exuperia dedicating a tomb to her husband Helladius and herself.

EDCS-30301437.text_edition: \n Caio Titio Maximo annorum XXX Sextus Titius Celsus pater\n
EDCS-30301437.translation: To Gaius Titius Maximus, aged 30. Sextus Titius Celsus, his father, [made this].
EDCS-30301437.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Titius Maximus, set up by his father, Sextus Titius Celsus.

EDCS-30301439.text_edition: \n ANOI Lucius Publicius Tertius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30301439.translation: Lucius Publicius Tertius gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30301439.summary: This is a votive inscription by Lucius Publicius Tertius, likely dedicated to a deity, dated by its formula to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-30301440.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Cassia Paulina ex voto posuit\n
EDCS-30301440.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Cassia Paulina set this up in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-30301440.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Cassia Paulina.

EDCS-30301441.text_edition: \n Libero Patri sacrum Iulia Firmilla votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30301441.translation: To Liber Pater, Iulia Firmilla gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-30301441.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Liber Pater by Iulia Firmilla.

EDCS-30301442.translation: To the Great Mother, Gaius Petronius Philippus [dedicated this].
EDCS-30301442.summary: This is a dedication to the Great Mother by Gaius Petronius Philippus.

EDCS-30301443.translation: Gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30301443.summary: This is a votive inscription, but the dedicant and deity are missing, likely dated to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-30301444.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucillae defunctae annorum XII Lucida mater filiae pientissimae fecit\n
EDCS-30301444.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucida, her most dutiful mother, made this for her daughter Lucilla, who died aged 12.
EDCS-30301444.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucilla, erected by her mother Lucida.

EDCS-30301445.text_edition: \n ae quaae vixit nnos LXII lius Se\n
EDCS-30301445.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of an individual.

EDCS-30301449.text_edition: \n Vesia Cai filia Quincta votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30301449.translation: Vesia Quincta, daughter of Caius, gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-30301449.summary: This is a votive inscription by Vesia Quincta, daughter of Caius.

EDCS-30301450.text_edition: \n Tiberius Claudius Epetinus Caius Avillius Clemens Lucius Coelius Capella Publius Raecius Libo Publius Valerius Secundus iudices dati a Marco Pompeio Silvano legato Augusti pro praetore inter rem publicam Asseriatium et rem publicam Alveritarum in re praesenti per sententiam suam determinaverunt\n
EDCS-30301450.translation: Tiberius Claudius Epetinus, Gaius Avillius Clemens, Lucius Coelius Capella, Publius Raecius Libo, Publius Valerius Secundus, judges appointed by Marcus Pompeius Silvanus, legatus Augusti pro praetore, determined, in the present matter, by their sentence, the dispute between the public of Asseriatum and the public of Alveritarem.
EDCS-30301450.summary: This inscription records a judicial decision made by a panel of judges appointed by the governor Marcus Pompeius Silvanus, resolving a dispute between two communities, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30301451.text_edition: \n Quintus Magius Cai filius Publilia domo Verona veteranus legionis VII nnm L stipendiorum XXV testamento fieri iussit hic situs est\n
EDCS-30301451.translation: Quintus Magius, son of Caius, of the Publilia tribe, from Verona, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, 25 years of service, ordered this to be made in his will; he lies here.
EDCS-30301451.summary: A tombstone for Quintus Magius, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, who made arrangements for his burial through his will.

EDCS-30301453.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Publicio Sa<t=I>urnino <f=E>ratri omn<i=L>bus honoribus patriae suae func<t=I>o S<t=I>ennia Faus<t=I>ina soror \n
EDCS-30301453.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Titus Publicius Saturninus, brother of all honors to his fatherland. Septennia Faustina, his sister, [erected this].
EDCS-30301453.summary: A funerary dedication by Septennia Faustina to her brother, Titus Publicius Saturninus, honoring his service to his fatherland.

EDCS-30301455.text_edition: \n Egnatia Quinta Valentinae filiae pientissimae et posterisque suis in fronte pedes XXX in agro pedes XX\n
EDCS-30301455.translation: To Egnatia Quinta, for her most devoted daughter Valentina, and for her descendants, 30 feet in front and 20 feet in the field.
EDCS-30301455.summary: A funerary dedication or property boundary marker for Egnatia Quinta, her daughter Valentina, and their descendants.

EDCS-30301460.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Hel<v=S>(iae FIti quae vixit annos plus minus XXX Felix maritus pientissimus posuit \n
EDCS-30301460.translation: To the Divine Spirits of Helvia Fita, who lived more or less 30 years. Felix, her most devoted husband, placed this.
EDCS-30301460.summary: A funerary dedication by Felix to his devoted wife Helvia Fita, who died at approximately 30 years of age.

EDCS-30301461.summary: An incomplete inscription possibly recording a length of service or age related to daughters, date uncertain.

EDCS-30301462.summary: A very fragmentary inscription possibly mentioning two female names, purpose unknown.

EDCS-30301463.text_edition: \n Victorinae coniu<g=C>i carissimae Aurelius Ursulus maritus fecit \n
EDCS-30301463.translation: To Victorina, his dearest wife, Aurelius Ursulus, her husband, made this.
EDCS-30301463.summary: A funerary dedication by Aurelius Ursulus to his beloved wife Victorina.

EDCS-30301466.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo sacrum ex voto Marcus Ac\n
EDCS-30301466.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest, in fulfillment of a vow, Marcus Ac[---]
EDCS-30301466.summary: A dedication of a sacred object or site to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, initiated by Marcus Ac[---] in fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-30301467.text_edition: \n Recus Sentius Iovi Op<t=I>(imo) Maxumo votum solvit libens merito \n
EDCS-30301467.translation: Recus Sentius paid his vow willingly and deservedly to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-30301467.summary: A dedication recording Recus Sentius fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-30301468.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo sacrum votum solvit Vincentius votum posuit\n
EDCS-30301468.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest. Vincentius fulfilled his vow; he placed the vow.
EDCS-30301468.summary: A dedication recording Vincentius fulfilling and placing a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-30301469.summary: This inscription records Certus' action of enclosing something, likely a space or structure.

EDCS-30301470.text_edition: \n Marcus Plusius V Ammianus nnrum XXIII Siscia natus Titius Genialis amico fecit\n
EDCS-30301470.translation: Titius Genialis made this for his friend Marcus Plusius V. Ammianus, aged 23, born in Siscia.
EDCS-30301470.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Titius Genialis for his friend Marcus Plusius V. Ammianus.

EDCS-30301472.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Claudiae Gratillae annorum XXVIII Claudia Eutychia mater posuit\n
EDCS-30301472.translation: To the Divine Shades. Claudia Eutychia set this up for her daughter Claudia Gratilla, aged 28.
EDCS-30301472.summary: This funerary inscription honors Claudia Gratilla, erected by her mother Claudia Eutychia.

EDCS-30301473.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription with an unknown dedication or purpose.

EDCS-30301475.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius M posuit\n
EDCS-30301475.translation: Publius Aelius M. set this up for Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-30301475.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Aelius M.

EDCS-30301477.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo ter Luci libertus Severus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30301477.translation: Severus, freedman of Lucius, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-30301477.summary: This inscription records Severus, a freedman, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-30301481.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelia Procula Iuliae Veturiae matri suae pientissimae ob memoriam posuit\n
EDCS-30301481.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aelia Procula set this up in memory of her most devoted mother, Iulia Veturia.
EDCS-30301481.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated by Aelia Procula to her mother, Iulia Veturia.

EDCS-30301482.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Calpurnio Polioni Caio Calpurnio Maximo avo Lucio Calpurnio Polioni Sextiliae Proculae parentibus Lucio Calpurnio Polioni fratri Lucius Calpurnius Agapetus\n
EDCS-30301482.translation: To the Divine Shades. Lucius Calpurnius Agapetus [set this up] for his grandfather Gaius Calpurnius Polio, for his great-uncle Gaius Calpurnius Maximo, for his parents Lucius Calpurnius Polio and Sextilia Procula, and for his brother Lucius Calpurnius Polio.
EDCS-30301482.summary: This extensive funerary inscription lists multiple family members honored by Lucius Calpurnius Agapetus.

EDCS-30301483.translation: Gaius Julius [---] for Gaius Julius Rufinus [---]
EDCS-30301483.summary: This is a partial inscription likely related to Gaius Julius Rufinus, with an unknown purpose.

EDCS-30301484.text_edition: \n sius e<t=I> Maxima matri suae faciendum curaverunt \n
EDCS-30301484.translation: [---]sius and Maxima had this made for their mother.
EDCS-30301484.summary: This inscription records that [---]sius and Maxima commissioned a monument for their mother.

EDCS-30301486.text_edition: \n Iuliae Augustae divi Augusti matri Tiberi Caesaris Augusti Lucio Volusio Saturnino legato pro praetore Caius Iulius Cai filius Sulla ob decurionatum\n
EDCS-30301486.translation: To Julia Augusta, mother of Emperor Tiberius Caesar Augustus, Lucius Volusius Saturninus, legate with proatorial power, [erected this] for Gaius Julius Sulla, son of Gaius, because of his decurionate.
EDCS-30301486.summary: This is a dedication to Julia Augusta by Lucius Volusius Saturninus, honoring Gaius Julius Sulla for his position as a decurion, likely dating to the early 1st century AD.

EDCS-30301487.text_edition: \n Ex edictu sic Publi Corneli Dolabellae legati pro praetore determinavit Spurius Titius Geminus princeps posterior legionis VII inter Neditas et Corinienses restituti iussu Auli Duceni Gemini legati Augusti pro praetore per Aulum Resium Maximum |(centurionem) legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis principem posteriorem et Quintum Aebutium Liberalem hastatum posteriorem legionis eiusdem\n
EDCS-30301487.translation: By decree, thus, of Publius Cornelius Dolabella, legate with proatorial power, Spurius Titius Geminus, chief of the second line of the VIIth legion, established [the boundary], restored by order of Aulus Ducenius Geminus, imperial legate with proatorial power, through Aulus Resius Maximus, a centurion of the XIth Claudian legion, pious and faithful, chief of the second line, and Quintus Abutius Liberalis, a junior officer of the second line of the same legion.
EDCS-30301487.summary: This inscription records the establishment of a boundary by imperial officials and military officers of the VIIth and XIth legions, likely in a provincial context during the early imperial period.

EDCS-30301488.text_edition: \n Marco Aelio Aurelio Vero Caesari Imperatoris Antonini Augusti Pii patris patriae filio consuli II decreto decurionum publice\n
EDCS-30301488.translation: To Marcus Aelius Aurelius Ver Caesar, son of Emperor Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the fatherland, consul for the second time, publicly, by decree of the town council.
EDCS-30301488.summary: This is a public dedication by the town council to Marcus Aurelius Ver Caesar (later Marcus Aurelius) during his second consulship, probably in the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-30301490.text_edition: \n Appuleio Cai filio Sergia Balbino pontifici equum publicum habenti nnum XXI Epidia Titi filia Paullina avia\n
EDCS-30301490.translation: To Appuleius Balbinus, son of Gaius, of the Sergia tribe, pontifex, who had a public horse, aged 21 years; by Epidia Paullina, daughter of Titus, his grandmother.
EDCS-30301490.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Appuleius Balbinus, a young man who held equestrian status, set up by his grandmother, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30301492.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Autronio Decimi filio Atratino OVINM equiti Romano \n
EDCS-30301492.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. To Marcus Autronius Atratinus, son of Decimus, Roman knight.
EDCS-30301492.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Marcus Autronius Atratinus, a Roman knight, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30400554.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Luci Lentidi Saturnini veterani legionis primae Minerviae piae fidelis vivus posuit sibi AVG et coniugi Ulpiae Crispinae annorum LX hic sita est\n
EDCS-30400554.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Lucius Lentidius Saturninus, a veteran of the First Minervia legion, pious and faithful, erected this while alive for himself and his wife Ulpia Crispina, aged 60 years.
EDCS-30400554.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a veteran of the I Minervia legion for himself and his wife, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30400555.text_edition: \n Silvano Caius O C pro salute sua\n
EDCS-30400555.translation: To Silvanus. Gaius O C, for his own health.
EDCS-30400555.summary: This is a votive offering to the god Silvanus, dedicated by Gaius O C for his well-being, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30400556.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Caio Messio Quinto Traiano Decio Pio Felici Augusto \n
EDCS-30400556.translation: To Emperor Caesar Gaius Messius Quintus Traianus Decius Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-30400556.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Trajan Decius, likely erected during his reign in the mid-3rd century AD.

EDCS-30400557.text_edition: \n Ulpiae linae carissimae defunctae annorum LII se vivo sibi liberisque\n
EDCS-30400557.translation: To Ulpia Lina, dearest, deceased, aged 52 years. [Erected by] himself while alive, for himself and his children.
EDCS-30400557.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpia Lina, set up by her husband for himself and their children, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30400559.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Marcus Annius Florianus Pius Felix Augustus S\n
EDCS-30400559.translation: Emperor Caesar Marcus Annius Florianus Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-30400559.summary: This is an inscription honoring Emperor Florian, likely erected during his brief reign in 276 AD.

EDCS-30400560.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Autronio Decimi filio Atratino qui cum vixit a bonis homine sic audiv<i>t bene set sic in locum peregre iniquo fato decessit et vixit annos XXX\n
EDCS-30400560.translation: To the spirits of the departed. For Marcus Autronius Atratinus, son of Decimus, who lived as a good man, so it is said, but died abroad by unjust fate, and lived for 30 years.
EDCS-30400560.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Marcus Autronius Atratinus, recording his character and death.

EDCS-30400562.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription dedicating the monument to a woman named Calliste, likely his wife and daughter.

EDCS-30400563.text_edition: \n Tito Gabinio P Marc- Licini- Tr BENIISA matri viv CALE MIRT Euhem<e=I>ris FLII \n
EDCS-30400563.translation: To Titus Gabinius, son of Marcus, son of Licinius. To his mother Benisa, alive. To Calemirtus, son of Euhemerus. [---]
EDCS-30400563.summary: A funerary inscription mentioning Titus Gabinius and his mother Benisa, likely placed by Titus Gabinius himself while alive.

EDCS-30400564.text_edition: \n Hostuciae ex testam<e>nto Titi filius Severus L Hostucia AeT heredes lo in fronte pedes V\n
EDCS-30400564.translation: For Hostucia, according to the will of Titus Severus, her son. For L. Hostucia and her heirs, in front 5 feet.
EDCS-30400564.summary: A funerary inscription recording a land allotment of 5 feet for the heirs of Hostucia, established by her son Titus Severus.

EDCS-30400565.text_edition: \n CE Isidorae nnorum XIX Marcus Minicius Zosimus vivus fecit delicatae suae\n
EDCS-30400565.translation: For Isidora, aged 19 years. Marcus Minicius Zosimus made this for his beloved, while alive.
EDCS-30400565.summary: A funerary monument made by Marcus Minicius Zosimus for his beloved Isidora, who died at the age of 19.

EDCS-30400566.translation: For himself and for Titus Julius. [---]
EDCS-30400566.summary: A fragmentary inscription intended for the benefit of two individuals, one being Titus Julius.

EDCS-30400567.text_edition: \n Titus Iulius Hermes Letoidi uxori piissimae in fronte \n
EDCS-30400567.translation: For Titus Julius Hermes. For his most dutiful wife. [---] in front.
EDCS-30400567.summary: A funerary inscription dedicated to Titus Julius Hermes's wife, described as most dutiful.

EDCS-30400569.translation: To the son Magn... along the road. [---]
EDCS-30400569.summary: A fragmentary inscription, possibly funerary, mentioning a son named Magn and a location along a road.

EDCS-30400570.text_edition: \n Quinto Petronio Gallioni Appuleia Pau<l=I>ina uxor posuit \n
EDCS-30400570.translation: Appuleia Paulina placed this for Quintus Petronius Gallio, her husband. [---]
EDCS-30400570.summary: A funerary monument erected by Appuleia Paulina for her husband, Quintus Petronius Gallio.

EDCS-30400571.text_edition: \n Petronia Sexti filia Secunda sibi et Caio Voccio viro suo testamento poni iussit\n
EDCS-30400571.translation: Petronia Secunda, daughter of Sextus, ordered this to be placed by her will for herself and for her husband Gaius Voccius.
EDCS-30400571.summary: A funerary inscription indicating Petronia Secunda's posthumous arrangements for herself and her husband Gaius Voccius.

EDCS-30400572.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tertullinae bene merenti Marcus Rubrius et sibi vivus fecit\n
EDCS-30400572.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Marcus Rubrius made this for his deserving Tertullina and for himself while alive.
EDCS-30400572.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Tertullina by Marcus Rubrius, who also provided for himself, likely dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-30400573.summary: A very brief inscription, likely a name on a tombstone or marker, identifying Publius Tituleius, probably Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30400574.text_edition: \n Quintus Tullius Quinti filius Rufus Vettia Titi filia Sec\n
EDCS-30400574.translation: Quintus Tullius Rufus, son of Quintus, from the Vettia tribe, and Secundus, son of Titus Vettius, from the Vettia tribe.
EDCS-30400574.summary: A fragmentary inscription recording the names and tribal affiliations of two individuals, Quintus Tullius Rufus and Secundus Vettius, likely from a tomb or public record, dating to the Roman Republican or Imperial period.

EDCS-30400575.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Tulliae Victorinae E \n
EDCS-30400575.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Tullia Victorina. E...
EDCS-30400575.summary: A funerary inscription honouring Tullia Victorina, likely from the Roman Imperial period, with the final letter possibly indicating a dedicant or location.

EDCS-30400578.text_edition: \n Cai- Vibi R AN SCR POIV Iulia mater viva Cai- Vibi VI in fronte pedes X hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur et libertis libertabusque posterisque eorum\n
EDCS-30400578.translation: Of Caius Vibius... the mother, living, placed this monument. Ten feet in front, this monument will not pass to an heir, but to his freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-30400578.summary: A funerary monument inscription from the Roman Imperial period, specifying that the plot and monument are to remain with the descendants of freedpeople, excluding direct inheritance, and indicating its dimensions.

EDCS-30400580.text_edition: \n Lucius Cornelius Sabinus Silvano votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30400580.translation: Lucius Cornelius Sabinus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Silvanus.
EDCS-30400580.summary: A votive inscription from the Roman Imperial period, recording Lucius Cornelius Sabinus fulfilling a promise made to the god Silvanus.

EDCS-30400581.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aemilio Silvestro filio pientissimo qui vixit annos XXXXV Aemilia Ursina mater et Silvinus pater bene merenti fecerunt Dis Manibus sacrum Semele coniugi carissimae nnorum XXVI Victor consservus\n
EDCS-30400581.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Aemilius Silvester, dearest son, who lived 45 years. Aemilia Ursina, mother, and Silvinus, father, made this for the deserving. Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Semele, dearest wife, 26 years. Victor, fellow-slave.
EDCS-30400581.summary: A complex funerary inscription from the Roman Imperial period, honouring a son named Aemilius Silvester and a wife named Semele, erected by their parents and a fellow-slave respectively.

EDCS-30400583.text_edition: \n Lucio Sestio Luci filio Sergia Silvestri decurioni nnum XXIII Lucius Sestius Titi filius Sergia Clemens pater testamento poni iussit\n
EDCS-30400583.translation: For Lucius Sestius, son of Lucius, from the Sergia tribe, Silvester, decurion, 23 years old. Lucius Sestius, son of Titus, and Silvestri Clemens, father, ordered this to be placed in his will.
EDCS-30400583.summary: A funerary inscription from the Roman Imperial period, commemorating Lucius Sestius Silvester, a decurion aged 23, whose monument was commissioned by his father via his will.

EDCS-30400585.summary: A very brief inscription, possibly identifying a person named Galerius, with a speculative addition of 'here', likely from a tomb or marker of the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30400586.text_edition: \n RCVCVBIS Carpus annorum C vivos sic sibi posuit\n
EDCS-30400586.translation: Carptus, 100 years old, set this up for himself while alive.
EDCS-30400586.summary: An unusual funerary inscription from the Roman Imperial period where Carptus, aged 100, erected his own monument during his lifetime.

EDCS-30400587.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus s(acrum) Nantia IIvir infelicissim \n
EDCS-30400587.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Nantia, twice a decurion, in unfortunate circumstances.
EDCS-30400587.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the 'Dis Manes' (Spirits of the Departed) for Nantia, who was a decurion and appears to have died under unhappy circumstances, possibly indicating a tombstone.

EDCS-30400589.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Sabinus decurio TER alae Claudiae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30400589.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Titus Flavius Sabinus, decurion of the Third Cohort of the Claudia cavalry, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30400589.summary: This is a votive inscription from Titus Flavius Sabinus, a decurion in a Roman cavalry unit, dedicating a fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter.

EDCS-30400590.summary: This is a name inscription, likely a tombstone or dedication by Aurelius Maximus Pantadienus, with no further context provided.

EDCS-30400591.text_edition: \n Silvano Silvestro sic sacrum Andes posuit\n
EDCS-30400591.translation: To Silvanus Silvestris, sacred thus, Andes placed this.
EDCS-30400591.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus Silvestris, erected by Andes, indicating a sacred monument or offering.

EDCS-30400592.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Rufus annorum XXV mi<l=I>is sic legionis sicundae sic Aurelius <T=I>urranius pater <f=I>(ilio) hic situs est \n
EDCS-30400592.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Rufus, aged 25, a soldier of the Second Legion, and Aurelius Turranius, his father, placed here lies his son.
EDCS-30400592.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Aurelius Rufus, a soldier of the Second Legion, and his burial by his father, Aurelius Turranius.

EDCS-30400594.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureli Ursi annorum LXXXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-30400594.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Ursius, aged 85, lies here.
EDCS-30400594.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating the burial of Aurelius Ursius, who died at the age of 85.

EDCS-30400595.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Di Anadri annorum LXII Gaius Iulius Certus amitae dignissimae et nutrici posuit hic sita est\n
EDCS-30400595.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Gaius Iulius Certus placed this for his most worthy aunt and nurse, Diandria, aged 62; she lies here.
EDCS-30400595.summary: This funerary inscription records Gaius Iulius Certus dedicating a tomb for his aunt and nurse, Diandria, who died at 62.

EDCS-30400596.text_edition: \n Caio Iulio Celeri Cai liberto annorum LX et Cla<r=I>o annorum XVI Priscus patri et fratri \n
EDCS-30400596.translation: For Gaius Iulius Celer, freedman of Gaius, aged 60, and for Clarus, aged 16, Priscus, for his father and brother.
EDCS-30400596.summary: This funerary inscription records Priscus commemorating his father, Gaius Iulius Celer (a freedman), and his brother, Clarus.

EDCS-30400597.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iulio Nepoti nnorum L Ditueio marito incomparabili posuit hic situs (est)\n
EDCS-30400597.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Iulius Nepos, aged 50. Ditueius placed here his incomparable husband (he lies here).
EDCS-30400597.summary: This is a funerary inscription where Ditueius dedicates a burial place for her husband, Iulius Nepos, who died at 50.

EDCS-30400598.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iuliae GTMA CF N\n
EDCS-30400598.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. For Iulia GTM A, daughter of CF N.
EDCS-30400598.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription dedicated to Iulia GTM A, likely the daughter of someone named CF N.

EDCS-30400599.text_edition: \n Leoni F P R O I R S\n
EDCS-30400599.translation: To Leonis, son of... free man, from the [collegium] of [those who repair?]...
EDCS-30400599.summary: This fragmentary inscription, likely from a funerary monument, is too incomplete to provide a definitive summary.

EDCS-30400602.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Lucius...
EDCS-30400602.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, begun by Lucius, but the text is too incomplete for further detail.

EDCS-30400606.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Iulius Sextilius beneficiarius consularis <e=L>x legione XIIII gemina votum solvit libens merito \n
EDCS-30400606.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Julius Sextilius, a beneficiary of the consular governor, from the Fourteenth Legion Gemina, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30400606.summary: This inscription records a soldier, Julius Sextilius of the Legio XIV Gemina, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-30400607.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelia Procula Iulio Secundo coniugi pientissimo Laurenti Lavinatium sic qui vixsit annos XXXXII\n
EDCS-30400607.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aelia Procula to her most devoted husband, Julius Secundus, a Laurentian from Laurentum, who lived for 42 years.
EDCS-30400607.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Julius Secundus, set up by his wife Aelia Procula, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30400612.text_edition: \n Gessia Cai filia Maxima viva fecit sibi et suis hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-30400612.translation: Gessia Maxima, daughter of Caius, made this monument for herself and her family while alive; she will not be succeeded by an heir.
EDCS-30400612.summary: This is a self-funded funerary monument inscription by Gessia Maxima, specifying that it is not bound by inheritance laws.

EDCS-30400614.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci municipii Metulensium Aurelius Maximus |(centurio) legionis II adiutricis votum posuit libens numini maiestatique eius Imperatore domino nostro Diocletiano \n
EDCS-30400614.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest and to the divine spirit of the municipality of Metulum, Aurelius Maximus, centurion of the Second Legion Adiutrix, gladly set up this vow to His divine majesty, during the reign of our lord Emperor Diocletian.
EDCS-30400614.summary: This inscription records Aurelius Maximus, a centurion of the Legio II Adiutrix, fulfilling a vow to Jupiter and the Genius Loci of Metulum during the reign of Diocletian.

EDCS-30400616.translation: Cronius fulfilled his vow to Janus the Father.
EDCS-30400616.summary: This inscription records Cronius fulfilling a vow to the god Janus Pater.

EDCS-30400618.text_edition: \n Lucius Granius Voltimesis filius Rufus Iutossicae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30400618.translation: Lucius Granius Voltimesis, son of Rufus, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Iutossica.
EDCS-30400618.summary: This inscription records Lucius Granius Voltimesis Rufus fulfilling a vow to the deity Iutossica.

EDCS-30400619.text_edition: \n Geminus Boninus Hostiducis Sentonae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30400619.translation: Geminus Boninus, son of Hostiducis, gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to Sentona.
EDCS-30400619.summary: This inscription records Geminus Boninus fulfilling a vow to the goddess Sentona.

EDCS-30400620.text_edition: \n Sentonae Felix Augusti nostri votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30400620.translation: Felix fulfilled his vow to Sentona, our Augusta, gladly and deservedly.
EDCS-30400620.summary: This inscription records Felix fulfilling a vow to the goddess Sentona, identified as an Augusta.

EDCS-30400621.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the god Silvanus by an individual named Avillius, likely from the Roman period.

EDCS-30400622.text_edition: \n Sextus Ceion(ius) Voltimesis filius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30400622.translation: Sextus Ceionius, son of Voltimesis, willingly fulfilled his vow as deserved.
EDCS-30400622.summary: This is a votive inscription by Sextus Ceionius, fulfilling a vow, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30400623.translation: His mother erected this for Sextus Cliticus.
EDCS-30400623.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a mother for her son, Sextus Cliticus, probably from the Roman period.

EDCS-30600011.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Fortunatae quae vixit nnos XVIII Maximus Ianuari TITVSLAT suae delicatae et sibi posuit libens\n
EDCS-30600011.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Maximus Ianuarius willingly erected this monument, as deserved, for Fortunata, who lived 18 years, his cherished and for himself.
EDCS-30600011.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fortunata, who died at 18, erected by Maximus Ianuarius for her and himself, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30600012.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Maximinae filiae infelici Domitia mater bene merenti\n
EDCS-30600012.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Domitia, the mother, for her unfortunate daughter Maximina, worthy of remembrance.
EDCS-30600012.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an unfortunate daughter named Maximina, erected by her mother Domitia, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30600013.text_edition: \n Petronius et mater Maximilla Titivillae defunctae nnorum XXX filiae infelicissimae titulum posuerunt\n
EDCS-30600013.translation: Petronius and his mother Maximilla erected this monument for their deceased daughter Titivilla, who lived 30 years, their most unhappy child.
EDCS-30600013.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titivilla, erected by her father Petronius and grandmother Maximilla, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30600017.translation: Caius Toberius for his dearest wife Basilla.
EDCS-30600017.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Caius Toberius for his wife Basilla, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30600018.text_edition: \n pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate Blaeso legato pro praetore\n
EDCS-30600018.translation: To Blaesus, legate pro praetore, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power.
EDCS-30600018.summary: This inscription records the titles of a high-ranking Roman official named Blaesus, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.
