EDCS-30600019.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelius filius Aeliae Severae matri suae pientissimae libens posuit defunctae nnorum XXXVII\n
EDCS-30600019.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius willingly erected this for his most dutiful mother, Aelia Severa, deceased at 37 years.
EDCS-30600019.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aelia Severa, who died at 37, erected by her son Aelius, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30600020.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Veloci defuncto nnorum LXXV Iulius Saturninus filius patri posuit\n
EDCS-30600020.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Iulius Saturninus erected this for his father, Gaius Iulius Velox, deceased at 75 years.
EDCS-30600020.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Iulius Velox, who died at 75, erected by his son Iulius Saturninus, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-30600021.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Apludus MEVERTENS Pladomeni filius A magistro - publice\n
EDCS-30600021.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Apludus, son of Pladomenus, from the magistracy, dedicated this publicly.
EDCS-30600021.summary: This is a public dedication to Jupiter made by Apludus, son of Pladomenus, in his role as a magistrate.

EDCS-30600022.text_edition: \n Tritus Germu<l=I>lius Germani filius decurio hic situs est \n
EDCS-30600022.translation: Tritus Germulius, son of Germanus, a decurion, is buried here.
EDCS-30600022.summary: This is a tombstone for Tritus Germulius, son of Germanus, a decurion.

EDCS-30600023.text_edition: \n Aplis Lunnicus Triti filius annorum XV\n
EDCS-30600023.translation: Aplis Lunnicus, son of Tritus, aged fifteen.
EDCS-30600023.summary: This epitaph records the death of Aplis Lunnicus, son of Tritus, at the age of fifteen.

EDCS-30600024.text_edition: \n Publius Cornelius Publi filius Eporediensis miles legionis XI nnorum XXX stipendiorum VIII\n
EDCS-30600024.translation: Publius Cornelius, son of Publius, of Eporedia, a soldier in the Eleventh Legion, aged thirty, with eight years of service.
EDCS-30600024.summary: This epitaph commemorates Publius Cornelius, son of Publius, a soldier of the Eleventh Legion from Eporedia, who died at age thirty after eight years of service.

EDCS-30600025.inscription_type: carmina, tituli operum, tituli sacri, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-30600025.text_edition: \n Conditus infelix in ista sede perenni Principius habitat huic vitae finis in ipsis ter senis misero et quattuor paene peractis annis acer<b=V>a fuit nam studiis iam Romae laetantem invida fortuna repenti funere mersit cuiusque reversum crudeli funere corpus exequitur genitor iniquo cum honore sepulcri iam securi suo <g=C>audete munere Manes Elysios per campos et dulcia prata vagantes \n
EDCS-30600025.translation: Unfortunate Conditus, dwelling eternally in this place, Principius lived a miserable life, ending in his thirty-fourth year. Envious fortune suddenly submerged him in death as he was rejoicing in his studies in Rome. His father, carrying out the funeral rites with undue honor, had his body returned. Now, Manes, rejoice in your granted gifts, wandering through Elysian fields and sweet meadows.
EDCS-30600025.summary: This is a funerary epigram for Conditus, lamenting his untimely death at age 34 while he was studying in Rome, and addressing the underworld spirits.

EDCS-30600026.text_edition: \n Tiberius Claudius Tiberi <f=I>(ilius) Quadratus Claudio Aequo -- nnorum XXXV sibi et Pelsoniae Tertullae uxori testamento fieri iussit \n
EDCS-30600026.translation: Tiberius Claudius Quadratus, son of Tiberius, to his adoptive father Claudius Aequus, aged thirty-five, ordered this to be made for himself and his wife Pelsonia Tertulla in his will.
EDCS-30600026.summary: This tombstone, commissioned by Tiberius Claudius Quadratus for himself and his wife, is dedicated to his adoptive father, Claudius Aequus, and dates to his lifetime.

EDCS-30600027.inscription_type: carmina, inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-30600027.text_edition: \n Publius Fannius Aniensis Cremona eques legionis XI annorum XXIX stipendiorum XII hic situs est frater fratri posuit hospes resiste et tumulum contempla meum morare gressum litteras nostras lege mando et stiloilos alter [ut sciat ------ II legem --- nam? fatus aliquid? ex? improviso facit titulum intuere scriptum quo fato occidi rumore sancto Illyrici iaceo in solo infunde lacrumas sic quisquis es mihi misericors aetate cuncta ceciderunt mecum brevi aequo animo vive vitam quoad fieri potest OIAM CNORIS AL R quaedam SADEMII nomen in P quorum prima nocte vivo fieri satis \n
EDCS-30600027.translation: Publius Fannius, of Anienis, from Cremona, a cavalryman in the Eleventh Legion, aged twenty-nine, with twelve years of service, is buried here. A brother placed this for his brother. Stranger, stop and contemplate my tomb, delay your steps, read our letters, and I entrust this to my stylus. [lacuna] The law II [lacuna] for in law? [lacuna] does something suddenly. Look at this inscription, written about my fate. I lie in the sacred soil of Illyricum. Pour out tears for me, whoever you are, mercifully. All things decay in time, like me. Live life with an even mind as much as possible.
EDCS-30600027.summary: This is a tombstone set up by a brother for Publius Fannius, a cavalryman in the Eleventh Legion from Cremona, who died in Illyricum, urging travelers to pause and reflect on mortality.

EDCS-30600028.text_edition: \n Lucius Icconius Luci filius Aniensis Surio Caesaraugusta miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis centuria Titi Silvani stipendiorum XXIII testamento fieri iussit heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-30600028.translation: Lucius Icconius Surio, son of Lucius, of Anienis, from Caesaraugusta, a soldier in the Eleventh Claudian Legion, dutiful and loyal, in the century of Titus Silvanus, with twenty-three years of service, ordered this to be made in his will; the heir took care to have it constructed.
EDCS-30600028.summary: This tombstone was built according to the will of Lucius Icconius Surio, son of Lucius, a soldier in the Eleventh Claudian Legion from Caesaraugusta, who served for twenty-three years.

EDCS-30600029.text_edition: \n Aulus Sentius Auli filius Pomptina Arretio veteranus legionis XI hic situs est testamento fieri iussit hic est occisus finibus Varvarinorum in agello secus Titum flumen ad Petram Longam faciendum curavit heres Quintus Calventius Luci filius Vitalis\n
EDCS-30600029.translation: Aulus Sentius, son of Aulus, of Pomptina, from Arretium, a veteran of the Eleventh Legion, is buried here. He ordered this to be made in his will. He was killed here in the territory of the Varvarini in a small field near the river Titu, at Petra Longa. The heir, Quintus Calventius Vitalis, son of Lucius, took care to have it constructed.
EDCS-30600029.summary: This tombstone commemorates Aulus Sentius, a veteran of the Eleventh Legion from Arretium, who was killed in Illyricum and was buried there, with the monument constructed by his heir.

EDCS-30600031.text_edition: \n Sexto Saenio Sexti liberto Hermeroti nnorum LXX Saenia Campana marito suo posuit et sibi hic situs est\n
EDCS-30600031.translation: Saenia Campana placed this for her husband Hermerotes, freedman of Sextus Saenius, aged seventy, and for herself. He is buried here.
EDCS-30600031.summary: This is a tombstone for Hermerotes, freedman of Sextus Saenius, and for his wife Saenia Campana, placed by Saenia Campana.

EDCS-30600032.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Parthenope aviae suae bene merenti quae vixit \n
EDCS-30600032.translation: To the Divine Spirits, for Parthenope, his well-deserving grandmother, who lived...
EDCS-30600032.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a grandmother named Parthenope, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30600033.text_edition: \n LICISI qvi bixit! s plus minus annis XLlia Trio titulum \n
EDCS-30600033.translation: Licisus, who lived about 40 years, Trio made this monument...
EDCS-30600033.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Licisus, who died at approximately 40 years of age, erected by Trio, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30600034.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Augusto sacrum Caius Valius Festus conditor vineae huius loci qui nunc Valianus a Festo dicitur aeternumque tenet per saecula nomen voto succepto sic aram adampliavit et tauro immolando dedicavit\n
EDCS-30600034.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Best and Greatest, August. Caius Valius Festus, founder of this vineyard, which is now called Valianus after Festus and eternally holds the name through the ages, having undertaken a vow, thus enlarged this altar and dedicated it by sacrificing a bull.
EDCS-30600034.summary: This is a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Augustus by Caius Valius Festus, who expanded an altar and dedicated it to mark his vineyard, named after himself, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30600035.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Crescentia mater Crescentio filio infelicissimo qui vixit annos VII menses III\n
EDCS-30600035.translation: To the Divine Spirits, for Crescentia, a most unhappy son, who lived 7 years and 3 months.
EDCS-30600035.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Crescentius, a child who died at age 7 years and 3 months, dedicated by his mother, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30600036.text_edition: \n Placido infelicissimo annorum XXX Dicatina mater\n
EDCS-30600036.translation: For Placido, a most unhappy [son], 30 years of age. Dicatina, mother.
EDCS-30600036.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Placido, who died at age 30, dedicated by his mother, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30600037.text_edition: \n Lucius T Lysonis filius Longinus testamento iussit fieri\n
EDCS-30600037.translation: Lucius Longinus, son of T. Lyson, ordered this to be made in his will.
EDCS-30600037.summary: This is a funerary inscription commissioned by Lucius Longinus, son of T. Lyson, through his will, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30600084.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae Baetae defunctae annorum LXX Aureliae Aioia et Pinenta matri pro parte et Marcus Aurelii Memor et Baeta Aurelio Vindici filio huius Baetae defuncto annorum XXXV patri pro parte procurante i<n=M> parte huius tituli et praebente sumptus Aurelia Supera matre sua \n
EDCS-30600084.translation: To the Divine Spirits, for Aurelia Baeta, deceased, 70 years of age. Aurelia Aioia and Pinenta, for their part, and Marcus Aurelius Memor and Baeta for their part for their son Aurelius Vindicius, deceased, 35 years of age, the father, supervised by [M.?] of this title and providing the expenses, Aurelia Supera, his mother.
EDCS-30600084.summary: This is a complex funerary inscription for Aurelia Baeta and her son Aurelius Vindicius, with multiple family members contributing and dedicating the monument, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30600085.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ulpia Enena vixit annos XL hic sita est Publius Aurelius Quintinus nepti piissimae posuit\n
EDCS-30600085.translation: To the Divine Spirits, Ulpia Enena lived 40 years, she rests here. Publius Aurelius Quintinus placed this for his most devoted granddaughter.
EDCS-30600085.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ulpia Enena, who died at age 40, erected by her devoted grandfather, Publius Aurelius Quintinus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30600086.text_edition: \n Libero Patri Titus Aurelius Proculus libens posuit\n
EDCS-30600086.translation: For Liber Pater, Titus Aurelius Proculus willingly placed this.
EDCS-30600086.summary: This is a dedication to Liber Pater (Bacchus) by Titus Aurelius Proculus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30600087.text_edition: \n Pro salute Augusti Iulius Nepotianus ex voto pro pietate\n
EDCS-30600087.translation: For the health of Augustus, Julius Nepotianus, in accordance with his vow and piety.
EDCS-30600087.summary: This is a dedication made by Julius Nepotianus for the health of the Emperor Augustus, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-30600088.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Aurelius Menander \n
EDCS-30600088.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Titus Aurelius Menander [dedicated this].
EDCS-30600088.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter by Titus Aurelius Menander.

EDCS-30600089.text_edition: \n Dianae Augustae Titus Statius Crispinus decurio equitum cohortis II Delmatarum\n
EDCS-30600089.translation: To the August Diana, Titus Statius Crispinus, decurion of the cavalry of the II Cohort of Dalmatians, [dedicated this].
EDCS-30600089.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Diana Augusta by Titus Statius Crispinus, a military officer.

EDCS-30600090.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo et Genio loci sacro sic dicavit Iulius Maternus |(centurio) cohortis VIII voluntariorum Laterano et Rufino consulibus\n
EDCS-30600090.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, and to the divine spirit of the place, Iulius Maternus, centurion of the VIII Cohort of Volunteers, dedicated this in the consulship of Lateranus and Rufinus.
EDCS-30600090.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter and the genius loci by Iulius Maternus, a centurion, during the consulship of Lateranus and Rufinus.

EDCS-30600121.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Quinto Statio Festo vixit nnos II menses II dies XXII Quintus Statius Eperastus filio\n
EDCS-30600121.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus Statius Festus lived for 2 years, 2 months, and 22 days. Quintus Statius Eperastus [dedicated this] to his son.
EDCS-30600121.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the short life of Quintus Statius Festus, dedicated by his father.

EDCS-30600123.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo advento sic et pro salute et victorias sic Impperatorum nnostrorum Auggustorum populus posuit\n
EDCS-30600123.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, for the arrival, for the safety, and for the victories of our Emperors, the people dedicated this.
EDCS-30600123.summary: This inscription is a public dedication to Jupiter, likely made for the well-being and military success of the emperors.

EDCS-30600124.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Aemilius Titia<n=II>us IIvir quinquennalis libens posuit \n
EDCS-30600124.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, Marcus Aemilius Titius, quinquennial duovir, gladly dedicated this.
EDCS-30600124.summary: This inscription is a voluntary dedication to Jupiter by Marcus Aemilius Titius, a quinquennial duovir.

EDCS-30600125.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Fulguratori Marcus Aemilius Antonius IIvir libens posuit\n
EDCS-30600125.translation: To Jupiter, Best and Greatest, the Thunderer, Marcus Aemilius Antonius, duovir, gladly dedicated this.
EDCS-30600125.summary: This inscription records a voluntary dedication to Jupiter Fulgurator by Marcus Aemilius Antonius, a duovir.

EDCS-30600126.text_edition: \n quinquennali et sacerdoti sacerdotali et Aureli? VM Publius Aelius Firminianus \n
EDCS-30600126.translation: Publius Aelius Firminianus, quinquennial priest and priestly official, and Aurelia VM, [dedicated this].
EDCS-30600126.summary: This inscription records a dedication by Publius Aelius Firminianus, a quinquennial priest, and Aurelia VM.

EDCS-30600128.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Tito Aelio Scaeviano qui vixit annos LXX Aelii Titianus et Aelianus filii posuerunt pro pietate\n
EDCS-30600128.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Tito Aelius Scaevianus, who lived for 70 years. His sons Aelii Titianus and Aelianus dedicated this out of filial duty.
EDCS-30600128.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Tito Aelius Scaevianus, dedicated by his sons out of piety.

EDCS-30600129.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelio Apro qui vixit annos L et Aureliae Candidae coniugigi\n
EDCS-30600129.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Aurelius Aper, who lived for 50 years, and for his wife Aurelia Candida, [this was dedicated].
EDCS-30600129.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Aurelius Aper and his wife Aurelia Candida.

EDCS-30600130.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aurelio Apro qui vixit annos XXXX Lucida coniugi pientissimo posuit\n
EDCS-30600130.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucida placed this for her most devoted husband, Aurelius Aper, who lived 40 years.
EDCS-30600130.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelius Aper, set up by his wife Lucida, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30600131.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aureliae Pantoni quae vixit annos XXXX Titus Aurelius Quintus coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-30600131.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Titus Aurelius Quintus placed this for his deserving wife, Aurelia Panto, who lived 40 years.
EDCS-30600131.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Panto, set up by her husband Titus Aurelius Quintus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30600132.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aureliae Tittoni quae vixit annos XLV Aurelius Carus coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-30600132.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aurelius Carus placed this for his deserving wife, Aurelia Tittonis, who lived 45 years.
EDCS-30600132.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Tittonis, set up by her husband Aurelius Carus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30600133.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aureliae Tritanoni quae vixit annos XXX Aurelius Maxsimus sic coniugi \n
EDCS-30600133.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aurelius Maxsimus thus for his wife, Aurelia Tritanonis, who lived 30 years.
EDCS-30600133.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Tritanonis, set up by her husband Aurelius Maxsimus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30600134.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aureliae Vendoni quae vixit annos XXIII Titus Aurelius Us \n
EDCS-30600134.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Titus Aurelius Us for his wife, Aurelia Vendo, who lived 23 years.
EDCS-30600134.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Vendo, set up by her husband Titus Aurelius Us, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30600135.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Sextus Statius Restitutus sibi et Aureliae Testoni coniugi posuit\n
EDCS-30600135.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Sextus Statius Restitutus placed this for himself and for his wife, Aurelia Testonis.
EDCS-30600135.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Statius Restitutus and his wife Aurelia Testonis, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30600137.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Catoni Statariae Tuionis quae vixit annos L Quintus Aelius Statianus coniugi bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-30600137.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Quintus Aelius Statianus placed this for his deserving wife, Catonis Statariae Tuionis, who lived 50 years.
EDCS-30600137.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Catonis Statariae Tuionis, set up by her husband Quintus Aelius Statianus, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30600138.text_edition: \n et Fusca M D P et sibi viva posuit\n
EDCS-30600138.translation: and Fusca placed this for herself and for [her husband] while alive.
EDCS-30600138.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fusca, who set it up for herself and her husband during her lifetime, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30600139.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Statiae Frunitae Statius Vindemiator maritus bene merenti fecit quae vixit annos LVI\n
EDCS-30600139.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Statius Vindemiator made this for his deserving wife, Statia Frunita, who lived 56 years.
EDCS-30600139.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Statia Frunita, set up by her husband Statius Vindemiator, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30600140.text_edition: \n Caius Statius Cai filius Sergia Celsus evocatus Augusti donis donatus bis corona aurea torquibus phaleris armillis ob triumphos belli Dacici ab Imperatore Caesare Nerva Traiano Augusto Germanico Dacico Parthico Optimo |(centurio) legionis VII geminae in Hispania testamento poni iussit et epulo dedicavit\n
EDCS-30600140.translation: Caius Statius Celsus, son of Caius, of the Sergia tribe, a recalled soldier, honored with the Emperor Caesar Nerva Traianus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus Parthicus's gifts twice with a golden crown, neck chains, military decorations, and arm bands for victories in the Dacian War, ordered this to be set up in his will and dedicated it with a feast, (he was) a centurion of the VII Gemina legion in Hispania.
EDCS-30600140.summary: This is a military diploma or funerary monument honoring Caius Statius Celsus, a centurion of the VII Gemina legion, who received multiple honors from Emperor Trajan for his service in the Dacian Wars, dating to Trajan's reign (98-117 AD).

EDCS-30600564.text_edition: \n Caio Statio Cai filio Sergia Restituto annorum XV mensium VI horarum VI et semis horae Caius Statius Valens <e=F>t Caesia Secunda parentes filio posuerunt \n
EDCS-30600564.translation: To Gaius Statius Restitutus, son of Gaius, of the Sergia tribe, aged fifteen years, six months, and six and a half hours; Gaius Statius Valens and Caesia Secunda, his parents, set this up for their son.
EDCS-30600564.summary: This is a tombstone for a young boy, Gaius Statius Restitutus, set up by his parents.

EDCS-30600565.text_edition: \n Papia Luci filia Brocchina sacerdos divae Augustae testamento poni iussit\n
EDCS-30600565.translation: Papia Brocchina, daughter of Lucius, priestess of the deified Augusta, ordered this to be set up in her will.
EDCS-30600565.summary: This records a funerary monument ordered by Papia Brocchina, a priestess, in her will.

EDCS-30600566.text_edition: \n Lucius Herennius Luci filius Papiria Muliade sic veteranus legionis VII annorum LX stipendiorum XXX hic situs est\n
EDCS-30600566.translation: Lucius Herennius Muliade, son of Lucius, of the Papiria tribe, a veteran of the VII legion, aged sixty years, with thirty years of service, lies here.
EDCS-30600566.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Herennius Muliade, a veteran of the VII legion.

EDCS-30600567.text_edition: \n Marcus Plaetorius P miles cohortis VIII voluntariorum centuria Artani Marcelli Noni Valentis centuria easdem Nonio Valenti bene merenti titulum posuit annorum XXX stipendiorum VII\n
EDCS-30600567.translation: Marcus Plaetorius, a soldier of the VIII cohort of volunteers, in the century of Artanius Marcellus Noni Valentis, erected this monument to Nonius Valens of the same century, for his distinguished service, aged thirty years with seven years of service.
EDCS-30600567.summary: This is a monument erected by a soldier to a fellow soldier, Nonius Valens, likely in the early Imperial period.

EDCS-30600568.text_edition: \n Primus Titti filius tubicen domo Caturix miles cohortis III Alpinorum annorum XLIIX stipendiorum XXIII hic situs est testamento fieri iussit Lucius optio et Tullius veteranus heredes posuerunt\n
EDCS-30600568.translation: Primus, son of Tittius, a trumpeter, from Caturix, a soldier of the III cohort of Alpiners, aged forty-eight years with twenty-three years of service, lies here; Lucius the optio and Tullius the veteran, his heirs, ordered this to be made by will and set it up.
EDCS-30600568.summary: This is a tombstone for Primus, a soldier and trumpeter, who died after 23 years of service, with his heirs setting up the monument.

EDCS-30600569.text_edition: \n Lucio Livio Rufino filio pientissimo Aelia \n
EDCS-30600569.translation: To Lucius Livius Rufinus, a most dutiful son. Aelia.
EDCS-30600569.summary: This is a dedication to Lucius Livius Rufinus, set up by Aelia, likely a parent or other relative.

EDCS-30600570.text_edition: \n Mopsus Sexti Vari servus nnum XIIX hic situs \n
EDCS-30600570.translation: Mopsus, slave of Sextus Varius, aged eighteen years, lies here.
EDCS-30600570.summary: This is a tombstone for Mopsus, a slave of Sextus Varius.

EDCS-30600574.text_edition: \n Publius Valerius Publi filius Rufus Lymphis sacrum\n
EDCS-30600574.translation: Publius Valerius Rufus, son of Publius, sacred to the Lymphs.
EDCS-30600574.summary: This records a dedication to the Nymphs by Publius Valerius Rufus.

EDCS-30600575.text_edition: \n Imperatore Caesare Marco Aurelio Antonino Augusto pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate XXIIII consule III patre patriae cohors II |(milliaria) Delmatarum pedes DCCC in his turris I sub cura Luci Annaei Serviliani tribuni vice tertia\n
EDCS-30600575.translation: Under Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, in his 24th year of tribunician power, Consul for the 3rd time, Father of the Fatherland, 800 feet of the II Cohort (Milliaria) of Delmatae, including 1 tower, under the command of Lucius Annaeus Servilianus, tribune, for the third time.
EDCS-30600575.summary: This inscription records a construction project, possibly a fortification or road, by the II Cohort of Delmatae during the reign of Marcus Aurelius.

EDCS-30600577.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caio Iulio Saturnino militi legionis X geminae beneficiario consularis defuncto nnorum XXXVIII Caius Iulius Pusinnio frater et Domitia Marci filia Bellica coniux\n
EDCS-30600577.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Gaius Julius Saturninus, soldier of the X Gemina legion, a beneficiary of a consul, who died aged thirty-eight years. His brother Caius Julius Pusinnio and Domitia Bellica, daughter of Marcus, his wife, set this up.
EDCS-30600577.summary: This is a tombstone for Gaius Julius Saturninus, a soldier of the X Gemina legion, set up by his brother and wife.

EDCS-30600578.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Aelius sevir Salonitanorum? --- sibi se vivo posuit et Claudiae carissimae concubinae? --- \n
EDCS-30600578.translation: To the divine shades. Publius Aelius, a sevir of Salona, erected this for himself while alive and for his dearest concubine Claudia.
EDCS-30600578.summary: This is a tomb inscription by Publius Aelius, likely from Salona, for himself and his concubine, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30600579.text_edition: \n Caio Titio Cai filio Velina Primo aedili IIIIviro iure dicundo Argyrus libertus fecit\n
EDCS-30600579.translation: Argyrus, a freedman, made this for Gaius Titius Primus, son of Gaius, of the Velina tribe, aedile and duovir for administering justice.
EDCS-30600579.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by a freedman for a local magistrate, Gaius Titius Primus, likely in a Roman town.

EDCS-30600582.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Paenio Fido collegae bene merenti\n
EDCS-30600582.translation: To the divine shades, for Paenius Fidus, a colleague who deserved well.
EDCS-30600582.summary: This is a tomb inscription honoring a well-deserving colleague named Paenius Fidus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30600585.text_edition: \n Quinto Aeronio Firmino defuncto nnorum XIIII mensium IIII Quintus Aeronius Crescens et Caetrania Firma parentes filio pientissimo libertis libertabusque suis invida Parcarum series livorque malignus bis septena mea<e> ruperunt stamina lucis parcite iam lachrimis sic miseri solique parentes sat fletus vestros prima favilla bibit corpus habe<n>t cineres animam sacer abstulit aer\n
EDCS-30600585.translation: To Quintus Aeronius Firmus, who died at the age of 14 years and 4 months. Quintus Aeronius Crescens and Caetrania Firma, parents, erected this for their most devoted son, and for their freedmen and freedwomen. The envious series of the Fates and their malicious spite broke the threads of light twice seven times for me. Spare your tears now; thus, unhappy and orphaned parents, your tears are enough; the first spark has drunk them. Let the body have [a place]; the sacred air has carried away the soul.
EDCS-30600585.summary: This is a poignant epitaph for a young boy, Quintus Aeronius Firmus, erected by his grieving parents, featuring elaborate poetic language about fate and death.

EDCS-30600594.text_edition: \n Iulius Martyrius et Aurelia Procula parentes Gelliae filiae dulcissimae quae vixit annos XV menses VIIII dies V bene merenti posuerunt et sibi\n
EDCS-30600594.translation: Julius Martyrius and Aurelia Procula, parents, erected this for their most sweet daughter Gelliah, who lived 15 years, 9 months, and 5 days, who deserved well, and for themselves.
EDCS-30600594.summary: This is a tomb inscription by parents for their deceased daughter, Gelliah, and for themselves, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30700715.text_edition: \n Avitio Augurino militi legionis XIIII geminae Martiae victricis Ulpius Calla miles legionis eiusdem heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-30700715.translation: Ulpius Calla, a soldier of the same legion, the heir, saw to the construction of this [monument] for Avitus Augurinus, a soldier of the 14th Gemina Martia Victrix legion.
EDCS-30700715.summary: This is a military epitaph for a soldier named Avitus Augurinus, with the monument erected by a fellow soldier and heir from the same legion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30700721.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Helviae Marroniae defunctae nnorum XXX Vitalis posuit\n
EDCS-30700721.translation: To the divine shades. Vitalis erected this for Helvia Marronia, deceased, aged 30 years.
EDCS-30700721.summary: This is a tomb inscription erected by Vitalis for Helvia Marronia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30700724.summary: This is a simple tombstone inscription for a young woman named Rhodope, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30700729.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marci Aureli Marci fili Lucani decurionis municipii Magni IIviri iure dicundo? \n
EDCS-30700729.translation: To the divine shades. For Marcus Aurelius Lucanus, son of Marcus, decurion of the municipality, duovir for administering justice, [erected by his family].
EDCS-30700729.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aurelius Lucanus, a local magistrate and decurion, likely from a Roman municipality.

EDCS-30800055.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Statius Victor Brizidia voto libens posuit\n
EDCS-30800055.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Statius Victor erected this gladly in fulfillment of a vow.
EDCS-30800055.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Statius Victor, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30800057.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum Marcus Aemilius Antonius votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30800057.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Marcus Aemilius Antonius willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30800057.summary: A dedication to the god Silvanus Augustus by Marcus Aemilius Antonius, likely a private vow fulfilled. Dating is uncertain but likely Imperial period.

EDCS-30800059.summary: A simple dedication by Ol Cambrianus, possibly a funerary monument, erected during the Imperial period.

EDCS-30800060.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Publio Aelio Pladomeno Carvanio annorum praefecto civitatium Pirustarum praefecto iure dicundo municipii Aureli Slo et Aelia<e> Pantoni coniugi eius viva<e> parentibus pientissimis Ate<li=IL>(ii) Titus Lupus et Firminus heredes ponendum curaverunt hic situs est \n
EDCS-30800060.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Aelius Pladomenus, prefect of the Pirustae tribes and magistrate of the municipium of Aurelia Slo, and Aelia Pantonia, his wife, for their most dutiful parents, Ate[li=IL](ii) Titus Lupus and Firminus, their heirs, arranged for this to be erected; he is buried here.
EDCS-30800060.summary: A funerary monument for Publius Aelius Pladomenus, a Roman official, and his wife Aelia Pantonia, erected by their heirs, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30800061.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Tito Aelio Martiali decurioni municipii IIviro defuncto Salona annorum XL Aurelia Varra coniugi pro pietate\n
EDCS-30800061.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Varra, for her husband Titus Aelius Martialis, decurion of the municipium, former chief magistrate (IIvir), deceased at 40 years old, out of duty.
EDCS-30800061.summary: A funerary dedication by Aurelia Varra for her husband Titus Aelius Martialis, a local official, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30800062.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publius Aelius Maximus decurio municipii Malvesatium IIvir functus memoriae Publi Aeli Proculi defuncti annorum XXXVI et Publi Aeli Quintiliani defuncti annorum XXV filiorum piissimorum et Aureliae Annae neptis dulcissimae defunctae nnorum III et sibi et Aureliae Marcianae coniugi fideli et obsequentissimae vivis fecit\n
EDCS-30800062.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Publius Aelius Maximus, decurion of the municipium of Malvesatium, former chief magistrate (IIvir), in memory of his most dutiful sons Publius Aelius Proculus, deceased at 36 years old, and Publius Aelius Quintilianus, deceased at 25 years old, and his sweetest granddaughter Aurelia Anna, deceased at 3 years old, and for himself and for his faithful and most obedient wife Aurelia Marciana, he made this while alive.
EDCS-30800062.summary: A funerary monument erected by Publius Aelius Maximus for his family members and himself, including his wife and children, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30800064.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelio Victori decurio municipii Malvesatium defuncto annorum XXXVII Aurelia Nindia filia et Aurelia Paulina marito pro pietate\n
EDCS-30800064.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Nindia and Aurelia Paulina, for their husband Aelius Victor, decurion of the municipium of Malvesatium, deceased at 37 years old, out of duty.
EDCS-30800064.summary: A funerary dedication by Aurelia Nindia and Aurelia Paulina for their husband Aelius Victor, a local official, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30800065.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Capitoni decurioni IIviro municipii Malvesatium defuncto annorum X FLIEIV et Severa sorores et Ulpiae Ursae annorum LX matri pro pietate\n
EDCS-30800065.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. FLIEIV and Severa, sisters, and for their mother Ulpia Ursa, 60 years old, out of duty. [Also for] Capito, decurion, former chief magistrate (IIvir) of the municipium of Malvesatium, deceased.
EDCS-30800065.summary: A funerary monument erected by sisters for their mother and brother, Capito, a local official, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30800066.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus Aurelius Victor vixit annos I Aurelius Memor A\n
EDCS-30800066.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Titus Aurelius Victor lived 1 year. Aurelius Memor [erected this].
EDCS-30800066.summary: A funerary dedication for a child, Titus Aurelius Victor, erected by Aurelius Memor, likely his parent, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30800067.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Flaviae Tattae libertae et nutrici defunctae annorum L Flavia Prisca clarissima femina et Dazio fratri ipsius vilico vivo posuit\n
EDCS-30800067.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Flavia Tatta, freedwoman and nurse, deceased at 50 years old. Flavia Prisca, a most distinguished woman, and for her brother Dazio, bailiff, alive, placed this.
EDCS-30800067.summary: A funerary dedication for a freedwoman and nurse, Flavia Tatta, erected by Flavia Prisca and her brother Dazio, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-30800069.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Parthino Caius Iulius Rufus tribunus cohortis I milliariae Delmatarum pro salute [[]] []i libens posuit\n
EDCS-30800069.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Parthian. Caius Iulius Rufus, tribune of the I Cohort of Dalmatians, voluntarily placed this for the safety of [his] [...]
EDCS-30800069.summary: A military dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Parthinus by Caius Iulius Rufus, a tribune, likely for the safety of his men or himself, dating to the Roman military presence.

EDCS-30800070.text_edition: \n Libero Patri Cap Titus Aurelius Provincialis ob honorem IIviratus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30800070.translation: To Liber Pater, Titus Aurelius Provincialis, having fulfilled his vow for the office of duovir, willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-30800070.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Liber Pater by Titus Aurelius Provincialis upon completing his term as duovir, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-30800072.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Flavius Albanus IIvir quinquennalis votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30800072.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Flavius Albanus, quinquennial duovir, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30800072.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Flavius Albanus, a quinquennial duovir, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-30800073.text_edition: \n Libero Baccho Publius Aelius Clemens veteranus E AETSOSE bene votum solvit Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius Clemens libens posuit V sua pecunia TAE LV E votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30800073.translation: To Liber Bacchus, Publius Aelius Clemens, a veteran, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow; Publius Aelius Clemens willingly set this up at his own expense to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, willingly and deservedly fulfilling his vow.
EDCS-30800073.summary: This inscription records a double vow to Liber Bacchus and Jupiter Optimus Maximus by the veteran Publius Aelius Clemens, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-30800074.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Caius Iulius Maximus veteranus cohortis I Belgarum vivus fecit sibi ed sic Avilliae Amabili coniugi suae\n
EDCS-30800074.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Caius Iulius Maximus, a veteran of the 1st Cohort of Belgae, made this for himself while alive, and thus for his wife Avillia Amabilis.
EDCS-30800074.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Caius Iulius Maximus, a veteran, and his wife Avillia Amabilis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30800075.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Gallus defunctus annorum LXV et Aurelia Madussa defuncta annorum LX Aurelius Capito parentibus pro pietate\n
EDCS-30800075.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, Aurelius Gallus, deceased aged 65, and Aurelia Madussa, deceased aged 60; Aurelius Capito for his parents, out of filial piety.
EDCS-30800075.summary: This funerary inscription honors Aurelius Gallus and Aurelia Madussa, dedicated by their son Aurelius Capito out of piety, likely from the 3rd century CE.

EDCS-30800076.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication or a funerary marker, mentioning the name Clementi, with an uncertain date.

EDCS-30800077.text_edition: \n Sextus Caesernius Romanus beneficiarius consularis legionis XIIII geminae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-30800077.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Sextus Caesernius Romanus, a beneficiarius of the consular legate of the 14th Legion Gemina, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-30800077.summary: This votive inscription records the fulfillment of a vow by Sextus Caesernius Romanus, a soldier of the 14th Legion Gemina, likely from the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-30800078.text_edition: \n MVLD cohortis XI Gallorum domo Patavi nnorum XLVI stipendiorum XV\n
EDCS-30800078.translation: From the 11th Cohort of Gauls, from the town of Patavium, aged 46, with 15 years of service.
EDCS-30800078.summary: This is a fragmentary military inscription recording the origin, age, and service length of a soldier from the 11th Cohort of Gauls, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30800079.text_edition: \n Publius Mescenius Pastor Publius Mescenius Pothinianus IIIIIIviri magistri Mercuriales ob honorem\n
EDCS-30800079.translation: Publius Mescenius Pastor and Publius Mescenius Pothinianus, IIIIviri magistrates of the Mercuriales, for the office.
EDCS-30800079.summary: This inscription records an offering or dedication by two IIIIviri magistrates of the collegium Mercurialium, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30800080.text_edition: \n Publius Spedius Publi libertus Adiutor Publius Lastus Caius Minidius Cai libertus Hieronomus Lucius Cer\n
EDCS-30800080.translation: Publius Spedius Adiutor, freedman of Publius; Publius Lastus; Caius Minidius Hieronomus, freedman of Cai; Lucius Cer[---]
EDCS-30800080.summary: This inscription appears to be a list of names, possibly part of a dedication, tombstone, or legal document, with an uncertain date and context.

EDCS-30800082.text_edition: \n Lucius Trebius Secundus praefectus castrorum inter Onastinos et Narestinos terminos posuit iussu Luci Volusi Saturnini legati pro praetore Cai C[ae]saris A[ugusti] Germanici ex sentententia quam iis athi<b=R>ito sic consilio dixit \n
EDCS-30800082.translation: Lucius Trebius Secundus, prefect of the camp, set up the boundary stones between the Onastini and Narestini peoples by order of Lucius Volusius Saturninus, governor with praetorian authority, in accordance with the judgment he gave to them in council.
EDCS-30800082.summary: This inscription records a military officer, Lucius Trebius Secundus, setting boundary markers between two peoples, acting under the authority of the governor Lucius Volusius Saturninus, likely in the 1st century AD.

EDCS-30800083.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aelii Pinnes et Temus parentes posuerunt filio pientissimo Pinnio militi legionis secundaes sic defuncto Bassianis annorum XXXII\n
EDCS-30800083.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. Aelius Pinnes and Temus, his parents, set this up for their most dutiful son, Pinnio, a soldier in the Second Legion, who died in Bassianis at the age of 32.
EDCS-30800083.summary: This is a tombstone set up by parents for their deceased son, Pinnio, a soldier in the 2nd Legion, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30800085.text_edition: \n Isante Suro coniugi inconparabili sic Dis Manibus Victorinae Annae bene merenti praecepto dominico factum miseram parentes perdederunt sic annis trigi<n=T>ta \n
EDCS-30800085.translation: To Isante Sura, her incomparable husband. To the spirits of the underworld. To Victorina Anna, well-deserving, made by ordinance of her master. Her parents lost her, aged thirty.
EDCS-30800085.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a deceased wife, Victorina Anna, by her husband and parents, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30800341.text_edition: \n Iunoni Augustae Appuleia Marci filia Quinta suo et Luci Turpilii Brocchi Liciniani filii nomine testamento poni iussit\n
EDCS-30800341.translation: To Juno Augusta. Appuleia Quinta, daughter of Marcus, ordered this to be set up in her own name and in the name of Lucius Turpilius Brocchus Licinianus, by her will.
EDCS-30800341.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Juno Augusta, commissioned in a will by Appuleia Quinta, likely during the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30900002.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication to the god Hermes, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30900015.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly a dedication to Jupiter, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30900090.summary: This is a dedication to a beloved wife named Eucarpia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30900091.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Severinae infelicissimae defunctae nnum plus minus XL incomparabili conservae Poetus bene merenti posuit \n
EDCS-30900091.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. To Severina, most unfortunate, who died aged more or less 40. Poetus set this up for his incomparable, deserving slave.
EDCS-30900091.summary: This is a tombstone dedicated by Poetus for his deceased slave Severina, who died around age 40, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30900092.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-30900092.text_edition: \n Valentinianus memoratus est <c=Q>um coniuge sua Sabbatia super E piscina sua testatus est ne aliquis velet sic alium IOC acepite \n
EDCS-30900092.translation: Valentinianus, along with his wife Sabbatia, made his will concerning his fishpond, stating that no one should allow another to take it.
EDCS-30900092.summary: This inscription records a legal testament made by Valentinianus and his wife Sabbatia regarding their fishpond, likely from the late Roman period.

EDCS-30900093.text_edition: \n Lucius Rustius Picens tribunus militum vovit praefectus equitum fecit\n
EDCS-30900093.translation: Lucius Rustius Picens, a military tribune, vowed [this], and as a cavalry prefect, he carried it out.
EDCS-30900093.summary: This inscription records Lucius Rustius Picens fulfilling a vow made when he was a military tribune, which he completed as a cavalry prefect, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-30900095.text_edition: \n cus memor fratri suo bene amanti posuit qui fuit annorum numero XXX \n
EDCS-30900095.translation: Cus placed this for his well-loved brother, who was thirty years old.
EDCS-30900095.summary: A funerary monument recording the death of Cus's brother, who died at age 30.

EDCS-30900099.translation: To the Unconquered [god], Octavius Geminus [dedicated this].
EDCS-30900099.summary: A dedication to the unconquered deity by Octavius Geminus.

EDCS-30900100.text_edition: \n Lucio Baebio Opiavi filio Sergia Oplo Malavico aedili decurioni IIviro nnorum LX et Seiae Opli filiae Tertullae uxori eius nnorum LV testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-30900100.translation: He ordered this to be made by will for Lucius Baebius Opiavus, son of Opiavus, of the Sergia tribe, aedile, decurion, and twice-chancellor, aged 60, and for his wife Seia Tertulla, daughter of Oplus, aged 55.
EDCS-30900100.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by will for Lucius Baebius Opiavus, a local official, and his wife Seia Tertulla.

EDCS-30900101.text_edition: \n Publio Octavio filio Sergia Certo decurioni Publius Octavius Placidus Luci filius frater vivus fecit\n
EDCS-30900101.translation: Publius Octavius Placidus, son of Lucius, made this for his brother Publius Octavius, son of Certus, a decurion, while he was alive.
EDCS-30900101.summary: A monument erected during the lifetime of the dedicant, Publius Octavius Placidus, for his brother Publius Octavius.

EDCS-30900102.text_edition: \n IIII Vibius Silvester filio piissimo vivus fecit\n
EDCS-30900102.translation: Vibius Silvester made this for his most dutiful son while he was alive.
EDCS-30900102.summary: A monument erected during the lifetime of the dedicant, Vibius Silvester, for his son.

EDCS-30900104.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sacri
EDCS-30900104.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto sacrum Valerius Valerianus miles cum insisterem ad capitella co<l=I>umnarum ad thermas Licinianas quas sic fiunt Sirmi votum libens solvit \n
EDCS-30900104.translation: Valerius Valerianus, a soldier, gladly fulfilled his vow to Hercules Augustus when he was stationed at the capitals of the columns of the Licinian Baths, which are being constructed thus in Sirmium.
EDCS-30900104.summary: A military votive dedication to Hercules Augustus by Valerius Valerianus, a soldier involved in the construction of the Licinian Baths in Sirmium.

EDCS-31200486.text_edition: \n Publius Gosselius Quinti filius Aniensis Cremona miles legionis XI signifer annorum XL stipendiorum XIIII hic situs est\n
EDCS-31200486.translation: Publius Gosselius, son of Quintus, of the Aniensis tribe, from Cremona, a soldier of the 11th Legion, standard-bearer, aged 40, with 14 years of service, lies here.
EDCS-31200486.summary: A funerary inscription for Publius Gosselius, a standard-bearer in the 11th Legion, who died at age 40 after 14 years of service.

EDCS-31200488.text_edition: \n Marcus Valerius Liberalis miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis annorum XIIX stipendii I domu Claudia Aequo mater memoriae viro et sibi et filio\n
EDCS-31200488.translation: Marcus Valerius Liberalis, soldier of the 11th Claudian Legion, pious and faithful, aged 18, with 1 year of service. His mother Claudia Aequa [placed this] in memory of her husband and for herself and her son.
EDCS-31200488.summary: A funerary inscription for Marcus Valerius Liberalis, a soldier of the 11th Legion, erected by his mother.

EDCS-31200489.text_edition: \n Lucius Valerius Maximus Valerio Proclo nnorum IXXX legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis DT Valeriae GIAE VET EN Valeriae Proclinae nepti nnorum V CV F M V p fecit\n
EDCS-31200489.translation: Lucius Valerius Maximus [placed this] for Valerius Proclus, aged 80, of the 11th Claudian Legion, pious and faithful; and for Valeria Giae Vet [or similar name], and for Valeria Proclina, granddaughter, aged 5. He made [this].
EDCS-31200489.summary: A funerary monument for Lucius Valerius Maximus, a soldier of the 11th Legion, and his granddaughter Valeria Proclina, possibly with other named individuals.

EDCS-31200490.text_edition: \n L Menenia V Feltria miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis stipendiorum XVI heres posuit\n
EDCS-31200490.translation: L. Menenia V. Feltria, a soldier of the 11th Claudian Legion, pious and faithful, with 16 years of service, the heir placed this.
EDCS-31200490.summary: A funerary inscription for L. Menenia V. Feltria, a soldier of the 11th Legion with 16 years of service, erected by their heir.

EDCS-31200498.text_edition: \n A LIIOI Ito Mesia matri suae fecit\n
EDCS-31200498.translation: Aelius dedicated this to his mother Mesia.
EDCS-31200498.summary: A funerary dedication by Aelius to his mother Mesia, likely from a tomb.

EDCS-31200500.summary: A fragmentary inscription with a partially preserved name, Quintus, origin and purpose unknown.

EDCS-31200501.translation: Quin to his wife Publi- Quintus.
EDCS-31200501.summary: A funerary dedication by Quin to his wife, likely part of a tombstone or monument.

EDCS-31200503.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Septimae Marcellinae defunctae annorum XXVII Septimius Marcellinus pater filiae pientissimae posuit et sibi\n
EDCS-31200503.translation: To the Divine Shades. Septimius Marcellinus placed this for his most dutiful daughter Septimia Marcellina, who died at twenty-seven years old, and for himself.
EDCS-31200503.summary: A funerary monument set up by Septimius Marcellinus for his deceased daughter Septimia Marcellina and for himself, likely a tombstone or cenotaph.

EDCS-31200504.translation: Sura Gemelli for himself and his family.
EDCS-31200504.summary: An ownership or dedication inscription by Sura Gemelli for himself and his household, purpose unclear but possibly funerary or property related.

EDCS-31200510.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription indicating burial.

EDCS-31200512.text_edition: \n Sarcofagum ita de<f=E>endunt ut super avorum suorum corpora nullus extraneum ponat quod si quis posuerit det fisco auri pondo dua \n
EDCS-31200512.translation: The sarcophagus is to be constructed such that no stranger is placed upon the bodies of their ancestors; whoever does so shall pay two pounds of gold to the treasury.
EDCS-31200512.summary: A legalistic inscription regulating the use of a sarcophagus, ensuring it remains for ancestral burials and imposing a penalty for violations, likely found on or near a family tomb.

EDCS-31200514.text_edition: \n Lucius Vibius Maximus titulum posuit Proclii sic <f=C>ili sic annorum II \n
EDCS-31200514.translation: Lucius Vibius Maximus placed this marker for Proclius, who was... two years old.
EDCS-31200514.summary: A funerary inscription by Lucius Vibius Maximus for Proclius, a child who died at two years old, likely a tomb marker.

EDCS-31200515.text_edition: \n Domitius Rufus Latrae votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-31200515.translation: Domitius Rufus gladly fulfilled his vow to Latra as deserved.
EDCS-31200515.summary: A votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by Domitius Rufus to the deity Latra, indicating personal piety.

EDCS-31200516.summary: A simple inscription with the name Lucius Domitius Rufus, possibly a name tag on a tomb or object.

EDCS-31200518.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari divi Nervae filio Nervae Traiano optimo Augusto Germanico Dacico pontifici maximo tribunicia potestate XVII imperatori VI consuli VI patri patriae Lucius Laelius Luci filius Claudia Proculus testamento fieri iussit epuloque dedicari\n
EDCS-31200518.translation: To Emperor Caesar Nerva Trajan Optimus Augustus, son of divine Nerva, Germanicus, Dacicus, chief priest, with tribunician power for the 17th time, acclaimed imperator for the 6th time, consul for the 6th time, father of the fatherland. Lucius Laelius, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, ordered by his will that this be made and dedicated at a feast.
EDCS-31200518.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Trajan, commissioned by Lucius Laelius in his will for a feast, likely dating to Trajan's reign (98-117 AD).

EDCS-31200519.translation: To our emperor and lord Licinius Augustus.
EDCS-31200519.summary: This is a dedication to Emperor Licinius, likely an honorific inscription from the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-31200521.text_edition: \n Titus Iulius Titi filius Claudia Celer legionis II Augustae am ex HSXX--- milibus testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-31200521.translation: Titus Julius Celer, son of Titus, of the Claudia tribe, a soldier of the Second Augustan Legion, ordered by his will that this be made from 20,000 sesterces.
EDCS-31200521.summary: This inscription records a posthumous donation by Titus Julius Celer, a soldier of Legio II Augusta, for a monument or event, the value of which is partially restored.

EDCS-31200527.translation: Lucius Antestius Secundus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-31200527.summary: This is a simple funerary or commemorative inscription identifying Lucius Antestius Secundus.

EDCS-31200528.text_edition: \n Gaio Arruntio Celeri Gaius Arrunttius Sedatus pater\n
EDCS-31200528.translation: Gaius Arruntius Sedatus, father, [erected this] to Gaius Arruntius Celer.
EDCS-31200528.summary: This inscription records a father, Gaius Arruntius Sedatus, setting up a monument for his son, Gaius Arruntius Celer.

EDCS-31200529.summary: This is a dedication or funerary inscription for Lucius Arruntius Maro.

EDCS-31200531.translation: To Caesias Tertullina, daughter of Titus.
EDCS-31200531.summary: This inscription is a dedication or funerary monument to Caesias Tertullina.

EDCS-31200534.text_edition: \n Tito Laelio Maximo annorum XIX Trosia Severa mater filio posuit\n
EDCS-31200534.translation: Trosia Severa placed this for her son Titus Laelius Maximus, aged 19.
EDCS-31200534.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a mother, Trosia Severa, for her deceased son, Titus Laelius Maximus.

EDCS-31200536.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Maximae feliae sic karissimae nnorum V mensium III Maxima et Secundinus parentis sic pientissimi \n
EDCS-31200536.translation: To the divine shades of Maxima, beloved daughter, aged 5 years and 3 months. Maxima and Secundinus, most devoted parents, [placed this].
EDCS-31200536.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young girl named Maxima, set up by her parents, Maxima and Secundinus.

EDCS-31200538.text_edition: \n Caio Oppio Cai filio Claudia Clementi Rusticello annorum XVI Baebia Titi filia Oppia mater filio posuit\n
EDCS-31200538.translation: Baebia Oppia, daughter of Titus, mother, placed this for her son Gaius Oppius Clementi Rusticellus, son of Gaius, aged 16.
EDCS-31200538.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a mother, Baebia Oppia, for her deceased son, Gaius Oppius Clementi Rusticellus.

EDCS-31200666.text_edition: \n Quintus Gnorius Quinti libertus Faustus Octaviae Luci filiae Secundae uxori annorum XXII\n
EDCS-31200666.translation: To Octavia Secunda, daughter of Lucius, wife of Quintus Gnorius Faustus, freedman of Quintus, aged 22.
EDCS-31200666.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a woman named Octavia Secunda, erected by her husband Quintus Gnorius Faustus.

EDCS-31200670.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus parens infelicissim- filio Severo carissimo qui vixit annos VIII menses X dies XVIII bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-31200670.translation: To the departed spirits. A most unhappy parent set up this for Severus, their dearest son, who lived 8 years, 10 months, and 18 days, well-deserving.
EDCS-31200670.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by a parent to their beloved son Severus, who died at a young age.

EDCS-31200671.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly recording the name Nero and his priestly office (pontifex).

EDCS-31200672.text_edition: \n torquibus armillis phaleris testamento fieri iussit Vibia Maxima soror faciendum curavit hoc monumentum heredem non sequetur\n
EDCS-31200672.translation: Vibia Maxima ordered this to be made by will, with torcs, armlets, and phalerae; she also saw to its construction. This monument will not follow an heir.
EDCS-31200672.summary: This funerary monument, commissioned by Vibia Maxima and including military decorations, states that it is not to be inherited.

EDCS-31200673.text_edition: \n Orthinae Luci Semproni Rufi Iuvenalis servae nnorum XV Sirio et Lamias filiae\n
EDCS-31200673.translation: To Orthina, slave of Lucius Sempronius Rufus Iuvenalis, aged 15, daughter of Sirius and Lamia.
EDCS-31200673.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young slave girl named Orthina, daughter of Sirius and Lamia.

EDCS-31200686.summary: This is a very short inscription that appears to be a name, possibly a dedication or a tombstone inscription.

EDCS-31200688.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliis Fortunioni et Felicissimae qui vixserunt sic annus sic XC fecit <i=E>en EMm<e=I>moriam Aurelia Quarta Aurelio Procino carissimo marito et sibi ipsaius qui vixserunt! in uno annos XIX Procinus vixit annos LXXX \n
EDCS-31200688.translation: To the departed spirits. Aurelia Quarta erected this memorial for Aurelius Fortunio and Felicissima, who lived together for 90 years, and for Aurelius Procino, her dearest husband, and for herself. Procino lived 80 years.
EDCS-31200688.summary: This is a funerary epitaph erected by Aurelia Quarta for her husbands Aurelius Fortunio, Felicissima (likely a wife of Fortunio), and Aurelius Procino, and for herself, recording their lifespans.

EDCS-31200872.translation: Sacred to Venus Augusta, by Flavia Bassilla.
EDCS-31200872.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Venus Augusta, made by Flavia Bassilla.

EDCS-31200873.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Iulio Philippo Pio Felici Augusto\n
EDCS-31200873.translation: To Emperor Caesar Marcus Iulius Philippus, the Pious, the Fortunate, Augustus.
EDCS-31200873.summary: This inscription is an dedication to Emperor Philip the Arab.

EDCS-31200875.text_edition: \n Quinto Cassio Aquilae decurioni Epidia Celerina uxor et Cassia Aquilina filia patri piissimo et sibi et suis vivae fecerunt\n
EDCS-31200875.translation: Epidia Celerina, his wife, and Cassia Aquilina, his daughter, set up this monument while alive for their most devoted father, Quintus Cassius Aquila, decurion, and for themselves and their family.
EDCS-31200875.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Epidia Celerina and Cassia Aquilina for Quintus Cassius Aquila, a decurion, and for themselves and their descendants.

EDCS-31200876.inscription_type: tituli honorarii, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-31200876.text_edition: \n Marco Flavio Marci filio Quirina Balbino huic defuncto ordo Docleatium honores omnes et statuam equestrem decrevit Flavius Fronto et Flavia Tertulla filii Marco Flavio Marci filio Quirina Balbino huic defuncto ordo Docleatium honores omnes et statuam equestrem decrevit Flavius Fronto et Flavia Tertulla parentes inauraverunt Marco Flavio Titi filio Quirina Frontoni praefecto fabrum pontifici flamini divi [Titi ------ Marco Flavio Marci filio Quirina Balbino huic defuncto ordo Docleatium honores omnes et statuam equestrem decrevit Flavius Fronto et Flavia Tertulla parentes inauraverunt\n
EDCS-31200876.translation: To Marcus Flavius Balbinus, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, upon his death, the council of Docleatians decreed all honors and an equestrian statue; his parents Flavius Fronto and Flavia Tertulla dedicated it. (Also records) Marcus Flavius Fronton, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, prefect of the engineers, pontiff, flamen of the deified [Titus...]
EDCS-31200876.summary: This inscription is a funerary monument honouring Marcus Flavius Balbinus, detailing the posthumous civic honours and a statue decreed to him by the council, and also mentions his parents and another relative, Marcus Flavius Fronton. It likely dates to the 1st-2nd century CE.

EDCS-31200880.text_edition: \n Titus Flavius Verecundus Thamarianus IIvir iure dicundo praefectus fabrum testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-31200880.translation: Titus Flavius Verecundus Thamarianus, duumvir with judicial authority, prefect of the engineers, ordered this to be made in his will.
EDCS-31200880.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Titus Flavius Verecundus Thamarianus, a prominent local official, commissioned his tomb by his will. It likely dates to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-31200881.text_edition: \n Serveniae Marcellae matri optimae Flavia Cai filia Prisca locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-31200881.translation: To Servenia Marcella, his most excellent mother, a burial place was granted by decree of the town councillors, by Flavia Prisca, daughter of Caius.
EDCS-31200881.summary: This inscription records the granting of a burial plot by decree of the town councillors to Servenia Marcella, a deceased woman, dedicated by her daughter Flavia Prisca. It likely dates to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-31200882.text_edition: \n MII Pro Ma piissimae locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-31200882.translation: A burial place was granted by decree of the town councillors to the most dutiful mother, [---]pius.
EDCS-31200882.summary: This inscription records the granting of a burial plot by decree of the town councillors to a highly respected mother, with the name partially lost. It likely dates to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-31200883.text_edition: \n Caio Caninio Valenti Canini Iulianus et Proculus patri piissimo fecerunt\n
EDCS-31200883.translation: Caninius Iulianus and Proculus made this for their most dutiful father, Gaius Caninius Valens.
EDCS-31200883.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Caninius Iulianus and Proculus for their esteemed father, Gaius Caninius Valens. It likely dates to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-31200884.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Claudio Aniceto marito pientissimo Claudia Olympia uxor memoriam posuit qui vixit annos LVIIII menses VIIII dies V has pro meritis sedes carissimo coniugi memoriam posuit\n
EDCS-31200884.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Claudia Olympia erected this monument to her most devoted husband, Claudius Anicetus, who lived 59 years, 9 months, and 5 days; she placed this tomb for her dearest husband in return for his merits.
EDCS-31200884.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Claudia Olympia to her devoted husband, Claudius Anicetus, providing his age at death and expressing her love and gratitude. It likely dates to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-31200885.translation: [---]geminus, Seviri Augustales, for the honour of [---].
EDCS-31200885.summary: This fragmented inscription records a dedication by Gemellinus, a member of the Seviri Augustales, an important local priesthood, likely related to a public honour or religious office. It likely dates to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-31200886.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iadestinus Baebiorum servus sibi et suis vivus fecit\n
EDCS-31200886.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Iadestinus, slave of the Baebii family, made this for himself and his kin while alive.
EDCS-31200886.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Iadestinus, a slave, for himself and his family, indicating he acquired the means to do so during his lifetime. It likely dates to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-31200893.text_edition: \n Dianae Augustae sacrum Titus Aurelius Saturninus eques Romanus ex voto libens posuit dea virago Delia votum faventi numine quod exigor felix ago libens posui OCcus\n
EDCS-31200893.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Titus Aurelius Saturninus, a Roman knight, set this up willingly in fulfilment of a vow; goddess warrior Delia, I gladly fulfil the vow made under your favourable divinity. Occcus.
EDCS-31200893.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the goddess Diana Augusta by Titus Aurelius Saturninus, a Roman knight, expressing gratitude for divine favour. It likely dates to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-31200894.text_edition: \n Iovi Cohortali sacrum Marcus Aemilius Antonius voto libens posuit\n
EDCS-31200894.translation: Sacred to Jupiter Cohortalis. Marcus Aemilius Antonius set this up willingly in fulfilment of a vow.
EDCS-31200894.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Jupiter Cohortalis by Marcus Aemilius Antonius, who set it up willingly in fulfilment of a vow. It likely dates to the 1st-3rd century CE.

EDCS-31300270.text_edition: \n Marco Flavio Titi filio Quirina Frontoni sacerdoti in coloniis Narona et Epidauro IIviro iure dicundo Iu<l=I>io Risinio IIviro <q=C>uinquennali <p=I>on<t=I>(ifici) in co<l=I>(onia) Sc<o=C>dra IIviro iure dicundo qui<n=M>quennali <fl=II>amini praefecto <f=I>abrum pleps sic ex aere conla<to=IC> \n
EDCS-31300270.translation: To Marcus Flavius Frontonius, son of Titus, of the Quirina tribe, priest in the colonies of Narona and Epidauros, twice duovir with judicial authority, twice quinquennial duovir, pontiff, and in the colony of Scodra, twice duovir with judicial authority, twice quinquennial duovir, flaminius, prefect of the plebs, thus from the bronze contributed.
EDCS-31300270.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument or work by Marcus Flavius Frontonius, listing his various civic and religious offices in several colonies, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31300271.text_edition: \n Imperatore Caesare Marco Opellio Severo Macrino Pio Felici Augusto pontifice maximo tribunicia potestate consule IIIII I[]MNIANS macellum vi ignis conflagr<a>tum curante Val<e=I>(erio) Supero viro egregio procuratore argentariarum res publica Domaviana ad pristinam faciem restituit XXI Kalendas \n
EDCS-31300271.translation: Under the Emperor Caesar Marcus Opellius Severus Macrinus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, with tribunician power, consul for the third time. The market building, which had been consumed by fire, was restored to its former appearance by the state of Domaviana, under the supervision of Valerius Superus, a distinguished man, procurator of the argentariae, on the 21st day before the Kalends of [Month].
EDCS-31300271.summary: This inscription documents the rebuilding of a market (macellum) in Domaviana that was destroyed by fire, carried out under the supervision of Valerius Superus during the reign of Macrinus (217-218 AD).

EDCS-31300272.text_edition: \n Termini positi inter prata legionis et fines roboreti Flavi Marciani per Augustianum Bellicium procuratorem Augusti\n
EDCS-31300272.translation: Boundary stones set between the fields of the legion and the lands of Augustiana Bellicus, procurator of Augustus.
EDCS-31300272.summary: This inscription records the placement of boundary markers between legionary land and private property, overseen by the procurator Augustiana Bellicus, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31300277.text_edition: \n Apollini Tadeno Charmidis coloniae donum dedit\n
EDCS-31300277.translation: Charmidis of the colony gave this gift to Apollo Tadeno.
EDCS-31300277.summary: This is a dedicatory inscription by Charmidis to the god Apollo Tadeno, probably dating from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31300287.text_edition: \n Tito Flavio Simili IIviro quinquennali huic primo ex ordine statuam in basilica posuit et dedicavit eam victima percussa sportulis datis natale Augusti VI Kalendas Maias Quinto Sosio Prisco Publio Coelio Apollinare consulibus Titus Flavius Rufinus filius locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-31300287.translation: Titus Flavius Rufinus, son of Titus Flavius Similus, a quinquennial duovir, erected and dedicated a statue in the basilica to him [Similus], the first in rank, after a victim was sacrificed and gifts were distributed, on the birthday of Augustus, on the VI Kalendas of May, with Quintus Sosius Priscus and Publius Coelius Apollinaris as consuls. The place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-31300287.summary: This inscription records the erection and dedication of a statue to Titus Flavius Similus, a former duovir, by his son Titus Flavius Rufinus, likely in the late 1st or early 2nd century AD, to commemorate Similus on Augustus' birthday.

EDCS-31300288.text_edition: \n Deo Magno Conservatori loci huius pro salute sua et suorum et numero convivii Aurelii Valerianus et Terentianus votum libentes laeti solverunt\n
EDCS-31300288.translation: For the great god Conservator of this place, for their own health and that of their family and members of their association, Aurelius Valerianus and Terentianus gladly and joyfully fulfilled their vow.
EDCS-31300288.summary: This is a votive inscription to the god Magno Conservatore by Aurelius Valerianus and Terentianus for their well-being and that of their group, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31300289.text_edition: \n Armato sacrum Sestia Onesime ex voto posuit libens\n
EDCS-31300289.translation: Sestia Onesime erected this sacred object to Armatus, fulfilling her vow willingly.
EDCS-31300289.summary: This is a votive inscription by Sestia Onesime dedicating an object to the god Armatus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31300290.text_edition: \n Quintus Baebius filius Zuprico aedilis tabernam pergulam maenianum rei publicae dedit\n
EDCS-31300290.translation: Quintus Baebius, son of Zupricus, as aedile, gave a shop with a projecting upper story to the state.
EDCS-31300290.summary: This inscription records the donation of a shop with an upper story by Quintus Baebius Zupricus, serving as aedile, to the community, likely in the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31300291.text_edition: \n Bindo Neptuno sacrum Proculus Parmanicus praepositus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-31300291.translation: Proculus Parmanicus, appointed official and resident of Bindo, willingly fulfilled his vow to Neptune, as is fitting.
EDCS-31300291.summary: This votive inscription records Proculus Parmanicus fulfilling a vow to Neptune, likely from the Roman Imperial period in the region of Bindo.

EDCS-31300292.text_edition: \n Bindo Neptuno sacrum Licinus Teuda praepositus et princeps Iapodum votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-31300292.translation: Licinus Teuda, appointed official and chief of the Japodes of Bindo, willingly fulfilled his vow to Neptune, as is fitting.
EDCS-31300292.summary: This votive inscription records Licinus Teuda fulfilling a vow to Neptune, identifying him as an official and chief of the Japodes people in Bindo, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-31300303.text_edition: \n Tiberius Claudius Ligomarus Carstimari filius Claudia Salinis eques cohortis III Alpinae nnorum LVII stipendiorum XXX hic situs est testamento fieri iussit heredes posuerunt Tiberius Claudius Silenus et Tiberius Claudius Abascianus libertus\n
EDCS-31300303.translation: Tiberius Claudius Ligomarus, son of Carstimarus, an equestrian of the III Alpine Cohort, aged 57 years and serving for 30 years, lies here. He ordered this to be made by his will; his freedmen Tiberius Claudius Silenus and Tiberius Claudius Abascianus erected it.
EDCS-31300303.summary: A funerary epitaph for Tiberius Claudius Ligomarus, an equestrian, erected by his freedmen, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-31300304.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Iovino ex collegio saccariorum qui vixit annis XLV Aurelia Primilla virginio infelicissimo I fecit\n
EDCS-31300304.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelia Primilla made this for her most unhappy husband, Aurelius Iovinus, from the guild of grain merchants, who lived 45 years.
EDCS-31300304.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelius Iovinus, a member of the grain merchants' guild, dedicated by his wife, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-31300305.text_edition: \n Amabili secutori natione Dacus pugnarum XIII fato deceptus non ab homine\n
EDCS-31300305.translation: To Amabilis the follower, a Dacian by birth, deceived by fate in 13 battles, not by a man.
EDCS-31300305.summary: An epitaph commemorating Amabilis, a Dacian soldier who died in battle, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-31300306.text_edition: \n Lucio Anicio Luci filio Paetinati IIIIviro iure dicundo nni praefecto quinquennali Drusi Caesaris Germanici praefecto quinquennali Publi Dolabellae pontifici flamini Iuliae Augustae praefecto fabrum praefectura Phariaca Salonitana\n
EDCS-31300306.translation: To Lucius Anicius Paetinatus, son of Lucius, a *quattuorvir* with judicial power, quinquennial prefect under Drusus Caesar Germanicus, quinquennial prefect under Publius Dolabella, pontiff, flamen of Julia Augusta, prefect of mechanics, of the Pharos prefecture in Salona.
EDCS-31300306.summary: A dedication honoring Lucius Anicius Paetinatus, a local magistrate and priest with various civic and religious titles, likely dating to the early imperial period.

EDCS-31300307.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelio Maximo a militiis negotiatori celeberrimo qui vixit nnos plus minus Papirius Maximinus a Maxim \n
EDCS-31300307.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Aurelius Maximus, distinguished in military service, a very famous merchant, who lived more or less... Papirius Maximinus [erected this] for Maximus.
EDCS-31300307.summary: A fragmentary funerary epitaph for Aurelius Maximus, a successful merchant and former soldier, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-31300395.summary: A very brief inscription possibly identifying an owner or builder, too fragmentary for a precise date.

EDCS-31400159.text_edition: \n Divo Tito Aug Lucius Flavius Quirina Epidianus IIIIvir iure dicundo quinquennalis municipii Docleatium ob honorem IIIV\n
EDCS-31400159.translation: To Divine Titus Augustus. Lucius Flavius Epidianus of the Quirina tribe, *quattuorvir* with judicial power and quinquennial prefect of the municipality of Doclea, for the honor of *quattuorvir*...
EDCS-31400159.summary: A dedication by Lucius Flavius Epidianus, a local magistrate in Doclea, likely dating to the Flavian period (late 1st century CE) in honor of Emperor Titus.

EDCS-31400167.text_edition: \n praefecto frumenti dandi procons(uli) provinciae le[gato ------\n
EDCS-31400167.translation: ... prefect for distributing grain, proconsul of the province, legate...
EDCS-31400167.summary: A fragmentary inscription listing official titles, including grain distribution and provincial governorship, likely from the imperial period.

EDCS-31400168.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marci Marii Ambacti Corneli domo Br<i=Y>xia Publius Scrasius Naeolus aequo sic publico scriba quaestorius amico inconparabi[li! ------ \n
EDCS-31400168.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For Marcus Marius Ambaxtus Corneli, from Brixia, Publius Scrasius Naeolus, a quaestorian scribe in public service, for his incomparable friend...
EDCS-31400168.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Marius Ambaxtus Corneli, dedicated by his friend Publius Scrasius Naeolus, a public scribe, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-31400169.text_edition: \n Marco Epidio Publi filio Quirina Latino decrecto decurionum\n
EDCS-31400169.translation: To Marcus Epidius, son of Publius, of the Quirina tribe, the Latin. By decree of the decurions.
EDCS-31400169.summary: A dedication or honorific inscription for Marcus Epidius, likely a local notable, enacted by the town council, dating to the imperial period.

EDCS-31400171.text_edition: \n Flavia Pinnia testamento fieri iussit Flavius Epidianus faciendum curavit locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-31400171.translation: Flavia Pinnia ordered this to be made in her will; Flavius Epidianus took care of its construction; the site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-31400171.summary: This is a funerary monument established by will and overseen by a named individual, with the land provided by local council decree.

EDCS-31400172.text_edition: \n IIviro iure dicundo II Flavia Cai filia Rufina filio posuit locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-31400172.translation: Flavia Rufina, daughter of Caius, erected this for her son, having held the office of *duovir* with judicial authority; the site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-31400172.summary: This records a funerary monument dedicated by a woman for her son, mentioning her civic role and the land grant by the decurions.

EDCS-31400173.text_edition: \n Marco Licinio Probo decurioni qui vixit nnos L Marcus Licinius Severus patri optimo\n
EDCS-31400173.translation: To Marcus Licinius Probus, council member, who lived 50 years, Marcus Licinius Severus dedicated this to his excellent father.
EDCS-31400173.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by a son to his father, Marcus Licinius Probus, a council member who lived to be 50.

EDCS-31400174.text_edition: \n Marco Novio Quinti filio Iusto decurioni ex testamento eius Titus Novius Maximus frater ponendum curavit locus datus decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-31400174.translation: From his will, Titus Novius Maximus, brother, took care of the erection of this for Marcus Novius Justus, son of Quintus, a council member; the site was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-31400174.summary: This funerary inscription records a monument erected by a brother for his deceased brother, a council member, as stipulated in the will, with land granted by decree.

EDCS-31400175.text_edition: \n Aeliae Maximae coniugi carissimae quae vixit annos XXXV Lucius A[elius ------\n
EDCS-31400175.translation: To Aelia Maxima, his dearest wife, who lived 35 years, Lucius Aelius [...] erected this.
EDCS-31400175.summary: This is a funerary monument dedicated by Lucius Aelius to his wife, Aelia Maxima, recording her age at death.

EDCS-31400176.summary: This is a very brief inscription, possibly a dedication or ownership mark, with a personal pronoun and a verb.

EDCS-31400177.translation: To Claudia Probilla, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-31400177.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, to Claudia Probilla, daughter of Quintus.

EDCS-31400179.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus miserrimae infelicissimae F<l=E>(aviae) Ursillae quae vixit annos VI menses II dies XXV Flavius Ursus et Flavia Baebia parentes filiae innocentissimae \n
EDCS-31400179.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Flavia Ursula, most wretched and unfortunate, who lived 6 years, 2 months, and 25 days; Flavius Ursus and Flavia Baebia, parents, erected this for their most innocent daughter.
EDCS-31400179.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of a young child, Flavia Ursula, with details of her age and a dedication from her grieving parents.

EDCS-31400180.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Iulio Laconi qui vixit annos XLV huic de<f=E>(uncto) Baeb<i=T>a Moderata marito bene merenti <f=E>(ecit) \n
EDCS-31400180.translation: To the spirits of the underworld for Marcus Julius Laconi, who lived 45 years; Baebia Moderata erected this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-31400180.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Marcus Julius Laconi at age 45, with a dedication from his wife, Baebia Moderata.

EDCS-31400181.text_edition: \n ann(os) ---VIIII menses II dies VII et innocentissimo pusino Ienuario sic filio eius vixit annos --I menses III dies XVII Secundianus et Ianuaria parentes <f=E>ilia<e=I> et nepoti <f=E>ecerunt \n
