EDCS-32700561.summary: A private dedication recording the fulfillment of a vow by Sicilius Secundus.

EDCS-32700563.summary: A funerary inscription likely recording epithets or triumphs of an individual, possibly a soldier, with no further context.

EDCS-32700565.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelio Messiano veterano defuncto nnorum LXX Tesonia Iulianae sic pientissimo marito et sibi se viva fecit\n
EDCS-32700565.translation: To the Divine Manes. Tesonia Iuliana erected this for her most devoted husband Aelius Messianus, a veteran aged 70, and for herself while alive.
EDCS-32700565.summary: A funerary monument erected by a wife for her deceased veteran husband and herself, likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-32700566.text_edition: \n Sextus Anternius Quinti filius Scaptia Severus domo Florentia \n
EDCS-32700566.translation: Sextus Anternius Severus, son of Quintus, of the Scaptia tribe, from Florence.
EDCS-32700566.summary: An epitaph recording the name, filiation, tribe, and origin of an individual named Sextus Anternius Severus.

EDCS-32700568.text_edition: \n Caius Av Cai filius Fa miles legionis XXI hic situs est\n
EDCS-32700568.translation: Gaius Avilius, son of Caius, a soldier of the 21st legion, lies here.
EDCS-32700568.summary: A tombstone marking the burial place of Gaius Avilius, a soldier of the Legio XXI.

EDCS-32700570.text_edition: \n Publio Carsidio Publi filio Galeria Calvo Lugduno aquilifero legionis IIII Flaviae felicis annn(orum) XLI stipendiorum XIIX hic situs est\n
EDCS-32700570.translation: To Publius Carsidius Calvo, son of Publius, of the Galeria tribe, from Lugdunum, standard-bearer of the IVth Flavian Felix legion, aged 41, with 18 years of service, lies here.
EDCS-32700570.summary: A funerary inscription for Publius Carsidius Calvo, an aquilifer (standard-bearer) in Legio IV Flavia Felix, likely from the late 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-32700572.text_edition: \n Caius Coccius Cai filius Veturia Tertius Placentia miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis stipendiorum XII centuria Caecili Sabini beneficiarius tribuni heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-32700572.translation: Gaius Coccius Tertius, son of Gaius, of the Veturia tribe, from Placentia, a soldier of the 11th Claudian Pia Fidelis legion, with 12 years of service, a beneficiarius of the tribune in the century of Caecilius Sabinus, his heir had this constructed.
EDCS-32700572.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Coccius Tertius, a soldier of Legio XI Claudia, whose heir arranged for the monument, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-32700574.text_edition: \n Caius Fabricius Cai filius Oufentia Primus Como miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis centuria Gemini Flavi stipendiorum XX nnorum XXXVIII testamento fieri iussit heres faciundum curavit\n
EDCS-32700574.translation: Gaius Fabricius Primus, son of Gaius, of the Oufentia tribe, from Como, a soldier of the 11th Claudian Pia Fidelis legion, in the century of Geminius Flavius, with 20 years of service, aged 38, ordered this to be made in his will; his heir had it constructed.
EDCS-32700574.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Fabricius Primus, a soldier of Legio XI Claudia, who left instructions in his will for his burial monument, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-32700575.text_edition: \n Lucius Flavius Luci filius Fabia Valens Heraclea miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis centuria Iuli Prisci annorum XLII stipendiorum XXII centuria Iuli Prisci heres faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-32700575.translation: Lucius Flavius Valens, son of Lucius, of the Fabia tribe, from Heraclea, a soldier of the 11th Claudian Pia Fidelis legion, in the century of Iulius Priscus, aged 42, with 22 years of service, his heir had this constructed.
EDCS-32700575.summary: A funerary inscription for Lucius Flavius Valens, a soldier of Legio XI Claudia, whose heir arranged for the monument, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-32700946.text_edition: \n Seneca Sestenius Rustici filius annorum XXXXV hic situs est\n
EDCS-32700946.translation: Seneca Sestius, son of Rusticus, aged 45, lies here.
EDCS-32700946.summary: An epitaph recording the name, filiation, and age of Seneca Sestius.

EDCS-32700953.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vitell<i=F>ae Ammidae nnorum L et Aelio Publi filio Prisco nnorum IIII Quintus Octa<v=O>(ius) Quintilianus commentariensis matri pientissimae et fratri \n
EDCS-32700953.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Quintus Octavius Quintilianus, a clerk, dedicated this to his most devoted mother Vitellia Ammida, aged 50, and his brother Priscus Aelius, aged 4.
EDCS-32700953.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by Quintus Octavius Quintilianus to his mother and brother, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-32700956.text_edition: \n Lucio Ficilio Proclino procuratori qui vixit nnos L menses XI dies XVIIII Postumia Vitalis marito fecit\n
EDCS-32700956.translation: Postumia Vitalis erected this for her husband Lucius Ficilius Proclinus, who lived 50 years, 11 months, and 19 days.
EDCS-32700956.summary: A funerary monument erected by a wife for her husband, indicating his lifespan and likely dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-32700963.summary: A religious dedication to the deity Valens, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-32700966.text_edition: \n Marco Decidio Marci filio Maximo aedili IIviro auguri\n
EDCS-32700966.translation: To Marcus Decidius Maximus, son of Marcus, former aedile, duovir, and augur.
EDCS-32700966.summary: An inscription honoring Marcus Decidius Maximus, listing his civic and religious offices, likely from a Roman town.

EDCS-32700969.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Marco Aurelio Pio Felici Augusto \n
EDCS-32700969.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Marcus Aurelius Pius Felix Augustus.
EDCS-32700969.summary: An imperial dedication to Emperor Marcus Aurelius, dating to his reign (161-180 CE).

EDCS-32700972.summary: A fragmentary imperial dedication, likely honoring a Roman emperor.

EDCS-32805509.text_edition: \n Silvinae carissimae quod ego sperabam paulam mihi Silvinam facturam ego feci Silvinae delicatae nnorum XV quae est sepulta hic te rogo qui legis hunc titulum ut dicas Silvina puella sit tibi terra [levis ------\n
EDCS-32805509.translation: To my dearest Silvina, whom I hoped would do a little for me, I have made this for Silvina, my darling, aged 15, who is buried here. I ask you who read this inscription to say: May the earth be light for you, Silvina, girl...
EDCS-32805509.summary: A poignant funerary epitaph for a young girl named Silvina, expressing the grief of the dedicator and including a wish for a light burial, from the Imperial period.

EDCS-33600282.text_edition: \n Primigenivola Mevi serva votum libens merito solvit\n
EDCS-33600282.translation: Primigenivola, the slave woman of Mevinus, gladly and deservedly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-33600282.summary: A votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by a slave woman, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-34100064.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Publio Aelio Dasio et Paiioni coniugi posuit Aelius Baebius filius\n
EDCS-34100064.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelius Baebius erected this for Publius Aelius Dasus and his wife Paiio.
EDCS-34100064.summary: A funerary monument erected by Aelius Baebius for his father Publius Aelius Dasus and his mother Paiio, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-34100065.text_edition: \n Dasas posuit sibi et Aeliae Datoni coniugi suae ---\n
EDCS-34100065.translation: Dasas erected this for himself and his wife Aelia Datona...
EDCS-34100065.summary: A funerary monument erected by Dasas for himself and his wife, dating to the Imperial period, with a lacuna indicating missing text.

EDCS-34100066.text_edition: \n Arrius Rufinus veteranus legionis I adiutricis\n
EDCS-34100066.translation: Arrius Rufinus, a veteran of the I Adjutrix legion.
EDCS-34100066.summary: This inscription records the name and military status of Arrius Rufinus, a veteran of the I Adjutrix legion.

EDCS-34100068.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus nIano filio nnorum Publius Aelius Maximinus pater posuit\n
EDCS-34100068.translation: To the Divine Shades. Publius Aelius Maximinus, his father, erected this for his son, Nianus, aged [unknown].
EDCS-34100068.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Publius Aelius Maximinus for his deceased son, Nianus.

EDCS-34100069.text_edition: \n Publio Aelio Epicati Edano defuncto annorum LXV Publia Aelia Mo coniux pro pietate\n
EDCS-34100069.translation: For Publius Aelius Epicati Edanus, deceased aged 65. Publia Aelia Mo, his wife, [erected this] out of devotion.
EDCS-34100069.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Publia Aelia Mo for her deceased husband, Publius Aelius Epicati Edanus.

EDCS-34100070.summary: This inscription simply records the name Iulia Secundilla, likely on a tombstone or memorial.

EDCS-34100071.text_edition: \n Rubria Quinti filia Rufina infelicissima sibi posuit\n
EDCS-34100071.translation: Rubria Rufina, daughter of Quintus, most unhappy, erected this for herself.
EDCS-34100071.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Rubria Rufina for herself, indicating she was unhappy.

EDCS-34100072.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Rubria Restuta sibi viva fecit\n
EDCS-34100072.translation: To the Divine Shades. Rubria Restuta made this for herself while alive.
EDCS-34100072.summary: This is a funerary inscription made by Rubria Restuta for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-34100134.summary: This inscription consists of initials, likely representing a name or dedication.

EDCS-34100135.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aletius Barbius infelicissimae coniugi suae Marcellae quae vixxi<t=X> bene mecum annos plus minus LV filios reliquit quattuor quae nullum dolum de se vidit \n
EDCS-34100135.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aletius Barbius [erected this] for his most unhappy wife Marcella, who lived well with me for more or less 55 years, left four sons, and who was never seen to grieve for herself.
EDCS-34100135.summary: This funerary inscription honors Marcella, the wife of Aletius Barbius, detailing her lifespan, children, and her good character.

EDCS-37900107.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Panianti qui vixit nnos V \n
EDCS-37900107.translation: To the Divine Shades. For Panianti, who lived 5 years.
EDCS-37900107.summary: This is a funerary inscription commemorating Panianti, who died at the age of 5.

EDCS-41300186.text_edition: \n Lucius Tertius Luci filius Tertianus Heraclea nnorum HIR ACLRA miles legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis nnorum XL stipendiorum XVII Lucius Pomponius Pollio? ---\n
EDCS-41300186.translation: Lucius Tertius Tertianus, son of Lucius, a soldier of the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, aged 40, with 17 years of service. Lucius Pomponius Pollio(?) [erected this for him].
EDCS-41300186.summary: This inscription records the details of Lucius Tertius Tertianus, a soldier of the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, including his age, service length, and likely a dedicant.

EDCS-41300187.text_edition: \n Lucius Tertius Luci filius Sergia Tertianus Heraclea <mil=NV>(es) legionis XI Claudiae piae fidelis nnorum XL stipendiorum XVII testamento fieri iussit Lucius Ponponius sic Pollio heres ponendum curavit \n
EDCS-41300187.translation: Lucius Tertius, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, Tertianus Heraclea of the legion XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, aged 40, with 17 years of service, ordered this to be made by his will. Lucius Ponponius, called Pollio, his heir, saw to its erection.
EDCS-41300187.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Tertianus Heraclea, a soldier of the legion XI Claudia, funded by his will and erected by his heir, dating to the late 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-41600067.summary: This is a very brief inscription recording that Publius Rutilacius Rufus built or dedicated something.

EDCS-41600068.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that likely records the name of Alfisius Men[...].

EDCS-41600117.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Marcus Ulpius Iustus vixit annos XI hic situs est Ulpius Priscus pater faciendum curavit\n
EDCS-41600117.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Marcus Ulpius Iustus lived for 11 years. He lies here. Ulpius Priscus, his father, saw to its construction.
EDCS-41600117.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Ulpius Iustus, who died at the age of 11, erected by his father, Ulpius Priscus.

EDCS-41600119.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Vibius NMVS V Vibius Priscus CVYVS nepos BINCV H\n
EDCS-41600119.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Vibius [...] V. Vibius Priscus, his grandson, [...]
EDCS-41600119.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary epitaph mentioning individuals named Vibius, likely related.

EDCS-41600120.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Pyrami vixit annos XVI Aemilius Placidus maritus et Euplus et Ieroitice mater posuerunt\n
EDCS-41600120.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Pyrami lived for 16 years. Her husband Aemilius Placidus and her mother Euplus and Ieroitice set this up.
EDCS-41600120.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Pyrami, who died at 16, erected by her husband and mother.

EDCS-41600122.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Lucio Septimio Severo Pertinaci Augusto tribunicia potestate II imperatori III consuli II proconsuli patri patriae decreto decurionum\n
EDCS-41600122.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus, holding the tribunician power for the second time, acclaimed imperator for the third time, consul for the second time, proconsul, father of the fatherland, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-41600122.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Emperor Septimius Severus, likely honoring a public work commissioned by the local town council (decurions) during his consulship.

EDCS-41600124.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum in memoriam aeternam coniugi infelicissimo Aurelio Lupo aequiti sic Romano qui vixit annos XXXVIII Aurelia Notalie marito pientissimo\n
EDCS-41600124.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits, in eternal memory of a most unhappy husband, Aurelius Lupus, an equite of the Roman people, who lived for 38 years. Aurelia Notalie, to her most devoted husband.
EDCS-41600124.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelius Lupus, an equite who died at 38, erected by his wife Aurelia Notalie.

EDCS-41600125.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Lupinus et Rufilla parentes filiae Fuscinae pientissimae quae vixit a<n=AI>nos XXVII fecerunt \n
EDCS-41600125.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Lupinus and Rufilla, parents, made this for their most devoted daughter Fuscina, who lived for 27 years.
EDCS-41600125.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Fuscina, who died at 27, erected by her parents, Lupinus and Rufilla.

EDCS-41600126.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely related to a governor or his staff.

EDCS-41600127.text_edition: \n VT et VO et AP filius sibi et suis vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-41600127.translation: Vt and Vo and Ap, a son, made this for himself and his family while alive.
EDCS-41600127.summary: A funerary monument erected by a son, Vt, for himself and his family, likely from the early imperial period.

EDCS-41600128.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Falia Murci filia viva memoriam fecit sibe sic et filiis suis\n
EDCS-41600128.translation: To the Divine Manes. Falia Murci, a daughter, made this memorial for herself while alive, and for her children.
EDCS-41600128.summary: A funerary monument dedicated to the Divine Manes by Falia Murci for herself and her children, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-41600129.text_edition: \n Gentio Sai filio patri et Platurae matri parentibus Clevata Eutychum servum facere iussit\n
EDCS-41600129.translation: Clevata, a freedwoman, ordered this monument to be made for Gention, her father, and Platura, her mother, his parents.
EDCS-41600129.summary: A funerary monument commissioned by a freedwoman for her parents, Gention and Platura, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-44200188.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Fulguratori K Patricius et Paula pro incolumitate sua suorumque pridie Nonas Septembres\n
EDCS-44200188.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, the Thunderer. K. Patricius and Paula, for their own safety and that of their family, on the day before the Nones of September.
EDCS-44200188.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by K. Patricius and Paula for their well-being, dated to September 4th, likely in the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-44200205.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus hic positus est Titus Aurelius Aurelianus filius pientissimus vixit annis VIIII\n
EDCS-44200205.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus Aurelius Aurelianus, a most dutiful son, lies here, having lived 9 years.
EDCS-44200205.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Titus Aurelius Aurelianus, a 9-year-old boy, dedicated to the Divine Manes, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-44200207.text_edition: \n Tito Flavio Titi filio Pollia castrensi Lucilio vexillario equitum singularium Augusti sesquiplicario eiusdem numeri duplicario et summo curatori equitum singularium Augusti |(centurioni) cohortis VIII voluntariorum honesta missione commodis acceptis omnibus ab Imperatore Hadriano Augusto Titus Flavius Eulogus libertus et heres vivus fecit ex voluntate sua et sibi et libertis libertabusque suis posterisque eorum\n
EDCS-44200207.translation: Titus Flavius Eulogus, freedman and heir of Titus Flavius Titi, a Pollia who served with the vexillation of the elite horseguards of Augustus, as a sesquiplicarius of the same unit, and a duplicarius, and chief curator of the elite horseguards of Augustus, and a centurion of the 8th cohort of volunteers, honorably discharged with all benefits by the Emperor Hadrian Augustus, made this while alive, according to his will, for himself and for his freedmen, freedwomen, and their descendants.
EDCS-44200207.summary: A funerary monument erected by Titus Flavius Eulogus, a freedman and heir of a distinguished soldier, for himself and his household, with explicit mention of honors and discharge granted by Emperor Hadrian, likely from the mid-2nd century AD.

EDCS-44200209.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Iu<l=I>(ius) Trofimus Trosiae Thycini sic bene merenti Dis Manibus Leoni \n
EDCS-44200209.translation: To the Divine Manes. Iulius Trofimus, for Troesia Thycina, who deserved well, to the Divine Manes. To Leon.
EDCS-44200209.summary: A funerary dedication to the Divine Manes for Iulius Trofimus and Troesia Thycina, and also for Leon, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-44200212.text_edition: \n Ursus mortuus nnorum XVIII mater Pia fecit et posuit bene merenti filio\n
EDCS-44200212.translation: Ursus, deceased, 18 years old. His mother Pia made and placed this for her deserving son.
EDCS-44200212.summary: A tombstone for Ursus, aged 18, erected by his mother Pia, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-44200213.text_edition: \n Victorino decurioni municipii Aquae filioque infelicissimo vixit annos X sic menses LI sic dies XIIII et Ulpiae Galliae coniugi incomparabili Publius Aelius Victorinus quaestor IIIvir quinquennalis ed sic sibi vivus fecit\n
EDCS-44200213.translation: For Victorinus, decurion of the municipality of Aquae, and his most unfortunate son, who lived 10 years, 51 months, and 14 days, and for Ulpia Gallia, his incomparable wife. Publius Aelius Victorinus, quaestor, quinquennial duovir, made this for himself while alive.
EDCS-44200213.summary: A funerary monument erected by Publius Aelius Victorinus for his son Victorinus and wife Ulpia Gallia, and for himself, noting the deceased son's age and Victorinus's civic offices, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century AD.

EDCS-44200214.text_edition: \n na Iacu ipantis fili- nnorum LV\n
EDCS-44200214.summary: A fragmentary tombstone recording the age of a 55-year-old individual, son of Iacupanthis, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-46400015.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, for the safety of Emperor [[Aemilianus]] Augustus and his entire divine family, the Second Cohort of Thracians, newly raised, equestrian, [[Aemiliana]], with Quintus Sextilius Marinus as tribune and Asinius Maximus as consular.
EDCS-46400015.summary: This inscription is a dedication to Jupiter by the Second Cohort of Thracians for the safety of Emperor Aemilianus, dating to his reign (mid-3rd century AD).

EDCS-46400961.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Luccius Valens |(centurio) cohortis I milliariae cum Marco Luccio Frontone filio libens merito posuit\n
EDCS-46400961.translation: Marcus Luccius Valens, a centurion of the First Cohort, with his son Marcus Luccius Fronto, willingly placed this in fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Best and Greatest.
EDCS-46400961.summary: This is a votive dedication to Jupiter by a centurion, Marcus Luccius Valens, and his son, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-46600036.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Ampliatus Iuliae Mollatinae nnum V idem sic Servando annuculo mensium VII hic siti sunt filiis suis posuit\n
EDCS-46600036.translation: Caius Iulius Ampliatus placed these here for his sons: Julia Mollatina, for one year, and Servandus, for one year and seven months.
EDCS-46600036.summary: This tomb inscription records the burial of two young sons for whom Caius Iulius Ampliatus provided memorial stones, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-46600037.text_edition: \n Nimphis sic Et Lucius Baebidius Cassius |(centurio) et Marcus Flavius \n
EDCS-46600037.translation: Lucius Baebidius Cassius, a centurion, and Marcus Flavius, to the Nymphs.
EDCS-46600037.summary: This fragmentary inscription records a dedication to the Nymphs by a centurion, Lucius Baebidius Cassius, and Marcus Flavius, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-46600038.text_edition: \n Herculi Augusto sacrum Donatus Cnaei Corneli Certi votum solvit libens\n
EDCS-46600038.translation: Donatus, freedman of Gnaeus Cornelius Certus, willingly fulfilled his vow to Hercules Augustus.
EDCS-46600038.summary: This is a votive inscription to Hercules Augustus by his freedman Donatus, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-46600039.text_edition: \n Salaciae Augustae sacrum Salvius Cai libertus Panus\n
EDCS-46600039.translation: Salvius Panus, freedman of Salacia Augusta, [dedicated this].
EDCS-46600039.summary: This is a dedication to Salacia Augusta by her freedman Salvius Panus, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-46600040.text_edition: \n Titus Buzetius Diti filius Tura Ti filia Tito Buzetia Titi? filia mtena Gelli filia testamento fieri iussit tus Buzetius Titi libertus patrono arbitratu Titonis Buzetiae sororis et Sextonis Buzetiae sororis\n
EDCS-46600040.translation: Titus Buzetius, son of Ditus, from Tura; Titus Buzetius, son of Titus; Titus Buzetius, son of Titus, appointed by testament to be made by Titus Buzetius, freedman of Tita Gella, at the arbitration of Titonia Buzetia, sister, and Sextonia Buzetia, sister.
EDCS-46600040.summary: This complex funerary inscription details the arrangements made by Titus Buzetius, at the arbitration of his sisters, for the burial of himself and other family members, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-46600041.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Adiutori coniugi bene merenti con sic quo vixi annos XXXVIII menses III Sextilia Infgenua fecit\n
EDCS-46600041.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead, for Adiutor, a well-deserving husband, with whom I lived 38 years, 3 months, Sextilia Infgenua made this.
EDCS-46600041.summary: This tomb inscription is a dedication to the deceased husband of Sextilia Infgenua, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-46600042.text_edition: \n Quinto Rubrio Apli filio Rufo Rubria Rufina patri posuit\n
EDCS-46600042.translation: Rubria Rufina placed this for her father, Quintus Rubrius Rufus, son of Aplus.
EDCS-46600042.summary: This tomb inscription records a daughter's dedication to her father, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-46600043.text_edition: \n Lucio Veratio Martiali Lucius Veratius Lupus patri posuit\n
EDCS-46600043.translation: Lucius Veratius Lupus placed this for his father, Lucius Veratius Martialis.
EDCS-46600043.summary: This tomb inscription records a son's dedication to his father, likely from the 1st to 3rd century AD.

EDCS-46600044.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus P<e=F>tronia<e=F> Ursa<e> Sallustius Paramythius pat<e=F>r filiae ntatura<l=I>i bene merenti fec<i=L>t \n
EDCS-46600044.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Sallustius Paramythius made this for his well-deserving daughter Petronia Ursa.
EDCS-46600044.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a father for his daughter, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46600045.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aemil<i=L>o Attacino <q=C>(ui) vixit bonae memoriae annorum LXXXVII Paulus libertus posuit \n
EDCS-46600045.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Paulus, his freedman, placed this for Aemilius Attacinus, who lived with a good memory for 87 years.
EDCS-46600045.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated by a freedman for his patron, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46600046.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sibi et coniugi Maximae annorum XL posui<t=S> Maximus III \n
EDCS-46600046.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Maximus III placed this for himself and his wife Maxima, aged 40.
EDCS-46600046.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Maximus III for himself and his wife, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46600047.text_edition: \n - Procline vixit annos L Maximus et Melio filii annos IX\n
EDCS-46600047.translation: Procline lived 50 years. Maximus and Melio, sons, [lived] 9 years.
EDCS-46600047.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription recording the ages of Procline and two sons, Maximus and Melio, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46700052.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name and age of Seneca, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46700053.text_edition: \n Aurelius Ursus annorum XXXX coniugi posuit\n
EDCS-46700053.translation: Aurelius Ursus placed this for his wife, aged 40.
EDCS-46700053.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Aurelius Ursus for his wife, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46700054.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription recording the name and age of Aurelius, possibly with another name Saria, from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46700055.text_edition: \n enoni a filia matri posuit bene merenti\n
EDCS-46700055.translation: To her deserving mother, from her daughter.
EDCS-46700055.summary: A funerary epitaph from a daughter to her mother, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46700056.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the name and age of Aurelius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46700057.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by Martinus, indicating his devotion, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-46700058.summary: A tombstone recording the age of a woman named Ansilla.

EDCS-46700059.text_edition: \n annorum LX pius posuit bene merenti\n
EDCS-46700059.translation: At the age of 60, the devoted one set this up for the well-deserving.
EDCS-46700059.summary: A funerary inscription indicating that a devoted person erected a monument for a well-deserving individual who died at age 60.

EDCS-46700061.text_edition: \n Festae annorum VIIII Crescens pater posuit filiae hic sita est\n
EDCS-46700061.translation: Crescens, her father, set this up for Festa, aged 9. She lies here.
EDCS-46700061.summary: A funerary epitaph by a father for his daughter Festa, who died at age 9.

EDCS-47800005.text_edition: \n nus M sibi et Clemen ssimo F \n
EDCS-47800005.translation: M.nus for himself and his son Clemens.
EDCS-47800005.summary: A dedication, likely for a tombstone or memorial, by M.nus for himself and his son Clemens.

EDCS-47800126.summary: A fragment of a Latin inscription with illegible content, possibly part of a name or title.

EDCS-47800131.summary: A fragmentary inscription that begins with the name Flavius, indicating the start of a personal name or title.

EDCS-47800136.summary: A very fragmented inscription, likely part of a longer text, with only partial letters remaining.

EDCS-47800186.summary: A fragmented inscription possibly related to a burial or a place of deposition, with the name 'Sinia' or similar suggested by the last legible letters.

EDCS-47800189.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales

EDCS-47800241.summary: A fragment of an inscription mentioning a female name ending in '-mava', possibly identifying her as a daughter.

EDCS-47800263.text_edition: \n ae quae vixit annos XX et Gel \n
EDCS-47800263.summary: A funerary inscription recording the lifespan of a woman, [---]ae, who lived 20 years, with the beginning of another name possibly following.

EDCS-47800364.summary: This is a funerary inscription, likely a dedication, with heavily restored text.

EDCS-47800390.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-47800390.summary: This appears to be a label or inscription related to a sarcophagus, possibly indicating capacity or ownership.

EDCS-47800404.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus oriae simae VD si quis \n
EDCS-47800404.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, for Oria Sima, a respected woman. If anyone...
EDCS-47800404.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Oria Sima, beginning with a common invocation to the deities of the Underworld.

EDCS-47800495.text_edition: \n vianae defunctae annorum plus minus VII pater \n
EDCS-47800495.translation: ...for the deceased Viana, who lived approximately seven years, her father...
EDCS-47800495.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Viana, recording her approximate age and mentioning her father as the dedicator.

EDCS-47800512.summary: This is a brief inscription indicating that someone erected this monument for a person named Ianuarius.

EDCS-47800533.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, Constant[ia?]...
EDCS-47800533.summary: This is a funerary inscription beginning with an invocation to the Spirits of the Underworld, dedicated to Constantia (or a related name).

EDCS-47800534.text_edition: \n cus ex nomine Mes cae qua[e vixit ------\n
EDCS-47800534.translation: ...from the name of Mesca, who lived ------
EDCS-47800534.summary: This funerary inscription records the name Mesca and the duration of her life, which is missing.

EDCS-47800539.summary: This is a fragmented inscription that likely records a gentilicium or cognomen and possibly an origin (Salona).

EDCS-47800566.summary: This is a highly fragmented inscription that seems to mention a son.

EDCS-47800725.text_edition: \n Domitio Vincentio qui vixit annis quinque diebus numero XX- Domitius Eufrasus filio karissimo in pace fecit\n
EDCS-47800725.translation: Domitius Eufrasus made [this] for his dearest son, Domitius Vincentius, who lived for five years and twenty days, in peace.
EDCS-47800725.summary: This is a touching funerary epitaph for a young boy, Domitius Vincentius, erected by his father, Domitius Eufrasus.

EDCS-47800730.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-47800730.translation: A tomb for himself, built in kindness.
EDCS-47800730.summary: This is a tomb inscription recording the construction of a burial chamber for the deceased.

EDCS-47800734.text_edition: \n Leuntius sic vir respectabilis praepositus p et ex curatoribus mihi posuit annorum ? LXXX\n
EDCS-47800734.translation: Leuntius, a man of respect, prefect and former curator, set this up for me, at the age of 80 years.
EDCS-47800734.summary: This funerary inscription records that Leuntius, a former prefect and curator, erected a monument for someone who lived to be 80 years old.

EDCS-47800735.text_edition: \n Sit tibi notum recordationi QV Patricius hic iacet qui DOCI in officio praefecturae et BINEC vitam suam QVAD CIRM \n
EDCS-47800735.translation: Let it be known to you that Patricius lies here, who [served?] in the office of the prefecture and [lived?] his life...
EDCS-47800735.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Patricius, who served in the prefecture.

EDCS-47800737.text_edition: \n Depositio viri devoti T IORISD NSVD \n
EDCS-47800737.translation: The burial of a devoted man...
EDCS-47800737.summary: This is a fragmented funerary inscription recording the burial of a devoted man.

EDCS-47800739.text_edition: \n NA Ingenuposuit filiae III IERENTRONIIE illae quae I vixit annis \n
EDCS-47800739.translation: Ingenuus set this up for his three daughters, who lived...
EDCS-47800739.summary: This inscription records that Ingenuus set up a monument for his three daughters.

EDCS-48000051.summary: This is a fragmented inscription with largely illegible text.

EDCS-48000063.text_edition: \n a Publicia virginia vixit annos posuit\n
EDCS-48000063.translation: Publicia Virginia lived [for?] ... years; she set this up.
EDCS-48000063.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Publicia Virginia lived for an unstated number of years and erected this monument.

EDCS-48000066.translation: Marcus, his son, [erected this] to his mother.
EDCS-48000066.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording that Marcus erected a monument to his mother.

EDCS-48000067.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription recording an action made in a specific year.

EDCS-48000069.translation: To a most unhappy son, from his parents.
EDCS-48000069.summary: This funerary inscription records parents dedicating a monument to their unfortunate son.

EDCS-48000079.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli honorarii, tituli sacri
EDCS-48000079.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Flavio Constanti victori ac triumfatori Flavius Iulius Rufinus Sarmentinus vir clarissimus praeses provinciae Dalmatiae devotus numini maiestatique eius\n
EDCS-48000079.translation: To our lord Flavius Constantius, victor and triumphator, Flavius Iulius Rufinus Sarmentinus, most excellent, governor of the province of Dalmatia, devoted to his divine majesty.
EDCS-48000079.summary: This is a dedication to the emperor Constantius II by the governor of Dalmatia, Flavius Iulius Rufinus Sarmentinus, likely from the 4th century CE.

EDCS-48100001.translation: Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus, chief priest, in his fifth tribunician power, commander for the 13th time, father of the country, consul for the fifth time, designated for the sixth, censor, rebuilt this, under Gnaeus Pedius Cascus, imperial legate pro praetor.
EDCS-48100001.summary: This is a dedication from the reign of Vespasian (69-79 CE), recording the rebuilding of something by a legate under his authority.

EDCS-48100007.inscription_type: inscriptiones christianae, tituli operum, tituli sepulcrales
EDCS-48100007.text_edition: \n Arca Andreae VMERVNOBVCN NICATE coniugi eius\n
EDCS-48100007.translation: To Andrea and Iulius, and their wife Merunia, to his wife.
EDCS-48100007.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Andrea and Iulius, and his wife Merunia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-48100010.summary: This is a dedication to a lord named Menna, possibly from the late Roman period.

EDCS-48100017.summary: This is a dedication, possibly funerary or votive, to Vplaa Sabinianus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-48100053.text_edition: \n Aeli-- Victor mensium V dierum XX \n
EDCS-48100053.translation: Aelius Victor, for 5 months and 20 days.
EDCS-48100053.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the lifespan of Aelius Victor, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51000060.text_edition: \n Invicto Mithrae sacrum Caius O C pro salute sua votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-51000060.translation: Sacred to the Unconquered Mithras. Gaius freely paid his vow for his own well-being.
EDCS-51000060.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the god Mithras by Gaius for his health, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51200024.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caesidio Quadratiano patri pientissimo qui vixit annos LXXX Tertianus et Quadratianus filii fecerunt\n
EDCS-51200024.translation: To the Divine Manes. Caesidius Quadratus, most dutiful father, who lived 80 years, Tertianus and Quadratus, his sons, made this.
EDCS-51200024.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by two sons, Tertianus and Quadratus, for their pious father Caesidius Quadratus, who lived 80 years, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51200026.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Aelius, aged 35, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51400001.text_edition: \n ae puellae quae vixit nnos XXIII menses IIII diesI Valentinianus et Cassianilla filiai sic piissimai sic quai sic deposita die III Nonas Martias in ove\n
EDCS-51400001.translation: To our daughter who lived 23 years, 4 months, and some days, Valentinianus and Cassianilla, her most dutiful daughters, who was laid to rest on the 3rd day before the Nones of March, in the year...
EDCS-51400001.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a daughter, set up by her two daughters Valentinianus and Cassianilla, dating to the 3rd day before the Nones of March (likely from the 4th-5th century CE due to the names).

EDCS-51400003.text_edition: \n Sividius miles Catullinus de numero prum TVTIQ\n
EDCS-51400003.translation: Sividius Miles Catullinus from the number of the Tiquti.
EDCS-51400003.summary: This inscription records a soldier named Sividius Catullinus, possibly indicating his unit affiliation, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-51400004.text_edition: \n Lucius Vettius Marci filius Maecia Secundus Libarna veteranus legionis VII vivus fecit sibi et Vettiae Luci filiae Verae et Vettiae Luci libertae S\n
EDCS-51400004.translation: Lucius Vettius Secundus, son of Marcus, of the Maecia tribe, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, made this while alive for himself and for Vettia Vera, daughter of Lucius, and for Vettia, freedwoman of Lucius.
EDCS-51400004.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Lucius Vettius Secundus, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, for himself and two women, likely his wife and a freedwoman, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-51400005.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Arria Ner sibi et Arrrio Platori coniugi vivi sibi fecerunt\n
EDCS-51400005.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Arria Ner and her husband Arrrius Plator made this for themselves while alive.
EDCS-51400005.summary: This inscription is a funerary dedication to the Manes, set up by a couple, Arria Ner and Arrrius Plator, for themselves during their lifetime, probably from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-51400006.text_edition: \n aurus defunctus Aelia Maxima posuit marito suo et sibi\n
EDCS-51400006.translation: Aurelius, deceased. Aelia Maxima placed this for her husband and for herself.
EDCS-51400006.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument placed by Aelia Maxima for her deceased husband, Aurelius, and herself, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-51400007.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Messius CIRBN vivi sibi et Aureliae Surae carissimae coniugi posuerunt\n
EDCS-51400007.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aurelius Messius and his dearest wife Aurelia Sura placed this while alive.
EDCS-51400007.summary: This funerary dedication to the Manes was set up by Aurelius Messius and his wife Aurelia Sura for themselves during their lifetime, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-51400008.text_edition: \n defunctae nnorum XL posuit Aelia Ursa filia matri pientissimae\n
EDCS-51400008.translation: deceased, aged 40. Aelia Ursa, his daughter, placed this for her most devoted mother.
EDCS-51400008.summary: This inscription records a funerary monument set up by Aelia Ursa for her deceased mother, who was 40 years old, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-51400010.text_edition: \n suis Buo contubernali et bonis NI\n
EDCS-51400010.translation: for his companions Buo and the good Ni...
EDCS-51400010.summary: This fragmentary inscription likely records a dedication to companions, possibly military, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-51400011.translation: Publius Aelius son of Titus Aelius...
EDCS-51400011.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription that begins to record the name and filiation of Publius Aelius, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-51400012.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus patri Iulio --- pienitissimo posuit monumentum \n
EDCS-51400012.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. For his most devoted father Iulius... he placed this monument.
EDCS-51400012.summary: This funerary inscription dedicates a monument to a highly devoted father named Iulius, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-51400013.summary: This extremely fragmentary inscription begins with the name Agrippa, offering little context but likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-51400014.text_edition: \n Isiorum tribuno militum marito karissimo fecit\n
EDCS-51400014.translation: She made this for Isior, her most beloved husband, a military tribune.
EDCS-51400014.summary: A wife dedicates this monument to her husband, a military tribune, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-51400015.text_edition: \n Lucius Peticius Luci libertus Helius vivus fecit sibi et suis et Fabiae Veneriae uxori\n
EDCS-51400015.translation: Lucius Peticius Helius, freedman of Lucius, made this while alive for himself, his family, and his wife Fabia Veneria.
EDCS-51400015.summary: A freedman sets up a tombstone for himself and his family, including his wife, during his lifetime.

EDCS-51400016.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Siliciae Ditueioni Aulo Sellio Pub coniugi\n
EDCS-51400016.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Silicia Ditueio, wife of Aulus Sellius Publius.
EDCS-51400016.summary: A tombstone dedicated by a husband to his deceased wife, invoking the spirits of the underworld.

EDCS-51400046.text_edition: \n Calvidiae Publi filiae Secundae uxori piissimae Annaeus Publi filius Tromentina Aper aedilis IIIIvir iure dicundo et Annaeo Publi filio Tironi aedili IIIIviro iure dicundo et Annaeo Publi filio Apro et sibi et suis\n
EDCS-51400046.translation: For Calvidia Secunda, daughter of Publius, his most dutiful wife; Annaeus Publius Aper, from the tribe Tromentina, aedile and holder of the office of duovir for administering justice; and for Annaeus Publius Tiron, son of Publius, an aedile and duovir for administering justice; and for Annaeus Publius Aper; and for himself and his family.
EDCS-51400046.summary: A complex funerary inscription set up by Annaeus Publius Aper for his wife, sons, and himself, listing their magistracies, likely dating to the late Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-51400050.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aemilius Felici et Melitine MP \n
EDCS-51400050.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Aemilius Felici and Melitine.
EDCS-51400050.summary: A dedication to the deceased Aemilius Felici and Melitine, invoking the spirits of the underworld, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-51400052.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus ius Celer MACVM a sodali \n
EDCS-51400052.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Celer, a comrade.
EDCS-51400052.summary: A tombstone dedicated to Celer, identified as a comrade, invoking the spirits of the underworld.

EDCS-51400058.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Iunius Messor ex iussu posuit\n
EDCS-51400058.translation: Lucius Iunius Messor erected this by order to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-51400058.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Iunius Messor, likely on behalf of himself or others following a vow or command.

EDCS-51400059.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Fructuosae defunctae annorum X Fructula filiae\n
EDCS-51400059.translation: To the spirits of the underworld, for Fructuosa, deceased, aged ten years, [for] Fructula, his daughter.
EDCS-51400059.summary: A tombstone for a young girl named Fructuosa, dedicated by her daughter Fructula, invoking the spirits of the underworld.

EDCS-51800031.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Maxitto vixit annos XXX Vipius coniunx coniugi carissimae vivus pro pietate\n
EDCS-51800031.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Maxitto lived thirty years. Vipius, her husband, [erected this] for his dearest wife, while alive, out of devotion.
EDCS-51800031.summary: A tombstone set up by a husband for his wife, stating her age and his devotion, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51800032.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Aplis Aurelius Argurianus \n
EDCS-51800032.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Aplis. Aurelius Argurianus.
EDCS-51800032.summary: A funerary dedication by Aurelius Argurianus to Aplis, invoking the spirits of the underworld, likely a tombstone.

EDCS-51800037.text_edition: \n Herculenti sic Aurelius Dassto sic Triti posuit \n
EDCS-51800037.translation: Herculenti, thus Aurelius Dassto and Tritus placed it.
EDCS-51800037.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Aurelius Dassto and Tritus for Herculenti, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51800048.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning Aelius Urso, possibly a dedication or funerary marker from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51800137.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Conservator Augustus, Aelius...
EDCS-51800137.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus Conservator Augustus by Aelius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51800138.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelia Sergia Marci filia Maxima Gemellina Luci liberta votum solverunt libentes merito\n
EDCS-51800138.translation: Aurelia Sergia, daughter of Marcus, Maxima Gemellina, freedwoman of Lucius, fulfilled their vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-51800138.summary: This is a votive inscription by Aurelia Sergia and Lucius, dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51800141.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aureliae annorum XXX Secundae coniugi piae posuit\n
EDCS-51800141.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, he placed [this] for Aurelia Secunda, his devoted wife, aged thirty.
EDCS-51800141.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by a husband for his wife Aurelia Secunda, who died at the age of thirty, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51800150.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Publius Aelius AriRES IIE \n
EDCS-51800150.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, Publius Aelius AriRES IIE...
EDCS-51800150.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription dedicating something to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Publius Aelius, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51800151.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Titus Flavius Albanus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-51800151.translation: Titus Flavius Albanus fulfilled his vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-51800151.summary: This is a votive inscription by Titus Flavius Albanus dedicated to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-51800206.summary: This is a brief votive inscription by Silvia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-52400214.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Papinio Firmo heredes Papinius Laetinus et Papinia Paula et Papinius Laetio filii bene merenti posuerunt\n
EDCS-52400214.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed, his heirs Papinius Laetinus, Papinia Paula, and Papinius Laetio erected [this] for Lucius Papinius Firmus, their deserving relation.
EDCS-52400214.summary: This is a funerary inscription erected by the heirs of Lucius Papinius Firmus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-53500001.translation: To the Unconquered Sun God, Metherae...
EDCS-53500001.summary: This is a dedication to the Unconquered Sun God, possibly by someone named Metherae, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-54701268.text_edition: \n Iulia Cai filia Quieta viva fecit sibi\n
EDCS-54701268.translation: Iulia Quieta, daughter of Caius, made this for herself while alive.
EDCS-54701268.summary: A tombstone erected by Iulia Quieta for herself during her lifetime.

EDCS-54900234.text_edition: \n Tiberius Caesar divi Augusti filius Augustus Imperator pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XIIX consul II viam a colonia Salonitana ad fines provinciae Illyrici cuius viai sic millia passus sic sunt CLXVII munit per vexillarios legionum VII et XI item viam Gabinianam ab Salonis Andetrium aperuit et munit per legionem VII\n
EDCS-54900234.translation: Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the deified Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 18th year, consul for the 2nd time, constructed the road from the colony of Salona to the borders of the province of Illyricum, which is 167 miles long, by detachments of the 7th and 11th legions; likewise, he opened and constructed the Gabinian road from Salona to Andetrium with the 7th legion.
EDCS-54900234.summary: An inscription detailing the road construction projects undertaken by Emperor Tiberius, likely in the mid-1st century AD.

EDCS-54900235.text_edition: \n Tiberius Caesar divi Augusti filius Augustus imperator pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate XXI consul III viam a Salonis ad Hedum castellum Daesitiatium per millia sic passuum CLVI munit et idem viam ad Batinum flumen quod dividit Breucos Oseriatibus a Salonis munit per millia sic passuum CLVIII et idem viam munit ad imum montem Ditionum Ulcirum per millia sic passuum a Salonis LXXVIID Publio Dolabella legato pro praetore\n
EDCS-54900235.translation: Tiberius Caesar Augustus, son of the deified Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 21st year, consul for the 3rd time, constructed the road from Salona to Hedum, a fortress of the Daesitiates, which is 156 miles long, and he also constructed the road to the river Batinus, which separates the Breuci from the Oseriates, which is 158 miles long from Salona, and he also constructed the road to the foot of the Ditiones mountains of the Ulciuri, which is 77 miles from Salona, under Publius Dolabella, governor and propraetor.
EDCS-54900235.summary: This inscription records Emperor Tiberius's extensive road building activities in the region of Illyricum, under the governor Publius Dolabella, likely in the late 1st century AD.

EDCS-54900279.text_edition: \n Domino nostro Galerio Valerio Maximino nobilissimo Caesari\n
EDCS-54900279.translation: To our lord, Galerius Valerius Maximinus, most noble Caesar.
EDCS-54900279.summary: A dedication to Galerius Valerius Maximinus, a Roman Caesar, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-54900288.translation: To the Emperor, our lord, Probus Augustus.
EDCS-54900288.summary: A dedication to Emperor Probus, reigning in the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-54900336.text_edition: \n Imperatori Caesari Galerio Valerio Maximiano Pio Felici Invicto\n
EDCS-54900336.translation: To the Emperor Caesar Galerius Valerius Maximianus, the Pious, Fortunate, Invincible.
EDCS-54900336.summary: A dedication to Emperor Galerius Valerius Maximianus, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-54900345.text_edition: \n Pius Felix pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate II imperator III consul pater patriae et Caius Iulius Verus Maximus nobili[ssimus Caesar ------\n
EDCS-54900345.translation: The Pious, Fortunate, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 2nd year, Emperor for the 3rd time, Father of the Fatherland, and Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, most noble Caesar ------
EDCS-54900345.summary: A fragment of a dedication mentioning Emperor Maximinus Daia and likely another emperor, dating to the early 4th century AD.

EDCS-54900348.text_edition: \n Imperatoribus dominis nostris Caio Valerio Diocletiano Pio Felici Augusto et Marco Aurelio Maximiano Augusto\n
EDCS-54900348.translation: To our lords Emperors Gaius Valerius Diocletian, the Pious, Fortunate, Augustus, and Marcus Aurelius Maximian, Augustus.
EDCS-54900348.summary: A dedication to co-emperors Diocletian and Maximian, likely from the late 3rd or early 4th century AD.

EDCS-54900353.text_edition: \n Marco Aurelio Carino nobilissimo Caesari Imperatori domino nostro Diocletiano Augusto\n
EDCS-54900353.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Carinus, most noble Caesar, Emperor, our lord, and to Diocletian Augustus.
EDCS-54900353.summary: A dedication to Emperors Carinus and Diocletian, likely from the late 3rd century AD.

EDCS-55100445.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar Vespasianus Augustus pontifex maximus tribunicia potestate VIII imperator XVIII pater patriae\n
EDCS-55100445.translation: Emperor Caesar Vespasian Augustus, Pontifex Maximus, with tribunician power for the 8th year, Emperor for the 18th time, Father of the Fatherland.
EDCS-55100445.summary: An inscription detailing the titles and honors of Emperor Vespasian, likely from the late 1st century AD.

EDCS-55500822.text_edition: \n Aurelio Titiano decurioni municipii IIviro avo et proavo defuncto nnorum \n
EDCS-55500822.translation: To Aurelius Titianus, councilor of the municipality, twice a magistrate, whose grandfather and great-grandfather died [in office],
EDCS-55500822.summary: This inscription likely records an honorific monument for Aurelius Titianus, highlighting his family's political career, possibly dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-55701978.text_edition: \n Neptuno sacrum periculorum absolutori Petronius Asper votum solvit libens animo\n
EDCS-55701978.translation: To Neptune, averter of dangers. Petronius Asper gladly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-55701978.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Neptune by Petronius Asper, who offered thanks for deliverance from peril, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-55701979.text_edition: \n Mandetae Victorinae nepotiae quae vixit annos XII posuit\n
EDCS-55701979.translation: Victorina Mandeta erected [this] for her granddaughter who lived 12 years.
EDCS-55701979.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Victorina Mandeta for her young granddaughter, probably dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-55701980.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aelius Acutianus vivus posuit filio suo Gentio qui vixit annos III\n
EDCS-55701980.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Aelius Acutianus erected [this] while alive for his son Gentius, who lived 3 years.
EDCS-55701980.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Aelius Acutianus for his young son Gentius, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-55701981.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cipius Statianus vixit annos L et Aurelia Panto vixit annos LX\n
EDCS-55701981.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Cipius Statianus lived 50 years and Aurelia Panto lived 60 years.
EDCS-55701981.summary: This inscription records the ages at death for Cipius Statianus and Aurelia Panto, likely a married couple, dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-55701982.text_edition: \n Silvano Augusto sacrum Aurelius Plares libens posuit\n
EDCS-55701982.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Aurelius Plares gladly erected [this].
EDCS-55701982.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Silvanus Augustus by Aurelius Plares, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-55701983.text_edition: \n us Nov Maximus pientissimi nepotes MOV vivi et Samiano et Rufinus vivi ipsi sibi fecerunt\n
EDCS-55701983.translation: Nov Maximus' most devoted grandsons MOV, living, and Samianus and Rufinus, living, made [this] for themselves.
EDCS-55701983.summary: This inscription records a self-funded monument created by grandsons for their grandfather Nov Maximus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-55701984.text_edition: \n Caius Iulius Mara veteranus cohortis II Cyrrhestarum nnorum XC domo Beroea testamento fieri iussit Magnus libertus idemque heres posuit\n
EDCS-55701984.translation: Gaius Julius Mara, a veteran of the II Cohort of Cyrrhestans, aged 90, from Beroea, ordered [this] to be made by his will. Magnus, his freedman and likewise heir, erected [it].
EDCS-55701984.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Julius Mara, a veteran, erected by his freedman and heir, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-55701985.text_edition: \n Magia Luci filia Maxima Nimphis sic votum solvit libens laeta\n
EDCS-55701985.translation: Magia Maxima, daughter of Lucius, fulfilled her vow to the Nymphs gladly and joyfully.
EDCS-55701985.summary: This is a votive inscription by Magia Maxima to the Nymphs, expressing gratitude, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-55701987.text_edition: \n Dianae Augustae sacrum Titus R S votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-55701987.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Titus R S gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-55701987.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to Diana Augusta by Titus R S, expressing gratitude for divine favor, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-55701989.text_edition: \n Imperatori Marco Aurelio Probo Pio Felici Invicto Augusto \n
EDCS-55701989.translation: To the Emperor Marcus Aurelius Probus, Pious, Fortunate, Invincible, Augustus.
EDCS-55701989.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Roman Emperor Marcus Aurelius Probus, likely from the late 3rd century CE.

EDCS-55701990.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the Roman Emperor Maximinus Caesar, likely from the early 4th century CE.

EDCS-55701992.summary: This inscription appears to record a land division or boundary, possibly related to a military unit or a property owner named Pompeius, date uncertain.

EDCS-55701993.text_edition: \n Lucius NARION filius miles cohortis Cyrrhestarum domo Beroea nnorum L stipendiorum XV hic situs est testamento fieri iussit\n
EDCS-55701993.translation: Lucius Narion, son of Narion, a soldier of the cohort of Cyrrhestae, from the town of Beroea, aged 50 years, with 15 years of service, is buried here. He ordered this to be made by his will.
EDCS-55701993.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Narion, a soldier from Beroea serving in the Cyrrhestae cohort, dated to his death, likely between the 1st and 3rd centuries CE.

EDCS-55900002.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Harmonia, set up by Naius, the date of which is uncertain.

EDCS-56900018.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Statiae Provinciali Statia Messaliane matri bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-56900018.translation: To the Divine Shades, Statia Messaliane, her well-deserving mother, placed this.
EDCS-56900018.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Statia for her mother, Statia Messaliane, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century CE.

EDCS-56900048.text_edition: \n patri bono defunctus in sepultus in n annorum CX <v=F>ixit \n
EDCS-56900048.translation: He lived 110 years and was buried... [due to damage and restoration] to a good father, deceased.
EDCS-56900048.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph recording the age and burial of an individual, possibly honoring a father, date uncertain.

EDCS-56900079.summary: This inscription is a very brief personal name, likely an owner or dedicator, date uncertain.

EDCS-56900081.translation: Slator, son of Oeplos, aged 10 years.
EDCS-56900081.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph recording the name and age of a child, Slator, who died at age 10, date uncertain.

EDCS-56900082.text_edition: \n nnos XXI Sinus? --- Andes Sini fi[lius ------\n
EDCS-56900082.translation: 21 years... Andes, son of Sinus, son of Andes [restored names and details].
EDCS-56900082.summary: This is a fragmentary epitaph recording the age of a person named Andes, son of Sinus, and possibly his father, date uncertain.

EDCS-56900090.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Titus defunctus annorum L Baezo uxor et sibi viva posuit\n
EDCS-56900090.translation: To the Divine Manes. Titus, deceased, aged 50. Baezo, his wife, erected this monument for herself and him while alive.
EDCS-56900090.summary: A funerary epitaph for Titus, erected by his wife Baezo, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-56900091.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Celtemio vixit annos LXX Titus Plati vivus titulum vivo ipsi posuit sibi et contubernali\n
EDCS-56900091.translation: To the Divine Manes. Celtemius lived 70 years. Titus Plati, alive, erected this monument for his living self and his companion.
EDCS-56900091.summary: A funerary epitaph for Celtemius and his companion, erected by Titus Plati, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-56900119.translation: To the Divine Manes for Publia Aelia Severina, mother.
EDCS-56900119.summary: A funerary epitaph for Publia Aelia Severina, dedicated by her child, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-56900123.summary: A fragment of a Roman inscription, possibly a name or title, with illegible parts, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-56900126.summary: A fragment of a Roman inscription, possibly containing a name, with illegible parts, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-56900134.summary: A fragment of a Roman inscription, possibly containing names or titles, with illegible parts, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-56900138.summary: An inscription recording an individual's membership in the vigintivirate and the imperial cult, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-56900139.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Valeria nata V -ertu fili \n
EDCS-56900139.translation: To the Divine Manes. Valeria, daughter of V[...].ertus.
EDCS-56900139.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria, daughter of V[...].ertus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-56900141.translation: To Octavius Pothinus, freedman of Cnaeus.
EDCS-56900141.summary: A funerary epitaph for Octavius Pothinus, freedman of Cnaeus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-56900149.summary: A fragment of a Roman inscription, possibly a name or dedication, with illegible parts, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-56900151.text_edition: \n Colchide sic uxori merenti qui sic vix<it=LI> annos XXX Dibus Manibus \n
EDCS-56900151.translation: To Colchis, my deserving wife, who lived thus for 30 years. To the Shades Below.
EDCS-56900151.summary: This is a funerary dedication by a husband to his deceased wife, Colchis.

EDCS-56900164.summary: This is a brief funerary dedication, likely to a person named Cretius from the Isti region.

EDCS-56900169.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, possibly recording names of individuals, perhaps deceased.

EDCS-56900170.translation: [Three letters missing] Marcus? [Three letters missing] VRO.
EDCS-56900170.summary: This is a highly fragmentary inscription, likely containing a personal name and possibly a cognomen or title.

EDCS-56900173.translation: C. Stertinius to his son, Licinius, PAII.
EDCS-56900173.summary: This is a funerary dedication from C. Stertinius to his son, Licinius, with an unclear final word.

EDCS-56900180.translation: To VL, my most unfortunate son.
EDCS-56900180.summary: This is a funerary dedication to a son, referring to him as 'most unfortunate', possibly indicating a premature death.

EDCS-56900183.text_edition: \n pientissimo dierum X VII Iulia Philia \n
EDCS-56900183.translation: To the most dutiful, for 17 days. Iulia Philia.
EDCS-56900183.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the brief life of a person, commemorated by Iulia Philia.

EDCS-56900188.text_edition: \n D(is) M(anibus) Flavio Aphrodisio Aphrodisia coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-56900188.translation: To the Shades Below. To Flavius Aphrodisius. To his wife Aphrodisia, the well-deserving.
EDCS-56900188.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Aphrodisia to her deceased husband, Flavius Aphrodisius.

EDCS-57000046.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a 'follower' and a significant lacuna, possibly a dedication or memorial.

EDCS-57000047.text_edition: \n Silvianus nnorum X pugnarum VII de suo sibi cui dolet posuit\n
EDCS-57000047.translation: Silvanus, 10 years old, 7 battles, erected this for himself because he suffers.
EDCS-57000047.summary: This unusual inscription records a young boy, Silvanus, who apparently fought in battles and erected this monument for himself.

EDCS-57000049.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Caesidio Primo defuncto nnorum V parentes infelicissimi\n
EDCS-57000049.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Lucius Caesidius Primus, deceased at five years of age. His most unhappy parents [set this up].
EDCS-57000049.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young child named Lucius Caesidius Primus, set up by his parents, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-57000054.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Marco Attio Verecundiano vetera qui vixit nnos LV Cania Ursina coniugi incomparabili posuit\n
EDCS-57000054.translation: To the spirits of the underworld. For Marcus Attius Verecundianus the Elder, who lived 55 years. Cania Ursina placed this for her incomparable husband.
EDCS-57000054.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Attius Verecundianus the Elder, dedicated by his wife Cania Ursina, probably from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-57100061.summary: A very brief inscription, likely a tombstone or boundary marker, identifying an individual named Verzo and his grandfather Bbai, date uncertain.

EDCS-57100085.text_edition: \n Imperator Caesar divi f[ilius Domitia][nus] Augustus pontifex maximus tribuniciae potestatis [XIIII] imperator [XXII] consul [XVI] censor perpetuus pater patriae aquam Flaviam Augustam novis fontibus collectis Flavio Fulfino induxit Lucius Sestius Dexter veteranus cohortis III praetoriae de sua pecunia faciundum curavit\n
EDCS-57100085.translation: Emperor Caesar Domitian Augustus, son of the deified [Nerva], chief priest, holder of tribunician power for the 14th time, hailed as imperator for the 22nd time, consul for the 16th time, perpetual censor, father of the fatherland. Lucius Sestius Dexter, a veteran of the III Praetorian cohort, arranged for the Aqua Flavia Augusta to be brought by new springs and to be constructed, at his own expense.
EDCS-57100085.summary: A dedication by a veteran, Lucius Sestius Dexter, for the construction of the Aqua Flavia Augusta, initiated by Emperor Domitian, likely dating to the late 1st century AD.

EDCS-57100086.translation: Boundary of the shepherds of Tarso.
EDCS-57100086.summary: A boundary marker defining the territory of shepherds from Tarso, date uncertain.

EDCS-57200001.text_edition: \n Turus Livius Hospotis filius et Volses Oplinocus Noventi filius praetores aedem Veneris ex pecunia publica reficiendum curaverunt idemque probaverunt\n
EDCS-57200001.translation: Taurus Livius Hospotis and Volses Oplinocus, son of Noventius, chief magistrates, arranged for the temple of Venus to be repaired with public funds and approved the work.
EDCS-57200001.summary: A record of public works concerning the repair of the temple of Venus, undertaken by two local magistrates (praetores), likely from a Roman provincial town, date uncertain.

EDCS-57200002.text_edition: \n Titus Baebius Titi filius Iustus decurio annorum XXXIX Tettia nis f(ilia) annorum XXI Titus Baebius Titi filius Iustinus\n
EDCS-57200002.translation: Titus Baebius Iustus, son of Titus, a decurion, aged 39 years. Tettia, daughter of Nis, aged 21 years. Titus Baebius Iustinus, son of Titus.
EDCS-57200002.summary: A tombstone for Titus Baebius Iustus, a decurion, and Tettia, likely his wife or daughter, possibly also commemorating another individual named Titus Baebius Iustinus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-57200003.text_edition: \n Marco Cassio Quinti filio Sergia Rufo equo publico pontifici nnum XVI decreto decurionum Sestia Marci filia Ravonilla filio piissimo inpensa remissa posuit\n
EDCS-57200003.translation: Sestia Ravonilla, daughter of Marcus, placed this for her most dutiful son Marcus Cassius Rufus, son of Quintus, who held the public equestrian position and was pontiff for 16 years, with the expenses waived by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-57200003.summary: A funerary epitaph for Marcus Cassius Rufus, a young man honored for his public service and religious role, set up by his mother Sestia Ravonilla, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-57200006.text_edition: \n Silvano sacrum Claudia Saturnina solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-57200006.translation: Claudia Saturnina paid [the vow] to Silvanus willingly and deservedly.
EDCS-57200006.summary: A votive inscription dedicated to the god Silvanus by Claudia Saturnina, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-57200010.text_edition: \n Petiliae Petili filiae Valentinae defunctae annorum VI mensium VIII dierum XVIII Primus et Seccia Silvia parentes filiae dulcissimae posuerunt\n
EDCS-57200010.translation: For Petilia Valentina, daughter of Petilius, deceased at six years, eight months, and eighteen days. Primus and Seccia Silvia, parents, placed this for their sweetest daughter.
EDCS-57200010.summary: A funerary epitaph for Petilia Valentina, a young child, erected by her parents Primus and Seccia Silvia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-57200011.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Ancharia Paulina Anchariae Primitivae libertae bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-57200011.translation: To the Divine Shades. Ancharia Paulina dedicated this to her freedwoman Ancharia Primitiva, who deserved well.
EDCS-57200011.summary: A funerary inscription from an unknown date recording a dedication by Ancharia Paulina to her freedwoman.

EDCS-57200025.text_edition: \n Priapo Augusto sacrum Lucius Cornelius Lupio aedem ampliavit votum solvit\n
EDCS-57200025.translation: Sacred to Priapus Augustus. Lucius Cornelius Lupio enlarged the temple and fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-57200025.summary: A dedication to Priapus Augustus, likely from the Imperial period, recording Lucius Cornelius Lupio's enlargement of a temple and fulfillment of a vow.

EDCS-57200031.summary: A very brief funerary inscription from an unknown date, simply recording the name of Anchiarius.

EDCS-57200032.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelius Titus Statiae Ursulae coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-57200032.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aurelius Titus for his wife Statia Ursula, who deserved well.
EDCS-57200032.summary: A funerary inscription from an unknown date recording Aurelius Titus's dedication to his wife.

EDCS-57200033.text_edition: \n uci Narcissus et Felicitas parentes loco concesso EI et Si\n
EDCS-57200033.translation: Narcissus and Felicitas, parents, in a plot granted to them and to Si.
EDCS-57200033.summary: A funerary inscription from an unknown date, likely from parents Narcissus and Felicitas, recording a burial plot.

EDCS-57200035.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Crescentio coniugi suo pientissimo posuit bene merenti\n
EDCS-57200035.translation: To the Divine Shades. Dedicated by his most dutiful wife to Crescentius, her husband, who deserved well.
EDCS-57200035.summary: A funerary inscription from an unknown date recording a wife's dedication to her husband, Crescentius.

EDCS-57200036.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Antonia Secunda Antoniae Secundae matri infelicissimae defunctae nnorum L bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-57200036.translation: To the Divine Shades. Antonia Secunda dedicated this to her most unhappy mother, Antonia Secunda, deceased at 50 years, who deserved well.
EDCS-57200036.summary: A funerary inscription from an unknown date recording Antonia Secunda's dedication to her mother, also named Antonia Secunda.

EDCS-57200037.text_edition: \n Magnae Matri Mescenia Publi filia porticum fecit de sua pecunia\n
EDCS-57200037.translation: To the Great Mother. Mescenia, daughter of Publius, built the portico with her own money.
EDCS-57200037.summary: A dedication to the Magna Mater, likely from the Imperial period, recording Mescenia's construction of a portico at her own expense.

EDCS-57200038.translation: To Iuliana and Ba and Donata.
EDCS-57200038.summary: A brief inscription from an unknown date, likely funerary, mentioning Iuliana, Ba, and Donata.

EDCS-57200039.summary: A fragment of a funerary inscription from an unknown date, recording parents' dedication to their daughter.

EDCS-57200040.text_edition: \n Caius Baebius Proculus de sua pecunia\n
EDCS-57200040.translation: Gaius Baebius Proculus, at his own expense.
EDCS-57200040.summary: This inscription records a private donation by Gaius Baebius Proculus.

EDCS-57200041.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Magiae Laetinae annorum XXVIIII Caius Magius Valens et Silia Laeta parentes filiae piissimae sibi et suis vivi fecerunt\n
EDCS-57200041.translation: Sacred to the Divine Manes. Gaius Magius Valens and Silia Laeta, parents, made this for their most devoted daughter, Magia Laetina, aged 29, for themselves and their family, while still alive.
EDCS-57200041.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Magia Laetina, set up by her parents, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-57200048.translation: To the Divine Manes, for Titus Statius Asprenas.
EDCS-57200048.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the departed soul of Titus Statius Asprenas.

EDCS-57200049.translation: To the Divine Manes, for Aurelius Quintus.
EDCS-57200049.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the departed soul of Aurelius Quintus.

EDCS-57200136.text_edition: \n Latrae Aug(ustae) libentes Gellii sub Clodio Gemino aedile [fecerunt]\n
EDCS-57200136.translation: The Gellii willingly made this for Latra Augusta, under the aedileship of Clodius Geminus.
EDCS-57200136.summary: This inscription records the construction of a monument by the Gellii family, dedicated to Latra Augusta, during the term of aedile Clodius Geminus, likely in the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-57200164.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus ICT DI VAPI A \n
EDCS-57200164.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the departed souls, though the recipient is illegible.

EDCS-57200167.text_edition: \n Species martialis orum VI V |L IIII P\n
EDCS-57200167.summary: This inscription seems to record measurements for a construction or monument, possibly related to 'Species Martialis', with dimensions indicated.

EDCS-57200180.text_edition: \n Helviae Luci filiae Maximillae Lucius Cassius posuit\n
EDCS-57200180.translation: Lucius Cassius placed this for Helvia Maximilla, daughter of Lucius.
EDCS-57200180.summary: This inscription records the placement of a monument by Lucius Cassius for Helvia Maximilla, his daughter.

EDCS-57200181.translation: Ursio, brother, placed this for Lupe of the Kaninii.
EDCS-57200181.summary: This inscription records the placement of a monument by Ursio for his sister, Lupe of the Kaninii family.

EDCS-57200183.text_edition: \n Baebiae Saturninae Iulia Iadestina amicae et sodali bene meritae viva posuit\n
EDCS-57200183.translation: Iulia Iadestina placed this for Baebia Saturnina, her friend and colleague, while alive and well-deserving.
EDCS-57200183.summary: This inscription records a tombstone erected by Iulia Iadestina for her deserving friend and colleague, Baebia Saturnina, during their lifetimes.

EDCS-57200184.text_edition: \n Lucio Papirio Rufo S Lucius Papirius Didimus sic pater filio se vivo posuit\n
EDCS-57200184.translation: To Lucius Papirius Rufos. Lucius Papirius Didimus erected this for his son while he was still alive.
EDCS-57200184.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a father to his son, Lucius Papirius Didimus, erected during the son's lifetime.

EDCS-57200186.summary: A tombstone recording the name of Lucius Paconius Fronto.

EDCS-57200190.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Lucio Bennio Honorato decurioni coloniae Aequensium qui vixit nnos X menses III Lucius Bennius Maximus et Flavia Tertulla parentes filio pientissimo et infelicissimo posuerunt\n
EDCS-57200190.translation: To the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Bennius Maximus and Flavia Tertulla erected this for their most dutiful and unfortunate son, Lucius Bennius Honoratus, decurion of the colony of Aequum, who lived 10 years and 3 months.
EDCS-57200190.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Bennius Honoratus, a 10-year-old decurion, erected by his parents.

EDCS-57200191.translation: Lucius Magius Celer, son of Sextus.
EDCS-57200191.summary: A tombstone recording the name of Lucius Magius Celer, son of Sextus.

EDCS-57300131.text_edition: \n Gaio Arruntio Felicissimo Sempronia Maximilla marito defuncto nnorum LXX bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-57300131.translation: Sempronia Maximilla erected this for her well-deserving husband, Gaius Arruntius Felicissimus, aged 70.
EDCS-57300131.summary: A funerary monument dedicated by a wife to her deceased husband, Gaius Arruntius Felicissimus, who was 70 years old.

EDCS-59500029.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Tiberius Claudius Gallus tribunus cohortis II Aureliae Delmatarum\n
EDCS-59500029.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Tiberius Claudius Gallus, tribune of the II Cohort of Aurelians from Dalmatia.
EDCS-59500029.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Tiberius Claudius Gallus, a military tribune.

EDCS-59500031.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Aurelia Tirentia sic pro se et pro s(uis) libens merito\n
EDCS-59500031.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Aurelia Tirentia dedicates this willingly and deservedly for herself and her family.
EDCS-59500031.summary: A votive dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Aurelia Tirentia on behalf of herself and her relatives.

EDCS-59500034.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Varro...
EDCS-59500034.summary: A partial dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by someone named Varro.

EDCS-59500035.summary: A sacred dedication to the god Silvanus Viator.

EDCS-59500036.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, [dedicated at] Scardona.
EDCS-59500036.summary: A dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, likely from the Roman settlement at Scardona.

EDCS-59500037.text_edition: \n Matri Deum Magnae Publius Domitius Publicola\n
EDCS-59500037.translation: To the Great Mother of the Gods, Publius Domitius Publicola.
EDCS-59500037.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the Great Mother of the Gods by Publius Domitius Publicola.

EDCS-59500038.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Lucius Apuleius Crispinianus dedicavit\n
EDCS-59500038.translation: Lucius Apuleius Crispinianus dedicated this to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.
EDCS-59500038.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by Lucius Apuleius Crispinianus.

EDCS-59500040.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Jupiter Tanarus.

EDCS-59500042.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Liber Pater, likely an abbreviated dedication.

EDCS-59500043.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo MGP EG beneficiarius consularis votum libens solvit\n
EDCS-59500043.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus, MGP EG, a soldier serving under a legate, willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-59500043.summary: This inscription records a dedication and fulfillment of a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a soldier.

EDCS-63400226.translation: To Gnaeus Cornelius Sabinus, son of Gnaeus, of the Papiria tribe, decurion, aedile(?), duovir, quinquennial duovir, pontifex, curator of public works, appointed by the Emperor Antoninus Pius Felix (?), judge from the five decuries, Vettidia Sabina, his mother, ordered this to be set up by her will; the place was granted by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-63400226.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gnaeus Cornelius Sabinus, detailing his civic and military career, set up by his mother.

EDCS-63400228.translation: To Castricius, son of Quintus, of the Camilia tribe, aedile(?), IIIvir, augur, (serving) Alba(?), awarded by Tiberius Caesar Augustus with torques, armillae, phaleris, and two gold crowns, decurion of Cremona, decurion of Iaderis, leader(?) of the Liburni and Iapydes, Castricius Severus, patron.
