EDCS-63400228.summary: This is an honorific inscription for Castricius, likely a military leader and civic official, who received awards from Emperor Tiberius.

EDCS-64100437.text_edition: \n Porciliae Tertullae aviae et Aeliae Marci filiae Marcellae matri Iulia Quinti filia Tertullina fecit\n
EDCS-64100437.translation: Iulia Tertullina, daughter of Quintus, made this for her grandmother Porcilia Tertulla and her mother Marcella, daughter of Marcus Aelius.
EDCS-64100437.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a monument by Iulia Tertullina to her grandmother and mother.

EDCS-64100438.summary: This inscription records the name Vinicia Paulina, likely a dedication or funerary marker.

EDCS-64100439.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed for Vinicius-
EDCS-64100439.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the underworld spirits (Dis Manibus) for someone named Vinicius.

EDCS-64100440.summary: This is a dedication to Gaius Octavius Silvester.

EDCS-64100441.text_edition: \n Tito Pinnio Titi liberto Cavario fratri Plecte et Ianuario nns Paetinae Pisseri sororis suae filiis Pinnia Plecte fecit\n
EDCS-64100441.translation: Pinnia Plecte made this for her brother Titus Pinnio Cavarius, freedman of Titus, and for the sons of her sister Pisseria, Plecte and Ianuarius, of the Paetina tribe.
EDCS-64100441.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Pinnia Plecte for her brother and nephews, likely from the late Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-64100442.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iulia Politta mater fecit Octaviae Celerinae filiae suae quae vixit annos XXXVI\n
EDCS-64100442.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Julia Politta made this for her daughter Octavia Celerina, who lived for 36 years.
EDCS-64100442.summary: This is a tombstone erected by Julia Politta for her deceased daughter Octavia Celerina, dating to the Imperial period.

EDCS-64100443.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Cordio Casio vixit annos LIV semis patri bene merenti\n
EDCS-64100443.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. To Cordius Cassius, who lived for 54 and a half years, a well-deserving father.
EDCS-64100443.summary: This tombstone commemorates Cordius Cassius, who lived 54.5 years, set up by a family member, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-64100444.summary: This is a dedication to Gaius Calpurnius Caesianus.

EDCS-64100445.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum dulcissimae filiae Epidiae quae vixit annos XXXVIII menses IIII infelicissima mater Eutichia sic memoriam fecit\n
EDCS-64100445.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For her most beloved daughter Epidia, who lived for 38 years and 4 months, the most unhappy mother Eutychia thus made this memorial.
EDCS-64100445.summary: This tombstone is a memorial set up by Eutychia for her daughter Epidia, who died at age 38 years and 4 months, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-64100446.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Titus Statius Lupus hic situs est Titus Statius Threptus\n
EDCS-64100446.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Titus Statius Lupus lies here. Titus Statius Threptus.
EDCS-64100446.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Titus Statius Lupus, with Titus Statius Threptus also mentioned, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-64100447.text_edition: \n Iuliae Crispinae ex testamento Stati Cilonis Egnatius Kapito <g=C>ener posuit \n
EDCS-64100447.translation: Egnatius Kapito Vener placed this according to the will of Julia]; Crispinus Statius Cilo.
EDCS-64100447.summary: This monument was erected by Egnatius Kapito Vener in accordance with the will of Statius Cilo for Iulia]; Crispinus, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-64100449.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Nonius Valens hic situs\n
EDCS-64100449.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Nonius Valens lies here.
EDCS-64100449.summary: This funerary inscription records the burial of Nonius Valens, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-64100450.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Caius Statius Paulinus vixit annos XXXIIII\n
EDCS-64100450.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Gaius Statius Paulinus lived for 34 years.
EDCS-64100450.summary: This tombstone commemorates Gaius Statius Paulinus, who lived 34 years, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-64100451.translation: To Octavia Quintina, daughter of Quintus.
EDCS-64100451.summary: This is a tombstone inscription for Octavia Quintina, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64100452.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iuliae Maximae vixit annos LXII Statius Lupus coniugi sanctissimae\n
EDCS-64100452.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Statius Lupus to his most holy wife, Iulia Maxima, who lived 62 years.
EDCS-64100452.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Iulia Maxima, set up by her husband Statius Lupus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64100453.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Sexto Caesonio Pulch<r>o <an=NA>norum XXXVII \n
EDCS-64100453.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. To Sextus Caesontus Pulcher, aged 37 years.
EDCS-64100453.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Sextus Caesontus Pulcher, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64100454.text_edition: \n Amarantho vixit nnos VI menses II Titus Flavius Zosimus filio vivus fecit sibi et suis\n
EDCS-64100454.translation: Amaranthus, who lived 6 years and 2 months. Titus Flavius Zosimus made this, while alive, for himself and his family, for his son.
EDCS-64100454.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Amaranthus, a child, set up by Titus Flavius Zosimus for his son and family, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64100457.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Maronio qui vixit annos \n
EDCS-64100457.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. For Maronius, who lived...
EDCS-64100457.summary: This is a partial funerary epitaph for Maronius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64100459.summary: This inscription records the name and origin of Sextus Manlius, likely related to a funerary or commemorative context.

EDCS-64100460.text_edition: \n Ipse implevi omni et lucernas accendi Ditus\n
EDCS-64100460.translation: I myself filled [this] and lit the lamps, Ditus.
EDCS-64100460.summary: This inscription records an action performed by Ditus, possibly related to a funerary rite or a dedication, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64100461.text_edition: \n Cereri Augustae sacrum Victoriae et Veneri Augustis Cnaeus \n
EDCS-64100461.translation: Sacred to Ceres Augusta. To Victoria and Venus Augustae. Gnaeus...
EDCS-64100461.summary: This is a dedication to the goddesses Ceres, Victoria, and Venus, likely by Gnaeus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64100462.text_edition: \n Silvano Viatori Augusto Libero Augusto Iuli--- \n
EDCS-64100462.translation: To Silvanus Viator Augustus, Liber Augustus. Iulius...
EDCS-64100462.summary: This is a dedication to the deities Silvanus Viator Augustus and Liber Augustus, likely by someone named Iulius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64100467.text_edition: \n Larciae Amarullini sic nnorum LXXX et filiae Gentillae nnorum XXXX filii fecerunt matri sui sic carissimae et serori sic\n
EDCS-64100467.translation: For Larcia Amarullina, aged 80 years, and her daughter Gentilla, aged 40 years. Her sons made this for their dearest mother and sister.
EDCS-64100467.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Larcia Amarullina and her daughter Gentilla, set up by their sons, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-64100468.text_edition: \n Publius Coelius Pamphilus vivus fecit sibi et Coeliae Muntillae filiae defunctae annorum XXI et Publio Coelio Niciphoro sic filio defuncto annorum XXVII et Coeliae Arescusae uxori vivae\n
EDCS-64100468.translation: Publius Coelius Pamphilus made this for himself while alive, and for his deceased daughter Coelia Muntilla, aged 21, and for his deceased son Publius Coelius Niciphorus, aged 27, and for his wife Coelia Arescusa while alive.
EDCS-64100468.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Publius Coelius Pamphilus for himself and his deceased family members.

EDCS-64100469.text_edition: \n Marco Aurelio Carino nobilissimo C<a>esari Imperatori domino nostro Diocletiano Augusto\n
EDCS-64100469.translation: To Marcus Aurelius Carinus, most noble Caesar, Emperor, our lord, Diocletian Augustus.
EDCS-64100469.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the emperor Diocletian, dating to his reign.

EDCS-64100470.text_edition: \n l Constan defeNsori rio MC piissimo Caesari bono rei publicae nato X\n
EDCS-64100470.translation: To Constantine, defender, most pious Caesar, born for the good of the republic.
EDCS-64100470.summary: This is a dedication to Constantine, likely as Caesar, praising his role in the state.

EDCS-64100473.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Marcus Valerius Sotericus votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-64100473.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Marcus Valerius Sotericus gladly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-64100473.summary: This is a votive inscription by Marcus Valerius Sotericus to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-64100474.translation: Sextus Atilius Rufus, son of Sextus.
EDCS-64100474.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely recording the name and parentage of an individual.

EDCS-64100475.text_edition: \n annis XL Ursus coniugi carissimaeP SIT\n
EDCS-64100475.translation: To Ursus, for 40 years, his most beloved spouse. May he rest in peace.
EDCS-64100475.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Ursus, who was a spouse for 40 years.

EDCS-65100038.translation: To the Divine Spirits, Septimia Bussumara, daughter of.
EDCS-65100038.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Septimia Bussumara.

EDCS-65100039.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum INF Secundina MV RIANO SecundVIVINAQVO OQTNVLEDX annos XXV M menses III bene merenti posuit\n
EDCS-65100039.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Secundina placed this for her deserving husband, who lived 25 years, 3 months.
EDCS-65100039.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Secundina for her deceased husband.

EDCS-65400055.text_edition: \n C Gelasius IIIbiae Plaiae et filiae baenae sic maerentibus sic fecit\n
EDCS-65400055.translation: C. Gelasius made this for his wife Plaia and his daughter Baena, who were grieving.
EDCS-65400055.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by C. Gelasius for his wife and daughter.

EDCS-65500044.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum Iuli-- MaLV II dies VII Iuli-- M \n
EDCS-65500044.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Iulius M. for 7 days. Iulius M.
EDCS-65500044.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, possibly recording the name and a duration of mourning or death for an individual named Iulius M.

EDCS-65500050.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus sacrum AILNV PLEPTO cissimae minia anno--- XIIII\n
EDCS-65500050.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ailnv Plepto, most excellent minia, in the year... the 14th.
EDCS-65500050.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the departed spirits and mentions Ailnv Plepto, possibly a woman, and a date possibly indicating her age or the year of death.

EDCS-65500082.translation: In the year... the 55th, Milius Cus to his wife.
EDCS-65500082.summary: This inscription records that Milius Cus dedicated something to his wife in the 55th year, likely referring to her age at death or a significant anniversary.

EDCS-65500084.summary: This inscription is fragmentary and its meaning is unclear, but it seems to record a name, Cisilla Iibus, and possibly an action or epithet.

EDCS-65500087.summary: This is a very brief inscription, likely the beginning of a funerary monument, recording the name 'S.' with a possible patronymic.

EDCS-65500088.translation: Carosor Ucivara to Vatina, IA INE.
EDCS-65500088.summary: This inscription is fragmentary and the precise meaning is obscure, but it seems to record names, potentially Carosor and Vatina, and is likely a dedication or funerary marker.

EDCS-66100163.summary: This inscription records a dedication, likely of a votive offering, to the deities 'ltia' and possibly 'Bellona'.

EDCS-66100164.translation: To the departed spirits. Messiano, infant student.
EDCS-66100164.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the departed spirits, commemorating Messiano, an infant who was a student.

EDCS-66100165.text_edition: \n ------ vi]xit annos XXX Munatia Secunda coniugi bene merenti\n
EDCS-66100165.translation: ...lived 30 years. Munatia Secunda to her well-deserving wife.
EDCS-66100165.summary: This funerary inscription records that someone lived 30 years and was dedicated by Munatia Secunda to her deserving wife.

EDCS-66200101.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Apulaeiae sic Primulae Urbicus Augusti libertus coniugi rarissimae \n
EDCS-66200101.translation: To the departed spirits. Apulaia, the rarest wife, Urbicus, freedman of Augustus, to his most rare wife.
EDCS-66200101.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to the departed spirits and records that Urbicus, a freedman of Augustus, set up the monument for his very rare wife, Apulaia.

EDCS-66200102.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Claudius Ursus iunior votum solvit\n
EDCS-66200102.translation: To Jupiter Optimus Maximus. Claudius Ursus the younger fulfilled [his] vow.
EDCS-66200102.summary: This inscription records Claudius Ursus the younger fulfilling a vow made to Jupiter Optimus Maximus.

EDCS-66200106.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus is HAER Q V<e=I>rinae C coniugi \n
EDCS-66200106.translation: To the spirits of the departed and to Haerina, his wife.
EDCS-66200106.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to the deceased wife, Haerina.

EDCS-66200163.text_edition: \n Lucius Oppius Luci filius Sergia Signinus domo Constantia veteranus legionis VII nnum LXXI hic situs est\n
EDCS-66200163.translation: Lucius Oppius, son of Lucius, of the Sergia tribe, from Signia, resident of Constantia, a veteran of the Seventh Legion, aged 71, is buried here.
EDCS-66200163.summary: This is a tombstone for Lucius Oppius, a 71-year-old veteran of the Seventh Legion.

EDCS-66400016.translation: To Crispinus, his most devoted husband, now deceased.
EDCS-66400016.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to a deceased husband named Crispinus.

EDCS-66600371.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly the beginning of a name and title.

EDCS-67300045.text_edition: \n AV et Catoni coniugi et nepoti annorum XXX et Dassio annorum XXV et \n
EDCS-67300045.translation: To Augustus and to Cato, husband and grandson aged 30, and to Dassius aged 25, and...
EDCS-67300045.summary: This inscription dedicates something to Augustus and lists several individuals, including a grandson and Dassius, with their ages.

EDCS-67300048.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription mentioning a patronymic and a son.

EDCS-67300051.summary: This inscription likely records the names of Aelius Ferox and Aelia Platino, possibly as dedicants or deceased individuals.

EDCS-67300056.text_edition: \n Iovi Optimo Maximo Cohortali posuit Titius Surus votum posuit libens\n
EDCS-67300056.translation: Titius Surus set up this vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Cohortalis, fulfilling it willingly.
EDCS-67300056.summary: This is a votive inscription where Titius Surus willingly fulfilled a vow to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Cohortalis.

EDCS-67300057.text_edition: \n Augusto sacrum Publius Aelius Secundus dec(urio) et Varro Dasantis posuerunt pro salute sua et collegio aerariorum BMILIAN\n
EDCS-67300057.translation: Publius Aelius Secundus, a decurion, and Varro Dasantis dedicated this sacred monument to Augustus for their own health and that of the guild of treasury officials.
EDCS-67300057.summary: This is a dedication to Augustus by Publius Aelius Secundus and Varro Dasantis for the well-being of themselves and the guild of treasury officials.

EDCS-67300133.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription beginning with the name Metinius Nilus.

EDCS-67400476.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Aurelia Turoni VIO VEIT heres posuit\n
EDCS-67400476.translation: To the Divine Shades. Aurelia Turoni erected this as heir.
EDCS-67400476.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Turoni, set up by her heir.

EDCS-67500106.text_edition: \n conservatrici potenti Calpurnia Luci Pisonis auguris filia Cnaei Pisonis neptis donum dedit\n
EDCS-67500106.translation: Calpurnia, daughter of the augur Lucius Piso and granddaughter of Gnaeus Piso, gave this gift to the powerful conservator.
EDCS-67500106.summary: This is a dedication to a powerful divine figure, made by Calpurnia, a woman of senatorial rank, likely from the Imperial period.

EDCS-67800034.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. For the safety of himself, his sons, all his freedmen, and his household, Publius Cornelius Donatus, a veteran, set this up in accordance with a vision.
EDCS-67800034.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus by a veteran named Publius Cornelius Donatus, dedicating it for the welfare of his family and household.

EDCS-68800075.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Father of his Country. Aurelia Maxima set this up.
EDCS-68800075.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus Patris, dedicated by Aurelia Maxima.

EDCS-69200413.translation: To the Divine Shades. To Lucius Adennius Philetianus, a soldier of the 8th cohort of volunteers, who served for 3 years, [restored text indicating some military details].
EDCS-69200413.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Adennius Philetianus, a soldier of the 8th cohort of volunteers, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-71300207.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. [Restored text indicating a dedication] by the cohort, possibly the 1st milliary, by order, willingly.
EDCS-71300207.summary: This is a partial votive inscription to Jupiter Optimus Maximus, possibly by a military cohort, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-71300208.translation: Most fortunate, Scarabantia willingly fulfilled her vow.
EDCS-71300208.summary: This is a short votive inscription recording the fulfillment of a vow by a woman named Scarabantia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-71300214.translation: To Jupiter Capitolinus. Marcus Ulpius Marcianus willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-71300214.summary: This is a votive inscription to Jupiter Capitolinus, dedicated by Marcus Ulpius Marcianus for fulfilling a vow.

EDCS-71400008.translation: Sacred to Diana Augusta. Aurelius Victor, Memor, and Paulinus set this up willingly on behalf of their relative Maximo Scenocalus.
EDCS-71400008.summary: This is a dedication to Diana Augusta made by Aurelius Victor, Memor, and Paulinus on behalf of their relative Maximo Scenocalus.

EDCS-71400190.translation: To Marcus Servilius, son of Gaius, consul, member of the board of seven for public banquets, governor under Caesar Augustus.
EDCS-71400190.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Marcus Servilius, a prominent Roman official who served under Augustus and held important priesthoods and governorships.

EDCS-71400191.translation: To the spirits of the departed. To Cattius Marcianus, deceased, aged 40 years. Cattia Valentina, for her most devoted brother.
EDCS-71400191.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Cattius Marcianus, set up by his sister Cattia Valentia.

EDCS-71400192.translation: To Valerius Licinius Felix Maximus, Father of the Country.
EDCS-71400192.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication, likely funerary, for Valerius Licinius Felix Maximus, a prominent figure.

EDCS-71400193.translation: To Bellia Laetina, for herself and her family, made while alive. Property...
EDCS-71400193.summary: This is a dedication for a property, set up by Bellia Laetina for herself and her family, likely for burial use.

EDCS-71400194.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication to Martialis, likely a veteran.

EDCS-71400582.translation: To Aurelia Prisca, a most noble lady.
EDCS-71400582.summary: This is a dedication to Aurelia Prisca, honoring her nobility.

EDCS-72200021.text_edition: \n Finis inter Neditas et Corinienses derectus mensuris actis iussu Auli Duceni Gemini legati per Aulum Resium Maximum |(centurionem) legionis XI principem posteriorem cohortis I et per Quintum Aebutium Liberalem |(centurionem) eiusdem legionis hastatum posteriorem cohortis I\n
EDCS-72200021.translation: The boundary between the Neditas and the Corinienses, set up by measured land under the orders of Aulus Ducenius Geminius, legate, through Aulus Resius Maximus, centurion of the 11th Legion, principal posterior of Cohort 1, and through Quintus Abutius Liberalis, centurion of the same legion, hastatus posterior of Cohort 1.
EDCS-72200021.summary: This inscription records the official demarcation of a boundary between two groups, the Neditas and the Corinienses, overseen by military officers.

EDCS-72700155.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aurelius Filo set up (this) for Julia Victorina, his deserving wife, while he was alive.
EDCS-72700155.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Aurelius Filo for his wife Julia Victorina, erected during his lifetime.

EDCS-72700156.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Petilia Saturnina, freedwoman, set up (this) for her deserving patron, P. Petilius Satus.
EDCS-72700156.summary: This is a funerary dedication by the freedwoman Petilia Saturnina for her patron P. Petilius Satus.

EDCS-72700157.summary: This is a dedication recording Firmus setting up an altar.

EDCS-72900249.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Augustus. Valerius set (it) up.
EDCS-72900249.summary: This is a dedication of a shrine or monument to the god Silvanus Augustus, set up by Valerius.

EDCS-72900250.text_edition: \n Silvano Domestico sacrum Titus Aurelius Dasas libens votum solvit\n
EDCS-72900250.translation: Sacred to Silvanus Domesticus. Titus Aurelius Dasas gladly paid his vow.
EDCS-72900250.summary: This is a dedication to the god Silvanus Domesticus, an inscription recording a vow paid by Titus Aurelius Dasas, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72900253.text_edition: \n Dis Manibus Atecto infanti qui vixit nnos VII menses III dies XII Sterceia mater filio infelicissimo posuit\n
EDCS-72900253.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Sterceia, the mother, placed this for her son Atectus, an infant who lived 7 years, 3 months, and 12 days, her most unhappy child.
EDCS-72900253.summary: This is a funerary inscription for an infant named Atectus, set up by his mother Sterceia, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-72900254.text_edition: \n Triviam sic Marcus T M pro Dio votum solvit libens merito\n
EDCS-72900254.translation: To Trivia. Marcus T. M. gladly and deservedly paid his vow for Dio.
EDCS-72900254.summary: This is a dedication to the goddess Trivia, an inscription recording a vow paid by Marcus T. M. for Dio, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-73700315.translation: Sacred to Mars. Titus Aurelius Marcus, a veteran, with his own people, gladly placed this.
EDCS-73700315.summary: This is a dedication to Mars, likely by a veteran named Titus Aurelius Marcus and his family, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-73700316.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Aelius Silvanus lived 2 years. Aelius Quintilianus placed this for his son, a most dutiful person.
EDCS-73700316.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a child named Aelius Silvanus, set up by his father Aelius Quintilianus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-73700317.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Statius Veloxianus and Severina placed this for their parents, Gaius Statius Bessio and his wife Aeronia Severina.
EDCS-73700317.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Statius Bessio and his wife Aeronia Severina, set up by their children Statius Veloxianus and Severina, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-74800001.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. For Publius Aelius Nova[---]no, decurion of the municipality of Balo[---]esium [---].
EDCS-74800001.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Publius Aelius Nova[---]no, a decurion of the municipality of Balo[---]esium, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-75300231.translation: Gaius Aulius Secundus, son of Gaius, of the Aniensis tribe, from Cremona, a veteran of the VII Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, lies here. Arria Agathemeris placed this for her dearest, well-deserving husband, and for Quintus Arrius Priscus. She placed it for herself while alive.
EDCS-75300231.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Gaius Aulius Secundus, a veteran, and his wife Arria Agathemeris, who set it up for herself and her husband during their lifetime, with a mention of Quintus Arrius Priscus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-75300232.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld. Gnaeus Sulpicius Proculus, the most dutiful grandson, placed this for his most dutiful grandmother, Numeria Victorina.
EDCS-75300232.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Numeria Victorina, set up by her grandson Gnaeus Sulpicius Proculus, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-75300234.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription mentioning Caesar as consul, possibly from the Roman Republic or early Empire.

EDCS-75300236.translation: To his most dutiful and deserving parents, Gaius Alasinius Valentinus, a centurion of the 8th Cohort of Volunteers, set up this monument.
EDCS-75300236.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a centurion for his parents.

EDCS-75700071.translation: To perpetual security. Saufeius Valentinus, while alive, placed this tombstone for his beloved Ursino, who died less than 25 years old. Saufeius Valentinus also placed this tombstone, alive.
EDCS-75700071.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of a young man named Ursino and was set up by Saufeius Valentinus, possibly his partner or owner, who also set up his own tombstone while alive.

EDCS-77200008.translation: Emperor Caesar Gaius Julius Verus Maximinus, Pious, Fortunate, Pontifex Maximus, [with tribunician power II(?), Imperator III]...
EDCS-77200008.summary: This is a fragment of an inscription recording the titles and honors of the emperor Maximinus Thrax, likely from a public monument, dating to his second tribunician power and third acclamation as Imperator.

EDCS-78300155.translation: Pinnius Dalmatius placed this tomb alive for himself and his family. Anyone who wishes to move it from its place or bury bodies here will incur a penalty of one hundred thousand denarii to the treasury.
EDCS-78300155.summary: This is a funerary epitaph with a penalty clause, set up by Pinnius Dalmatius for himself and his family, intended to prevent disturbance of the tomb.

EDCS-78300157.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. C. Maxima placed this for her well-deserving husband, Gaius Tatius Sabinus.
EDCS-78300157.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a wife for her husband.

EDCS-78300160.translation: ... 6 months. M. MAR. Titus Tarc[---] Agath[---] ADH
EDCS-78300160.summary: This is a fragmented inscription, possibly funerary, with a duration of 6 months and some names or titles.

EDCS-78300161.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. Lucretius Ferox set this up for Lucretia Verecunda, his mother.
EDCS-78300161.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a son for his mother.

EDCS-78300162.translation: ... deceased [---] years. Titus Flavius Hospes [set this up] for the deserving.
EDCS-78300162.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph recording the death of an unnamed person and mentioning Titus Flavius Hospes as the dedicator.

EDCS-78300164.translation: Gaius Valerius Telesphorus, procurator of the twentieth (inheritance tax?), made this for his dearest freedwoman and wife, Aerina, daughter of Gaius, from Milan, who lived 30 years.
EDCS-78300164.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a freedman for his deceased wife, an Aerina, who was also a freedwoman, with details of her origin and age.

EDCS-78300167.translation: To the Spirits of the Departed. [---]osius Ve[---]
EDCS-78300167.summary: This is a very fragmented funerary epitaph, possibly beginning with a name.

EDCS-78300177.summary: This inscription is fragmentary and appears to be a dedication or memorial related to a daughter, with the dedicator unspecified.

EDCS-78300181.translation: To Liber and Libera. Tiberius Claudius Antonius willingly fulfilled his vow.
EDCS-78300181.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to the deities Liber and Libera by Tiberius Claudius Antonius.

EDCS-78300182.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest. Aurrelius...
EDCS-78300182.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a dedication to Jupiter Best and Greatest, with the dedicator partially preserved.

EDCS-78300749.translation: To the incomparable... Pollux, freedman, of the twentieth degree of freedom...
EDCS-78300749.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription possibly dedicated to an individual of high status by their freedman, Pollux.

EDCS-78300754.translation: ...of the magistri of the gate's construction. Gaius Catellius, son of Marcus, Gaius Vettius, son of Lucius, Marcus Annaeus, son of Quintus Med..., supervised the construction with funds from outside the portanum.
EDCS-78300754.summary: This inscription records the supervision of a gate's construction, funded externally, by several individuals acting as magistri, likely in the Imperial period.

EDCS-78300755.translation: ...son of Gaius, Vi..., son of Libo, ...us, son of Quintus, served as quaestors and supervised the construction of the tower.
EDCS-78300755.summary: This fragmentary inscription records that two individuals, identified by their patronymics and patronal relationships, served as quaestors and oversaw the construction of a tower.

EDCS-78400005.translation: Gaius(?) Ferasius, son of Gaius, Urbanus(?), a member of the college of six, an Augustalis.
EDCS-78400005.summary: This inscription records the name and civic/religious offices (member of the duoviri, Augustalis) of Gaius Ferasius Urbanus, likely from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-78400006.translation: Tertia, daughter of Marcus, and Gaius Petronius, a member of the college of six...
EDCS-78400006.summary: This fragmentary inscription appears to record a dedication or memorial involving Tertia and Gaius Petronius, who held the office of duovir.

EDCS-78400014.summary: This is a simple funerary or dedication inscription identifying Aninia Saturnina, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-78400018.summary: This is a simple inscription identifying Annaea Ermiona as the freedwoman of Quintus, likely on a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-78400027.translation: To Aelia, his wife. Gaius Iunius Cissus made this while alive.
EDCS-78400027.summary: This is a funerary monument set up by Gaius Iunius Cissus for his wife Aelia, likely dating to the 1st or 2nd century CE.

EDCS-78400028.translation: To Papirius, son of Lucius. Valeria, daughter of Valerius...
EDCS-78400028.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription honoring a man named Papirius and a woman named Valeria, with an uncertain date.

EDCS-78400029.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Baleria Posilla placed this for herself...
EDCS-78400029.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Baleria Posilla for herself and possibly others, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-79500127.translation: To Faustina Augusta, daughter of Marcus Aurelius Antoninus. From public funds.
EDCS-79500127.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Empress Faustina, daughter of Marcus Aurelius, funded by public money, likely from the Antonine period.

EDCS-80700045.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Quintus set this up while alive for his wife Danae and his daughter Quintina, and for himself.
EDCS-80700045.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by Quintus for his wife Danae and daughter Quintina, as well as himself, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-80700046.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Calvus, aged 29...
EDCS-80700046.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription recording the age of a deceased person named Calvus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-81300024.translation: To the spirits of the departed. ... daughter of ...artem...
EDCS-81300024.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription possibly honoring a daughter, with an uncertain date.

EDCS-81300027.translation: ... soldier of the XI Claudia Pia Fidelis legion, in the century of Caecilius Ma[--], aged 35 with [X] years of service. Quintinus, the optatio, his heir arranged for this to be made.
EDCS-81300027.summary: This is a funerary inscription for a soldier of the Legio XI Claudia Pia Fidelis, set up by his heir Quintinus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-82800027.translation: Marcus Valerius Macer, son of Marcus, of the Tromentina tribe, a veteran of the 10th cohort of the Urban Cohort, ordered this to be made by his will. His heir arranged for it to be made.
EDCS-82800027.summary: This funerary inscription records the posthumous arrangement for a tomb by Marcus Valerius Macer, a veteran of the urban cohort, likely dating to the Roman Imperial period.
